title: Mandarin Orange Bavarian
text: Mandarin Orange Bavarian. Ingredients: 1 (3 oz.) pkg. jello (pineapple, grapefruit or orange); 2 Tbsp. sugar; 1 c. boiling water; 2 (11 oz.) cans Mandarin orange sections; 1 tsp. grated orange rind; 1 (8 1/2 oz.) can crushed pineapple; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. Cool Whip. Directions: Dissolve gelatin and sugar in boiling water. Drain fruits, reserving 3/4 cup of the combined syrups. Add the measured syrup and orange rind to gelatin. Chill until slightly thickened (approximately 1 to 2 hours). Stir whipped topping into gelatin mixture until blended. Fold in fruits. Pour into mold and chill until firm.

title: No Boil Lasagna
text: No Boil Lasagna. Ingredients: 1 lb. Louis Rich ground turkey; 1 clove garlic, minced; 1 (32 oz.) jar spaghetti sauce; 3/4 c. water; 8 oz. lasagna noodles; 1/2 c. chopped onion; 16 oz. cottage cheese; 2 eggs, beaten; 1/8 tsp. salt; 1 Tbsp. chopped parsley; 1/2 c. grated Parmesan cheese; 16 oz. grated Mozzarella cheese. Directions: Brown ground turkey; add onion and garlic. Cook until onion is transparent. Reduce heat. Add spaghetti sauce and water. In a medium bowl, combine cottage cheese, eggs, salt, parsley and Parmesan cheese. Preheat oven to 375°.

title: Reuben Casserole
text: Reuben Casserole. Ingredients: 1 (7 oz.) can sauerkraut, drained; 1 small tomato, sliced; 2 Tbsp. Thousand Island dressing; 1 (4 oz.) pkg. sliced corned beef, cut in bite size pieces; 3/4 c. Swiss cheese, shredded; 1/8 tsp. caraway seed; 1 c. rye bread crumbs (soft); 1 Tbsp. butter or margarine. Directions: Spread sauerkraut in baking dish. Top with sliced tomato, dressing and corned beef. Sprinkle with cheese and caraway seed. Toss rye bread crumbs with butter and sprinkle over casserole. Bake, uncovered, at 375° for 20 to 25 minutes.

title: Merilee'S Chicken And Rice Salad
text: Merilee'S Chicken And Rice Salad. Ingredients: 3 c. cooked and diced chicken; 3 Tbsp. salad oil; 2 Tbsp. prepared orange juice; 2 Tbsp. vinegar; 1 tsp. salt; 3 c. cooked rice; 1 c. mayonnaise; 2 c. green grapes, sliced in half; 1 1/2 c. sliced celery (optional); 1 large c. pineapple chunks, sliced in half and drained; 1 small can mandarin oranges; 3/4 c. slivered almonds. Directions: Marinate chicken for at least 1 hour in salad oil, orange juice, vinegar and salt. Mix together in bowl grapes, celery, pineapple, mandarin oranges, almonds, rice and mayonnaise; add marinated chicken mixture and mix together well. Chill for several hours.

title: Taco Seasoning Casserole
text: Taco Seasoning Casserole. Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. ground beef; 1 pkg. taco seasoning; 1/2 c. chopped bell pepper; 1/2 c. onion, chopped; 1 c. whole kernel corn; 1 can tomato sauce; 1 small pkg. corn bread mix; 1 can fried onion rings; grated cheese. Directions: Brown ground beef, onion and bell pepper. Add taco seasoning. Pour into casserole dish, sprayed with Pam. Mix corn bread according to directions on package, with 1/2 can onion rings. Pour on top. Grate cheese and add rest of onion rings on top. Bake at 400° for about 10 minutes.

title: Oven Fry Orange Roughy
text: Oven Fry Orange Roughy. Ingredients: 1 lb. orange roughy; 1/2 c. flour; 1 c. bread crumbs; 2 egg whites; salt and pepper. Directions: Put bread crumbs in pan and toast at 375° for 8 minutes. Stir occasionally. Put flour on a piece of wax paper, mixed with salt and pepper. Beat egg white and place in shallow bowl. Pat dry orange roughy and coat with flour, then egg white and then toasted bread crumbs. Place fish on baking pan lined with foil and coat it with some vegetable oil. Bake at 375° for 8 minutes. Turn over and bake another 6 minutes.

title: Sugar-Free Apple Pie
text: Sugar-Free Apple Pie. Ingredients: 4 c. sliced, peeled apples; 1/2 c. frozen apple juice (undiluted); 1 1/2 tsp. flour or cornstarch; 1 tsp. cinnamon or apple pie spice. Directions: Mix apples, apple juice, flour and spice. Stir until apples are well coated. Pour into pie shell and top with another pie shell. Make slits on top. Bake at 400° for 40 minutes, until golden brown.

title: 5-Minute Blueberry Pie
text: 5-Minute Blueberry Pie. Ingredients: 1/2 c. sugar; 2 Tbsp. cornstarch; 2 pt. blueberries; 1 (9-inch) crumb crust. Directions: In a medium bowl, combine sugar, cornstarch and 1/2 cup water. Cook on High in microwave oven for 2 minutes or until mixture boils for one minute. Mixture should be thick; stir. Stir in blueberries; cook on High for 3 minutes, stirring once. Pour into crust. Frozen blueberries work well also.

title: Broccoli-Corn Casserole
text: Broccoli-Corn Casserole. Ingredients: 1 pkg. frozen broccoli; 1 can cream-style corn; 1/2 medium onion; 1/2 c. saltine crackers, crushed and divided; 1 egg, well beaten; pepper; 2 Tbsp. melted butter. Directions: Cook broccoli until about half tender. Drain and add 1/4 cup bread crumbs and remaining ingredients. Pour in casserole dish. Mix 1/4 cup bread crumbs with melted butter and sprinkle on top. Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

title: Pear Mincemeat
text: Pear Mincemeat. Ingredients: 8 lb. pears, peeled and cored; 4 large apples, peeled and cored; 4 large oranges, seeded; 1 large lemon, seeded; 3 lb. raisins; 6 c. sugar; 1 1/2 c. vinegar; 1 Tbsp. nutmeg; 1 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 2 Tbsp. ground cloves; 1 tsp. salt. Directions: Grind fruit including peels of lemon and oranges. Cook in a large roaster pan in a 300° oven for 2 hours. (Cook, uncovered.) Stir every 20 minutes.

title: Best Yet Pineapple Cake
text: Best Yet Pineapple Cake. Ingredients: 3 baked layers yellow cake; 1 stick butter (at room temperature); 1 (3 oz.) pkg. cream cheese; 8 oz. sour cream; 1 c. granulated sugar; 1 (20 oz.) can crushed pineapple, undrained; 1 (6 oz.) pkg. frozen coconut; 1 (4 serving size) pkg. instant vanilla pudding mix; 1 (8 oz.) carton whipped topping. Directions: Blend together butter, cream cheese, sour cream and sugar. Add undrained pineapple and coconut; blend. Sprinkle with dry pudding mix as it comes in the package. Fold in whipped topping. Use to fill and frost layers of yellow cake.

title: Zucchini Cake(Bread)
text: Zucchini Cake(Bread). Ingredients: 3 eggs, beaten; 2 c. sugar; 2 c. all-purpose flour; 2 tsp. soda; 1/2 tsp. baking powder; 2 c. peeled, coarsely grated zucchini; 1 c. vegetable oil; 2 tsp. vanilla extract; 2 tsp. ground cinnamon; 1 tsp. salt. Directions: Combine eggs, oil, sugar and vanilla. Mix well. Combine dry ingredients. Add to oil mixture, mixing well. Stir in grated zucchini. Pour batter into a greased and floured 10-inch Bundt pan. Bake at 350° for 55 to 60 minutes. Allow to cool 10 minutes before removing from pan. Serve as is or spoon glaze of powdered sugar over cake.

title: Orange Julius(Drink)
text: Orange Julius(Drink). Ingredients: 1 Concord orange juice; 15 ice cubes; 1 c. sugar; 1 c. water; 1 c. milk. Directions: Put all ingridents into a blender and blend until no more lumps.

title: Apple Dumpling
text: Apple Dumpling. Ingredients: 2 cans crescent rolls, separated; 1 1/4 c. sugar; 1/4 tsp. cinnamon; 2 Granny Smith apples; 1 stick butter; 1 (12 oz.) can Mountain Dew. Directions: Peel and cut each apple into 8 pieces and place in shallow dish. Cook in microwave for 1 minute. Wrap each piece of apple in a crescent roll. Place in greased 9 x 13 pan.

title: German Red Cabbage
text: German Red Cabbage. Ingredients: 1 small head red cabbage; 1 small chopped onion; 2 Tbsp. sugar; 1 tsp. salt; 1 bay leaf; 1/4 c. water; 2 Tbsp. red wine vinegar; 1 chopped apple; 3 slices bacon. Directions: Remove cabbage core; shred cabbage. Add onion, sugar, salt, bay leaf and water. Bring to a boil, then simmer 20 minutes. Add vinegar and apple. Cook additional 3 or 4 minutes. Sprinkle with bacon bits.

title: Chicken Or Turkey Strudel
text: Chicken Or Turkey Strudel. Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese, softened; 1 c. butter or margarine, softened; 2 1/4 c. flour; 1 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. mixed herbs. Directions: In large bowl of electric mixer (or by hand), beat cheese and butter until smooth. Gradually add flour mixed with salt and herbs. When dough holds together, form into a ball, wrap in waxed paper and refrigerate overnight.

title: Easy Holiday Salad
text: Easy Holiday Salad. Ingredients: 1 pkg. frozen raspberries; 1 can whole cranberry sauce; 1 large or 2 small pkg. raspberry jello; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese. Directions: Make jello as usual, using 2 cups boiling water; stir until dissolved. Add frozen raspberries; stir until mixed. Stir in cranberry sauce. Pour half of mixture into dish. Chill until firm. Mix softened cream cheese with small amount of sour cream. Spread over jello mixture. Top with rest of jello mixture. Chill. Cut into squares.

title: Bonbons Mound Candy
text: Bonbons Mound Candy. Ingredients: 2 boxes 10x powdered sugar; 1 can Eagle Brand milk; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 (7 oz.) pkg. fine coconut; 1 stick melted butter; 1 c. chopped pecans; 2 (9 oz.) pkg. semi-sweet chocolate; 1/5 lb. Esso wax paraffin. Directions: Roll the first 6 ingredients into small balls. Combine the chocolate and paraffin together in a double boiler. Dip balls into the chocolate mixture and put chocolate balls on wax paper until hardened.

title: Milk Punch(Enough For Approximately 25 People)
text: Milk Punch(Enough For Approximately 25 People). Ingredients: 1 1/2 qt. softened vanilla ice cream; 3 c. chilled pineapple juice; 1/3 c. orange juice; 3 tsp. lemon juice; 4 c. milk. Directions: Beat softened ice cream in large mixer bowl, gradually adding fruit juices, then milk. Beat until frothy. Pour into chilled bowl. Great for parties and showers.

title: Pork Chop And Potato Bake
text: Pork Chop And Potato Bake. Ingredients: 6 pork chops; 1/2 tsp. seasoned salt; pepper to taste; 1 pkg. hash brown potatoes, thawed; 1/2 c. sour cream; 1 c. cream of celery soup; 1/2 c. milk; 1 c. shredded cheese; 1 can French fried onions. Directions: Brown pork chops. Sprinkle with seasoned salt and pepper; set aside.

title: Holiday Cranberry Muffins(Heartwarming Christmas 1991)
text: Holiday Cranberry Muffins(Heartwarming Christmas 1991). Ingredients: 1 c. raw cranberries, chopped; 1/2 c. raw sugar; 2 c. flour; 3/4 tsp. baking soda; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/4 c. sugar; 1 egg, beaten; 3/4 c. buttermilk; 1/4 c. shortening, melted. Directions: Let cranberries stand overnight in 1/2 cup sugar. Combine dry ingredients. Add liquid ingredients all at once. Stir until moistened. Add cranberries and stir. Fill greased muffin pans 2/3 full. Bake for 20 minutes at 400°. Yields 18.

title: W. V. Coal Miners Strawberries
text: W. V. Coal Miners Strawberries. Ingredients: 1 lb. pkg. pinto beans; 1 ham halks; salt; 2 Tbsp. lard; water. Directions: Wash beans and then put in large pot. Cook pinto beans and ham halks together in water. When half done, add lard and salt. Then cook low until done. You can use hog jowls instead of ham halks but omit the lard.

title: Chocolate Eclair
text: Chocolate Eclair. Ingredients: 1 lb. box graham crackers; 1 large container whipped cream; 1 (6.8 oz.) box vanilla pudding; 1 container premade milk chocolate frosting (approximately 1 1/2 c.). Directions: Layer bottom of a 9 x 13-inch cake pan with graham crackers, then whip pudding as directed (less one cup milk). Let stand for 1 minute, then add container of whipped cream to pudding. Put thin layer on crackers, then another layer of crackers and so on until you end up with a layer of crackers on top. Put frosting in microwave until runny and pour on top of mixture. Refrigerate about 3 hours.

title: Chicken Breasts In Champagne
text: Chicken Breasts In Champagne. Ingredients: 4 whole chicken breasts, boned and skinned; 1/3 c. all-purpose flour; 1 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. ground white pepper; 4 Tbsp. butter; 2 Tbsp. olive oil; 1 1/2 c. champagne; 1 c. heavy cream; sauteed mushrooms. Directions: Flatten chicken breasts with palm of hand. Combine flour, salt and pepper and dust breasts on both sides. Saute full side down in butter and oil for 5 minutes, shaking the pan to prevent sticking. Turn the chicken and add champagne. Cook for about 15 minutes until chicken is done and champagne is reduced by half. Remove to heated platter and keep warm. Add the cream to the pan juices and cook until thickened. Add sauteed mushrooms and pour sauce over the chicken. Serves 6.

title: Pork And Beans To Can
text: Pork And Beans To Can. Ingredients: 8 1/2 lb. beans; 1/3 c. salt; 3 1/2 lb. fresh pork; 8 qt. tomatoes; 2 lb. brown sugar; 1 lb. white sugar; 2 tsp. dry mustard; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1 qt. ketchup; 2 qt. water; 1 tsp. pepper; 1/2 c. cornstarch. Directions: Soak beans overnight; cook with pork. When nearly done add salt. Fill canning jars 3/4 full with beans. Mix rest of ingredients together for sauce. Pour over beans in jars and cold pack for 2 hours.

title: Sopaipillas
text: Sopaipillas. Ingredients: 2 c. flour; 2 tsp. baking powder; 1 tsp. salt; 2 Tbsp. shortening; 3/4 c. cold water; oil (for frying). Directions: Combine dry ingredients in a bowl. Cut in shortening with a pastry blender until mealy. Add water, a tablespoon at a time, and toss with a fork to blend. Turn onto a lightly floured surface and knead until smooth. Roll out as thin as possible. Cut into 2 or 3-inch squares and fry in hot oil, 370°, until puffed and browned. Makes 20 to 30, depending on size. Serve warm with cinnamon sugar or syrup, if desired.

title: Cup Cookies
text: Cup Cookies. Ingredients: 1 c. peanut butter; 1 c. sugar; 1 egg. Directions: Mix all ingredients together. Drop by spoonfuls onto cookie sheet. Press flat. Bake at 325° for approximately eight minutes. Watch carefully for these goodies burn easily.

title: Quesadillas(Appetizers)
text: Quesadillas(Appetizers). Ingredients: 1 pkg. 6-inch flour tortillas; oil; 1 small bag shredded Monterey Jack cheese; 1 small bag shredded sharp Cheddar cheese; 1 c. finely chopped tomato (tiny pieces); dried cilantro (in a jar) or fresh minced. Directions: Fry each tortilla in 1/4 inch of hot oil until crisp and golden, turning once. Drain and place tortillas on paper towels. Sprinkle each tortilla with combined cheese, then tomato pieces and sprinkle of dried cilantro. Microwave each tortilla approximately 1 minute until cheese is melted.* Using scissors cut tortillas into quarters. Serve warm.

title: Soft And Easy Cheese Ball
text: Soft And Easy Cheese Ball. Ingredients: 8 oz. cream cheese; 1 pkg. cheese spread (squeeze pkg.); 1 tsp. Worcestershire sauce. Directions: Set cheeses out until softened to room temperature and mix together. Add Worcestershire sauce. Chill for several hours. Form into ball and roll in chopped nuts. Cheese ball will be very soft. Keep cool.

title: Grandma Honey'S Butter Cookies
text: Grandma Honey'S Butter Cookies. Ingredients: 1/2 lb. butter, softened; 2 Tbsp. vanilla; 1 c. chopped pecans or walnuts; confectioners sugar for dusting; 2 Tbsp. water; 4 large Tbsp. sugar; 2 c. flour. Directions: Cream butter and sugar. Add remaining ingredients. Roll into balls the size of quarters. Bake at 350° for 15 to 20 minutes until lightly browned. Cool. Roll in confectioners sugar. Yields 36 cookies.

title: Yogurt Pies
text: Yogurt Pies. Ingredients: 1 medium tub Cool Whip; 1 small carton yogurt (flavor of fruit); 1/2 c. unsweetened fruit; graham cracker crust. Directions: Fold ingredients together and put in graham cracker crust. If your family doesn't like yogurt, they will like these. (These are great for diabetics or heart patients.)

title: Low-Fat Chicken And Rice Soup(Serves 6 To 8)
text: Low-Fat Chicken And Rice Soup(Serves 6 To 8). Ingredients: 3 c. defatted sodium-reduced chicken broth; 1/3 c. uncooked wild rice; 1/2 c. sliced green onions; 2 shredded carrots; 1 c. skim milk; 2 Tbsp. all-purpose flour; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 1 Tbsp. dry sherry; fresh parsley or chives. Directions: Combine broth and uncooked rice. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 40 to 45 minutes. Stir in onions and carrots and cook for 5 more minutes. Combine milk, flour and pepper. Stir into rice mixture. Cook and stir until slightly thickened and bubbly. Cook and stir 1 minute more. Stir in sherry. Garnish with fresh parsley or chives before serving.

title: Mom Hornke'S Czeck Plum Kuchen
text: Mom Hornke'S Czeck Plum Kuchen. Ingredients: 1/2 c. milk; 1 cake yeast (dry yeast may be used); 2 c. flour; 3 egg yolks; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. sugar; 1/4 lb. sweet butter; 2 tsp. sugar; 1 tsp. lemon rind. Directions: Combine milk and 1/2 teaspoon of sugar. Heat to lukewarm. Crumble in yeast and let rise. With fingers, crumble butter into sifted flour. Add sugar/milk/yeast mixture, salt, slightly beaten egg yolks and lemon rind. Mix well until dough is medium stiff. Divide into 2 parts and refrigerate or freeze one-half for another time.

title: Jello Cream Salad
text: Jello Cream Salad. Ingredients: 1 (15 oz.) can crushed pineapple; 2 (3 oz.) pkg. lime Jell-O; 2 (3 oz.) pkg. cream cheese; 1 c. whipped cream or Cool Whip; 1 c. chopped nuts. Directions: Bring pineapple to boiling point. Add Jell-O and stir until dissolved. Refrigerate until it begins to set. Stir in cream cheese. Fold in whipping cream and nuts. Add cherries, if desired. Mold. Serves 8 to 10.

title: The 8-Minute Light 'N Fruity Pie
text: The 8-Minute Light 'N Fruity Pie. Ingredients: 1 pkg. (3 oz.) jello, any flavor; 2/3 c. boiling water; 2 c. ice cubes; 1 tub (8 oz.) Cool Whip; 1 c. fresh fruit (sliced strawberries, peaches, bananas, raspberries or blueberries).; OR you can use 1 (8 oz.) can of drained apricots, sliced peaches, fruit cocktail, or pineapple.; 1 pkg. graham cracker crust. Directions: Dissolve jello completely in boiling water; stirring about 3 minutes. Add ice cubes and stir until jello is thickened, about 2-3 minutes. Remove any unmelted ice. Using a wire whisk, blend in Cool Whip; then whip until smooth. Fold in fruit. Chill, if necessary, until mixture will mound up. Spoon into prepared pie crust. Chill 2 hours. Garnish, if desired.

title: Garlic Roasting Chicken
text: Garlic Roasting Chicken. Ingredients: 1 roasting chicken; 4 cloves garlic, peeled, smashed and finely chopped; 3 Tbsp. oleo; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Rinse chicken and pat dry with paper towels. Stir butter (oleo) and garlic together in small bowl until well blended. Using your fingers, gently loosen skin on breast and smear about 2 teaspoons of oleo mixture under skin. Spread remaining mixture over outside of chicken. Add salt and pepper. Bake on rack in pan at 375° for approximately 65 minutes.

title: Shrimp Cheese Ball
text: Shrimp Cheese Ball. Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese, softened; 1/2 tsp. prepared mustard; 1 tsp. soy sauce; 1 tsp. grated onion; 1 tsp. lemon juice; dash of paprika; 1 (4 1/2 oz.) can tiny shrimp, drained; chopped nuts. Directions: Combine all ingredients (except nuts) and chill for several hours. Form into balls and roll in nuts. Serve with crackers.

title: Wonderful Meat Loaf
text: Wonderful Meat Loaf. Ingredients: 2 slices bread (remove crusts); 1 c. milk; 1 1/2 lb. ground beef; 2 eggs; 1/4 c. onion, chopped; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 can cream of celery soup; 3 Tbsp. brown sugar; 1/4 c. catsup; 1/4 tsp. nutmeg; 1 tsp. mustard. Directions: Soak bread in milk. Add meat, eggs, onions, salt, pepper and soup. Mix thoroughly. Shape in 9 x 5 x 3-inch loaf pan. Combine brown sugar, catsup, nutmeg and mustard; spread over meat loaf. Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

title: Yummy Apple Pie
text: Yummy Apple Pie. Ingredients: 3 c. grated apples; 1 1/2 c. white sugar; 1 stick margarine; 1 egg, beaten; 1/2 tsp. allspice; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon. Directions: Mix all ingredients. Pour into unbaked pie shell. Bake at 325° for 1 hour. Cool before slicing.

title: Million Dollar Bars
text: Million Dollar Bars. Ingredients: 1 stick margarine; 2 c. graham cracker crumbs; 1 (6 oz.) pkg. chocolate chips; 1 (6 oz.) pkg. butterscotch chips; 1 c. chopped pecans; 1 can coconut; 1 can Eagle Brand condensed milk. Directions: Melt margarine in bottom of a 9 x 13-inch Pyrex baking dish. Sprinkle crumbs over margarine and stir. Add layers of chocolate chips, pecans, butterscotch chips and coconut. Pour the milk over top and bake at 325° for 30 minutes. Cool in pan and cut into bars.

title: Southern Pork Loin
text: Southern Pork Loin. Ingredients: 2 to 2 1/2 lb. fresh pork loin (bone-in); 1/2 c. chopped onion; 1/2 c. chopped celery; 2 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 3 Tbsp. tomato puree; 2 Tbsp. cider vinegar; 2 Tbsp. molasses. Directions: For basting sauce, saut onions and celery in the butter until soft. Add tomato puree, vinegar and molasses. Blend well. Place meat on a roasting rack in pan. Brush with part of the sauce. Bake at 350° for about 1 1/2 hours or until pork is tender and juices run clear when pierced with a fork. Do brush the pork from time to time during cooking for best flavor. Cooking time depends on thickness of meat. Makes 4 servings.

title: Strawberry Cake-Pie
text: Strawberry Cake-Pie. Ingredients: 1 baked pie crust; 3 egg yolks; 1 c. granulated sugar; 1/4 c. orange juice; 2 Tbsp. lemon juice; 1 tsp. grated orange rind; 1 c. strawberries; 3 egg whites, beaten; 1/4 tsp. salt. Directions: Beat yolks. Add sugar and beat for 1 minute. Add juices, rind, berries, whites and salt. Pour into baked shell and bake for 30 minutes in slow oven at 350°. Cool and serve plain or topped with whipped or ice cream.

title: Six Can Casserole
text: Six Can Casserole. Ingredients: 1 small can evaporated milk; 1 small can boned chicken; 1 can chicken and rice soup; 1 can cream of celery or mushroom soup; 1 can English peas, drained; 4 hard-boiled eggs, chopped; 1 small can chow mein noodles. Directions: Mix all together in casserole and bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

title: Iced Tea
text: Iced Tea. Ingredients: 2 c. water; 6 tea bags; sugar. Directions: Bring water to a boil. Pour tea bags in a teapot or glass container. Pour the hot water over the tea bags and let sit for 5 minutes. Remove the tea bags. If you want to sweeten the tea, now is the best time to add sugar. Add water until it is the strength you like. Let sit until cool and pour over ice to serve.

title: Dip(Microwave)
text: Dip(Microwave). Ingredients: 1 (6 oz.) pkg. cream cheese; 1 can Hormel chili (no beans); 1 small can green chili peppers, drained and chopped; 1 onion, chopped; 1 (8 or 10 oz.) pkg. yellow/white shredded cheese; tortilla chips or crackers. Directions: Spread cream cheese on glass pie tin. Pour chili over. Add onion and peppers. Top with cheese. Microwave until cheeses melt and chili is heated.

title: Greek Salad Dressing
text: Greek Salad Dressing. Ingredients: 3 Tbsp. lemon juice; 3 Tbsp. water; 1 envelope Good Seasons lemon and herbs or Italian salad dressing mix; 2/3 c. olive oil; 3 Tbsp. Feta cheese, crumbled (optional); 1/2 tsp. dried oregano leaves. Directions: Combine lemon juice, water and salad dressing mix in cruet or jar. Shake well. Add olive oil, Feta cheese and oregano. Shake well. Makes 1 cup.

title: Nancy'S Chili
text: Nancy'S Chili. Ingredients: 1 lb. Jimmy Dean pork sausage (regular or hot); 1 1/2 lb. ground beef; 2 large onions, chopped; 1 T. olive oil; 1 (28 oz.) can stewed tomatoes; 1 (28 oz.) can red kidney beans, drained; 1 (7 oz.) can chopped green chilies; 1 (10 3/4 oz.) can condensed chicken broth; 1 t. ground cumin; 1 t. chili powder; 1 t. black pepper; 1 t. oregano; 1/4 t. ground cloves; 1 medium clove garlic, chopped; 1 stalk celery, chopped; 1 green pepper, chopped. Directions: In frying pan, cook sausage well and drain off excess fat; set aside. Brown ground beef in frying pan and drain off excess fat; set aside. Brown onions in olive oil in large heavy pot. Add cooked sausage, ground beef and remainder of ingredients except green pepper. Stir well and simmer covered for 45 minutes. Add chopped green pepper and simmer 15 minutes longer.

title: Glazed Carrots
text: Glazed Carrots. Ingredients: 2 lb. carrots; 1/2 c. water; 2 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 1/4 tsp. cinnamon; 2 Tbsp. honey; 2 tsp. lemon juice; 1/2 c. chopped toasted walnuts (optional). Directions: Peel carrots and cut into 3-inch strips. Place in a large skillet. Add water and butter. Cover and cook until tender-crisp, about 5 to 10 minutes, adding more water if necessary. When carrots are tender, liquid should be nearly gone. Gently stir in the cinnamon, honey and lemon juice. Simmer for a few minutes. Add walnuts, if desired and heat for 1 minute longer.

title: Mexican Pizza
text: Mexican Pizza. Ingredients: 8 oz. cream cheese; 15 oz. can chili with beans; 8 oz. grated Monterey Jack cheese; 2 chopped green onions; 4 oz. can diced green chilies; 1 can sliced black olives. Directions: Place ingredients in order listed in greased baking dish. Bake at 325° for about 30 minutes. Serve warm with corn chips.

title: Meatballs In Sauce
text: Meatballs In Sauce. Ingredients: 2 lb. ground beef; 1 c. bread crumbs; 2/3 c. minced onion; 1/2 c. milk; 2 eggs; 2 Tbsp. parsley; 1 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 1 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1 jar spaghetti sauce. Directions: Mix. Form into small balls (walnut size). Bake in a 350° oven for 25 minutes. Turn as needed. Prepare sauce.

title: Herbed Broccoli
text: Herbed Broccoli. Ingredients: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen broccoli; 1 c. cooked rice; 1 c. shredded sharp cheese; 2 eggs, slightly beaten; 2 Tbsp. margarine; 1/2 c. milk; 2 Tbsp. chopped onion; 1/2 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. ground thyme. Directions: Cook broccoli according to package directions; drain. Combine broccoli and remaining ingredients. Spoon into greased, shallow 1-quart casserole dish. Bake at 350° for 20 to 25 minutes or until set.

title: Taters
text: Taters. Ingredients: 1 stick margarine, melted; 1/2 c. flour; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. garlic salt; 1/2 tsp. chili powder; 6 potatoes; 1/2 c. Parmesan cheese; 1 tsp. pepper; 1/2 tsp. onion salt. Directions: Melt butter in a 9 x 13-inch pan. Wash and wedge potatoes. Soak wedges in cold water for 1/2 hour. Mix together dry ingredients. Coat wedges with mixture. Place in pan on top of melted butter. Bake at 375° for 40 minutes, turning once after 20 minutes.

title: Indian Fry Bread
text: Indian Fry Bread. Ingredients: 2 c. self-rising flour; 1 c. buttermilk. Directions: Mix together and put out on floured board and knead. Let stand 10 minutes. Pinch off and pull apart pieces big enough to roll out to the size of a tostada shell. Roll out each piece and fry in deep fat until light brown. Turn only once.

title: Biftech Au Poivre Vert
text: Biftech Au Poivre Vert. Ingredients: 1 to 1 1/2-inch thick sirloin steak or 1 1/2 lb. rib steak; 1/2 tsp. Dijon mustard; 1 Tbsp. crushed green peppercorns; 1 Tbsp. margarine; 1/4 c. evaporated skimmed milk; 1 Tbsp. cognac. Directions: Trim steak of all fat and quickly dip into cold water. Drain, but do not pat dry. Apple Pam to a skillet; sear the steak on both sides over high heat. Lower the heat to cook as desired, adding a little extra water as necessary. Place the cooked steak on a warm serving platter while preparing the sauce.

title: Jello Cheesecake
text: Jello Cheesecake. Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese; 1 c. sugar; 1/4 tsp. vanilla; 1 small pkg. lemon jello; juice of 1 lemon; 8 oz. carton Cool Whip; 1 graham cracker crust; 1 can cherry pie filling. Directions: Cream first three ingredients together well. Dissolve jello in 1 cup boiling water. Refrigerate until cold, not thick. Pour into cheese and sugar mixture. Add juice of lemon. Stir in Cool Whip and mix lightly. Pour lemon mixture into a graham cracker crust. Top with cherry pie filling.

title: No-Crust Coconut Pie
text: No-Crust Coconut Pie. Ingredients: 2 c. milk; 1 c. sugar; 4 eggs; 1/2 c. self-rising flour; 1/2 c. margarine or butter; 2 tsp. vanilla; 1 1/2 c. coconut. Directions: Combine all ingredients, except coconut; mix well. Stir in coconut. Pour into a 9-inch pie plate. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes.

title: Chicken Cacciatore(30 Minutes)
text: Chicken Cacciatore(30 Minutes). Ingredients: 1 lb. boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut into strips; 1 medium onion, sliced; 1 medium green pepper, cut into thin strips; 1 Tbsp. oil; 1 (8 oz.) can stewed tomatoes; 1 (8 oz.) can tomato sauce; 1 tsp. garlic powder; 1/4 tsp. dried oregano leaves; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper. Directions: Cook and stir chicken, onion and green pepper in hot oil in large skillet until lightly browned. Add tomatoes, tomato sauce, garlic powder, oregano, salt and pepper. Bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer 5 minutes. Meanwhile, prepare 4 servings rice. Serve chicken and vegetables over rice.

title: Jewish Apple Cake(From 1975 Edition)
text: Jewish Apple Cake(From 1975 Edition). Ingredients: 4 medium apples; 4 tsp. sugar; 2 tsp. cinnamon; 4 eggs; 2 c. sugar; 1 c. oil; 3 c. flour; 3 tsp. baking powder; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. orange juice. Directions: Grease and flour a 9-inch tube pan. Slice apples and in separate bowl mix 4 teaspoons sugar and the cinnamon.

title: American Cheese Tortilla Roll-Ups
text: American Cheese Tortilla Roll-Ups. Ingredients: 2 (7-inch) flour tortillas; 4 Tbsp. peanut butter, divided; 4 tsp. honey, divided; 4 slices (2/3 oz. each) American cheese, divided; 2 small lettuce leaves, flattened and divided. Directions: Place tortillas on a flat surface. Spread each tortilla with 2 tablespoons peanut butter. Drizzle with 2 teaspoons honey. Arrange cheese slices over honey. Top with lettuce leaves. Roll up tightly jelly-roll style. Secure with a toothpick. Cut rolls crosswise in halves, then into 1-inch thick slices. Or, wrap uncut rolls in plastic wrap. Refrigerate up to 24 hours. Slice just before serving. Makes 4 portions.

title: Grape Blueberry Salad
text: Grape Blueberry Salad. Ingredients: 1 c. blueberry pie filling; 2 small boxes grape Jell-o; 2 c. boiling water; 1 small can crushed pineapple, drained. Directions: Dissolve Jell-o in boiling water. Add all other ingredients, stirring well. Congeal in 13 x 9 glass dish.

title: Roast Leg Of Lamb
text: Roast Leg Of Lamb. Ingredients: 1 (5 lb.) leg of lamb; 1/2 c. Madeira; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 c. water. Directions: Heat oven to 325°. Wipe leg of lamb with all natural paper towels. Place fat side up in a roasting pan. Sprinkle with 1/4 teaspoon of salt. Roast, uncovered, until lamb reaches desired doneness, about 2 to 2 1/2 hours. Remove roast to serving platter. Slowly drizzle 1/4 cup of the Madeira over the lamb. Let stand 15 minutes before carving the roast.

title: Sundae Dessert
text: Sundae Dessert. Ingredients: 1 cake, baked and cut into pieces, pound cake or shortcake; 2 boxes instant vanilla pudding; 4 bananas, sliced and mixed in pudding; 2 cans crushed pineapple, drained; 2 cans cherry pie filling; 1 large carton Cool Whip (super size!); chopped nuts; chocolate topping. Directions: In a large punch bowl, layer in this order: 1/2 of cake on bottom, pudding with bananas, cherry pie filling, pineapple and Cool Whip. Repeat layers, then top with nuts and cherries. Refrigerate. Enjoy!

title: Tangy Sachet
text: Tangy Sachet. Ingredients: 1 tsp. mint leaves; 1 tsp. dried orange peel; 6 whole cloves; 4 pieces of material, about 2 1/4 x 3 inches. Directions: Mix together mint, orange peel and cloves. Divide in half. Take 2 pieces of material, placing the wrong sides together. Sew 3 sides together. Fill with half the mixture. Makes great gifts.

title: Easy Cranberries
text: Easy Cranberries. Ingredients: 1 (12 oz.) bag cranberries; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 1/2 c. water. Directions: Place the cranberries in a shallow baking dish. Sprinkle with sugar and add water to cover. Bake in a 350° oven for 45 minutes to 1 hour, until the cranberries pop and form their own glaze. Serve warm. Serves 4.

title: Bill Webber'S Baked Beans
text: Bill Webber'S Baked Beans. Ingredients: 4 (1 lb. 12 oz.) cans B&M baked beans; 1 1/2 c. brown sugar; 4 tsp. mustard; 1 c. ketchup; enough bacon to cover the top of baking dish. Directions: Put half of beans in a baking dish. Add 3/4 cup sugar and ketchup. Add remaining beans and ketchup and add mustard. Break up bacon to cover the top of the baking dish. Bake, uncovered, at 325° for 2 1/2 hours.

title: Low-Fat Snicker Pie
text: Low-Fat Snicker Pie. Ingredients: 1/2 gal. fat free vanilla yogurt, softened; 1 large sugar free jello (instant chocolate pudding); 1/2 c. crunchy peanut butter (Smart Choice) Peter Pan; 1 (8 oz.) carton lite Cool Whip. Directions: Combine all ingredients and mix well. Pour into 2 graham cracker crust pies. Freeze! Cut each pie into 8 pieces. One slice counts 2 fat grams.

title: Cheese And Chilies
text: Cheese And Chilies. Ingredients: 2 eggs; 2 Tbsp. milk; 2 (4 oz.) cans chopped green chilies, drained; 4 oz. Swiss cheese, grated; 4 oz. cheddar cheese, grated; 4 oz. monterey jack cheese, grated. Directions: Beat eggs and milk together. Add green chilies and mix well. Add grated Swiss and cheddar cheeses. Pour into 8x9-inch square pyrex dish. Sprinkle monterey jack cheese on top. Bake at 350° for 30 mins. or until bubbly and brown on top. Serve with crackers.

title: Blackberry Jam Cake
text: Blackberry Jam Cake. Ingredients: 5 eggs; 1 c. margarine, softened; 1 c. sugar; 1 (16 oz.) jar seedless blackberry jam; 1 c. strawberry preserves; 3 c. self-rising flour; 2 tsp. allspice; 2 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. cloves; 1 c. buttermilk. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. In a large bowl, beat margarine and sugar until fluffy. Add eggs, 1 at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir in jam and preserves. Stir together all dry ingredients. Add alternately with buttermilk to jam mixture. Bake for 40 minutes in 3 well-greased cake pans or Bundt pan. Cool for 5 minutes and remove from pan.

title: Cantaloupe Meringue Pie
text: Cantaloupe Meringue Pie. Ingredients: 1 unbaked pie shell; 4 eggs, separated; 1/2 tsp. cream of tartar; 1/2 c. sugar; 2 c. pureed cantaloupe; 1 c. sugar; 1/4 c. cornstarch; 1/2 c. milk; 3/4 c. evaporated milk; 1/4 c. butter or margarine; 1/8 tsp. salt; 2 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Bake pastry shell 9 to 11 minutes. Let cool. Beat 4 egg whites, at room temperature, and cream of tartar at high speed on mixer for 1 minute. Gradually add 1/2 cup sugar, 1 tablespoon at a time, beating until stiff peaks form, 2 to 4 minutes. Combine egg yolks, cantaloupe and next 6 ingredients in a heavy saucepan; stir well. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until thick and bubbly. Remove from heat; stir in vanilla. Pour mixture into prepared pie shell. Spread meringue over hot filling, sealing to edge of pastry. Bake at 400° for 8 minutes, until browned. Let cool.

title: Brazos River Casserole
text: Brazos River Casserole. Ingredients: 1 lb. hamburger; 6 oz. pkg. noodles; 1 can tomato soup; 1 can mushroom soup; pepper; 1 onion, chopped; 1 (8 oz.) jar Cheez Whiz; dash of garlic salt. Directions: Cook noodles and drain. Brown meat and onion with pepper and garlic. Add soups and mix well. Pour in baking dish and top with cheese. Bake 25 minutes in a 350° oven.

title: Raspberry-Rhubarb Salad
text: Raspberry-Rhubarb Salad. Ingredients: 3 c. rhubarb; 1 Tbsp. sugar or sugar substitute; 1 small box raspberry Jell-O (sugar-free); 1 c. unsweetened pineapple juice; 1 tsp. lemon juice; 1 c. diced apples; 1 c. diced celery; 1 c. chopped walnuts. Directions: Heat rhubarb with sugar until soft. Add Jell-O and stir. Add pineapple juice and lemon juice. Refrigerate until partially set. Add the rest of the ingredients and refrigerate again until completely set.

title: Light And Fruity Pie
text: Light And Fruity Pie. Ingredients: 1 (3 oz.) pkg. gelatin (any flavor); 2/3 c. boiling water; 2 c. ice cubes; 1 (8 oz.) carton Cool Whip, thawed; fruit (optional) *; 1 graham cracker crust. Directions: *Orange sections, strawberries or bananas, 1 (8 3/4 ounce) can drained, crushed pineapple or fruit cocktail.

title: Cream Cheese Spread
text: Cream Cheese Spread. Ingredients: 1 pkg. cream cheese; 1/4 c. onion; 12 olives, sliced; clove of garlic; 1 pkg. Buddig chipped beef. Directions: Mix together all ingredients. Serve on crackers.

title: Napple-O-Tata
text: Napple-O-Tata. Ingredients: 5 large sweet potatoes; 3 Tbsp. margarine or butter, divided; 1 (16 oz.) can sliced pineapple; 1/4 c. molasses; 1 c. dark brown sugar; 1/3 c. granulated sugar; 1/3 c. orange juice; 1/2 tsp. vanilla extract; 1 1/2 to 2 tsp. cornstarch; 1/2 tsp. nutmeg; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. ginger. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Peel sweet potatoes. Slice into rounds 1 to 1 1/2-inches thick. Melt 2 of the tablespoons of margarine over low heat. In small bowl, mix cinnamon, nutmeg and ginger.

title: Apple Pie Delight(Diabetic)
text: Apple Pie Delight(Diabetic). Ingredients: pastry for 9-inch two crust pie; 2 Tbsp. Equal Measure or 20 pkg. Equal; 1/4 c. flour; 1/4 tsp. nutmeg; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; dash of salt; 6 c. thinly sliced pared tart apples (4 to 6 Granny Smith); 1 Tbsp. margarine. Directions: Preheat oven to 425°. In a mixing bowl combine Equal Measure, flour, nutmeg, cinnamon and salt. Add dry mixture to apples; toss gently to coat fruit. Fill a pastry lined 9-inch pie plate with apple mixture. Dot with margarine. Cover with top crust, that has slits in it. Seal and flute edge. Bake 15 minutes at 425° and turn oven down to 350° and bake an additional 30 minutes.

title: Golden Harvest Muffins
text: Golden Harvest Muffins. Ingredients: 2 c. all-purpose flour; 2 c. whole wheat flour; 2 c. sugar; 4 tsp. soda; 4 tsp. cinnamon; 1 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. cloves; 4 c. shredded, peeled apples; 1 c. shredded carrots; 1 c. coconut; 1 c. raisins; 1 c. chopped nuts; 1 1/2 c. oil; 4 tsp. vanilla; 3 eggs, beaten; 1/2 c. milk. Directions: Heat oven to 350°. In a 4 quart bowl combine flour, sugar, baking soda, cinnamon, salt and cloves. Add apples, carrots, coconut, raisins and walnuts. Mix well. Add oil, milk, vanilla and eggs. Stir till moistened. Fill cups 3/4 full. Bake at 350° for 20-25 minutes.

title: Butterscotch Cream Cheese Bars
text: Butterscotch Cream Cheese Bars. Ingredients: 1 (12 oz.) pkg. butterscotch morsels; 1/3 c. margarine; 2 c. graham cracker crumbs; 1 (14 oz.) can sweetened condensed milk; 1 c. chopped nuts; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 egg. Directions: In saucepan, melt morsels and margarine. Stir in crumbs and nuts. Press half of mixture firmly into the bottom of a greased 13 x 9-inch pan.

title: Pork Roast Barbecue
text: Pork Roast Barbecue. Ingredients: 1 (4 to 5 lb.) pork loin; 1 1/2 c. water; 1 c. vinegar; 1/2 c. catsup; 1/2 c. Worcestershire sauce; 1 medium onion, chopped; 3 Tbsp. dry mustard; 3 Tbsp. brown sugar; 1 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. crushed red pepper; 1/4 tsp. pepper. Directions: Place roast fat side up in a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan. Insert meat thermometer (not touching bone or fat). Combine remaining ingredients and mix well. Pour over roast. Bake, uncovered, at 325° for 2 1/2 to 3 hours or until thermometer reaches 170°, basting frequently with pan drippings. Let stand 10 to 15 minutes. Serve with remaining sauce.

title: Glazed Ham Steaks
text: Glazed Ham Steaks. Ingredients: 1 c. honey; 1 Tbsp. dry mustard; 1 Tbsp. prepared mustard; 1 tsp. lemon juice; 8 medium ham steaks. Directions: Combine honey, mustard and lemon juice in mixing bowl; beat thoroughly. Grill ham steaks slowly over charcoal or cook under broiler. Brush frequently with honey sauce until brown and glazed on both sides.

title: Fresh String Beans
text: Fresh String Beans. Ingredients: 3 lb. fresh string beans; 1/2 tsp. salt or more to taste; 2 cloves garlic, cut up; 1/2 red bell pepper in strips; 1 medium onion, sliced; 1/4 tsp. dried ground rosemary; 1/2 c. water. Directions: Cook string beans in a steamer or (if no steamer available) in a pot with 1/2 cup water. When almost done, add all ingredients. Toss and let them finish cooking slowly.

title: Shrimply Delicious Cauliflower
text: Shrimply Delicious Cauliflower. Ingredients: 1 medium cauliflower, cooked (flowerets); 1 can cream of shrimp soup; 1/2 c. sour cream; 1/4 c. toasted almonds; parsley. Directions: Cook cauliflower in salted water until tender. Drain. Heat soup; stir in sour cream. Do not boil. Season to taste. Place flowerets in dish; pour on sauce. Sprinkle with almonds and parsley. Serves 6 to 8.

title: Plain Yellow Cake
text: Plain Yellow Cake. Ingredients: 1 3/4 c. flour; 1 tsp. vanilla; 2 eggs; 2/3 c. milk; 1 c. sugar; 1/3 c. butter or other shortening. Directions: Cream shortening and 3/4 cup sugar. Beat egg whites. Add 1/4 cup sugar. Add egg yolks and vanilla. Beat until fluffy. Then fold in other ingredients; blend thoroughly. Pour in two (8-inch) layer cake pans. Grease bottom of pans and line with wax paper. Bake at 350° about 25 minutes.

title: Jello Cookies
text: Jello Cookies. Ingredients: 3/4 c. shortening-part butter; 1/2 c. sugar; 3 oz. pkg. Jello, any flavor; 2 eggs; 1 tsp. vanilla; 2 1/2 c. flour; 1 tsp. baking powder; 1 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. food coloring, match Jello color. Directions: Mix shortening with sugar and Jello. Add eggs and vanilla. Stir together flour, baking powder and salt, add to Jello mixture. Add food coloring. The food coloring should match the flavor of the cookie: lime = green, lemon = yellow, cherry = red. Mix until well combined. The flavor of the Jello you use is the flavor of your cookies. Drop dough by teaspoonfuls on lightly greased cookie sheet. Press with the bottom of a glass that has been buttered and dipped in sugar. Bake at 400° for 6 to 8 minutes. Do not brown.

title: Catfish In Beer Batter
text: Catfish In Beer Batter. Ingredients: 1 c. beer; 2 c. flour; 1 to 2 Tbsp. oil; salt to taste; 2 egg whites, beaten until stiff; 12 catfish fillets. Directions: Let beer stand open for 2 hours. Mix all ingredients, folding in egg whites last. Coat fillets and fry. Very light batter.

title: Fluffy Pink Stuff
text: Fluffy Pink Stuff. Ingredients: 1 can Eagle Brand milk; 1 tsp. lemon juice; 1 can cherry pie filling; 1 medium size can pineapple, drained; 8 oz. sour cream; 1 c. chopped pecans; 1 large carton Cool Whip. Directions: Mix lemon juice with Eagle Brand milk. Add cherry pie filling, pineapple, sour cream and nuts and fold in Cool Whip. Mix well and chill. Will serve 12 to 15 people.

title: Peach Muffins
text: Peach Muffins. Ingredients: 1 (6 oz.) jar baby food peaches; 1 tsp. soda; 10 Tbsp. butter, softened; 1 c. sugar; 2 eggs; 1 1/4 c. flour; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Grease muffin tins for 12 muffins. Stir baking soda into peaches; they will foam up. Cream butter and sugar. When smooth, add eggs, one at a time. Alternately add flour and peaches, then salt and vanilla. Fill each cup nearly to the top. Bake 20 to 25 minutes.

title: 8 Layer Casserole(2-Quart)
text: 8 Layer Casserole(2-Quart). Ingredients: 1 c. uncooked rice; 1 can drained corn; 1 can diced tomatoes; 1/2 c. water; 1/2 c. chopped onion; 1/2 c. chopped green pepper; 1 lb. ground meat (hamburger, venison, sausage); 1 can tomato sauce (small). Directions: Layer. Cover and bake 1 hour. Uncover and bake 30 minutes.

title: Sugar-Free Fudge
text: Sugar-Free Fudge. Ingredients: 1/4 c. diet margarine; 2 oz. unsweetened baking chocolate, chopped; 24 packets Equal sweetener; 1 tsp. vanilla extract; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. reduced-calorie cream cheese, softened; 1/2 c. chopped nuts. Directions: In a medium saucepan, melt margarine over low heat. Add chocolate and stir just until melted. Remove from heat; stir in sweetener and vanilla. Add cream cheese and beat until smooth. Stir in nuts. Spread mixture in a buttered 8-inch square pan. Cover and refrigerate until firm. Cut into 1-inch squares. Store in an airtight container in refrigerator. Yields 64 squares of fudge.

title: Old Time Buttermilk Pie
text: Old Time Buttermilk Pie. Ingredients: 9 inch unbaked pie shell; 1/2 c. butter; 2 c. sugar; 3 tsp. flour, rounded; 3 eggs; 1 c. buttermilk; 1 tsp. vanilla; dash of nutmeg (optional). Directions: Soften butter and add sugar; cream together. Add flour and eggs; beat well. Stir in buttermilk, vanilla, and nutmeg. Pour into unbaked pie shell. Bake for 45 to 50 minutes at 350°. Place on wire rack to cool before serving.

title: Fish Turbot
text: Fish Turbot. Ingredients: 1 lb. haddock fillet; 1/4 c. butter; 1/4 c. flour; 1/2 tsp. salt; 2 c. milk; 1/2 to 3/4 c. white Cheddar cheese, shredded; seasoned bread crumbs. Directions: Gently simmer fish (covered) in 1/2 cup water until flakes; drain. In saucepan, melt butter, add flour and salt and slowly add milk; stir until thickened. Add cheese; when melted, pour over the haddock in a casserole. Spread bread crumbs on top. Cover and bake at 350° until bubbly.

title: Caramel Dip
text: Caramel Dip. Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese; 6 oz. caramel ice cream topping; 6 oz. butterscotch ice cream topping; chopped peanuts (optional). Directions: In a mixing bowl, blend all ingredients by heating until smooth. Serve with sliced apples and chopped peanuts.

title: Chocolate Marshmallow Slices
text: Chocolate Marshmallow Slices. Ingredients: 2 c. semi-sweet chocolate chips (12 oz. pkg.); 1/2 c. butter or margarine; 6 c. miniature marshmallows (10 1/2 oz. pkg.); 1 c. finely chopped nuts; additional chopped nuts. Directions: In medium saucepan, over low heat, melt chocolate chips and butter, stirring constantly, until blended. Remove from heat; cool 5 minutes. Stir in marshmallows and 1 cup nuts; do not melt marshmallows.

title: Icebox Slaw
text: Icebox Slaw. Ingredients: 1 medium head cabbage, chopped; 1 chopped onion; 1 diced green pepper; 1 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. celery seed; 3/4 c. sugar; 3/4 c. cooking oil; 3/4 c. white vinegar. Directions: In saucepan, bring to a boil the last 5 ingredients and pour over the vegetables. Cover tightly and put in refrigerator. This keeps for several days.

title: Cap'N Crunch Cookies
text: Cap'N Crunch Cookies. Ingredients: 1 c. Cap'n Crunch cereal; 1 c. Rice Krispies cereal; 1 c. pretzels; 1 c. pecans, crushed; 1 pkg. almond bark. Directions: Melt almond bark. Add all other ingredients. Drop on wax paper.

title: Handy Casserole
text: Handy Casserole. Ingredients: 1 c. chopped onion; 2 Tbsp. margarine; 1 green pepper, cut in strips; 1 1/2 lb. lean ground beef; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 1 tsp. sugar; 1 qt. or 1 (1 lb. 12 oz.) can whole tomatoes; 1 (15 oz.) can tomato sauce; 2 c. water; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. uncooked lasagna noodles; 8 oz. sliced Mozzarella cheese. Directions: Saute for 3 minutes the onion, margarine and green pepper. Brown beef, breaking up the meat as it cooks. Add salt, pepper and sugar. Stir in tomatoes, tomato sauce and water. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat; simmer 15 minutes.

title: Jiffy Fudge
text: Jiffy Fudge. Ingredients: 2 1/2 c. miniature marshmallows or 10 oz. marshmallow cream; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 2/3 c. evaporated milk; 1/4 c. butter; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1 (12 oz.) pkg. Nestle semi-sweet chocolate chips; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1/2 c. chopped nuts. Directions: Bring to full boil on medium heat, marshmallows, sugar, milk, butter and salt, stirring constantly. While boiling, add chocolate chips until melted. Add nuts, then vanilla. Pour into square pan, buttered. Chill until firm. Makes 2 1/2 pounds.

title: Polish Soup
text: Polish Soup. Ingredients: 1 medium beef roast; 1 small head cabbage; 6 to 8 medium size carrots; 2 whole onions; 2 to 3 potatoes; 1 whole chicken, cooked and deboned; broth; noodles; parsley flakes. Directions: Cook roast, cabbage, carrots and onions together for about 2 hours; add potatoes and cook on low heat another hour. Add chicken and broth; cook low for 1 more hour. Serve over noodles. Sprinkle with parsley flakes.

title: Bauernfruhstuck(Farmer'S Breakfast)
text: Bauernfruhstuck(Farmer'S Breakfast). Ingredients: 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 c. cooked ham, in small cubes; 2 medium tomatoes, peeled; 1 Tbsp. chives, chopped; 4 medium potatoes; 4 bacon strips, cubed; 3 large eggs; 3 Tbsp. milk. Directions: Boil unpeeled potatoes 30 minutes. Rinse under cold water. Peel and set aside to cool. Slice potatoes. In a large frypan cook bacon until transparent. Add the potato slices. Cook until lightly browned.

title: Cornbread Squares
text: Cornbread Squares. Ingredients: 1 c. all-purpose flour; 1 c. yellow cornmeal (plain); 1/4 c. sugar; 2 tsp. baking powder; 3/4 tsp. salt; 2 eggs, beaten; 1 c. milk; 1/4 c. vegetable oil. Directions: In a mixing bowl, combine flour, cornmeal, sugar, baking powder and salt. Add eggs, milk and oil. Blend just until moistened. Spoon into a greased baking pan (8 or 9-inch square). Bake at 400° for 20 to 25 minutes.

title: Venison Spiedies
text: Venison Spiedies. Ingredients: 3 pkg. Good Seasons herb and garlic dressing; 2 Tbsp. Lawry's season salt; 1 gal. venison, cubed; 1 pkg. Good Seasons Italian dressing. Directions: Mix Good Seasons dressings by package directions; add seasoned salt. Add meat. Marinate for 7 days, stirring daily. Use and or freeze in juice.

title: Joyce'S Salad
text: Joyce'S Salad. Ingredients: 1 pkg. lemon gelatin; 1 c. hot water; 1/2 c. cold water; 1/2 c. mayonnaise; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. thinly sliced radishes; 1/2 c. diced celery; 2 Tbsp. chopped green pepper. Directions: Dissolve the gelatin in the hot water and add the cold water. Cool slightly. Add the remaining ingredients. Mix well and chill in mold. Serve.

title: Traveling Cupcakes
text: Traveling Cupcakes. Ingredients: 3 sq. semi-sweet chocolate or white chocolate; 1 3/4 c. pecans, broken; 1 3/4 c. sugar; 1 c. plain flour; 4 eggs; 1 tsp. vanilla; 2 sticks margarine. Directions: Melt margarine and chocolate in heavy saucepan. Add nuts and stir until well coated.

title: Dinner Mints
text: Dinner Mints. Ingredients: 3 boxes powdered sugar; 2 1/2 sticks butter; 2 tsp. essence of peppermint; small amount Pet milk. Directions: Work with hands. Knead into play dough consistency. Refrigerate overnight. Mold into any shape on waxed paper. Store in refrigerator or freezer. Serves 300.

title: Pumpkin, Oatmeal And Peanut Cake
text: Pumpkin, Oatmeal And Peanut Cake. Ingredients: 1 stick oleo; 2 c. light brown sugar; 1 c. pumpkin; 2 eggs; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 1/2 c. flour; 1 1/2 c. Quick oatmeal; 2 tsp. pumpkin pie spice; 1 tsp. baking powder; 1 tsp. baking soda; 1/2 c. dry roasted peanuts; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 (12 oz.) pkg. butterscotch chips; 1 c. creamy peanut butter; 1 stick oleo; 1 tsp. vanilla; 2 c. powdered sugar. Directions: Mix by hand and add more powdered sugar, if needed.

title: Irish Cream
text: Irish Cream. Ingredients: 4 large eggs; 1 1/2 c. whiskey; 1/2 tsp. instant coffee; 1 Tbsp. chocolate syrup; 1 (14 oz.) can sweetened condensed milk; 1/2 tsp. coconut extract; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1/8 tsp. almond extract. Directions: Mix in blender 2 minutes; refrigerate.

title: Pumpkin Bars
text: Pumpkin Bars. Ingredients: 1 c. oatmeal; 1 c. all-purpose flour (unsifted); 1 c. sugar; 1 tsp. baking powder; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1 c. fresh or canned pumpkin; 1 egg, slightly beaten; 1 stick butter; 1 tsp. vanilla flavoring; 1/2 c. raisins; 1/2 c. nuts. Directions: In medium bowl, combine oatmeal, flour, sugar, baking powder and salt. Stir to mix. Make well in center; add pumpkin, egg, butter and vanilla. Stir until dry ingredients are moistened. Stir in raisins and nuts. Spread mixture in a 9 x 9 pan that has been greased. Bake at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes or until firm and lightly browned. Let cool in pan. Cut into bars.

title: Yellow Squash Casserole
text: Yellow Squash Casserole. Ingredients: 4 or 5 medium yellow squash, peeled and sliced; 1 medium onion, chopped; 1 can condensed cream of chicken soup; 1 c. sour cream; 1 c. shredded raw carrots; 1 stick (1/2 c.) margarine; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. chicken stuffing mix. Directions: Cook squash and onion in water for 5 minutes. Drain. Mix together soup and sour cream. Fold in shredded carrot, squash and onion. Melt margarine in medium saucepan. Stir in stuffing mix. Spread half of stuffing in greased 8 x 8 x 2-inch baking dish. Pour vegetable mixture over stuffing and top with other half of stuffing. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes. Let stand about 15 minutes before serving.

title: Fish Stew For 25
text: Fish Stew For 25. Ingredients: 10 lb. filleted flounder or rockfish or a mixture; 3 lb. Irish potatoes; 3 lb. bacon, diced; 3 lb. chopped onions; 2 large cans Campbell's tomato juice; 1 (44 oz.) bottle ketchup; 3 large cans tomatoes; 2 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/2 small bottle hot sauce; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Peel and cut up the potatoes. Add water to cover and boil until tender. When cooked, mash potatoes with a potato masher. Cook bacon until brown, then add onions. When the onions are brown, place bacon, onions and potatoes in large pot (12 quart) and combine the mixture. Add tomato juice, tomatoes and ketchup and bring to a boil. Add Worcestershire sauce, hot sauce, salt and pepper. Simmer for 30 minutes. Add fish and bring to a rolling boil. Turn down the heat and let simmer.

title: Buttermilk Bread
text: Buttermilk Bread. Ingredients: 5 to 6 c. flour; 1/4 c. sugar; 2 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 2 pkg. active dry yeast; 1 1/2 c. buttermilk; 1/2 c. water; 1/2 c. margarine; 1 Tbsp. margarine, melted. Directions: Grease two 8 x 4-inch or 9 x 5-inch loaf pans. Lightly spoon flour into measuring cup; level off. In large bowl, combine 2 cups flour, sugar, salt, baking soda and yeast; blend well. In small saucepan, heat buttermilk, water and 1/2 cup margarine until very warm (120° to 130°). (Mixture will look curdled). Add warm liquid to flour mixture. Blend at low speed until moistened; beat 3 minutes at medium speed. Stir in an additional 2 1/2 to 3 cups flour until dough pulls cleanly away from sides of bowl. On floured surface, knead in 1/2 to 1 cup flour until dough is smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes. Place dough in greased bowl; cover loosely with plastic wrap and cloth towel. Let rise in warm place (80° to 85°) until light and doubled in size, 30 to 45 minutes.

title: Christmas Wreath
text: Christmas Wreath. Ingredients: 1/2 c. butter; 30 regular marshmallows; 1 tsp. green food coloring; 3 c. corn flakes; 1 box cinnamon candies. Directions: Melt butter and marshmallows. Allow to melt gradually over low heat. Stir until creamy. Add food coloring; mix good. Add corn flakes. Drop by teaspoon on cookie sheet. Press hole in middle to form a wreath shape. Press cinnamon candy on to appear as berries. Allow to harden. Place layers of wax paper between layers of wreath.

title: Chicken And Spaghetti Casserole
text: Chicken And Spaghetti Casserole. Ingredients: 1 (3 or 4 lb.) fryer; 1 pkg. spaghetti; 1 can cream of mushroom or cream of chicken or cream of celery (if desired); Velveeta cheese; garlic salt; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Debone and cut up chicken into chunks. Cook spaghetti. Add soup and cheese. Cut into chunks. Season with garlic salt, salt and pepper to taste. Heat in a 350° oven until cheese is melted and heated well.

title: Excellent Beef Casserole
text: Excellent Beef Casserole. Ingredients: 3 Tbsp. all-purpose flour; 1 Tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 2 lb. boneless round steak, cut into cubes; 2 Tbsp. cooking oil; 1 c. water; 14 oz. can beef broth; 1 garlic clove, minced; 1 Tbsp. dried minced onion; 1/2 tsp. thyme; 1/4 tsp. crushed dried rosemary; 2 c. sliced fresh mushrooms (or canned); 2 c. frozen peas, thawed or 1 can whole kernel corn drained; 3 c. mashed potatoes (mashed with milk & butter); 1 Tbsp. butter/margarine, melted; Paprika. Directions: In a large resealable bag combine flour, salt & pepper; add beef cubes and shake to coat. In a skillet over medium heat, brown the beef in oil. Place beef and dripping in a greased shallow 2 1/2 qt. baking dish. To skillet add water, broth, garlic, onion, thyme and rosemary; bring to a boil. Simmer uncovered for 5 minutes. Stir in mushrooms. Pour over meat; mix well. Cover and bake at 350° for 1 1/2 to 1 3/4 hours or until beef is tender. Sprinkle corn or peas over meat. Spread potatoes evenly over top. Brush with butter; sprinkle with paprika. Bake 15-20 minutes longer. Yield: 6-8 servings. Great with homemade dinner rolls and a salad.

title: Pepper Steak
text: Pepper Steak. Ingredients: round steak; 2 Tbsp. oil; 1 can tomatoes, undrained; 2 medium green peppers; 2 Tbsp. soy sauce; 1/4 tsp. garlic salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 1/4 tsp. sugar; 1/4 tsp. ginger; 1/4 c. water with 1 beef bouillon cube, dissolved; 1 Tbsp. cornstarch. Directions: Cook round steak in oil. Add tomatoes and peppers to round steak. Add soy sauce, garlic salt, pepper, sugar and ginger to steak mixture. Cook for 10 minutes. Blend cornstarch with beef bouillon. Add to steak mixture. Cook 10 minutes until thickened. Serve over rice.

title: Death By Chocolate
text: Death By Chocolate. Ingredients: 1 chocolate cake mix; 2 (8 oz.) containers Cool Whip; 2 envelopes Dream Whip; 2 pkg. chocolate Jell-O instant pudding; 3 c. cold milk; 4 Heath bars (optional). Directions: Bake cake mix according to instructions on box. Cool completely; break into small pieces. Mix together Jell-O, Dream Whip and milk; beat well. Refrigerate 1 hour.

title: Chicken Desirie
text: Chicken Desirie. Ingredients: 6 or 8 chicken breasts (boneless); 6 or 8 slices Swiss cheese; 1 can cream of chicken soup; 1/2 c. white wine; 1/2 stick margarine. Directions: Spray Pam on a 9 x 13 Pyrex dish. Place cheese on top of each chicken breast. Mix soup and white wine and pour over chicken. Sprinkle herb seasoned bread crumbs over the top and pour margarine over crumbs. Bake, uncovered, at 350° about an hour.

title: Walnut Waffles
text: Walnut Waffles. Ingredients: 3 eggs, beaten; 3/4 c. buttermilk; 3/4 to 1 c. milk; 2 tsp. vanilla; 1/2 c. butter, melted; 2 c. flour; 1 Tbsp. baking powder; 2 tsp. sugar; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. walnuts, chopped. Directions: Combine eggs, milk and vanilla. In separate bowl, combine dry ingredients. Add wet ingredients to dry. Add melted butter. Whisk until batter is smooth, but do not overmix. Nuts can be added at this point, or sprinkled on waffle prior to cooking. Cook waffles until golden brown. Top with whipped cream and grated walnuts, if desired.

title: Mrs. Bretel'S Noodle Recipe
text: Mrs. Bretel'S Noodle Recipe. Ingredients: 1 egg; 1 Tbsp. butter per egg; salt; flour. Directions: Mix well; roll out and cut as desired.

title: Health Food Fudge
text: Health Food Fudge. Ingredients: 2 c. sugar; 1/3 c. cocoa; 1/2 c. milk; 1/4 c. butter or margarine; 3/4 c. high protein baby cereal; 1/2 c. crunchy peanut butter; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Boil sugar, cocoa, milk and butter together over medium heat for one minute. Add cereal, peanut butter and vanilla. Stir fast. Drop on waxed paper to harden.

title: Homemilled Corn Meal Corn Bread
text: Homemilled Corn Meal Corn Bread. Ingredients: 1 c. corn meal, sifted; 1/8 tsp. soda; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1 egg; 1/4 c. self-rising flour; 2 Tbsp. oil; 3/4 c. buttermilk. Directions: Sift together all the dry ingredients. Add egg, buttermilk, and oil. Mix well and pour into hot iron skillet. Preheat oven to 400°. Bake 15 minutes. Flip in pan and brown.

title: Speedy Bean Salad
text: Speedy Bean Salad. Ingredients: 3/4 c. sugar; 2/3 c. vinegar; 1/3 c. vegetable oil; 1 tsp. salt; dash of pepper; 1 can chickpeas; 1 can kidney beans; 1 can Great Northern beans; 1/2 medium chopped onion; 3/4 c. chopped green pepper. Directions: Mix together sugar, vinegar, oil and salt. Rinse and drain beans. Add to vinegar mixture. Add onion and pepper. Let stand overnight.

title: Monster Bars
text: Monster Bars. Ingredients: 6 eggs; 1 lb. brown sugar (2 1/2 c.); 2 c. sugar; 1 Tbsp. vanilla; 1/2 Tbsp. white syrup; 4 tsp. baking soda; 2 sticks margarine; 9 c. oatmeal; 8 oz. chocolate chips; 8 oz. plain M&M's; 1 (18 oz.) jar peanut butter; walnuts (optional). Directions: Mix in order given. Spread on greased cookie sheet pan. Makes 2 (10 1/2 x 15 1/2-inch) pans and 1 (8 x 8-inch) pan or something similar. Bake at 350° for 15 minutes.

title: Mexican Rice Casserole
text: Mexican Rice Casserole. Ingredients: 1 lb. hamburger meat; 1/4 chopped onion; 2 c. Minute rice (uncooked); 1 can zucchini squash; 1 can cream of mushroom soup; 1 can cream of chicken soup; 1 can Ro-Tel; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 1/4 tsp. cumin; 1/4 tsp. garlic powder; 1/4 tsp. oregano; grated Colby or Cheddar cheese. Directions: Brown hamburger; drain. Mix with all ingredients, except cheese, in casserole dish. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes at 350°. Top with cheese the last 5 minutes of baking time. Freezes well.

title: Chicken Pillows
text: Chicken Pillows. Ingredients: chicken breasts; 3 tsp. lemon juice; 2 tsp. oil; oregano; salt; 1 tsp. chopped garlic; butter, melted; phyllo dough. Directions: Cut chicken breasts in one-inch pieces. Marinate in lemon juice, oil, oregano, salt and garlic. Unwrap phyllo dough; place on wax paper and keep covered to keep moist. Cut dough in half lengthwise forming 2 strips each 6-inches wide. Fold in half crosswise; brush with melted butter. Put two pieces of chicken at short end and roll; form a package. Brush with melted butter and bake at 400° for about 15 minutes.

title: Sweet Pickle Recipe
text: Sweet Pickle Recipe. Ingredients: 2 gal. cucumbers, cut in 3/4-inch rounds; boiling water; 1 teacup salt; 1 Tbsp. powdered alum. Directions: Put cucumbers in stone jar or glass. Pour over them 1 gallon boiling water with 1 teacup salt dissolved. Let stand 8 days. On the 8th day, drain off water and wash good. Then pour over gallon boiling water and let stand 24 hours. Ninth day, pour over a gallon boiling water with 1 tablespoon powdered alum; let stand 24 hours. Tenth day, drain and pour over 1 gallon boiling water and let stand 24 hours. Eleventh day, drain and pour over boiling syrup.

title: Better Baked Ziti
text: Better Baked Ziti. Ingredients: 1/2 lb. ziti; 1 (15 oz.) container Ricotta cheese; 3 c. (12 oz.) shredded Mozzarella cheese, divided; 3 c. spaghetti sauce, divided; 1/2 c. grated Parmesan cheese. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. In a large bowl cook ziti until just barely tender; drain and place in a large bowl. Mix the Ricotta and 1/2 of Mozzarella cheese with ziti. Grease a 9 x 13-inch pan. Cover bottom of pan with 1/2 spaghetti sauce. Spoon the ziti mixture into pan. Cover with remaining sauce. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese and top with remaining Mozzarella. Bake 20 to 30 minutes until lightly brown.

title: Cheese Wafers (Snacks)
text: Cheese Wafers (Snacks). Ingredients: 1 1/2 cups (6 oz.) Shredded sharp cheddar cheese; 1/2 cup butter or margarine, softened; 1 cup all-purpose flour; dash of salt; dash of paprika; 1 1/2 cups Cornflakes cereal, crushed; 1/2 cup finely chopped almonds. Directions: Process cheese and butter in a food processor until blended. Add flour, salt and paprika; process until mixture forms a ball, stopping often to scrape down the sides. Add cereal and almonds; process until blended. Stop twice to scrape the sides. Shape dough into 1 inch balls. Place balls about 2 inches apart on an ungreased baking sheet. Flatten each ball in a crisscross pattern with a fork dipped in flour. Bake at 350° for 12 -14 minutes or until lightly browned. Remove to wire racks to cool. Store in an airtight container.

title: Barbara Simon'S Chicken With Cranberries
text: Barbara Simon'S Chicken With Cranberries. Ingredients: 1 (3 to 3 1/2 lb.) chicken or boneless breasts; 1 c. flour; salt and pepper to taste; 1 c. peanut oil; 1 (16 oz.) can whole sweetened cranberries; 1/4 c. peeled and chopped yellow onions; 3/4 c. orange juice; 1/4 tsp. ground cinnamon; 1/4 tsp. ginger. Directions: Cut up chicken; pat dry with paper towels. Mix flour with salt and pepper and dredge each piece of chicken. Pan-fry in peanut oil just until brown, turning once. In the meantime, mix remaining ingredients in a stainless steel saucepan and bring to a boil. Pour this over the chicken and cover. Cook slowly for 35 to 40 minutes or until chicken is very tender.

title: Swiss And Salmon Bites
text: Swiss And Salmon Bites. Ingredients: 3 eggs; 1 can (14 3/4 oz.) premium red or pink salmon, drained (skin and bones removed); 2 tsp. instant minced onion; 1 tsp. parsley flakes; 1 1/2 tsp. lemon and pepper seasoned salt; 1 tsp. dill weed; 1/2 tsp. dry mustard; 1/4 c. dry bread crumbs; 1 c. shredded Swiss cheese; 1 c. milk. Directions: With an electric mixer; beat eggs. Add remaining ingredients and mix well. Spoon salmon mixture into lightly greased mini muffin tins to fill. Bake in 350° oven for 25 to 30 minutes until lightly browned on edges. Cool 5 minutes. Loosen with knife and remove to serving plate. Serve warm. Makes 26 to 28.

title: Miniature Vegetable Crepes
text: Miniature Vegetable Crepes. Ingredients: 1 Tbsp. butter; 1/2 c. all-purpose flour; 1/8 tsp. black pepper; 2 large eggs, slightly beaten; 3/4 c. milk; 1 Tbsp. finely minced green onion; 1 Tbsp. finely minced bell pepper; 1 Tbsp. finely grated carrots. Directions: Melt butter in small saucepan or in the microwave. In a medium bowl, combine the flour, black pepper, eggs and milk. Whisk until smooth. Stir in the melted butter and vegetables. Cover and let the batter rest for at least 45 minutes.

title: Bunch Punch
text: Bunch Punch. Ingredients: 1 (46 oz.) can unsweetened pineapple juice, chilled; 1 liter ginger ale, chilled; 3 qt. raspberry sherbet, softened. Directions: Combine all ingredients in a punch bowl. Makes 5 1/2 quarts.

title: Banana-Apple Bread
text: Banana-Apple Bread. Ingredients: 1/2 c. shortening; 1 c. sugar; 2 eggs; 1 c. bananas, mashed; 1 1/2 c. plain flour; 1 tsp. baking soda; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 c. apple, peeled and finely chopped. Directions: Cream shortening; gradually add sugar, beating well. Add eggs one at a time, beating after each addition. Stir in apple and bananas. Combine flour, soda and salt. Add to creamed mixture, stirring just enough to blend. Spoon batter into greased and floured pan. Bake for 1 hour. Cool in pan 10 minutes. Cool completely on wire rack.

title: Daddy'S Brunswick Stew
text: Daddy'S Brunswick Stew. Ingredients: 1 (5 lb.) Boston butt; 5 lb. stew beef; 1 (4 to 5 lb.) hen; 10 lb. potatoes; 5 lb. onions; 3 cans creamed corn; 3 cans tomatoes; 1 stick margarine; 1/2 bottle ketchup; 1/2 bottle barbecue sauce; 1/2 c. mustard; 1/2 big bottle Tabasco sauce; 1/2 bottle French dressing; 1/2 bottle Worcestershire sauce; 2 Tbsp. pepper and salt. Directions: Cook meat until it comes off the bone; chip up meat. Cook potatoes and onion in meat broth. When done, put meat back in with potatoes and onions. Add other ingredients. Simmer about 1 hour. Stir often.

title: Cranberry Glazed Chef'S Prime
text: Cranberry Glazed Chef'S Prime. Ingredients: 2 to 4 lb. boneless pork rib end roast; 2 tsp. cornstarch; 1/8 tsp. cinnamon; 1/8 tsp. salt; 4 Tbsp. orange juice; 1 c. (16 oz.) whole cranberry sauce. Directions: Combine cornstarch, cinnamon and salt in small saucepan. Stir in orange juice and cranberry sauce. Cook and stir over medium heat until thickened. Set aside.

title: Stuffed Rigatoni
text: Stuffed Rigatoni. Ingredients: 1 stick butter; 1 clove garlic, chopped; 1 lb. ground beef; 1 lb. ground pork; 1/2 c. cream or milk; 2 eggs; 1/2 c. bread crumbs; salt and pepper to taste; 1/2 c. grated Italian cheese. Directions: Saute in pan. When cooked, stuff into partially cooked large rigatoni. In baking dish, put layer of rigatoni and cover with spaghetti sauce over all. Put in oven at 350° for 30 minutes to consume.

title: Pioneer Potato Candy
text: Pioneer Potato Candy. Ingredients: 1/4 c. hot mashed potatoes; 1 tsp. melted margarine; 1 3/4 c. powdered sugar; 1 1/2 c. flaked coconut; dash of salt; 1/4 tsp. grated lemon or orange rind; 1/2 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Combine potatoes and margarine. Gradually add sugar. Beat thoroughly. Add remainings. Mix well. Drop by teaspoons on waxed paper. Let set until firm.

title: Spanish Onions
text: Spanish Onions. Ingredients: 4 or 5 whole onions; 1 can tomatoes; salt; pepper, lots; 2 to 3 Tbsp. dried thyme leaves; butter. Directions: Place onion in baking dish. Add tomatoes. Dot with butter. Sprinkle with salt, pepper and thyme. Bake at 350° for 1 1/2 hours till onion is done.

title: Tomato Soup Congealed Salad
text: Tomato Soup Congealed Salad. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. Knox gelatin; 1/2 c. cool water; 1 c. mayonnaise; 1 1/2 c. chopped celery; 1 Tbsp. chopped bell peppers; 1 small bottle stuffed olives; 3 (3 oz.) cream cheese; 1 c. cream of tomato soup; 1 tsp. grated onion; 1 c. chopped nuts. Directions: Dissolve gelatin in cool water. Heat cream cheese in tomato soup to mix. Cool. Add balance of ingredients and congeal.

title: Pink Lady Day Salad
text: Pink Lady Day Salad. Ingredients: 1 c. crushed pineapple, drained; 1 large pkg. strawberry Jell-O; 1 small pkg. mini marshmallows; 1 c. cottage cheese. Directions: Drain pineapple; save juice. Add enough liquid to dissolve Jell-O. Mix Jell-O as directed. Place over medium heat, stir until marshmallows melt and cool for 10 minutes. Add remaining ingredients. Pour into mold and chill.

title: Hamburger-Cornbread Casserole
text: Hamburger-Cornbread Casserole. Ingredients: 1 lb. hamburger; 1 medium onion; 2 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 2 tsp. chili powder; 3/4 tsp. salt; 1 1/2 c. tomato juice; 1 can drained kidney beans; 1 1/2 c. cornbread mixture. Directions: Brown hamburger and onion. Add Worcestershire sauce, chili powder, salt and tomato juice; cook for 15 minutes, then add kidney beans. Pour into greased casserole dish and top with cornbread mixture. Bake at 425° for about 20 to 25 minutes.

title: German Steamed Slaw
text: German Steamed Slaw. Ingredients: 3 lb. cabbage shredded; 3 large onions, chopped; 1 lb. bacon; 1 1/2 Tbsp. salt; 1 Tbsp. pepper; 2 Tbsp. celery seed; 1 (28 oz.) can tomatoes; 2 c. vinegar; 3/4 c. sugar. Directions: Fry bacon and reserve the grease. Chop cabbage and onions. In a large, heavy pot, mix cabbage, onions, salt, pepper, celery seed and tomatoes. Add sugar and vinegar to warm bacon grease and then add to the cabbage mixture. Cook slow until cabbage is very soft and translucent. Stir occasionally.

title: Refrigerator Bran Muffins
text: Refrigerator Bran Muffins. Ingredients: 1 (15 oz.) box Raisin Bran; 5 c. flour; 3 c. sugar; 4 eggs, beaten; 1 c. oil; 1 qt. buttermilk. Directions: Combine all ingredients. Mix well. Cover batter and store in refrigerator. Use as needed. Bake at 350° for 20 minutes. Remove from muffin tin at once.

title: Scalloped Oysters
text: Scalloped Oysters. Ingredients: 1 pt. oysters; 2 c. cracker crumbs (Ritz); 1/2 c. melted butter; 1/2 tsp. salt; dash of pepper; 3/4 c. cream; 1/4 c. oyster liquid; 1/4 tsp. Worcestershire sauce. Directions: Drain oysters, saving liquid. Combine crumbs, butter, salt and pepper. Spread 1/3 of butter and crumbs in greased casserole (2-quart). Cover with 1/2 of the oysters. Using another 1/3 of buttered crumbs, spread second layer and add remaining oysters. Finish with last 1/3 of buttered crumbs on top. Combine liquid ingredients and pour over top. Bake at 350° for 40 minutes.

title: Marshmallow Marble-Top Fudge
text: Marshmallow Marble-Top Fudge. Ingredients: 3 c. (1 1/2 pkg.) Hershey's semi-sweet chocolate chips; 4 Tbsp. butter or margarine, divided; 1 (14 oz.) can Eagle Brand sweetened condensed milk (not evaporated milk); 1 1/2 tsp. vanilla extract; dash of salt; 1/2 to 1 c. chopped nuts; 2 c. miniature marshmallows. Directions: Line an 8 or 9-inch square pan with foil and set aside. In a heavy saucepan, over low heat, melt chocolate chips and 2 tablespoons butter with sweetened condensed milk, vanilla and salt. Remove from heat and stir in nuts. Spread evenly into prepared pan.

title: Lo-Cal Pineapple Cake
text: Lo-Cal Pineapple Cake. Ingredients: 2 eggs; 2 tsp. baking powder; 1 tsp. soda; 1 tsp. salt; 2 c. flour; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 1 Tbsp. vanilla; 1 (15 1/4 oz.) can crushed pineapple (juice and all). Directions: Cream together the sugar and eggs. Add pineapple. Add all dry ingredients. Stir well. Add vanilla. Pour into greased 13 x 9-inch pan. Bake at 350° for 35 minutes or until done.

title: California Potato Casserole
text: California Potato Casserole. Ingredients: 1 bag frozen hash browns; 2 c. shredded cheese (Cheddar); 1/2 c. onion, chopped; 1 (8 oz.) sour cream; 1 stick butter, melted; 1 tsp. pepper; 1 can cream soup; 1 c. corn flakes or crackers, crushed. Directions: Combine all ingredients and top with crushed topping. Bake in a 350° oven for 45 minutes to 1 hour. Put in a 9 x 13-inch pan. Good.

title: Bow Ties
text: Bow Ties. Ingredients: 2 whole eggs; 3 egg yolks; 1/2 tsp. salt; 2 Tbsp. sugar; 1 Tbsp. butter; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 jigger (3 Tbsp.) rum. Directions: Beat the eggs until well mixed. Add butter, salt, sugar and vanilla. Mix in flour, starting with 1 cup, until you form a soft dough. Roll dough out real thin. Cut in strips of 1 x 4-inches. Make a slit in center. Pull one end through slit. Fry in hot fat in frying pan until slightly tan in color. Drain on absorbent paper and sprinkle with confectioners sugar.

title: Honey Cookies
text: Honey Cookies. Ingredients: 4 eggs; 1 c. honey; 1 c. cold coffee (or 1 c. water and 1 tsp. instant coffee); 1 c. shortening; 2 tsp. baking soda; 8 c. flour; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 c. sugar. Directions: Cream shortening, sugar and honey. Add eggs, coffee, vanilla and dry ingredients. Mix well. Cover and chill overnight.

title: Savory Turkey Casserole
text: Savory Turkey Casserole. Ingredients: 1 pkg. scalloped potato mix (with onion flavor); 1/2 tsp. poultry seasoning (if desired); 2 c. boiling water; 10 3/4 oz. can condensed cream of chicken soup; 2 c. cubed, cooked turkey or chicken; 1 c. shredded carrots; 1/2 c. chopped celery; 1/4 c. chopped onion. Directions: Heat oven to 375°. In large bowl, combine contents of seasoning packet, poultry seasoning and water; mix well. Add soup; blend well. Add potato slices, turkey, carrots, celery and onion, stirring until thoroughly combined. Spoon mixture into ungreased 2-quart casserole dish. Bake at 375° for 35 to 40 minutes or until potatoes are tender.

title: Fried Venison Backstrap
text: Fried Venison Backstrap. Ingredients: 2 c. flour; 1 3/4 c. milk; 3 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. red pepper; 1/2 tsp. black pepper; 1/4 tsp. garlic powder. Directions: Mix flour and milk in medium sized bowl with whisk until all lumps are out. Add spices and mix well.

title: Crystalized Nuts
text: Crystalized Nuts. Ingredients: 3/4 c. sugar; 1/4 c. water; 1 c. nuts; 2 dashes cinnamon; 1 dash salt. Directions: Mix sugar and water in heavy skillet. Bring to rolling boil. Add nuts, cinnamon and salt and stir constantly until sugar mixture is absorbed. Spread out on waxed paper to cool.

title: Norwegian Betasuppe
text: Norwegian Betasuppe. Ingredients: 1 lb. yellow split peas; 1 lb. ham hocks or 1/2 c. finely chopped ham; 1 onion, finely chopped; 1 Tbsp. thyme; 10 c. water; 2 c. each: potatoes, diced, rutabaga and celery root; 1 1/2 c. chopped leek; 1 Tbsp. salt. Directions: Combine peas, ham hocks or ham, onion, thyme and water. Add potatoes, rutabaga, celery root, leek and salt. Combine ingredients in a large Dutch oven and simmer for 1 1/2 to 2 hours. Add vegies and salt and cook for another 30 to 45 minutes. If using ham hocks, remove, trim, cut finely and return to the soup!

title: Zucchini Casserole Imperial
text: Zucchini Casserole Imperial. Ingredients: 4 c. sliced zucchini; 2 c. boiling water; 2 eggs; 1 c. mayonnaise; 1 medium onion, chopped; 1/2 c. chopped green pepper; 1/4 c. chopped celery (optional); 1 c. grated Parmesan cheese; salt and pepper to taste; 1 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 2 Tbsp. buttered breadcrumbs (optional). Directions: Cook zucchini until tender (not mushy); drain. In large bowl, beat eggs; stir in mayonnaise, onion, green pepper, celery, salt and pepper. Add zucchini. Turn into a greased 1 1/2-quart baking dish. Dot with butter and sprinkle with buttered breadcrumbs.

title: Hash Brown Potatoes
text: Hash Brown Potatoes. Ingredients: 1 (32 oz.) pkg. cubed hash brown potatoes; 1 can cream of chicken soup; 1 (8 oz.) carton sour cream; 1/2 small onion, chopped; 12 oz. grated cheese; 1/2 c. melted butter; 1 tsp. salt; 2 c. corn flakes; 1/2 c. melted butter. Directions: Mix ingredients together and put in greased 9 x 13-inch. Top with 1 cup crushed corn flakes mixed with 1/2 cup melted butter. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes.

title: Sunday Steak
text: Sunday Steak. Ingredients: 1 round steak (about 3 lb.); 2 Tbsp. oil; 1/2 c. chopped onions; 3/4 c. catsup; 1/2 c. vinegar; 3/4 c. water; 1 Tbsp. brown sugar; 1 Tbsp. mustard; 1 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper. Directions: Heat oven to 350°. Cut steak in serving size pieces. Pour oil in skillet. Roll steak in flour and brown on both sides. Remove to baking dish. Add onions to skillet and brown lightly. Add rest of ingredients to make sauce. Simmer a few minutes and pour over browned meat. Bake in covered baking dish until tender, about 3 hours.

title: Fiesta Crab Crisps
text: Fiesta Crab Crisps. Ingredients: 2 Mission light flour tortillas; vegetable oil cooking spray; 6 oz. cooked Alaskan crab meat; 1/2 c. diced red bell pepper; 3 Tbsp. reduced calorie mayonnaise; 2 Tbsp. Italian parsley; 2 Tbsp. chopped chives; 1 Tbsp. fresh lime juice; 1 Tbsp. Dijon mustard; 1 Tbsp. grated Parmesan cheese; 4 drops Tabasco sauce. Directions: Cut tortillas into 6 wedges each and spray both sides with cooking spray. Place pieces on an ungreased baking pan and bake in a preheated 400° oven for 10 minutes or until crisp. Combine remaining ingredients in a medium bowl and mix well. Spread a rounded tablespoonful of crab mixture on each tortilla wedge; place wedges on an ungreased baking pan. Broil for 5 minutes until lightly brown. Serve immediately. Serves 6.

title: Opossum Pie
text: Opossum Pie. Ingredients: 1 medium opossum (dead, fresh as possible); 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 2 Tbsp. lard; 1/2 clove minced garlic; 3 small onions; 1 oz. ginger; pinch of rosemary; 2 Tbsp. flour; 6 carrots, cut up; 3 stalks celery; 1 (10 3/4 oz.) can beef broth; 4 oz. cooking sherry; 1 c. fresh peas; 8 Irish potatoes. Directions: Wash opossum inside and out and salt. Heat lard in roasting pan. Stir-fry garlic, onions and ginger. Heat until starts to smoke. Shake flour over opossum and brown in lard for two minutes. Place opossum in roasting pan with paws crossed over chest, adding carrots, onions, potatoes and celery. Add stock and sherry and cook at 325° for 2 hours. Add peas. Recover and heat 8 more minutes. Best served with rice, turnip greens and corn bread. Serves 8 to 12 or whoever you can get to eat it!

title: Stuffed Wieners
text: Stuffed Wieners. Ingredients: wieners; mashed potatoes; grated cheese (any variety); paprika. Directions: Split wieners in half lengthwise and put in a pan or on a cookie sheet. Pile mashed potatoes on top. Sprinkle grated cheese and paprika on top of potatoes. Bake at 425° for 15 to 20 minutes.

title: Greek Pizza
text: Greek Pizza. Ingredients: 1 lb. chopped meat; 1 lb. can tomatoes; 1/4 c. bread crumbs; 1 small eggplant; 5 Tbsp. olive oil; 6 to 8 oz. Monterey Jack cheese; pepper and oregano or basil to taste; 1 roll crescent rolls. Directions: Fry chopped meat and drain. Add tomatoes. Saute eggplant in olive oil, then add to meat mixture. Grate cheese. Set aside. Line a 9 x 13-inch pan with crescent rolls. Spread meat mixture on dough. Top with cheese and season to taste. Bake at 375° for 25 minutes or until crust is golden.

title: Best Punch
text: Best Punch. Ingredients: 46 oz. can pineapple juice; 46 oz. can orange juice; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 2 qt. ginger ale; 1/2 gal. water. Directions: Mix ingredients together. Chill and serve over crushed ice.

title: Coconut Pineapple Pie
