text: Coconut Pineapple Pie. Ingredients: 4 eggs, well beaten; 3/4 c. self-rising flour; 2 c. milk; 3 1/2 oz. coconut; 1 c. sugar; 1/3 c. melted oleo; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 (No. 2) can crushed pineapple, drained. Directions: Put in large well-greased 13 x 9-inch pan. Bake 40 to 50 minutes at 350°.

title: Cucumber Pickle
text: Cucumber Pickle. Ingredients: cucumbers; 4 Tbsp. salt; 4 Tbsp. powdered alum; 4 Tbsp. mixed pickling spice. Directions: Wash cucumbers; put in a gallon jug (enough to fill it). Put salt, powdered alum and pickling spices in jug with cucumbers; then fill jug with white vinegar. Put lid on and let stand 3 months. After 3 months, wash quickly after taking out of jug. Slice and put light cups sugar over slices. Stir until sugar is dissolved. Put back in jar with liquid made from sugar dissolving.

title: Simple Baked Crab
text: Simple Baked Crab. Ingredients: 1 medium green pepper, chopped; 1 medium onion, chopped; 1 c. celery, chopped; 1 lb. crabmeat or half crab and half shrimp; 1/2 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1 c. mayonnaise; 1 c. fresh bread crumbs; 4 Tbsp. melted butter or margarine. Directions: Combine first 6 ingredients in a casserole. Mix bread crumbs and butter and sprinkle over top of casserole. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes or until browned. Serves 4.

title: Pepperoni Pizza Dip
text: Pepperoni Pizza Dip. Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese, softened; 1/2 c. sour cream; 1 tsp. crushed dried oregano; 1/8 tsp. garlic powder; 1/8 tsp. crushed red pepper; 1/2 c. pizza sauce; 1/2 c. chopped pepperoni; 1/4 c. sliced green pepper; 1/2 c. shredded Mozzarella; 1/4 c. sliced green onions. Directions: In a small mixing bowl, beat together the cream cheese, sour cream, oregano, garlic powder and red pepper. Spread evenly in 9 or 10-inch quiche pan or pie plate. Spread pizza sauce over the top. Sprinkle with pepperoni, green onions and green pepper. Bake in preheated 350° oven for 10 minutes. Top with cheese. Bake 5 minutes more, until cheese is melted and mixture is heated through.

title: Shahi Pilaf
text: Shahi Pilaf. Ingredients: 4 c. basmati rice; 1 stick butter or clarified butter; cinnamon stick, crushed; 2 bay leaves; 6 pods cardamon; 6 cloves; 1 tsp. salt; 2 Tbsp. raisins; 2 Tbsp. pistachios; 2 Tbsp. almonds. Directions: Wash the rice and set aside to drain. Heat the butter in a nonstick pan. Add cinnamon stick, cardamon pods, bay leaves and cloves and stir for 1 1/2 minutes. Add salt and water (8 cups) to rice, then bring to boil, stirring it a couple of times. Reduce heat to low. Add raisins, almonds and pistachios. Cook, covered, for 10 minutes. Serve piping hot.

title: Baked Lasagna
text: Baked Lasagna. Ingredients: 2 cloves fresh garlic; 1 Tbsp. olive oil; 1 large can tomato puree; 1/2 can water; 2 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. pepper; 2 tsp. oregano; 1 Tbsp. sugar; 1 lb. lasagna; 1 lb. chopped beef; 2 Tbsp. parsley; 2 lb. Ricotta cheese; 1/2 lb. grated Parmesan cheese; 1 lb. Mozzarella cheese. Directions: Saute garlic in tablespoon of olive oil (do not brown garlic). Add tomato puree, water, salt, pepper, oregano and sugar. Bring to a boil. Lower heat and simmer for 2 hours. Brown chopped beef; add parsley, season to taste and add 2 tablespoons grated Parmesan cheese. Simmer until all chopped beef is cooked through. Set aside. Using a 6-quart kettle, boil water (with 1 tablespoon salt added). When a rolling boil starts, add lasagna. Cook according to directions on package, drain and blanch with cold water.

title: Marinated Orange And Onion Salad
text: Marinated Orange And Onion Salad. Ingredients: 2 c. fresh orange sections; 1 medium purple onion, thinly sliced and separated into rings; 1/4 c. red wine vinegar; 1 Tbsp. minced fresh parsley; 3 c. torn mixed salad greens. Directions: Combine orange and onion in a large shallow dish. Pour vinegar over mixture and toss. Sprinkle with parsley. Cover and refrigerate 4 hours; stir occasionally. Toss gently. Serve over mixed greens using slotted spoon. Makes 3 servings.

title: Salad Delight
text: Salad Delight. Ingredients: 1 c. sour cream; 1 c. crushed pineapple, drained; 1 c. shredded coconut; 1 c. mandarin oranges; 1 c. miniature marshmallows. Directions: Mix all together and chill for 2 hours.

title: Rich Pecan Pie
text: Rich Pecan Pie. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. Log Cabin syrup; 1/4 c. (1/2 stick) margarine; 1/4 c. sugar; 1 1/2 c. pecan halves; 1 unbaked 9-inch pie shell; 3 eggs, slightly beaten; 1 tsp. vanilla; dash of salt. Directions: Heat oven to 375°. Combine syrup, margarine and sugar in saucepan. Bring to boil; boil gently 5 minutes, uncovered, stirring occasionally. Cool slightly. Place pecans in pie shell. Mix eggs, vanilla and salt in bowl. Gradually stir in cooled syrup mixture. Pour over pecans in pie shell. Bake 35 to 40 minutes or until knife inserted near center comes out clean. Cool on wire rack.

title: Cheese And Onion Potatoes
text: Cheese And Onion Potatoes. Ingredients: 1/4 c. butter/margarine; 4 medium unpeeled red potatoes; 2 Tbsp. dried minced onion; pepper to taste; 1/3 c. Cheddar cheese, shredded; chopped fresh parsley. Directions: In skillet, melt butter; add potatoes, which have been sliced 1/4-inch thick. Add onion and pepper and toss to coat. Cover and cook, stirring occasionally, until potatoes are tender, about 10 minutes. Uncover; cook until potatoes are browned, about 5 to 7 minutes. Top with cheese; cover and remove from heat. Let set for several minutes. Sprinkle with parsley before serving. Serves 4.

title: Cheesy Lima Beans
text: Cheesy Lima Beans. Ingredients: 1 (No. 303) can small all green lima beans; 1/2 c. liquid from can; 1 c. grated Cheddar cheese, divided; 1 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 1 c. chopped celery; 1/4 tsp. onion powder; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 1/2 c. mayonnaise; 1 c. small cheese crackers, crushed. Directions: Drain beans, reserving 1/2 cup liquid from can. Put beans, reserved liquid, chopped celery, onion powder, pepper, mayonnaise and 1/2 cup of the cheese into greased casserole dish. Stir to mix well. Dot with butter. Sprinkle remaining 1/2 cup cheese on top. Then spread crushed crackers over all. Bake 30 to 40 minutes in a 300° oven.

title: Baked Sauerkraut And Pork Chops
text: Baked Sauerkraut And Pork Chops. Ingredients: 1 grated green apple; 1 grated onion (medium); 1 grated potato; 2 tsp. caraway seed; 4 pork chops; 1/2 c. sugar; 1 c. dark German beer; 2 bay leaves; 32 oz. jar sauerkraut. Directions: Mix everything but chops. Put in 9 x 12-inch pan. Lay chops on top. Sprinkle with salt and pepper to taste. Cover with foil. Bake 50 minutes at 350°. Serve with mashed potatoes. Serves 4.

title: Lung Fever Salve - Draw Salve
text: Lung Fever Salve - Draw Salve. Ingredients: 1/2 lb. fresh lard; 1/4 lb. bees wax; 2 oz. pulverized camphor; 3 oz. white rosin; 2 drams balsam of Peru; 2 drams oil of cedar; 1 oz. turpentine. Directions: First put the lard and wax together and melt; then melt camphor in it. Then put in all the other ingredients except for turpentine and cook until they are all melted. Remove from stove and then add turpentine (turpentine is inflammable).

title: Zucchini Stuffing Casserole
text: Zucchini Stuffing Casserole. Ingredients: 4 medium zucchini, sliced 1/2-inch thick; 6 Tbsp. butter; 3/4 c. shredded carrots; 1/2 c. chopped onion; 2 1/4 c. herbed stuffing cubes; 1 (10 3/4 oz.) cream of chicken soup; 1/2 c. sour cream. Directions: Cook zucchini in boiling salt water until just tender. Drain. In saucepan, melt 4 tablespoons butter. Add carrots and onion. Saute until tender. Remove from heat. Stir in 1/2 cup stuffing cubes, soup and sour cream. Gently stir in zucchini. Spoon into 1 1/2-quart casserole. Melt 2 tablespoons butter. Add remaining stuffing cubes and toss gently. Sprinkle over casserole. Bake at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes.

title: Heart Of The Nation Bread
text: Heart Of The Nation Bread. Ingredients: 3/4 c. sugar; 1 c. shortening; 1 c. boiling water; 2 pkg. yeast, dissolved in 1 c. lukewarm water; 2 eggs, beaten lightly; 6 c. flour, sifted; 1 tsp. salt, sifted with flour. Directions: Cream together sugar and shortening. Add water. Cool. Add remaining ingredients. Cover and place in refrigerator for several hours or overnight. Shape into rolls and let rise 2 hours. Bake at 325° about 20 minutes or until lightly browned. (This dough may be stored in refrigerator for several days.)

title: Applesauce Cake With Lemon Glaze
text: Applesauce Cake With Lemon Glaze. Ingredients: 3 1/2 c. plain flour; 3 tsp. baking powder; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 1/2 tsp. ground cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. ground nutmeg; 1 c. shortening; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 1 c. raisins; 1 c. confectioners sugar; 2 tsp. lemon juice; 1/2 tsp. grated lemon rind. Directions: Combine flour, baking powder, salt, cinnamon and nutmeg; set aside. Cream shortening with sugar. Add eggs and applesauce. Gradually stir in flour mixture. Beat at medium speed of electric mixer for 2 minutes. Fold in raisins. Spoon batter into a nonstick 12-cup fluted pan. Bake at 350° for 55 to 60 minutes, or until wooden pick inserted into center of cake comes clean. Cool 10 minutes. Invert onto a wire rack for cooling. Drizzle sugar and lemon juice over cake. Makes 16 servings.

title: Frozen Cherry Salad
text: Frozen Cherry Salad. Ingredients: 1 can cherry pie filling; 1 can Eagle Brand milk; 1 small can crushed pineapple; 1/2 c. chopped nuts; 1 small carton Cool Whip. Directions: Put all ingredients in mixing bowl, stir and freeze. Take out of freezer 10 minutes before serving.

title: Apricot Sauce
text: Apricot Sauce. Ingredients: 1/2 lb. dried apricots; water; 1/2 c. sugar or less if using apricot brandy; 1/16 tsp. salt; Cognac or apricot brandy to taste (approximately 3 to 4 Tbsp.). Directions: Wash and cook apricots in water as directed on package. Put the cooked apricots through a food mill, or puree them, about 3/4 cup at a time in a blender (or use a food processor). Add sugar and salt. Stir and cook 4 to 5 minutes over moderate heat. Add Cognac or apricot brandy to taste. Serve over puddings, or plain cake. Makes approximately 1 1/2 cups sauce.

title: Broiled Chicken(Or Maybe Oiled-Foil-Broiled Chicken)
text: Broiled Chicken(Or Maybe Oiled-Foil-Broiled Chicken). Ingredients: skinless boneless chicken breasts; lemon juice; herbs de Provence; avocado oil or olive oil. Directions: Marinate the chicken breasts in lemon juice and herbs de Provence for 8 to 24 hours. Cover the broiler pan with aluminum foil, then oil the foil. Heat the broiler. Arrange the chicken on the oiled foiled pan; broil it on one side, then turn it over and broil it on the other side.

title: Mexican Wedding Cakes
text: Mexican Wedding Cakes. Ingredients: 1 1/3 c. butter; 2/3 c. confectioners sugar; 1/2 tsp. vanilla; 2 1/4 c. all-purpose flour; 1/8 tsp. salt; 1 c. finely chopped pecans; confectioners sugar. Directions: In large mixing bowl, cream butter, confectioners sugar and vanilla until light and fluffy. Add flour, salt and pecans; mix well. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours.

title: Perfect Apple Pie
text: Perfect Apple Pie. Ingredients: 6 or 7 apples, sliced; 3/4 to 1 c. brown sugar; 2 Tbsp. flour; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 2 Tbsp. butter; dash of salt. Directions: Place apples in crust. Sprinkle brown sugar over apples. Dust the flour over apples. Dust cinnamon and salt over the apples. Dot butter over apples. Place crust on top. Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

title: Raspberry Cherry Pie
text: Raspberry Cherry Pie. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. sugar; 3 Tbsp. quick-cooking tapioca; 2 c. fresh or frozen unsweetened raspberries; 1 c. pitted fresh, frozen or canned tart cherries; 1 tsp. lemon juice. Directions: For filling, in a bowl, combine sugar and tapioca. Add the raspberries, cherries and lemon juice; toss to coat. Let stand for 15 minutes. Spoon filling into pastry. Dot with butter. Add top crust over filling. Trim, seal and flute edges. Cut slits in pastry. Cover edges loosely with foil. Bake at 350° for 60 to 70 minutes or until golden brown. Cool on a wire rack. Store in the refrigerator. Yield: 8 servings.

title: Ann'S Chicken
text: Ann'S Chicken. Ingredients: 8 to 10 chicken breasts (without the bone or you can use the frozen chicken, honey mustard from Sams); 1 can cream of chicken soup; 1 can water; 3 large onions, sliced; 2 large green bell peppers, sliced; 1 can mushrooms (if desired); 3 c. grated Mozzarella cheese. Directions: If you use the fresh chicken, cook it for about 45 minutes in the oven or grill. Place chicken breasts in a deep baking dish, then add the cream of chicken soup mixed with the water. Cook peppers and onions in a small amount of butter until tender, then spread over the chicken. Add the mushrooms. Sprinkle with the Mozzarella cheese. Bake, covered with foil, for about 1 hour at 375° to 400°.

title: Scalloped Potatoes And Peas
text: Scalloped Potatoes And Peas. Ingredients: 1/2 lb. potatoes, sliced paper thin (about 1 1/2 c.); 1 medium onion, slivered (1/2 c.); 1 Tbsp. whole wheat or unbleached flour; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 c. fresh or frozen peas; 1 c. rich soy milk; a sprinkle of crumbled wheat; Nutrigrain cereal (Kellogg's). Directions: Set oven to 400°. Scrub potatoes and remove eyes or bad spots. Slice paper thin (a food processor does job quickly). Oil-rub a 1-quart baking dish or 2 (1/2-quart) dishes. Layer half the potatoes, onions, flour and salt in the dish. Cover with all the peas and top with layers of remaining onions, potatoes, flour and salt (in that order).

title: Pappy'S Spoon Biscuits
text: Pappy'S Spoon Biscuits. Ingredients: 2 c. self-rising flour, sifted; 1 1/4 c. buttermilk; 1/2 c. oil; butter. Directions: Preheat oven to 425°. Sift flour into large bowl. Take a spoon and make a hole in flour about the size of your fist. Pour buttermilk and oil in the hole. With a spoon begin stirring buttermilk and oil, pulling in flour gradually as you go. After all flour has been stirred in, drop by large spoonfuls onto greased biscuit pan. (Dough will be sticky and have the consistency of a very thick batter.) Bake in preheated 425° oven for about 15 to 20 minutes or until bottom of biscuit turns brown. Place under broiler for few seconds until tops turn light brown. Remove from oven. Run a stick of butter across the top of the hot biscuits. Good! Easy! No messy hands!

title: Cranberry-Mustard Turkey Tenderloins(Low-Fat)
text: Cranberry-Mustard Turkey Tenderloins(Low-Fat). Ingredients: 1 1/4 lb. boneless fresh turkey breast tenderloins; 2 Tbsp. Dijon mustard; 4 tsp. brown sugar; 1 Tbsp. cornstarch; 16 oz. can whole berry cranberry sauce; 16 oz. can apricot halves in extra-light syrup, drained (save syrup); 2 Tbsp. sliced green onions. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. In 9-inch square or 9 x 11-inch baking pan, arrange turkey tenderloins. In small bowl, combine mustard and 1 teaspoon brown sugar; spread evenly over turkey. Bake, uncovered, 20 minutes.

title: Holiday Hors D'Oeuvres
text: Holiday Hors D'Oeuvres. Ingredients: 1 can ripe olives, sliced; 2 c. Cheddar cheese, shredded; 1 onion, diced; 1 c. mayonnaise; 1 loaf party rye bread. Directions: Mix olives, cheese, onion and mayonnaise together. Spread on small square of rye bread. Bake for 10 minutes at 350°. Serve warm.

title: Baby Food Salad
text: Baby Food Salad. Ingredients: 1 small box orange Jell-O (add no water); 1 small can crushed pineapple, undrained; 3/4 c. sugar; 2 small jars apricot baby food; pecans (optional); 8 oz. cream cheese, very soft; 12 oz. Cool Whip. Directions: Heat orange Jell-O and pineapple until dissolved. Add sugar. Mix in cream cheese (mash with fork until mixed well; do not beat). Add baby food. Chill until slightly jelled. Fold in Cool Whip. Add nuts on top.

title: Crock-Pot Chicken Casserole
text: Crock-Pot Chicken Casserole. Ingredients: 6 potatoes, peeled and quartered; 6 carrots, sliced; 2 onions, quartered; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. oregano; 1 tsp. garlic powder; black pepper to taste; 2 to 3 lb. chicken parts; 1 pt. canned, whole tomatoes. Directions: Place in crock-pot in the order given, mixing seasonings with vegetables and putting chicken on top. Chop the tomatoes and pour over all. Sprinkle additional salt, garlic powder and oregano on top. Cook on low for at least 5 hours. Good meal for a busy day. Serves 6.

title: Sugar Mountain Chicken Salad
text: Sugar Mountain Chicken Salad. Ingredients: 1/2 to 3/4 c. mayonnaise; 1/2 tsp. curry powder; 4 c. coarsely cut up cooked chicken; 1 c. chopped celery; 1/2 (5 oz.) can water chestnuts, sliced; 1 c. toasted almonds; 1/2 lb. green seedless grapes, halved; 1/2 Tbsp. soy sauce; 1 tsp. lemon juice; pineapple chunks; cantaloupe; paprika. Directions: Combine mayonnaise and curry powder. Set aside. Combine next seven ingredients. Mix enough mayonnaise into chicken mixture until moistened. Slice cantaloupe into 1/2-inch sliced rings. To serve chicken salad, place ring of cantaloupe on lettuce. Heap salad on cantaloupe. Garnish with chunks of pineapple and paprika. Serve with hot bread. Serves 6.

title: Broccoli-Onion Deluxe
text: Broccoli-Onion Deluxe. Ingredients: 2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen cut broccoli; 2 c. frozen or canned whole small onions; 4 Tbsp. butter; 2 Tbsp. flour; 1 c. milk; 1 (3 oz.) pkg. cream cheese; 1 c. bread crumbs; 1/4 c. (1 oz.) grated Parmesan cheese; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Cook broccoli according to package directions. Drain. Cook onions in boiling salted water until tender. If frozen onions are used, drain. In saucepan, melt 2 tablespoons butter. Blend in flour, salt and pepper. Add milk all at once. Cook, stirring, until thick and bubbly. Reduce heat and blend in cream cheese until smooth. Stir in vegetables. Place mixture in a 1 1/2-quart casserole and bake for 20 minutes at 350°. Melt remaining butter. Toss with bread crumbs and Parmesan cheese and sprinkle over casserole. Bake 20 minutes more.

title: Beef Patties
text: Beef Patties. Ingredients: 1 can condensed golden mushroom soup; 1 1/2 lb. ground beef; 1/2 c. fine, dry bread crumbs; 1 egg, slightly beaten; 1/4 c. finely, chopped onion; dash of pepper; 1/3 c. water. Directions: Combine 1/4 cup soup with remaining ingredients except water. Mix good; shape into 6 patties. Place in dish. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes. Drain off fat. Combine rest of soup and water. Pour over patties. Bake 10 minutes more.

title: Fresh Zucchini Bread
text: Fresh Zucchini Bread. Ingredients: 1 c. walnuts; 1 medium zucchini; 1 c. sugar; 2 eggs; 1/2 c. vegetable oil; 2 tsp. vanilla; 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. baking powder; 1 1/2 c. all-purpose flour. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Grease and flour a 9 x 5-inch loaf pan. Chop walnuts. Grate zucchini. Mix together sugar, eggs, oil, cinnamon, vanilla, baking soda, baking powder and salt. Mix well. Add flour. Mix well. Add walnuts and zucchini. Pour into a greased pan and bake 1 1/4 hours.

title: Johnny Appleseed Cake
text: Johnny Appleseed Cake. Ingredients: 1/2 c. margarine; 1 3/4 c. (1 lb. can) applesauce; 2 c. flour; 1 c. sugar; 1 tsp. soda; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. nutmeg; 1/4 tsp. ground cloves; 1 c. raisins or chopped nuts. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. In large saucepan melt margarine over medium heat. Remove from heat. Blend in remaining ingredients. Mix well. Pour into 9-inch square or 11 x 17-inch pan, greased bottom only. Bake 30 to 35 minutes or until top springs back when touched in center. Serve warm or cool, plain or with whipped cream or frosting.

title: Party Beans
text: Party Beans. Ingredients: 2 lb. ground beef; 2 c. chopped onion; 1 c. chopped celery; 1 (10 3/4 oz.) can cream of tomato soup; 1 (6 oz.) can tomato paste; 1/2 c. ketchup; 1 (16 oz.) can green beans, drained; 1 (17 oz.) can lima beans, drained; 1 (15 1/2 oz.) can wax beans, drained; 1 (16 oz.) can chili beans; 1 (16 oz.) can pork and beans; 1/2 c. brown sugar; 2 Tbsp. prepared mustard. Directions: In a large Dutch oven, brown beef over medium heat. Drain fat. Add onion and celery; cook until tender. Stir in soup, ketchup and tomato. Simmer 15 to 20 minutes. Spoon into a large kettle or roaster. Add all remaining ingredients; stir well. Bake, uncovered, at 350° for 1 hour. Serves 25.

title: Apples And Carrot Dish
text: Apples And Carrot Dish. Ingredients: 2 cans apple pie filling; 2 cans Lesueur carrots; 1 cup brown sugar; cinnamon. Directions: Drain 1/2 juice from carrots. Mix apple filling and carrots. Put in a baking dish, sprinkle with cinnamon. Bake about an hour in 350 degree oven.

title: Frozen Lemon Dessert
text: Frozen Lemon Dessert. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. butter; 16 graham crackers, crushed; 1 (21 oz.) can lemon pie filling; 1 c. sweetened condensed milk; 1/2 c. lemon juice; 1 1/2 c. whipped topping. Directions: In a saucepan, melt butter. Stir in crumbs. Reserve 1 tablespoon for garnish. Press remaining mixture on bottom of a buttered 8 x 8-inch baking pan; set aside.

title: Polish Kolaches
text: Polish Kolaches. Ingredients: 1/2 c. lukewarm water; 1 1/2 c. milk; 2 pkg. yeast cakes; 3 eggs; 1 c. sugar; 1/2 c. oil; 1 tsp. salt; 6 or 7 c. flour to mix dough; cooked prunes or peaches. Directions: Mix all ingredients well. Let rise. Fill with cooked prunes or peaches. Bake in 350° to 400° oven for 15 minutes or until light brown and done.

title: Herb Tea Blend
text: Herb Tea Blend. Ingredients: 3/4 c. loose black tea leaves; 1/4 c. dried sage, chopped; 3 Tbsp. dried thyme; 1 Tbsp. dried lemon peel. Directions: Combine all ingredients in a resealable plastic bag.

title: Mexican Lasagna(Low-Fat)
text: Mexican Lasagna(Low-Fat). Ingredients: 16 oz. ground turkey; 1/2 c. chopped onions; 1/2 c. chopped green peppers; 2 c. canned tomatoes, chopped; 1 envelope taco seasoning mix; 2 c. (16 oz.) cottage cheese; 6 oz. shredded Cheddar cheese; 2 eggs; 6 (6-inch) flour or corn tortillas; 1 c. chopped fresh tomatoes; 2 c. lettuce, shredded. Directions: In large skillet brown turkey with onion and pepper. Add tomatoes and taco seasoning. Simmer, uncovered, 5 minutes. In bowl, combine cottage cheese, 4 ounces Cheddar cheese and eggs. Place 3 tortillas on bottom of a sprayed 9 x 13-inch or 8 x 12-inch baking dish. Spoon meat mixture evenly over tortillas. Top with 3 tortillas then cheese mixture. Bake, covered, at 350° for 40 minutes. Sprinkle remaining cheese over top of lasagna and continue baking, uncovered, for 5 minutes or until cheese melts. Garnish with tomatoes and lettuce. May add taco sauce or sour cream, if desired.

title: Curried Chicken Or Turkey
text: Curried Chicken Or Turkey. Ingredients: 1/4 c. oil; 1 bunch green onions, chopped; 1 small celery stalk, chopped; 1 green pepper, chopped; 2 Tbsp. sliced almonds; 2 c. water chestnuts; 3 c. sliced chicken or turkey; 3 Tbsp. flour; 1 tsp. curry powder; 1 tsp. paprika; 1/2 tsp. sweet basil; 1 1/2 c. chicken broth; 1/4 c. pimento; 1 c. pineapple bits, drained; rice. Directions: Heat oil; saute onions, celery and green pepper until brown. Add almonds, water chestnuts and turkey. Mix in flour and seasonings. Saute lightly, stirring until well blended. Mix in broth, pimento and pineapple. Cover and let steam briefly. Season with salt and pepper. Serve with rice.

title: Sweet And Sour Carrot Salad(Serves 12)
text: Sweet And Sour Carrot Salad(Serves 12). Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. carrots, pared and thinly sliced; 1 medium red onion, chopped; 1/2 c. sweet green pepper, chopped; 1 (10 3/4 oz.) can tomato soup; 3/4 c. sugar; 1/2 c. vegetable oil; 1/2 c. red wine vinegar; 1 tsp. Worcestershire sauce (optional); 1/2 tsp. salt. Directions: Cook carrots in boiling water until crisp-tender, about 5 minutes. Drain and cool. Combine carrots, onion and green pepper in large serving bowl. Combine soup, sugar, oil, vinegar, Worcestershire and salt. Pour over vegetables. Stir gently to combine. Cover. Refrigerate at least 24 hours to blend flavors.

title: Banana Layer Cake
text: Banana Layer Cake. Ingredients: 2 eggs; 1/2 c. Crisco; 1/2 tsp. baking powder; 3/4 tsp. baking soda; 3/4 c. ripe mashed bananas; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 2 c. flour; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. milk (sour, add 1/2 tsp. vinegar); 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Cream Crisco and sugar; add eggs and beat well. Add dry ingredients alternately with milk. Add mashed bananas and vanilla. Bake in 350° oven for 30 to 35 minutes.

title: Squash Casserole #1
text: Squash Casserole #1. Ingredients: 2 lb. summer squash, cooked and mashed; 1 medium onion, chopped; 1 grated carrot; 1 can cream of chicken soup; 1/2 pt. sour cream; 1 pkg. Pepperidge Farm dressing mix; 1 stick margarine, melted. Directions: Mix margarine and dressing mix. Reserve 1/2 for topping. Mix squash, sour cream, onion, carrots, chicken soup and 1/2 dressing with margarine. Place in 1 large or 2 small casserole dishes. Top with reserved dressing mix. Bake at 350°. Serves 8 generously.

title: Spanish Lima Beans
text: Spanish Lima Beans. Ingredients: 4 c. lima beans (2 cans); 1/4 c. bell pepper; 1/4 c. onion; 1/4 c. margarine; 3 c. tomatoes, drained; 1 can corned beef, cut in pieces; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Cook pepper and onion in margarine, about 5 minutes. Mix with other ingredients and bake in casserole dish for about 30 minutes at 350°.

title: Anise Candy
text: Anise Candy. Ingredients: 3/4 c. light corn syrup; 1/2 c. water; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 1 1/2 tsp. anise flavoring; food coloring. Directions: Mix syrup, water and sugar in a heavy medium-sized saucepan. Stir over low heat until sugar is completely dissolved. Boil, covered, about 3 minutes; uncover and continue boiling slowly to 310°.

title: Sweet-N-Sour Chicken
text: Sweet-N-Sour Chicken. Ingredients: 2 c. diced cooked chicken; 2 Tbsp. shortening; 1/2 c. onion (large chunks); 2 c. carrot chunks; 1 1/4 c. water; 3 chicken bouillon cubes; 1/4 c. packed brown sugar; 2 Tbsp. cornstarch; 1/4 tsp. ginger; 1/4 c. catsup; 2 Tbsp. vinegar; 1 Tbsp. soy sauce; 1 c. green pepper (large chunks); 8 oz. pineapple chunks. Directions: Saute onion in 2 tablespoons shortening. Add carrots, water and bouillon cubes. Simmer 5 minutes. Combine next 6 ingredients. Add to vegetable mixture and cook until clear. Add pepper, pineapple and chicken. Cover and simmer 5 minutes or until heated through. Serve with rice or angel hair noodles. Serves 6 people.

title: Mock Hamburger Patties
text: Mock Hamburger Patties. Ingredients: 1 onion; 2 eggs; 2 c. quick oats; 4 c. Rice Krispies; 1 c. chopped pecans; 1 pkg. George Washington; 1 can mushroom soup; 4 oz. liquid. Directions: Mix ingredients together. Form into patties. Brown in skillet. Put in a 9 x 13-inch pan and pour 1 can of mushroom soup over; bake at 350° for 30 to 45 minutes.

title: Chewy Fudge Brownies
text: Chewy Fudge Brownies. Ingredients: 1 box (Martha White) chewy fudge brownie mix; 2 eggs; 3 Tbsp. vegetable oil; 2 Tbsp. water; 1 pkg. Planters pecan pieces. Directions: Blend ingredients well; batter will be stiff. Add pecan pieces. Bake 25 minutes at 350° in glass oblong dish greased on bottom with nonstick cooking spray or vegetable shortening. Do not overbake. Cool completely in pan on wire rack. Cut into squares. Cover tightly after cooling to keep.

title: Coconut Yule Trees
text: Coconut Yule Trees. Ingredients: 2 c. flaked coconut; 2 c. confectioners sugar; 1/4 c. butter or margarine, softened; 1 tsp. almond extract; 2 to 4 oz. dark chocolate confectioners coating; green sugar and red hot candies. Directions: Combine the first 5 ingredients; mix well. Drop by tablespoon onto a waxed paper-lined cookie sheet. Chill for 1 hour. Shape into trees.

title: Delicious Broccoli Salad
text: Delicious Broccoli Salad. Ingredients: 1 large bunch fresh broccoli; 1/2 c. chopped, sugared dates; 8 to 10 slices bacon, crisped and crumbled; 1 small purple onion, diced; 1/2 c. cashews or sunflower seeds; 1 c. light mayonnaise; 1 Tbsp. sugar; 2 Tbsp. red vinegar; 1 to 2 Tbsp. milk. Directions: Combine broccoli flowerets, chopped dates, bacon, onion and nuts or seeds in a large bowl. In separate bowl, combine mayonnaise, sugar, vinegar and milk. Pour over salad, mix well and serve!

title: Corn Dodgers
text: Corn Dodgers. Ingredients: 1 can creamed corn; 1/4 c. butter; pancake mix (enough to make thick batter). Directions: Fry in patties dropped by spoonfuls into a small amount of cooking oil. Serve hot.

title: Apple Streusel Pie
text: Apple Streusel Pie. Ingredients: 3 1/2 c. sliced apples; 1 (9-inch) unbaked pie shell; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 8 Tbsp. butter; 2/3 c. sugar; 2 eggs; 1 tsp. salt; 1 1/2 tsp. vanilla extract flavor; 6 oz. cream cheese, softened; 1/2 c. brown sugar; 3/4 c. flour; 1 c. chopped pecans. Directions: Place apples in pie shell and sprinkle with cinnamon. Cream 4 tablespoons of butter, sugar and eggs; add salt, vanilla flavor and cream cheese until just smooth. Pour over apples. Combine brown sugar, flour, 4 tablespoons butter and pecans. Crumble this mixture on top of pie. Bake at 375° for 45 minutes. Reduce heat to 350° and continue to bake for 15 minutes.

title: Creamy Broccoli Soup
text: Creamy Broccoli Soup. Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. broccoli, cut up; 2 c. water; 1 large stalk chopped celery; 1 medium onion, chopped; 2 Tbsp. margarine; 2 1/2 c. water; 1 Tbsp. instant chicken bouillon; 3/4 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 1/2 c. whipping cream. Directions: Heat 2 cups water to boiling. Add broccoli, celery and onion. Cook until tender. Do not drain. Heat margarine until melted. Stir in flour until smooth. Add 1 1/2 cups water until boiling. Add cooked vegies, broth, bouillon, salt and pepper. Heat until just boiling. Stir in cream. Heat but do not boil.

title: Oma'S Crepes
text: Oma'S Crepes. Ingredients: 3 eggs (room temperature); 1 c. milk; 1 c. flour; Crisco shortening (for frying; I use butter flavored); powdered sugar, jam or fresh fruit (for filling). Directions: Whisk together eggs, milk and flour. Melt about a heaping teaspoonful of shortening in large frying pan (cast-iron or Teflon works best). When hot, put large ladle of batter in pan, moving pan side to side to thin out batter to size of bottom of pan. When lightly browned, quickly turn with spatula and brown a minute on other side. Put on plate. Fill center with fruit, jam or sprinkle with powdered sugar. Roll up and serve. Serves 4, about 8 crepes.

title: Brad'S Pasta Salad
text: Brad'S Pasta Salad. Ingredients: 2 lb. spiral pasta noodles; 1 lb. stick pepperoni, diced in small pieces; 8 oz. jar olives, diced; 2 green peppers, chopped; 1 large sweet onion, chopped; 12 oz. Ranch style dressing; 4 oz. Italian dressing. Directions: Boil pasta and drain. Add pepperoni, olives, green peppers and onion. Add dressings and mix well. Let chill for about one hour before serving. I prefer to let set overnight.

title: Hero Hamburgers
text: Hero Hamburgers. Ingredients: 2 lb. ground beef; 1 c. all-purpose barbecue sauce (any flavor); 1/4 c. pickle slices or relish; 1 medium onion, thinly sliced; 2 eggs, slightly beaten; 1/4 c. diced or grated cheese; 8 hamburger buns, split; 1/2 tsp. salt. Directions: Combine beef and 1/4 of barbecue sauce, eggs and salt. Mix lightly and divide into 16 portions. Gently shape into thin round patties. Place 1 teaspoon each: barbecue sauce, cheese, relish and onions on 8 of the patties. Top them with remaining 8 patties. Press edges together; broil or fry 4 minutes on one side, turn, baste with remaining barbecue sauce and cook 4 minutes longer or until meat is done. Serve on buns. Makes 8 servings.

title: Finger Paint
text: Finger Paint. Ingredients: 1/3 c. cornstarch; 3 Tbsp. sugar; 2 c. cold water; food coloring. Directions: Mix cornstarch, sugar and water in saucepan. Cook and stir over medium heat, about 5 minutes. Divide the mixture into separate cups or containers. Tint mixture in each container with a different food color. Stir several times until cool. Store in an airtight container.

title: Quick Lunch Box Cake
text: Quick Lunch Box Cake. Ingredients: 2 1/4 c. all-purpose flour; 2 tsp. soda; 1 tsp. salt; 1 c. firmly packed brown sugar; 2 eggs; 1/4 c. butter, softened; 1 (17 oz.) can fruit cocktail (undrained); 1/2 c. semi-sweet chocolate pieces; 1/2 c. chopped nuts. Directions: In large mixing bowl, combine all ingredients except chocolate and nuts. Blend at low speed until moistened; beat 2 minutes at medium speed, scraping bowl occasionally. Add chocolate and nuts and mix. Pour batter into greased and floured 13 x 9-inch pan. Bake at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes or until cake springs back when touched lightly in center.

title: Teenage Scramble
text: Teenage Scramble. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; 1 small onion, chopped; 3 Tbsp. olive oil; 1 small can sliced mushrooms; 1 pkg. frozen spinach, cooked; 6 eggs, well beaten; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Saute ground beef with onion in oil in skillet until ground beef is browned, stirring frequently. Add mushrooms and spinach, mixing well. Stir in eggs gradually. Cook until eggs are set, stirring constantly. Season with salt and pepper. Yields 6 servings.

title: Strawberry Patch Punch
text: Strawberry Patch Punch. Ingredients: 1 cup Apple juice; Two 10-oz. packages frozen strawberries; 1/4 cup lemon juice; sugar to taste; ice cubes; Ginger ale or 7-Up. Directions: Combine first four ingredients in a blender. Whirl to consistency of thick slush. Place 2 ice cubes in each glass. Fill 3/4 full of fruit slush. Fill remainder with ginger ale or 7-Up. Serves 4.

title: Sausage And Egg Casserole
text: Sausage And Egg Casserole. Ingredients: 1 lb. sausage meat; 6 eggs, beaten; 2 c. milk; 1 tsp. salt; 2 slices day-old bread, crushed; 1 c. Cheddar cheese, grated; 3 Tbsp. chopped onions. Directions: Brown sausage in skillet. Drain. Mix all ingredients together. Pour into 11 x 7 x 2-inch casserole dish. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes. Serves 6 to 8.

title: Tater Tot Bake
text: Tater Tot Bake. Ingredients: 3/4 to 1 lb. ground beef or turkey; 1 small onion, chopped; salt and pepper to taste; 1 (16 oz.) pkg. frozen Tater Tot potatoes; 1 (10 3/4 oz.) can cream of mushroom soup (undiluted); 1/2 soup can milk or water; 1 c. (4 oz.) shredded Cheddar cheese. Directions: In skillet, brown the meat and onion; drain fat. Season with salt and pepper. Place in a greased 2-quart casserole. Top with potatoes. Combine soup and milk; pour over potatoes. Sprinkle with cheese. Bake at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes. Yields 2 to 3 servings.

title: Mexican Bites
text: Mexican Bites. Ingredients: 6 English muffins, halved; 1 c. black olives, chopped; 1/4 c. chopped onions; 2 c. sharp Cheddar cheese, grated; 1/2 c. mayonnaise; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. curry powder; 1/2 tsp. chili powder. Directions: Blend all ingredients, except muffins. Toast muffins until half dark. Top with mixture and bake at 350° for 15 minutes. Cut into 4 pieces.

title: Speedy Stroganoff
text: Speedy Stroganoff. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; 3/4 c. beef gravy; 1 (4 oz.) can sliced mushrooms; 2 Tbsp. catsup; 2 Tbsp. dry sherry; 1/2 c. sour cream dip with onion; hot cooked noodles or rice. Directions: In a saucepan, brown hamburger and drain. Stir in gravy. Stir in undrained mushrooms and catsup. Add 1 cup water. Cover; bring to boiling. Simmer, covered, 15 minutes. Stir in wine. Gradually blend 1 cup beef mixture into sour cream dip. Return to saucepan. Heat through. Do not boil. Serve over noodles. Sprinkle with paprika.

title: Sausage Italian
text: Sausage Italian. Ingredients: 2 lb. Italian sausage; 2 cans crushed tomatoes, pureed; 1 (8 oz.) can mushrooms; 2 green peppers, chopped; 2 onions, chopped; 1/4 c. Romano cheese; 1 tsp. garlic powder; 1/2 tsp. basil; 1 tsp. sugar; 1 c. cooking wine. Directions: Brown sausage in a large frying pan. When sausage is cooked, cut into 1-inch pieces. Put remaining ingredients into crock-pot; add sausage. Cook on high 1 hour. Cook on low 7 to 8 hours. Serve over spaghetti.

title: Chicken Breasts Florentine
text: Chicken Breasts Florentine. Ingredients: 2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen chopped spinach; 4 whole large chicken breasts, skinned, boned and halved; 1 rib celery; 1/2 medium onion, cut up; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/4 c. butter; 1/4 c. flour; dash of white pepper; 1 c. light cream; 1/2 c. Parmesan cheese; dash of ground nutmeg. Directions: Cook spinach according to package directions; drain well. Place chicken in saucepan with celery, onion, salt and 1 cup water. Bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer until meat is tender, about 20 minutes. Remove chicken from broth. Strain broth, reserving 1 cup. Discard vegetables.

title: Marshmallow Fruit Dip
text: Marshmallow Fruit Dip. Ingredients: 1 Tbsp. grated orange rind, divided; 1 Tbsp. orange juice; 1 (7 oz.) jar marshmallow cream; 1/2 c. mayonnaise; 1 tsp. grated lemon rind; 1 tsp. lemon juice. Directions: Set aside 1 teaspoon of orange rind. Combine the remaining 2 teaspoons of orange rind and next 5 ingredients in a bowl. Beat at medium speed of electric mixer until smooth and blended. Spoon into serving container and sprinkle with reserved orange rind. Serve with assorted fresh fruits. Yields about 1 1/2 cups.

title: Broccoli Almond
text: Broccoli Almond. Ingredients: 2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen chopped broccoli; 1/4 c. chopped onion; 1/4 c. butter; 2 Tbsp. flour; 1/2 c. milk; 1 (8 oz.) jar pasteurized process cheese spread; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 2 eggs, well beaten; 1/2 c. bread crumbs; 2 Tbsp. melted butter; 1/4 c. toasted slivered almonds. Directions: Cook broccoli in boiling, salted water. Drain thoroughly. Cook onion in 1/4 cup butter until soft. Blend in flour, then milk. Cook, stirring constantly until thickened. Stir in cheese spread, salt and pepper until thoroughly blended. Remove from heat. Add cheese mixture to eggs, a little at a time, stirring constantly. Stir in broccoli. Place in a greased 1 1/2-quart casserole. Toss crumbs with melted butter and sprinkle on top. Top with almonds. Bake in a slow oven (325°) until set, 40 to 45 minutes. Makes 6 servings.

title: Exercise Energy Cookies
text: Exercise Energy Cookies. Ingredients: 2 c. sugar; 2 c. brown sugar; 3/4 c. oleo or butter, softened; 1 c. shortening; 4 eggs; 2 tsp. vanilla; 3 c. flour; 2 tsp. salt; 2 tsp. soda; 3 c. uncooked oats; 2 c. coconut; 2 c. raisins; 1 c. chocolate chips; 1 c. chopped nuts. Directions: Cream together butter, shortening and both sugars. Beat in eggs and vanilla. Combine flour, salt and soda; beat into first mixture. Add remaining ingredients and knead into mixture.

title: "Good Friday" Vegetable Soup
text: "Good Friday" Vegetable Soup. Ingredients: 1/4 c. butter; 3 medium carrots, sliced; 2 medium onions; 1 c. shredded cabbage; 1/4 c. chopped parsley; 1/2 tsp. salt; 2 (13 3/4 oz.) cans chicken broth; 1 (9 oz.) pkg. French-cut green beans; 1/2 tsp. caraway seed; 1 (16 oz.) pkg. American cheese slices. Directions: In covered Dutch oven or saucepan (4-quart) over medium heat, cook butter, carrots, onions, cabbage, parsley and salt for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add chicken broth, green beans and caraway seed. Reduce to low heat, cover and simmer 15 minutes or until vegetables are fork-tender.

title: Orange Tapioca
text: Orange Tapioca. Ingredients: 4 c. boiling water; pinch of salt; 1/2 c. baby pearl tapioca; 1 (3 oz.) pkg. orange jello; 1 tsp. orange Kool-Aid; 2/3 c. sugar; 4 oranges, cut up; 1/2 c. sugar. Directions: Cook the first 3 ingredients. When cooked, add the jello, Kool-Aid and sugar. When cold, add the cut up oranges and put in the sugar overnight. Pineapple or fruit cocktail added also makes it good!

title: Special Potatoes
text: Special Potatoes. Ingredients: 4 large potatoes; 1/2 pt. whipping cream; salt and pepper; butter. Directions: Boil potatoes in skins. Chill thoroughly. Peel and grate coarsely. Spread in baking dish, season to taste and dot with butter. Pour whipping cream over. Bake at 350° for 30 to 45 minutes until brown around edges. Can be doubled or tripled. Also, onions or cheese can be added.

title: Tang Pie
text: Tang Pie. Ingredients: 1/2 c. Tang; 1 can Eagle Brand milk; 8 oz. sour cream; 8 oz. Cool Whip; graham cracker pie crust. Directions: Mix Tang with Eagle Brand milk. Make sure Tang is dissolved and add sour cream and Cool Whip; mix well. Put in graham cracker pie crust. Chill 2 to 3 hours.

title: Deep Dish Apple Dish
text: Deep Dish Apple Dish. Ingredients: 6 c. pared and sliced apples; 1/2 c. light corn syrup; 1 tsp. lemon juice; 1 1/2 tsp. cinnamon, divided; 1/4 tsp. nutmeg; 1/2 c. sugar; 1/8 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. butter, softened; 3/4 c. flour; 1/4 c. old-fashioned oats (uncooked); 1/2 gal. vanilla ice cream. Directions: Preheat oven to 375°. Lightly grease 8-inch glass baking dish. Place half of apples in dish; top with corn syrup. Add remaining apples, lemon juice, 1 teaspoon cinnamon and nutmeg. Combine 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon, sugar, salt, butter, flour and oatmeal. Mix well and press firmly over apples. Bake 50 minutes. Top with ice cream and serve.

title: Rainbow Butter Cake
text: Rainbow Butter Cake. Ingredients: 3/4 c. butter, softened; 1 c. plus 2 Tbsp. sifted granulated sugar; 2 large eggs; 1 tsp. lemon juice; 1/2 tsp. vanilla extract; 2 1/4 tsp. baking powder; 2 3/4 c. sifted cake flour; 1/2 c. milk. Directions: Note: Have all ingredients at room temperature.

title: Sweet 'N Sour Meatballs
text: Sweet 'N Sour Meatballs. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; 1 pkg. Lipton onion soup mix; water (amount on back of soup box); pepper and salt to taste; 1 c. pineapple juice; 1 Tbsp. cooking oil; 3 Tbsp. cornstarch; 1 Tbsp. soy sauce; 3 Tbsp. vinegar; 6 Tbsp. water; 1/2 c. brown sugar; 1 small can pineapple chunks; 1 large pepper, cut into cubes. Directions: Mix first 4 ingredients and shape mixture into bite size balls; brown. Add pineapple juice to oil and cook slightly over low heat. Add a mixture of cornstarch, soy sauce, vinegar, water and sugar. Cook until thick, stirring constantly. Add meatballs, pineapple chunks and green/red pepper. Cook for about 3 minutes, until heated through. Enjoy!

title: Cranberry Freeze
text: Cranberry Freeze. Ingredients: 1 pkg. frozen strawberries; 1/2 c. frozen peaches; 1 ripe banana; 2 c. cranberry juice; juice of 1 lemon or lime. Directions: Place all ingredients in a blender and blend at low speed. Add more cranberry juice if necessary

title: Acini De Pepe
text: Acini De Pepe. Ingredients: 1 (16 oz.) can crushed pineapple, drained (save juice); 2 small cans mandarin oranges, drained (save juice); 3/4 c. sugar; 2 eggs; 3 Tbsp. flour; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 box acini de pepe (pasta); 13 oz. container Cool Whip. Directions: In a pot, cook sugar, eggs, flour and salt; mix well. Add fruit juices. Bring to a boil. Cool well. Cook and strain acini de pepe. Stir macaroni into cooked custard juices. Chill overnight.

title: Sage-Onion Potatoes
text: Sage-Onion Potatoes. Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. potatoes (small), scrubbed (but unpared) and cut into 1/4-inch slices; 2 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 3 Tbsp. oil; 1 to 1 1/2 c. boiling water; 8 scallions, finely chopped; 6 sage leaves or 1/2 tsp. dried sage; 3 Tbsp. chopped fresh parsley; salt; pepper. Directions: Heat butter and oil in a large heavy skillet. Add the sliced potatoes; turn them to coat thoroughly in the butter and oil, then cook over medium heat for 20 minutes. Turn potatoes often so they cook evenly. Carefully add 1 cup boiling water to the potatoes in the pan. Add the scallions, sage, parsley and season with salt and pepper. Boil, uncovered, for 10 minutes, until water evaporates. If potatoes aren't done, add 1/3 cup boiling water and cook 5 minutes more. Serve at once. Serves 4; 315 calories per serving.

title: Double Chocolate Chip Cookies
text: Double Chocolate Chip Cookies. Ingredients: 1 c. corn oil margarine; 1 c. white sugar; 1/2 c. firmly packed brown sugar; 1 large egg; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1/3 c. cocoa; 2 Tbsp. milk; 1/4 tsp. soda; 1 3/4 c. flour; 1 c. chopped walnuts; 1 c. chocolate chips. Directions: Cream the sugars and shortening. Beat in egg, vanilla, cocoa, soda and milk. Mix in flour, nuts and chips. Drop by teaspoonful on ungreased cookie sheet. Bake in a 350° preheated oven about 10 minutes. Cool 5 minutes before removing from sheet. Cool on wire rack. Store in airtight container in freezer.

title: Applesauce-Raisin Cookies(Diabetic)
text: Applesauce-Raisin Cookies(Diabetic). Ingredients: 5/8 c. flour; 1/2 tsp. soda; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. nutmeg; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 c. quick cooking rolled oats; 2/3 c. raisins; 1/2 c. unsweetened applesauce; 1/4 c. oil; 1 egg, beaten; 1 tsp. vanilla extract; 1 tsp. liquid sweetener. Directions: Sift flour, soda, salt, nutmeg and cinnamon into a bowl. Add rolled oats and raisins. Mix applesauce, oil, egg, vanilla and sweetener together. Add to dry ingredients; mix just until moistened. Drop by teaspoonfuls onto a greased cookie sheet. Bake at 375° for 10 minutes, or until done. Yields 2 dozen cookies.

title: Mile High Biscuits
text: Mile High Biscuits. Ingredients: 3 c. flour; 1/2 c. shortening; 1/4 c. sugar; 1 egg; 4 tsp. baking powder; 1/8 tsp. cream of tartar; 1 tsp. salt; 1 c. milk. Directions: Sift all ingredients together. Blend in shortening. Add milk and egg (one at a time). Stir to mix. Turn out on floured board and knead. Roll out and cut biscuits. Bake at 375° until light brown.

title: Grandma Fay'S Cake Donuts(1898-1974)
text: Grandma Fay'S Cake Donuts(1898-1974). Ingredients: 1 c. sugar; 2 Tbsp. shortening; 2 eggs; 1 c. buttermilk; 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 1 Tbsp. baking powder; 1 tsp. vanilla; 6 c. sifted flour; dash of nutmeg. Directions: Mix first four ingredients. Sift flour and add baking soda and baking powder. Mix into other ingredients and add vanilla and nutmeg. Refrigerate dough overnight.

title: Italian Dream Cake
text: Italian Dream Cake. Ingredients: 2 c. sugar; 1/2 c. Crisco; 1 stick oleo; 5 eggs, separated; 1 tsp. vanilla; 2 c. flour; 1 tsp. soda; 1 c. buttermilk. Directions: Cream well the sugar, Crisco and oleo. Add 5 egg yolks, one at a time, and beat well after each addition. Add vanilla. Mix flour and soda and add alternately with the buttermilk to the creamed mixture. Beat egg whites and fold into the batter. Pour batter into 3 greased and floured pans (approximately 8-inch). Bake for 25 minutes at 350°.

title: Microwave Peanut Clusters
text: Microwave Peanut Clusters. Ingredients: 6 oz. semi-sweet chocolate chips; 6 oz. peanut butter chips; 1 small can (6 1/2 oz.) cocktail peanuts. Directions: You will need 1 1/2-quart casserole dish.

title: Seven Different Things Bars
text: Seven Different Things Bars. Ingredients: 1 stick margarine; 1 pkg. graham crackers, crushed; 1 (6 oz.) pkg. chocolate chips; 1 (6 oz.) pkg. butterscotch chips; 1 1/2 c. coconut; 1 c. nuts; 1 can Eagle Brand sweetened condensed milk. Directions: Melt butter in a 9 x 13-inch pan. Layer in pan as listed and pour milk over top. Bake 20 to 25 minutes at 350°. Cool and cut into squares.

title: Ambrosia Cream Cheese Mold
text: Ambrosia Cream Cheese Mold. Ingredients: 1 envelope unflavored gelatin; 1/2 c. cold water; 1 (15 1/2 oz.) can unsweetened pineapple chunks (undrained); 1/3 c. sugar; juice of 1 lemon; 2 (3 oz.) pkg. cream cheese, softened; 1 orange, peeled, sectioned and diced; 1/2 c. chopped pecans; 1/2 c. flaked coconut. Directions: Soften gelatin in water; let mixture stand 5 minutes. Drain pineapple and reserve juice. Add enough water to juice to make 1 cup. Pour juice into saucepan; heat to boiling. Add gelatin mixture; stir until dissolved. Remove from heat. Stir in sugar, lemon juice and cream cheese (a wire whisk helps to blend). Chill until gelatin is partially set. Fold in pineapple chunks, orange, pecans and coconut. Spoon mixture into lightly greased 1-quart mold. Chill until firm. Unmold.

title: Our Basic Waldorf Salad
text: Our Basic Waldorf Salad. Ingredients: 3 c. chopped apples; 2 c. miniature marshmallows; 1 c. thin celery slices; 1/2 c. chopped walnuts; Miracle Whip salad dressing. Directions: Combine apples, marshmallows, celery and walnuts. Add enough Miracle Whip to moisten. Toss lightly. Chill. Add additional Miracle Whip and garnish with apples before serving, if desired. Yields 6 to 8 servings.

title: Corn And Dumplings
text: Corn And Dumplings. Ingredients: 2 bags frozen corn; 2 c. water; 1 tsp. salt; 1/4 c. sugar; 1/4 c. flour; 1/2 stick butter; 1 can biscuits; 2 lb. frozen corn; 1 c. sugar; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. vanilla; 3 c. milk; 1/4 c. cornstarch, mixed with a little milk; 2 eggs. Directions: Mix well in glass baking dish all the ingredients. Dot with 1/2 stick butter. Bake at 350° until set or it barely shakes in middle.

title: Lemon-Paprika Chicken
text: Lemon-Paprika Chicken. Ingredients: 1 chicken breast (for 2 people; skinless or skin on); 1 lemon (for 2 chicken breasts); white or black pepper to taste; garlic powder to taste; salt (if desired); paprika to taste. Directions: Rinse chicken with cold water; pat dry with paper towels. Place skin side down on broiler pan. Squeeze about 1 teaspoon lemon juice on each piece of chicken. Sprinkle on lightly the pepper, garlic powder, salt and paprika. Place broiler pan 6 to 8-inches below broiler element or gas flame. Broil chicken until crispy. Turn pieces of chicken over; apply lemon and spices on skin side and broil until done next to bone and surface is crispy.

title: Turtle Cake
text: Turtle Cake. Ingredients: 1 box German Chocolate cake mix; 1 (14 oz) bag Kraft caramels; 3/4 c. butter; 1/2 c. evaporated milk; 1 c. semi sweet chocolate chips; 1 c. chopped pecans. Directions: Mix cake according to directions. Grease and flour a 9x13 inch pan. Bake one half of cake mixture in 350° oven for 15 minutes. In sauce pan (over low heat) melt caramels with butter and milk. Remove cake from oven and pour sauce mixture over half baked cake. Top with chocolate chips and pecans. Pour second layer of cake batter over top and return to oven. Bake 20 minutes at 350°.

title: Orange Sweet Potatoes
text: Orange Sweet Potatoes. Ingredients: 6 medium sweet potatoes; 2 tsp. grated orange rind; 1 Tbsp. cornstarch; 1/3 c. sugar; 1/3 c. brown sugar, firmly packed; pinch of salt; 1 c. orange juice; 3 Tbsp. butter or margarine. Directions: Cook sweet potatoes in jackets in 1/2 inch of boiling, salted water until tender, about 30 minutes. Remove skins.

title: Hamburger Chili Dip
text: Hamburger Chili Dip. Ingredients: 1 lb. hamburger, cooked and drained; 1 can chili, no beans; 1 box Mexican Velveeta (hot or mild). Directions: Mix into crock-pot, set on low. Cook about 1 hour until hot. Serve with chips. Serves 4 to 6.

title: Christmas Jello Salad
text: Christmas Jello Salad. Ingredients: 1 can condensed milk; 1 small box red jello; 1 small box green jello; 2 envelope unflavored jello. Directions: Mix 2 envelopes jello with milk. Add 1 1/4 cups of boiling water and mix well. Set aside. Mix red jello and 1 cup of hot water; set aside. Mix green jello and 1 cup hot water; set aside. Add 1 package of gelatin with first and second jellos. Place both colors of jello in icebox until firm. Add milk mixture, then add other color on top. Chill!

title: Orange Pound Cake
text: Orange Pound Cake. Ingredients: 1 c. shortening; 2 c. flour; 5 eggs; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 2/3 c. sugar; 1/4 tsp. salt; 5 Tbsp. orange juice. Directions: Combine shortening and sugar well; add 1 egg at a time, beating well after each egg. Sift flour and salt; add alternately with the orange juice and flavoring. Bake in tube pan at 325° for 1 hour.

title: Chicken Pate
text: Chicken Pate. Ingredients: 2 tsp. gelatin; 125 ml white wine; 100 g cooked minced chicken breast; 125 g mushrooms, chopped finely; 2 shallots, chopped finely; 1 Tbsp. parsley, chopped; 5 ml Worcestershire sauce; 1 egg, beaten; freshly ground black pepper. Directions: Dissolve gelatin in white wine over hot water. Combine with remaining ingredients. Place in a lightly oiled loaf tin, pressing down well. Cover with foil and allow to set overnight in refrigerator.

title: Tuna Vegetable Souffle
text: Tuna Vegetable Souffle. Ingredients: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen vegetables, cooked; 1/4 c. chopped pimiento; 1 Tbsp. minced onion; 2 cans tuna, drained; instant mashed potatoes; 1/2 c. mayonnaise; 4 eggs, separated. Directions: Combine first 5 ingredients in large bowl. Prepare 4 servings of potatoes, omitting butter. Fold in mayonnaise. Add egg yolks, one at a time; beat until fluffy. Stir into vegetable mixture. Beat egg whites until stiff and fold in. Pour into greased 1/2-quart casserole. Bake at 350° for 1 hour or until knife inserted in center comes out clean. Makes 6 servings.

title: Pineapple Nut Bread
text: Pineapple Nut Bread. Ingredients: 2 c. flour; 1 c. sugar; 1 c. chopped walnuts; 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 2 tsp. baking powder; 3/4 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. mace; 1 egg; 1/2 c. milk; 1/4 c. melted butter; 1 (8 1/2 oz.) can crushed pineapple with juice. Directions: In a large bowl, mix first 7 dry ingredients well. In a small bowl, mix remaining ingredients. Stir into dry ingredients until just moist. Pour batter into greased 9 x 5-inch loaf pan. Bake at 350° for 50 minutes. Cool in pan 10 minutes before removing.

title: Eggplant Soup
text: Eggplant Soup. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. butter; 1 Tbsp. oil; 1 c. chopped onion; 3/4 c. chopped celery; 2 lb. eggplant, peeled and diced; 2 c. peeled, thinly sliced potatoes; pinch of cayenne; 1 1/4 tsp. curry powder; 1/2 tsp. thyme; 1/8 tsp. basil; 1 tsp. salt; freshly ground black pepper; 6 to 7 c. chicken stock or other stock; 2 c. heavy cream. Directions: Steam onion and celery in hot oil and butter in tightly covered pot for about 10 minutes on low heat. Add eggplant and potatoes and steam 15 minutes. Stir in next 6 ingredients and bring to a boil. Lower heat and simmer 30 minutes. Cool for 10 minutes. Puree in blender. Add cayenne and cream. Serve hot or cold. Freezes well. Yields 1 1/2 to 2-quart. You may add green onion tops if you wish.

title: Little Cucumber Sandwiches
text: Little Cucumber Sandwiches. Ingredients: 8 oz. cream cheese, softened; 2 Tbsp. mayonnaise; 1 packet Good Seasons Italian dressing; dill weed; cucumber, thinly sliced; party size pumpernickel bread slices. Directions: Mix together cream cheese, mayonnaise and dry package of Italian dressing mix. Spread on bread slices. Top with thin cucumber slices. Sprinkle with dill weed.

title: Piquant Chicken(Serves 4)
text: Piquant Chicken(Serves 4). Ingredients: 4 whole chicken breasts or equivalent amount cut-up chicken pieces; 1 1/2 c. dry red wine; 1/2 c. salad oil; 2 cloves garlic, sliced; 1/2 tsp. oregano; 1/2 c. soy sauce; 4 Tbsp. water; 2 tsp. ground ginger; 2 Tbsp. brown sugar; 4 lb. brisket *; 2 Tbsp. salad oil; 1 clove garlic, peeled and chopped; 6 oz. tomato paste; 1/2 c. cider vinegar; 2 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 2 medium onions, sliced; 8 oz. tomato sauce; 2/3 c. packed light brown sugar; 1 Tbsp. salt; 1 tsp. dry mustard; 2 bay leaves; 2 1/2 lb. stewing beef, cut in cubes; 2 medium onions, sliced; 1/2 c. pale dry sherry; 1 can cream of mushroom soup. Directions: Place beef cubes in single layer in large, ovenproof casserole. Spread soup over cubes. Distribute sliced onions over soup/beef mixture. Pour sherry over top. Cover and cook for 2 hours at 325°. Stir and then cook for at least 1 more hour or until meat tests fork-tender. Serve over rice or noodles.

title: Texas Beef Skillet
text: Texas Beef Skillet. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; 3/4 c. chopped onion; 1 (16 oz.) can tomatoes, cut up; 1 (15 1/2 oz.) can red kidney beans; 1/2 c. quick cooking rice; 1/2 c. water; 3 Tbsp. chopped green pepper; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 1/2 tsp. chili powder; 1/2 tsp. garlic salt; 3/4 c. shredded American cheese (3 oz.); corn chips, crushed. Directions: In a skillet, cook ground beef and onion until meat is browned and onion is tender. Drain off fat. Stir in the undrained tomatoes, beans, rice, water, green pepper, chili powder, salt and garlic salt. Reduce heat. Simmer covered for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally. Top with cheese. Cover and heat about 3 minutes or until cheese melts. Sprinkle corn chips around edge. Serves 6.

title: Frosting For "Lazy Daisy" Cake
text: Frosting For "Lazy Daisy" Cake. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. evaporated milk; 6 Tbsp. brown sugar; 3 Tbsp. melted margarine; 1/2 c. coconut; 1/2 c. chopped nut meats (optional). Directions: Mix all ingredients together and spread on a baked Lazy Daisy Cake. Place under the heated broiler in oven until it bubbles. Delicious!

title: Melissa'S Coffee Cake
text: Melissa'S Coffee Cake. Ingredients: 3 1/4 c. sifted all-purpose flour; 3 tsp. baking powder; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. soft butter; 1 c. light brown sugar; 2 eggs; 1 c. milk; 1/2 c. white sugar; 1/4 c. butter or margarine; 1 Tbsp. cinnamon. Directions: Preheat oven to 375°. Lightly grease a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan. Sift 2 3/4 cups flour with baking powder and salt. In large mixing bowl, with electric mixer at medium speed, beat butter with brown sugar and eggs. Beat until mixture is light and fluffy. At low speed, blend in milk, then flour mixture; beat just to combine. Turn batter into prepared pan, spreading evenly.

title: Jalapinto Rice Casserole
text: Jalapinto Rice Casserole. Ingredients: 1/2 c. chopped onion; 1 Tbsp. melted butter; 1 lb. lean ground beef; 2 (15 oz.) cans jalapeno pinto beans; 2 c. cooked rice; 1 (16 oz.) can tomatoes, drained and chopped; 1 c. sharp Cheddar cheese, shredded. Directions: Saute onion in butter until tender. Add ground beef and brown. Stir in pinto beans, rice and tomatoes. Mix well. Pour into a 2-quart casserole dish. Sprinkle cheese over top. Bake at 350° for 20 minutes or until cheese is melted. Serves 6 to 8.

title: Tomato Sauce
text: Tomato Sauce. Ingredients: 2 small cans tomato paste; 1 large can whole tomatoes; 1 small onion, chopped; 1 small green pepper, chopped; 1 Tbsp. parsley flakes; 1 tsp. oregano; 1 tsp. paprika; 2 bay leaves; 1 tsp. garlic powder; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Cook onion and green pepper in oil for about 5 minutes. Add tomato paste and four cans of water; stir well. Add whole tomatoes and break them up in the saucepan, then add all your herbs, stir well, cover and simmer for about 2 hours. Taste for flavor; if not enough, add more herbs. Season with salt and pepper.

title: Beet Top Greens
text: Beet Top Greens. Ingredients: 1 lb. beet greens; 2 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 1 Tbsp. prepared horseradish; 1 tsp. lemon juice; salt and pepper. Directions: Beet greens may be prepared in the same ways that you prepare spinach or Swiss chard, but they must be young and small to be tasty. Try cooking the greens in boiling salted water for 5 to 8 minutes. Drain, chop and add the butter or margarine. Toss with a fork until butter is melted. Add the horseradish and lemon juice; mix well. Season with salt and pepper. Serves 4.

title: Oreo Cookie Cheesecake
text: Oreo Cookie Cheesecake. Ingredients: 1 (1 1/4 lb.) pkg. Oreos; 1/3 c. butter or margarine, softened; 4 (8 oz. each) pkg. cream cheese, cut into chunks; 1 c. sugar; 1/3 c. whipping cream; 6 large eggs; 2 Tbsp. all-purpose flour; 2 tsp. vanilla. Directions: In a food processor, whirl 1/2 package of the cookies, including filling, until they form fine crumbs; mix in butter. Scoop crumbs into a 9-inch cheesecake pan with removable bottom and press crumbs evenly over bottom of pan and about 1/2-inch up sides. Chill crust to firm, about 20 minutes.

title: Nan Marshall'S Baked Chicken
text: Nan Marshall'S Baked Chicken. Ingredients: 1/4 c. shortening (part butter); 1/4 c. all-purpose flour; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 1 1/2 lb. chicken pieces or 2 lb. chicken. Directions: Preheat oven to 425°. Heat shortening in 9 x 9 x 2-inch or 12 x 8 x 2-inch baking pan. Mix all dry ingredients in bag. Add 2 or 3 pieces of chicken and shake until coated.

title: Mom'S Biscuits
text: Mom'S Biscuits. Ingredients: 3 c. flour; 3 tsp. baking powder; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. shortening; milk. Directions: Mix dry ingredients with shortening until shortening is thoroughly mixed. Add enough milk to make a stiff, but stirable dough. Put on floured table or board and knead until not sticky. Roll out to 1/2 inch thick; cut biscuits and turn in melted fat in baking pan. Makes about 12 or 15 biscuits.

title: Shawn'S Deer Steak
text: Shawn'S Deer Steak. Ingredients: 2 venison ham steaks; 1 small onion, chopped; 1 can diced tomatoes; 1 green pepper, chopped; 1 1/2 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce. Directions: Brown the venison in a skillet with 1/2 stick of margarine. When browned, add onion and green pepper. Continue cooking on medium heat. Season with salt, pepper and garlic salt. When onion is tender, add Worcestershire sauce. Top with one can of diced tomatoes. Cover and simmer until meat is tender.

title: Chilies 'N Ham Cups
text: Chilies 'N Ham Cups. Ingredients: 10 oz. can Hungry Jack biscuits; 3 oz. pkg. cream cheese, softened; 1 c. cubed cooked ham; 1/4 c. sliced ripe olives; 1 c. (4 oz.) Cheddar cheese, shredded; 1/2 c. (4 oz.) chopped green chilies, drained; salsa and sour cream (if desired). Directions: Separate biscuits and place one each into greased muffin tins. Press firmly onto bottom and up sides, forming a 1/4-inch rim.

title: Pineapple-Cheese Christmas Casserole
text: Pineapple-Cheese Christmas Casserole. Ingredients: 2 large cans pineapple chunks; 2 c. shredded Cheddar cheese; 1 c. sugar; 6 Tbsp. flour; 1 (3 oz.) tube crushed Ritz crackers; 4 oz. melted butter. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Mix together pineapple and cheese; set aside. Mix sugar and flour together. Add to fruit and cheese mixture. Spread mixture in an 8 x 11-inch or 9 x 12-inch casserole dish. Spread crushed Ritz crackers over top. Pour melted butter over all. Bake 35 to 40 minutes. Let cool and serve. This dish is good hot or cold.

title: Sweet And Sour Asparagus
text: Sweet And Sour Asparagus. Ingredients: 1/4 c. wine vinegar; 1/2 c. sugar; 1/4 c. water; 1/2 tsp. celery seed; 1 cinnamon stick; 3 whole cloves; salt to taste; 1 can asparagus. Directions: Drain asparagus. Combine rest of ingredients, except asparagus, and cook over medium heat for about 45 minutes. Remove cinnamon stick and cloves. Pour the liquid over asparagus. Cover and refrigerate immediately. Let marinate at least overnight.

title: Quick Cornbread Mix
text: Quick Cornbread Mix. Ingredients: 4 1/4 c. flour; 2 1/2 c. cornmeal; 1 1/2 c. nonfat dry milk; 1/4 c. baking powder; 1 Tbsp. salt; 1 1/2 c. shortening. Directions: Sift together dry ingredients. Cut in shortening with pastry blender or two knives to mix thoroughly. Place mix in a jar or canister and store in a dry place until ready to use. This mix will keep in a cool place for about a month.

title: Sis Haack'S Black-Eyed Peas(Beans)
text: Sis Haack'S Black-Eyed Peas(Beans). Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. oleo or margarine; 1 lb. hamburger (lean); 1 can tomato sauce; 1 tsp. sugar; 1/2 c. catsup; 1 Tbsp. vinegar; 1 can black-eyed peas (green; not dried). Directions: Fry until cooked the oleo and hamburger. Add the other ingredients and let simmer 30 to 40 minutes. Add a little water, if dry.

title: Scorcher
text: Scorcher. Ingredients: 1 tsp. ground cumin; 1 tsp. oregano; 1/2 c. olive oil; 1 tsp. red pepper; 10 cloves garlic, minced; 1 Tbsp. sugar; 2 bay leaves; 3 Tbsp. flour; 6 Tbsp. cornmeal; 2 lb. lean beef, coarsely ground; 1 qt. water; 8 chili peppers; 3 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. pepper. Directions: In a large kettle, fry meat in oil until dark brown. Then add 1-quart of water. Cover and simmer two hours. Add remaining ingredients, except flour and cornmeal. Cook at gentle simmer for 30 minutes, removing bay leaves after 15 minutes. Make a paste of flour, cornmeal and water. Stir into mixture, cooking another five minutes at simmer while stirring often. Serve immediately with a fire extinguisher at your right elbow.

title: Ketchup Swiss Steak
text: Ketchup Swiss Steak. Ingredients: 1 round steak; ketchup; 2 large onions; garlic powder; seasoning salt; little water. Directions: Brown round steak. Quarter onions and add water. Add seasoning. Simmer approximately 3 hours. Add ketchup to taste, approximately 8 to 12 ounces. Simmer 1 hour more.

title: Never Fail Dumplings
text: Never Fail Dumplings. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. flour; 1/2 tsp. salt; 3 Tbsp. shortening; 5 Tbsp. chicken broth (warm); 1 egg, beaten. Directions: Boil a whole chicken in a Dutch oven pot with water. (Cover chicken over half way with water) after chicken is done, take out of broth to cool. Mix flour and shortening in together first. Then add all other ingredients. (I beat my egg in with the 5 tablespoons of chicken broth.) After mixing all ingredients, mixture should be a soft dough. Roll out dough very thin and let stand for about 10 minutes. Then cut into strips and drop into boiling broth. Simmer about 15 minutes, then add deboned chicken to dumplings.

title: Chicken Pizza
text: Chicken Pizza. Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. refrigerated crescent dinner rolls; 2 whole chicken breasts, split, skinned and boned; 1/4 c. vegetable oil; 1 large onion, sliced into thin rings; 1 large green bell pepper, sliced into thin rings; 1/2 lb. fresh mushrooms, sliced; 1/2 c. pitted ripe olives, sliced; 1 (10 1/2 oz.) can pizza sauce with cheese; 1 tsp. garlic salt; 1 tsp. dried oregano; 1/4 c. grated Parmesan cheese; 2 c. (8 oz.) shredded Mozzarella cheese. Directions: Separate rolls into 8 triangles. Press triangles into lightly oiled 12-inch pizza pan, covering it completely. Cut up chicken into bite size pieces.

title: Marinated Beef Tips
text: Marinated Beef Tips. Ingredients: 1 lb. beef tips; 2 Tbsp. diced onion; 1 Tbsp. brown sugar; 1/2 c. Burgundy wine. Directions: Combine onion, brown sugar and wine; marinate beef tips in mixture for at least 1 hour. Place on small skewers and grill.

title: Frozen Champagne Salad
text: Frozen Champagne Salad. Ingredients: 8 oz. cream cheese; 3/4 c. sugar; 2 bananas, sliced; 1/2 c. chopped nuts; 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen strawberries (with juice); 1 large can crushed pineapple, drained; 1 large container Cool Whip. Directions: Soften and blend cream cheese with sugar. Mix together drained pineapple, strawberries with juice, bananas, nuts and whipped topping. Combine 2 mixtures. Put in a 9 x 13-inch pan and freeze.

title: Pat'S Jello Pie
text: Pat'S Jello Pie. Ingredients: 1 (3 oz.) pkg. Jell-O (lime, orange or strawberry); 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese; 1/4 c. sugar; 3 Tbsp. lemon juice; 3 Tbsp. orange juice; 1 Tbsp. lemon or lime rind, grated (optional); 1 pkg. Dream Whip or 1 pt. whipping cream; graham cracker crust. Directions: Blend cheese and sugar; blend in Jell-O (dry), a little at a time. Add lime/lemon juice or rind and orange juice (optional). Fold in whipped topping. When filling gets thick, pour into graham cracker crust. Chill.

title: Fluffy Pancakes
text: Fluffy Pancakes. Ingredients: 1 c. flour; 1 tsp. salt; 4 tsp. baking powder; 2 tsp. sugar; 1 Tbsp. oil; 1 egg; 1 c. milk (approximately). Directions: Mix all the ingredients together, adding the milk last, while mixing to the desired texture. Pour onto hot griddle. Cook and enjoy!

title: Honeyed Grapes
text: Honeyed Grapes. Ingredients: 1/4 c. honey; 1/4 c. loosely packed fresh mint leaves, chopped; 1 lb. seedless red grapes; 1 lb. seedless green grapes. Directions: Combine honey and mint in a large mixing bowl; set aside. Remove and discard grape stems. Wash grapes. Drain and pat dry. Add grapes to honey mixture. Toss gently to coat. Cover and chill at least 2 hours. Yields 6 servings.

title: Broccoli Noodle Kugel *
text: Broccoli Noodle Kugel *. Ingredients: 1 lb. thin noodles; 2 pkg. chopped broccoli, thawed but do not cook; 1 stick melted unsalted margarine; 6 eggs, beaten; 2 pkg. (1 box) onion soup mix (Goodman's); 16 oz. Coffee Rich. Directions: Cook noodles and drain. Add all ingredients together. Bake at 375° for 40 to 50 minutes, uncovered, in a large 9 x 13-inch pan. It can serve 10 to 12 people, depending on appetites.

title: Dog Peanut Butter Biscuits
text: Dog Peanut Butter Biscuits. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. whole wheat flour; 1/2 c. soy flour; 1 Tbsp. baking powder; 1 tsp. salt; 1 c. peanut butter (any kind); 3/4 c. milk. Directions: Preheat oven to 400°. Grease two baking sheets. In mixing bowl combine flours, baking powder and salt. In blender combine peanut butter and milk. Blend until smooth. Pour into dry mixture and blend well. The dough will be soft. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface and knead lightly. Roll out 1/4-inch thick and cut into 2-inch squares. Place on baking sheet, about 1-inch apart.

title: Two Fruit Smoothie
text: Two Fruit Smoothie. Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) carton plain yogurt; 1/2 to 3/4 c. skim milk; 1 c. frozen unsweetened strawberries; 2 bananas, sliced and frozen; ice cubes. Directions: Combine all ingredients in electric blender. Add enough ice cubes to bring to 4-cup level. Blend until smooth. Serve immediately. Each serving contains 100 calories/cup. Nutritious during pregnancy (consult your physician).

title: Creamy Lo-Cal Dressing
text: Creamy Lo-Cal Dressing. Ingredients: 1 c. cottage cheese; 1/2 c. tomato juice; 1/4 c. lemon juice or vinegar. Directions: Blend all ingredients with hand beater until very smooth. Store in covered jar in refrigerator. Makes 1 3/4 cups.

title: 24-Hour Layered Salad
text: 24-Hour Layered Salad. Ingredients: 1 small iceberg lettuce, cut into bite-size; 1/2 green pepper, chopped; 1/2 c. celery, chopped; 1/2 c. onion, chopped; 1 pkg. frozen peas (uncooked); 4 oz. Cheddar cheese, shredded; bacon bits if desired; 1 1/2 c. mayonnaise, mixed with 2 Tbsp. sugar. Directions: Layer all the ingredients, ending with cheese and bacon bits.

title: Grilled Cheeseburgers
text: Grilled Cheeseburgers. Ingredients: hamburger patties; bread; cheese; butter; condiments. Directions: Cook desired number of hamburgers and grilled cheese sandwiches. Before putting the 2 pieces of bread together, add a hamburger patty and your favorite condiment (ketchup, mustard, onion, pickle, etc.). Delicious!

title: Mystery Pie
text: Mystery Pie. Ingredients: 1 pie shell; 1 pkg. Ritz crackers; 5 egg whites; 1 c. sugar; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 c. pecans. Directions: Mix crackers, sugar and pecans together. Beat egg whites and add to cracker mixture. Add vanilla and pour in a greased pie shell. Serve with Cool Whip.

title: Chicken Breast Monterey
text: Chicken Breast Monterey. Ingredients: 6 chicken breasts; salt and pepper to taste; flour; 2 Tbsp. butter; 1/2 c. cooking sherry; 1 small onion, sliced; 6 fresh mushrooms, sliced; 1/2 green pepper, sliced; 6 1/4-inch slices Monterey Jack cheese. Directions: Sprinkle chicken filets with seasoning. Coat with flour. Brown for 5 minutes on each side in butter over medium heat. Reduce heat to low. Add next 4 ingredients. Simmer, covered, for 45 minutes or until chicken is tender. Top each filet with one piece of cheese. Let stand, covered, for 5 minutes or until cheese begins to melt.

title: Frosty Pineapple Punch
text: Frosty Pineapple Punch. Ingredients: 1 (46 oz.) can pineapple juice, chilled; 1 (12 oz.) can frozen pineapple-orange juice concentrate, thawed and undiluted; 1 (6 oz.) can frozen lemonade concentrate, thawed and undiluted; 1/2 gal. pineapple or lemon sherbet, softened. Directions: Combine first 3 ingredients in a punch bowl. Just before serving, spoon sherbet into punch bowl by heaping tablespoonfuls; stir gently. Serve immediately. Yields 3 quarts.

title: Fat Free Pizza Treats
text: Fat Free Pizza Treats. Ingredients: toasted English muffins; pizza sauce; 1 Tbsp. (skim) Mozzarella cheese; Parmesan cheese; grated onion; sliced mushrooms; chopped green pepper. Directions: Top English muffins with pizza sauce and toppings of your choice ending with cheese. Toast in toaster oven until cheese melts.

title: Spinach Madeleine
text: Spinach Madeleine. Ingredients: 2 pkg. frozen (chopped) spinach, cooked and drained; 4 Tbsp. butter; 2 Tbsp. flour; 2 Tbsp. chopped onion (fine); 1/2 c. evaporated milk; 1/2 c. vegetable liquor (from spinach); 1/2 tsp. black pepper; 3/4 tsp. celery salt; 3/4 tsp. garlic salt; 6 oz. roll jalapeno cheese; 1 tsp. Worcestershire sauce. Directions: Cook spinach and drain. Reserve liquor. Melt butter in saucepan over low heat; add flour, stirring until smooth (but not brown). Add liquids, slowly, constantly stirring. Cook until smooth and thick. Add seasonings and cheese. Stir until all melted. Combine spinach. This can be frozen and heated slowly before needed.

title: German Farina Glsse (Dumplings)(Makes 4 To 5 Servings)
text: German Farina Glsse (Dumplings)(Makes 4 To 5 Servings). Ingredients: 6 Tbsp. farina (Cream of Wheat cereal); 3 Tbsp. potato starch; 1 tsp. salt; 3 large potatoes; 1 c. croutons (cubed white bread lightly browned in butter). Directions: Boil peeled and quartered potatoes in small amount of water. When cooked, do not drain. Mash the potatoes in the water and add the farina and potato starch mixture. Mix well. While mixing mixture, bring to a boil a large pot of water. Form the mixture into balls. Stuff a few croutons in each ball as you make them. Be careful, the mixture will be very hot! Run your hands under cold water between making each ball. Place balls into boiling water until they float to the top. Remove with slotted spoon. Serve with meat gravy.

title: Oven Baked Halibut A La Orange
text: Oven Baked Halibut A La Orange. Ingredients: 4 halibut steaks (about 5 to 6 oz. each); 2 tsp. vegetable oil; grated peel of 1/2 orange; 1/8 to 1/4 tsp. dill weed; 2 green onions, cut into 2-inch strips; 2 Tbsp. margarine; 1/2 c. freshly squeezed orange juice (from one of the 3 oranges); 1/3 c. apple juice; 1 Tbsp. cornstarch; 1/2 tsp. chicken bouillon (optional); 3 oranges, peeled, cut in half cartwheel slices and well drained (use 1 for juice). Directions: Line a 12 x 8 x 2-inch shallow baking dish with foil. Lightly brush both sides of halibut steaks with oil. Arrange in dish. Sprinkle with orange peel and dill. Cover dish with foil and bake at 450°, allowing 10 to 12 minutes cooking time per inch of thickness measured at its thickest part or until halibut flakes when tested with a fork.

title: Made-In-The-Shade Pork Chops
text: Made-In-The-Shade Pork Chops. Ingredients: 12 oz. frozen lemonade concentrate, thawed; 2/3 c. Kikkoman soy sauce; 2 tsp. seasoned salt; 1 tsp. celery salt; 1/4 tsp. garlic powder; 4 (1 1/2-inch thick) pork chops. Directions: In shaker jar, combine lemonade concentrate, soy sauce and seasonings. Shake ingredients vigorously to blend. Pour half of marinade into shallow pan and add chops in single layer. Pour remaining marinade over steaks; cover pan and place in refrigerator approximately 24 hours. Meat should be turned once or twice during refrigeration. Remove from refrigerator three or four hours before grilling. Grill over medium coals 40 to 45 minutes. Baste steaks occasionally with remaining marinade.

title: Almond Arfa
text: Almond Arfa. Ingredients: 24 oz. almond bark; 1 c. peanut butter; 4 c. Rice Krispies; 2 c. salted Spanish peanuts; 2 c. mini marshmallows. Directions: Melt almond bark and mix in peanut butter. When smooth, stir in Rice Krispies, peanuts and marshmallows. Drop by teaspoonfuls onto waxed paper and let set up.

title: Cheesy Eggplant Grill
text: Cheesy Eggplant Grill. Ingredients: 1 slice eggplant, 1/2-inch thick; 1 slice onion; 1 slice tomato; salt, pepper and oregano to taste; 1 slice Mozzarella cheese. Directions: Layer all ingredients, starting with eggplant and ending with Mozzarella. Place in foil. Make a ball of foil and close loosely on top, so it does not stick to cheese. Bake on barbecue, lid down for 10 minutes.

title: Pineapple Cherry Dessert
text: Pineapple Cherry Dessert. Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) container Cool Whip, thawed; 1 (20 oz.) can crushed pineapple; 1 angel food cake, baked; 1/2 jar maraschino cherries, drained. Directions: Break up angel food cake into 1-inch pieces. Place cake cubes into serving dishes. Fold pineapple and cherries into Cool Whip and spoon over the cake cubes. Refrigerate several hours. Makes 6 to 8 servings.

title: Lorna Doone Dessert
text: Lorna Doone Dessert. Ingredients: 1 pkg. Lorna Doone cookies; 1 stick margarine; 2 small pkg. instant vanilla pudding; 2 c. milk; 1 qt. vanilla ice cream; 1 medium Cool Whip; 2 or 3 Heath bars. Directions: Mix crushed Lorna Doones and melted margarine. Spread on bottom of 9 x 13-inch pan. Bake for 15 minutes at 350°.

title: Beef Burgundy
text: Beef Burgundy. Ingredients: 1 lb. boneless beef stew meat; 1/4 c. chopped onion; 2 chopped carrots; 1 can beef broth and 1/2 can water; garlic salt, salt and pepper to taste; 4 oz. can mushrooms; 1 c. Burgundy; 1 can peas; parsley. Directions: Brown beef; add onion, carrots and seasonings, parsley, broth and water. Cover and bake at 350° for at least 2 hours. Add mushrooms, drained and peas, drained. Thicken with cornstarch. Serve with hot rice.

title: Cherry Angel Cream Cake
text: Cherry Angel Cream Cake. Ingredients: 1 (10 to 12 oz.) angel food or pound cake; 1 (14 oz.) can Eagle Brand milk; 1 c. cold water; 1 tsp. almond extract; 1 (4 serving size) pkg. vanilla instant pudding mix; 2 c. Cool Whip; 2 (21 oz.) cans cherry pr peach pie filling or topping. Directions: Cut cake into 1/4-inch slices. Arrange half the slices on bottom of a 13 x 9-inch baking dish. In a large mixing bowl, combine milk, water and extract. Mix well. Add pudding mix and beat well. Chill 5 minutes. Fold in Cool Whip. Spread half the cream mixture over cake slices. Top evenly with 1 can cherry filling. Top with remaining cake slices, cream mixture and cherry filling. Chill 4 hours until set. Cut into squares to serve. Refrigerate leftovers.

title: Fluffy Coconut Pudding
text: Fluffy Coconut Pudding. Ingredients: 1 envelope unflavored gelatin; 1/4 c. cold water; 3 Tbsp. boiling water; 3 egg whites; 1/8 tsp. salt; 6 Tbsp. sugar; 1/2 tsp. vanilla extract; 1 c. whipping cream, whipped; 2/3 c. canned moist-type flaked coconut; Mandarin oranges (for garnish). Directions: Soften gelatin in cold water. Add boiling water and stir until dissolved. Cool to room temperature. Beat egg whites with salt until frothy. Add sugar, 1 tablespoon at a time, and beat well after each addition. Continue beating until mixture holds a peak when beater is lifted. Slowly beat gelatin. Add vanilla to whipped cream. Mix and fold into egg whites. Fold in coconut. Cover and chill at least 4 hours. Garnish with Mandarin oranges and serve.

title: Tamale Pie Casserole
text: Tamale Pie Casserole. Ingredients: 2 small cans tamales; 1 large can Chili without beans; 1 large bag of Fritos; 1 white onion, chopped; 1 jar Velveeta cheese; jalapenos in oil, optional; 1/2 c. grated cheddar cheese. Directions: Butter casserole dish. Spread ingredients in layers (Chili, cheese, Fritos, onion, tamales, cheese then repeat.)

title: Cabbage With Caraway
text: Cabbage With Caraway. Ingredients: 1 tsp. margarine; vegetable cooking spray; 8 c. coarsely shredded cabbage; 1 Tbsp. chopped fresh parsley; 1 tsp. sugar; 1 tsp. chicken flavored bouillon granules; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 1 tsp. caraway seed. Directions: Melt margarine in a large nonstick skillet coated with cooking spray. Add cabbage and next 4 ingredients; cover and cook over medium heat for 5 minutes. Sprinkle with caraway seed; cover and cook 1 minute. Yield: 5 cups. Contains 39 calories per 1 cup serving.

title: Chicken And Vegetable Salad
text: Chicken And Vegetable Salad. Ingredients: 1 pkg. frozen Green Giant mixed vegetables; 3 chicken breasts; 1/2 bottle coleslaw dressing. Directions: Chop chicken up in bite size and fry until done after seasoning and flouring. Remove all grease from skillet. Open frozen vegetables and pour into bowl. Mix chicken and dressing and vegetables. Refrigerate overnight for best results.

title: Squash Puppies
