text: Squash Puppies. Ingredients: 1 c. self-rising cornmeal; 1/2 c. self-rising flour; 1/2 c. chopped onion; 1 egg, beaten; 2 Tbsp. milk; 1 tsp. sugar; 1 c. grated squash; vegetable oil (for cooking). Directions: Mix meal and flour. Add onions and squash. Stir in eggs and milk to make stiff batter. Drop by teaspoonfuls into hot oil.

title: Hot Crab, Artichoke And Jalapeno Dip With Pita Triangles
text: Hot Crab, Artichoke And Jalapeno Dip With Pita Triangles. Ingredients: 1 large green bell pepper, chopped; 1 Tbsp. vegetable oil; 2 (14 oz.) cans artichoke hearts, drained and chopped fine; 2 c. bottled mayonnaise; 1/2 c. thinly sliced scallions; 1/2 c. drained and chopped bottled pimiento or roasted red pepper; 1 c. freshly grated Parmesan; 1 1/2 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice or to taste; 4 tsp. Worcestershire sauce or to taste; 3 bottled pickled jalapeno peppers or to taste, seeded and minced (wear rubber gloves); 1 tsp. celery salt; 1 lb. crab meat, thawed and drained, if frozen, picked over; 1/3 c. sliced almonds, toasted lightly; Pita Triangles (recipe follows). Directions: In a small heavy skillet, cook the bell pepper in the oil over moderate heat, stirring, until it is softened and let it cool. In a large bowl, combine the bell pepper, artichokes, mayonnaise, scallions, pimiento, Parmesan, lemon juice, Worcestershire sauce, jalapeno peppers and celery salt. Blend the mixture until it is combined well and stir in the crab meat gently.

title: Crispy Oven Fried Chicken
text: Crispy Oven Fried Chicken. Ingredients: 6 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves (or 2 1/2 - 3 pounds chicken pieces with skin removed); 1/3 c. flour; 2 egg whites lightly beaten; 1 c. cornflake crumbs. Directions: Preheat oven to 375°. Lightly spray cookie sheet with nonstick spray. Roll chicken pieces in flour to evenly cover all sides. Dip chicken in egg white and roll in cornflake crumbs. Place on prepared cookie sheet. Bake 20 - 30 minutes for boneless breast halves or 45 - 55 minutes for chicken pieces.

title: Asparagus Lasagne Bianco
text: Asparagus Lasagne Bianco. Ingredients: 5 lasagne noodles (2 inches wide); 2 cloves garlic, minced; 1/2 tsp. thyme, crushed; 2 Tbsp. butter; 2 Tbsp. flour; 1 1/3 c. milk; 1 c. shredded Mozzarella cheese; 1 c. julienne ham; 1 (15 oz.) can Del Monte extra long asparagus spears, drained. Directions: Cook noodles as directed on package. Drain and cut in half crosswise. In saucepan, cook garlic and thyme in butter. Blend in flour. Cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes. Stir in milk; cook until thickened. Season sauce with salt and pepper. In buttered 2-quart baking dish, layer 1/3 of noodles, sauce, cheese, ham and asparagus. Repeat layers twice. Cover and cook on high for 9 to 10 minutes.

title: Strawberry Crumble
text: Strawberry Crumble. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. plain flour; 1/3 c. brown sugar; 3/4 c. soft margarine; 1 c. chopped pecans; 2 egg whites; 10 oz. strawberries (I use more); 1 c. sugar; 2 tsp. lemon juice; 1 (10 oz.) Cool Whip. Directions: Beat egg whites stiff, adding sugar and lemon juice. Add this to whipped topping. Add strawberries last.

title: Apple Dessert Cake
text: Apple Dessert Cake. Ingredients: 2 c. diced apples; 1 c. sugar; 1 egg; 1 tsp. soda; 1 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 c. nuts; 1 c. flour. Directions: In a bowl, combine the apples and sugar; let this sit until the sugar is dissolved into the apples. Add remaining ingredients. Bake in 8-inch pan 30 minutes at 400° oven. Let cool slightly then put topping on cake.

title: Cheesecake Bars
text: Cheesecake Bars. Ingredients: 1/2 c. nuts; 2 c. brown sugar; 2/3 c. butter. Directions: Mix ingredients. Save 3/4 cup of mixture for topping. Pat remainder in 9 x 13-inch pan and bake for 8 to 10 minutes at 350°. Cool.

title: Best Ever Potato Salad
text: Best Ever Potato Salad. Ingredients: 10 slices bacon; 3/4 c. mayonnaise; 1 Tbsp. prepared mustard; 2 tsp. sugar; 1 tsp. salt; 4 c. cubed and diced potatoes; 4 eggs, hard-cooked; 1 stalk celery, chopped; 1 red onion (small), chopped; 1/2 medium size green pepper. Directions: Cut bacon into 1-inch pieces. Cook in skillet on medium heat until crisp. Drain bacon. Reserve 2 tablespoons drippings in skillet. Mix mayonnaise, mustard, sugar and salt. Combine potatoes, chopped eggs, celery, onion and green peppers in bowl. Mix mayonnaise mixture into potato mixture. Stir in bacon. Chill several hours before serving.

title: Turtle Cake #1
text: Turtle Cake #1. Ingredients: German chocolate cake mix; 1 1/2 sticks margarine; 1/2 c. Pet milk; 14 oz. bag caramels; 1 c. milk; 1 pkg. chocolate chips; 1 c. chopped pecans. Directions: Mix cake mix as on package; pour 1/2 of batter in 13 x 9-inch greased and floured pan. Bake for 15 minutes at 350°.

title: Honey Chocolate Chip Cookies
text: Honey Chocolate Chip Cookies. Ingredients: 1 c. shortening, creamed (generous cup); 1 c. honey; 2 eggs; 1 large pkg. chocolate chips; 1 c. nut meats; 3 c. flour; 1 tsp. soda; 2 tsp. vanilla; 1 tsp. salt. Directions: Drop by spoonfuls onto ungreased cookie sheet. Bake for 12 minutes at 375°.

title: Crab-Broccoli Bisque
text: Crab-Broccoli Bisque. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. butter; 1/2 onion, diced; 2 Tbsp. flour; 2 c. half and half; 2 c. milk; fresh ground black pepper to taste; 2 cans lump crab meat; 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen broccoli (thawed); lots of fresh thyme to taste. Directions: Saute butter and onion until transparent. Add flour, half and half and milk. Stir until thick. May thicken with cornstarch, if preferred. Add pepper, crab meat and broccoli and heat thoroughly. Add grated Parmesan cheese just before serving. Top with croutons or oyster crackers. May serve with warm, crusty French bread.

title: Easy Golden Layer Cake
text: Easy Golden Layer Cake. Ingredients: 1 (20 oz.) can crushed pineapple; 1 (8 oz.) carton frozen whipped topping; 1 small pkg. instant vanilla pudding mix; 1 (16 oz.) frozen pound cake, thawed; 1/3 c. almond flavored liqueur; 1/2 tsp. almond extract; 1/4 c. sliced almonds, toasted (optional). Directions: Combine undrained pineapple, thawed whipped topping and pudding mix. Let stand 5 minutes. Cut cake lengthwise in thirds. Drizzle with liqueur. Spread 1/3 of pudding mixture over bottom layer of cake. Top with second layer. Repeat layering with pudding. Chill 30 minutes. Sprinkle with toasted, sliced almonds. Serves 12.

title: Biscotti
text: Biscotti. Ingredients: 1/2 lb. butter; 8 eggs; 1 Tbsp. vanilla or almond extract; 8 c. flour; 2 lb. almonds, chopped; 4 Tbsp. anise; 3 c. sugar; 1/2 tsp. salt. Directions: Mix all ingredients together and knead. Bake at 350° until firm. Let cool for 10 minutes. Slice and rebake on ungreased cookie sheet.

title: Cheese Fondue
text: Cheese Fondue. Ingredients: 2 lb. Switzerland Swiss cheese, cut up; 1 1/3 c. white wine (inexpensive); 2 1/4 tsp. cornstarch; pinch of nutmeg; pinch of pepper; pinch of baking soda. Directions: In large pot on stove, rub a clove of garlic and discard. Bring wine to bubble (not quite boil) and gradually add cheese (already cut and tossed with cornstarch) stirring constantly. When cheese is melted, add spices and pour into fondue pot. If too stringy when eating, increase heat.

title: Saucy Asparagus
text: Saucy Asparagus. Ingredients: 1 (10 3/4 oz.) can Campbell's new cream of asparagus soup; 2 Tbsp. milk; 2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen asparagus cuts or 1 1/2 lb. fresh asparagus spears, trimmed and cut into 1-inch pieces (about 24 to 30). Directions: In a 2-quart saucepan over medium heat, combine soup and milk. Heat to boiling, stirring often. Add asparagus. Cover; cook over low heat 10 minutes or until asparagus is tender, stirring occasionally. Serve with grated Parmesan cheese, if desired. Makes 8 servings. Preparation time: 10 minutes. Cook time: 15 minutes.

title: Beets In Orange Sauce
text: Beets In Orange Sauce. Ingredients: 4 to 5 c. sliced beets; 2 Tbsp. flour; 1/4 c. sugar; 1/2 c. orange juice; 2 Tbsp. margarine; 1 tsp. salt. Directions: Mix all ingredients except beets. Boil for 5 minutes. Pour over cooked beets and serve.

title: Chocolate Peanut Butter Dessert
text: Chocolate Peanut Butter Dessert. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. skim milk; 2 Tbsp. chunky peanut butter; 1 c. thawed whipped topping; 2 c. cold skim milk; 1 (4 serving size) pkg. chocolate flavor sugar-free instant pudding and pie filling. Directions: Mix 2 tablespoons milk into peanut butter in a large bowl. Gently stir in whipped topping. Pour 2 cups milk into a large bowl. Add the pudding mix. Beat with wire whisk 1 to 2 minutes or until well blended. Spoon whipped topping mixture and pudding alternately into glasses. Refrigerate 1 hour. Makes 6 servings.

title: Spinach Casserole
text: Spinach Casserole. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. sour cream; 1 Tbsp. onion salt; 1 garlic clove, minced; 2 pkg. frozen chopped spinach, cooked and drained until very dry; 1/2 tsp. ginger; salt and pepper to taste; 1 c. bread crumbs. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Put sour cream, onion salt and garlic in blender or mix with fork. Add to all other ingredients and mix thoroughly. Put in casserole. Top with bread crumbs. Bake for 25 minutes.

title: Montgomery Pie
text: Montgomery Pie. Ingredients: 2 c. sugar; 2 eggs; 1/2 c. lard; 1 c. milk; 3 c. flour; 3 tsp. baking powder; 2 tsp. vanilla; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 4 eggs; 1 c. Kings molasses; 1 c. warm water; some vanilla. Directions: Mix first 7 ingredients; makes cake part. Place into 9-inch glass pie plate with pie crust shell. Then mix ingredients in second column; it will be very runny. Note: 9-inch pie glass; 350° for 35 minutes.

title: Creamy Peanut Dessert
text: Creamy Peanut Dessert. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. graham cracker crumbs; 1/2 c. salted peanuts, chopped; 1/4 c. butter or margarine, melted; 2 Tbsp. peanut butter; 3 to 4 Tbsp. chocolate syrup or grated chocolate; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese, softened; 1/2 c. peanut butter; 1/2 c. sugar; 2 tsp. vanilla extract; 1 (16 oz.) carton Cool Whip. Directions: In a bowl, combine cracker crumbs and peanuts. Stir in 1/4 cup butter and 2 tablespoons peanut butter; mix well. Set aside 1/2 cup for topping. Press the remaining crumb mixture into a greased 9 x 13-inch pan. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Meanwhile, in a mixing bowl, beat cream cheese and 1/2 cup peanut butter until smooth. Beat in sugar and vanilla. Fold in Cool Whip; spoon over crust. Drizzle with chocolate; sprinkle with reserved crumb mixture. Cover and freeze for up to 3 months. Remove from the freezer 15 minutes before serving.

title: Texoma Beans
text: Texoma Beans. Ingredients: 1 (16 oz.) pkg. pinto beans; 1 lb. lean ground beef; 1 (4 oz.) can taco sauce; 1 (4 oz.) can green chilies; 1/2 tsp. cumino; 1/2 tsp. garlic powder; 1 large onion; 1 large can tomatoes; 2 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. oregano. Directions: Cook pinto beans. Saute onions and garlic with the ground beef. Mix all other ingredients into this mixture; add to the cooked beans and cook all together until the flavors are well blended.

title: Vinegar Pie
text: Vinegar Pie. Ingredients: 1 stick margarine, melted and cooled; 2 Tbsp. flour; 1 Tbsp. vinegar; 1 unbaked pie shell; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 1 Tbsp. vanilla; 3 eggs, beaten. Directions: Mix ingredients well. Pour into pie crust and bake at 300° for about 45 minutes.

title: *Kinklings
text: *Kinklings. Ingredients: 1 c. milk; 1/4 c. shortening; 1/4 c. water; 1 yeast cake; 1 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/4 c. sugar; 2 eggs; 3 to 4 c. flour. Directions: Dissolve yeast in water. Scald milk and shortening. Beat eggs and add sugar and salt. Add all ingredients to yeast mixture. Add flour until all has been used. Let rise until double in bulk. Roll on lightly floured surface and cut in 1 1/2 to 2-inch strips, diamond shapes or triangles. Let rise again and fry in heated oil.

title: Greek Bread
text: Greek Bread. Ingredients: 1/2 cup oleo, melted; 1/2 cup mayonnaise (must use); 2 cups mozzarella cheese; 1/4 cup onion, chopped; 1/4 - 1/2 cup black olives, sliced; 1/2 tsp garlic powder; 1 loaf French bread. Directions: Blend mayonnaise and oleo. Add other ingredients. Slice French bread in half-horizontally. Spread with mixture. Bake on a cookie sheet at 350 degrees for 20 minutes. Slice--serve hot.

title: 24 Hour Layered Salad
text: 24 Hour Layered Salad. Ingredients: 1 head lettuce; 1 1/2 c. green pepper, chopped; 1 1/2 c. celery; 1 1/2 c. onions; 1 1/2 pkg. green peas (frozen); 5 hard-boiled eggs, chopped; 2 1/2 c. mayonnaise, mixed with a little sugar and milk. Directions: Layer all the ingredients as stated, the mayonnaise with milk and sugar last. Add 8 ounces Cheddar cheese. Add bacon, that has been cut into bits and fried, on top of the cheese. Refrigerate overnight.

title: Chocolate Covered Pretzels
text: Chocolate Covered Pretzels. Ingredients: small hard pretzels; chocolate candy wafers. Directions: Microwave chocolate wafers in microwave until melted on medium setting. Dip pretzels. Place on wax paper on cookie sheet. Refrigerate until set. Can decorate with colored sugar before chocolate sets.

title: Cranbrosia Salad
text: Cranbrosia Salad. Ingredients: 2 c. fresh cranberries, chopped or ground; 1 c. sugar; 2 (3 oz.) pkg. pineapple Jell-O; 3/4 c. pineapple juice; 1 c. drained mandarin oranges; 1 c. drained pineapple chunks; 1 large banana; 1 c. sour cream; 1 c. coconut; 1 c. Cool Whip. Directions: Combine cranberries and sugar. Mix to dissolve sugar. Heat pineapple juice. Add Jell-O and stir until dissolved. Add to cranberries; stir well. Stir in fruits, sour cream and Cool Whip. Chill until firm.

title: Filete Steak Salteado
text: Filete Steak Salteado. Ingredients: 2 potatoes, peeled and diced; vegetable oil; 1 lb. beef tenderloin; 1/2 c. virgin olive oil; 2 garlic cloves, chopped; 1 Spanish onion, chopped; 1 green bell pepper, chopped; 2 Spanish chorizos; 15 fresh mushrooms, stems removed; salt to taste; pinch of white pepper; 1/2 c. dry red wine. Directions: In deep fryer or Dutch oven, heat vegetable oil to 350°. Fry potatoes in hot oil until brown, about 10 minutes; drain on paper towels. Cut meat into cubes. In large skillet, heat olive oil; brown meat on all sides. Add remaining ingredients except wine. Heat thoroughly; add wine just before serving. Serve over white or yellow rice.

title: Dorcas Beef Casserole
text: Dorcas Beef Casserole. Ingredients: 1 can cream of mushroom soup; 1 can golden mushroom soup; 1 can onion soup; 1 1/2 to 2 lb. stew meat. Directions: Pour the 3 soups, undiluted, into casserole and mix. Cut meat and add to soup mixture. Cover and cook in a 350° oven for 3 to 4 hours. Serve over noodles. Can be made the day prior and kept in the refrigerator.

title: Shrimp And Cheese Strata
text: Shrimp And Cheese Strata. Ingredients: 6 slices firm white bread, crusts removed; 4 Tbsp. melted butter; 1 c. grated Swiss cheese; 2 scallions, chopped; 2 Tbsp. chopped fresh parsley; 1/2 lb. tiny cooked shrimp; 3 eggs; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. Dijon mustard; 1 1/2 c. milk; 1/2 c. sour cream. Directions: Cut each slice of bread in half on the diagonal or cut into cubes. Dip in melted butter. Arrange half the bread slices or cubes in an unbuttered 8-inch baking dish. Sprinkle with half the cheese, onions, parsley and shrimp. Add remaining bread and repeat process. Beat eggs, salt, mustard, milk and sour cream together. Pour over casserole. Cover and chill overnight. Bake at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes or until puffed and golden. Cut into squares and serve.

title: Bean And Vegetable Salad
text: Bean And Vegetable Salad. Ingredients: 16 or 20 oz. pkg. frozen mixed vegetables; 16 oz. canned red beans; 1 c. diced celery; 1 c. diced peppers (red and green); 1 c. diced onions; 1 c. sugar; 1 scant c. vinegar; 4 Tbsp. flour; 2 Tbsp. dry mustard. Directions: Cook frozen vegetables as directed; drain and cool. Add drained red beans. Add diced vegetables and mix. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and mix. Mix sugar, vinegar, flour and mustard in a small saucepan. Cook, stirring until clear. Cool and pour over vegetables.

title: American Lasagna
text: American Lasagna. Ingredients: 1 lb ground beef; 2 cloves garlic, minced; 1 tbsp hot oil; 1 can 6 oz tomato paste; 1 can, 2 1/2 c, tomatoes; 1 tsp salt; 3/4 tsp pepper; 1/2 tsp oregano; hot boiled wide noodle (8 oz uncooked); 8 oz swiss cheese cut up; 1 12 oz carton cottage cheese. Directions: Brown beef and drain; add garlic in hot oil. Add tomato paste, tomatoes, seasonings. Cover and simmer 20 min. heat oven to 350°. In 13x9 baking dish, alternate layers of cooked noodles, swiss cheese, cottage cheese, and meat sauce. Bake 20 to 30 min. Serve with grated parmesan cheese. 6 to 8 servings.

title: Deluxe Lemon Bars
text: Deluxe Lemon Bars. Ingredients: 2 c. flour; 1/2 c. confectioners sugar; 1 c. butter; 4 eggs, beaten; 2 c. sugar; 1/3 c. lemon juice plus a little extra (about 2 lemons); 1/4 c. flour; 1/2 tsp. baking powder. Directions: Cook the first 3 ingredients until the mixture clings together. Press into a 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan. Bake at 350° for 15 to 20 minutes. Beat together the eggs, sugar and lemon juice and add flour and baking powder. Pour over crust. Bake at 350° for 25 minutes. Sprinkle confectioners sugar on top. Cool and cut in bars.

title: Easy Indian Curry
text: Easy Indian Curry. Ingredients: 1 lb. medium shrimp, cleaned and deveined; 3 Tbsp. vegetable oil; 1 large onion, cut; 2 large tomatoes, cut; 1 green chili; juice of 1 lemon; 1 tsp. sugar; 1 Tbsp. paprika; 1 tsp. curry powder; salt to taste; 3 cloves garlic; 1/2 pkg. cream of coconut or 1/2 can coconut milk; 1 c. water; white rice. Directions: Boil shrimp in 2 cups of water with a little salt for about 5 minutes. Drain and keep aside. Using a blender, combine onion, tomatoes, green chili, lemon juice, sugar, paprika, curry powder, salt and garlic into a paste. Heat oil in large pot and saute ground paste and the coconut over low heat, stirring constantly until you get the aroma of all the spices and the oil floats to the top (about 15 minutes). Add 1 cup water and bring to a boil. Lower heat and add shrimp. Simmer gently until liquid is reduced a little. Serve with boiled white rice. Serves 6.

title: Punch Bowl Cake(Put In Punch Bowl)
text: Punch Bowl Cake(Put In Punch Bowl). Ingredients: 1 yellow cake with no pudding in mix; 1 (20 oz.) can crushed pineapple; 1 large can cherry pie filling; 1 c. chopped pecans; 1 large container Cool Whip; 1 large or 2 small instant vanilla pudding, mixed up. Directions: Mix up cake in sheet pan. After cake cools, cut in half. Crumble half of cake in bottom of punch bowl. Divide ingredients in half and layer over cake. Add second half of cake, crumbled. Repeat second half of ingredients. Top with last half of Cool Whip. Sprinkle with nuts or coconut and whole cherries if desired.

title: Strawberry Angel Squares
text: Strawberry Angel Squares. Ingredients: 1/2 angel food cake; 1 c. boiling water; 1 (3 oz.) sugar-free strawberry jello; 1 (15 oz.) crushed pineapple, drained (reserve 1/2 c. juice); 1 c. low-fat cottage cheese; 1 (10 oz.) frozen sweetened strawberries; 1 c. sweetened or sliced strawberries. Directions: Cut half of the angel food cake into 1-inch cubes. Pour boiling water on gelatin in a large bowl; stir until the gelatin is dissolved. Stir in reserved pineapple juice. Place cottage cheese in blender container. Cover and blend on medium speed, stopping blender frequently to scrape sides (about 15 seconds). Return mixture to large bowl. Stir in pineapple, strawberries and cake cubes. Pour into square baking dish (8 x 8 x 2-inches). Refrigerate until firm, at least 6 hours. Garnish with fresh strawberries, if desired.

title: Garden Patch Marinade
text: Garden Patch Marinade. Ingredients: 3/4 c. V-8 vegetable juice; 1/4 c. salad oil; 2 Tbsp. vinegar; 1 (about 0.6 oz.) pkg. Italian flavored salad dressing; 2 c. cauliflowerets; 1 c. sliced carrots; 1 c. diced zucchini; 1/2 c. green pepper squares. Directions: In shallow dish, combine first 4 ingredients. Add vegetables. Toss to coat. Cover. Chill 4 hours or more. Stir often. Makes 4 cups. Yields 8 servings.

title: Peanut Butter Ball Cookies
text: Peanut Butter Ball Cookies. Ingredients: 2 1/3 c. graham cracker crumbs; 1 1/2 c. crushed pecans; 1 c. peanut butter; 1/2 c. coconut (optional); 1 box confectioners sugar; 1 can sweetened condensed milk; 2 sticks melted butter; 1/2 stick paraffin; 1 bag chocolate chips. Directions: Combine all ingredients except the last 2 and form into small balls. Melt chocolate with paraffin in double boiler; dip each ball into chocolate. Remove and place on wax paper.

title: Tapioca Pudding
text: Tapioca Pudding. Ingredients: 1/2 c. mini-pearl tapioca; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1 c. sugar; marshmallows; 5 c. water; 6 oz. jello; whipped cream; fruit. Directions: Boil tapioca, water and salt for 1/2 hour. Two hours later, add jello and sugar. When cool, top with whipped cream and fruit. Mix in marshmallows.

title: Chocolate Almond Cream Pie
text: Chocolate Almond Cream Pie. Ingredients: 1 graham cracker pie crust; 6 1.15 oz. chocolate almond candy bars; 1 c. heavy cream; 1/4 tsp. almond extract. Directions: Melt candy bars and set aside. Whip cream with almond extract. Stir chocolate into whipped cream until blended. Pour mixture into pie crust. Refrigerate 4 hours or more.

title: Fannie Farmer Chocolates("A Favorite From The '76 Cookbook.")
text: Fannie Farmer Chocolates("A Favorite From The '76 Cookbook."). Ingredients: 2 lb. confectioners sugar; 1/2 lb. butter; 1 can condensed milk; 2 (16 oz.) pkg. chocolate chips; 1/4 of 1 slab wax (paraffin as in canning. Directions: Mix sugar, butter and condensed milk. Roll into little balls. Put on a cookie sheet and put a toothpick in middle of each one and freeze overnight.

title: Jello Kisses(Easy)
text: Jello Kisses(Easy). Ingredients: 4 egg whites; 1 c. sugar; 1 c. coconut; 1 pkg. Jell-O (preferably cherry, raspberry or strawberry). Directions: Beat egg whites until stiff. Fold in Jell-O and sugar; continue beating until stiff and glossy. Add 1 cup coconut. Drop by teaspoon on cookie sheet and bake at 275° for 50 minutes. Yields 4 dozen.

title: Dad'S Barbecue Mop
text: Dad'S Barbecue Mop. Ingredients: 1/2 c. mustard; 2 Tbsp. white vinegar; 5 to 10 dashes Worcestershire sauce. Directions: Mix together ingredients. Mop on meat while it is in the pit. Will serve 4 large sirloins.

title: White Conch Chowder
text: White Conch Chowder. Ingredients: 2 large conch, mantle included, pounded; 2 slices bacon, chopped; 2 onions chopped; 2 garlic cloves chopped; 2 potatoes, chopped; 2 carrots, sliced; 3 c. water; 3 chicken bouillon cubes; pinch cayenne pepper; pinch rosemary; 2 Tbsp. parsley; 1 can (6 oz.) evaporated milk; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Pound and dice conch. Fry bacon, saute onions, add conch and all other ingredients except the milk. Simmer for one hour, add milk and heat.

title: Liver Sausage Appetizer Ball
text: Liver Sausage Appetizer Ball. Ingredients: 1 lb. liver sausage (liverwurst); 1/4 c. real mayonnaise; 2 Tbsp. dill pickle juice; 1 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 3 drops Tabasco sauce; 1/4 tsp. garlic salt; 1/3 c. chopped dill pickle; 1/4 c. finely chopped onion; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese. Directions: Mash liverwurst to make smooth. Blend in all of rest of ingredients, except add only 1/3 of the cream cheese. Pack into a 2-cup mixing bowl lined with foil or plastic wrap. Chill several hours until firm. Turn out and frost with remaining cheese. Chill well. Serve with crackers.

title: Mom'S Christmas Pecan Cake
text: Mom'S Christmas Pecan Cake. Ingredients: 1 lb. butter; 1 lb. brown sugar; 6 eggs; 4 c. all-purpose flour; 1 lb. pecans, chopped; 4 oz. green candied cherries, chopped; 4 oz. red candied cherries, chopped; 8 oz. candied pineapple, chopped; 1 oz. bottle lemon extract. Directions: Cream butter and brown sugar. Add eggs. Beat well. Add flour, pecans, cherries and pineapple. Mix well. Add lemon extract and blend well.

title: Herb Marinated Chicken
text: Herb Marinated Chicken. Ingredients: 1/2 c. vegetable oil; 1/3 c. cider vinegar; 1/4 c. water; 1/4 c. finely chopped onion; 1 1/2 tsp. salt (optional); 1/2 tsp. garlic powder or 1 small clove, finely chopped; 1/4 tsp. pepper. Directions: Prick chicken parts on all sides with a fork. Marinate chicken, turning occasionally for at least 2 hours. Bake at 350° for 50 minutes, uncovered.

title: Laura'S Biscuits(Hand Rolled)
text: Laura'S Biscuits(Hand Rolled). Ingredients: White Lily self-rising flour; 1/2 c. Crisco; 1 1/2 c. buttermilk; soft liquid Parkay margarine. Directions: In a large bowl or wooden tray, spread out about 4 cups flour and make a hole in center patting it down so flour will not be loose. Pour milk into the hole, then add Crisco. Start with hands working flour into the milk and Crisco gradually. Do not make the dough too stiff. If you do, biscuits will be tough and hard. When you get dough medium thick, then knead 6 to 12 times adding more flour if needed.

title: "Uptown" Beer Meat Balls
text: "Uptown" Beer Meat Balls. Ingredients: 2 lb. ground beef (lean); 1 medium onion, chopped fine; celery and green peppers to taste, chopped fine; 1/2 c. finely rolled bread crumbs; 1 egg, thoroughly beaten; 1 c. ketchup (spicy); 1 c. beer; 4 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 4 Tbsp. wine vinegar; 3 Tbsp. sugar. Directions: Combine first five ingredients and mix well. Make small size (1-inch diameter) meat balls and fry in small amount of oil (if necessary) evenly on all sides. Drain on paper towel.

title: Chocolate Caramel Brownies
text: Chocolate Caramel Brownies. Ingredients: 1 box (18.25 oz.) German or other chocolate cake mix; 3/4 c. (1-1/2 sticks) butter, melted; 2/3 c. evaporated milk, divided; 1 c. chopped pecans; 2 packages (7 oz. each) chocolate covered caramels (If you prefer, you can use plain caramels); 1 c. (6 oz.) semisweet chocolate chips. Directions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Coat a 9 x 13 baking dish with nonstick cooking spray.

title: Ginger Snaps
text: Ginger Snaps. Ingredients: 3/4 cup shortening; 1 cup sugar; 1 tsp. ginger; 1 tsp. cloves; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 4 Tbsp. molasses; 1 egg; 2 cups flour; 2 tsp. soda. Directions: Mix in order, roll into balls the size of a walnut. Roll in white sugar and bake at 350°. Cookie will flatten and crack.

title: Miniature Brownies
text: Miniature Brownies. Ingredients: 1 c. butter or margarine; 12 oz. chocolate chips; 1 c. granulated sugar; 2 tsp. vanilla; 4 large eggs; 1 c. flour; 1 c. finely chopped walnuts. Directions: Line 72 mini muffin tins with baking cups or arrange baking cups on cookie sheets. Melt butter in a 3-quart saucepan over medium heat. Add chocolate chips; stir until melted. Remove from heat. Stir in sugar and vanilla. Add eggs; stir briskly. Gradually stir in flour, then nuts. Fill baking cups 3/4 full. Bake for 22 to 24 minutes at 350°.

title: Filled Hungarian Cookies
text: Filled Hungarian Cookies. Ingredients: 2 sticks oleo, softened; 6 oz. cream cheese, softened; 2 c. flour; 1 can filling or preserves. Directions: Cream all ingredients oleo, cheese and flour together until mixed thoroughly. Roll on floured board of mixed flour and powdered sugar. Cut into 2-inch squares or 2-inch circles with cookie cutter. Place 1/2 teaspoon filling on each and fold 2 sides towards center. Press hard enough to hold. Bake at 350° for 10 to 15 minutes.

title: Crock-Pot Swedish Cabbage Rolls
text: Crock-Pot Swedish Cabbage Rolls. Ingredients: 12 large cabbage leaves; 1 beaten egg; 1/4 c. milk; 1/4 c. finely chopped onion; pinch of salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 1 lb. ground turkey or hamburger; 1 c. cooked rice; 1 (8 oz.) can tomato sauce; 1 Tbsp. brown sugar; 1 Tbsp. lemon juice; 1 tsp. Worcestershire sauce. Directions: Place cabbage leaves in microwavable dish with 2 to 4 tablespoons water and microwave for about 3 minutes or until cabbage is limp. Combine egg, milk, onion, salt, pepper, meat and cooked rice. Place about 1/4 cup meat mixture in center of each leaf; fold in sides and roll ends over meat. Place in crock-pot. Combine tomato sauce with brown sugar, lemon juice and Worcestershire sauce. Pour over cabbage rolls. Cover and cook on low 7 to 9 hours. Serves 6.

title: Quick Mince Pie
text: Quick Mince Pie. Ingredients: 1/2 c. hamburg; 1 1/2 c. chopped apple; 1/2 c. seedless raisins; 2 Tbsp. butter; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1/4 tsp. ground cloves; 1 Tbsp. molasses; 2/3 c. sugar; 2 Tbsp. water; 2 Tbsp. vinegar; 1/2 tsp. salt. Directions: Mix all together and place in pastry lined pie plate. Cover with top crust. Bake 15 minutes at 425°. Turn heat down to 350° and cook another 45 minutes.

title: Vegetable Stir-Fry With Light Sauce
text: Vegetable Stir-Fry With Light Sauce. Ingredients: 1 or 2 fresh zucchini, chopped; 1 medium onion, chopped; margarine; 1 c. whole kernel corn; 1/4 c. fresh tomato; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Melt margarine in skillet over medium to low heat. Add all vegetables, but tomatoes. Add salt and pepper. Stir in tomato; cover and steam for 2 minutes. Remove from heat.

title: Two-Corn Casserole
text: Two-Corn Casserole. Ingredients: 1 stick oleo; 3/4 c. chopped green pepper; 1/3 c. chopped onion; 1 (17-oz.) can cream-style corn; 1 (16-oz.) can whole kernel corn; 3 eggs, beaten; 1 (8-oz.) pkg. corn muffin mix; 1 c. shredded cheddar cheese. Directions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Grease a 2-quart casserole dish. Saute pepper and onion in butter until soft. Mix all ingredients together and pour into casserole dish. Bake until set.

title: Mashed Potato Casserole
text: Mashed Potato Casserole. Ingredients: 8 c. peeled, quartered potatoes (2 1/2 lb.); 1/2 c. Miracle Whip salad dressing; 1 pkg. Philadelphia brand cream cheese, softened (8 oz.); 1 tsp. onion powder; 3/4 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper; Paprika. Directions: Add potatoes and enough water to cover in 4 quart saucepan. Bring to boil. Reduce heat to medium; cook 25 to 30 minutes or until tender. Drain well. Mash potatoes, gradually stirring in salad dressing, cream cheese, onion powder, salt and pepper until light and fluffy. Spoon into 1 1/2 quart casserole. Sprinkle with paprika. Cover tightly and freeze. To serve, thaw casserole. Heat oven to 350°. Bake 1-1 1/4 hours or until thoroughly heated. Serves 10 to 12. (Bake at 350° for 45 minutes if you don't freeze casserole.)

title: Applesauce
text: Applesauce. Ingredients: Apples (McIntosh, Dr. Mathews or Jonathans); 1 c. water; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 2 heaping tsp. cinnamon. Directions: Peel apples, then slice. Put apples and water in pan and cook about 15 minutes or until tender. Use a potato masher to mash apples. Add sugar--about 1 1/2 c., to your own taste. Add cinnamon; heat for 5 minutes.

title: Chocolate Chipper Champs
text: Chocolate Chipper Champs. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. packed brown sugar; 3/4 c. margarine or butter, softened; 1 tsp. vanilla; 2 eggs; 2 1/4 c. flour; 1 c. M&M's plain chocolate candies; 1/2 c. chopped nuts; 1 tsp. baking soda; 1 tsp. salt. Directions: Heat oven to 350°. Mix brown sugar, margarine, vanilla and eggs in a large bowl until well blended. Stir in remaining ingredients. Drop dough by rounded tablespoonfuls about 3-inches apart onto lightly greased cookie sheet. Press 3 or 4 additional candies in each cookie, if desired. Bake until light brown, 10 to 12 minutes. Cool slightly. Remove to wire rack. Makes about 2 1/2 dozen cookies.

title: Microwave Party Mix
text: Microwave Party Mix. Ingredients: 6 Tbsp. butter; 1 tsp. seasoned salt; 2 c. Wheat Chex; 2 c. thin stick pretzels; 4 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 2 c. Corn Chex; 2 c. Rice Chex; 1 1/2 c. mixed nuts. Directions: In 9 x 13-inch dish, place butter, Worcestershire sauce and salt. Microwave at High for 1 minute or until butter is melted. Stir well. Add cereal, pretzels and nuts, mixing thoroughly to coat. Microwave at High for 5 to 6 minutes, stirring until evenly toasted. Makes about 2 1/2 quarts.

title: Corned Beef In Rolls
text: Corned Beef In Rolls. Ingredients: 1 can corned beef, chopped; 6 hard-boiled eggs, chopped; 1/2 c. pickle relish; 2 Tbsp. prepared mustard; 2 Tbsp. mayonnaise. Directions: Mix ingredients together and toss. Fill 8 hot dog rolls with mixture. Wrap each in foil and heat on cookie sheet for 20 minutes at 250°. These are simple to make and may be wrapped in foil and frozen for later use.

title: Stuffed Squash
text: Stuffed Squash. Ingredients: 12 medium squash; 1 lb. ground beef; 1 c. long grain rice (uncooked); 1 medium onion; 2 cans tomato paste; 1 tsp. salt or to taste; 1 tsp. pepper or to taste. Directions: Cut necks off squash and core inside out and save. Mix all ingredients except 1 can tomato paste and squash hulls. Stuff squash loosely; put neck in top and secure with toothpick. Cover with water and add 1 can tomato paste. Bring to a boil and simmer 1 hour.

title: Soft Butter Cookies
text: Soft Butter Cookies. Ingredients: 2 c. butter or margarine; 3 c. xxxx sugar; 2 eggs; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 1/4 qt. (5 c.) all-purpose flour; 2 tsp. soda; 2 tsp. cream of tartar; 1/2 tsp. salt. Directions: Cream butter and sugar until fluffy. Add unbeaten eggs and vanilla. Beat well. Sift together dry ingredients. Blend into creamed mixture. Chill. Roll on floured board and cut. Bake at 400° for 6 minutes.

title: Peanut Butter Swirl Brownies
text: Peanut Butter Swirl Brownies. Ingredients: 1/2 c. margarine; 1 c. sugar; 2 eggs; 1 c. flour; 2 oz. unsweetened chocolate, melted and cooled; 1/3 c. chunky peanut butter; 1/4 c. miniature semi-sweet chocolate pieces. Directions: Heat margarine until melted and remove from heat. Stir in sugar and eggs. Beat slightly by hand just until combined; stir in flour. Remove 3/4 c. of batter and set aside. Stir melted chocolate into remaining batter. Spread chocolate batter into a greased 9 x 9 x 2 baking pan; set aside. Stir peanut butter into reserved batter. Dollop on top of chocolate batter, then sprinkle with chocolate pieces. Swirl batter with a knife. Bake at 350 degrees for 20 to 25 minutes. Do not overbake.

title: Italian Slaw
text: Italian Slaw. Ingredients: 1 cabbage head; 2 green peppers; 2 onions; 1 c. sugar; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. oil; 1 c. vinegar (white). Directions: Chop cabbage and leave in bowl. Dice green peppers and onions. Add to chopped cabbage. Stir in sugar, salt, oil and vinegar. Let stand 30 minutes. Refrigerate for crispness. May keep in closed glass container up to 2 weeks in refrigerator.

title: Mini-Cheesecakes
text: Mini-Cheesecakes. Ingredients: Nilla wafers; 2 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese, softened; 1/2 c. sugar; 1 tsp. vanilla; 2 eggs; 1 can cherry pie filling. Directions: Place 1 wafer in each foil-lined muffin pan. Combine cream cheese, sugar and vanilla. Beat well and add eggs. Mix well. Fill muffin cups 3/4 full. Bake at 325° for 25 minutes. Let cool. Chill. Top with pie filling. Makes 1 dozen.

title: Moravian Christmas Cookies
text: Moravian Christmas Cookies. Ingredients: 3/4 c. butter and shortening, mixed; 3/4 c. brown sugar; 1 pt. black molasses; 7 1/2 c. sifted flour; 4 Tbsp. ground cloves; 4 Tbsp. ground cinnamon; 4 Tbsp. ground ginger; 1 tsp. salt; 1 Tbsp. soda; 1/4 c. boiling water. Directions: Cream butter and shortening with sugar. Add molasses. Add soda to boiling water and add to creamed mixture. Sift flour with spices and salt. Add small amount at a time to creamed mixture and work in well. Work well with hands and knead dough well. Cover and store in a cold place overnight and longer if you don't want to bake all at one time. Roll to infinite thinness on board. (Board should be covered with cloth stretched tight and tacked on edges of board.) Flour board (cloth) evenly before rolling out cookies. Bake on a greased cookie sheet in 375° oven for a few minutes or just until they begin to brown.

title: Vegetable Quiche
text: Vegetable Quiche. Ingredients: 1 c. sliced zucchini; 1 c. chopped onions; 2 garlic cloves; 1 sliced tomato; 1 c. cottage cheese; 2 eggs; 1 c. milk; dash of Tabasco sauce; Parmesan cheese. Directions: Saute onions, zucchini and garlic in margarine or oil. Prebake 1 pie crust until slightly cooked. Mix together cheese, eggs, milk and dash of Tabasco sauce. Add sauteed vegetables to egg mixture. Pour into crust. Bake at about 350° until set, about 45 to 60 minutes. Sprinkle Parmesan cheese on top after it is baked.

title: Hashburgers
text: Hashburgers. Ingredients: 1 can corned beef; 1 c. chopped olives (green); 1 c. chopped American cheese; 1 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/2 c. ketchup. Directions: Garnish liberally with love and keep cool until it sparkles. If you have followed God's cookbook in preparing your marriage, your home is ready now!

title: Homemade Orange Sherbet
text: Homemade Orange Sherbet. Ingredients: 1 can Eagle Brand milk; 6 bottles or cans Orange Crush. Directions: Mix well and pour into can of ice cream freezer. Freeze until freezer stops or, if you use hand-turned freezer, until firm.

title: Broccoli-N-Cheese Casserole
text: Broccoli-N-Cheese Casserole. Ingredients: 1 (16 oz.) Cheez Whiz; 1 c. white rice, cooked; 1 medium onion, chopped; 1 can cream of mushroom soup; 1 can cream of celery soup; 1 pkg. frozen broccoli. Directions: Saute onion in skillet with butter. Cook rice and broccoli. Mix all ingredients and bake, uncovered, at 350° for 45 minutes to 1 hour. Let sit for 10 minutes before baking.

title: Tomatoes With Mozzarella
text: Tomatoes With Mozzarella. Ingredients: tomatoes; Mozzarella; basil; salt and pepper; extra virgin olive oil. Directions: Layer slices of tomatoes and Mozzarella. Sprinkle with chopped fresh basil, salt and freshly ground pepper. Drizzle with extra virgin olive oil.

title: Salmon Party Spread
text: Salmon Party Spread. Ingredients: 1 (3 oz.) pkg. Jell-O lemon flavored gelatin; 1/4 tsp. salt; 3/4 c. boiling water; 1 c. sour cream; 1/2 c. mayonnaise; 2 Tbsp. lemon juice; 2 Tbsp. grated onion; 2 Tbsp. horseradish; 2 c. red salmon, drained and flaked (15 to 16 oz. can); 2 Tbsp. chopped dill weed. Directions: Dissolve gelatin and salt in boiling water. Blend in sour cream, mayonnaise, lemon juice, onion and horseradish; chill until thickened. Fold in salmon and dill weed. Spoon into mold. Chill until firm at least 3 hours. Unmold. Serve as an appetizer surrounded with crackers or raw vegetables.

title: Lettie'S Peanut Butter Candy
text: Lettie'S Peanut Butter Candy. Ingredients: 3 c. sugar; 1 Tbsp. vinegar; 1 1/2 c. milk (1 c. evaporated, 1/2 water); 1/2 stick margarine; dash of salt; 1 jar (8 oz.) creamy peanut butter. Directions: Combine first five ingredients and cook until it forms a soft ball. Add peanut butter. Grease cookie sheet. Pour. Cut in squares.

title: Hot Cranberry Apple Orange Compote
text: Hot Cranberry Apple Orange Compote. Ingredients: 2 c. sugar; 1 1/2 c. water; 1 lb. whole raw cranberries; 4 apples, peeled and sliced (a tart, cooking apple); 2 oranges, peeled and sliced; 1/2 c. nuts (optional). Directions: Bring water and sugar to a boil. Add fruit and boil for 5 minutes or until berries pop. Add nuts. Serve for breakfast, hot or serve cold. Serves 8 to 10.

title: Squash Bread
text: Squash Bread. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. unsifted flour; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1 tsp. baking powder; 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 1/4 tsp. salt; 2 eggs; 3/4 c. sugar; 1/2 c. oil; 2 tsp. vanilla; 1 1/3 c. summer squash, coarsely shredded, lightly packed. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Grease a 9 x 5 x 3-inch loaf pan. Mix dry ingredients, except sugar, thoroughly. Beat eggs until frothy; add sugar, oil and vanilla. Beat until lemon colored, about 3 minutes. Stir in squash. Add dry ingredients; mix just until dry ingredients are moistened. Pour in loaf pan. Bake for 40 minutes or until toothpick inserted in center of loaf comes out clean. Cool on rack. Remove from pan after 10 minutes. Makes 1 loaf or 16 slices.

title: Broiled Cake
text: Broiled Cake. Ingredients: 4 eggs; 2 c. sugar; 2 c. flour; 2 tsp. baking powder; 2 Tbsp. melted butter; 1 c. warm butter; 1/2 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Beat eggs until fluffy. Add sugar and dry ingredients. Melt butter and pour into warm milk. (Do not boil.) Add vanilla and mix with other ingredients. Pour into 13 x 9-inch baking pan. Bake in a 350° oven for about 30 minutes.

title: Italian Stuffed Zucchini
text: Italian Stuffed Zucchini. Ingredients: 4 large (10 oz.) zucchini; 2 Tbsp. olive oil; 1 c. minced onion; 3 garlic cloves, minced; 1/4 c. dried bread crumbs; 1/4 c. raisins, chopped; 1/4 c. parsley; 1 1/2 oz. Parmesan cheese; 1 oz. pine nuts, chopped; 1 oz. anchovy paste; pepper. Directions: Preheat oven to 375°. Line a baking sheet with foil. Trim end stem off each zucchini. Cut a thin slice along bottom of each zucchini so it sets straight. Trim top of length 1/2-inch, then hollow out each, leaving a 1/2-inch thick shell. Do not pierce. Dice the slices and pulp; add onion. Cook 10 minutes. Add garlic. Stuff each shell. Place on baking sheet. Cover with foil and bake for 20 minutes. Remove foil and bake for 25 to 30 minutes more.

title: Ruby'S Egg Pie
text: Ruby'S Egg Pie. Ingredients: 1 c. sugar; 2/3 c. flour; 3 c. milk; 4 egg yolks (save whites for meringue); 1 tsp. vanilla; 1/4 stick butter; dash of salt. Directions: Mix flour and sugar in iron skillet. Add milk. Mix well. Stir in beaten egg yolks, vanilla and salt. When hot, put in butter. Cook until thick. Stir constantly so it won't get lumpy or stick. Pour into baked pie shells. Top with beaten egg whites. Brown in oven. Makes 2 pies.

title: Chocolate Ice Cream (Frosty)
text: Chocolate Ice Cream (Frosty). Ingredients: 1 can Eagle Brand milk; 1 (12 oz.) Cool Whip; 1/2 gal. chocolate milk. Directions: Mix Eagle Brand milk and Cool Whip, then chocolate milk. Put into electric freezer. Makes 1 gallon.

title: Turkey Barbecue
text: Turkey Barbecue. Ingredients: 4 c. chopped, cooked turkey (small pieces); medium chopped onion; 1 c. chopped celery; 2 Tbsp. brown sugar; 2 Tbsp. butter; 2 Tbsp. vinegar; 3 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/2 c. water; 1/2 bottle catsup. Directions: Mix all together. Add salt and pepper to taste. Simmer 10 minutes.

title: Almond Roca
text: Almond Roca. Ingredients: 1 c. sugar; 1 c. butter; 6 small Hershey bars; 1 c. chopped nuts. Directions: Combine sugar and butter in heavy pot. Bring to a boil and cook for 9 minutes or hard crack stage, stirring constantly. Pour into 2 well buttered 8-inch pie tins.

title: Hacienda Hamburger
text: Hacienda Hamburger. Ingredients: 2 lb. ground beef; 1 1/2 c. chopped celery; 6 oz. (1 can) tomato paste; 2 c. uncooked noodles; 1 c. sliced ripe olives; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 2 Tbsp. cooking oil; 1/2 c. chopped onion; 2 1/4 c. (3 cans) water; 1 c. diced American cheese; 2 tsp. salt. Directions: Brown meat in oil in electric skillet or large frying pan. Add celery and onion; cover and cook slowly 10 minutes. Add tomato paste, water, noodles, cheese, olives, salt and pepper; stir to combine. Cook slowly until noodles are tender, about 30 minutes.

title: Stew Beef With Gravy
text: Stew Beef With Gravy. Ingredients: 2 lb. stew beef; 2 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1 tsp. Texas Pete; 2 Tbsp. oil; flour. Directions: Roll stew beef in flour. Heat oil and brown stew beef. When well browned, place in saucepan and add Worcestershire sauce and Texas Pete. Cover with water. Cover and simmer until tender. Delicious served with mashed potatoes or rice.

title: Chinese Chicken Bites
text: Chinese Chicken Bites. Ingredients: 2 c. water; 1/2 c. soy sauce; 6 Tbsp. vinegar; 4 garlic gloves; 2 bay leaves. Directions: Combine ingredients in large electric skillet. Heat until just a boil. Add 5 pounds thawed chicken drummettes (mini drumsticks). Cook approximately 20 minutes on medium heat.

title: French-Style Green Beans
text: French-Style Green Beans. Ingredients: 2 cans French-style green beans; 1 white onion, chopped; 6 slices bacon; A.1. sauce. Directions: Fry and drain bacon. Mix the chopped onions, bacon and A.1. sauce into the green beans. Heat 10 minutes.

title: Sherbet Punch
text: Sherbet Punch. Ingredients: 2 pkg. strawberry Kool-Aid; 2 c. sugar; 1 can pineapple juice; Sprite or 7-Up. Directions: Mix Kool-Aid, sugar and pineapple juice. Add enough water to make 1 gallon of punch. Pour over sherbet (pineapple or orange sherbet). Add Sprite or 7-Up to punch.

title: Creamless Mushroom Soup
text: Creamless Mushroom Soup. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. butter; 1 c. carrots; 1 c. onions; 1 c. leeks; 1/2 c. celery; 1 tsp. thyme; 2 lb. mushrooms; 2 c. chicken stock; 4 tsp. salt or salt to taste; pepper to taste (optional). Directions: Saute vegetables in butter. Saute mushrooms until just tender. Add stock and spices. Pour in food processor or blender and quickly chop the vegetables until it looks creamy. Reheat and serve.

title: Grilled Honey Chicken Breasts
text: Grilled Honey Chicken Breasts. Ingredients: 6 to 8 chicken breasts (with bone in); 1/4 c. honey; 1/4 c. vegetable oil; 1/4 c. mayonnaise; 2 Tbsp. prepared mustard; 1 Tbsp. white vinegar; 1 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/2 tsp. onion powder. Directions: Place chicken breast halves on a preheated barbecue grill for 20 to 30 minutes or until cooked through. Meanwhile, in a medium-sized bowl, combine the remaining ingredients, then brush over cooked chicken and grill for 5 minutes more.

title: Mocha Bundt Cake
text: Mocha Bundt Cake. Ingredients: 1 pkg. white cake mix; 1 (3 3/4 oz.) pkg. chocolate pudding mix; 4 eggs; 1 c. oil; 2/3 c. vodka; 1/3 c. coffee liqueur; 1/4 c. water; 1/4 c. coffee liqueur; 1/4 c. powdered sugar. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Grease and flour Bundt cake pan, shaking out excess. Combine first 7 ingredients in large bowl and beat thoroughly. Pour into pan. Bake until tester inserted in center comes out clean, about 50 to 60 minutes. Let cool 5 minutes in pan, then invert onto rack.

title: Old Time Molasses Dried Apple Stack Cake
text: Old Time Molasses Dried Apple Stack Cake. Ingredients: 1 c. sugar; 1 c. shortening (maybe 3/4c.); 1 c. sorghum molasses; 1/4 c. buttermilk; 1 egg; about 5 c. flour; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 2 tsp. soda in 1 Tbsp. vinegar; 1/2 tsp. ginger. Directions: Cream sugar, shortening and molasses. Add egg and buttermilk. Mix well. Stir in dry ingredients together with vinegar and soda mixture. Divide dough into 6 balls. Press each ball into well-greased 8-inch cake pan. Bake at 350° for 15 minutes or until slightly browned. Remove from pans while warm and set aside to cool.

title: Clay Play Dough(Uncooked)
text: Clay Play Dough(Uncooked). Ingredients: 1 c. flour; 1/2 c. salt; 1/3 to 1/2 c. water; few drops cooking oil; food coloring. Directions: Mix dry ingredients together in a bowl. Slowly add water and oil and knead to a pliable dough. Add a little salt and flour if sticky. Add drops of food coloring and knead until the desired color is achieved. Keep in an airtight container. Do not refrigerate.

title: Chocolate Milk Shake
text: Chocolate Milk Shake. Ingredients: 3/4 c. chocolate sauce; 1/2 to 1 c. milk; 4 to 5 scoops ice cream; whipped cream; chocolate sprinkles; small piece of chocolate. Directions: Place the chocolate sauce, milk and ice cream in a blender. Blend until it turns a chocolate milk color. Pour in a cup. Top with whipped cream and sprinkles. Place the piece of chocolate on top of the whipped cream.

title: Diabetic Persimmon Pudding
text: Diabetic Persimmon Pudding. Ingredients: 1 c. plus 2 Tbsp. persimmon pulp; 1 egg; 1 tsp. baking powder; 1 c. flour; 1/2 c. cinnamon; 1 1/2 c. milk; 2 Tbsp. butter; 1/2 tsp. soda; 2 Tbsp. sweetener and 1 tsp.. Directions: Mix pulp, sweetener and egg. Add dry ingredients and a little of the milk. Blend well. Add rest of milk and butter. Bake 55 minutes at 325°.

title: Get Together Punch
text: Get Together Punch. Ingredients: 2 cans (46 oz.) juice-punch, chilled; 2/3 c. pineapple juice; 1 can frozen lemonade concentrate, thawed; 12 oz. club soda, chilled. Directions: Day before: Pour 1 can punch into 1 1/2-quart ring mold, freeze. Mix remaining punch, pineapple juice, lemonade and club soda in punch bowl. Just before serving, unmold frozen punch ring carefully. Place in bowl, garnish as desired. Makes approximately 2 quarts, 16 (4-ounce) servings.

title: Pasta Fruit Salad
text: Pasta Fruit Salad. Ingredients: 3 c. medium pasta shells (uncooked); 1 (20 oz.) can unsweetened pineapple chunks, drained; 1 large navel orange, peeled, sectioned and halved; 1 c. red grapes, halved; 1 c. green grapes, halved; 1 medium apple, chopped; 1 large firm banana, cut into 1/4-inch slices; 1 (8 oz.) carton plain yogurt; 1/4 c. orange juice concentrate. Directions: Cook pasta; drain and rinse well in cold water. Place in a large bowl. Add the fruit. Combine yogurt and orange juice concentrate. Pour over salad and toss to coat. Cover and chill for several hours. Yield: 10 servings.

title: Cheesy Mashed Potatoes
text: Cheesy Mashed Potatoes. Ingredients: 9 medium potatoes; 2 c. Cheddar cheese; 1/4 c. butter; 1 (8 oz.) sour cream; 1 Tbsp. chopped green onion; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper; paprika. Directions: Add water to unpeeled potatoes just to cover. Sprinkle with salt. Bring to boiling for 30 to 35 minutes. Drain. Peel and shred potatoes with a grater. In a large mixing bowl combine potatoes, cheese and butter; stir until butter is melted. Stir in sour cream, green onion, salt and the pepper. Transfer to a greased pan. Bake, uncovered, in a 325° oven for 45 minutes. Sprinkle with paprika. Makes 8 servings.

title: Egg Custard Pie(9-Inch Pie)
text: Egg Custard Pie(9-Inch Pie). Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. sugar; 3/4 stick margarine; 1 Tbsp. plus 1 tsp. flour; 4 large eggs; 1 1/2 c. milk or cream; few drops egg color; 1 Tbsp. vanilla; deep dish crust (purchase or make your own recipe). Directions: Cream sugar, margarine, flour and vanilla. Beat eggs well and add to creamed mixture. Mix well. Add milk and egg color. Mix well. Pour into crust and bake at 350° for 15 minutes. Lower temperature to 325° and bake approximately 30 minutes or until done.

title: Fabulous Beef Fajitas
text: Fabulous Beef Fajitas. Ingredients: 1/2 c. prepared Italian salad dressing, divided; 1/2 tsp. chili powder; 1 lb. boneless sirloin steak, cut into 1/4 inch strips; 1 medium sweet pepper, sliced; 1 medium green pepper, sliced; 1 medium onion, sliced and separated into rings; flour tortilla. Directions: In a large resealable plastic bag, combine 1/4 cup salad dressing and chili powder; add steak. Seal and turn to coat; refrigerate for 30 minutes. In a skillet, saute peppers and onion in remaining salad dressing until crisp and tender. Remove and keep warm. Drain steak, discarding marinade in skillet, saute steak for 6-10 minutes or until no longer pink. Return vegetables to pan. Heat through and spoon meat and vegetables down center of tortillas; fold in sides. Yield: 4-6 servings.

title: Carlie'S Hot Fudge Pudding Cake
text: Carlie'S Hot Fudge Pudding Cake. Ingredients: 1 c. self-rising flour; 3/4 c. granulated sugar; 2 Tbsp. cocoa; 1/2 c. milk; 2 Tbsp. melted shortening; 1 c. chopped pecans; 3/4 c. brown sugar; 1/4 c. cocoa; 1 1/2 c. hot water. Directions: Heat oven to 350°. Measure flour, granulated sugar and 2 tablespoons cocoa into bowl. Blend in milk and shortening; stir in nuts. Pour into ungreased square 9 x 9 x 2-inch pan. Stir together brown sugar and 1/4 cup cocoa; sprinkle over batter. Pour "hot" water over batter. Bake for 45 minutes.

title: Caramel Sticky Buns
text: Caramel Sticky Buns. Ingredients: 1 loaf frozen bread dough; 1 pkg. vanilla pudding (cook type); 1/2 c. brown sugar; 1/2 stick margarine; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1 Tbsp. milk; walnuts/pecans (optional). Directions: Thaw bread dough. When thawed, break half the loaf into small pieces and place in well-greased pie plate. Melt margarine in saucepan, adding dry pudding, brown sugar, cinnamon and milk. Bring just to a boil. Pour over broken pieces. Add walnuts or pecans, if desired. Break up remaining bread dough and place on top. Let rise to top of pan and bake at 350° for 30 minutes or until brown. Invert on plate and serve warm.

title: Creamy Frozen Fruit Salad
text: Creamy Frozen Fruit Salad. Ingredients: 2 c. sour cream; 2 Tbsp. lemon juice; 3/4 c. sugar; 1/8 tsp. salt; 9 oz. crushed pineapple, drained; 1/4 c. sliced maraschino cherries; 1/4 c. nuts, chopped; 1 large banana, sliced. Directions: Mix all ingredients together and freeze.

title: Oranges In Liqueur
text: Oranges In Liqueur. Ingredients: 7 oranges; 4 Tbsp. butter; 1/3 c. sugar; 1/3 c. orange liqueur; 1 c. walnuts; juice of 1 orange. Directions: Boil the zest from one orange (zest is the peel without the bitter white portion) for 5 minutes. Drain and dry. Melt butter. Add sugar and liqueur. When sugar is dissolved, add the orange zest and the juice. Simmer until medium thickness. Peel and slice remaining oranges 1/3-inch thick. Place overlapped oranges on a serving platter. Sprinkle walnuts over top. Pour sauce over the orange slices and serve.

title: Chocolate Devil'S Food Cake
text: Chocolate Devil'S Food Cake. Ingredients: 1 c. Crisco or butter; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 2 eggs; 1 c. buttermilk; 1 c. hot coffee; 3 c. flour, sifted; 1 Tbsp. cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. cloves; 1 tsp. soda; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. chocolate; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1/4 tsp. red cake coloring. Directions: Cream Crisco and sugar; add eggs. Sift and add flour and spices together with salt. Dissolve chocolate in hot coffee and soda in buttermilk. Stir until it foams. Add to other ingredients with vanilla and cake coloring. Bake 45 to 50 minutes at 350°. Ice with your favorite icing such as chocolate, cream cheese or 7 minute icing.

title: Roast Pheasant
text: Roast Pheasant. Ingredients: 2 young pheasants (about 2 lb. each); 1/4 c. melted, unsalted butter; 1 1/2 tsp. salt; wild rice and mushroom dressing. Directions: Cut off necks of birds close to bodies, leaving neck skin on to help hold the stuffing. Remove pin feathers; singe if needed. Rinse inside and out in warm water; drain and pat dry.

title: Secret Beef Stew(Serves 6)
text: Secret Beef Stew(Serves 6). Ingredients: 2 c. grapefruit juice; 1 1/2 lb. beef (stew); 1 1/4 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 2 cloves garlic, chopped; 1 large onion, chopped; 2 small bay leaves; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 1/2 tsp. paprika; 1/4 tsp. cloves; 5 carrots, cut in quarters; 4 potatoes, cut in quarters; 6 onions, cut in halves. Directions: Place grapefruit juice in saucepan. Place over medium heat. Add stew meat and mix well. Add Worcestershire sauce, garlic, onion, bay leaves and seasonings; mix well. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 2 hours. Add vegetables. Bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 20 to 25 minutes. Remove bay leaves.

title: Caramel Delight
text: Caramel Delight. Ingredients: 1 roll Pillsbury cookie dough; 1 bag Oreos, crushed; 1 jar caramel topping, melted; 1 bag chocolate chips. Directions: Put cookie dough in bottom of a 9 x 13-inch pan. Cover with crushed Oreos. Pour melted caramel on top and bake until crust is done. Pour melted chips on top and chill. Add sprinkles too, if you wish. Chill for 1 hour before serving.

title: Crazy Caramel Rolls
text: Crazy Caramel Rolls. Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. refrigerated biscuits; 1 Tbsp. water; 1 c. pecans; 3 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 1/3 c. dark brown sugar. Directions: Combine water, butter and sugar. Cook until butter melts and mixes with other ingredients. Stir in pecans. Cut biscuits into quarters. Stir and coat each quarter in mixture. Bake in tube pan at 400° until biscuits are done.

title: Buck Snort Slaw
text: Buck Snort Slaw. Ingredients: 1 head cabbage; 1 large onion; 1 c. sugar; 1 c. vinegar; 1 tsp. celery seed; 1/2 c. oil; 2 tsp. salt. Directions: Early in day (6 to 8 hours) ahead, cut up cabbage and place in bowl. Slice onion and place on top of cabbage. Mix together and heat: sugar, vinegar, celery seed, oil and salt. Heat until sugar dissolves. Pour over cabbage; do not stir. Cover and refrigerate. Serve cold.

title: Easy Chicken Stir-Fry
text: Easy Chicken Stir-Fry. Ingredients: 1 Tbsp. vegetable oil; 1 lb. boneless breast, cut in strips; 1 pkg. Hidden Valley Ranch dressing mix; 1 pkg. frozen stir-fry vegetables; 2 Tbsp. soy sauce; chow mein noodles; Chinese rice. Directions: Heat oil in large skillet. Add chicken breast and stir-fry for 5 minutes. Add Hidden Valley mix, vegetables and soy sauce. Stir-fry over medium heat about 5 minutes. Serve over chow mein noodles or Chinese rice.

title: Yummy Brisket
text: Yummy Brisket. Ingredients: 1 (4 lb.) beef brisket; 1 pkg. Lipton dried onion soup mix; Worcestershire sauce; garlic powder. Directions: Trim fat on brisket. Line cake pan with aluminum foil, 6 inches extra on each side. Put brisket on foil. Rub Worcestershire sauce on brisket generously so there is some on the pan. Rub with garlic powder and pepper. Top with soup mix. Top with another sheet of foil and seal well with bottom sheet. Bake at 250° for 3 to 3 1/2 hours. Slice into thin pieces.

title: Eggrolls
text: Eggrolls. Ingredients: 1/2 to 1 lb. crumbled tofu; 1 to 1 1/2 heads shredded cabbage; 1 shredded carrot; 4 oz. chopped mushrooms; 2 green onions, minced; 1 Tbsp. minced fresh ginger root; 8 oz. bean sprouts; 3 Tbsp. soy sauce; 1 Tbsp. roasted sesame oil; 1/4 tsp. pepper; eggroll wrappers; oil for frying; water; salt. Directions: Drain everything well. Add everything together except oil, water and eggroll skins. Add a large spoonful of mixture to skin and roll (rolling directions on package). Adding a touch of water will make eggroll skin stay closed. To fry (and boil) spread 2 tablespoons oil in a 10-inch nonstick frypan. Arrange enough dumplings to cover the bottom without overlapping. Add 2/3 cup cold water; cover pan, bringing it to a boil. Turn to medium heat until water has evaporated. Add 1 tablespoon oil if needed. Fry until both sides are brown.

title: Barbecue Beef Brisket
text: Barbecue Beef Brisket. Ingredients: 6 to 8 lb. brisket; 3 oz. liquid smoke; garlic powder; onion salt; Worcestershire sauce; salt and pepper. Directions: Marinate overnight the brisket in liquid smoke, garlic powder and onion salt. Sprinkle generously. Cover tightly with foil and refrigerate overnight.

title: Zesty Beans
text: Zesty Beans. Ingredients: 2 cans pork and beans; 1/2 c. onion; 1/2 c. green pepper; 1/2 c. ketchup; 1 c. pepperoni; 1/2 barbecue sauce; 1/2 c. brown sugar. Directions: Mix all ingredients together. Bake at 350° for 30 to 45 minutes.

title: Blueberry Boy Bait
text: Blueberry Boy Bait. Ingredients: 2 c. flour; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 3/4 c. shortening; 2 tsp. baking powder; 3 eggs; 1 c. milk; 1 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 (2 lb.) can drained blueberries. Directions: Blend first 3 ingredients together until it resembles coarse meal. Remove a cup for topping and set aside. Add baking powder, eggs, milk, salt and vanilla; mix until smooth. Grease and flour a 9 x 13-inch pan. Add blueberries. Bake at 350° for 40 minutes.

title: Pierogie Casserole
text: Pierogie Casserole. Ingredients: 1 pkg. lasagne noodles or 1 bag wide egg noodles; 1 (15 oz.) large jar Cheez Whiz; 4 to 6 boiled potatoes (no seasoning or butter); 1 to 1 1/2 small onions, diced. Directions: Boil noodles and drain. (If using lasagne, lay flat until ready to use.)

title: Chicken Cutlets With Spaghetti
text: Chicken Cutlets With Spaghetti. Ingredients: 1 large pkg. chicken cutlets; 1 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. pepper; 1 1/2 tsp. minced garlic; 1 bag potato chips (any flavor); 4 eggs; 1 lb. spaghetti; 1 jar Ragu meatless spaghetti sauce. Directions: Clean chicken cutlets the day before and season with salt, pepper and garlic so that the chicken will soak up the seasonings. The following day, crush the potato chips and place them in a bowl. Beat the eggs in another bowl. Dip the chicken cutlets in eggs and then in the potato chip crumbs until they are fully covered. Bake at 325° for 45 minutes, then check with a fork to see if done inside. If not, let cook a little longer. While chicken is baking, cook spaghetti according to directions on package. Heat spaghetti sauce. Combine sauce and spaghetti and serve with chicken cutlets.

title: Buckwheat Pancakes
text: Buckwheat Pancakes. Ingredients: 2 c. buckwheat flour; 2 c. white self-rising flour; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. soda; 2 tsp. baking powder. Directions: Mix dry ingredients. Add buttermilk to pour consistency.

title: Never Fail Divinity
text: Never Fail Divinity. Ingredients: 2 c. sugar; 1/2 c. water; dash of salt; 1 pt. marshmallow cream; 1/2 c. nutmeats. Directions: Combine sugar, water and salt. Bring to a rolling boil. Boil for 2 minutes. Place marshmallow cream in a large bowl and pour hot syrup all at once into it. Stir until candy loses its gloss. Add nuts. Drop by spoonfuls onto waxed paper.

title: Half And Half Deli Pickles
text: Half And Half Deli Pickles. Ingredients: 7 c. cold water; 1/2 c. kosher salt; 2 Tbsp. pickling spice; 2 Tbsp. garlic powder; 3 to 4 garlic buds; 15 to 20 pickling cukes. Directions: Combine all ingredients in large pot; add washed cukes. Cover top with foil. Let stand 2 days (stir). Will keep fresh in refrigerator in jars or plastic containers for about 2 weeks.

title: Oreo Icebox Cookies
text: Oreo Icebox Cookies. Ingredients: 24 to 28 Oreos, crushed; 8 oz. cream cheese, softened; 2 c. confectioners sugar; 2/3 c. peanut butter; 3/4 c. milk; 2 (8 oz.) containers Cool Whip; 1/2 c. chopped nuts (optional). Directions: Put crumbled Oreos in bottom of a 9 x 13-inch pan.

title: "Yummy" Strawberry Pie
text: "Yummy" Strawberry Pie. Ingredients: 1 (3 oz.) pkg. strawberry flavored gelatin; 1/4 c. sugar; 3/4 c. hot water; 9-inch single crust baked pie shell; 3 c. whipped topping; 1 1/2 c. sliced fresh strawberries or 12 oz. pkg. frozen strawberries. Directions: Mix the strawberry gelatin with sugar in a small bowl. Heat 3/4 cup water in a saucepan until water is very hot. Pour the water over gelatin and sugar. Stir the gelatin with a spoon to dissolve it. Put the bowl in refrigerator about 20 minutes, so that gelatin will thicken, but not "set." Fold 3 cups of topping and gelatin together. Mix the strawberries into the pie filling (gelatin mixture) with a spoon. Pour into a cooled baked pie crust. Put in refrigerator. Chill 1 to 2 hours.

title: Baklava (Greek Pastry)
text: Baklava (Greek Pastry). Ingredients: 2 large pkg. filo dough; 3 c. sugar; 5 Tbsp. cinnamon; 1 Tbsp. cloves; 2 sticks butter; 1/4 c. lemon juice; 3 c. finely chopped pecans; 1 c. honey; 1/2 c. water. Directions: In saucepan, melt butter; set aside. In bowl, mix sugar, cinnamon, cloves and pecans; set aside. In large 13 x 9-inch pan, layer filo (3 or 4 sheets) and brush with butter. Sprinkle with pecan mixture. Layer more filo brushed with butter, pecan mixture, etc., ending with filo. Cut into 4-inch square and cut 4-inch squares into 2-inch triangles. Bake at 375° until golden brown. Cool.

title: Carrot Cabbage Slaw
text: Carrot Cabbage Slaw. Ingredients: 1/2 head cabbage; 2 celery stalks; 2 pkg. sweetener; 2 Tbsp. vinegar; 1 Tbsp. mayonnaise; 1 small onion; 2 carrots; 1/2 tsp. black pepper; 2 Tbsp. lemon juice. Directions: Shred cabbage and carrots. Finely chop onion and celery. Mix together in a large bowl. In a separate bowl, mix remaining ingredients. Pour over shredded vegetables and refrigerate. Yields 10 servings. Serving Size: 1/2 cup. Exchanges: 1 vegetable.

title: Sauteed Greens
text: Sauteed Greens. Ingredients: 1 lb. or bunch greens, cooked as described in Master Recipe; 2 tsp. virgin olive oil or corn oil; 2 garlic cloves, minced; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: You can saute spinach, chard or beet greens without pre-boiling; just saute them as below for about 5 minutes or until wilted. Serve with fresh lemon wedge or about 1/2 cup mushrooms (optional).

title: Spinach Balls With Mustard Sauce
text: Spinach Balls With Mustard Sauce. Ingredients: 2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen spinach, chopped, thawed and squeezed dry; 2 c. stuffing mix, crushed; 1 c. grated Parmesan cheese; 1 stick butter, melted; 4 scallions, finely chopped; 3 eggs; dash of freshly grated nutmeg; 1/2 c. powdered dry mustard; 1/2 c. white vinegar; 1/4 c. sugar; 1 egg yolk. Directions: Combine spinach, stuffing mix, cheese, butter, scallions, 3 eggs and nutmeg. Shape into 1-inch balls. Refrigerate or freeze until ready to use. Bake on greased baking sheet at 350° until golden brown, 10 to 15 minutes, if thawed, and 20 minutes, if frozen. Makes 70 balls.

title: Light Fruit Cake Cookies
text: Light Fruit Cake Cookies. Ingredients: 1 c. shortening; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 3 eggs; 4 c. flour; 1 tsp. nutmeg; 2 tsp. cinnamon; 1 tsp. baking soda; 1 lb. chopped dates; 1 c. raisins; 1 c. nuts; 1 c. crushed pineapple with juice; 1 c. chopped candied fruit. Directions: Cream sugar, shortening and eggs until light. Sift together flour, nutmeg, cinnamon and baking soda. Add to egg mixture, then add the rest of the ingredients. Add 1/4 cup of milk, if necessary. Coat fruits with part of the flour. Drop by teaspoonful on ungreased cookie sheet. Bake at 375° until light brown.

title: Chicken-Fried Steak
text: Chicken-Fried Steak. Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. beef top round steak (1/2-inch thick); 1 Tbsp. milk; 1 beaten egg; 1 c. fine soda cracker crumbs; 1/4 c. salad oil. Directions: Pound steak 1/4-inch thick; cut into serving pieces. Blend egg and milk. Dip meat in egg mixture, then in crumbs. Slowly brown meat in hot oil, turning once. Cover; cook on low heat for 45 to 60 minutes, until tender. Season to taste. Serves 6.

title: Beef Ball
text: Beef Ball. Ingredients: 1 pkg. pressed beef; 1 large pkg. cream cheese; 4 garden onions. Directions: Mix beef and onions together in food processor. Add cream cheese and mix. Take out and form ball. Wrap a slice of pressed beef around ball.

title: Lemon Or Orange Icebox Pie
text: Lemon Or Orange Icebox Pie. Ingredients: 1 small can frozen lemonade or orange juice; 1 small carton Cool Whip; 1 can Eagle Brand milk, chilled; 1 (9-inch) graham cracker crust. Directions: Mix all ingredients until smooth and creamy. Pour into pie crust. Chill until ready to serve.

title: Sour Cream Pecans
text: Sour Cream Pecans. Ingredients: 1/2 c. sour cream; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 1/2 qt. roasted pecan halves. Directions: Mix sugar, sour cream and vanilla in a large pot. Stir and bring to a boil. Boil 3 minutes, stirring often. Pour in roasted pecans. Stir until liquid is absorbed. Pour onto waxed paper; separate so pecans will dry without sticking together. Allow to dry. Store in airtight container.

title: Minga Beans
text: Minga Beans. Ingredients: 4 (28 oz.) cans baked beans; 1 1/2 lb. hamburger meat; 3 large onions; 3 green peppers; 3 stalks celery; 1/2 bottle chili sauce; 1/2 c. brown sugar; vegetable oil; parsley; salt, pepper and Tabasco sauce to taste. Directions: Saute chopped peppers, onions and celery until soft in large pan. In separate pan, brown hamburger and drain. Place beans in a 12 x 18-inch baking dish. Add hamburger and onion mixture. Combine gently.

title: Chinese Chicken Casserole
text: Chinese Chicken Casserole. Ingredients: 1/2 lb. cooked chicken, cut up; 2 cans cream of mushroom soup; 2 cans chicken and rice soup; 2 c. cooked celery; 6 oz. can evaporated milk; 1 large Chinese chow mein noodles; 2 Tbsp. soy sauce. Directions: Mix all together and bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

title: Polenta
text: Polenta. Ingredients: 1 c. corn meal; 1 c. cold water; 3 c. hot water; little salt; grated cheese; tomato sauce. Directions: In pan mix corn meal with 1 cup cold water. Add 3 cups hot water and salt. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly for 7 to 8 minutes, bringing heat down to low. Place in two dishes, cover with gravy and sprinkle with grated cheese.

title: Brandied Sweet Potatoes
text: Brandied Sweet Potatoes. Ingredients: 6 large, unpared yams; 2 tsp. cornstarch; 1/2 tsp. nutmeg or cinnamon; 2 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. granulated sugar; 1 c. water; 1 Tbsp. lemon juice; 1/3 c. brandy. Directions: One and one-fourth hours before serving, cook yams in boiling, salted water for 25 minutes or until tender. Drain and cool. In a 1-quart saucepan, mix cornstarch, nutmeg or cinnamon, salt and granulated sugar. Stir in water. Cook over low heat until sauce is clear. Stir in lemon juice and brandy. Heat oven to 375°. Peel potatoes; slice crosswise, 1/4 to 1/2-inch thick. Put into buttered shallow baking dish. Pour on sauce, then bake 30 minutes or until glazed, basting occasionally. When glazed, sprinkle with miniature marshmallows and broil until golden, if desired.

title: Meatless Spaghetti Sauce
text: Meatless Spaghetti Sauce. Ingredients: 1 Tbsp. margarine; 2 tsp. olive oil; 2 cloves crushed garlic; 1 small diced white onion; 2 Tbsp. crushed basil; 1 Tbsp. crushed oregano; 1 tsp. crushed red pepper; 2 (14 oz.) cans stewed tomatoes; 1 (7 oz.) can tomato paste; 2 Tbsp. honey; 1 pinch thyme. Directions: In a 2-quart saucepan under medium heat, saute onion, garlic and crushed red pepper in margarine and olive oil until tender. Add basil, oregano and thyme. Pour in stewed tomatoes, roughly chopping up the individual tomatoes to desired consistency. Add tomato paste and mix thoroughly. Mix in the honey and turn down heat to low. Simmer, stirring frequently, for about 20 minutes. Serve with pasta of choice.

title: Crispy Crunchy Brittle
text: Crispy Crunchy Brittle. Ingredients: 2 c. coarsely chopped walnuts; 1 c. granulated sugar; 1/2 c. light corn syrup; 1/4 c. water; 1 Tbsp. butter; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. baking soda. Directions: Place walnuts in 9-inch glass pie plate. Microwave at High 5 minutes, stirring halfway through until toasted; set aside. In 2-quart glass measuring cup with handle, combine sugar, corn syrup, water, butter and salt. Microwave at High 5 to 6 minutes, stirring well at least once until sugar dissolves. Microwave at High 4 1/2 to 7 minutes, stirring once after 2 minutes until mixture reaches 300°. Stir in baking soda; add toasted walnuts. Turn at once into buttered 15 x 10-inch jelly roll pan. Spread thin. Let stand until cooled. Break into pieces. Makes 1 1/2 pounds.

title: Simple Bread
text: Simple Bread. Ingredients: 2 1/2 c. warm Water; 2 pkgs. active Dry Yeast; 1/2 c. Instant non-fat Dry Milk; 2 tbsp. Sugar; 1 tbsp. Salt; 1/2 c. Oil; 7 to 7 1/2 c. Flour (all purpose or self-rising). Directions: Preheat oven to 375 degrees.

title: Potato-Vegetable Chowder
text: Potato-Vegetable Chowder. Ingredients: 5 c. vegetable broth; 1 c. tomato or vegetable juice; 1 c. peeled chopped tomato; 1 c. chopped carrots; 1 c. green or yellow wax beans; 1 c. chopped onions; 1 c. sliced zucchini; 1 c. chopped leek; 2 c. diced unpeeled red potatoes; 1 cheesecloth bag fresh herbs; 2 tsp. salt. Directions: Bring liquid to a boil in a deep soup kettle. Add all vegetables and seasonings. Simmer, partially covered, until vegetables are tender. Serve with crusty whole wheat bread.

title: Mexican Style Chicken Kiev
text: Mexican Style Chicken Kiev. Ingredients: 8 chicken breast halves; 1 (7 oz.) can diced green chiles, divided into 8 portions; 1/4 lb. Jack cheese, cut into 8 strips; 1/2 c. fine dry bread crumbs; 1/4 c. grated Parmesan; 1 Tbsp. chili powder; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. cumin; 1/4 tsp. pepper; butter; tomato sauce. Directions: Pound chicken pieces to about 1/4-inch thickness. Put chiles and Jack cheese strip in center of each chicken piece. Roll up and tuck ends under. Combine bread crumbs, Parmesan cheese, chili powder, salt, cumin and pepper. Dip each stuffed chicken in shallow bowl of 6 teaspoons melted butter and roll in crumb mixture. Place chicken, seam side down in 9 x 13-inch baking dish and drizzle with a little melted butter. Cover and chill 4 hours or overnight. Bake uncovered at 400°, 20 minutes, or until done. Serve with tomato sauce, if desired. Makes 8 servings.

title: Tortellini And Spinach Tomato Soup
text: Tortellini And Spinach Tomato Soup. Ingredients: 1 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 2 cloves garlic, minced; 1 qt. chicken broth (4 c.); 9 oz. cheese tortellini; 1/4 c. grated Parmesan cheese; salt and pepper to taste; 14 1/2 oz. can stewed tomatoes, chopped; 1/2 (10 oz.) bag spinach, stemmed and chopped; 1 tsp. dried basil leaves; 2 cans tomato soup; 1 1/2 cans milk; 4 c. canned tomato soup; 1 c. consomme; 1/4 tsp. soy sauce; 1 egg, slightly beaten. Directions: On top of stove, combine soup and consomme; bring to a boil. Add soy sauce. Pour in egg and stir constantly for 2 minutes, until egg separates into shreds. Pour and serve.

title: Cheese Wafers
text: Cheese Wafers. Ingredients: 2 c. grated sharp Cheddar cheese (room temperature); 2 c. plain flour; 2 sticks margarine, softened; 2 c. Rice Krispies; 1/2 tsp. hot sauce; 1/4 tsp. cayenne pepper. Directions: Cream the cheese and margarine. Mix in flour. Stir in Rice Krispies. Drop by teaspoon on ungreased cookie sheet. Flatten with back of spoon. Bake at 350° for 10 to 12 minutes. Store in airtight container.

title: Chicken And Tortilla Casserole
text: Chicken And Tortilla Casserole. Ingredients: 1 medium chicken; 1 can (12) soft tortillas; 1 large chopped onion; 1 large chopped green pepper; 1 can mushroom soup; 1 can cream of chicken soup; 1 can tomatoes and green chilies; 1/2 tsp. chili powder; 1/2 tsp. garlic salt; 1 lb. Cheddar cheese, grated. Directions: Boil chicken; bone and cut up meat. To the meat, add onion and green pepper. Mix soups, tomatoes, chili powder and garlic salt. Dip each tortilla in chicken broth, then in a buttered 9 x 12-inch baking dish. Place a layer of tortilla, 1/2 the chicken, 1/2 the soup mixture and 1/2 cheese. Repeat layers; bake 30 to 40 minutes in 350° oven until cheese melts.

title: Sausage Omelet
text: Sausage Omelet. Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. brown and serve sausage links; 1/4 c. chopped onion; 1/4 c. chopped green pepper; 5 eggs; 1/4 c. milk; 1 tomato, chopped; 1 1/2 Tbsp. butter or margarine. Directions: Heat sausage in heavy 8-inch ovenproof skillet according to package directions. Remove sausage and keep warm. Saute the onion and green pepper in sausage skillet over medium-high until tender. Combine eggs and milk in bowl. Add the onion, green pepper and tomato. Melt the butter in the skillet over low heat. Add the egg and vegetable mixture and cook until eggs are almost set. Arrange the sausage on top of eggs. Place under the broiler and broil until center is firm and top is lightly browned. Cut into wedges and serve immediately.

title: Five Flavor Pound Cake
text: Five Flavor Pound Cake. Ingredients: 2 sticks margarine; 1/2 c. Crisco; 3 c. sugar; 5 eggs; 3 c. flour; 1 tsp. baking powder; 1 c. milk; 1 tsp. butter extract; 1 tsp. lemon extract; 1 tsp. coconut extract; 1 tsp. rum extract; 1 tsp. vanilla extract. Directions: Cream together margarine, Crisco and sugar. Add eggs one at a time. Add alternately dry ingredients and liquid. Bake about 1 hour at 325° in Bundt pan or tube pan.

title: Dog Bone Treats
text: Dog Bone Treats. Ingredients: 1 1/4 c. whole wheat flour; 1/2 c. quick cooking oats; 1 egg, lightly beaten; 1/2 c. all-purpose flour; 1 tsp. sugar; 3/4 c. dry powdered milk; 1/4 c. yellow cornmeal; 1/2 c. hot water; 1/3 c. vegetable oil; 1 Tbsp. chicken or beef granules. Directions: Mix all ingredients in a large bowl until well blended. Roll the dough out on a floured board. Cut out treats with a dog bone shaped cookie cutter. Bake at 350° for 15 to 20 minutes, or until dry.

title: Barbara Mandrell'S Four Layer Dessert
text: Barbara Mandrell'S Four Layer Dessert. Ingredients: 1 c. flour; 1 c. chopped nuts; 1/2 c. melted margarine; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese, softened; 1 c. whipped topping; 1 small pkg. instant chocolate pudding; 1 small pkg. instant vanilla pudding; 4 c. milk, divided; 1 c. sifted powdered sugar; whipped topping; chopped nuts. Directions: Combine flour, nuts and melted margarine and mix well. Press into bottom of 11 3/4 x 7 1/2-inch baking dish. Bake at 350° for 15 minutes. Cool. Combine softened cream cheese and powdered sugar and mix well until blended. Fold in whipped topping. Spread over crust. Chill. Prepare chocolate pudding; mix as directed on package. Spread over cream cheese mixture. Prepare vanilla pudding mix as directed on package and spread over chocolate pudding. Cut into squares and garnish with additional whipped topping and chopped nuts.

title: Honey Pork
text: Honey Pork. Ingredients: 2 lb. pork shoulder steak; 2 Tbsp. oil; 1 pkg. brown gravy mix; 1 1/4 c. hot water; 1/4 c. honey; 3 Tbsp. soy sauce; 2 Tbsp. red wine vinegar; 1 tsp. ginger; 1/4 tsp. garlic salt; 4 carrots, thinly sliced; 1 medium onion, cut into 6 wedges; 1 green pepper, cut in squares. Directions: Cut pork in 1-inch cubes, discarding bone. Brown pork in oil in large skillet, stirring frequently, for 15 minutes. Combine gravy mix, water, honey, soy sauce, vinegar, ginger and garlic salt. Add to skillet. Cover; cook for 20 minutes. Add carrots, onion and green pepper; cook at least 10 minutes. Serve with rice.

title: Apple Pumpkin Dessert
text: Apple Pumpkin Dessert. Ingredients: 1 (21 oz.) can apple pie filling; 1 (16 oz.) can pumpkin; 1 (14 oz.) can sweetened condensed milk; 2 eggs; 1 tsp. ground cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. ground nutmeg; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 c. gingersnap crumbs (about 18 cookies); 2 Tbsp. melted butter. Directions: Heat oven to 400°. Spoon apple filling into 8 to 10 custard cups.

title: Happy Holiday Recipe
text: Happy Holiday Recipe. Ingredients: 4 c. love; 3 c. friendship; 2 c. good cheer; 1 c. thanks. Directions: Mix well and share.

title: Brownies(Club Member: Clint Hudson)
text: Brownies(Club Member: Clint Hudson). Ingredients: 1/2 c. shortening; 7 1/2 Tbsp. cocoa; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 1 c. flour; 3 eggs; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1/2 c. nuts. Directions: Melt shortening and cocoa with sugar and eggs. Beat in flour. Add nuts and vanilla. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

title: Ed Harmon'S Rolls
text: Ed Harmon'S Rolls. Ingredients: 2 c. warm water; 2 pkg. yeast; 1 Tbsp. salt or less; 1/3 c. sugar; 1/3 c. vegetable oil (can use melted butter; makes it flaky); 2 eggs; 4 c. flour. Directions: Dissolve yeast in the water. Add salt, sugar and vegetable oil and mix together. Add eggs and 3 cups flour; mix. Add 1 more cup flour and knead. (May need to add a little flour if sticky.) Let rise; knead again into rolls or bread. Use a greased pan and bake at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes or until golden brown on top. Rub butter on top of each roll. This is a good recipe to use for cinnamon rolls/bread.

title: Crock-Pot Stew
text: Crock-Pot Stew. Ingredients: 1 can Castleberry barbecue beef; 1 can Castleberry barbecue pork; 1 can Sweet Sue barbecue chicken; 1 can whole kernel corn; 1 can cream-style corn; 1 can mixed vegetables; 1 can stewed tomatoes; 1 onion, chopped; 3 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; Tabasco sauce to taste; salt to taste; pepper to taste; 3 medium potatoes, diced small. Directions: Open cans; dump contents into crock-pot. Add remaining ingredients. Stir until well mixed. Cook on low setting all day.

title: Hidden Valley Ranch Chicken Stir-Fry(Dinner In Ten Minutes Or Less)
text: Hidden Valley Ranch Chicken Stir-Fry(Dinner In Ten Minutes Or Less). Ingredients: 1/2 lb. boneless, skinless chicken breast; 1 pkg. Hidden Valley original Ranch dressing; 16 oz. thawed vegetable medley; 1 Tbsp. vegetable oil; 2 Tbsp. water. Directions: Heat 1 tablespoon vegetable oil in large skillet or wok. Add chicken, cut into thin strips. Stir in Hidden Valley original Ranch salad dressing. Mix to coat chicken. Add thawed vegetable medley and 2 tablespoons water. Stir-fry about 2 minutes. Makes 4 servings.

title: Fruit Cocktail
text: Fruit Cocktail. Ingredients: 1 oz. Amaretto; 1/2 oz. light rum; 1/3 oz. strawberry syrup; 1 1/2 oz. half and half. Directions: Blend with ice and strain champagne flute. Garnish with strawberry.

title: Sour Cream Meat Loaf
text: Sour Cream Meat Loaf.
