Ingredients: 2 lb. ground lean beef; 1 1/2 c. grated raw carrot; 1/4 c. grated unpeeled apple; 1 1/2 c. grated raw potato; 1 onion, quartered and studded with 4 cloves; 6 slices fried bacon, crumbled; 2 beaten eggs; 2 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 1/8 tsp. nutmeg; 1/8 tsp. dry mustard; 2 c. sour cream. Directions: Combine all ingredients in large mixing bowl; blend well. Pack in a 9 x 5 x 3-inch loaf pan. Bake at 350° for 1 1/2 to 1 3/4 hours. Serves 6.

title: Vanilla Pudding
text: Vanilla Pudding. Ingredients: 3/4 c. sugar; 2 c. milk, scalded; 5 round Tbsp. flour; 2 eggs, slightly beaten; dash of salt; 1 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Scald milk. Mix sugar, flour and salt together. Add to scalded milk in heavy pan or double boiler. Stir constantly. Cook until thick; add slightly beaten eggs and cook 2 minutes more. Remove from heat and add butter and vanilla. Stir until smooth and butter melts.

title: Low-Fat Spanish Rice
text: Low-Fat Spanish Rice. Ingredients: 1 3/4 c. fat-free chicken broth; 1 c. long grain brown rice; 3 cloves garlic, minced; 3/4 c. chopped onion; 1/2 c. finely chopped green pepper; 1/3 c. chopped celery; 1 (15 oz.) can tomatoes (undrained), coarsely chopped; 1 tsp. chili powder; 1/2 tsp. crushed dried oregano; 1/2 tsp. crushed dried basil; 1/2 tsp. paprika; 2 tsp. honey. Directions: In medium saucepan, over high heat, bring broth to a boil. Add rice, cover and reduce to low setting. Cook 50 minutes or until rice is tender. Saute garlic, onion, green pepper and celery in 3 to 4 tablespoons juice from tomatoes in a large skillet over medium-high heat for 5 to 7 minutes or until softened. Add tomatoes to skillet with remaining juice. Stir in seasonings and honey and bring to a boil. Cover and reduce heat to simmer. Cook for 10 minutes. When rice is tender, stir into tomato mixture. Heat until hot, approximately 5 minutes.

title: Gobble Gobble Meat Loaf
text: Gobble Gobble Meat Loaf. Ingredients: 2 lb. ground turkey; 2 c. Italian seasoned bread crumbs; 1/2 c. minced onions; 1/4 tsp. minced fresh garlic; 1 tsp. parsley; 1/2 c. tomato sauce; 1/2 tsp. celery powder; 1/2 tsp. black pepper; 1/8 tsp. salt; 1 egg. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. In a large bowl, mix all ingredients well. Spoon mixture into loaf pan. Cook for 1 to 1 1/2 hours.

title: Mexican Chip Casserole
text: Mexican Chip Casserole. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; 1 medium onion, chopped; 1 garlic clove, minced; 1 10 3/4 oz. can cream of mushroom soup; 1 11 oz. can Mexicorn; 1 4 oz. can green chilies, chopped; 1 10 oz. pkg corn chips; 1 10 oz. can red enchilada sauce, hot; 1 to 2 c. Colby Monterey jack cheese, shredded. Directions: In a skillet, cook beef, onion, and garlic over medium heat and drain. Add soup, corn and chilies; mix well. In an ungreased shallow 3-quart baking dish, layer meat mixture, corn chips and enchilada sauce. Top with cheese. Bake uncovered at 350° for 8 to 10 minutes. Serves 6.

title: Cinnamon Monkey Bread
text: Cinnamon Monkey Bread. Ingredients: 3 cans flaky biscuits; 1/2 c. sugar; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 c. butter; 3/4 c. sugar; 3/4 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 c. pecans, chopped. Directions: Grease and flour a Bundt pan. Quarter cut biscuits and roll each quarter in 1/2 cup sugar and cinnamon mixture. In a saucepan, melt butter. Add 3/4 cup sugar and cinnamon. Heat until sugar melts. Pour over biscuits. Sprinkle chopped pecans over biscuits. Bake in a 350° oven for 30 to 35 minutes. Remove from oven. Cool 10 minutes. Remove onto cake plate. Enjoy!

title: Currant Scones
text: Currant Scones. Ingredients: 2 c. flour; 3 tsp. baking powder; 1/4 c. butter; 5.33 oz. can evaporated milk; 1 egg; 1/4 c. sugar; 1/4 tsp. salt; 2/3 c. currants; sugar. Directions: Heat oven to 400°. Lightly spoon flour into measuring cup. In medium bowl, combine flour, sugar, baking powder and salt. Blend well. Using fork etc., cut butter until mixture is crumbly. Stir in currants. Combine milk and egg; add all at once, stirring just until moist.

title: Honey Spice Fruit Cup
text: Honey Spice Fruit Cup. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. honey; 1 1/2 tsp. lime, lemon or orange juice; 1/2 tsp. ground cinnamon; 1/2 small pineapple, sliced and quartered; 1 kiwi, peeled, sliced and halved; 1/2 pt. strawberries, halved; 1/2 pt. blueberries. Directions: In medium bowl, stir honey, juice and cinnamon. Toss with all the fruit. Makes 4 servings.

title: Pecan-Crunch Sweet Potato Pie
text: Pecan-Crunch Sweet Potato Pie. Ingredients: 3/4 c. finely chopped pecans; 1/3 c. brown sugar, firmly packed; 2 Tbsp. soft butter; 9-inch unbaked pie shell; 2/3 c. brown sugar, firmly packed; 2 eggs, beaten frothy; 1 c. mashed, cooked sweet potatoes; 1 Tbsp. flour; 1/2 tsp. each: cloves, cinnamon and salt; 1/4 tsp. each: nutmeg and ginger; 1 c. light cream. Directions: Blend together pecans, 1/3 cup brown sugar and butter. Press gently onto bottom of pie shell. Mix and beat gently to blend remaining ingredients. Pour into pie shell. Bake for 55 to 60 minutes at 400° or until knife inserted in center comes out clean. Makes 8 servings.

title: Quick Taco Salad
text: Quick Taco Salad. Ingredients: 3/4 lb. lean ground beef; 1/2 c. chopped onion; 1 pkg. Rice-A-Roni beef flavor; 1/2 c. salsa; 1 tsp. chili powder; 4 c. shredded lettuce; 1 medium tomato chopped; 1/2 c. shredded Monterey Jack or Cheddar cheese; 1/2 c. crushed tortilla chips. Directions: Step 1: In a large skillet, brown ground beef and onion. Drain. Remove from skillet. Set aside.

title: Pumpkin Bread Or Muffins
text: Pumpkin Bread Or Muffins. Ingredients: 1/2 c. vegetable oil; 2 eggs; 1 2/3 c. sifted flour; 1 1/4 c. sugar; 1/2 tsp. nutmeg; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1/4 tsp. baking powder; 1/3 c. water; 1 c. canned or homemade pumpkin puree; 1 tsp. baking soda; 1/2 tsp. salt. Directions: Combine eggs, oil, water and pumpkin puree (use straight pumpkin, not pumpkin pie mix). Sift dry ingredients together and gradually beat into first mixture. Bake in greased and floured 9 x 4 x 3-inch loaf pan for 1 hour at 350°. Or bake in muffin pans 25 minutes. Makes about 20 muffins. Chopped walnuts may be added to the mixture.

title: Cranberry Sweet Potatoes
text: Cranberry Sweet Potatoes. Ingredients: 1/2 c.flour; 1/2 c. brown sugar; 1/2 c. old fashioned or quick oats (uncooked); 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1/3 c. margarine; 2 (17 oz.) cans sweet potatoes; 1 or 2 c. fresh cranberries to taste; 1 1/2 c. mini marshmallows. Directions: Combine flour, brown sugar, oats and cinnamon and cut in margarine until the mixture resembles coarse crumb mixture. Toss 1 cup of crumb mixture with sweet potatoes (drained) and the cranberries. Place in 1 1/2-quart casserole. Top with remaining crumbs. Bake at 350° for 35 minutes. Sprinkle marshmallows over top and broil until lightly brown. Serves 6.

title: Easy Spaghetti
text: Easy Spaghetti. Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. spaghetti; 1 lb. ground beef; 4 Tbsp. olive oil; 1 medium onion, chopped; 1 clove garlic, chopped; 2 Tbsp. chopped parsley; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 2 1/4 c. (No. 2 can) tomato juice; 1 can tomato paste (2/3 c.); 1 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1 tsp. oregano; 1 pkg. spatini; 1 Tbsp. brown sugar. Directions: Saute onion, garlic and parsley in olive oil until soft; add meat and seasonings. Brown lightly. Blend in remaining ingredients and simmer for approximately 1 hour. Makes 4 to 6 servings.

title: Christmas Morning Egg Strata
text: Christmas Morning Egg Strata. Ingredients: 6 slices bread; 1 lb. sausage, cooked; 1 lb. Velveeta, sliced; 8 to 10 eggs (whisk); 1 tsp. dry mustard; 2 1/2 c. milk; salt and pepper. Directions: In a large baking dish, spray Pam all over. Put 6 slices of bread on the bottom. Cover bread with cooked sausage (drain off grease). Next layer is Velveeta slices. In a large bowl, mix eggs, dry mustard, milk, salt and pepper. Pour over layers. Bake at 325° for 1 hour, uncovered.

title: Mexican Style Chicken
text: Mexican Style Chicken. Ingredients: 1 medium onion, chopped; 3 Tbsp. margarine; 1 can mushroom soup; 1 can cream of celery soup; 1 1/2 lb. bag Fritos; 8 oz. grated cheese; 6 chicken breasts, boiled and deboned; 1 can Ro-Tel tomatoes and chilies. Directions: Blend onion sauteed in margarine with soups and tomatoes as it heats. Add deboned chicken (cut into chunks) and Fritos. Stir until mixture is blended with chips. Pour in baking dish and top with cheese. Heat 20 minutes at 350° or until cheese bubbles. Freezes well, omitting cheese.

title: Oatmeal Harvest Cake
text: Oatmeal Harvest Cake. Ingredients: 1 c. oatmeal; 1 1/2 c. boiling water; 1/2 c. shortening; 1 c. brown sugar; 1 c. white sugar; 2 eggs, beaten; 1 1/3 c. flour; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1 tsp. soda; 1/2 tsp. salt. Directions: Pour water over oatmeal and let stand 20 minutes. Cream shortening, brown sugar and white sugar. Add eggs, oatmeal mixture, flour, cinnamon, soda and salt. Grease 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan. Bake 30 to 35 minutes in 350° oven.

title: Baked Eggplant Parmigiana
text: Baked Eggplant Parmigiana. Ingredients: 1 large eggplant, peeled and sliced thin; 3 eggs, beaten; 1/4 c. oil; 2 c. seasoned bread crumbs; 2 c. Mozzarella, shredded; Romano or Parmesan cheese, grated; 1 large can tomatoes, crushed; salt and pepper; oregano; garlic powder; basil. Directions: Dip sliced eggplant in eggs, then bread crumbs and fry until golden. Layer the slices with marinara sauce, then cheeses in rectangular baking dish.

title: Praline Cake
text: Praline Cake. Ingredients: 1 stick melted oleo; 1 c. brown sugar; 4 Tbsp. corn syrup; 2 c. chopped pecans; 1 box butter pecan cake mix; 1 1/4 c. water; 1/3 c. vegetable oil; 3 eggs. Directions: Mix oleo, brown sugar, corn syrup and pecans. Spray 9 x 13-inch pan. Add mixture in pan. Prepare cake mix and pour over pecan mixture and bake at 350° for 45 minutes. Great with ice cream!

title: Apricot Strawberry Muffins
text: Apricot Strawberry Muffins. Ingredients: 2 c. all-purpose flour; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. plus 2 Tbsp. sugar; 1 c. apricot preserves; 1 tsp. pure vanilla extract; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 2 tsp. baking powder; 1/2 c. unsalted butter, softened; 2 large eggs; 1/2 c. milk; 1 1/2 c. fresh strawberries, cut into chunks. Directions: Heat oven to 375°. Line 12 muffin cups with paper liners. Stir together flour, baking powder and salt; set aside. Beat butter and 1/2 cup of sugar with an electric mixer until light. Add eggs, one at a time, mixing well after each addition. Stop mixer and add preserves, milk and vanilla. Mix in on low speed. Mix in dry ingredients with a spoon; fold in strawberries. Divide batter among muffin cups. Mix remaining 2 tablespoons of sugar with cinnamon. Sprinkle over muffins. Bake until set in center, 25 to 30 minutes. Cool in pan for 20 minutes before removing muffins from the cups.

title: Peanut Butter Banana Bars
text: Peanut Butter Banana Bars. Ingredients: 1/2 c. creamy style peanut butter; 2 ripe bananas, mashed; 1/2 c. butter or margarine, melted; 1 lb. light brown sugar; 2 eggs; 2 3/4 c. flour; 2 1/2 tsp. baking powder; 1 c. lightly salted peanuts; 3/4 to 1 c. chocolate chips. Directions: Mix together the peanut butter, mashed bananas, butter, brown sugar and eggs with an electric mixer on medium speed. Sift together the flour with the baking powder. Add to the peanut butter-banana mixture. Mix on low speed just until blended. Stir peanuts and chocolate chips into batter. Spread into a greased 15 x 10-inch jelly roll pan. Bake at 325° for 30 minutes. Cool completely in pan on a wire rack. Cut into bars.

title: Frozen Pineapple Torte
text: Frozen Pineapple Torte. Ingredients: 3 egg yolks; 3 egg whites; dash of salt; 1/2 c. sugar; 1 (9 oz.) can crushed pineapple, drained and saved; 2 Tbsp. lemon juice; 2 Tbsp. sugar; 1 c. heavy whipping cream; 2 c. Salerno vanilla wafer crumbs or more. Directions: Beat egg yolks, salt and 1/2 cup sugar. Add pineapple syrup and lemon juice. Cook in double boiler, stirring constantly, until thick. Add pineapple. Cool.

title: Turkey Scalloppine
text: Turkey Scalloppine. Ingredients: 1 split turkey breast, boned (about 3 lb.); salt; pepper; flour; 6 Tbsp. olive or salad oil; 6 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 3/4 c. Marsala wine or dry sherry; pinch of thyme; pinch of oregano; 1/4 lb. fresh mushrooms, sliced; 2 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 3/4 c. canned beef broth, undiluted; 1 egg yolk; 1 Tbsp. heavy cream. Directions: Skin turkey, slice meat across grain, as thin as possible. Salt and pepper slices, coat with flour. Heat oil and butter in large skillet. Lightly brown slices, a few at a time, pour off fat, return meat to skillet. Add Marsala, thyme and oregano; cook until almost dry, turning slices gently as they cook (about 10 minutes). Saute mushrooms in 2 tablespoons butter, add to turkey. Add beef broth. Cook turkey over gentle heat until almost dry. Beat egg yolk with cream, remove skillet from heat. Pour cream mixture over turkey slices, turn gently to glaze. Makes 6 servings.

title: Butterscotch Brownies
text: Butterscotch Brownies. Ingredients: 1/4 c. melted butter; 1 1/2 c. flour (self-rising); 1 c. chopped nuts; 2 c. brown sugar; 2 eggs; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Mix all ingredients together. Then pour into a brownie pan and bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes.

title: Something Else Cheesecake
text: Something Else Cheesecake. Ingredients: 1 devil's food cake mix. Directions: Prepare cake mix according to directions. Pour into floured and greased 9 x 13-inch pan.

title: Beer Bread
text: Beer Bread. Ingredients: 3 c. self rising flour; 1 - 12 oz. can beer; 1 Tbsp. sugar. Directions: Stir the ingredients together and put in a greased and floured loaf pan. Bake at 425 degrees for 50 minutes. Drizzle melted butter on top.

title: Cream Cheese Ball
text: Cream Cheese Ball. Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese; 2 Tbsp. chopped onions; 3 Tbsp. chopped green pepper; 1 small can crushed pineapple, drained; 1/2 c. chopped nuts, pecans or walnuts. Directions: Mix cream cheese, onion, green peppers and pineapple together and shape into a ball. Cover ball with nuts. Serve with your favorite crackers.

title: Chocolate Chunk Brownies
text: Chocolate Chunk Brownies. Ingredients: 6 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 3/4 c. packed light brown sugar; 1 egg; 1 Tbsp. milk; 1 tsp. vanilla extract; 1 c. all-purpose flour; 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 1/8 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. coarsely chopped walnuts; 1 3/4 c. (10 oz. pkg.) Hershey's Treasures or chocolate chunks. Directions: Heat oven to 350°. Grease square 9 x 9 x 2-inch pan. In large mixing bowl, cream butter and brown sugar until light and fluffy. Add egg, milk and vanilla; beat well. Combine flour, baking soda and salt. Add to creamed mixture. Stir in nuts, if desired, and chocolate treasures or chunks. Spread into prepared pan. Bake 20 to 25 minutes or until lightly browned. Cool completely; cut into bars.

title: Shrimp Gumbo
text: Shrimp Gumbo. Ingredients: 1 medium onion; 1 medium bell pepper; 1 tsp. oregano; 1/2 tsp. basil; 1 tsp. black pepper; 3 cans chicken broth; 3 cans water; 1 bag okra (frozen), cut; 1 tsp. gumbo file; 1 stalk celery, cut; 1 tsp. thyme; 1/2 tsp. cayenne pepper; 2 tsp. salt; 1 only bay leaf (optional); 1 c. tomatoes; 1 lb. hot smoked sausage; 3 c. shrimp. Directions: Saute onion, celery, bell pepper, sausage, thyme, oregano, cayenne pepper and basil. Add chicken broth and water. Cook. Add tomatoes, okra, salt and black pepper. Cook. Add shrimp. Cook, adding file.

title: Jo Sims' Rolls
text: Jo Sims' Rolls. Ingredients: 1 c. hot water; 1 tsp. salt; 6 Tbsp. lard (Crisco); 1/4 c. sugar; 1 pkg. yeast; 2 Tbsp. water; 1 egg, well beaten; 3 1/2 to 4 c. flour. Directions: Combine water, salt, lard and sugar. Cool to lukewarm. Add to yeast dissolved in water. Add egg and half of flour. Beat well. Stir in rest of flour. (I use 1/2 cup of flour to knead the dough.) Cover and let rise until double in bulk; punch down and put in refrigerator and let rise again.

title: Vegetable Beef And Biscuit Casserole
text: Vegetable Beef And Biscuit Casserole. Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. ground beef; 1 can golden corn soup; 1 can tomato soup; 3/4 c. water; 1 (15 oz.) can mixed vegetables, drained; 1 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1 (10 oz.) pkg. refrigerated biscuits; 1/2 c. shredded Cheddar cheese. Directions: In a 10-inch skillet over medium-high heat, cook beef until browned, stirring to separate meat. Spoon off fat. Stir in soups, water, mixed vegetables and Worcestershire sauce. Spoon into a 3-quart baking dish. Bake at 400° for 10 minutes or until hot and bubbling. Meanwhile, cut each biscuit into quarters. Remove dish from oven; stir. Arrange biscuits on hot beef mixture. Top with cheese. Bake 10 minutes or until biscuits are golden brown. Serves 6.

title: Pineapple Sherbet Punch
text: Pineapple Sherbet Punch. Ingredients: 1 (12 oz.) can frozen lemonade; 1 (12 oz.) can frozen orange juice; 3 qt. water; 5 pt. pineapple sherbet; 1 qt. vanilla ice cream. Directions: Mix lemonade, orange juice and water in punch bowl. Spoon in ice cream and sherbet. Serve.

title: Oriental Dish
text: Oriental Dish. Ingredients: 4 chicken breasts, cut into strips; 1/4 c. soy sauce; 1/4 c. cherry cooking wine; 1 Tbsp. cornstarch; 1 onion, sliced; 1 clove garlic, sliced; 1 bell pepper; 1 broccoli slaw mix; 2 carrots, grated; pinch of sesame seed; pinch of ground ginger; red pepper flakes (optional). Directions: Cut chicken breasts into strips. Marinate chicken strips in soy sauce, cherry wine and cornstarch for 20 minutes. Saute chicken in cooking oil until brown. Add onion, garlic and bell pepper with carrots. Cook until vegetables turn a bright color. Add broccoli slaw mix, sesame seed and ginger. Serve with white rice. Serves 4.

title: Italian Zucchini Soup
text: Italian Zucchini Soup. Ingredients: 1 lb. Italian sausage; 1 c. chopped onion; 2 c. chopped celery; 1 medium green pepper, chopped; 2 Tbsp. sugar; 2 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. basil; 1/2 tsp. oregano; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 1 qt. canned tomatoes, cut up; 4 c. diced zucchini; grated Parmesan cheese. Directions: In a Dutch oven, brown sausage with onion; drain excess fat. Add the celery, green pepper, sugar, salt, basil, oregano, pepper and tomatoes. Cover and simmer 1/2 hour. Add zucchini; simmer 1/2 hour or more. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese, if desired. Yield: 2 quarts.

title: 14 Carat Cake
text: 14 Carat Cake. Ingredients: 2 c. sifted plain flour; 2 tsp. baking powder; 1 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 1 1/2 tsp. salt; 2 tsp. cinnamon; 2 c. sugar; 1/2 c. chopped nuts; 1 1/2 c. oil; 4 eggs; 2 c. shredded carrots; 1 (8 1/2 oz.) can crushed pineapple, drained; 1 (3 1/2 oz.) can flaked coconut. Directions: Sift flour, baking powder, soda, salt and cinnamon. Add sugar, oil and eggs. Beat at medium speed for 1 minute. Stir in carrots, pineapple, nuts and coconut. Divide between 3 greased and floured 9-inch cake pans. Bake in 350° oven for 40 minutes.

title: Toffee Squares
text: Toffee Squares. Ingredients: 1 c. butter or margarine; 1 c. firmly packed brown sugar; 1 egg yolk; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1/4 tsp. salt; 2 c. sifted flour; 1/2 lb. sweet chocolate, melted; 1/2 c. chopped nuts. Directions: Cream butter; add sugar gradually. Blend in egg yolk, vanilla, salt and flour. Spread in greased 10 x 15 x 1-inch pan. Bake at 350° for about 20 minutes. (Turn heat down if edges are browning too fast.) Spread while hot with melted chocolate. Sprinkle with chopped nuts. Cool and cut into small squares. Makes about 6 dozen.

title: Garlic And Herb Dipping Oil
text: Garlic And Herb Dipping Oil. Ingredients: 1 c. extra virgin olive oil; 2 Tbsp. Tone's garlic and herb seasoning. Directions: Stir together oil and seasoning. Let stand for 10 minutes to blend flavors. Pour onto bread plates and serve with crusty bread.

title: Kelly Beans
text: Kelly Beans. Ingredients: 1 can pinto beans with jalapeno; 1 can pork and beans with jalapeno; 1 can lima beans; garlic salt to taste; 1 lb. smoked sausage; 1 lb. hamburger meat; 1/2 c. brown sugar; 1 c. ketchup; 1 bell pepper, chopped; 1 onion, chopped. Directions: Saute onion and bell pepper. Brown hamburger meat and brown sausage. Put in crock-pot. Add all ingredients and simmer 1 hour.

title: Ham Fluff
text: Ham Fluff. Ingredients: 3 Tbsp. butter; 4 Tbsp. flour; 1/4 tsp. salt; dash of pepper; 1 tsp. grated onion; 1 tsp. dry mustard; 1 c. milk; 4 eggs, separated; 1 c. ham, ground. Directions: Melt butter in saucepan. Blend in flour, salt, pepper, onion and mustard. Stir in milk and cook, stirring constantly, until very thick. Beat egg yolks until thick and lemon-colored. Fold a little sauce into egg yolks and then add this to remaining sauce. Fold in ham. Whip egg whites until stiff, but not dry. Fold ham mixture into egg whites. Place in greased 1 1/2-quart casserole dish. Bake at 325° for 1 hour, until top is brown and puffy. Yields 4 servings.

title: Chocolate Delight Dessert
text: Chocolate Delight Dessert. Ingredients: 1/2 c. margarine, softened; 1 1/2 c. flour; 1 c. finely chopped pecans; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese, softened; 1 1/2 c. powdered sugar; 1 1/2 c. whipped topping; 1 (3 1/2 oz.) pkg. vanilla instant pudding; 1 (3 1/2 oz.) pkg. chocolate instant pudding; 3 c. milk. Directions: Mix together the first 3 ingredients in 9 x 13 baking dish; spread out for crust. Bake at 350° for 20 minutes. Remove from oven and cool.

title: Dorito Casserole
text: Dorito Casserole. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; salt to taste; 1 small, diced onion; plain Doritos; 1 can Ro-Tel tomatoes; 1 can cream of mushroom soup; 1 can cream of chicken soup; grated cheese. Directions: Cook hamburger, salt and onion. Layer baking dish with Doritos. Put meat mixture on top of Doritos. Mix soups and Ro-Tel and pour mixture over meat. Bake at 350° until it bubbles. Remove from oven and top with grated cheese. Return to oven until cheese melts. Sprinkle with crumbled Doritos.

title: Josette'S Lasagna Roll-Ups
text: Josette'S Lasagna Roll-Ups. Ingredients: 1 box lasagna noodles (approximately 20); 2 c. Mozzarella cheese; 1 c. Ricotta cheese; 1 c. Parmesan cheese; 1 jar spaghetti sauce (non chunky); 2 lb. ground beef; Italian seasoning. Directions: Brown ground beef in skillet and set aside. Boil noodles until cooked and rinse with water. Blend cheeses together and set aside. Add browned ground meat with sauce and seasoning in a pan and simmer. Spread cheeses onto noodle on one side. Roll noodles, curly side out. Cut in half. Do so with remainder of noodles. Pour sauce into baking dish and place noodles with non-curly side down into sauce. Cover and bake at 375° for 1 hour. Serve warm.

title: Rhubarb Coffee Cake
text: Rhubarb Coffee Cake. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. sugar; 1/2 c. butter; 1 egg; 1 c. buttermilk; 2 c. flour; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. soda; 1 c. buttermilk; 2 c. rhubarb, finely cut; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Cream sugar and butter. Add egg and beat well. To creamed butter, sugar and egg, add alternately buttermilk with mixture of flour, salt and soda. Mix well. Add rhubarb and vanilla. Pour into greased 9 x 13-inch pan and add Topping.

title: Chicken Bake
text: Chicken Bake. Ingredients: 1 1/4 c. cooked chicken; 2 c. cooked elbow macaroni, drained; 1 can cream of mushroom soup; 1 can cream of chicken soup; 1 c. cheese, cubed; 2 hard-cooked eggs, chopped; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Mix macaroni, chicken, eggs, cheese and cream of mushroom soup. Pour into a greased casserole dish. Pour cream of chicken soup over the top. Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

title: Ruth'S Carrot Cake
text: Ruth'S Carrot Cake. Ingredients: 4 eggs; 2 c. flour; 2 c. sugar; 2 tsp. soda; 1 tsp. salt; 1 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1 1/4 c. oil; 2 (7 oz.) jars junior baby food carrots. Directions: Mix all ingredients well. Pour into two 8-inch round cake pans. (Pans should be prepared with Crisco and flour.) Bake at 350° for 30 minutes or until cake springs back to touch.

title: Peanut Butter Cups
text: Peanut Butter Cups. Ingredients: 1 c. peanut butter; 1/2 lb. margarine; 1 tsp. vanilla extract; 1 1/2 boxes confectioners sugar; 1 1/2 pkg. milk chocolate (6 oz.); 2 sq. paraffin. Directions: Combine and heat until smooth, peanut butter, margarine and vanilla. Gradually beat in 1 1/2 boxes confectioners sugar (when mixture gets too stiff for mixer, knead remaining sugar in with hands). Refrigerate until mixture is firm. Form into small balls. Melt over low heat the milk chocolate and paraffin pushed into chocolate. Dip the balls in chocolate; place on wax paper to harden. Makes 4 dozen.

title: Hawaiian Coconut Pie
text: Hawaiian Coconut Pie. Ingredients: 1/2 c. light corn syrup; 1/2 c. sugar; 2 eggs, well beaten; 1 Tbsp. butter; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. vanilla; 2 tsp. lemon juice; 1 c. Post 40% Bran Flakes; 2/3 c. coconut; 1/4 c. milk. Directions: Bring syrup and sugar to a boil in a small saucepan. Slowly pour over beaten eggs, beating constantly to blend. Add butter; stir until melted. Stir in salt, vanilla, lemon juice, cereal, coconut and milk. Pour into unbaked pie shell. Bake at 375° about 30 minutes or until top is set and lightly browned. Serve warm or cooled with or without whipped topping.

title: Chinese Slaw
text: Chinese Slaw. Ingredients: 1 head nappa cabbage; 5 green onions; 1/2 c. canola oil; 1/2 c. sugar; 1/4 c. vinegar; salt and pepper to taste; 1/2 c. butter; 1/3 c. sesame seeds; 1 small pkg. slivered almonds; 2 pkg. Ramen Oodles of Noodles. Directions: Remove stalks from cabbage and chop cabbage; wash and refrigerate. Dice onion. Over low heat, melt butter in pan. Crush Ramen noodles (discard flavor packets) and add to pan with sesame seeds and slivered almonds; stir often until brown. Drain on paper towel.

title: Fruit Ginger Ale Punch
text: Fruit Ginger Ale Punch. Ingredients: 1 (6 oz.) can frozen orange juice; 1 (6 oz.) can frozen lemonade; 1 (6 oz.) can frozen limeade; 4 c. cold water; 1 large bottle ginger ale. Directions: Combine all ingredients except ginger ale. Pour over ice cubes. Add ginger ale just before serving.

title: Colorful Candy Popcorn Balls
text: Colorful Candy Popcorn Balls. Ingredients: 12 c. popped popcorn; 1 c. peanuts; 3/4 c. M&M's; 1/4 c. margarine; 1 bag Kraft mini marshmallows; 1 (4 serving size) pkg. Jell-O gelatin (any flavor); margarine (for greasing hands). Directions: Pour popcorn, peanuts and M&M's into huge bowl. Mix. Put margarine and marshmallows in large microwavable bowl. Microwave on High 1 1/2 to 2 minutes. Using pot, remove bowl. Mix. Add Jell-O gelatin to marshmallow mixture. Stir until mixture is same color. Pour mixture over popcorn. Stir with spoon until popcorn is covered. Grease hands with margarine. Shape mixture into balls. Place on waxed paper until cool.

title: Grilled Pico Burgers
text: Grilled Pico Burgers. Ingredients: 1 lb. lean ground beef; 1/4 c. raisins; 2 Tbsp. minced green chilies; 1/8 tsp. cinnamon; 1 garlic clove, minced. Directions: In bowl combine all ingredients. Divide meat mixture into 4 hamburger patties. Sear 5 inches from hot coals for 2 minutes. Turn and cook for 6 minutes for medium done. Serve hot, topped with avocado, onion, lettuce and tomato.

title: "A B C" Sandwich
text: "A B C" Sandwich. Ingredients: 2 c. shredded Cheddar cheese; 1 medium apple, finely chopped; 3/4 c. salad dressing or mayonnaise; 1/2 c. finely chopped walnuts; 12 slices bread, toasted and buttered; 12 bacon strips, cooked; 6 hard-cooked eggs, sliced; 6 tomato slices (optional). Directions: In a bowl, combine cheese, apple, salad dressing and walnuts. Spread on 6 slices of bread and place 2 slices of bacon on each. Cover with egg slices. Top with tomatoes, if desired, and remaining slices of bread.

title: Caramel Turtles
text: Caramel Turtles. Ingredients: 48 light caramels (1 lb.); 7 1/2 Tbsp. cream; 1 1/2 c. flour; 1 1/2 c. quick oats; 1/3 tsp. salt; 2/3 tsp. soda; 1 1/8 c. brown sugar; 1 1/8 c. melted margarine; 1 pkg. chocolate chips. Directions: Melt caramels and cream in double boiler. Combine oat mixture without chocolate chips. Spread 1/2 mixture into 9 x 13-inch ungreased pan. Bake at 350° for 10 minutes.

title: Noodle Supreme
text: Noodle Supreme. Ingredients: 2 lb. ground chuck; 6 green onions; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 1 tsp. sugar; 1 (10 oz.) pkg. medium egg noodles; 3 cans (8 oz.) tomato sauce; 1/2 pt. sour cream; 2 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. garlic powder; 1 pkg. Longhorn Cheddar. Directions: Chop green onions and mix with sour cream and cream cheese. Set aside. Brown ground chuck; drain. Add tomato sauce, salt, pepper, garlic powder and sugar. Simmer while cooking egg noodles.

title: Potato Kugel(Pudding)
text: Potato Kugel(Pudding). Ingredients: 2 eggs; 2 c. grated potatoes, well drained; 1 small chopped onion; 2 Tbsp. butter; 1/2 c. flour; 1 tsp. baking powder; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Beat 2 eggs until fluffy. Add rest of ingredients. Spoon into buttered baking dish. Bake at 400° for 15 minutes, then at 350° for 45 minutes until golden brown and crispy.

title: Dump Salad
text: Dump Salad. Ingredients: 1 can Eagle Brand milk; 1 large can crushed pineapple with juice; 1 can cherry pie filling; 12 oz. Cool Whip; chopped nuts (optional). Directions: Mix all ingredients and chill for 1 hour.

title: Easy Breakfast Rolls
text: Easy Breakfast Rolls. Ingredients: 2 (10 count) cans biscuits; 1/2 c. melted margarine; canned pie filling; 2 Tbsp. margarine; 1/4 c. sugar; 3 Tbsp. flour. Directions: Dip each biscuit into 1/2 cup melted butter. Place buttered side up on a baking sheet. Make an indention on each; fill with favorite pie filling. Sprinkle with mixture of 2 tablespoons butter, sugar and flour. Bake at 375° for 15 minutes.

title: Spring Breakfast Strata
text: Spring Breakfast Strata. Ingredients: 8 eggs; 3 c. milk; 1 Tbsp. Dijon mustard; 2 tsp. dried basil; 1 tsp salt; 2 Tbsp. butter or margarine, melted; 2 Tbsp all-purpose flour; 2 c. (8 oz.) shredded cheddar cheese; 1 lb. fully cooked ham, cubed; 1 pkg. (10 oz) frozen cut asparagus, thawed or 2 c. fresh cut asparagus, cooked; 2 c. sliced fresh mushrooms; 10 c. cubed bread. Directions: In a large bowl, beat eggs; add milk, mustard, basil and salt. Gently stir in remaining ingredients until well mixed. Pour into a greased 13 x 9 x 2 inch baking dish. Cover and refrigerate 8 hours or over night. Remove from the refrigerator 30 minutes before baking. Bake, uncovered, at 350° for 1 hour or until knife inserted near the center comes out clean. Let stand 5 minutes before cutting.

title: Carrot-Nut Cake
text: Carrot-Nut Cake. Ingredients: 1 1/4 c. salad oil (scant); 2 c. granulated sugar (I use 1 3/4 c.); 2 c. all-purpose flour; 2 tsp. baking powder (I use 1/2 tsp.); 1 tsp. baking soda; 1 c. pineapple, crushed and drained (optional); 1 tsp. salt (I omit); 2 tsp. cinnamon; 4 eggs (3 if large); 3 c. raw carrots, grated; 1 c. nuts, finely chopped. Directions: Beat oil and sugar with mixer for 5 minutes. Sift flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt and cinnamon together; stir half of this mixture into oil and sugar mixture. Blend thoroughly, then add remaining dry ingredients alternately with eggs (one at a time), mixing well after each addition. Add carrots and nuts. Pour into lightly oiled 9 or 10-inch tube pan. Bake at 325° for 1 hour.

title: Lemon Daffodil Dessert
text: Lemon Daffodil Dessert. Ingredients: juice of 4 lemons; 1/2 c. sugar; 1 (14 oz.) can sweetened condensed milk; 2 c. whipping cream; 1 small commercial angel food cake. Directions: Combine juice, sugar and milk. Add whipped cream. Tear cake into small pieces and line the bottom of a 9 x 13-inch baking dish. Pour mixture over the cake and mix together a little. Chill for 24 hours before serving. Serves 8 to 10.

title: Tortellini Salad
text: Tortellini Salad. Ingredients: 1 (7 oz.) pkg. frozen cheese tortellini; 1 c. broccoli flowerets; 1 carrot, shredded; 1/3 c. sliced olives; 3/4 c. mayonnaise; 1/3 c. pesto sauce; 1/4 c. milk; 1 Tbsp. oil; 1 tsp. vinegar; 1/2 tsp. white pepper; 1 clove garlic, minced; 2 Tbsp. grated Parmesan cheese. Directions: Cook and drain tortellini as per package directions. Set aside in a large bowl.

title: My Mom'S Hollywood Squares
text: My Mom'S Hollywood Squares. Ingredients: 1 3/4 c. peanut butter; 1/2 lb. oleo; 4 c. confectioners sugar; 1 c. brown sugar; 2 c. graham cracker crumbs; 2 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Mix together and press into jelly roll pan. Melt 1/2 large Hershey bar. Spread over mixture. Cut into 2-inch squares.

title: Frozen Vegetable Casserole
text: Frozen Vegetable Casserole. Ingredients: 2 16-oz. pkgs. frozen California mixed vegetables; 1 lb. Velveeta cheese, cubed; 2 cubes of butter or margarine; 2 tubes of Ritz crackers, crushed. Directions: Place vegetables in a large bowl and pour boiling water over them. Drain the veggies and put them into a greased 9 x 13 inch casserole dish. Melt 1 cube of butter and drizzle it over the vegetables. Add the cheese, distributing it evenly through the dish. Melt the over cube of butter and mix it with the crushed crackers. Cover the vegetables with the cracker mixture, right to the edges of the dish. Bake at 350° for 35-40 minutes.

title: Honey Mustard Baked Beans
text: Honey Mustard Baked Beans. Ingredients: 1/4 c. canned, crushed tomatoes; 2 Tbsp. honey; 2 Tbsp. Dijon mustard; 2 Tbsp. cider vinegar; several drops hot pepper sauce; 1 medium garlic clove, crushed; 1 (15 1/2 oz.) can red kidney beans, rinsed well and drained; salt and freshly ground pepper to taste. Directions: In small saucepan, combine first 6 ingredients and cook over medium heat for 5 minutes. Add beans and simmer for 2 minutes. Serves 2 to 4.

title: Nicaraguan Fried Rice
text: Nicaraguan Fried Rice. Ingredients: 1 onion (medium), chopped; 1 carrot, grated; 1 green pepper, chopped; 1 c. rice; salt to taste; 2 Tbsp. oil; 1 tomato, chopped. Directions: Wash and drain rice. Set aside. In skillet, heat oil and saute onion, green pepper and carrot. Add 2 cups water and salt on medium heat until rice is done. Three minutes before, spread chopped tomato on top. When rice is done, top with lid.

title: Savory Skillet Meal
text: Savory Skillet Meal. Ingredients: 1 lb. round steak; 1/3 c. flour; 1 tsp. salt; 3 Tbsp. cooking oil; 4 medium potatoes, sliced; 1 large onion, sliced; 1 can cream of mushroom soup. Directions: Cut meat into serving pieces; salt and dip in flour. Brown in skillet in hot oil. Place vegetables on top of meat. Add mushroom soup. Cover skillet; turn heat to simmer (when steam escapes freely). Simmer 50 minutes.

title: Pizzacato Pizza Casserole
text: Pizzacato Pizza Casserole. Ingredients: 3 lb. sweet Italian sausage; 1 lb. shell macaroni; 2 (10 1/2 oz.) cans tomato puree; 2 (8 oz.) cans tomato sauce; 1/4 c. water; 1/2 tsp. garlic powder; 1/2 tsp. oregano; 1 (8 oz.) jar mushroom pieces (optional); 1 lb. grated Mozzarella. Directions: Prepare macaroni according to package directions. Drain and set aside. Take sausage out of casing and crumble. Brown and drain off grease. Stir in tomato puree, sauce, water, mushrooms and seasonings. Simmer 15 minutes. Add cooked macaroni and stir well. Put 1/3 of meat and macaroni mixture into a greased 3-quart casserole. Sprinkle with 1/3 Mozzarella cheese. Repeat with remaining 2/3. Bake 15 minutes at 350°. Serves 8 to 10.

title: Steak With Lobster Tail
text: Steak With Lobster Tail. Ingredients: 4 beef top loin steaks, cut 1-inch thick; salt; pepper; 4 (4 oz.) frozen lobster tails; 1/4 c. butter; 2 tsp. lemon juice; 1/4 tsp. salt; dash of paprika. Directions: Slash fat edge of steaks at 1-inch intervals. In skillet, cook steaks over medium-high heat to desired doneness; turn occasionally. Allow 9 to 10 minutes total time for rare or 11 to 12 minutes for medium. Season with salt and pepper. Drop lobster tails in boiling salted water to cover. Return to boiling; reduce heat. Simmer 8 minutes; drain.

title: Golden Capped Tomatoes
text: Golden Capped Tomatoes. Ingredients: 6 medium tomatoes; salt and pepper to taste; 1/4 c. mayonnaise; 1 Tbsp. prepared mustard; 1 tsp. minced onion; 1/2 tsp. sugar; 2 Tbsp. fine dry bread crumbs. Directions: Cut tomatoes in half. Place cut side up in a shallow baking dish. Add salt and pepper. Combine remaining ingredients. Spread a portion on top of each tomato. Bake at 400° for 15 minutes.

title: Salsa Cheese Bake
text: Salsa Cheese Bake. Ingredients: 2 cloves garlic; 1 tbsp. oil; 12 oz. jar salsa; 4 oz. chopped green chilies (drained); 2 tbsp. raisins; 1/4 tsp. cinnamon; 1/8 tsp. ground cloves; dash ground cumin; 1/2 lb. sausage; 16 oz. monterey jack cheese; 1/4 c. sliced olives; tortilla chips. Directions: Cook garlic in oil 1 minute. Stir in next 6 ingredients. Heat to boiling constantly stirring. Reduce heat & simmer until thickened about 3 minutes. Cook & drain sausage, stir in 1 c. salsa mixture. In ungreased casserole dish layer 1/2 of cheese (sliced 1/4 inches) then sausage mixture. Top with 1/2 cheese. Bake uncovered at 400 degrees 8-10 minutes. Heat remaining salsa mixture & pour over cheese. Serve with chips.

title: Vegetable Cheese Bake
text: Vegetable Cheese Bake. Ingredients: 2 (16 oz.) pkg. frozen vegetables (broccoli, cauliflower and carrots); 1 can mushroom soup; 1 can Durkee French fried onions; 1 (16 oz.) jar Cheez Whiz. Directions: Cook frozen vegetables in boiling water for 5 minutes; drain. Mix soup and Cheez Whiz together. Add vegetables and 1/2 of fried onions. Put in a casserole (9 x 13-inch); bake, uncovered, at 350° for 35 minutes. Top with rest of onions and bake another 5 minutes.

title: Diabetic Fruit Cookies
text: Diabetic Fruit Cookies. Ingredients: 1/2 c. margarine; 4 tsp. granulated sugar substitute; 2 eggs; 2 c. flour, sifted; 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. nutmeg; 1/4 c. hot apple juice; 1/4 c. raisins, chopped; 1/4 c. currants. Directions: Cream together margarine and sugar replacement. Add eggs; beat until fluffy. Combine flour, baking soda, salt and nutmeg in sifter. Add alternately with hot apple juice to creamed mixture. Fold in raisins and currants. Allow to rest 15 minutes.

title: Microwave Snack Cake
text: Microwave Snack Cake. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. flour; 1 c. sugar; 1/4 c. cocoa; 1 tsp. baking soda; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 c. water; 2 Tbsp. vinegar; 2 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Mix flour, cocoa, soda and salt in a 9-inch square microwave-safe pan. Mix remaining ingredients in 4-cup measure. Stir into flour mixture until well blended. Microwave on High for 6 to 7 minutes, rotating dish 1/4 turn twice until pick inserted near center comes out clean. Cool 10 minutes on flat surface. Moist spots will dry. Then place on wire rack. Serve warm.

title: Fresh Peach Chiffon Pie
text: Fresh Peach Chiffon Pie. Ingredients: 3/4 c. sugar; 1 1/2 c. chopped fresh peaches; 1 Tbsp. (1 envelope) unflavored gelatin; 1/4 c. cold water; 1/2 c. hot water; 1 Tbsp. lemon juice (fresh, frozen and canned); dash of salt; 1/2 c. heavy whipped cream (can substitute skim evaporated, chilled until ice crystals form, then whip); 1 graham cracker pie shell. Directions: Add sugar to peaches; let stand 30 minutes. Soften gelatin in cold water, dissolve in hot water and cool. Add peach mixture, lemon juice and salt. Chill until partially set. Fold in whipped cream. Pour into pie shell (graham cracker); chill thoroughly.

title: Grandma Mary'S Butter Cookies
text: Grandma Mary'S Butter Cookies. Ingredients: 1 c. sweet butter; 1 c. granulated sugar; 3 egg yolks; 2 1/2 c. sifted flour; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Cream butter. Beat into sugar. Add egg yolks and vanilla. Beat well after adding each yolk. Add flour and beat after each 1/2 cup is added. Chill about 1 hour.

title: Old-Time Biscuit Pudding
text: Old-Time Biscuit Pudding. Ingredients: 6 to 8 baked biscuits; milk (enough to make batter); 1/4 c. butter; 1/2 c. sugar; 1 egg, beaten; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Soak biscuits in milk, making a thin batter. Add remainder of ingredients. Mix thoroughly. Bake at 400° until done.

title: Fast Strawberry Mousse
text: Fast Strawberry Mousse. Ingredients: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen sweetened strawberries; 1/2 pt. sour cream; 1/2 c. sugar; 1 tsp. vanilla; 2 lb. walnut meats, chopped fine in blender; 1 c. sugar; 1 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. nutmeg; 1 lb. melted butter; 1 lb. filo dough (tissue thin pastry sheets; can be purchased frozen). Directions: Chop walnuts and mix with sugar and spices. Grease an 11 x 16-inch pan with melted butter. Place 4 filo in pan, brushing each with melted butter. Sprinkle with thin layer of nut mixture. Cover with 3 filo, brushing each with melted butter. Continue this procedure until all filo and mixture is used. Finish with 4 filo on top, brushing each with butter. Cut into about 75 small squares (before baking). Bake in slow oven (300°) for 1 hour or longer, until lightly brown. Remove from oven and cool. Pour warm syrup over pastry.

title: Family Favorite Chip Dip
text: Family Favorite Chip Dip. Ingredients: 1 (5 oz.) jar Kraft sharp cheese or 2/3 c. Kraft cold pack cheese; 8 oz. cream cheese; 1/4 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1 Tbsp. yellow mustard; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. mayonnaise or Miracle Whip. Directions: Have cheeses at room temperature.

title: Homemade Pimento Cheese
text: Homemade Pimento Cheese. Ingredients: 2 to 3 lb. Kraft hunk cheese, grated; 1 large jar chopped pimentos; 1 to 2 tsp. vinegar; salt to taste; pepper to taste; 4 to 5 Tbsp. Duke mayonnaise. Directions: Grate cheese. Chop up pimentos small and add in with 1/2 of juice with cheese. Add vinegar, salt, pepper and mayonnaise. Beat with a mixer until smooth. If not smooth, add mayonnaise.

title: Oven Caramel Popcorn
text: Oven Caramel Popcorn. Ingredients: 2 c. brown sugar; 2 cubes margarine; 6 qt. popped corn; 1/2 c. corn syrup; 1 tsp. soda. Directions: Boil sugar, margarine, syrup and salt for 5 minutes (stir). Remove from heat and add soda and vanilla. Stir well over popped corn (add nuts if desired). Spread on deep cookie sheet or shallow roaster. Place in 200° oven for 1 hour stirring at 15 minute intervals. Remove from oven, cool and store in tightly covered containers.

title: Vegetable Manicotti
text: Vegetable Manicotti. Ingredients: 4 uncooked manicotti shells; 1/2 c. zucchini; 1/2 c. broccoli; 1 (2.5 oz.) jar mushrooms, drained; 1/4 c. Parmesan cheese; 2 Tbsp. chopped green pepper; 1/2 c. spaghetti sauce; 1 1/2 oz. (1/3 c.) shredded Mozzarella cheese. Directions: Microwave shells to desired doneness. In small bowl combine zucchini, broccoli, mushrooms, Parmesan cheese and green pepper. Mix well and fill each shell with 1/2 cup of mixture. Place in 2-quart casserole dish. Pour spaghetti sauce over shells. Cover with wax paper and microwave on High 4 to 5 minutes, turning dish 1/4 turn halfway through cooking. Sprinkle with cheese and microwave uncovered on High 1 to 2 minutes until cheese is melted. Makes 2 servings.

title: Chicken Corn Soup
text: Chicken Corn Soup. Ingredients: wings, back, giblets and upper legs of a fryer; 3 potatoes; 1 large onion; 1 pt. corn; 3 ribs celery; few flakes crushed red pepper; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Cover chicken with water, salt and pepper and cook until done. Take chicken out of broth to cool. Chop potatoes, onion and celery. Add to the chicken broth along with the corn. Add crushed pepper flakes. Cook until done. Pick chicken off bones and add to soup. This can be used as is or thicken with rice, noodles or rivels. If you use any of these, cook again until done.

title: "Sean'S Favorite" Cranberry Pie
text: "Sean'S Favorite" Cranberry Pie. Ingredients: 1 1/4 c. cranberries; 1 apple, chunked; 1/4 c. brown sugar; 1/4 c. chopped walnuts; 1 egg; 1/2 c. sugar; 1/2 c. flour; 1/3 c. melted butter. Directions: Grease a 9-inch pie plate. Layer with cranberries and apple. Sprinkle with brown sugar and nuts. Beat egg until thick; slowly add 1/2 cup of sugar, flour and melted butter until blended well. Pour egg mixture over cranberry mixture. Bake in a 350° oven for 45 minutes. Serve with vanilla ice cream or whipped cream.

title: Diabetic Applesauce Cookies
text: Diabetic Applesauce Cookies. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. flour; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. nutmeg; 1/2 tsp. cloves; 1/2 c. butter; 2 eggs; 2 Tbsp. food sweetener; 1 c. applesauce; 1/2 c. raisins; 1 c. oatmeal; 1 tsp. baking soda; 1/3 c. nuts. Directions: Sift together flour, salt, spices and soda. Mix shortening, sweetener and eggs; beat until light and fluffy. Add flour and applesauce alternately, mixing well after each addition. Fold in raisins and oatmeal. Drop by level tablespoons onto greased cookie sheet 1 inch apart. Bake at 375° for 20 minutes or until golden brown.

title: Betty'S Glazed Ham
text: Betty'S Glazed Ham. Ingredients: 1 picnic ham; 3 c. water; 1 c. brown sugar; 6 Tbsp. mustard; 1 can pineapple rings. Directions: Bake ham in 3 cups water for 2 1/2 hours at 350°. Take ham out of water.

title: Amber'S Top Ramenlubbock, Texas
text: Amber'S Top Ramenlubbock, Texas. Ingredients: 1 pkg. Top Ramen - any flavor cuz I like them all!. Directions: Take the package of Top Ramen and smash it till it's in small pieces. Cut open the package and put it in a microwave bowl. Empty seasoning into the bowl too. Add enough water to cover the noodles and microwave for 1 1/2 minutes. Stir and microwave for another 1 1/2 minutes. It's done. My Nan adds salsa to hers but I don't.

title: Broiled Scallops
text: Broiled Scallops. Ingredients: 1 lb. scallops; 2 Tbsp. butter; 1 Tbsp. oil; 1 clove garlic, chopped; black pepper to taste; Parmesan cheese; lemon slices. Directions: Melt butter in saucepan. Saute garlic. Place scallops in oiled baking dish; pour butter over them. Sprinkle salt, pepper and cheese over scallops and broil 6 to 8 minutes. Serve with lemon slices.

title: Broccoli Noodle Bake
text: Broccoli Noodle Bake. Ingredients: 1/4 c. margarine; 1/2 c. chopped onions; 1/2 c. milk; 1 (4 oz.) can sliced mushrooms, drained; 2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen chopped broccoli; 2 c. grated sharp cheese; 2 (10 3/4 oz.) cans cream of mushroom soup; 1 green pepper, chopped; 1 c. chopped celery; 8 or 9 oz. spaghetti. Directions: Melt margarine in large saucepan; saute onion, green pepper and celery. Put broccoli on top; cover and simmer until broccoli is thawed, cook spaghetti according to directions. In a bowl combine soup and milk. In a buttered 3-quart casserole dish layer spaghetti, vegetables, mushrooms, soup and 1 1/2 cups cheese. Sprinkle remaining cheese on top. Bake at 350° for 35 to 45 minutes or until hot and bubbling. Serves 10.

title: Pizza Pie
text: Pizza Pie. Ingredients: 1 refrigerated pizza crust; 2 c. shredded Mozzarella cheese; 1/2 c. pizza sauce; 1 pkg. (smaller 1 3/4 oz.) pepperoni; grated Parmesan cheese. Directions: Preheat oven to 400°. Spray 8-inch round cake pan with nonstick spray. Unroll dough; form 14 x 10-inch rectangle. Place in sprayed pan with dough extending evenly over sides of pan. Press dough in bottom and up sides of pan. Sprinkle 1 cup cheese over dough. Spread pizza sauce over cheese. Top with pepperoni and remaining Mozzarella cheese. Cut 2-inch cut at each corner of dough. Bring all ends at center together. Twist to secure at top of pizza. Sprinkle with Parmesan. Bake at 400° for 20 to 25 minutes.

title: Scott'S Sour Cream Biscuits
text: Scott'S Sour Cream Biscuits. Ingredients: 2 sticks butter, melted; 2 c. self-rising flour; 1 c. sour cream. Directions: Mix all ingredients and spoon in miniature muffin pan. Bake at 350° for 15 minutes.

title: Carrots Supreme
text: Carrots Supreme. Ingredients: 2 lb. carrots, peeled and sliced; 1/2 medium onion, diced; 1/2 green pepper, diced; 1 c. tomato soup, undiluted; 1/2 c. sugar; 1/4 c. cider vinegar; 1/4 c. vegetable oil; 1/2 tsp. dry mustard; 1/2 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper. Directions: Cook carrots in a small amount of water until just barely tender; drain. Combine remaining ingredients and pour over carrots. Refrigerate for several hours or overnight.

title: Pork Tenderloin(Marinated And Stuffed With Spinach)
text: Pork Tenderloin(Marinated And Stuffed With Spinach). Ingredients: oil; garlic; salt & pepper; Worcestershire sauce; soy sauce; brown sugar; honey. Directions: Blend all ingredients together well. Marinate tenderloin in sauce and turn several times. Marinate overnight. Cut tenderloin lengthwise. Then fill with spinach. Roll and tie together. Saute tenderloin on high heat for just a few minutes to seer in juices. Rub tenderloin with mustard thoroughly. Roll in bread crumbs and Romano cheese. Place in pyrex dish and bake to your desired doneness. Cut into 1/2 inch slices. Serve with baked potato or rice and a favorite vegetable.

title: Sweet Pickle Chips
text: Sweet Pickle Chips. Ingredients: 6 lb. small cucumbers; 1 large sweet red pepper, chopped; 1 onion, sliced and separated into rings; 2 cloves garlic; 3/4 c. pickling salt; 2 qt. crushed ice; 4 c. sugar; 3 1/2 c. vinegar (5% acidity); 2 Tbsp. mustard seed. Directions: Wash cucumbers; trim ends. Cut cucumbers into 1/4-inch slices. Combine cucumber slices, red pepper, onion and garlic in a large bowl; add salt and stir well. Pack crushed ice over cucumbers; cover and let stand 24 hours. Drain well. Rinse cucumber mixture several times in cold water; drain well. Remove and discard garlic. Pack cucumber mixture into hot, sterilized jars, leaving 1/2 inch headspace.

title: Pasta Salad (With Fruit)
text: Pasta Salad (With Fruit). Ingredients: 1 (1 lb.) box small pasta; 1 can crushed pineapple (large); 1 can mandarin oranges (small, 11 oz.); 1 can fruit salad (large); 2 eggs; 3 Tbsp. flour; 10 oz. Cool Whip. Directions: Boil pasta 10 minutes; drain and cool. Drain fruits, saving juice. Add to juice the eggs and flour and cook until thick. Add to pasta. Fold in fruit. Before serving, add about 10 ounces Cool Whip.

title: Nadine Krall'S Pork Chops And Rice
text: Nadine Krall'S Pork Chops And Rice. Ingredients: pork chops; Minute rice; onion soup mix. Directions: Brown chops in frypan. Pour onion soup mix and enough water over chops to keep chops covered. Cook for 1/2 hour. Add Minute rice and simmer. For two people, use 1/2 cup rice.

title: Barbecued Franks
text: Barbecued Franks. Ingredients: 1 lb. hot dogs; 1/2 lb. ground beef; 1/3 c. chopped green pepper; 1/2 c. chopped onion; 2 Tbsp. margarine; 1 c. tomato soup; 1 Tbsp. brown sugar; 1 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1 Tbsp. vinegar; 1 Tbsp. prepared mustard; dash of hot pepper sauce. Directions: In skillet, brown ground beef and hot dogs in margarine. Add green pepper and onion. Stir in remaining ingredients. Cover; cook over low heat for 20 minutes. Stir. Serve in hot dog buns and spoon sauce over.

title: Amaretto Cream
text: Amaretto Cream. Ingredients: 1 envelope unflavored gelatin; 1/4 c. cold water; 3 eggs; 1/3 c. sugar; 1/4 c. Amaretto liqueur; 1 c. whipping cream; 1/4 c. sliced almonds; sliced strawberries (for garnish; optional); mint leaves (for garnish). Directions: In a 1 to 2-quart pan, sprinkle gelatin over water; let stand for 5 minutes to soften. Heat over medium heat for 30 seconds or until mixture begins to simmer. Remove from heat. In a small mixer bowl, beat eggs on high speed for 2 minutes or until slightly thick. Slowly beat in sugar, liqueur and gelatin. In a large mixer bowl, beat cream until it mounds softly. Add egg mixture and fold in to blend. Pour into a 1-quart mold or into individual serving dishes. Refrigerate for 3 hours or until mold is set. Unmold onto serving plate or serve from individual dishes. Garnish with fresh sliced strawberries and mint, if available. Sprinkle servings with toasted almonds.

title: Jello Plum Pudding
text: Jello Plum Pudding. Ingredients: 1 pkg. (3 oz.) Lemon Jello; 1/4 tsp. Cinnamon; 1/4 tsp. Cloves; 1 c. Boiling Water; 1/2 c. Cold Water; 3/4 c. Grape-Nuts Cereal; 3/4 c. Cooked Chopped Prunes; 3/4 c. Chopped Candied Peel (no citron); 3/4 c. Chopped Nuts (optional). Directions: Dissolve gelatin dessert mix in boiling water. Stir in cold water and spices. Chill until very thick. Fold in the remaining ingredients and chill until firm. Good with hard sauce or whipped cream.

title: No-Roll Pie Crust
text: No-Roll Pie Crust. Ingredients: 2 c. flour; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. vegetable oil; 1/4 c. cold milk. Directions: Mix flour and salt in bowl. Mix vegetable oil and milk in measuring cup. Add liquid to flour mixture and cut with pastry knife or stir lightly with fork. Press onto bowl or pie pan to fit; flute edges. Do not roll.

title: Coconut-Pineapple Pie
text: Coconut-Pineapple Pie. Ingredients: 3 eggs; 1/4 c. plus 2 Tbsp. butter or margarine, melted; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 1 c. flaked coconut; 3 Tbsp. all-purpose flour; 1 (15 1/4 oz.) can crushed pineapple, drained; 1 unbaked (9-inch) deep dish pastry shell. Directions: Beat eggs at medium speed with electric mixer until thick and lemon colored. Add butter and next 4 ingredients. Mix well. Pour into pastry shell. Bake at 350° for 50 minutes or until filling is set. Yields one 9-inch pie.

title: Sweet And Sour Chicken Stir-Fry
text: Sweet And Sour Chicken Stir-Fry. Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) can pineapple chunks in own juice; 1 Tbsp. vegetable oil; 2 half boneless chicken breasts, skinned and cubed; 1/4 to 1/2 tsp. red pepper flakes; 1 (16 oz.) can cut green beans, drained; 3/4 c. sweet and sour sauce. Directions: Drain pineapple; reserve 1/4 cup juice. In a wok or skillet, heat oil over medium heat. Add chicken; cook 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Season with salt and pepper, if desired. Stir in reserved juice and red pepper. Reduce heat uncovered 2 minutes. Add green beans, pineapple and sauce. Cover and cook until heated through. Serve over hot cooked rice. Makes 4 servings.

title: Baked Bluefish Fillets With Creole Sauce
text: Baked Bluefish Fillets With Creole Sauce. Ingredients: 1/4 c. salad oil; 2 stalks celery, sliced; 1/2 c. diced onions; 1 c. diced green bell pepper; 2 cloves garlic, minced; 12 oz. tomato paste; 24 oz. College Inn chicken broth; 1 bay leaf; 1 tsp. basil; 1 tsp. oregano; black pepper to taste; 1 Tbsp. fresh chopped parsley; 1 c. mushrooms, sliced; bluefish fillets. Directions: Mix the first 5 ingredients together in a 2-quart pan and saute together for 10 minutes over medium heat. Add remaining ingredients, all but bluefish fillets and mix well over medium-low heat until vegetables are fully cooked, about 15 minutes. While this is cooking, bake bluefish fillets in a 400° oven until fully cooked, approximately 30 minutes. When fish is cooked, portion on plates and top with Creole sauce.

title: Great Grandma'S Sugar Cookies
text: Great Grandma'S Sugar Cookies. Ingredients: 2 sticks butter or margarine; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 2 eggs; 1/2 tsp. nutmeg; 1/2 tsp. vanilla extract; 1/2 tsp. almond extract; 3 1/4 c. flour. Directions: Cream butter and sugar until fluffy; add eggs, nutmeg, vanilla and almond extracts. Blend well. Gradually add in flour. Chill in fridge for at least 1 hour or until dough is stiff. Spread out and use cookie cutters to cut out shapes. Bake on ungreased cookie sheet at 400° until you think they are done. Bake longer for crispy cookies and shorter time for softer cookies.

title: Cold Tomato - Yogurt Soup
text: Cold Tomato - Yogurt Soup. Ingredients: 2 1/2 lb. ripe tomatoes, peeled, seeded and chopped; 2 c. plain yogurt; 1 clove garlic, pressed; 1/4 tsp. celery salt; 1/4 tsp. curry powder; juice of 1 lemon; 1 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. freshly ground pepper; plain yogurt and minced fresh parsley (garnish). Directions: Puree tomatoes in processor or blender. Add remaining ingredients except garnish and mix until smooth. Taste and adjust seasoning. Pour into bowl, cover and chill well. Garnish each serving with yogurt and minced parsley.

title: Green Tomato Pie(Great Grandma'S)
text: Green Tomato Pie(Great Grandma'S). Ingredients: green tomatoes, diced (about as many tomatoes as you would apples); 1 c. sugar; nutmeg; butter; 2 Tbsp. cornstarch; 1 Tbsp. vinegar. Directions: Slice (or dice) green tomatoes ahead so juice runs off and pie won't be soggy.

title: Peanut Butter Cup Pie(Lite Choices)
text: Peanut Butter Cup Pie(Lite Choices). Ingredients: 1 1/2 oz. Rice Krispies; 4 Tbsp. peanut butter; 1 Tbsp. honey; 1 pkg. chocolate sugar-free cooked pudding; 2 c. skim milk; 1 medium banana, sliced (optional). Directions: Mix together first 3 ingredients. Press crust ingredients into a 9-inch pie plate; chill.

title: Artichokes And Cheese
text: Artichokes And Cheese. Ingredients: 1 c. mayonnaise; 1 small can diced green chilies; 1 (8 1/2 oz.) can artichoke hearts, drained and chopped; 1 c. Parmesan cheese, grated. Directions: Mix all together. Bake in glass dish at 350° until golden brown, about 10 to 15 minutes. Serve with tortilla chips - El Grande Chips.

title: Sunset Cherry Pie
text: Sunset Cherry Pie. Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese, softened; 1/2 c. powdered sugar; 1 (21 oz.) can cherry pie filling; 1 (8 oz.) can pineapple tidbits, well drained; 1 (8 oz.) container frozen whipped topping, thawed; 1 (9-inch) graham cracker crumb crust. Directions: Combine cream cheese and sugar with electric mixer, mixing until well blended. Stir in 1 cup pie filling and 1/4 cup pineapple. Fold whipped topping into cream cheese mixture. Pour into crust. Top with remaining pie filling and pineapple. Chill until firm. Yields 6 to 8 servings.

title: Nut Cups
text: Nut Cups. Ingredients: 2 c. flour; 2 sticks margarine; 6 oz. cream cheese; 1 1/2 c. light brown sugar; 2 eggs, well beaten; 2 tsp. vanilla; pinch of salt; 2 Tbsp. melted margarine; 1 1/2 c. chopped walnuts. Directions: Mix flour, margarine and cream cheese and refrigerate. Mix brown sugar, eggs, vanilla, salt and margarine. Cook over low heat. Watch closely as it burns easily. Add walnuts. Roll out dough 1/8-inch thick. Cut with a scalloped cookie cutter. Press into 1-inch muffin tin. Drop 1 teaspoon of filling into each cup about 3/4 full. They will swell. Bake at 325° for 20 to 25 minutes. Avoid overbrowning.

title: Hornet'S Nest Cake
text: Hornet'S Nest Cake. Ingredients: 1 (3.4 oz.) pkg. vanilla instant pudding mix; 2 c. whole milk; 1 pkg. plain yellow cake mix; 1 (11 oz.) pkg. butterscotch chips; 1 c. chopped pecans. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Lightly mist a 13 x 9-inch baking pan with vegetable oil spray. Place pudding mix and milk in a large bowl and blend according to package directions. Fold in the dry cake mix and stir with a wooden spoon until the batter is combined, although still a little lumpy. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and smooth top with rubber spatula. Scatter the butterscotch chips on top of batter, then sprinkle pecans on top of chips. Bake until cake springs back when lightly pressed with finger, 35 to 40 minutes. Let cool for 30 minutes before serving.

title: Pork Chop Dinner Bake
text: Pork Chop Dinner Bake. Ingredients: 6 (1-inch) thick pork chops; oil for browning chops; 3 large potatoes, thinly sliced; 2 large onions, thinly sliced; salt and pepper to taste; 1 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1 can cream of mushroom soup; 1 c. milk. Directions: Heat oil in heavy skillet and brown chops on both sides. Butter a large baking dish which has a well fitting cover. Arrange potatoes and onions in layers, seasoning with salt and pepper. Place browned pork chops on top. Salt and pepper them. Mix Worcestershire sauce, soup and milk and pour over all ingredients. Cover tightly and bake at 375° for 50 minutes or until chops are very tender. Remove lid for the last few minutes for browning. Yield: 6 servings.

title: Clam Sauce For Vermicelli
text: Clam Sauce For Vermicelli. Ingredients: 2 flat cans clams (1 minced and 1 chopped); 1 Tbsp. dried parsley; 1/4 c. butter; 1/4 c. olive oil; 1 tsp. Italian herb seasoning; 2 cloves garlic, minced or powder equivalent; a shake of coarse pepper; 1/2 c. Parmesan cheese, grated. Directions: In a saucepan, combine all ingredients. (Do not drain clams.) Gently heat until very hot, but not boiling (makes clams tough). Makes enough sauce for 8 ounces prepared vermicelli. Pass extra cheese when serving. Serve with a simple tossed salad and French bread slices, toasted and buttered.

title: Broiled Spareribs
text: Broiled Spareribs. Ingredients: 3 lb. spareribs; 3 c. water; 1 c. cider vinegar; 1 clove garlic, minced; 1 large onion, chopped; 1/2 c. butter or oleo; 1 bay leaf; 1 1/2 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/2 tsp. Tabasco sauce; 1/4 c. sugar; 1/4 c. ketchup; 1 Tbsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper. Directions: Broil spareribs in broiler oven until golden brown on both sides. Meanwhile, combine remaining ingredients in large pan and boil for 10 minutes uncovered. Place ribs in uncovered roaster pan and pour some of the sauce over them. Bake in a hot 450° oven for 1 hour basting every 10 minutes with rest of sauce which should be kept boiling. Serves 5.

title: Kathy'S Chocolate Chip Cookies
text: Kathy'S Chocolate Chip Cookies. Ingredients: 3 1/3 c. flour; 1 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 3/4 c. butter or margarine; 3/4 c. Crisco shortening; 1 c. firmly packed brown sugar; 3 eggs; 1/2 c. sugar; 1 (6 serving size) pkg. instant vanilla pudding; 1 1/2 tsp. vanilla; 3 c. chocolate chips. Directions: Cream shortening and sugars. Add pudding mix, vanilla and eggs. Beat well. Stir in flour and soda, mixed. Add chips. Bake on ungreased sheets for 8 to 10 minutes at 375°.

title: Canjica(Corn Pudding)
text: Canjica(Corn Pudding). Ingredients: 1 (1 lb.) pkg. white corn, dried; 1/2 c. sugar; 1 bottle coconut milk; 2 c. milk; cinnamon. Directions: Cook the corn as you would dried beans or peas until tender (20 minutes in the pressure cooker is one way). Don't use a lot of water. After the corn is done, add other ingredients. (You may need more milk, coconut and sugar, depending on how the corn comes out.) Before serving, sprinkle with cinnamon. The texture should be creamy, but lumpy and not too thick. Serve warm.

title: Beefy Spanish Rice
text: Beefy Spanish Rice. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; 1 medium onion, chopped; 1 green pepper, chopped; 1 garlic clove, minced; 1 (14 1/2 oz.) can stewed tomatoes; 1 1/2 c. water; 1 c. uncooked long grain rice; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 to 1 tsp. chili powder; 1/2 tsp. dried thyme; 1/4 tsp. dried basil; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 2 Tbsp. tomato paste; French bread. Directions: In a large skillet, cook ground beef, onion, green pepper and garlic until the meat is browned; drain. Stir in the next eight ingredients; bring to a boil. Reduce heat; cover and simmer for 20 minutes or until the rice is tender. Stir in tomato paste and heat through. Serve with French bread. Yield: 4 to 6 servings.

title: Popcorn Ball Clowns
text: Popcorn Ball Clowns. Ingredients: 1 c. butter; 1 (1 lb.) pkg. marshmallows; 2 c. popped popcorn for each clown; pointed ice cream cones (1 for each clown); large gumdrops; chocolate coated candies (M&M's). Directions: You will also need 8-inch paper doilies (1 for each clown) and plastic foam cups (1 for each clown).

title: Cajun Meat Loaf
text: Cajun Meat Loaf. Ingredients: 1/4 stick butter; 1/2 large onion, chopped; 1/2 c. chopped bell pepper; 1 tsp. salt; 3/4 tsp. cayenne pepper; 1/2 tsp. dried thyme; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 1/4 tsp. ground cumin; 1 lb. lean ground beef; 1 egg, beaten; 1/2 c. fine dry bread crumbs; 1/2 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/2 c. ketchup. Directions: Preheat oven to 375°. Melt butter in a skillet over medium-low heat. Add next 7 ingredients and cook until veggies are tender, stirring frequently (about 10 minutes).

title: Sandies
text: Sandies. Ingredients: 1 c. butter (margarine); 1/3 c. sugar; 2 tsp. vanilla; 2 c. all-purpose flour; 1 c. chopped pecans; 1/4 c. powdered sugar. Directions: Beat butter for 30 seconds; add granulated sugar and beat until fluffy. Add vanilla and 2 teaspoons water, beat well. Stir in flour and pecans. Shape into 1-inch balls or 1 1/2 x 1/2-inch fingers. Place on ungreased cookie sheet. Bake in 325° oven for 20 minutes. Cool completely. Gently shake a few cookies at a time in a bag with powdered sugar. Yields 36.

title: Chicken Creole
text: Chicken Creole. Ingredients: 1/4 c. vegetable oil; 1/4 c. all-purpose flour; 1 green pepper, chopped; 1 medium onion, chopped; 2 c. chopped cooked chicken; 1 (28 oz.) can tomatoes (stewed); 2 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 2 or 3 cloves garlic, minced; 1 tsp. soy sauce; 1 tsp. sugar; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 1/4 tsp. salt; 3 dashes hot sauce; hot cooked rice. Directions: Heat vegetable oil in a heavy skillet until hot. Stir in 1/4 cup flour. Stir constantly until mixture is about the color of a copper penny, about 5 minutes. Reduce heat. Add pepper and onion; cook until tender (about 10 minutes), stirring constantly. Add chicken, tomatoes, Worcestershire sauce, parsley, garlic, soy sauce, sugar, pepper, salt and hot sauce. Cover and simmer about 20 minutes. Serve over rice.

title: Dove Load Burritos
text: Dove Load Burritos. Ingredients: 36 dove breast, boned and diced; 1/2 lb. butter; 1 large purple onion, diced; lemon pepper to taste; 1-1/2 lbs. potatoes, diced; garlic salt to taste; 1 (16 oz.) jar salsa; 4 oz. can chili peppers, diced; 12 large flour tortillas; 1 lb. monterey jack cheese, shredded; green taco sauce. Directions: In large skillet over medium heat, cook dove meat and onion in 1/2 the butter; season with lemon pepper. Cook for 10 minutes; set aside. In same skillet melt remaining butter and fry potatoes adding lemon pepper and garlic salt to taste. Place meat in with potatoes; adding salsa and chili peppers. Simmer 15 minutes; stirring frequently. Spoon into warm tortillas; cover with cheese and green taco sauce. Fold and serve.

title: Oven Omelet
text: Oven Omelet. Ingredients: 3/4 c. chopped ham; 1/2 lb. Cheddar cheese, cubed; 1/2 c. green pepper; 1/2 tsp. salt; 6 or 8 eggs (even more can be used); 1 c. milk; 1 (4 oz.) can mushrooms; 12 crackers, crushed. Directions: Mix all ingredients and pour into a buttered 9 or 12-inch pan, depending on how many eggs are being used. Bake at 350° for 40 to 45 minutes.

title: Smoked Salmon Log
text: Smoked Salmon Log. Ingredients: 1 lb. can salmon, drained; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese; 1 Tbsp. lemon juice; 2 tsp. grated onion; 1 tsp. prepared horseradish; 1 tsp. liquid smoke (comes in bottle); 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. chopped pecans; 2 Tbsp. chopped parsley. Directions: Mix first 7 ingredients together and chill. Then form into log shape. Roll/coat "log" in pecan/parsley mixture. Chill several hours. Serve with crackers.

title: Bacon Layered Salad
text: Bacon Layered Salad. Ingredients: 2 small heads lettuce; 6 to 8 boiled eggs, chopped; 1 lb. bacon, crumbled; 4 oz. Swiss cheese, grated; 1 c. mayonnaise; salt; pepper; sugar to taste. Directions: Layer lettuce in the bottom of a large salad bowl (glass makes very pretty salad). Sprinkle sugar, salt and pepper over lettuce. Slice or chop eggs on top of lettuce. Layer lettuce again. Sprinkle with salt, sugar and pepper. Add bacon (crumbled) layer. On top of bacon, add layer of Swiss cheese. Spread 1 cup mayonnaise on top of cheese. Can sit overnight for more flavor.

title: Cloverleaf Rolls
text: Cloverleaf Rolls. Ingredients: 3 Tbsp. sugar; 2 1/2 tsp. salt; 2 pkg. active dry yeast; 6 c. all-purpose flour; 1 1/2 c. water; 1/2 c. milk; 1/2 c. butter. Directions: In large bowl combine sugar, salt, yeast and 2 cups flour. In 1-quart saucepan over low heat, heat water, milk and 3 tablespoons butter until very warm (120° to 130°). With mixer on low speed, gradually beat liquid into dry ingredients just until blended. Increase speed to medium; beat 2 minutes, occasionally scraping sides. Beat in 1 cup flour to make a thick batter. Continue beating 2 minutes with a wooden spoon. Stir in 2 1/2 cups flour to make a soft dough. Turn dough onto lightly floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes. Work in about 1/2 cup of flour while kneading. Shape dough into a ball; place in greased large bowl, then turn dough over so that the top is greased. Cover bowl with towel and let rise in warm place away from drafts about 1 hour, until size is doubled.

title: Velvety Homemade Pumpkin Pie
text: Velvety Homemade Pumpkin Pie. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. mashed pumpkin; 3/4 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. sugar; 1/2 c. brown sugar; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1 tsp. nutmeg; 3/4 tsp. ginger; 2 eggs; 1 c. evaporated milk; 1 unbaked pie shell (9-inch deep will do). Directions: Cook 1/2 fresh pumpkin in oven. Remove seeds to use later in a snack. Cook until soft. Scoop out and mash. Mix sugar and seasonings with mashed pumpkin. Add eggs, one at a time. Add evaporated milk. Place pumpkin pie mixture into unbaked homemade pie shell. Bake at 450° and cook for 10 minutes. Lower heat to 350° and bake for about another 40 minutes or until a knife inserted comes out clean.

title: Chili Beans
text: Chili Beans. Ingredients: 1 lb. dry beans (pinto, kidney or October beans or a combination); 1 qt. stewed tomatoes; 1 c. dry barley; 1 large onion, diced; 1 green pepper, diced; 4 stalks celery, diced; 6 oz. tomato paste; 2 Tbsp. paprika; 2 tsp. dry mustard; 2 tsp. salt; 1 Tbsp. raw honey; 3 bay leaves. Directions: Cook beans and barley according to package directions in large pot with onion, pepper and celery. Add stewed tomatoes, tomato paste and remaining ingredients. Add beans and simmer 45 minutes. Serve hot or cold.

title: Lemon Lush
text: Lemon Lush. Ingredients: 1 stick margarine; 1 cup flour; 1/2 cup crushed pecans; 8 oz. cream cheese, softened; 1 cup powdered sugar; 1 cup cool whip; 2 small pkg. instant lemon pudding; 1/3 cup lemon juice; 2 cups + 2 Tbsp. cold milk; cool whip for topping. Directions: Mix together first 3 ingredients and press in 9x13 pan. Bake at 350 degrees about 15-20 minutes. Set aside to cool.

title: Peanut Butter And Banana Cookies(Diabetic)
text: Peanut Butter And Banana Cookies(Diabetic). Ingredients: 1/4 c. butter or margarine; 1/2 c. mashed ripe banana (about 2 medium bananas); 1/2 c. no-sugar-added natural peanut butter; 1 egg; 1/4 c. thawed frozen unsweetened apple juice concentrate; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 c. all-purpose flour; 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. chopped salted peanuts; whole peanuts (optional). Directions: Preheat oven to 375°. Beat butter in large bowl until creamy. Add banana and peanut butter; beat until smooth. Blend in egg, apple juice concentrate and vanilla. Beat in flour, baking soda and salt. Stir in chopped peanuts. Drop rounded tablespoonfuls of dough, 2 inches apart, onto lightly greased cookie sheets. Top each with one peanut, if desired. Bake for 8 minutes, or until set. Cool completely on wire rack. Store in tightly covered container. Makes 2 dozen.

title: Southern Stuffed Peppers
text: Southern Stuffed Peppers. Ingredients: 6 large green peppers; 1/2 c. chopped celery; 1 lb. ground beef; 3 c. cooked rice; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 2 chicken bouillon cubes; 1/2 c. chopped onion; 1 glove garlic; 1 (4 oz.) can mushroom slices; 1 tsp. salt; 1 can mushroom soup. Directions: Cut thin slice from stem end of peppers. Remove seed and ribs. Parboil peppers in boiling water for 2 to 3 minutes and then drain.

title: Shoe Peg Corn Salad
text: Shoe Peg Corn Salad. Ingredients: 2 (16 oz.) cans Shoe Peg corn, drained; 1 (2 oz.) jar chopped pimento, drained; 1/2 c. chopped green pepper; 1/2 c. chopped onion; 2 stalks celery, chopped; 1/2 c. sugar; 1/2 c. vegetable oil; 1/2 c. vinegar; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. black pepper. Directions: Mix corn, pimento, green pepper, onion and celery. Combine sugar, oil, vinegar, salt and black pepper. Pour over vegetables. Cover. Chill overnight. Drain well before serving. Makes 8 servings.

title: Chocolate Cream Cheese Cake
text: Chocolate Cream Cheese Cake. Ingredients: 1 box devil's food cake mix; 1 pkg. vanilla instant pudding; 3 eggs; 1/2 c. oil; 1 c. water; 1/2 c. sugar; 1 (6 oz.) pkg. chocolate chips; 1 (8 oz.) cream cheese; 1 egg. Directions: Combine cake mix and pudding. Add 3 eggs, water and oil. Blend well.

title: Hearty Potatoes
text: Hearty Potatoes. Ingredients: 2 lb. frozen hash browns, thawed; 1 can cream of chicken soup; 1 lb. sour cream; 1 stick oleo, melted; 1 c. diced onions (optional); salt and pepper to taste; 1 c. crushed potato chips (optional); 8 oz. grated or shredded cheese. Directions: Mix ingredients together in a large bowl by hand. Spread in a 9 x 13-inch pan. Bake 1 hour at 325°.

title: Apple Kugel
text: Apple Kugel. Ingredients: 4 large Delicious apples; 1/2 c. matzo meal; 4 eggs, separated; 1/2 c. sugar; juice of 1/2 lemon; pinch of salt. Directions: Peel apples and grate fine. Beat egg yolks with sugar until lemon colored. Add lemon juice, apples and matzo meal. Fold in whites which have been stiffly beaten. Pour into greased casserole and bake at 350° for 40 minutes.

title: Danish Cucumbers
text: Danish Cucumbers. Ingredients: 3 large cucumbers; 1/3 c. white vinegar; 1 Tbsp. salt; 1 1/2 tsp. sugar; 1/4 tsp. pepper. Directions: Peel and slice cucumbers thin. Sprinkle with the salt. Let stand at least an hour or overnight. A plate should be placed on top of cucumbers and weighted down. Drain and rinse well. While they are draining, mix the vinegar, sugar, salt and pepper. Pour over the cucumbers and let them stand at least 2 hours. Serves 6.

title: Bean Recipe
text: Bean Recipe. Ingredients: 1 small pkg. navy beans; 1 can sauerkraut; 3 potatoes, cut up; garlic; salt and pepper; 1/2 onion, cut. Directions: Soak beans overnight. Then cook until tender. Add rest of ingredients.

title: Fran'S Broccoli Casserole
text: Fran'S Broccoli Casserole. Ingredients: 1 c. milk; 2 Tbsp. butter; 2 Tbsp. flour; 1/4 tsp. salt; 3 oz. softened cream cheese; 1/2 c. shredded Cheddar cheese; 2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen broccoli. Directions: In saucepan, melt butter; add flour, salt, milk, cream cheese and Cheddar cheese. Cook and stir until sauce boils. Remove from heat. Cook broccoli until crisp/tender. Combine broccoli and cheese sauce; pour into casserole dish.

title: Grapefruit And Crab Meat Cocktail
text: Grapefruit And Crab Meat Cocktail. Ingredients: 1 (No. 2) can grapefruit sections; 1 (6 1/2 oz.) can crab meat; 1 c. mayonnaise; 1 Tbsp. vinegar; 1 tsp. lemon juice; 2 Tbsp. catsup; 1 drop Tabasco sauce or Worcestershire. Directions: Chill grapefruit and crab meat in cans. Drain grapefruit. Flake crab meat, removing bony tissue. Alternate grapefruit and crab meat in cocktail glasses. Mix remaining ingredients. Pour over grapefruit and crab meat. Serves 8.

title: Citrus Frost
text: Citrus Frost. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. orange sherbet; 1 (6 oz.) can frozen tangerine juice concentrate; 4 ice cubes; 3 (12 oz.) cans lo-cal grapefruit carbonated beverage, chilled. Directions: In blender, combine orange sherbet, tangerine juice concentrate, ice cubes and 1 cup cold water. Cover; blend until smooth. Pour into 6 tall glasses. Pour carbonated beverage down sides of glasses. Stir gently. Makes 6 servings.

title: Ham Chowder
text: Ham Chowder. Ingredients: 4 carrots, diced; 4 stalks celery, diced; 1 onion, diced; 4 large potatoes; 5 c. water; 2 to 3 c. chopped ham; 2 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 6 Tbsp. flour; 2 1/2 c. milk. Directions: Cook vegetables until tender and blend flour into milk. Simmer 10 minutes. Garnish with paprika and parsley. Add salt to taste. Serve with garlic bread and tossed salad.

title: Twister Pie
text: Twister Pie. Ingredients: 1/2 of 12 oz. can strawberry, banana and orange Tropicana Twister, thawed; 1 (4 oz.) carton of Cool Whip; 1 (14 oz.) can sweetened condensed milk; 1 (9-inch) graham cracker crust. Directions: Combine Tropicana Twister, Cool Whip and condensed milk. Beat thoroughly. Pour into pie crust and chill. Serves 6.

title: Angel Food Chocolate Dessert
text: Angel Food Chocolate Dessert. Ingredients: 1 angel food cake, cut in 3 layers; 1 c. cold milk; 1/2 pt. whipping cream; 1 pkg. chocolate pudding (instant). Directions: Combine milk, cream and pudding in a large bowl. Beat with a rotary beater until well mixed (about 1 to 2 minutes). Spread between and on top layers of angel food cake.

title: Lemon Coffee Cake
text: Lemon Coffee Cake. Ingredients: 1 lemon cake mix; 1 box lemon instant pudding; 3/4 c. Crisco oil; 3/4 c. water; 4 eggs; 1/2 c. sugar; 1 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 3/4 c. chopped pecans (optional). Directions: Mix first 5 ingredients. Pour 1/2 of batter into a 10 x 12-inch greased baking dish. Mix sugar and cinnamon. Spread 1/2 over batter and 1/2 of nuts. Then add rest of batter, cinnamon and nuts. Bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes. You can use any flavor cake mix and instant pudding you like.

title: Homemade Ice Cream
text: Homemade Ice Cream. Ingredients: 1 gallon whole milk; 2 to 3 c. sugar; 3 eggs, beaten well; 1 can sweetened condensed milk; 1 tsp. or more vanilla. Directions: Add milk to the line in your churn. Add sugar, sweetened condensed milk, and vanilla. Mix well. Add the beaten eggs last and keep the paddle on the churn stirring until you get the freezer ready. This will prevent the eggs from settling to the bottom. Churn and enjoy!

title: Apricot Ice Cream
text: Apricot Ice Cream. Ingredients: 1 large can (No. 2 1/2) apricot halves; 2 oranges; 1 lemon; 12 oz. apricot nectar; 3 c. sugar; 1/2 pt. whipping cream; 2 c. milk. Directions: Blend apricots plus juice in blender. Squeeze oranges and lemon. Add juice to apricot mixture. Add remaining ingredients. Makes one gallon.

title: Vegetables Mexicali
text: Vegetables Mexicali. Ingredients: 2 tsp. vegetable oil; 3 to 4 medium zucchini, sliced (approximately 1 lb.); 1 medium white onion, chopped; 1 small green pepper, chopped; 4 eggs, slightly beaten; 2 (4 oz.) cans diced green chilies; 6 (6-inch) corn tortillas; 2 Tbsp. all-purpose flour; 1 c. sour cream; 3 c. shredded Monterey Jack cheese; salt, pepper and ground cumin to taste. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Heat oil in a large skillet. Add zucchini, onion and green pepper and saute for 5 minutes. Add eggs and stir until barely set. Remove skillet from heat; stir in chilies, salt, black pepper and cumin. In a small bowl, blend flour and sour cream. Arrange 1/2 of tortilla wedges in a shallow 2-quart casserole or 8-inch square baking dish. Add 1/2 of vegetable mixture, then 1/2 of sour cream mixture. Top with 1/2 of the cheese. Repeat layers ending with cheese. Bake uncovered for 30 minutes or until bubbly. Let stand for 5 to 10 minutes before serving.

title: Chili Rellenos Casserole
text: Chili Rellenos Casserole. Ingredients: 1 (7 oz.) can whole green chiles; 1 lb. Cheddar cheese, grated; 1/2 lb. Jack cheese, grated; 1 tall can evaporated milk; 4 eggs; 2 Tbsp. flour; 1/2 tsp. salt. Directions: Rinse chiles; remove seeds and place 1/2 of the chiles in a layer in a greased casserole dish. Sprinkle with half of both cheeses; add remaining chiles and top with remaining cheese. Beat eggs, flour, milk and salt together until smooth. Pour over chili-cheese layers. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes.

title: Buttery Toasted Rye Loaf
text: Buttery Toasted Rye Loaf. Ingredients: 1/3 c. margarine; 1 Tbsp. horseradish; 1 Tbsp. mustard; 1/4 tsp. garlic salt; 1 (1 lb.) round or oblong loaf rye bread (unsliced). Directions: Heat oven to 350°. Combine all ingredients, except bread; mix well. Slice loaf into 1/2-inch slices, cutting but not through bottom of loaf. Spread one side of each slice with margarine mixture. Wrap in foil; bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes.

title: Susie'S Chocolate Fudge Icing
text: Susie'S Chocolate Fudge Icing. Ingredients: 2 c. sugar; 6 Tbsp. cocoa; 2/3 c. evaporated milk; about 1 Tbsp. butter; pinch of salt. Directions: Mix first 3 ingredients and bring to boil, cooking until drops from spoon and barely holds its shape. Remove from heat and add butter and dash of salt. Bake 3 thin layers of cake and ice with mixture, poking holes in bottom layers so the fudge will soak through.

title: Jellied Apricot And Pineapple Salad
