text: Jellied Apricot And Pineapple Salad. Ingredients: 3 1/2 c. canned apricots, cut in 4 pieces; 2 1/2 c. crushed pineapple; 6 oz. pkg. orange jello; 2 c. boiling water; 1/2 c. apricot juice; 1/2 c. pineapple juice; 1 c. miniature marshmallows. Directions: Mix jello with fruit and marshmallows; jell and top with topping in 9 x 13 pan.

title: Possum Stew
text: Possum Stew. Ingredients: 1 road killed possum; 1 lb. potatoes; 4 carrots; 4 ears fresh cut corn-off-the-cob; 1 bag butter beans; 1 ripe red tomato; 4 dashes red wine; 1 Tbsp. salt; 1 Tbsp. pepper. Directions: Paw 'n the chillun' allus worked so hard a' catchin' little fish outta the creek that they just had to be cooked! ... but they iz sweet 'n gud eatin'. Scales, take insides out'n the haid off ... clean in cold watah ... salt 'n roll in 1/2 meal 'n 1/2 flour ... drop in hot fat 'n brown on both sides. To eat small bream ... furst pull fins off ... then start at the tail 'n ease meat up with a fork along backbone all the way up to whair the haid wuz ... hit'll cum off in one piece widout bones iffn you're careful.

title: Braised Beef Tips Over Rice
text: Braised Beef Tips Over Rice. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. shortening; 2 lb. beef tip, cut into 1-inch cubes; 1 (10 1/2 oz.) can condensed beef consomme; 1/3 c. red Burgundy wine; 2 Tbsp. soy sauce; 1 clove garlic, minced; 1/4 tsp. onion salt; 2 Tbsp. cornstarch; 1/4 c. water; 4 c. hot cooked rice. Directions: Note: Marinate meat in wine, soy sauce and crushed garlic overnight. Meat will be very tender.

title: Honey Glorified Rice
text: Honey Glorified Rice. Ingredients: 1 c. cold cooked rice; 1/3 c. honey; 1 1/2 c. crushed pineapple, drained; 1/2 tsp. vanilla; 1 c. whipped cream; 8 marshmallows, cut up. Directions: Whip the cream; beat in honey gradually. Stir in vanilla. Fold in remaining ingredients. Chill. Makes 6 to 8 servings.

title: Cornish Hens With Wild Rice Casserole
text: Cornish Hens With Wild Rice Casserole. Ingredients: Cornish hens; 1 c. onion; 1/4 c. butter; 1 c. brandy; 2 Tbsp. cornstarch; 1 pt. sour cream. Directions: Split hens (salt, pepper and paprika). Brown onion in butter and add hens. Brown hens and add brandy and simmer until done. Remove hens. Add cornstarch to thicken and sour cream. Heat; do not boil.

title: Apple Chop Suey
text: Apple Chop Suey. Ingredients: 1 egg, beaten; 1 c. sugar; 1 c. dates; 1 c. nut meats; 5 to 6 apples, sliced and peeled; 1 c. flour; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 1/4 tsp. salt. Directions: Mix first 5 ingredients and add next 4 ingredients. Using a 9 x 9-inch pan, bake in 350° oven for about 45 minutes. Top with Cool Whip. Serves 9.

title: Stuffed Burger Bundles
text: Stuffed Burger Bundles. Ingredients: 1 c. herb-seasoned stuffing mix; 1/3 c. evaporated milk; 1 lb. ground beef; 1 (10 1/2 oz.) can condensed cream of mushroom soup; 2 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1 Tbsp. catsup. Directions: Prepare stuffing according to package directions. Combine evaporated milk and meat; divide into 5 patties. On wax paper, pat each to 6-inch circle. Put 1/4 cup stuffing in center of each; draw meat over stuffing and seal. Place in 1 1/2-quart casserole. Combine remaining ingredients, pour over meat. Bake, uncovered, at 350° for 45 minutes. Makes 5 servings.

title: Apricot Angel Brownies
text: Apricot Angel Brownies. Ingredients: 2 bars white baking chocolate; 1/3 c. butter or margarine; 1/2 c. brown sugar; 2 eggs; 1/4 tsp. vanilla; 3/4 c. flour; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. baking powder; 1 c. finely chopped dried apricots; 1/4 c. sliced almonds; 1/4 c. flaked coconut. Directions: Melt white chocolate and butter and cool. In medium bowl mix brown sugar, eggs and vanilla. Add the chocolate mixture. Add salt and baking powder and mix well. Add apricots, almonds and coconut. Blend in the flour and mix thoroughly. Pour into greased 9-inch pan. Bake at 350° for 25 minutes.

title: Luscious Lemon Bars
text: Luscious Lemon Bars. Ingredients: 3/4 c. (1 1/2 sticks) margarine; 3/4 c. sugar; 1 egg; 2 tsp. vanilla; 2 1/4 c. all-purpose flour; 1 c. (1/2 can) lemon pudding and pie filling; confectioners sugar. Directions: Preheat oven to 375°. In large mixing bowl with electric mixer, beat margarine, sugar, egg and vanilla until well blended. Mix in flour until incorporated. Divide dough in half. With your hand, shape each section of dough into a log about 14 inches long and 1 1/2 inches wide. Place on greased baking sheet. Using your finger, make a depression down the center of each log about 1/2-inch wide. Spoon in lemon filling, mounding slightly. Bake 20 to 25 minutes or until tops are set and bottoms are golden. Let cool 2 minutes. Loosen bottoms with spatula. Sprinkle with powdered sugar. Cool completely before cutting into 1-inch diagonal slices.

title: Sausage Pie(Breakfast)
text: Sausage Pie(Breakfast). Ingredients: 1 lb. sausage; 1 onion, diced; 1/3 bag frozen diced potatoes; 1 can sliced mushrooms or fresh (optional); 2 eggs; flour to thicken; salt and pepper to taste; 1 tsp. beef bouillon or 3 cubes. Directions: Cook sausage, salt, pepper and onion together (drain fat off). Add potatoes and mushrooms and cook. Put into pie crust, add top crust and bake at 350° until crust browns.

title: Aunt Winnie'S Orange Bread
text: Aunt Winnie'S Orange Bread. Ingredients: 2 large oranges, peeled; 1/2 c. water; 1/2 c. granulated sugar; 1/2 c. butter; 1/2 c. granulated sugar; 2 eggs; 1/2 c. milk; 1 1/2 c. flour; 1 tsp. baking powder; Pinch of salt. Directions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees.

title: Four Layer Pie
text: Four Layer Pie. Ingredients: 1 c. flour; 1 c. powdered sugar; 1 c. nuts (your choice); 1/2 c. butter; 1 (6 oz.) box instant chocolate pudding; 1 (8 oz.) cream cheese; 1 (16 oz.) Cool Whip. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Combine flour, nuts and melted butter in bowl. Mix into dough. Spread in ungreased baking pan in thin layer. Bake 15 minutes or until golden brown, then cool.

title: Graham Torte
text: Graham Torte. Ingredients: 2 (3 oz.) boxes French vanilla instant pudding; 1 (8 oz.) Cool Whip; 1 box graham crackers; 1 (16 oz.) chocolate frosting; 3 c. milk. Directions: Mix pudding with milk; stir until thickened and add Cool Whip. Line a 13 x 9-inch pan with graham crackers. Spread half of the pudding mixture on top. Then put another layer of graham crackers to cover pudding and put the other half of pudding mixture on top. Top that with another layer of graham crackers and spread softened chocolate frosting on top. Refrigerate.

title: Old Time Cinnamon Jumbles
text: Old Time Cinnamon Jumbles. Ingredients: 1/2 c. soft shortening (part butter); 1 c. sugar; 1 egg; 3/4 c. buttermilk; 1 tsp. vanilla; 2 c. sifted flour; 1/2 tsp. soda; 1/2 tsp. salt. Directions: Mix shortening, sugar and egg thoroughly. Stir in buttermilk and vanilla. Sift dry ingredients together and stir. Chill dough.

title: Peach Bread
text: Peach Bread. Ingredients: 1/2 c. butter; 1 c. sugar; 3 eggs; 2 3/4 c. flour; 1 1/2 tsp. baking powder; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 1 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 2 c. fresh peaches, peeled and sliced; 3 Tbsp. frozen orange juice concentrate; 1 tsp. vanilla extract. Directions: Cream butter and sugar. Add eggs; beat well. Combine flour, baking powder, salt, soda and cinnamon. Add to creamed mixture. Stir in orange juice concentrate, vanilla and peaches.

title: Pizza Snacks
text: Pizza Snacks. Ingredients: 6 english muffins; 1 jar pizza snacks; 1 pkg. Mozzarella cheese; 1 pkg. Pepperoni. Directions: Halve muffins, spread pizza sauce, pepperoni, and cheese on each half. Bake at 350 degrees until cheese melts

title: Mom Bekkering'S Stuffing
text: Mom Bekkering'S Stuffing. Ingredients: 3/4 c. butter, divided; 1/2 c. onion, chopped; 1 loaf bread, cubed; 2 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 1 tsp. sage; 3/4 tsp. thyme; 2 tsp. parsley; 1/2 c. water. Directions: In large pan, over low heat, saute onion in 1/2 cup butter until soft, but not brown. Add cubed bread. Sprinkle salt, pepper, sage, thyme and parsley over bread and then stir well. Simmer uncovered until bread becomes slightly browned and crispy, stirring every few minutes. Add 1/4 cup butter, melted and mixed into 1/2 cup water; mix well. Remove from heat and serve or refrigerate to reheat in the microwave before serving. (Can be made the day before.)

title: Mexican Soup Recipe
text: Mexican Soup Recipe. Ingredients: 1 can pinto beans (with jalapenos); 1 can hominy; 1 can Ro-Tel; 2 cans stewed tomatoes; 1 pkg. taco seasoning mix; 1 pkg. Ranch party dip mix; 1 lb. ground beef. Directions: Brown and drain the meat. Do not drain any juice from the can. Add everything to the pot. Turn on high, then low to simmer for about 45 minutes. Serve with shredded cheese and guacamole.

title: Raspberry Punch
text: Raspberry Punch. Ingredients: 1 (46 oz.) can Hawaiian Punch; 1 (6 oz.) can frozen lemonade concentrate; 3 (16 oz.) bottles 7-Up; 1 qt. raspberry sherbet. Directions: Combine punch, concentrated lemonade and 7-Up in bowl; mix well. Pour in punch bowl. Add sherbet at serving time. Yields 30 servings.

title: Aunt Tot Warner'S Egg Custard
text: Aunt Tot Warner'S Egg Custard. Ingredients: 2 c. milk; 1 1/2 c. sugar; pinch of salt; 1 Tbsp. vanilla; 1/2 c. self-rising flour; 1/2 block butter; 4 eggs; nutmeg to taste. Directions: Dump all ingredients in blender and blend 3 minutes. Grease a 9-inch pie plate. Sprinkle with nutmeg. Cook 40 minutes at 350°. Add coconut for different taste.

title: Vegetarian Four-Bean Chili(Low-Calorie, Low-Fat, Low-Cholesterol)
text: Vegetarian Four-Bean Chili(Low-Calorie, Low-Fat, Low-Cholesterol). Ingredients: 1 Tbsp. vegetable oil; 2 large onions, chopped; 1 medium size sweet green pepper, cored, seeded and chopped; 3 cloves garlic, finely chopped; 2 medium size carrots, pared and cut in 1/2-inch dice; 1 small zucchini, halved lengthwise and thickly sliced crosswise; 1 small summer squash, halved lengthwise and thickly sliced crosswise; 2 Tbsp. chili powder; 1/2 tsp. leaf oregano, crumbled; 1 tsp. ground cumin; 2 cans (28 oz. each) tomatoes, in puree; 1 can (12 oz.) beer; 1 tsp. salt; 1 can (1 lb.) black beans, drained and rinsed; 1 can (1 lb.) black-eyed peas, drained and rinsed; 1 can (1 lb.) kidney beans, drained and rinsed; 1 can (1 lb.) chickpeas, drained and rinsed. Directions: Heat oil in large pot over medium heat. Add onion, green pepper and garlic; saute 4 minutes or until onion is softened. Add carrot; cover and cook 2 minutes. Add zucchini, summer squash, chili powder, oregano and cumin. Saute 1 minute. Add tomatoes breaking up with wooden spoon the beer and salt. Bring to boiling. Lower heat; simmer, partially covered 15 minutes. Stir in all the beans. Simmer, stirring, until heated through.

title: Chicken-A-La-Can-Can
text: Chicken-A-La-Can-Can. Ingredients: 1 can cream of chicken soup; 1 can cream of celery soup; 1 soup can water; 1 (12 1/2 oz.) can or 2 small cans boned chicken or 1 1/3 c. chicken; 1 (3 1/2 oz.) can French fried onions; 1 1/3 c. Minute rice. Directions: Combine soup, water and chicken. Add dry rice and 1/2 can French fried onions. Stir to mix. Pour in buttered casserole. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes. Sprinkle remaining onions on top; bake 15 minutes more.

title: Fast Fudge Frosting
text: Fast Fudge Frosting. Ingredients: 1 pkg. confectioners sugar, sifted (4 1/2 c.); 1/4 c. cocoa; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/3 c. boiling water; 1/3 c. butter or margarine (soft); 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Combine sugar, cocoa and salt, mixing well. Add boiling water and butter; blend. Add vanilla. Beat until of spreading consistency.

title: Rev. Vriesen'S Potato Pancakes
text: Rev. Vriesen'S Potato Pancakes. Ingredients: 3 c. finely grated potatoes; 2 eggs; 1 tsp. salt; 5 Tbsp. flour; small onion, grated. Directions: Mix ingredients, drop on hot greased griddle and fry until golden brown.

title: Crispy Bacon Shrimp
text: Crispy Bacon Shrimp. Ingredients: 1 1/2 pounds medium peeled, deveined, raw shrimp; 3 Tbsp. red wine vinegar; 3 Tbsp. oil; 2 halved cloves garlic; 1 tsp. oregano; 1 tsp. garlic salt; 1 sliced medium onion; 1 Tbsp. parsley; dash pepper; 1/2 pound bacon. Directions: Combine all ingredients except bacon in a large bowl. Cover. Marinate in refrigerator overnight. Drain shrimp from marinade saving liquid. Wrap each shrimp in a half slice of bacon. Broil until bacon is crisp. Cook marinade slowly down to 1 cup liquid. Put in a cup. Serve shrimp surrounding liquid with toothpicks for dunking. Serves 6.

title: Pudding Cones
text: Pudding Cones. Ingredients: 1 pkg. (4 oz.) instant pudding (any flavor); 2 c. milk; 8 ice cream cones. Directions: Stir milk into pudding; beat 1 minute. Set cones in a small glass to keep them from falling over. Spoon pudding into cones and they're ready to eat.

title: Black Bean Dip
text: Black Bean Dip. Ingredients: 1 can (15 oz.) black beans, rinsed and drained; 1/2 c. salad dressing (fat free works good); 1/2 c. reduced calorie sour cream; 1 can (4 oz.) chopped green chilies, drained; 2 Tbsp. chili powder; 1/2 tsp. garlic powder; few drops hot pepper sauce. Directions: Mash beans with fork. Stir in remaining ingredients until well blended, refrigerate. Serve with tortilla chips.

title: Mama Lassie'S Butter Rolls
text: Mama Lassie'S Butter Rolls. Ingredients: 3 c flour; 1 Tbsp Shortening; 1/2 c buttermilk. Directions: Put flour in a large mixing bowl. Make a hole in the middle of flour. Add shortening and buttermilk. Gradually mix with flour until dough is stiff. Roll out thin. Cut circles out of dough with a small saucer. Cut up 1/2 stick butter or margarine and put on each circle. Roll each circle up and place in an iron skillet. Bake at 350° until golden brown.

title: Keeps Forever Coleslaw
text: Keeps Forever Coleslaw. Ingredients: 2 medium heads cabbage, chopped; 1 bunch celery, chopped; 2 green peppers, chopped; 1 bunch green onions, chopped; 2 c. cider vinegar; 1 c. water; 3 c. sugar; 2 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. mustard seed; 1 tsp. celery seed. Directions: Bring liquids, sugar, salt and spices to a boil, stirring frequently. Boil 3 minutes. Pour over vegetables. Mix well, cover and refrigerate. Keeps forever and improves with age.

title: French Onion Soup With Cheese Toast
text: French Onion Soup With Cheese Toast. Ingredients: 1/2 c. butter; 3 lb. onions, thinly sliced; 3 cloves garlic, crushed; 7 c. beef broth or 4 (14 1/2 oz.) cans ready to serve beef broth; 2 c. dry white wine or chicken broth; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 13 slices toasted French bread (1/2 inch thick); 13 oz. Swiss or Gruyere cheese, sliced; fresh parsley, chopped. Directions: Melt butter in 5-quart Dutch oven or stock pot over medium heat. Stir in onions and garlic. Cook 20 to 30 minutes or until onions are very soft. Stir in broth, wine, salt and pepper. Cook, uncovered, for 40 to 60 minutes or until flavors are blended. Just before serving, toast bread; place cheese on hot bread.

title: A J'S Secret Ice Cream
text: A J'S Secret Ice Cream. Ingredients: 3 eggs; 1 c. sugar; 3 c. whipping cream; 1 1/2 c. half and half; 3 tsp. vanilla extract; Oreo cookies or choice of candy bar. Directions: Beat eggs by hand until fluffy; slowly beat in sugar. Add cream, half and half and vanilla. Mix well. Pour mixture into an ice cream maker and freeze. While freezing, place cookies or candy bars into a plastic bag and break into small pieces. Add to ice cream mixture when frozen. Store in freezer.

title: Kentucky Hot Brown Sandwiches
text: Kentucky Hot Brown Sandwiches. Ingredients: 1/4 c. margarine; 1/4 c. flour; 2 c. milk; 2 chicken bouillon cubes; 6 slices bread, toasted; 6 slices ham; 6 slices turkey; 6 slices Cheddar cheese; 6 slices tomato; 6 slices bacon, fried crisp and crumbled. Directions: Melt margarine in pan over low heat. Add flour, stirring until smooth. Cook 1 minute, stirring constantly. Gradually add milk and bouillon cubes. Cover over medium heat, stirring until thickened and bubbly. Set aside. Place toast in buttered 9 x 13-inch Pyrex dish. Top each with turkey and ham. Spoon white sauce over meat. Layer cheese, tomato and bacon over sauce. Bake at 350° for 20 to 25 minutes. (Can use thinly sliced country ham or regular ham slices.)

title: Jamie'S P.B.J. Cookies
text: Jamie'S P.B.J. Cookies. Ingredients: 1 stick margarine; 1/2 c. vegetable shortening; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 2 eggs; 1 Tbsp. vanilla; 3/4 c. peanut butter; 1 tsp. baking powder; 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 1/2 tsp. salt; flour; your favorite jelly. Directions: Cream together margarine, shortening and sugar until very fluffy. Add eggs, one at a time, still creaming, then vanilla and peanut butter. Stir in salt, baking powder and soda. Add enough flour, stirring, to make a soft dough.

title: Breakfast Brunch Casserole
text: Breakfast Brunch Casserole. Ingredients: 1 lb. sausage, cooked and drained; 8 oz. can crescent rolls; 2 c. Mozzarella cheese, shredded; 4 eggs, beaten; 3/4 c. milk; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. pepper. Directions: Line bottom of a 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan with rolls, pressing firmly to seal perforations. Sprinkle on sausage and cheese. Combine remaining ingredients. Heat well and pour over sausage. Bake at 425° for 15 minutes. Let stand 5 minutes. Cut into squares for serving.

title: L. B. J. (Pres. Johnson'S) Favorite Pecan Pie
text: L. B. J. (Pres. Johnson'S) Favorite Pecan Pie. Ingredients: 1 deep dish frozen pie shell; 1 c. sugar; 3 eggs, beaten; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 stick butter or margarine; 1 c. white Karo syrup; 1/2 c. to 1 c. broken pecans. Directions: Mix together sugar, syrup, butter an salt and cook until dissolved. While this is cooking, beat eggs until foamy. Add small amount of hot mixture gradually to eggs (stirring constantly with whisk). This will raise temperature of eggs to prevent separation when gradually added to the remaining hot mixture (stirring rapidly). Add pecans and vanilla. Pour into unbaked pie shell and bake at 350 for 30 to 40 minutes, or until the pie has set. Cool on rack.

title: Okra Soup
text: Okra Soup. Ingredients: 3 doz. okra; 1/4 lb. salt pork; 1 lb. pig feet; 3 large tomatoes; 1 large onion; pepper and salt to taste; freshly cooked rice (optional). Directions: Cook pig feet and salt pork until done. Cut up okra, tomatoes and onion. Add to meat. Let boil until thick enough for a good soup. Can be served over rice.

title: Starlight Mint Surprise Cookies
text: Starlight Mint Surprise Cookies. Ingredients: 3 c. flour; 1 tsp. baking soda; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 c. butter; 1 c. sugar; 1/2 c. packed brown sugar; 2 eggs; 2 tsp. water; 1 tsp. vanilla; 9 oz. pkg. Andes mints; walnut halves. Directions: Sift flour, baking soda and salt. In separate bowl, cream butter, sugar and brown sugar. Blend in eggs, water and vanilla. Add dry ingredients and mix thoroughly. Cover and refrigerate 2 hours. Put about 1 tablespoon of dough around each mint. Place on greased baking sheet, about 3 inches apart. Top with walnut half. Bake at 375° for 10 to 12 minutes.

title: Beans Beverly
text: Beans Beverly. Ingredients: 1 lb. lean ground beef; 1/2 lb. bacon, cut into pieces; 1 c. chopped onion; 1 clove garlic, peeled and minced; 1/2 c. ketchup; 2 Tbsp. vinegar; 1 tsp. dry mustard; 1 (28 to 31 oz.) can pork and beans (undrained); 1 (15 oz.) can red kidney beans (undrained); 1 (15 oz.) can lima beans (undrained); 1 (15 oz.) can small butter beans (undrained); 1/2 c. firmly packed light brown sugar; 1 tsp. salt or to taste; 1/4 tsp. ground black pepper. Directions: In a large saucepot or Dutch oven, saute ground beef, bacon, onion and garlic. Add ketchup, dry mustard, all beans, vinegar, salt and pepper. Mix thoroughly. Pour mixture into a large beanpot or other type of casserole dish. Bake in a preheated 350° oven for about 1 hour or until thickened. Serves 10.

title: Chocolate Morsel Pound Cake
text: Chocolate Morsel Pound Cake. Ingredients: 1 pkg. yellow cake mix; 4 eggs; 1 c. water; 1/2 c. oil; 1 pkg. chocolate instant pudding; 1 pkg. vanilla instant pudding; 16 oz. pkg. chocolate morsels. Directions: Mix all together except morsels. After all is mixed, stir in morsels. Pour into tube pan and place in cold oven and bake at 350° for 55 minutes or until done.

title: Creole Flounder
text: Creole Flounder. Ingredients: 2 lb. flounder or pollack fillets; 1 1/2 c. chopped tomatoes; 1/2 c. chopped green pepper; 1/3 c. lemon juice; 1 Tbsp. salad oil; 2 tsp. salt; 2 tsp. minced onion; 1 tsp. basil leaves; 1/4 tsp. coarsely ground black pepper; 4 drops red pepper sauce; green pepper rings. Directions: Heat oven to 500°. Place fillets in single layer in baking dish, 13 1/2 x 9 x 2-inch. Stir together remaining ingredients except pepper rings. Spoon over fillets. Bake 5 to 8 minutes or until fish flakes easily with fork. Remove fillets to warm platter. Garnish with green pepper rings. Makes 4 to 6 servings.

title: Fish Patties
text: Fish Patties. Ingredients: 1 pt. canned salmon; 1 tsp. chopped garlic (bottled in oil); 3 Tbsp. catsup; 3 eggs or egg substitute; Cheddar cheese, sliced thin; 1 medium onion, chopped fine; 4 Tbsp. salsa (medium-hot); 1 tsp. prepared mustard; 1/3 c. bread crumbs. Directions: Combine all ingredients except cheese in a bowl. Form into patties. Fry until crisp in hot olive oil. Top with a slice of cheese. Serve with mustard or salsa sauce. Also makes a great burger sandwich. Serve on a bun with lettuce, tomato and mayonnaise.

title: Lite Ranch Dressing Mix(Lo-Fat Salad Dressing)
text: Lite Ranch Dressing Mix(Lo-Fat Salad Dressing). Ingredients: 1/4 c. dried parsley; 3 Tbsp. dried minced onion; 2 tsp. dried chives; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. garlic powder; 1/2 tsp. ground celery seed; 1/4 tsp. pepper. Directions: Combine all ingredients in a small jar. Store in dry place.

title: Tuna Miniatures
text: Tuna Miniatures. Ingredients: 2 c. corn flakes, crushed; 1/3 c. milk; 1/4 c. mayonnaise; 1 can tuna; 1 Tbsp. minced parsley; 1 tsp. lemon juice; 1/2 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; salt and pepper. Directions: Preheat oven to 425°. Combine 1 cup of corn flake crumbs with milk. Mix in remaining ingredients. Form into 1-inch balls. Roll in remaining corn flake crumbs. Place on greased pan and bake 10 to 15 minutes.

title: Chocolate Boil Cookies
text: Chocolate Boil Cookies. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. sugar; 1/2 c. butter or margarine; 1/2 c. milk (whole or canned); 1 tsp. vanilla; 1/2 c. cocoa; 3 c. oats. Directions: Boil first 3 ingredients about 3 to 4 minutes. Remove from heat. Add remaining ingredients. Drop by spoonful on wax paper.

title: Rhubarb Sauce For Ice Cream
text: Rhubarb Sauce For Ice Cream. Ingredients: 4 c. washed and chopped rhubarb; 1/2 c. brown sugar; 1 tsp. cinnamon. Directions: Barely cover the ingredients in a saucepan. Cook over low heat until tender. Sauce will be kind of thick.

title: Lemon Torte
text: Lemon Torte. Ingredients: 2 c. flour; 1 c. oleo; 1/2 c. sugar; 8 oz. cream cheese; 1 pkg. Dream Whip; 1 c. powdered sugar; 1 large pkg. cooked lemon pudding. Directions: Mix flour, oleo and sugar and press into 13 x 9-inch pan. Bake 15 minutes at 350°. Cool. Break up, crumble and press down again. Whip cream cheese. Add Dream Whip and powdered sugar. Put on first layer. Spread pudding over dessert. Top with whipped cream and chill.

title: Bonnymill Baked Eggs
text: Bonnymill Baked Eggs. Ingredients: 16 eggs; 3/4 c. dairy sour cream; 1 1/2 c. grated Cheddar cheese; 2 c. diced ham or cooked crumbled sausage; 1 Tbsp. chopped chives; 2 Tbsp. melted butter; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Beat eggs in large bowl. Add remaining ingredients. Beat well. Pour into an oiled 13 x 9-inch pan. Bake at 350° until mixture is set and top is golden, about 45 minutes. Yields 10 to 12 servings.

title: Apricot Cake
text: Apricot Cake. Ingredients: 2 c. sugar; 4 eggs; 1 1/2 c. Wesson oil; 2 junior jars apricot with tapioca baby food or 3 small jars; 2 c. flour; 2 tsp. baking soda; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 c. chopped nuts (English or pecans). Directions: Mix together sugar, eggs, oil and baby food. Add flour, baking soda, salt, cinnamon and nuts; bake at 350° for 1 hour and 20 minutes. Make sure cake is completely cooled before frosting it.

title: Low-Fat Cucumber Salad
text: Low-Fat Cucumber Salad. Ingredients: 1/4 c. onions; 1 pt. cottage cheese (fat free); 1 c. cucumbers, diced; 2 boxes lime Jell-O (sugar-free); 2 c. water (hot). Directions: Dissolve Jell-O in water; let slightly thicken. Add rest of ingredients. Store in refrigerator.

title: Angel Mallow Frosting
text: Angel Mallow Frosting. Ingredients: 1/2 c. sugar; 2 egg whites; 2 Tbsp. water; 7 oz. jar Kraft Marshmallow Creme; 1/2 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Combine sugar, egg whites and water in double boiler. Beat with electric mixer over boiling water until soft peaks form. Add Marshmallow Creme. Continue beating until stiff peaks form. Remove from heat. Beat in vanilla.

title: Crackling-Sausage Bread
text: Crackling-Sausage Bread. Ingredients: 1 pkg. yeast; 1 Tbsp. salt; 1 tsp. pepper; 5 c. flour; 1 lb. cracklings; 1 lb. sausage. Directions: Dissolve yeast in 1/2 cup warm water. Add 2 cups warm water, salt and pepper. Stir in large bowl. Add flour; turn out onto floured board. Knead dough until it becomes semi-firm and will not stick to hands. Put dough in bowl and rub 1 tablespoon oil over and around the dough so as not to form a crust. Let rise 1 hour.

title: Fat Free Vegetable Soup
text: Fat Free Vegetable Soup. Ingredients: 1 can V-8 juice; 1 can water; 3 or 4 packets Washington brown seasoning broth or bouillon cubes (find brand without palm oil); 4 stalks celery, sliced (with leaves); 2 potatoes, cubed; 1 sliced onion; 1 pkg. frozen soup vegetables; 1 clove garlic; cabbage or bok choy; 1/2 c. pasta. Directions: Can add 1 can of kidney beans, pinto beans or green beans. Add more water, if needed.

title: Good Relish
text: Good Relish. Ingredients: 1 red hot pepper; 4 green peppers; 8 apples; 8 green tomatoes; 4 red peppers; 8 onions; 4 Tbsp. salt; 3 c. sugar; 2 c. vinegar; 1 c. water. Directions: Grind or chop all vegetables together. Put on salt; stir and set aside for 5 minutes. Drain off excess water. Add sugar, vinegar and water. Put in hot jars and seal.

title: Apricot Spread
text: Apricot Spread. Ingredients: 1 small jar apricot preserves; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese; 1/3 c. pecans, chopped; 1 (3 oz.) box apricot jello. Directions: Mix all ingredients together. Chill. Serve on little pieces of bread.

title: Kids Favorite Morning Spiced Tea
text: Kids Favorite Morning Spiced Tea. Ingredients: 1/2 c. instant tea; 1 large jar Tang; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. ground cloves. Directions: Mix all ingredients in an airtight container. Use 1/3 cup (more or less to taste) per cup of hot water.

title: Best Ever Cucumber Sweet Pickles
text: Best Ever Cucumber Sweet Pickles. Ingredients: fresh cucumbers (firm and a good size for pickle). Directions: Slice, strip, chunk, halve or crinkle cut; whichever you want. Pack quart jars fairly tight. To each jar, add 1 teaspoon salt and 1 teaspoon alum. Fill jar with cider vinegar to cover. Apply tops. No need to sterilize. Just be new and clean. That's the easy part! Now, put away for 5 to 6 months, even a year. The curing process doesn't take that long actually. They are ready when rusty green. To make off: Turn jars upside down in sink (top removed) to drain. Drain 5 to 6 hours or longer. Invert jars; fill with sugar (right out of the bag) as much as jar will hold. Apply tops and shake, shake, shake until sugar dissolves. You will need to do this approximately 3 times until enough syrup to completely cover cucumbers. When this is accomplished, add per quart, 1 teaspoon celery seed and 1 teaspoon whole allspice. Apply tops and shake, shake. That's it. Let sit a few days. Simply delicious.

title: Tuna Ball
text: Tuna Ball. Ingredients: 2 (7 oz.) cans white tuna, drained; 8 oz. fat-free cream cheese; 1 tsp. horseradish; 2 Tbsp. grated onion; 1 tsp. lemon juice; about 1/3 c. chopped pecans. Directions: Mix all ingredients, except pecans. Chill, then roll into ball. Roll ball in chopped pecans.

title: Authentic Wassail
text: Authentic Wassail. Ingredients: 2 qt. apple cider; 1 qt. pineapple juice; 1 qt. orange juice; 1 small can nectar juice; 1 pt. tea (strong); 4 sticks cinnamon; 1 tsp. whole cloves. Directions: Mix together and let simmer. The taste is delicious and the aroma is to die for!

title: Hot Broccoli Dip(Microwave)
text: Hot Broccoli Dip(Microwave). Ingredients: 2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen chopped broccoli; 1/2 c. melted butter; 1 medium chopped onion; 2 chopped celery stalks; salt and pepper to taste; 1 (10 3/4 oz.) can mushroom soup; 1 (3 oz.) can sliced mushrooms; 2 Tbsp. lemon juice; 1 roll garlic cheese. Directions: Remove outer wrap of frozen broccoli and microwave on High for 10 to 12 minutes. Microwave onions and celery in butter until tender. Add soup and cheese. Microwave 4 to 5 minutes or until cheese melts. Stir twice during cooking. Add remaining ingredients. Mix and microwave until heated through. Serve hot with corn chips or fresh vegetables.

title: Scalloped Tomatoes(Heart Smart)
text: Scalloped Tomatoes(Heart Smart). Ingredients: 1 (16 oz.) can tomatoes in juice, chopped; 2 Tbsp. minced green pepper; 2 Tbsp. minced onion; 1 garlic clove, minced; 1 tsp. basil, crushed; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 2 slices stale or day old bread (whole wheat), cut into cubes (1 1/2 c.); 4 tsp. margarine, melted. Directions: Preheat oven to 375°. In a 1-quart casserole sprayed with nonstick cooking spray, place tomatoes in juice. Stir in green pepper, onion, garlic, basil and pepper. In a small bowl, combine bread cubes and margarine; sprinkle over tomatoes. Bake about 30 minutes until hot and bubbly. Makes 4 servings.

title: Mexican Stew
text: Mexican Stew. Ingredients: 1 Tbsp. Crisco; 1/2 lb. broken spaghetti; 1 1/2 lb. hamburger; 1 green pepper; 2 medium onions; 3 cloves garlic; 4 stalks celery; 1 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. pepper; 1 tsp. chili powder; 1 pkg. frozen mixed vegetables; 1 can stewed tomatoes; grated sharp cheese. Directions: Melt Crisco in large skillet, add broken spaghetti. Add browned hamburger and remaining ingredients, except cheese. Cook all together until well done. Sprinkle the top with grated cheese and let melt.

title: Onion Dish Salad
text: Onion Dish Salad. Ingredients: 6 onions, sliced; 1/2 c. water; 1/2 c. vinegar; 3/4 c. sugar; 2 tsp. salt. Directions: Place sliced onions in bowl. Boil next 4 ingredients and pour over onions. Soak overnight. Drain well and add 1 1/2 cups salad dressing and 2 teaspoons celery seed. Chill several hours.

title: Chinese Noodle Casserole
text: Chinese Noodle Casserole. Ingredients: 1 can cream of mushroom soup; 3 (5 oz.) cans chicken; 2 cans chow mein noodles; 1 c. chopped celery; 1 Tbsp. grated onion; 1 pt. jar or can of chicken broth; 1 pkg. cashew nuts. Directions: Combine all ingredients in a large casserole dish; mix well. Bake, uncovered, for 40 to 45 minutes in a 350° oven.

title: Saturday Night Special
text: Saturday Night Special. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; 1 can pork and beans, drained; 1 pt. canned tomatoes; 1 tsp. salt; 1 large onion, thinly sliced; 1/4 c. brown sugar; 2 slices bacon. Directions: Brown meat in hot skillet and drain. Add pork and beans, tomatoes and salt. Pour half the mixture in a baking dish, add layer of sliced onion and cover with remaining beef mixture. Top with strips of bacon and sprinkle with brown sugar. Bake at 375° for 1 hour.

title: Easy Coconut Pie
text: Easy Coconut Pie. Ingredients: 3 eggs; 1 1/4 c. sugar; 6 Tbsp. buttermilk; 6 Tbsp. melted butter; 1 c. coconut; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1 (9-inch) unbaked pie shell. Directions: Mix all ingredients together. Pour into pie shell. Bake at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes.

title: Choc-Oat-Chips Cookies
text: Choc-Oat-Chips Cookies. Ingredients: 1 c. (2 sticks) margarine or butter, softened; 1 1/4 c. firmly packed brown sugar; 1/2 c. granulated sugar; 2 eggs; 2 Tbsp. milk; 2 tsp. vanilla; 1 3/4 c. all-purpose flour; 1 tsp. baking soda; 1/2 tsp. salt (optional); 2 1/2 c. Quaker oats (Quick or old fashioned), uncooked; 1 (12 oz.) pkg. (2 c.) Nestle Toll House semi-sweet chocolate morsels; 1 c. coarsely chopped nuts (optional). Directions: Heat oven to 375°. Beat margarine and sugars until creamy. Add eggs, milk and vanilla; beat well. Add flour, baking soda and salt; mix well. Stir in oats, chocolate morsels and nuts; mix well.

title: Heavenly Salad
text: Heavenly Salad. Ingredients: 1 lb. white grapes or white canned cherries; 1 (No. 2) can pineapple (about 2 1/4 c.); 1 lb. marshmallows, chopped; 1 c. chopped nuts; 2 c. whipping cream (Cool Whip can be used); 3 egg yolks; 1/2 tsp. dry mustard; 1/4 tsp. salt; juice of 2 lemons. Directions: Split grapes in half lengthwise to remove seeds. Add chopped pineapple and marshmallows.

title: Choplet Patties
text: Choplet Patties. Ingredients: 1 can choplets, chopped fine in food processor; 1 c. onion, chopped; 1 c. chopped celery; 1/2 c. bread crumbs; 1 c. chopped walnuts; 2 eggs; 1 tsp. ground sage; 1 sq. plain tofu; 2 Tbsp. bulgur, cooked soft; 1 large clove garlic, finely chopped; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. cayenne pepper; 1 Tbsp. finely diced red sweet pepper; 1 Tbsp. finely diced celery; 1/4 tsp. thyme; 1/4 tsp. fresh parsley, finely chopped; 1 Tbsp. whole wheat flour; 1 small onion, finely chopped. Directions: Prepare tofu as in Tofu Salad Delight, cutting in strips instead of cubes. Whole wheat flour, plain white flour (unbleached) or cornmeal can be used to coat the tofu.

title: Cream Cheese Rounds
text: Cream Cheese Rounds. Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese, softened; 2 sticks margarine, softened; 2 c. sugar; 2 c. flour; 1 c. chopped pecans. Directions: Mix ingredients. Drop by teaspoon on ungreased cookie sheet. Bake at 350° for twelve minutes (until soft).

title: Brickle Bars
text: Brickle Bars. Ingredients: 1 box yellow cake mix; 2 eggs; 1/3 c. soft margarine; 1 can Eagle Brand milk; 1 c. pecans; 1/2 c. Bits 'O Brickle. Directions: Mix together with a fork: cake mix, 1 egg and margarine. Press into a 13 x 9-inch pan. Next mix Eagle Brand milk, 1 egg, pecans and Bits 'O Brickle. Pour over cake mixture. Bake 30 minutes at 350°. Let cool and cut into bars.

title: Jessie'S Potato Salad
text: Jessie'S Potato Salad. Ingredients: 5 lb. potatoes, cooked, peeled and diced; 16 oz. Hellmann's mayonnaise; 16 oz. Hellmann's sandwich spread; 3 medium eggs, hard-boiled and mashed; 2 stalks celery, diced; 1 medium onion, diced; salt to taste; celery seed (garnish). Directions: Mix all ingredients except salt. Add salt last to taste. Store in sealed container in refrigerator for at least 1 hour prior to serving. Serves 10.

title: Buttermilk Chicken
text: Buttermilk Chicken. Ingredients: 1 chicken, cut up; 1 can cream of chicken soup; buttermilk; 1 stick margarine; cooked rice. Directions: Melt 1 stick of butter in a flat casserole dish. Salt, pepper and flour chicken pieces. Place in dish and bake for 30 minutes at 350°. Turn over and bake for 15 minutes more. Mix cream of chicken soup with buttermilk and pour over chicken. Cover and bake for 15 minutes longer. Serve with rice.

title: Mushroom Appetizer
text: Mushroom Appetizer. Ingredients: 1 pkg. mushrooms, cleaned and stems removed; 1 lb. bacon, cooked and crumbled; 8 oz. cream cheese, softened. Directions: Mix cream cheese, bacon and mushroom caps. Broil about 10 minutes or until clear liquid seeps from mushrooms. Serve hot.

title: Spiced Watermelon Pickles
text: Spiced Watermelon Pickles. Ingredients: 1 1/2 qt. cubed rind (trim off green rind and pink flesh); 1 qt. water; 3 Tbsp. salt; 1 qt. water; 1 c. vinegar; 4 c. sugar; 2 tsp. cloves; 2 tsp. cinnamon; 2 tsp. allspice. Directions: For each salad, place a lettice leaf on plate. Place a chilled pear half, cut side down, on the lettice leaf. Make face on each pear half, using narrow end for the head. Use 2 raisens for eyes, 1 red cinnamon candy for the nose, 2 almonds (without skins) for ears. Add a ball of cottage cheese at the wide end for the bunnys tail.

title: "Miss" Jean'S Squash Casserole
text: "Miss" Jean'S Squash Casserole. Ingredients: 3 c. cooked squash; 1 can cream of chicken soup mixed with 1/2 c. water; 1 carton sour cream; 3 Tbsp. chopped pimento; 2 Tbsp. chopped onion; 1 stick melted butter; 1 pkg. Pepperidge Farm dressing mix, cornbread kind. Directions: Combine melted butter with dressing mix. Sprinkle small amount in casserole dish. Save enough to cover top of casserole and combine the rest of crumbs with the other ingredients. Pour into casserole dish. Sprinkle the reserved crumbs on top. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes or until bubbly and brown.

title: Korean Fried Rice(Bokgeum Bahb)
text: Korean Fried Rice(Bokgeum Bahb). Ingredients: 4 c. cold cooked rice; 3 eggs, beaten; 2 thin slices cooked ham; 3 fresh mushrooms, sliced or 4 oz. can mushrooms; 1 green onion, chopped; 1/2 c. small shrimp, canned or frozen, chopped (optional); 1 tsp. sherry (cooking sherry); 3 Tbsp. frozen green peas; 1 Tbsp. soy sauce; 4 Tbsp. oil for frying. Directions: Separate cold rice grains with a knife or chopsticks. Chop ham into small pieces. Rinse frozen green peas under cold water and set aside. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in frypan and make Egg Pancakes.* Chop Egg Pancakes into thin strips and set aside.

title: Crock-Pot Dip
text: Crock-Pot Dip. Ingredients: 1 lb. sausage; 1 small chopped onion; 1 large regular Velveeta; 1 can cream mushroom soup; 1 lb. ground beef; 1 large Mexican Velveeta; 1 can tomatoes and green chilies. Directions: Brown sausage and ground beef and add onions. Drain grease and mix with all of the following in a crock-pot: Velveeta cheese, mushroom soup and tomatoes and chilies. Heat and serve with chips.

title: Beef And Corn Cheese Bake
text: Beef And Corn Cheese Bake. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; 1/3 c. chopped onion; 1 can Cheddar cheese soup; 1/3 c. water; 1 c. drained chopped tomatoes; 1 tsp. prepared mustard; 1 (8 oz.) can whole kernel corn, drained; 2 c. cooked noodles. Directions: Brown beef well. Drain well. Add onion. Cook until tender. Pour in remaining ingredients. Pour into 1 1/2-quart casserole dish. Bake at 350° for 35 minutes or until hot. Stir. Garnish with parsley, if desired.

title: Apple Pound Cake
text: Apple Pound Cake. Ingredients: 2 1/2 c. flour; 1 Tbsp. baking powder; 1/4 tsp. baking soda; 1 c. sugar; 1/2 c. margarine; 1/4 c. buttermilk or plain nonfat yogurt; 8 oz. (1 c.) egg substitute; 2 medium apples, peeled and diced (2 c.); 2 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Heat oven to 350°. Spray a 10-inch tube pan with Pam. Mix flour, baking powder and soda until blended. In a large bowl, beat sugar, margarine and buttermilk about 4 minutes. With a mixer on medium speed, alternately add flour mixture and egg substitute. Beat until well blended and fluffy. Stir in apples and vanilla. Spread in pan. Bake 1 hour or until golden brown and a pick inserted in center of cake comes out clean. Cool on wire rack 10 minutes. Turn out onto rack and cool completely. Dust with confectioners sugar.

title: Maple Pralines
text: Maple Pralines. Ingredients: 2 c. white sugar; 1 c. dark brown sugar; 3 Tbsp. white corn syrup; 3 tsp. maple flavoring; pinch of salt; 1 c. whipping cream; 3 c. or more pecans. Directions: Cook sugars, syrup, cream and salt over high heat until mixture forms a soft ball (235°) when dropped in water. Remove from heat. Add flavoring and pecans. Beat until creamy. Drop from spoon onto waxed paper.

title: Sauerkraut And Noodle Dough
text: Sauerkraut And Noodle Dough. Ingredients: 1 lb. spicy sausage (bulk); 1 qt. sauerkraut; 3 c. water; 3 eggs; 3 c. flour; 1/2 c. water or milk; pinch of salt. Directions: Place sauerkraut and water in 4-quart size saucepan. Begin heating.

title: Easy Chicken Pie
text: Easy Chicken Pie. Ingredients: 1 fryer or 6 breasts; 1 stick margarine, melted; 1 c. self-rising flour; 2/3 c. milk; 1 3/4 c. chicken broth; 1 can cream of chicken soup. Directions: Boil chicken and pull off bone. Chop chicken and layer in 9 x 13-inch pan. Pour chicken broth over chicken. Spread chicken soup (undiluted) over chicken and broth. Melt margarine and combine with flour and milk. Spread mixture on top of other layers and bake at 350° for 40 minutes.

title: Chicken Biscuits
text: Chicken Biscuits. Ingredients: 1 pkg. Brown 'N Serve rolls; 1 stick margarine; 1 can Swanson canned chicken; 1 can cream of chicken soup. Directions: Cut center out of rolls. Melt margarine in saucepan. Add bread pieces, canned chicken and chicken soup. Fill holes with chicken mixture; spread remaining mixture across top of rolls. Bake at 400° until brown, 10 to 15 minutes.

title: Calico Beans
text: Calico Beans. Ingredients: 1 can lima beans, drained; 1 can pork and beans; 1 can red beans; 1 can black-eyed peas; 1 lb. hamburger; 1/2 lb. chopped bacon; 1 medium onion, chopped; 1/2 c. celery, chopped; 1 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/2 c. brown sugar; 1 c. catsup; 1 Tbsp. liquid smoke; 1 Tbsp. mustard; 1 Tbsp. vinegar. Directions: Brown hamburger and bacon. Add 1 medium chopped onion and the celery. Mix sauce, sugar, catsup, liquid smoke, mustard and vinegar. Mix beans and sauce with meat; bake in a casserole dish at 350° for 45 minutes.

title: Vegetables For Brunch
text: Vegetables For Brunch. Ingredients: 1 pkg. Shanghai frozen mixed vegetables; 2 c. sharp Cheddar cheese; 5 eggs, beaten with some milk like for scrambled eggs with salt and pepper to taste; lemon pepper. Directions: Pour frozen vegetables into 1 (9 x 13-inch) casserole, sprayed with Pam to prevent sticking, approximately 1/2-inch deep. Sprinkle some of the cheese over vegies. Sprinkle a small amount of lemon pepper over cheese. Then, add remaining vegies and cheese. Pour egg mixture over top. Bake in 350° oven until cheese melts and vegies are done (1 hour). Cooking time can be reduced if vegies are precooked 8 minutes in microwave. Shanghai vegetables are available at Price Club.

title: Toad-In-A-Hole
text: Toad-In-A-Hole. Ingredients: 1 egg; 1 slice bread; butter. Directions: Melt a bit of butter in skillet. Using the rim of a glass, press the center out of the slice of bread. Reserve center circle. Put slice of bread in pan. Crack egg into center hole of bread. Turn when egg is set. Cook over low heat until egg is done. Season with salt and pepper. Serve with bread hole covering egg center (Toad)!

title: Texas Barbecue Sauce
text: Texas Barbecue Sauce. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. vegetable oil; 1 large onion, chopped; 1 clove garlic, chopped; 2 c. ketchup; 1/4 c. apple cider vinegar; 1/2 tsp. dry mustard; 2 Tbsp. sugar; 1 tsp. chili powder; dash of cayenne; 2 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1 tsp. salt. Directions: Heat oil in a large saucepan and saute the onion for 5 minutes. Add garlic and saute 1 more minute. Add remaining ingredients and bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.

title: Garlic-Cheddar Biscuits
text: Garlic-Cheddar Biscuits. Ingredients: 2 c. buttermilk baking mix; 2/3 c. milk; 1/2 c. shredded Cheddar cheese; 1/4 c. butter or margarine; 1/4 tsp. garlic powder. Directions: Preheat oven to 450°. In a medium sized bowl, combine the baking mix, milk and cheese. Stir until just moistened. Drop tablespoons of dough 2 to 3-inches apart onto a greased baking sheet. Bake 10 to 12 minutes or until golden brown. In a small sized bowl, combine the margarine and the garlic powder, then brush it over the warm biscuits and serve. Yields 8 to 12 biscuits.

title: Magic Lemon Pie
text: Magic Lemon Pie. Ingredients: 1 baked (8-inch) pie shell, cooled; 1 can Eagle Brand sweetened condensed milk; 1/2 c. lemon juice (fresh or frozen); 1 tsp. grated lemon peel (fresh or dried); 2 egg yolks. Directions: Blend together milk, lemon juice, lemon peel and yolks until thickened. Turn into pie shell.

title: Grace'S Pecan Pie
text: Grace'S Pecan Pie. Ingredients: 3 eggs; 1 c. sugar; 1 c. chopped pecans; 1/2 c. white Karo syrup; 1/4 stick margarine; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1/2 c. pecan halves (save for topping). Directions: Mix all ingredients together. Pour in pie shell. Layer nuts on top. Bake at 375° for 40 minutes.

title: Cherry Crunch(Serves 8)
text: Cherry Crunch(Serves 8). Ingredients: 1 c. quick cooking oats; 1/2 c. unsifted all-purpose flour; 1 c. firmly packed brown sugar; 1/2 c. butter or margarine; 1 (1 lb. 5 oz.) can cherry pie filling. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Grease 8-inch square baking pan. Mix oats, flour and sugar. Cut in butter with pastry blender or 2 knives until mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Spoon half of mixture into prepared pan. Top with pie filling. Sprinkle with remaining crumbs. Bake for 40 to 45 minutes. Spoon into dessert dishes. Serve warm with sweetened whipped cream or ice cream.

title: Brownies With Marshmallows
text: Brownies With Marshmallows. Ingredients: 2 sticks butter, softened (do not use oleo); 1/3 c. dry Hershey's cocoa; 1 1/2 c. flour; 2 tsp. vanilla; 2 c. sugar; 4 eggs; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1 1/2 c. walnuts; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. miniature marshmallows. Directions: Mix all ingredients but marshmallows in a large pan. Bake at 350° for 20 to 25 minutes. Sprinkle marshmallows on top and return to oven for 3 minutes. Remove and let cool.

title: Canned Grape Juice
text: Canned Grape Juice. Ingredients: 1 c. grapes; 1/2 c. white sugar. Directions: Fill with hot water to neck of jar. Cold pack for 35 minutes. This is a mild juice.

title: Zucchini And Cheese
text: Zucchini And Cheese. Ingredients: 8 slices bacon, cut; 1 c. chopped onions; 1/2 c. chopped green peppers; 3 or more small zucchini, sliced; 3/4 c. ketchup; 1/2 tsp. salt; little pepper; other cheese to taste (Provolone, Parmesan, etc.); 2 Tbsp. brown sugar. Directions: Arrange bacon slices in single layer in a 2-quart glass baking dish. Microwave for 5 to 6 minutes on High or until crisp. Remove bacon. Crumble and set aside. Add onions and green peppers to bacon fat. Microwave for about 3 minutes on High. Layer all in dish and cover with glass lid on. Microwave for about 7 to 8 minutes.

title: Herbed Stuffed Eggplant(Low Calorie, Low Cholesterol And Low-Fat)
text: Herbed Stuffed Eggplant(Low Calorie, Low Cholesterol And Low-Fat). Ingredients: 2 large eggplant (1 1/2 lb. each); water; 2 Tbsp. medium size onion, chopped; 2 cloves garlic, sliced; 6 oz. boneless, skinless chicken breast, ground (see note); 1 (14 oz.) can plum tomatoes, drained and chopped (reserve juice); 1/4 c. orzo pasta, cooked according to label directions; 1 tsp. dried thyme, crumbled; 1 tsp. dried oregano; 1 tsp. dried basil; 1/4 tsp. ground black pepper; 1 1/2 tsp. kosher (coarse) salt (optional); 1 tsp. cornstarch; 1 1/2 tsp. lemon juice; 2 Tbsp. grated Parmesan cheese (optional). Directions: Wash eggplant under cold water; dry with paper toweling. Cut each eggplant in half lengthwise. Add 1 inch of water to a large skillet; bring to boiling over high heat. Add 2 eggplant halves, skin side down. Cover and reduce heat to low. Simmer 5 minutes or until flesh is soft. Remove to wooden cutting board. Repeat with remaining eggplant. When eggplant are cool enough to handle, scoop out flesh with a large spoon, leaving 1/4 inch thick shell. Be careful not to break through the skins. Place shells in a 13 x 9-inch pan. Coarsely chop eggplant flesh; set aside.

title: Italian Squares
text: Italian Squares. Ingredients: 1/2 lb. ground beef; 1 c. chopped onions; 1 clove garlic, crushed; 1 pkg. frozen, chopped spinach, thawed; 3 c. cooked rice; 1 c. Mozzarella cheese (or other); 4 eggs, beaten; 3/4 c. milk; 1 Tbsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 1 (15 oz.) can tomato sauce; 1/2 tsp. basil; 1/2 tsp. oregano; 1/2 c. grated Italian cheese; 1 tsp. garlic salt. Directions: Saute beef, onions and garlic until meat loses red color. Add spinach, rice and cheese; mix well. Mix milk and eggs, salt and pepper and add to rice mixture. Place in shallow casserole. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes or until set. Meanwhile, blend sauce and seasonings and simmer 5 minutes. Cut spinach meat mixture into squares. Top with tomato sauce and grated Italian cheese.

title: Chioppino
text: Chioppino. Ingredients: 1 bunch parsley; 1 lb. fresh mushrooms; 3/4 jar chopped garlic; 1 lb. butter; 3 cans tomato sauce (large); 1.5 Lambrusco wine (large); 1 pkg. Italian spice (dried); 1 palm full oregano; 1 palm full basil; 5 bay leaves; 2 cans clams; olive oil; 1 large red onion; 2 lb. large shrimp; 2 lb. cocktail shrimp; 1 lb. scallops; 2 lb. red snapper; 10 to 12 lb. halibut or bass; small bottle Lambrusco wine. Directions: Mince onion. Take 2 cubes of butter and cover the bottom of pan with olive oil. Saute. Add mushrooms, clam pieces and parsley. Saute. Add tomato sauce and large wine. Simmer. Can be 20 minutes or 4 hours. About 5 minutes before you serve, add fish. Five minutes before serving, add shrimp, small wine and 2 cubes butter.

title: White Chocolate Cake
text: White Chocolate Cake. Ingredients: 1/4 lb. white chocolate; 1/2 c. boiling water; 1 c. butter; 2 c. sugar; 4 egg yolks; 1 tsp. vanilla; 2 1/2 c. cake flour; 1 tsp. soda; 1 c. buttermilk; 4 egg whites; 1 c. pecans; 1 c. coconut. Directions: Melt chocolate in the boiling water. Set aside to cool. Cream the butter and sugar until fluffy. Mix in egg yolks one at a time, beating well after each addition. Add melted chocolate and vanilla. Sift together flour and soda. Add to creamed mixture with buttermilk. Do not overmix at this point. Beat egg whites until stiff and fold in batter. Stir in pecans and coconut. Pour in 3 greased and floured pans. Bake at 350° for 20 minutes.

title: Velvet Fudge
text: Velvet Fudge. Ingredients: 1 lb. Velveeta cheese; 1 lb. butter; 1 c. cocoa; 4 boxes powdered sugar; 1 lb. nuts, chopped; 1 Tbsp. vanilla. Directions: Melt cheese and butter; blend together. Add vanilla; mix cocoa with 2 boxes confectioners sugar and add this to cheese mixture. Work in other 2 boxes of sugar (use hands; mixture is stiff). Roll out flat on wax paper. Sprinkle with nuts and roll up like a jelly roll and slice. Makes 3 or 5 rolls. Store in refrigerator or freezer. Best if let set at room temperature for 1 hour before serving.

title: Chocolate Ladyfinger Dessert
text: Chocolate Ladyfinger Dessert. Ingredients: 1 (6 oz.) pkg. chocolate chips; 1 Tbsp. instant coffee; 3 eggs; 1 c. heavy cream (whipping cream); dash of salt; 2 pkg. ladyfingers. Directions: Melt chocolate in a double boiler pan. Stir in instant coffee. Cool. Beat egg yolks and fold in chocolate mixture. Beat egg whites, fold in chocolate mixture. Beat whipping cream with salt and fold in mixture.

title: Hamburger Dip
text: Hamburger Dip. Ingredients: 2 lb. beef; 2 cans tomato soup; 1 pkg. instant onion soup mix; 2 Tbsp. chili powder; 2 Tbsp. cumin; 1 lb. Velveeta cheese; dash of oregano. Directions: Brown hamburger; drain. Add all other ingredients, except cheese. Melt cheese last, very slowly on low heat. Serve with corn chips.

title: Taco Casserole(Appetizer)
text: Taco Casserole(Appetizer). Ingredients: 1 can refried beans; 1 pkg. taco seasoning; hot peppers (optional); 1 (8 oz.) pkg. shredded Cheddar cheese; 16 oz. sour cream; 1 small can chopped olives; 2 tomatoes, chopped; chips. Directions: Layer the following bottom to top; refried beans; mix sour cream and seasoning mix and spread over beans, olives, hot peppers, tomatoes and cheese.

title: Strawberry Pretzel Pie
text: Strawberry Pretzel Pie. Ingredients: 3/4 c. melted butter; 2 c. crushed pretzels; 3 Tbsp. sugar; 8 oz. cream cheese; 1 c. sugar; 1 small container Cool Whip; 1 (6 oz.) pkg. strawberry jello; 2 c. boiling water; 2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen strawberries; 1 c. crushed pineapple. Directions: Mix cream cheese, sugar and Cool Whip; blend these 3 in mixer. Spread on cooled crust.

title: Cherry Tomato Salad
text: Cherry Tomato Salad. Ingredients: 1/2 lb. bacon; 2 pt. cherry tomatoes, halved; 1 cucumber, peeled and diced; 1 bunch green onions, including tops, chopped; 1 tsp. garlic salt; 1 tsp. sweet basil; 1/2 tsp. black pepper; 1/4 c. red wine vinegar; 1/3 c. salad oil. Directions: Cook bacon until crisp. Drain and crumble. Combine bacon with tomatoes, cucumber and onions. Sprinkle with garlic salt, sweet basil and pepper. Mix well and chill. Combine vinegar and salad oil; shake well. Just before serving, pour over salad and toss. Serves 6 to 8.

title: 24-Hour Cauliflower Salad
text: 24-Hour Cauliflower Salad. Ingredients: 1 head lettuce; 1 head cauliflower; 1 onion, chopped; 1 lb. fried and crumbled bacon; 2 c. mayonnaise; 1/4 c. sugar; 1/3 c. Parmesan cheese. Directions: Layer the first 4 ingredients in a covered dish or Tupperware container.

title: Country Sausage
text: Country Sausage. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground turkey or lean ground pork; 1/2 c. breadcrumbs; 2 cloves garlic, finely chopped; 2 Tbsp. parsley, finely chopped; 1 tsp. thyme; 1 tsp. sage; 1/2 tsp. marjoram; 1/2 tsp. salt; ground black pepper to taste; 2 Tbsp. safflower oil. Directions: Combine all ingredients except oil and pat mixture into 8 small cakes. Heat oil in large skillet and fry cakes about 20 minutes, turning until brown and crisp on both sides. Serves 4.

title: Stuffed Zucchini
text: Stuffed Zucchini. Ingredients: 3 medium size zucchini; 2 Tbsp. margarine; 1 garlic clove, crushed; 1/4 c. finely chopped onion; 1/2 c. finely chopped celery; 1 tomato, chopped; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/4 tsp. oregano; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 2 c. corn flakes, crushed to 1 c.; 1 c. (4 oz.) shredded Mozzarella cheese. Directions: Cut zucchini in half lengthwise and scoop out centers, being careful not to break shells. Place on greased sheet the coarsely chopped zucchini centers. Melt margarine in saucepan and saute onion and garlic. Add zucchini, celery, tomato and Worcestershire. Brown beef and onion; drain grease. Add remaining ingredients. Simmer 10 to 15 minutes or bake at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes.

title: Healthful Banana Bread
text: Healthful Banana Bread. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. whole wheat flour; 2 tsp. baking powder; 1/2 tsp. baking soda; salt (optional); 3 Tbsp. oil; 2 Tbsp. honey; 1 c. ripe bananas, mashed. Directions: Sift dry ingredients together; add bananas, oil and honey and mix thoroughly. Bake in loaf pan for 45 to 50 minutes at 350°.

title: Oriental Brussels Sprouts
text: Oriental Brussels Sprouts. Ingredients: 4 slices bacon; 3 Tbsp. soy sauce; 1 (7 oz.) can sliced water chestnuts; 1 clove garlic, minced; 1 c. chicken broth; 1 lb. fresh or frozen Brussels sprouts; 1 Tbsp. cornstarch; 1/4 c. water. Directions: Cook bacon until crisp. Remove from pan and drain on paper towel. Stir soy sauce into the bacon drippings. Add chestnuts, garlic and broth. Bring to a boil and add Brussels sprouts. Return to a boil; cover and reduce heat, cooking about 8 minutes or until sprouts are just tender. Combine cornstarch and water and add to the pan liquid. Cook, stirring, until thickened. Sprinkle reserved bacon over the top to serve. Serves 6 to 8.

title: Pear Honey
text: Pear Honey. Ingredients: 11 lb. pears, ground; 6 lb. sugar; 1 lb. grated coconut; 1 (No. 2) can crushed pineapple. Directions: Cook the pears and sugar until real thick, then add coconut and pineapple about 1/2 hour before you take it up. Process in jars for long period of keeping.

title: Surprise Middle Cupcakes
text: Surprise Middle Cupcakes. Ingredients: 1 chocolate cake mix; 1/3 c. sugar; dash of salt; 8 oz. cream cheese; 1 egg; 1 c. chocolate chips. Directions: Mix cake as directed on package. Beat together above ingredients except chocolate chips. Stir in chips. Drop heaping tablespoonful of cake mix into cupcake paper holders, then add 1 teaspoon filling. Top with 1 tablespoon cake mix. Bake at 350° for 15 to 20 minutes.

title: Mock Clam Chowder
text: Mock Clam Chowder. Ingredients: 1 onion; 1 c. celery, chopped; 2 Tbsp. butter; 2 cans cream of potato soup; 1 can evaporated milk (1 1/2 can ok); 1 can water; 1 tsp. parsley; 1 tsp. chives; 12 oz. raw white fish (raw), cubed. Directions: Saute onion and chopped celery in butter. Add cream of potato soup with evaporated milk, water, parsley, chives and white fish. Simmer 1/2 hour.

title: Orzo With Onions And Peppers
text: Orzo With Onions And Peppers. Ingredients: 6 onions, chopped; 1 green pepper, chopped; 1 pkg. frozen, chopped green pepper; 1 stick butter; 2 pkg. George Washington powder beef soup mix; 1 (16 oz.) pkg. orzo. Directions: Cook orzo until al dente. Melt butter; fry onion, pepper and frozen peppers. Sprinkle with soup mix powder. Mix with orzo.

title: Dilled Mushrooms
text: Dilled Mushrooms. Ingredients: 1/2 lb. sliced fresh mushrooms; 1 tsp. butter or margarine; salt and pepper; 1/4 c. vinegar and oil dressing; 1/2 bunch fresh chopped dill. Directions: Lightly saute mushrooms in butter. Do not brown. Season with salt and pepper to taste and drain. Mix with remaining ingredients and marinate overnight or longer in refrigerator. Serve cold. Makes 6 servings.

title: Koch Kase
text: Koch Kase. Ingredients: 1 (16 oz.) carton small curd cottage cheese; 3 Tbsp. flour; 2 Tbsp. butter; 1 tsp. soda; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. caraway seed. Directions: Have everything at room temperature and mix all together, except caraway seed. Cover and let set in a warm place for one hour. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, for 15 minutes. (Takes a while, but it's worth it.) Pour into a buttered dish and top with caraway seed, if desired. Refrigerate with cover on, spread and enjoy.

title: Chicken With Hoisin Sauce
text: Chicken With Hoisin Sauce. Ingredients: 2 whole chicken breasts; 1 medium green pepper, cut in pieces; 1/4 lb. mushrooms, cut in cubes; 6 water chestnuts, cut in cubes; 1/2 c. roasted cashews or almonds; 1 Tbsp. cornstarch; 1 Tbsp. dry sherry; 1 Tbsp. soy sauce; 1/4 c. oil; 2 Tbsp. hoisin sauce. Directions: Cut chicken into 1/2-inch strips and then 1/2-inch squares. Place in bowl with cornstarch and toss. Pour in sherry, soy and toss. Heat wok; pour in 1 tablespoon oil and stir-fry peppers, water chestnuts, mushrooms and salt for 2 to 3 minutes. Remove, set aside and add remaining 3 tablespoons oil and stir-fry chicken. Add hoisin sauce and stir well with chicken. Add vegetables back in and cook 1 minute. Add nuts and stir to heat through. Serve hot.

title: Thick Spaghetti Sauce
text: Thick Spaghetti Sauce. Ingredients: 16 lb. tomatoes, peeled and cored; 1/2 c. oil (2 Tbsp. olive oil is better); 4 medium onions, diced; 4 garlic cloves, minced; 2 (12 oz.) cans tomato paste; 1/4 c. sugar; 2 Tbsp. parsley flakes; 2 Tbsp. oregano; 2 Tbsp. salt; 2 tsp. basil; 3/4 tsp. pepper; 2 bay leaves; 2 green peppers. Directions: Heat oil and cook onions, garlic and peppers until tender. Stir often. Add tomatoes and rest of ingredients. Bring to boil and simmer 2 hours. Take out bay leaves. I freeze in Tupperware.

title: Apples And Sweet Potatoes
text: Apples And Sweet Potatoes. Ingredients: 2 c. water; 1 c. sugar; 3 Tbsp. cornstarch; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. nutmeg; 1 stick oleo; 7 large sweet potatoes; 3 large apples (Winesap). Directions: Boil first six ingredients until thick. Boil sweet potatoes until tender. Layer sweet potatoes and apples (apples need to be sliced thin). Put in 9 x 13-inch casserole. Cook for 30 minutes in moderate (350°) oven.

title: "Tabooley" - Wheat Garden Salad
text: "Tabooley" - Wheat Garden Salad. Ingredients: 1 c. cracked wheat, fine; 2 large bunches parsley; 1/2 bunch mint (fresh), chopped fine; 4 large tomatoes (solid); juice of 1 large lemon; 1/2 c. oil; 1/2 tsp. salt; pepper to taste. Directions: Soak wheat in cold water for 45 minutes. Change water several times. Use sieve; press to squeeze dry. Chop parsley, mint leaves and tomatoes very fine. Add wheat, lemon juice, oil, salt and pepper. Mix well. Serve with fresh lettuce leaves or serve in pita bread. Serves 6.

title: Spanish Noodles
text: Spanish Noodles. Ingredients: 5 slices bacon; 1/2 bag wide noodles; 1/2 c. chopped onion; 3 c. tomatoes; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. chopped peppers; 12 oz. chili sauce; 1 lb. ground beef. Directions: Cut bacon into pieces and brown. Add ground beef and cook until all red is gone. Add noodles, onion, salt, pepper, chili sauce, peppers and tomatoes. Be sure noodles are moistened. Cook on high until steam appears. Turn burner down and continue cooking 45 minutes.

title: Baked Meat Balls
text: Baked Meat Balls. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; 1/4 c. finely chopped green pepper; 1 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1 egg, beaten; 1/2 c. finely chopped onion; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 1 c. crisp rice cereal; 1 can tomato soup. Directions: Combine beef with onion, green pepper, egg, seasonings and rice cereal. Form into 10 balls. Roll balls in flour and brown in hot shortening. Place balls in a large casserole and pour tomato soup which has been diluted with 1 can of hot water. Bake, covered, at 350° for 45 minutes.

title: Hawaiian Style Pork Chops, Rice And Savory Green Beans
text: Hawaiian Style Pork Chops, Rice And Savory Green Beans. Ingredients: 4 center cut pork chops; 1 (15 oz.) can Del Monte sliced pineapple; sweet and sour sauce, Chinese duck sauce, lerivaki baste and glaze; 4 c. rice, cooked in pre-made broth; 1 (20 oz.) pkg. cut green beans; onions, tomatoes, garlic and canola oil. Directions: Several hours before cooking, wash and season pork chops well. Cover and refrigerate about an hour.

title: Bacon Wrapped Chicken
text: Bacon Wrapped Chicken. Ingredients: 1 can cream of chicken soup; 1 (16 oz.) carton sour cream; 6 chicken breasts (boneless); 1 pkg. bacon; 1 jar Hormel chipped beef. Directions: Lay chipped beef on bottom of pan. Wrap bacon around chicken breasts. Mix sour cream and chicken soup; pour over chicken. Bake at 250° for 3 hours.

title: Canned Old Fashioned Mincemeat
text: Canned Old Fashioned Mincemeat. Ingredients: 5 lb. meat (pork); 1/2 gal. peaches, pears, etc.; 1 gal. ground or chopped apples; 1 pt. jelly; 2 Tbsp. cinnamon; 2 Tbsp. allspice; 2 Tbsp. cloves; 1 pt. vinegar; salt and sugar to taste. Directions: Cook meat and grind. Fruit must be cooked, canned fruit may be used, more or less as desired. Mix all ingredients. Put in jars. Cold pack 30 minutes or pressure to 10 pounds and turn off.

title: Sally'S Cornbread
text: Sally'S Cornbread. Ingredients: 1/2 c. cornmeal; 1/2 c. self-rising flour; 2 tsp. baking powder; 1 egg; 1 c. milk; dash of salt. Directions: Mix all ingredients. Heat black iron skillet with oil. Pour mixture into hot skillet. Bake at 400° for 25 to 30 minutes.

title: Chocolate-Peanut Bars(Chewy And Crisp At The Same Time.)
text: Chocolate-Peanut Bars(Chewy And Crisp At The Same Time.). Ingredients: 1/2 c. light corn syrup; 1/4 c. brown sugar; dash of salt; 1 c. peanut butter; 1 (6 oz.) pkg. semi-sweet chocolate chips; 1 tsp. vanilla; 2 c. crisp rice cereal; 1 c. corn flakes, slightly crushed. Directions: Combine syrup, sugar and salt in saucepan. Bring to a full boil. Stir in peanut butter. Remove from heat. Stir in vanilla, cereals and chocolate pieces. Press into buttered 9 x 9 x 2-inch pan. Chill about 1 hour. Cut in small bars or squares. (For easy serving, store in refrigerator.) Makes about 2 dozen bars.

title: Germany Potato Salad
text: Germany Potato Salad. Ingredients: 3 lb. red potatoes; 3 strips bacon, diced; 1/4 c. onion, chopped; 1 Tbsp. flour; 2 tsp. salt; 1 1/4 Tbsp. sugar; 1/4 tsp. black pepper; 2/3 c. vinegar; 1/3 c. water. Directions: Simmer potatoes in jackets until tender; peel and slice thin. Fry bacon until crisp; add onion and cook 1 minute. Blend in flour, salt, sugar and pepper. Stir in vinegar and water. Cook 10 minutes, stirring occasionally. Pour bacon mixture over warm potatoes. Serve warm.

title: Strawberry Supreme
text: Strawberry Supreme. Ingredients: 2 (3 oz.) Jell-O strawberry gelatin; 1/4 tsp. salt; 2 c. boiling water; 1 1/2 c. cold water; 1 (10 oz.) Birds Eye quick thaw strawberries; 1 c. softened vanilla ice cream or sour cream; 1 1/2 Tbsp. brandy; 1 1/2 Tbsp. rum; 2 Tbsp. Cointreau liqueur. Directions: Dissolve gelatin and salt in boiling water. Add cold water. Measure 2 cups into a separate bowl. Add the frozen fruit. Stir carefully until fruit thins and separates. Chill until mixture is almost set, about 15 minutes. Spoon into sherbet glasses, filling each about 2/3 full. Chill set but not firm. Chill remaining and blend in others, then chill for 3 hours.

title: Date And Nut Loaf
text: Date And Nut Loaf. Ingredients: 1 tsp. soda; 1 c. boiling water; 1 c. sugar; 1 egg; 2 c. flour; 1 tsp. baking powder; 1 c. dates, chopped; 1 Tbsp. vanilla; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. walnuts, chopped. Directions: In a bowl, sprinkle the soda over the boiling water. Cool. Add the sugar and egg; beat well. Add the flour, baking powder, dates, vanilla, salt and walnuts. Pour in greased pan. Bake 1 1/4 hours at 350°.

title: Halushki
text: Halushki. Ingredients: 4 finely grated potatoes; 1 egg; flour; 1 tsp. salt. Directions: Mix all together, making a slightly thickened paste. Place mix on a dish and scrape off in about nickel-sized amounts into boiling water. Stir occasionally.

title: Chicken Pan Pie
text: Chicken Pan Pie. Ingredients: 5 chicken breasts, cooked and cut up; 1 tsp. lemon juice; 1 can cream of chicken soup; 1 can cream of celery soup; 1 c. chicken broth; 1 stick margarine; 1 1/2 c. milk; 1 1/2 c. Bisquick. Directions: Debone chicken and place in 9 x 13-inch Pyrex dish. Mix soups and broth; pour over chicken. Cut margarine over mixture. Mix milk and Bisquick. Pour over all. Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

title: Barbara'S Kabobs
text: Barbara'S Kabobs. Ingredients: 2 lb. lean ground beef; 1 egg; 1/3 c. water; 2 tsp. turmeric; 1 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. pepper; fresh tomatoes; onion. Directions: Preheat oven to 450°. Put ground beef in large bowl. Fold in all ingredients. Keep folding ground beef until the texture becomes slightly stiff. Flatten mixture out in the bottom of a large baking pan. Top with quartered tomatoes and onions. Sprinkle some turmeric on top. Bake for 30 to 45 minutes. Serve with white rice and cucumber salad.

title: Reese Imposters
text: Reese Imposters. Ingredients: 1 2/3 c. graham cracker crumbs; 3 1/2 c. powdered sugar; 1 c. butter; 1 c. peanut butter; 2 c. chocolate chips. Directions: Combine crumbs, sugar, butter and peanut butter. Mix until smooth. Press into greased 9 x 13-inch pan. Melt chocolate chips in double boiler or microwave. Spread over dough. Refrigerate until firm. Cut into 1-inch squares.

title: Skillet Beef And Rice
text: Skillet Beef And Rice. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; 1 medium onion; 1 medium pepper; 1/2 c. regular long grain rice; 1 (16 oz.) can kidney beans; 1 (16 oz.) can tomatoes. Directions: Brown in skillet beef, onion and pepper. Add remaining ingredients and heat to boiling. Reduce heat, cover and simmer 25 minutes or until rice is tender.

title: Turkey A La King
text: Turkey A La King. Ingredients: 2 pkg. boxed dressing mix (chicken flavor); 1/2 c. raisins (optional); 1/2 c. celery, cut up; 4 to 5 c. turkey or chicken, cut up; 2 small cans mushrooms; 1 can cream of celery soup; 1 can cream of chicken soup; 1 can cream of mushroom soup; 1 c. crushed Ritz crackers; 1 c. Cheddar cheese, shredded; 1/4 c. margarine, melted. Directions: Mix dressing according to directions. Add raisins and celery. Press in bottom of buttered 9 x 13-inch pan. Mix chicken, mushrooms and three soups and put on top of dressing. Crush crackers. Mix cheese and butter into crackers. Sprinkle cracker mixture over hot dish. Cover with foil. Bake in 325° oven for 45 minutes to 1 hour. Take off foil for last 15 minutes.

title: Hawaiian Baked Beans And Franks
text: Hawaiian Baked Beans And Franks. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. crushed pineapple, drained; 2 Tbsp. onion, finely chopped; 2 Tbsp. brown sugar; 1 Tbsp. catsup; 1 tsp. prepared mustard; 2 (16 oz.) cans pork and beans; 1 lb. wieners or franks, sliced in 1-inch pieces. Directions: Drain pineapple well and combine with remaining ingredients in a 2-quart casserole. Bake at 350° for 1 hour. Makes 6 to 8 servings.

title: Marguerites
text: Marguerites. Ingredients: 1 large egg white; 1/4 c. sugar; salt; 1/4 c. coarsely ground nut meats; 18 saltines; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Beat egg white with dash of salt until bent over peaks form when beater is slowly withdrawn. Beat in sugar thoroughly one tablespoon at a time; add vanilla. Mixture will be thick and glossy. Fold in nuts. Drop a heaping teaspoonful of the meringue in a mound in the center of each cracker. Do not spread! Bake on ungreased cookie sheet in slow (325°) oven 15 minutes or until lightly browned. Allow to cool and recrisp before serving. Good 10 minutes or several hours after baking. Meringue will be soft or chewy, not crisp.

title: Ozark Pudding
text: Ozark Pudding. Ingredients: 1 egg, well beaten; 2 Tbsp. flour, heaped; 1 1/2 tsp. baking powder; 1 c. raw apples, diced; 1/4 c. chopped nuts; 3/4 c. sugar; pinch of salt; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Mix all together thoroughly, starting with egg and sugar. Preheat oven 325°. Turn into greased shallow pan and bake. When done, cut in 2-inch squares and serve with whipped cream or sprinkle with powdered sugar while warm. Bake 35 to 40 minutes.

title: Colorful Dragon Stir-Fry
text: Colorful Dragon Stir-Fry. Ingredients: 1 whole chicken breast, skinned and boned; 1 lb. fresh broccoli; 5 Tbsp. stir-fry sauce, divided; 3 Tbsp. vegetable oil, divided; 1 medium onion, cut into thin wedges; 1 medium carrot, cut diagonally into thin slices; 2 Tbsp. water. Directions: Cut chicken into 1/2-inch strips. Remove florets from broccoli. Set aside. Peel stalks and cut into thin slices. Coat chicken with 1 tablespoon stir-fry sauce. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in hot wok or large skillet over high heat. Add chicken and stir-fry three minutes. Remove.

title: Pasta With Primavera Sauce
text: Pasta With Primavera Sauce. Ingredients: 1 c. small broccoli florets; 1 small zucchini, sliced; 1 small red sweet pepper, cut in strips; 1/2 c. Chinese snow peas (optional); 1/4 c. butter; 1 c. half and half, warmed; freshly ground black pepper; 1 lb. spaghetti or other pasta, cooked and drained; grated Parmesan cheese (optional). Directions: In a large skillet, saute broccoli, zucchini, red pepper and snow peas in butter until crisp-tender. Add half and half and black pepper. Cook briefly until slightly reduced. Serve over pasta. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese. Makes 4 servings.

title: Raspberry Sauce
text: Raspberry Sauce. Ingredients: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen raspberries, thawed; 1 Tbsp. cornstarch. Directions: Press raspberries through a sieve to puree and strain seeds. Discard seeds. Combine puree and cornstarch in a small saucepan. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until mixture thickens. Cool. Yield: about 3/4 cup.

title: Cherry Cranberry Shakes
text: Cherry Cranberry Shakes. Ingredients: 1 c. cranberry juice, chilled; 1 c. cherry soda, chilled; 1 Tbsp. milk or half and half cream; 3/4 tsp. vanilla extract; 1 c. vanilla ice cream, softened. Directions: You will need chilled glasses..

title: Orange Coolius
text: Orange Coolius. Ingredients: 1 c. milk; 6 oz. frozen orange juice; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 c. water; 1/2 c. sugar; 16 oz. ice cubes. Directions: Blend all in blender.

title: Summer Punch
text: Summer Punch. Ingredients: 1 (6 oz.) can orange juice; 1 (6 oz.) can lemonade; 1 (6 oz.) can limeade; 4 c. cold water; 1 (28 oz.) bottle 7-Up, chilled; lemon or lime sherbet (if desired). Directions: In a large pitcher or punch bowl, combine all except sherbet. Stir until all juices are thawed and blended. Top with scoop of sherbet, if desired. Makes 18 (4 ounce) servings.

title: Christmas Mix (Snack)
text: Christmas Mix (Snack). Ingredients: 1 (14 to 16 oz.) box Wheat Chex; 1/2 box Rice Chex; 1 lb. skinless peanuts; 1 lb. small pretzels; 2 lb. white chocolate wafers; 3 Tbsp. vegetable oil. Directions: Melt chocolate and oil in double boiler.

title: Praline Sweet Potato Casserole
text: Praline Sweet Potato Casserole. Ingredients: 2 large cans sweet potatoes in heavy syrup; 1 c. sugar; 1 c. brown sugar; 1/2 c. flour; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1/2 c. butter. Directions: Mix all ingredients together in large bowl with electric mixer on medium.

title: Lazy Wife Pie
text: Lazy Wife Pie. Ingredients: 2 sticks margarine; 2 c. self-rising flour; 2 c. sugar; 2 c. milk; 2 c. chopped fruit*. Directions: *One can fruit pie filling can be used in place of fresh fruit.

title: Chinese Stir-Fry Lamb With Vegetables
text: Chinese Stir-Fry Lamb With Vegetables. Ingredients: 3 lb. lean lamb in 3/4-inch cubes; 1/4 c. soy sauce; 1/4 c. brandy or cognac; 2 cloves garlic, minced; 1/4 c. cornstarch; cooking oil; 1 oz. fresh ginger root, thinly sliced; salt to taste; 1 bunch green onions, chopped; 1 1/2 c. beef bouillon or water; 1 c. bean sprouts; 1 c. snow peas; 1/2 c. sliced water chestnuts; 1/2 c. cashew nuts; cooked rice. Directions: Marinate lamb in soy sauce, brandy, garlic and cornstarch for one hour or more. Heat a wok or large skillet. Add oil to thinly coat bottom of pan. Add salt and ginger. Cook until ginger begins to brown and dry up. Add lamb with marinade, all at once, and saute until lightly browned. Add onions and bouillon. Cover and cook until meat is as done as you like it (about 1 minute for rare). Remove cover and add bean sprouts, snow peas, water chestnuts and cashews. Cook about one minute more, stirring constantly. Serve over cooked rice.

title: Golden Corn Muffins
text: Golden Corn Muffins. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. flour; 3/4 c. cornmeal; 1/2 c. sugar; 4 tsp. baking powder; 1 tsp. salt; 2 eggs, beaten; 3 Tbsp. butter, melted; 1 c. milk. Directions: Combine dry ingredients. Add eggs, butter and milk, stirring until moistened. Spoon into greased muffin tins. Bake at 400° for 15 minutes. Makes 12 muffins.

title: Shoo-Fly Pie
text: Shoo-Fly Pie. Ingredients: 1/4 c. shortening; 1 1/2 c. flour; 1 c. brown sugar. Directions: Mix together to make crumbs.

title: Pat'S Cheese Cake
text: Pat'S Cheese Cake. Ingredients: 4 large pkg. cream cheese; 1 1/3 c. sugar; 6 eggs; 1 Tbsp. vanilla; 1 large container sour cream; 6 Tbsp. sugar; 2 Tbsp. vanilla. Directions: Mix cream cheese, 1 1/3 cups sugar, eggs and 1 tablespoon vanilla. Pour into baking pan. Bake at 350° for 35 minutes. Set aside for 20 minutes; leave oven on. Mix sour cream, 6 tablespoons sugar and 2 tablespoons vanilla. Pour on top of cake and spread. Bake for 15 minutes.

title: Taco Soup
text: Taco Soup. Ingredients: 1 lb. hamburger; 1 onion, chopped; 1/2 pkg. dry taco seasoning; 1 can kidney beans; 1 can butter beans; 1 can tomatoes; 1 small can tomato sauce; 1 (12 oz.) can tomato juice or V-8 juice. Directions: Brown hamburger with onion. Drain. Add taco seasoning. Combine meat mixture with all remaining ingredients. Simmer 20 minutes. Serve with Cheddar cheese, sour cream, crushed corn chips and chopped avocado. Let each person create their own. Delicious and fun!

title: Peas And Cheese
text: Peas And Cheese. Ingredients: 1 can English peas, drained; 1/2 lb. grated Velveeta cheese; 1 small minced onion; 2 heaping Tbsp. mayonnaise; 1 Tbsp. sugar; 1/8 c. pickle juice. Directions: Mix the mayonnaise, sugar and pickle juice. Toss all together and serve.

title: Fried Cornbread Cakes
text: Fried Cornbread Cakes. Ingredients: 1 c. cornmeal; 1/2 c. flour; 1 tsp. baking powder; 1 tsp. salt. Directions: Mix above ingredients. Stir in enough water to make dough thick. Roll into ball with wet hands; flatten. Fry in grease until golden brown; turn over and fry other side.

title: Loaf Bread
text: Loaf Bread. Ingredients: 2 1/2 c. self-rising flour; 1 (12 oz.) can beer; 1/4 c. oil; 1/4 c. sugar. Directions: Mix all ingredients together. Grease and flour a loaf pan. Bake at 350° for about 10 or 12 minutes or until done.

title: Crispy Bread And Butter Pickles
text: Crispy Bread And Butter Pickles. Ingredients: 4 qt. medium cucumbers, sliced; 2 green peppers, chopped; 1/3 c. medium coarse salt; 1 1/2 tsp. turmeric; 2 Tbsp. mustard seed; 6 medium white onions, sliced; 2 cloves garlic, diced; 5 c. sugar; 1 1/2 tsp. celery seed; 3 c. cider vinegar. Directions: Do not peel cucumbers. Slice thin. Add onions, peppers, garlic and salt. Cover with ice. Mix thoroughly. Let stand 3 hours. Drain.

title: Cola Barbecued Chicken
text: Cola Barbecued Chicken. Ingredients: 4 chicken breast halves; 2 Tbsp. flour; 2 Tbsp. vegetable oil; 1/2 c. sliced green onions; 1 c. tomato catsup; 1 c. cola soft drink; 1/2 tsp. chili powder; 1/2 tsp. cumin; 1/2 tsp. dried oregano; 1/4 tsp. black pepper. Directions: Preheat oven to 375°. Dust chicken with flour, shaking off excess. In a 10-inch skillet, heat oil over high heat. Add chicken and brown about 3 minutes on each side.

title: Layered Bean Dip
text: Layered Bean Dip. Ingredients: 1 can refried beans; 1 c. shredded Cheddar; 1/2 c. sour cream; 1/2 c. taco sauce; jalapeno slices (garnish). Directions: Layer ingredients with beans on bottom into serving dish. Garnish with jalapeno slices.

title: Slap Me Down Corn Bread
text: Slap Me Down Corn Bread. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. vegetable oil; 2 c. Aunt Jemima self-rising corn meal mix; 2 Tbsp. sugar (optional); 1 1/2 c. buttermilk; 1 egg, beaten; 1/2 stick butter. Directions: Heat oven to 425°. Put vegetable oil in 10-inch skillet. Heat in oven for 3 minutes; tilt skillet to coat bottom evenly. Combine corn meal, melted butter, sugar, buttermilk and egg. Mix well. Pour into hot skillet; bake 25 to 30 minutes.

title: Sausage And Onion Squares
text: Sausage And Onion Squares. Ingredients: 2 c. Bisquick; 3/4 c. milk; 2 eggs; 1 lb. sausage; 1 large onion; 1 c. shredded Cheddar cheese; 1 large container sour cream. Directions: Mix and brown in skillet; drain and spread over dough. Mix Cheddar cheese and sour cream. Spread over sausage. Sprinkle with paprika and cook in a 325° oven for 25 or 30 minutes.

title: Microwave Cherry Crunch
text: Microwave Cherry Crunch. Ingredients: 1 can cherry pie filling; 1/2 box yellow cake mix; 3 Tbsp. brown sugar; cinnamon; 3/4 stick margarine; chopped nuts. Directions: Pour pie filling in oblong Pyrex baking dish. Sprinkle cake mix over pie filling. Pat mix into cherries. Sprinkle brown sugar and a little cinnamon over that. Dot with margarine and sprinkle nuts on top. Cover with wax paper and microwave on High for 14 minutes.

title: Gary'S Favorite
text: Gary'S Favorite. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; 1/2 lb. sweet sausage; 1 can Cheddar cheese soup; 1 can tomato soup; 1 onion; 8 oz. pkg. wide noodles; salt and pepper; 1 can water. Directions: Brown beef, sausage and onion. Add soups and water, salt and pepper. Mix well. Cook noodles and add to meat mixture. Place in large, buttered baking dish and bake at 350° for 45 minutes or until brown on top.

title: Triple Good Veggies
text: Triple Good Veggies. Ingredients: 1 (20 oz.) pkg. frozen cauliflower; 1 (20 oz.) pkg. broccoli; 1 (10 oz.) pkg. Brussels sprouts; 8 oz. Cheez Whiz; 1 can cream of mushroom soup. Directions: Cook vegetables in boiling salted water until tender; drain and pour into greased casserole. Melt Cheez Whiz with soup and pour over vegetables. Bake at 350° for 1/2 hour.

title: Quick Apple Crisp
text: Quick Apple Crisp. Ingredients: 2 cans apple pie filling; cinnamon sugar; 1 box Jiffy Cake Mix; 1 stick margarine. Directions: Pour pie filling into greased baking dish. Sprinkle top with cinnamon sugar; put dry cake mix on top. Put pats of margarine on top. Bake in 350 degree oven for 25 minutes or until top is brown and bubbly. (Any flavor pie filling may be used.)

title: Strawberry Tunnel Cream Cake
text: Strawberry Tunnel Cream Cake.
