Ingredients: 1 (10 inch) angel food cake; 1 (8 oz.) pkg light cream cheese, softened; 1 (14 oz.) can low-fat sweetened condensed milk; 1/4 cup lemon juice; 1 tsp almond extract; 2 cups sliced fresh strawberries; 1 (12 oz.) container reduced-fat frozen whipped topping, thawed; fresh strawberries or mint springs, for garnish. Directions: Cut a 1-inch layer from top of cake; set aside. Hollow out center of cake, leaving a 1-inch shell; tear remaining cake into bite-size pieces. Place shell on serving platter, and set aside. Beat cream cheese at medium speed with an electric mixer until fluffy. Add condensed milk; beat until blended. Stir in lemon juice and extract. Fold in cake pieces and strawberries. Spoon into cake shell; replace top layer. Cover and chill 3 hours or until set. Spread whipped topping on top and sides of cake; garnish, if desired. Chill.

title: No Bake Peanutty Drops
text: No Bake Peanutty Drops. Ingredients: 1/2 c. packed brown sugar; 1/2 c. light corn syrup; 2 c. Rice Krispies or granola; 1/2 c. peanut butter; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: In saucepan over medium heat, bring sugar and syrup to boil; stir until sugar is dissolved. Remove from heat. Fold in remaining ingredients until well blended. Drop by heaping teaspoons on waxed paper. Cool until firm. Store in airtight container. Makes 36.

title: Mickey Mouse Rice Pilaf
text: Mickey Mouse Rice Pilaf. Ingredients: 1/2 c. margarine or butter (chicken fat can also be used); 1 clove garlic, finely chopped; 1 c. chopped onion; 1 c. chopped celery; 1 c. chopped mushrooms *; 2 c. raw rice; 4 c. chicken broth; 1 bay leaf; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Melt margarine in a large skillet and saute garlic, onion, celery and mushrooms until tender, not browned. Add rice and stir until each grain is coated. Add chicken broth, bay leaf, salt and pepper. Bring to a boil. Transfer to buttered 2-quart casserole. Cover and bake at 350° for 20 minutes or until rice is tender and all liquid absorbed. Remove bay leaf before serving.

title: Golfer'S Sandwich Spread
text: Golfer'S Sandwich Spread. Ingredients: 3 eggs, beaten; 3 Tbsp. vinegar; 3 Tbsp. sugar; 1 Tbsp. margarine; 8 oz. cream cheese; 1 Tbsp. pimento; 1 medium onion, cut very fine. Directions: In double boiler, cook the first 4 ingredients, stirring constantly until thick. In mixing bowl, add to first mixture the last 3 ingredients. Beat until smooth. Keep refrigerated.

title: Campfire Dump Cake
text: Campfire Dump Cake. Ingredients: 1 can fruit pie filling; 1 Jiffy cake mix; 1 stick butter. Directions: Put pie filling in Dutch oven lined with foil if you want easy cleanup. Sprinkle dry cake mix over filling. Dot with butter chunks. Bake for approximately 30 minutes in coals, below and above Dutch oven.

title: Best Ever Deer And Gravy
text: Best Ever Deer And Gravy. Ingredients: 1 pkg. deer steak or any deer, cut in small pieces to fry; oil; 1 can cream of mushroom soup; 1 large onion, sliced; salt; pepper; flour. Directions: Flour deer after salting and peppering. Fry in oil to brown. Meanwhile place a large skillet or pot on another burner. Depending on amount you cook, put water in pot. For one package of deer to feed 4, use about 2 cups of water. When meat is browned, drop directly into water that is simmering. Do not drain on paper. Continue until all meat is browned. Cook onions in oil until tender. Drain off most of oil. Brown small amount of flour and brown. Add all this to water. Add soup. Simmer, adding water if necessary.

title: Impossible Quiche Lorraine
text: Impossible Quiche Lorraine. Ingredients: 3 eggs; 1/2 c. Bisquick; 1/2 c. melted margarine; 1 1/2 c. milk; 1/4 tsp. salt; dash of pepper; 1 c. shredded Cheddar cheese; 1/2 c. bacon, cooked and crumbled or chopped ham. Directions: Place all ingredients in blender except meat and cheese. Place in a 9-inch deep pie plate. Sprinkle with cheese and bacon or ham. Press down. Bake 45 minutes at 350°.

title: House Ranch Dressing
text: House Ranch Dressing. Ingredients: 3/4 c. buttermilk; 3/4 c. mayonnaise; 3/4 c. sour cream; 1 Tbsp. chives or parsley; 1 Tbsp. white vinegar; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. onion powder; 1/4 tsp. garlic powder; 1/4 tsp. black pepper. Directions: Combine all ingredients in a bowl and whisk until thoroughly blended. Pour into a lidded container and refrigerate for up to a week. Shake before serving.

title: Holiday Egg Nog
text: Holiday Egg Nog. Ingredients: 6 eggs; 1 c. sugar; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 c. golden rum or 1 to 2 Tbsp. rum flavoring; 1 qt. light cream; nutmeg. Directions: In a large bowl beat eggs until light and foamy. Add sugar and salt, beating until thick and lemon colored. Stir in rum and cream. Chill 3 hours. Makes 12 servings.

title: Smothered Potatoes With Squash
text: Smothered Potatoes With Squash. Ingredients: 4 medium peeled potatoes; 1 large onion, peeled and sliced; 1 large bell pepper, sliced; 4 medium yellow squash, sliced; salt; pepper; dash of red pepper; oil. Directions: Cut potatoes into strips. Coat skillet with oil over medium heat. Saute lightly the onions and bell pepper. Add potatoes and stir to coat with oil. Cook, stirring for 2 minutes. Add squash and seasonings; gently mix. Cover and heat on low. Cook until potatoes are tender, checking and turning often.

title: Cheeseburger Pie
text: Cheeseburger Pie. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; 1 Tbsp. onions; 1 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 3/4 tsp. Italian seasoning; 1 (6 oz.) can tomato paste; 1 pizza crust; 8 oz. shredded Mozzarella cheese. Directions: Brown meat and seasoning in skillet. Drain off fat. Add tomato paste; blend. Turn mixture out on crust. Top with cheese. Bake at 375° for 10 to 15 minutes.

title: Pepperoni Pizza Casserole
text: Pepperoni Pizza Casserole. Ingredients: 2 bags rice, cooked; 1/4 cup parmesan cheese; 1 tsp. oregano; 1/2 tsp. garlic powder; 2 eggs; 1 jar (16 oz.) spaghetti sauce; Sliced green pepper; 4 oz. pepperoni, sliced; 4 oz. mushroom, sliced; 8 oz. mozzarella cheese. Directions: Mix rice, parmesan cheese, oregano, garlic powder and eggs. Blend together. Put in greased 9 x 13 casserole and bake 10 minutes. Pour jar of spaghetti sauce over rice mixture. Add sliced green pepper, pepperoni, mushrooms and mozzarella cheese. Bake 15 minutes longer.

title: New Orleans Green Rice
text: New Orleans Green Rice. Ingredients: 1 c. converted rice; 1 bay leaf; 1/4 c. minced green onions; 1/2 c. sliced white onion; 1/2 c. fresh chopped parsley; 1 c. chopped celery; 1/2 stick butter. Directions: Cook rice according to package directions, but add bay leaf to the cooking water. When done remove the bay leaf. Saute green onion, white onion, parsley and celery in butter, in a large frying pan; cook slightly so celery stays crisp. Add cooked rice and blend together. Serves 6.

title: Starlight Sugar Crisps
text: Starlight Sugar Crisps. Ingredients: 1 pkg. dry yeast; 1/4 c. water; 3 1/2 c. flour; 1 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. butter or margarine; 1/2 c. Crisco or lard; 2 eggs; 1/2 c. sour cream; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 2 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Soften 1 package dry yeast in 1/4 cup very warm water. Cut butter and Crisco into flour and salt mixture until particles are the size of small peas. Blend in the beaten eggs, sour thick or commercial cream, vanilla and the softened yeast. Mix thoroughly. Cover; chill at least 2 hours. Dough may be stored in refrigerator up to 4 days and baked as needed.

title: Cottage Cheese Salad Ring
text: Cottage Cheese Salad Ring. Ingredients: 1 lb. carton cottage cheese; 1 small pkg. lime-flavored gelatin; 1 c. hot water; 1 c. mayonnaise; 1 c. celery, diced; 1 c. green pepper, diced. Directions: Dissolve gelatin in hot water. Chill until partially set. Blend in mayonnaise. Fold in celery, green pepper and cottage cheese. Pour into a 4-cup ring mold. Chill until firm. Unmold on lettuce.

title: Chicken A L'Orange
text: Chicken A L'Orange. Ingredients: 2 broiler size (2 to 2 1/2 lb.) chickens, quartered; 2 Tbsp. butter; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 1/4 tsp. paprika; 1/4 tsp. dried tarragon; 1/2 c. minced green onion; 1 clove garlic, peeled and minced finely; 1/2 c. chopped celery; 4 more Tbsp. butter; 1/4 c. Cointreau; 2 Tbsp. lemon juice; 2 c. chicken stock or broth; 1/4 c. tomato paste; minced parsley (for garnish). Directions: Preheat oven to 375°. Arrange quartered chicken pieces in a long, shallow baking dish. Bake in preheated oven for 30 minutes, turning pieces twice during cooking. In a small skillet, melt butter; stir in salt, pepper, paprika and tarragon; pour evenly over chicken pieces. Continue to bake for about 15 minutes, until chicken is tender and browned. While chicken bakes, prepare sauce.

title: Hot Virginia Dip
text: Hot Virginia Dip. Ingredients: 1 c. chopped pecans; 2 tsp. butter; 16 oz. cream cheese (soft); 4 Tbsp. milk; 5 oz. dried beef, minced; 1 tsp. garlic salt; 1 c. sour cream; 4 tsp. minced onion. Directions: Saute pecans in butter. Reserve. Mix all ingredients. Place in 1 1/2-quart baking dish. Top with pecans. Chill until serving time. Bake at 350° for 20 minutes. Serve hot with crackers or small bread sticks.

title: Old Fashioned Cocoa Fudge
text: Old Fashioned Cocoa Fudge. Ingredients: 6 cups sugar; 1 cup Hershey's cocoa; 1/4 tsp salt; 1 (12 oz) can evaporated milk; 1 1/2 cup regular milk; 1 stick margarine; 2 tsp vanilla. Directions: Butter a 9 x 13 pan. Set aside. Mix cocoa, sugar, and salt in a large, heavy pan. Stir in both milks. Cook over med heat, stirring constantly until mixture comes to full boil. Boil without stirring until mixture reaches a soft ball stage (234° on candy thermometer). Remove from heat, add vanilla, and butter, but DO NOT STIR. Allow to cool until lukewarm (about 110°). Beat with wooden spoon until fudge thickens. Spread quickly into buttered pan. Cool completely. Cut into squares.

title: Creamed Spinach
text: Creamed Spinach. Ingredients: 4 Tbsp. butter; 1 large onion, chopped; 2 lb. fresh spinach; 2 Tbsp. flour; 1 c. milk; 1/2 tsp. nutmeg; 1/4 tsp. allspice; 1 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper. Directions: Melt 2 tablespoons butter in a large saucepan. Saute onion in butter until brown. Add washed, drained spinach to onion and cook, covered, for 3 to 5 minutes. Drain and chop. Return spinach to a saucepan. While spinach is cooking, work remaining butter and flour together with a fork. When spinach is cooked, lower heat and add butter-flour mixture a little at a time until all is smoothly incorporated. Add milk and seasonings. Reheat. Use as a garnish with other vegetables. Serves 6.

title: Garden Club Cookies
text: Garden Club Cookies. Ingredients: 2 sticks softened margarine; 1/2 c. sugar; 1 1/4 c. flour; 1/2 c. crushed potato chips; 1/2 c. chopped nuts; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Mix in order given. Drop by teaspoon on lightly greased cookie sheet. Bake at 350° about 12 minutes. Watch carefully after 10 minutes. Let cookies cool a minute. Transfer to cooling racks or towels. Immediately sift powdered sugar over cookies. Makes about 4 dozen cookies.

title: Crab-Cream Cheese Bake
text: Crab-Cream Cheese Bake. Ingredients: 1 pkg. (8 oz.) cream cheese; 1/4 c. chopped green onions; 1/2 tsp. dried dill weed; 1 can (8 oz.) refrigerated crescent dinner rolls; 1 can (6 1/2 oz.) crabmeat; 1 egg yolk, beaten. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. In a medium bowl, combine cream cheese, onions and dill weed. Unroll crescent roll dough onto a greased pan. Don't separate dough. Press dough into an 8 x 11-inch rectangle pan. Spoon crabmeat lengthwise along center of dough. Spoon cream cheese mixture over crabmeat. Fold long edges of dough over cream cheese mixture, slightly overlapping edges. Pinch together to seal. Place seam side down on baking sheet. Lightly brush top of dough with egg yolk. Cut slits in top of dough. Bake 20 to 22 minutes or until golden brown and flaky. Cut into 1-inch slices and serve warm.

title: Scampi Primavera
text: Scampi Primavera. Ingredients: 1 lb. spaghetti or vermicelli; 3/4 c. olive oil; 3 large garlic cloves, minced; 1/2 tsp. fine chopped lemon peel; 2 carrots; 1 medium zucchini; 1 medium red pepper; 1 1/2 lb. medium shrimp; 2 Tbsp. lemon juice; 3/4 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 2 Tbsp. torn fresh basil; 2 Tbsp. torn Italian parsley. Directions: Cut carrots into 2-inch thin strips. Cut zucchini and red pepper into thin strips. Cook pasta as directed. Heat oil in large skillet; cook and stir garlic and lemon peel about 30 seconds. Add vegetables and shrimp. Stir over medium heat until shrimp turn pink, about 3 to 4 minutes. Sprinkle with lemon juice, salt and pepper. Stir in basil and parsley. Spoon over pasta; toss well. Serves 8.

title: Apple-Walnut Cake
text: Apple-Walnut Cake. Ingredients: 4 c. coarsely chopped apples; 2 c. sugar; 2 eggs, slightly beaten; 1/2 c. oil; 2 tsp. vanilla; 2 c. flour; 2 tsp. soda; 2 tsp. cinnamon; 1 tsp. salt; 1 c. chopped nuts. Directions: Mix apples and sugar. Let stand while mixing other ingredients. Mix eggs, oil and vanilla. Add to apple mixture. Mix dry ingredients and walnuts into apple mixture. Bake for 40 minutes at 350°.

title: O'Reilly'S St. Patrick'S Day Green Soup
text: O'Reilly'S St. Patrick'S Day Green Soup. Ingredients: 1/2 lb. fresh spinach; 1 stalk (about 14 oz.) broccoli; 2 sprigs parsley; 1 small stalk celery (with a few leaves); 4 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 4 Tbsp. flour; 4 c. rich chicken broth; 1 c. heavy cream or half and half (less rich); salt and pepper. Directions: Wash spinach under cold, running water. Break off and discard coarse stems. Drain and pat dry with paper towels. Chop coarsely. Wash broccoli under cold, running water. Trim off and discard small green leaves on stalk. Coarsely chop top and tender stem. Wash, dry and chop parsley and celery. Set aside the 4 green preceeding vegetables.

title: Coconut Cream Cheese Pound Cake
text: Coconut Cream Cheese Pound Cake. Ingredients: 1/2 c. butter or margarine, softened; 1/2 c. shortening; 1 8 oz. pkg. cream cheese, softened; 3 c. sugar; 6 eggs; 3 c. all purpose flour; 1/4 tsp. baking soda; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1 6 oz. pkg. coconut; 1 tsp. vanilla flavoring; 1 tsp. coconut flavoring. Directions: Mix butter or margarine, shortening and sugar for 3 to 4 minutes. Add cream cheese and eggs. Add flour, soda, salt and vanilla. Mix until blended. Stir in coconut. Pour into a greased 10 inch tube pan and bake at 325° for 1 1/2 hours.

title: Lazy Macaroons
text: Lazy Macaroons. Ingredients: 2 egg whites; 2/3 c. sugar; 1 1/3 c. flaked coconut. Directions: Beat the egg whites with a dash of vanilla until stiff. Slowly add sugar and fold in the coconut. Drop by a teaspoon onto a greased cookie sheet and bake about 20 minutes at 325°. Makes about 20.

title: Chicken-Basil-Cashew Pat
text: Chicken-Basil-Cashew Pat. Ingredients: 3 Tbsp. butter; 10 oz. skinless boneless chicken breast; 1 Tbsp. chopped garlic; 1/3 c. Hellmann's mayonnaise; 1/2 c. lightly salted roasted cashews (2 oz.); 1/2 c. finely chopped onion; 1/4 c. grated Asiago cheese; 1/2 tsp. Tabasco sauce; 1/4 c. chopped fresh basil. Directions: Cut chicken into 1/2-inch pieces. Sprinkle with salt and pepper. Melt butter in heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Add chicken and garlic and saute until chicken is cooked, about 3 minutes. Stir in cashews. Remove from heat and cool. Pour cooled chicken mixture into food processor. Add mayonnaise, onion, cheese and Tabasco. Process until smooth. Add chopped basil and process. Transfer to bowl; cover and chill at least 2 hours. Can be made 2 days ahead.

title: Greek Herbal Chicken
text: Greek Herbal Chicken. Ingredients: 1 whole fryer; 1/4 c. chopped Chinese parsley; 1 Tbsp. anise seed; 2 cloves garlic, minced; 1/4 c. butter, melted. Directions: Combine seasonings with butter. Rub butter seasonings onto chicken. Bake in slow 300° oven 1 1/4 hours. Serves 4.

title: Deep Freeze Fruit Cake
text: Deep Freeze Fruit Cake. Ingredients: 1 can Dime Brand sweetened condensed milk; 1 bag vanilla wafers; 1 box raisins; 1 c. chopped pecans. Directions: Crush wafers in fine crumbs. Add sweetened condensed milk and mix thoroughly. Add raisins and pecans; mix well. Press out in a wax paper-lined pie tin. Chill or freeze. May be cut in thin slices.

title: Shipwrecked
text: Shipwrecked. Ingredients: 7 medium potatoes, sliced; 1 chopped onion; 1 lb. hamburger; 1/4 c. rice (uncooked); 1/4 c. celery; 1 1/2 c. cooked tomatoes; 1 can kidney beans, drained; 1/4 c. water; 2 c. Cheddar cheese. Directions: Brown hamburger and onion in skillet. Drain grease. In a greased casserole, layer hamburger, potatoes, rice, celery, tomatoes, kidney beans and Cheddar cheese. Make at least 2 layers, then pour water over top. Bake at 350° for 1 1/2 hours.

title: New York Cheesecake
text: New York Cheesecake. Ingredients: 1 lb. Ricotta cheese; 2 pkg. (8 oz.) cream cheese; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 4 eggs; 1/2 tsp. lemon juice; 1/2 tsp. vanilla; 3 Tbsp. cornstarch; 2 Tbsp. flour; 1/4 c. melted butter; 1 pt. sour cream; crushed graham crackers. Directions: Blend Ricotta and cream cheese; blend in sugar. Add eggs (one at a time); add vanilla, lemon juice, cornstarch, flour and butter. Fold in sour cream and blend well. Butter a 10-inch spring-form pan; crush graham crackers and spread in bottom and edges of pan. Pour mixture into pan. Place a pan with water directly until cake pan. Place in a cold oven, then cook for 1 hour at 325°. Turn off oven and leave door closed for 2 to 3 hours and refrigerate overnight.

title: Creole Seasoning (Make Your Own)(Club Member: Renee Evans)
text: Creole Seasoning (Make Your Own)(Club Member: Renee Evans). Ingredients: 26 oz. box salt; 1 1/2 oz. black pepper; 2 oz. ground red pepper; 1 oz. garlic; 1 oz. onion powder; 1 oz. Accent; 2 oz. chili powder. Directions: Mix well and use like salt. Store in airtight container.

title: Do Ahead Breakfast
text: Do Ahead Breakfast. Ingredients: 1 lb. breakfast sausage (or hot and spicy sausage); 12 slices bread; 9 eggs, beaten; 1/2 c. milk (approximately); 1 can cream of mushroom soup; 1 (16 oz.) container sour cream; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. shredded Cheddar cheese; mushrooms, bell peppers, onions (optional); 1 loaf French bread; 6 eggs; 1 1/2 c. milk; 4 Tbsp. sugar, divided; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1/8 tsp. salt; cinnamon; powdered sugar; 3 c. (1 lb.) strawberries, stemmed and sliced; 1/2 tsp. lemon zest; 1 tsp. lemon juice; 12 slices cinnamon bread; 1 pt. (16 oz.) half and half or light cream; 4 large eggs; 6 Tbsp. butter or margarine, melted; 2 Tbsp. brown sugar, firmly packed; 2 tsp. vanilla extract; 1 tsp. grated orange peel (optional); 1/4 tsp. cinnamon; 1/8 tsp. nutmeg; powdered sugar (used for garnish only). Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Lightly grease a 13 x 9 baking pan. Arrange bread slices in two layers.

title: Old-Fashion Egg Custard
text: Old-Fashion Egg Custard. Ingredients: 5 eggs; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 3 (8 oz.) cans Ensure; 2 tsp. vanilla; 2 pie shells; nutmeg. Directions: Mix first 4 ingredients well; pour into pie shells and bake at 350° until you can cut with a knife and it will come out clean. Sprinkle top with nutmeg while pies are hot.

title: Berry Banana Smoothie
text: Berry Banana Smoothie. Ingredients: 1 small banana, peeled and cut up; 1/4 c. fresh or frozen berries (strawberries, raspberries and/or blackberries); 1 c. orange juice; 3 Tbsp. vanilla low-fat yogurt. Directions: Combine ingredients in a blender; cover and blend until smooth. Makes 2 (8 ounce) servings.

title: Orange Sour Cream Dip
text: Orange Sour Cream Dip. Ingredients: 1 (6 oz.) can frozen orange juice, thawed; 1 1/4 c. cold milk; 1 (3 oz.) box instant vanilla pudding; 1/4 c. sour cream. Directions: Combine orange juice and milk in blender. Add pudding and blend on low speed 2 minutes. Stir in sour cream. Chill 2 hours. Serve with fresh fruit.

title: Deluxe Potato Bake
text: Deluxe Potato Bake. Ingredients: 2 eggs, beaten; 1/4 c. unseasoned dry bread crumbs; 2 green onions with tops, chopped; 2 Tbsp. milk; 3/4 tsp. Lawry's seasoned pepper; 1/2 tsp. Lawry's seasoned salt; 2 large potatoes, peeled, grated and laid on ice; 1 c. (4 oz.) grated Cheddar cheese; 4 bacon slices, cooked and crumbled. Directions: In a large bowl, combine eggs, bread crumbs, onion, milk, seasoned pepper and seasoned salt. Drain potatoes and stir in egg mixture. Add 1/2 of Cheddar cheese and 1/2 bacon. Spoon mixture into lightly greased 8 x 8 x 2-inch casserole. Bake, uncovered, in 350° oven for 20 minutes. Sprinkle with remaining cheese and bacon. Bake 5 minutes longer. Garnish with additional cooked bacon and parsley.

title: Fruited Ice Tea
text: Fruited Ice Tea. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. instant tea powder; 5 c. cold water; non-caloric liquid sweetener equal to 1/4 c. sugar; 1/2 c. orange juice; 1/2 c. lime juice. Directions: Combine instant tea powder and the cold water; stir until the tea is dissolved. Add sweetener, orange juice and lime juice. Mix thoroughly. Chill. Serve over ice in tall glasses. Makes 6 servings. Garnish with orange slices and fresh cranberries.

title: Beet And Carrot Bread
text: Beet And Carrot Bread. Ingredients: 3 eggs, unbeaten; 3/4 c. oil; 1/2 c. sugar; 2 Tbsp. water; 2 tsp. vanilla; pinch of salt; 2 c. flour; 1 1/2 tsp. soda; 1 c. carrot and 1 c. beet, raw and ground or shredded. Directions: Mix all together. Pour into greased and floured bread pans (1 pan for large loaf or 2 medium loaves). Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

title: Coconut Cake And Icing
text: Coconut Cake And Icing. Ingredients: 3 eggs; 1 c. coconut milk or 1/2 coconut milk and 1/2 regular milk; 3 c. sifted cake flour; 3/4 to 1 c. shredded coconut; 1 tsp. vanilla; 2 c. white sugar; 3/4 c. Crisco; 2 1/2 tsp. baking powder; pinch of salt; coconut extract to taste. Directions: Cream eggs, sugar and Crisco. Add milk, baking powder, flour, vanilla and coconut. Bake 30 minutes at 350°.

title: Chinese Egg-Drop Soup
text: Chinese Egg-Drop Soup. Ingredients: 3 c. chicken broth; 1 Tbsp. cornstarch; 2 Tbsp. cold water; 1 green onion, thinly sliced; 1 beaten egg. Directions: Put chicken broth in saucepan. Bring to a boil. Immediately reduce heat to simmer. Dissolve cornstarch in water; add to chicken broth, stirring until slightly thickened. Add onion and stir. Add egg very slowly, stirring to break it up. Serve immediately.

title: Chocolate Peanut Bars
text: Chocolate Peanut Bars. Ingredients: 1/2 c. sugar; 3/4 c. peanut butter; 1 tsp. vanilla; 4 c. Special K cereal; 1 c. butterscotch bits; 3 (1 oz.) chocolate bars (Hershey); 1/2 c. light corn syrup. Directions: Combine sugar and syrup until it comes to a boil. Remove from heat and stir in peanut butter and vanilla. Add cereal and press into buttered 9-inch square pan. Melt chocolate bars and butterscotch pieces and spread on top.

title: Meatball Yummies
text: Meatball Yummies. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground chuck; 1/3 c. dry cracker crumbs; 1 tsp. salt; 2 eggs; 2 Tbsp. cooking oil; 2 Tbsp. A.1. steak sauce; 1/3 c. A.1. steak sauce; 3 Tbsp. brown sugar; 2 Tbsp. margarine. Directions: Combine beef, cracker crumbs, eggs, salt and 2 tablespoons A.1. steak sauce. Mix well; shape into 1-inch balls. Brown in skillet. Drain off fat. Combine 1/3 cup steak sauce, brown sugar and margarine. Heat mixture; pour over meatballs. Simmer, covered, for 15 minutes, or until meatballs are done. Makes about 2 dozen meatballs.

title: Honey Mustard Dressing(Great Christmas Gift!)
text: Honey Mustard Dressing(Great Christmas Gift!). Ingredients: 1/3 c. oil; 1/3 c. Dijon mustard; 1/3 c. honey; 1 3/4 Tbsp. cider vinegar; 2 c. mayonnaise; onion salt; celery salt; cayenne pepper; 1/2 tsp. mustard seed (optional). Directions: Combine all ingredients in a food processor or blender. Blend until creamy. Refrigerate, covered. Will keep for weeks. Yields 2 1/2 cups.

title: Pineapple Bread Pudding
text: Pineapple Bread Pudding. Ingredients: 1/2 c. butter; 1 c. sugar; 4 large eggs; 1 (No. 2) can crushed pineapple (undrained); 5 slices white bread, crusts removed. Directions: Cream butter and sugar. Add eggs one at a time, beating well. Mix in pineapple and juice. Cube bread and add to pineapple mixture. Bake in a greased casserole at 350° for 1 hour. Serves 6.

title: Barbecued Roast Beef Sandwiches
text: Barbecued Roast Beef Sandwiches. Ingredients: Zesty Barbecue Sauce; 1 lb. cooked roast beef, thinly sliced and cut into 1-inch strips (3 c.); 6 hamburger buns, split. Directions: Make Zesty Barbecue Sauce. Stir beef into sauce. Cover and simmer about 5 minutes or until beef is hot. Fill buns with beef mixture.

title: Tangy Chicken
text: Tangy Chicken. Ingredients: 1 cut up fryer or 6 breasts; 1 pkg. dry onion soup mix; 1 (8 oz.) bottle Catalina dressing; 1 can whole cranberries; 1 large onion, cut fine; 1 large bell pepper, cut fine; 3 Tbsp. shortening; 1 lb. hamburger meat; 2 1/2 c. canned tomatoes (I use two cans stewed tomatoes and cut them up if they need it); 1/2 c. uncooked rice (I use Minute rice); 1 tsp. chili powder (you've got to be kidding! I put this in until I feel like I've got enough; I like to be able to taste the spicy taste, so put a lot of this in there!); 1 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. black pepper. Directions: Cook onion and green pepper slowly in shortening until onion is yellow. Add meat and cook until it gets brown. I always drain the grease off right here. Add tomatoes, rice and seasoning. Put in casserole dish (large size) and bake in oven at 350° for 1 hour. I hope this will be a super dish for you, too.

title: Ambrosia Bowl
text: Ambrosia Bowl. Ingredients: 4 large oranges; 1 can (13 1/4 oz.) pineapple chunks; 2 Tbsp. orange juice; 4 medium bananas; 4 Tbsp. confectioners sugar; 1 can (3 1/2 oz. flaked coconut; 6 maraschino cherries. Directions: Peel oranges, remove white membrane. Cut oranges crosswise into 1/8 inch thick slices. Drain pineapple, saving syrup. Combine syrup and orange juice; set aside. Peel bananas. Cut diagonally into 1/8 inch thick slices.

title: Dill Weed Potatoes
text: Dill Weed Potatoes. Ingredients: 8 medium size potatoes; 3/4 c. butter; 2 1/2 tsp. dill weed; salt. Directions: Wash potatoes and wrap individually in plastic wrap. Cook in microwave for 8 minutes on High on each side. Cool while still wrapped for easier peeling. Cut in bite sizes. Melt butter and dill weed. Pour over potatoes and salt to taste. Serve hot!

title: Caramel Topping
text: Caramel Topping. Ingredients: 1/4 c. butter or margarine; 2 Tbsp. cream; 1 c. packed brown sugar; 1/2 c. chopped nuts; 1/2 c. coconut (optional). Directions: Combine butter and cream in small mixing bowl. Microwave at Medium 1 1/4 to 3 minutes or until butter is melted. Stir in brown sugar, nuts and/or coconut. Spread carefully on warm cake. Microwave on High 2 to 3 minutes or until topping bubbles in a few spots. Use as frosting on Oatmeal Cake.

title: Prune Whip
text: Prune Whip. Ingredients: 1 (12 oz.) pkg. pitted prunes; 2 tsp. lemon juice; 2 egg whites; dash of salt; 1/4 c. sugar; 1/4 c. heavy cream, whipped; whipped cream or Custard Sauce (see recipe). Directions: Crook prunes as package label directs. Drain prunes, reserving liquid. Remove 1/2 cup cooked prunes for later. Puree remaining prunes in blender or food mill with 1/2 cup of cooking liquid. Add lemon juice. Let cool. With mixer at high speed, beat egg whites with salt until frothy. Gradually beat in sugar, beat until stiff peaks form. Add puree of prunes 1/4 cup at a time, beating well. Beat at high speed, 2 minutes. Chop remaining prunes and fold into mixture, along with 1/4 cup cream, whipped. Turn into 6 or 8 sherbet dishes. Refrigerate. Garnish each with a little whipped cream, if desire or serve with custard sauce. Makes 6 or 8 servings.

title: Christmas Morning Pie
text: Christmas Morning Pie. Ingredients: 1 lb. pork sausage, cooked and crumbled; 1 c. (4 oz.) shredded Swiss cheese; 1 c. (4 oz.) shredded Cheddar cheese; 2 (9-inch) unbaked pie shells; 6 eggs, lightly beaten; 1 c. milk; 1/2 c. chopped onion; 1/3 c. chopped green bell pepper (optional). Directions: Line pie pans with pie shells. In bowl, combine sausage and cheeses. Place half of mixture in each shell. Combine eggs, milk, onion and peppers. Pour half over sausage in each shell. Bake at 350° for 55 to 60 minutes or until knife inserted near center comes out clean. Let stand 5 minutes before cutting.

title: Picnic Stuffed Eggs
text: Picnic Stuffed Eggs. Ingredients: 12 hard-cooked eggs; 1/2 c. mayonnaise; 1/4 c. sweet pickle relish, drained; 1 Tbsp. honey mustard; 1 tsp. garlic salt; 1/2 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/4 tsp. pepper. Directions: Slice eggs in half lengthwise; remove yolks and set whites aside. In a small bowl, mash yolks with a fork. Add the mayonnaise, pickle relish, mustard, garlic salt, Worcestershire sauce and pepper; mix well. Stuff or pipe into the egg whites. Refrigerate until serving. Makes 2 dozen.

title: Onion Dip For Veggies Or Chips And Pretzels
text: Onion Dip For Veggies Or Chips And Pretzels. Ingredients: 2 chicken bouillon cubes; 1/4 c. water; 1 large (8 oz.) pkg. softened cream cheese; 1/4 c. onion, chopped. Directions: Boil bouillon cubes with water. Using an electric mixer, add cream cheese and onion. Should make a day ahead and refrigerate. Warm to almost room temperature before serving. Stir well and thin with a little milk if needed.

title: Easy Pot Roast
text: Easy Pot Roast. Ingredients: 2 cans Campbell's celery soup; 2 pkg. Lipton onion soup mix; 4 lb. pot roast meat (chuck, eye of the round, etc.). Directions: Put meat in heavy-duty aluminum foil in roasting pan; pour celery soup over meat (undiluted). Add onion soup mix. Close foil so steam doesn't get out. Roast at 350° for 3 hours. Makes delicious gravy.

title: Hot Cheese Ball
text: Hot Cheese Ball. Ingredients: 2 (8 oz.) cream cheese; 1 medium onion, chopped or minced; 2 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 3 tsp. Accent seasoning; 2 pkg. dried beef, chopped. Directions: Mix all ingredients. Place in small crock-pot until melted. Serve with crackers.

title: Nevada Beans
text: Nevada Beans. Ingredients: 6 slices bacon, cut in 1/2 inch pieces; 1 large onion, chopped; 1 (16 oz.) can baked beans; 1 (15 oz.) can red kidney beans (undrained); 1 (15 oz.) can butter beans, drained; 1/2 c. catsup; 1/4 c. yellow mustard; 1/4 c. Worcestershire sauce; 4 Tbsp. brown sugar; 1 Tbsp. dark molasses. Directions: Cook bacon and onion about 10 minutes. Combine with rest of ingredients and pour into greased 2 quart casserole. Bake, uncovered, at 350° for 1 hour. Makes 10 to 12 servings.

title: Apple Cinnamon Crunch Cereal
text: Apple Cinnamon Crunch Cereal. Ingredients: 4 c. rolled oats; 1/2 c. coconut (optional); 1 c. finely chopped nuts; 1/2 c. sesame seed; 3/4 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 c. honey; 1/3 c. vegetable oil; 1/2 tsp. vanilla; 8 oz. finely cut dried apples. Directions: Combine first 6 ingredients in a large bowl. Combine the honey, oil and vanilla together and add to dry ingredients. Spread on 2 large greased baking pans and bake 20 to 25 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add the apples. Store in covered container in refrigerator.

title: Round Steaks Smothered In Onions
text: Round Steaks Smothered In Onions. Ingredients: 2 1/2 to 3 lb. round steak; 1 large onion, sliced; salt and pepper to taste; 1 1/2 c. flour; 3 Tbsp. oil. Directions: Trim excess fat from steak and cut into serving size pieces. Season steak with salt and pepper. Dredge in flour and lightly pound floured steak with a mallet or edge of saucer. Brown steak in hot oil in skillet. Add onion and enough water, just to cover the steak and onions.

title: Gladys' Meat Loaf
text: Gladys' Meat Loaf. Ingredients: 2 1/2 lb. hamburger; 1/2 lb. sausage; 1 c. onion, fried in butter; 1/2 c. beef bouillon; 2/3 c. Cheddar cheese; 1 Tbsp. garlic powder; 1/2 tsp. oregano; 3 bay leaves; 2 eggs; 1 c. bread crumbs; 2 tsp. salt; 2 tsp. black pepper; 1/2 tsp. thyme; 1/2 tsp. allspice. Directions: Mix all ingredients together well, except cheese and bay leaves. Place in a baking pan with bay leaves on top. Bake at 350° for 1 1/2 hours. Remove bay leaves and top with cheese until cheese is melted.

title: Queso Napolitano(Cream Cheese Flan)
text: Queso Napolitano(Cream Cheese Flan). Ingredients: 2/3 c. sugar; 4 eggs; 6 oz. cream cheese (room temperature); 14 oz. sweetened condensed milk; 1 3/4 c. milk; 1/2 tsp. vanilla extract. Directions: Heat sugar in skillet over medium-high heat until melted, clear and light brown, stirring occasionally. Spoon syrup over bottom and around sides of 1 1/2-quart baking dish. Set aside.

title: Hearty Tomato Soup
text: Hearty Tomato Soup. Ingredients: 4 (10 oz.) cans chicken broth; 2 large cans V-8 juice; 5 pkg. George Washington seasoning. Directions: Mix all ingredients together and heat.

title: Christmas Morning Casserole
text: Christmas Morning Casserole. Ingredients: 1 lb. cooked sausage, drained and crumbled; 1 c. sharp cheese, grated; 6 eggs; 2 c. milk; dash of Tabasco sauce; dash of Worcestershire sauce; salt; pepper. Directions: Grease an oblong pan. Layer sausage, bread and cheese in pan. Mix all other ingredients. Slowly pour mixture over layers. Place in refrigerator overnight. The next morning, bake at 350° for 50 to 60 minutes.

title: Skyline Chili(Ala Clark)
text: Skyline Chili(Ala Clark). Ingredients: 2 lb. hamburger; 5 c. water; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 2 large onions; 2 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 16 oz. can tomato paste; 1 Tbsp. chili powder; 1/2 tsp. vinegar; 1/2 tsp. allspice; 3 bay leaves; 1 tsp. cumin; 1 tsp. red pepper; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. pepper. Directions: Chop onions. Put hamburger and water in a large pot first, then all the other ingredients at one time. Simmer for 3 hours. Makes 2 quarts. Serve over spaghetti. Top with Cheddar cheese and oyster crackers.

title: Cauliflower Soup
text: Cauliflower Soup. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. butter; 1 Tbsp. olive oil; 1 medium chopped onion; 6 to 8 chopped scallions; 1 clove chopped garlic; 2 stalks chopped celery; 2 medium heads cauliflower, cored and chopped; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. curry powder (optional). Directions: Cook onion, cauliflower and celery in water in a large saucepan until tender. Mix in remaining ingredients and simmer until thoroughly heated.

title: South Of The Border Chicken And Rice
text: South Of The Border Chicken And Rice. Ingredients: 1 can cream of mushroom soup; 1 can cream of chicken soup; 1 1/2 c. chicken broth; 1 can diced Ro-Tel tomatoes; 3 c. cubed, cooked chicken; 1 can Mexicorn; 2 c. shredded, Cheddar cheese; 2 1/2 c. instant rice. Directions: Combine soups, broth and Ro-Tel in large saucepan. Heat until well-blended. Spread chicken in bottom of a 9 x 13-inch pan.

title: Chicken Breasts In Sour Cream
text: Chicken Breasts In Sour Cream. Ingredients: 8 boned and skinned chicken breasts; bacon (1 strip around each breast); 1 can cream of chicken soup; 8 oz. sour cream. Directions: Wrap chicken breasts with bacon; arrange in casserole. Mix soup and sour cream and put on top of breasts. Bake at 275° for 3 hours.

title: Cream Squares
text: Cream Squares. Ingredients: 1 yellow cake mix; 1 egg; 1 stick margarine; 8 oz. pkg. cream cheese; 1 box powdered sugar; 1 tsp. vanilla; 2 eggs. Directions: Melt butter and pour into cake mix. Add egg. Mix and press into 9 x 13-inch pan or baking dish. Take cream cheese, powdered sugar, vanilla and eggs and pour over cake mix. Bake 30 to 35 minutes at 350°.

title: Hearts Of Romaine Salad
text: Hearts Of Romaine Salad. Ingredients: 1 large head romaine lettuce; 1 c. loosely packed basil leaves; 1 tsp. minced shallots; 1 tsp. white vinegar; 1 Tbsp. Dijon mustard; 1/2 c. olive oil; 1/4 c. freshly ground Parmesan cheese; salt and fresh ground pepper. Directions: Remove dark green outer leaves and discard. Tear remaining leaves into quarter pieces; rinse and dry. Add into food processor the basil, shallots, vinegar and mustard and process briefly (three or four short bursts). Add oil and cheese and process in two short bursts, just to combine. Divide romaine hearts among four chilled plates and drizzle dressing over. Garnish with basil sprigs and toasted pine nuts.

title: Hershey'S Hot Fudge Sauce
text: Hershey'S Hot Fudge Sauce. Ingredients: 2 (13 oz.) cans evaporated milk; 1 lb. butter; 14 1/2 oz. bag Kraft caramels; 2 c. sugar; 1/3 c. Karo syrup; 12 oz. Hershey chocolate chips; 1 tsp. vanilla flavoring. Directions: Combine in order, except vanilla, in large saucepan over medium heat. Stir until smooth and melted. Do not boil. Reduce to low heat and beat for 7 minutes with mixer, then add vanilla and beat 1 more minute. Cool and then refrigerate. Will keep in refrigerator about 3 weeks and this freezes well.

title: Tenderheart Tarts
text: Tenderheart Tarts. Ingredients: 1 c. smooth peanut butter; 1/2 c. honey; 1 1/4 c. dry milk; raspberry jam. Directions: Mix peanut butter and honey. Mix in dry milk. Squeeze it and pound it until it is a smooth ball (soft). Roll into the size of a marble; press to make cookie. Make into heart shape. Makes about 30 hearts. Put dab of jam in middle of each heart.

title: "Easy Divinity"
text: "Easy Divinity". Ingredients: 2 c. sugar; 1/2 c. water; 1 (7 oz.) Marshmallow Creme; 1/2 c. chopped nuts; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Boil sugar and water until forms hard ball in cold water. Place Marshmallow Creme in bowl. Pour sugar and water mixture over creme. Beat until stiff; feed in nuts and vanilla. Drop from teaspoon onto wax paper.

title: Herman Pancakes
text: Herman Pancakes. Ingredients: 1 c. Herman Starter; 1 c. flour; 2 1/4 tsp. baking powder; 1/4 tsp. each: baking soda and salt; 1 egg; 2 Tbsp. oil; 1/2 to 3/4 c. milk, depending on thickness desired. Directions: Mix well, using scant 1/4 cup batter per pancake. Bake on well-greased, preheated griddle. Makes 10 large pancakes.

title: Peach-Pear Jelly
text: Peach-Pear Jelly. Ingredients: about 25 medium pears; 8 c. sugar; 2 pkg. Sure-Jell; 8 c. pears, blended; 3 pkg. peach Jell-O. Directions: Peel and cut up pears. Cook until soft. Blend pears in blender, then follow directions for jelly on Sure-Jell package. Pour in sterile jars and seal.

title: Left Over White And Sweet Potatoes
text: Left Over White And Sweet Potatoes. Ingredients: left over mashed potatoes; left over whole sweet potatoes; butter; salt; pepper; paprika. Directions: Cut up sweet potatoes and mix into mashed potatoes. Grease baking dish with butter. Put potatoes into dish. Cover with chunks of butter. Sprinkle salt, pepper and paprika. Bake at 350° for 20 to 25 minutes or until potatoes are heated through.

title: Aunt T'S String Pie
text: Aunt T'S String Pie. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; 1/2 c. chopped onion; 1/4 c. chopped green pepper; 1 (15 1/2 oz.) jar Prego spaghetti sauce; 1 large can tomato sauce; 1/2 c. shredded Mozzarella; 8 oz. spaghetti hot, cooked and drained; 1/3 c. Romano cheese; 2 eggs, beaten; 2 Tbsp. butter; 1 c. Ricotta cheese. Directions: Cook beef, onion and green pepper in a large skillet over medium high heat until meat is brown, stirring to separate meat. Drain fat. Stir in sauces; mix well. Combine spaghetti, Romano cheese, eggs and butter in large bow; mix well. Place in bottom of 13 x 9-inch pan. Spread Ricotta cheese over top. Pour sauce mixture over Ricotta cheese. Sprinkle Mozzarella cheese over top. Bake in pre-heated oven 350° until cheese melts, approximately 20 minutes.

title: One Punch Pineapple Punch
text: One Punch Pineapple Punch. Ingredients: 1 (46 oz.) can pineapple juice, chilled; 3 c. apricot nectar; 1 qt. club soda; 1 qt. pineapple sherbet. Directions: In a large punch bowl, combine the pineapple juice and apricot nectar. Just before serving, add the club soda and sherbet. Makes 25 to 30 servings.

title: Grandma Dyer'S Potato Salad
text: Grandma Dyer'S Potato Salad. Ingredients: 5 lb. potatoes; milk and mayonnaise to taste; 1/2 c. sweet pickles; 1/2 c. chopped onion; 4 boiled eggs; salt, pepper and paprika to taste. Directions: Boil potatoes. Mash potatoes with milk and mayonnaise to taste. Add sweet pickles and chopped onion. Slice boiled eggs and put on top of potato salad. Salt, pepper and paprika to taste. Serve warm.

title: Pecan Pralines I
text: Pecan Pralines I. Ingredients: 1 c. white sugar; 1 c. brown sugar (1/2 light and 1/2 dark); 1 dash salt; 1/2 c. milk; 2 Tbsp. white Karo syrup; 2 Tbsp. butter; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 1/2 c. pecan halves. Directions: Mix sugar, salt, milk and Karo syrup in heavy, 3-quart pan and cook to soft ball stage. Add butter and vanilla. Beat until cool. Add pecans and drop by tablespoon onto wax paper. When firm, store in closed container. Makes approximately 2 dozen pralines.

title: Spinach Parma
text: Spinach Parma. Ingredients: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. spinach, thawed and drained; 2 c. seasoned crushed croutons; 1/4 c. melted oleo; 1 c. small curd cottage cheese; 4 oz. Swiss or other cheese, cubed; 3 eggs, beaten; 1/4 c. chopped onion; 2 Tbsp. sour cream; 1 clove garlic, minced; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/4 c. Parmesan cheese. Directions: Combine croutons and oleo; pat into quiche pan. Combine 1/4 cup Parmesan cheese and remaining ingredients; mix well. Spoon over crust. Bake at 350° for 35 minutes. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese. Let stand 5 minutes.

title: Lazy Daisy Cake
text: Lazy Daisy Cake. Ingredients: 2 eggs; 1 c. sugar; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 c. cake flour; 1 tsp. baking powder; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. milk; 2 Tbsp. butter. Directions: Combine first 3 ingredients and beat well, then add dry ingredients which have been sifted together. Scald milk and add butter. Combine the 2 mixtures and beat well. Pour into square, flat pan and bake in moderate oven at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes. Remove from oven and add frosting, then return to hot oven for frosting to brown.

title: Sweet-Sour Beans And Sprouts(5 Servings)
text: Sweet-Sour Beans And Sprouts(5 Servings). Ingredients: 1 lb. green beans (fresh), trim and cut into long strips; 1 Tbsp. vegetable oil; 2 Tbsp. chopped onions; 1 Tbsp. white vinegar and soy sauce; 1 Tbsp. sugar; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 c. fresh bean sprouts. Directions: Steam or boil green beans and bean sprouts until just tender. Saute onions in oil until golden; add remaining ingredients and bean sprouts cooking liquid. Heat, then add beans and sprouts. Stir to mix and heat through. Serve. Save liquid.

title: Skillet Beef Stew
text: Skillet Beef Stew. Ingredients: 1/2 (12 oz.) pkg. medium noodles; 1 lb. beef flank steak, cut in 1-inch cubes; 3 medium carrots, sliced thin; 2 medium onions, sliced thin; 1 (10 to 12 oz.) pkg. mushrooms, cut in half; 2 Tbsp. Kikkoman soy sauce; 1 Tbsp. catsup; 1 1/2 tsp. cornstarch. Directions: In saucepan, cook noodles and drain. In medium bowl, combine soy sauce, catsup, cornstarch and 1/2 teaspoon of salt. Add flank steak to mix and stir until covered. In skillet over medium-high heat, add 2 teaspoons of oil and cook carrots and onions until crisp and lightly browned. Remove to another bowl. In same skillet over high heat, add 2 more teaspoons of oil. Cook mushrooms until tender and liquid evaporates. Remove to bowl with carrots and onions. Add steak to oil with mixture. Cook about 5 minutes. Stir in veggies and 1/2 cup of water; heat through. Stir occasionally. Serve over noodles.

title: Dorothy'S Muffins
text: Dorothy'S Muffins. Ingredients: 3/4 c. milk; 1/4 c. melted margarine, cooked or oil; 1 egg; 1 c. all-purpose flour; 1 c. quick cooking oatmeal; 1/3 c. sugar; 1 Tbsp. baking powder; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. nutmeg; 1 c. orange marmalade. Directions: Heat oven to 400°. Grease bottoms only of muffin tins or use paper liners. Beat egg, melted margarine and milk. Combine dry ingredients and add to egg mixture. Stir until flour is moistened. Stir in marmalade, mixing well. Divide equally into 12 prepared muffin tin cups. Bake for 18 to 20 minutes. Remove from pan immediately.

title: Gingersnap Cookies
text: Gingersnap Cookies. Ingredients: 1 c. sugar; 3/4 c. shortening; 1/4 c. molasses; 1 beaten egg; 2 c. flour; 2 1/4 tsp. baking soda; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1 tsp. ginger; 1/4 tsp. salt. Directions: In large bowl, beat together sugar and shortening; add molasses. Mix well. Add beaten egg. Stir together all remaining ingredients. Mix into molasses mixture until thoroughly combined. Cover and chill at least 1 hour or overnight. Roll dough into 1-inch balls, then place balls into granulated sugar. Place balls 2 inches apart on ungreased cookie sheet. Bake at 375° for 8 to 10 minutes.

title: Date Filled Cookies
text: Date Filled Cookies. Ingredients: 1 box dates or 2 c., cut in pieces; 1 c. water; 1 c. sugar; 2 Tbsp. lemon juice; 1 c. Crisco (shortening); 1 1/2 c. brown sugar; 2 eggs; 3 c. flour, sifted; 1 tsp. cream of tartar; 1 tsp. soda; 1 tsp. salt; 1 1/2 c. rolled oats; 1 Tbsp. hot water; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Combine dates, 1 cup water, sugar and lemon juice. Cook until thick, then cool. While date mixture cools, make cookie dough.

title: Nana'S Cornbread(For 9-Inch Iron Skillet Or 8 X 8-Inch Pan)
text: Nana'S Cornbread(For 9-Inch Iron Skillet Or 8 X 8-Inch Pan). Ingredients: 1 c. corn meal; 1 c. flour; 1 to 2 Tbsp. sugar; 1/2 tsp. salt; 2 tsp. baking soda; 1 egg; 1 c. buttermilk; 1/4 c. bacon fat. Directions: Stir 1/2 teaspoon baking soda into 1 cup buttermilk. Melt 1/4 cup bacon fat into pan you are using in the oven you are preheating. Mix ingredients. Bake at 425° for 20 to 25 minutes or until golden brown.

title: 1 - 2 - 3 Chocolate Sauce
text: 1 - 2 - 3 Chocolate Sauce. Ingredients: 3 sq. Chocolate; 2 c. Sugar; 1 can Evaporated Milk; Pinch Salt. Directions: Mix and bring to a boil, stirring constantly. Let it boil 1 minute, then add 1 teaspoon vanilla. Mix well for 1 minute. Serve warm.

title: Harvard Beets
text: Harvard Beets. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 2 Tbsp. flour; 1/4 c. boiling water; 1/4 c. vinegar; 1/3 c. brown sugar; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 1/8 tsp. ground cloves; 2 1/2 c. diced, cooked beets. Directions: Melt fat and stir in flour. Gradually add boiling water and cook until thickened, stirring constantly. Add vinegar, sugar and seasonings. Mix well until sugar dissolves. Add beets and cook until thoroughly heated. If canned beets are used, substitute 1/4 cup liquid from the can of beets for the water. Makes 6 servings.

title: Cracker Jacks
text: Cracker Jacks. Ingredients: 3 Tbsp. dark syrup; 1 Tbsp. butter; 1/2 c. sugar; 1/3 c. water; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. soda. Directions: Boil the first 5 ingredients until it spins a thread. Then add soda and pour over one gallon popped corn.

title: Icebox Pie Crust
text: Icebox Pie Crust. Ingredients: 1 Tbsp. sugar; 1 stick oleo, melted in pie plate; 1 1/2 c. flour. Directions: Melt oleo in pie plate; add sugar. Dump in flour. Mix well with fork and pat with fingers. Press in pie plate and push up on sides. Prick with fork. Bake until light brown at 350°. Don't worry if it cracks! Let cool and fill with your favorite pie mixture.

title: Quick Corn Relish
text: Quick Corn Relish. Ingredients: 1 (12 oz.) can Mexican-style corn; 1/3 c. sugar; 1/3 c. white vinegar; 1/3 c. sweet pickle relish; 2 Tbsp. instant minced onion; 1/2 tsp. celery seeds. Directions: Combine first 6 ingredients in a small saucepan; bring to a boil over medium heat. Cover and simmer 10 minutes. Remove to a bowl; cover and refrigerate 4 to 8 hours. Yield: 2 cups.

title: Greens With Hot Dressing
text: Greens With Hot Dressing. Ingredients: lettuce and spinach greens; 1 or more chopped green onions; 6 slices bacon, cut into small pieces; 2 Tbsp. cider vinegar; 2 Tbsp. water; 1 tsp. light brown sugar; 1/2 tsp. salt; dash of black pepper (about 1/8 tsp.). Directions: Wash, drain and dry lettuce and greens. Add chopped green onions; set aside. Fry bacon in a large skillet until crisp; drain. Set aside skillet with the hot bacon fat still in it. Combine the vinegar, water, sugar, salt and pepper in a bowl. From the reserved skillet, pour off all but about 3 tablespoons of hot fat. Pour the vinegar mixture into the skillet. Place skillet over the hot coals; bring mixture to a boil. Remove from heat and pour over the lettuce and spinach greens.

title: White Buttermilk Cake
text: White Buttermilk Cake. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. sugar; 3 egg whites; 1 c. Crisco; 1 c. buttermilk; 2 tsp. baking powder; 1/4 tsp. soda; 2 c. flour; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Cream sugar and Crisco. Add buttermilk, egg whites and vanilla. Mix dry ingredients and add to mixture. Pour in greased cake pans and bake at 350° for 25 to 35 minutes.

title: Simply Easy Chicken(It Really Is!)
text: Simply Easy Chicken(It Really Is!). Ingredients: 1 chicken breast half per person (boneless and skinless); your favorite Italian dressing *. Directions: *Do not use fat-free or any of the diet dressings.

title: Very Best Burrito
text: Very Best Burrito. Ingredients: 1 c. milk; 1 small instant vanilla pudding; 3 1/2 c. Cool Whip; 2 c. sliced strawberries; 1/2 c. sugar; 1 Tbsp. cinnamon; 10 flour tortillas; 1/2 c. melted butter. Directions: Combine milk and pudding mix and blend 1 minute. Let stand 5 minutes. Fold in 2 cups of Cool Whip. Add about 1 1/2 cups of strawberries. In bowl combine sugar and cinnamon. Butter both sides of tortillas. Sprinkle one side with the sugar/cinnamon mixture. Put in 350° oven, sugar/cinnamon side up, only about 2 minutes until bubbly. Take out of oven and let stand 3 to 4 minutes. Fill shells with sugar side out. Top each with Cool Whip and add sliced strawberries.

title: Creamy Coleslaw
text: Creamy Coleslaw. Ingredients: 12 lb. shredded cabbage; 9 carrots, shredded; 3 c. chopped bell peppers; 3 tsp. salt; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 1 1/2 c. canned milk; 1 1/2 c. apple vinegar; 6 c. Miracle Whip salad dressing. Directions: Mix together and refrigerate. Reduce 1/3 to 1/4 for family.

title: Noodle Bake Supreme
text: Noodle Bake Supreme. Ingredients: 3 c. cooked wide noodles (4 c. uncooked); 1 (12 oz.) jar Heinz homestyle chicken gravy; 1/2 c. creamed cottage cheese; 1/2 c. dairy sour cream; 1/4 c. grated Parmesan cheese; paprika. Directions: Combine first 4 ingredients. Pour into a lightly greased 1 1/2-quart oblong baking dish. Sprinkle Parmesan cheese over noodle mixture, then dust with paprika. Bake in a 350° oven for 30 to 35 minutes or until hot. Let stand 5 minutes before serving. Makes 6 servings.

title: Popcorn Trash
text: Popcorn Trash. Ingredients: 6 qt. popped popcorn (not seasoned); 1 lb. pkg. plain M&M's; 1 large jar salted, roasted peanuts; 2 sticks margarine; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 1/2 c. white Karo syrup; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Mix first 3 ingredients in a large bowl. Cook last 4 ingredients to 234° or soft ball stage. Pour over popcorn mixture and mix well. As soon as mixture cools enough to work with, spread onto 2 to 3 buttered cookie sheets and press out. Bake 1 hour at 200°. Cool. Break into pieces and store in airtight container.

title: Open-Faced Chicken Sandwiches
text: Open-Faced Chicken Sandwiches. Ingredients: 2 1/2 c. diced, cooked chicken; 1 1/2 c. diced celery; 1/2 tsp. salt; 2 tsp. grated onion; 3/4 c. mayonnaise; 1 Tbsp. lemon juice; 8 buns; butter; 1/2 c. American cheese, shredded; 1 c. crushed corn chips. Directions: Combine chicken and celery. Mix salt, onion, mayonnaise and lemon juice. Mix into chicken. Split buns. Place, cut side down, on broiler rack. Brown lightly. Turn and butter cut side. Spread each with 1/4 cup chicken mixture. Toss cheese and corn chips together. Sprinkle over chicken. Broil 3-inches from heat until cheese is melted and slightly browned. Place on tray; garnish.

title: Chicken Veronique
text: Chicken Veronique. Ingredients: 2 whole, large chicken breasts; 2 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 1 c. sliced fresh mushrooms; 2 Tbsp. sliced green onion; 1/3 c. chicken broth; 1 Tbsp. flour; 1 c. seedless grapes, halved; 1/4 c. dry white wine; 3 c. hot cooked rice. Directions: Skin and bone chicken breasts lengthwise. Cut chicken into 1-inch pieces; set aside.

title: Reesie Cup
text: Reesie Cup. Ingredients: 1 box confectioners sugar; 1 1/2 stick butter; 1 c. peanut butter; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Dip in chocolate and wax.

title: Ham-Dijon Pasta Salad
text: Ham-Dijon Pasta Salad. Ingredients: 1 (7 oz.) pkg. refrigerated rigatoni, uncooked; 2 c. cubed, cooked ham; 1 c. (4 oz.) shredded Swiss cheese; 1 carrot, scraped and thinly sliced; 1/2 tsp. freshly ground pepper; 1 (8 oz.) carton plain low-fat yogurt or nonfat sour cream alternative; 1 Tbsp. Dijon mustard; lettuce leaves. Directions: Cook pasta according to package directions; drain. Rinse with cold water; drain. Combine pasta and next 4 ingredients. Set aside.

title: Artichoke And Shrimp Soup
text: Artichoke And Shrimp Soup. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. butter, melted; 2 Tbsp. flour; 1 minced onion; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 1 can beef broth; 3 cups milk; 2 cups grated American cheese (Velveeta may be used); 1/4 cup snipped fresh parsley; 1 lb. shrimp; 3 cans artichoke hearts (cauliflower or broccoli may be substituted). Directions: Melt butter; saute onion. Add flour and blend. Stir milk in slowly, stirring until smooth. Add broth and seasonings. Cook until slightly thickened on low heat. Add cheese, parsley and artichokes along with shrimp. Cook about 30-45 minutes. (If fresh cauliflower or broccoli are used, parboil until almost tender before adding to soup.)

title: Cherry Macaroon Parfaits
text: Cherry Macaroon Parfaits. Ingredients: 1 (6 serving) box instant vanilla pudding mix; 3 c. cold milk; 1 (21 oz.) can cherry pie filling; 1/4 tsp. almond extract; 6 macaroon cookies, crumbled; whipped topping (if desired). Directions: Prepare pudding with milk as directed on package. Combine cherry filling and extract. Alternate layers of pudding, crumbled cookies and filling in 6 parfait or dessert dishes. Garnish with topping. Makes 6 servings. Preparation time is 10 minutes.

title: Hearty Chicken Stew
text: Hearty Chicken Stew. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. vegetable oil; 1 medium onion, finely chopped; 3 cloves garlic, minced; 1 Tbsp. minced fresh ginger or 1 tsp. ground ginger; 2 Tbsp. flour; 2 medium tomatoes, diced; 8 to 10 peppercorns or to taste; 6 whole cloves; 1 cinnamon stick; 8 pieces chicken (about 2 1/2 lb.), skinned and fat removed; 2 c. water; 1 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 3/4 lb. baby carrots (fresh or frozen); 6 oz. green peas (fresh or frozen); 8 oz. red or white new potatoes, scrubbed and unpeeled. Directions: In a deep skillet or Dutch oven, heat oil over medium-high heat. Saute onion, ginger and garlic until onion is soft, about 3 minutes. Add flour and cook 1 minute, stirring quickly to prevent sticking. Stir in tomatoes and allow to cook another 2 minutes.

title: Lamb Roast
text: Lamb Roast. Ingredients: 3 lb. roast; 3/4 c. white wine; 4 Tbsp. lemon juice; 1 Tbsp. garlic powder; 1 bay leaf; favorite barbecue sauce; 3/4 c. water; flour. Directions: Flour lamb roast and place in baking pan with 3/4 cup of water. Cover pan and place in oven at 500° for 30 minutes. Remove roast from oven. Add the white wine, lemon juice, garlic powder and bay leaf. Cover pan again; cook for 3 hours at 350°. Lastly, remove pan from oven and debone roast. Pour favorite barbecue sauce over meat in pan to suit individual taste. Broil meat in oven for 5 minutes or less.

title: Easy Baked Crock Pot Beans
text: Easy Baked Crock Pot Beans. Ingredients: 1 pound ground meat, browned; 1 cup chopped green pepper; 3/4 cup chopped onion; 2 Tbsp. spicy mustard; 1/2 tsp. cumin; 1/2 tsp. oregano; 1/2 cup brown sugar; 1 12-oz. bottle Heinz chili sauce; 1 31-oz. and 1 16-oz. cans pork and beans (not Bushes). Directions: Brown meat, green peppers and onions. Combine all ingredients in slow cooker for 4 to 6 hours on low.

title: Mexi-Cali Chicken Soup
text: Mexi-Cali Chicken Soup. Ingredients: 2 cups onion, chopped; butter or margarine; 1 46 oz can cream of chicken soup; 1 46 oz can cream of mushroom soup; 3 quarts chicken broth; 1 quart Milnot; 6 small cans Rotel tomatoes and chilies; 3 1/2 pounds cooked, boned chicken; 1 1/2 tablespoons chili powder; 1 1/2 tablespoons garlic powder; 12 flour tortillas, chopped. Directions: Saut onion in butter. Mix all ingredients except tortillas. Bring to a boil. Add chopped tortillas. Top with grated cheese (cheddar and Mozzarella), chopped tomatoes, olives, crushed tortilla chips and chopped green onions. Serves 30-40.

title: Zesty Carrots
text: Zesty Carrots. Ingredients: 6 large carrots (about 1 1/4 lb.); 2 Tbsp. onion, finely chopped; 2 Tbsp. prepared horseradish; 1/2 c. mayonnaise; salt and pepper; 1/4 c. bread crumbs; 2 Tbsp. butter, melted. Directions: Cut carrots, julienne style. Cook until tender (about 6 to 8 minutes). Drain carrots, reserving 1/4 cup liquid. Turn carrots into a 1 1/2-quart baking dish. In small bowl, combine onion, horseradish, mayonnaise, reserved liquid from carrots and salt and pepper to taste. Pour over carrots, spreading evenly. Toss bread crumbs with butter; sprinkle over top. Bake 15 minutes at 375°. Makes 6 servings.

title: One Pan Dinner
text: One Pan Dinner. Ingredients: 6 small pork chops; 3 sweet potatoes; 1 onion, chopped; 1 (4 oz.) can mushrooms; 1 can cream of mushroom soup. Directions: In electric frypan, brown pork chops and onion. Wash sweet potatoes and microwave 10 minutes on High.

title: Pineapple Drops
text: Pineapple Drops. Ingredients: 1/2 c. Crisco shortening; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 2 eggs; 1/2 c. crushed pineapple; 1/2 tsp. soda; 3 c. flour; 1 tsp. baking powder; 1/2 tsp. salt. Directions: Mix shortening, sugar and eggs. Add crushed pineapple. In a separate bowl, stir all the dry ingredients together real good. Add dry ingredients. Drop by teaspoonful onto a lightly greased cookie sheet. Bake at 350° for about 10 minutes.

title: Tangy Ham Dip
text: Tangy Ham Dip. Ingredients: 1 c. sour cream; 2 (3 oz.) cans deviled ham; 2 tsp. chopped chives; 1 Tbsp. prepared horseradish; 1/4 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; potato or corn chips. Directions: Mix sour cream, deviled ham, chopped chives, horseradish and Worcestershire sauce in bowl. Blend well. Chill for 1 hour. Serve with potato or corn chips.

title: Ice Cream "Cake"
text: Ice Cream "Cake". Ingredients: 1 bag Oreo cookies, chop/crush fine; 1/4 stick margarine, softened/melted; 1/2 gallon vanilla ice cream, softened/melted; 1 jar fudge ice cream topping, softened in microwave for easier pouring; 1 (8 oz.) container Cool Whip. Directions: Mix first two ingredients and press firmly in a 9 x 13-inch pan. then layer ice cream, fudge and Cool Whip. It works best if you freeze each layer. Decorate top with whatever!

title: Bessie'S Ritz Cracker Pie
text: Bessie'S Ritz Cracker Pie. Ingredients: 3 egg whites; 1 tsp. baking powder; 1 c. chopped nuts; 1 c. sugar; 1 tsp. vanilla; 20 Ritz crackers, crumbled; 1/2 pt. whipped cream; 3 Tbsp. powdered sugar. Directions: Combine whipped cream and powdered sugar. Spread over crust when cooled. Let set 3 to 4 hours. When ready to serve, top with sweetened fresh fruit.

title: Butterscotch Crunch Squares(Frozen Dessert)
text: Butterscotch Crunch Squares(Frozen Dessert). Ingredients: 1 C. sifted flour; 1/4 C. quick oats; 1/4 C. brown sugar; 1/4 C. margarine; 1/2 C. chopped nuts; 12 oz. butterscotch ice cream topping; 1 qt. ice cream. Directions: Combine flour, oats, and sugar. Cut in butter until mix resembles coarse crumbs. Stir in nuts. Pat mix into 13 x 9 inch pan and bake in a 400 degree oven for 15 minutes. Stir while warm to crumble and then cool.

title: Holiday Hot Nuts
text: Holiday Hot Nuts. Ingredients: 1 1/3 c. (about 7 oz.) whole almonds; 1 1/4 c. (about 5 oz.) pecan halves; 1 c. (about 4 oz.) walnut halves; 1 Tbsp. vegetable oil; 1 tsp. cumin; 1 tsp. curry powder; 1/4 tsp. cayenne pepper; 1/8 tsp. white pepper; 1/4 tsp. salt (optional). Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. In a large bowl, toss nuts with oil. In a small bowl, combine all spices except salt. Add the spices to nuts, tossing nuts to cover evenly with spices. Spread the nuts on a baking sheet in a single layer. Bake for 10 minutes. If desired, sprinkle with salt. Makes about 3 cups.

title: Light Chicken And Pasta Salad
text: Light Chicken And Pasta Salad. Ingredients: 1 cup low fat cottage cheese, 2%; 1/3 cup fresh orange juice; 1 1/2 teaspoons white wine vinegar; 3/4 teaspoon basil; 1/2 teaspoon salt; 1/4 teaspoon pepper; 1/4 teaspoon grated orange zest; 1- 8 oz box of bow tie pasta, cooked and drained; 2 cups chicken breast, cooked and drained; 2 tablespoons chopped parsley; 1 tomato, diced. Directions: In a blender, puree the first 6 ingredients until smooth. When done, stir in orange zest. Then stir mix with cooked pasta. Add chicken, tomato, and parsley last. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour before serving.

title: Peanut Butter Delights
text: Peanut Butter Delights. Ingredients: 2 c. quick cooking oats; 1/2 c. peanut butter; 1 tsp. vanilla; 2 c. sugar; 1/3 c. cocoa; 1/2 c. butter; 1/2 c. milk; pinch of salt. Directions: Mix sugar, cocoa, butter, milk and salt in saucepan and bring to a rolling boil. Boil one minute, then remove from heat and add oats, peanut butter and vanilla. Blend well. Drop by teaspoon on waxed paper. Let stand until cool and firm; remove from paper. Ready to eat.

title: Fresh Tomato Pizza
text: Fresh Tomato Pizza. Ingredients: 6 medium, firm tomatoes, sliced thin; 1 (13 to 16-inch) baked pizza crust; 2 Tbsp. olive oil; 1 tsp. pepper; 1 (2 1/4 oz.) sliced, ripe olives, drained (optional); 1/2 c. diced green pepper; 1/2 c. diced onion; 1 Tbsp. chopped, fresh basil or 1 tsp. dried basil; 1/4 tsp. garlic powder; 1 tsp. dried oregano; 1 c. (4 oz.) shredded Mozzarella cheese; 1 c. (4 oz.) shredded Cheddar cheese. Directions: Place tomato slices in a circle on crust, overlapping slightly, until crust is completely covered. Drizzle with olive oil. Cover with olives, if desired, green pepper and onion. Sprinkle seasonings over all. Cover with cheese. Bake at 400° for 15 minutes or until cheese is melted. Serve immediately. Yields 8 servings.

title: Turkey Sopa
text: Turkey Sopa. Ingredients: 1 medium onion, chopped; 1 small green pepper, chopped; 1 c. celery, chopped; 2 Tbsp. oil; 1 can cream of chicken or mushroom soup; 1/2 (10 oz.) can tomatoes and green chilies; 1 (5.3 oz.) can evaporated milk; 2 c. turkey, chopped; 1 pkg. soft corn tortillas, cut in quarters; 1 1/2 c. grated cheese. Directions: Saute onion, green pepper and celery in oil. Stir in soup, tomatoes and milk. Mix well. Stir in turkey. Layer turkey mixture, tortillas and cheese in a greased casserole dish. Begin with the turkey mixture and end with it. Top with cheese. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes. Makes 6 to 8 servings.

title: Peachy Angel Food Dessert
text: Peachy Angel Food Dessert. Ingredients: 1 prepared angel food cake; 2 (1.3 oz.) envelopes whipped topping mix; 2 (3 oz.) pkg. cream cheese, softened; 1 c. confectioners sugar; 1 (21 oz.) can peach or cherry pie filling. Directions: Tear cake into bite size pieces; set aside. Prepare whipped topping according to package directions; set aside. In a large mixing bowl, beat the cream cheese and confectioners sugar. Fold in whipped topping. Add cake pieces; stir gently to coat evenly. Spoon into a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan. Top with pie filling. Chill until ready to serve. Yields 12 servings.

title: Mexican Beef In A Crock-Pot
text: Mexican Beef In A Crock-Pot. Ingredients: 1 1/2 to 2 lb. boneless round steak; 1 clove garlic, minced; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 Tbsp. chili powder; 1 Tbsp. mustard; 1 onion, chopped; 1 beef bouillon cube, crushed; 1 (15 oz.) can tomato sauce; 1 can red beans, drained. Directions: Spread meat with mixture of garlic, pepper, salt, chili powder and mustard. Cut into 1/2-inch wide strips. Place in slow cooking pot. Cover with onion, bouillon cube and tomato sauce. Cover and cook on low for 6 to 8 hours. Add beans and cook on high, covered for 30 minutes. Serve on a bed of rice. Makes 5 to 6 servings.

title: Champagne Salad
text: Champagne Salad. Ingredients: 8 oz. cream cheese; 10 oz. frozen strawberries, thawed; 2 bananas, diced; 1/2 c. walnuts, chopped; 12 oz. Cool Whip; 3/4 c. sugar; 1 c. crushed pineapple, drained. Directions: Mix together cheese and sugar. Fold in berries, bananas, pineapple and walnuts. Blend in 12 ounces Cool Whip. Pour in 9 x 13-inch pan and freeze. Remove from freezer 1 hour before you serve.

title: Cold Garden Pizza
text: Cold Garden Pizza. Ingredients: 2 cans crescent rolls; 2 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese, softened; 1 c. mayonnaise; 1 tsp. dill weed; 2 tsp. chopped onions (very fine); shredded Cheddar or Mozzarella cheese; chopped vegetables (tomatoes, bell peppers, etc.); black olives. Directions: Mix 2 packages of softened cream cheese with mayonnaise, dill weed and chopped onions. Bake crescent rolls at 350° for 12 to 15 minutes. Spread out on a cookie sheet or pizza pan; let cool.

title: Coconut Meringue Cookies
text: Coconut Meringue Cookies. Ingredients: 3 egg whites; 2/3 c. sugar; 2 tsp. vanilla (white); 2 cans (4 oz. each) flaked coconut. Directions: Beat egg whites until foamy, white and double in volume in a medium bowl. Beat in sugar, 1 tablespoon at a time, beating all the time until meringue stands in firm peaks. Fold in vanilla and coconut. Drop by teaspoon, 2 inches apart, on greased and floured large sheets. Bake in very slow oven (250°) for 35 minutes or until lightly golden. Remove from sheet to wire racks. Let stand to cool completely.

title: Stuffed Cherry Tomatoes
text: Stuffed Cherry Tomatoes. Ingredients: 1/2 c. chopped cooked ham; 1/4 c. Swiss cheese; 1/2 c. sour cream; 1/4 tsp. lemon juice; dash of pepper; 36 cherry tomatoes; parsley flakes. Directions: Combine first 5 ingredients in a small bowl. Refrigerate until ready to use. Cut tops from tomatoes and scoop out insides. Put 1/2 teaspoon of mixture in each tomato and garnish with parsley.

title: Sweet Potatoes In Apple Cups
text: Sweet Potatoes In Apple Cups. Ingredients: 4 medium sweet potatoes; 3 Tbsp. butter; 1/2 tsp. salt; 2 Tbsp. sugar; 4 red-skinned baking apples; 4 marshmallows. Directions: Boil sweet potatoes until tender. Peel and mash. Add butter, salt and sugar to potatoes and blend thoroughly. Scoop out inside of apples, leaving a margin of about 1/4-inch all around. Fill apples with mashed sweet potatoes. Place in a baking dish and bake in a moderate oven at 325° for about 15 minutes, until apples are cooked. Place marshmallows on top of potato mixture and return to oven to allow marshmallows to brown. Serves 4.

title: Marinade(For Boneless Chicken Breasts)
text: Marinade(For Boneless Chicken Breasts). Ingredients: 4 Tbsp. soy sauce; 4 Tbsp. oil; 1/2 tsp. basil; 1/2 tsp. garlic powder (adjust to preference); 1 Tbsp. brown sugar. Directions: Mix all together in Ziploc bag or container. Add 4 chicken breast halves. Turn occasionally. Marinate for at least 2 hours to overnight, whichever you prefer. Best done on gas grill. Great done under broiler 10 minutes each side.

title: Best Cake Ever
text: Best Cake Ever. Ingredients: 1 Duncan Hines golden butter cake mix; 1/2 c. water; 1/2 c. oil; 3 eggs; 1 (8 oz.) carton sour cream; 1 (4 serving size) pkg. instant vanilla pudding; 1 (4 to 6 oz.) bar German chocolate, grated well; 1 (6 oz.) pkg. chocolate chips. Directions: Mix all ingredients together, except chocolate chips and German chocolate; beat 2 minutes. Grate chocolate; mix into cake batter. Stir in chocolate chips. Bake at 350° for 1 hour in a greased and floured tube or Bundt pan.

title: Crispy Parmesan Chicken
text: Crispy Parmesan Chicken. Ingredients: 3 lb. frying chicken, cut in pieces; 1 egg, slightly beaten; 2 Tbsp. milk; 3/4 c. cornflakes, crushed; 1 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 1/2 c. Parmesan cheese. Directions: Combine cornflakes, salt, pepper and cheese; set aside. Wash and pat dry chicken pieces. Combine beaten egg and milk. Dip chicken pieces in egg mixture and roll in cornflakes mixture. Place in well-greased baking dish (can use Pam spray), skin side up. Bake for 1 hour at 350°. Do not cover or turn chicken while baking.

title: Filled Tartlets
text: Filled Tartlets. Ingredients: 2 c. milk; 3 beaten eggs; 1/2 c. grated Swiss cheese; dash of nutmeg; salt and freshly ground white pepper; 2 strips cooked bacon, crumbled; 1 (6 oz.) can shredded crabmeat, shell and cartilage removed; 28 tartlet shells (see following recipe). Directions: Scald milk. Remove from heat and let cool. Stir in eggs, cheese and seasoning. Divide mixture between two bowls. Add bacon to one mixture, crabmeat to the other. Preheat oven to 400°. Arrange tartlet shells on greased baking sheet. Fill 14 shells with bacon mixture and 14 with crabmeat mixture. Bake for 15 minutes. Serve warm. Makes 28 appetizers.

title: Spinach Dip For Fresh Vegetables
text: Spinach Dip For Fresh Vegetables. Ingredients: 1/2 c. plain low-fat yogurt; 1/4 tsp. prepared mustard; 2 drops Worcestershire sauce; pinch of garlic powder; 1 tsp. grated Parmesan cheese; 1/2 c. cooked chopped spinach. Directions: Combine all ingredients and stir well. Stir before serving. Makes 1/2 cup.

title: Lime Salad Chiffon With Graham Crackers
text: Lime Salad Chiffon With Graham Crackers. Ingredients: 1/4 c. butter, melted; 1 c. graham cracker crumbs; 1 (3 oz.) pkg. lime Jell-O; 1 c. boiling water; 1 can Eagle Brand milk; 1 (8 oz.) can crushed pineapple (undrained); 2 Tbsp. lime juice; 4 c. miniature marshmallows; 1 c. Cool Whip. Directions: Make the crust with butter and cracker crumbs. Put into an 8-inch square pan. Chill.

title: Chicken Rotel
text: Chicken Rotel. Ingredients: 1 large fryer; 1 can Ro-Tel tomatoes; 3 cans cream of chicken soup; 1 1/2 c. chicken broth; 1 large pkg. Doritos; 1/2 lb. grated cheese. Directions: Cook chicken. Remove from bone. Cut in bite size pieces. Add tomatoes, soup and chicken broth and heat until bubbly. In 9 x 13-inch baking dish, make a layer of Doritos, then chicken, soup mixture, then cheese. Repeat and finish with cheese. Bake at 350° for 25 minutes.

title: Linguini With Fresh Clam Sauce
text: Linguini With Fresh Clam Sauce. Ingredients: 20 cherrystone clams; 2 cloves garlic; pepper; 1/3 c. chopped parsley; 1/3 c. olive oil. Directions: Chop garlic and brown in skillet with olive oil. Shuck clams, reserving the juice. Chop clams and add to garlic and oil. Cook for 3 minutes. Add parsley and pepper; simmer for 5 minutes. Serve over freshly cooked linguini.

title: Potato Deluxe Casserole
text: Potato Deluxe Casserole. Ingredients: 2 lb. frozen hash browns*; 1 c. onions, diced; 1 can cream of chicken or cream of celery soup; 1 lb. carton sour cream; 1 stick melted butter or margarine; 8 oz. or more sharp Cheddar cheese, grated; salt and pepper to taste; dash of garlic salt. Directions: Thaw hash browns 30 minutes before using. Mix all ingredients in large bowl. Put into 9 x 13-inch pan. Sprinkle crushed potato chips over top. Dab with butter, if desired. Bake at 375° for 1 hour.

title: Butterscotch Morsels
text: Butterscotch Morsels. Ingredients: 3 (16 oz.) pkg. butterscotch morsels; 2 large cans chow mein noodles; 1 (12 oz.) bag salted peanuts. Directions: Melt butterscotch in double boiler. Then stir in peanut and noodles. Drop by teaspoonfuls onto wax paper and let harden (about 45 minutes).

title: Party Cheese Ball
text: Party Cheese Ball. Ingredients: 2 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese; thin sliced ham or turkey, cut in small pieces; 1/4 tsp. red pepper; 1 c. pecans, finely chopped; 1 bunch green onions, chopped fine; 1 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; crackers. Directions: Let cream cheese soften at room temperature for about 1 hour. Using plastic gloves, mix cream cheese, ham or turkey (use as much as desired), green onions, red pepper and Worcestershire sauce; mix well and form into a ball. Roll in pecans until covered and store in refrigerator. Serve with your favorite crackers.

title: Cooley'S Creamy Peaches
text: Cooley'S Creamy Peaches. Ingredients: 3 c. firm peaches, sliced; 2 c. water; 2/3 c. brown sugar; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 1/2 Tbsp. flour; 1/4 c. cream; cinnamon. Directions: Cook peaches in water until tender. Add brown sugar, salt and flour mixed with the cream into a smooth paste. Cook until mixture thickens, stirring constantly. Sprinkle with cinnamon before serving.

title: Hot And Tasty Dip
text: Hot And Tasty Dip. Ingredients: 8 oz. cream cheese; 1 c. sour cream; 1 pkg. dried beef; 1/4 c. onion, finely chopped; 2 tsp. Worcestershire sauce. Directions: Microwave at Medium the cream cheese, in 1-quart casserole, 30 to 45 seconds until soft. Blend in remaining ingredients. Microwave 5 to 6 minutes until hot, stirring after 3 minutes. Serve with cut vegetables, crackers or potato chips.

title: Dijon Chicken
text: Dijon Chicken. Ingredients: 6 whole chicken breasts, halved; pepper; 1 1/2 c. sour cream; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. Italian breadcrumbs; parsley; salt; garlic powder; 1 1/2 c. Dijon mustard. Directions: Lightly sprinkle chicken with salt, pepper and garlic powder. Combine sour cream and mustard in a shallow dish, mixing well. Dip each chicken breast into mustard mixture and dredge in breadcrumbs. Arrange chicken in single layer in baking pans. Bake at 375° for 50 minutes.

title: Feather Light Pancakes
text: Feather Light Pancakes. Ingredients: 1 egg; 1 c. milk; 2 Tbsp. salad oil; 1 c. flour; 2 Tbsp. baking powder; 2 Tbsp. sugar; 1/2 tsp. salt. Directions: Heat griddle to 400°. Mix all ingredients together in blender on high. Wipe small amount of salad oil over top of griddle. Pour batter onto griddle for pancake size desired. When bubbles occur, flip and brown. Makes 12 small or 8 large pancakes.

title: Sugar-Free Doughnuts
text: Sugar-Free Doughnuts. Ingredients: 1/2 c. shortening; 1/2 c. margarine; 1 1/2 c. mashed potatoes; 1 1/2 c. potato water; 1 c. artificial sweetener; 2 eggs, beaten; 1 pkg. yeast; 10 c. unbleached flour; oil for deep frying. Directions: Cream shortening and margarine in bowl. Add mashed potatoes, potato water, sweetener and egg; beat well. Sprinkle with yeast. Stir in gently. Let stand for 1 1/2 hours in warm place. Stir in flour gradually. Knead on floured surface until smooth. Let rise, covered, in warm place 5 to 6 hours or doubled in bulk. Roll out on floured surface 1/2-inch thick. Cut to desired size. Place on floured towel. Let rise in warm place 1 1/2 to 2 inches or until doubled in bulk. Deep fry in 375° oil until light brown. Drain on paper towels. Yields 50 doughnuts.

title: Beef Macaroni Casserole
text: Beef Macaroni Casserole. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; 1 c. chopped onion; 1 clove garlic, chopped; 1 Tbsp. paprika; 2 (8 oz.) cans Hunt's tomato sauce; 1 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 c. (8 oz.) sour cream; 1 (8 oz.) box macaroni. Directions: Brown beef; add onion and garlic. Add other ingredients. Turn into 2-quart casserole. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes. Serves 4. Prep time: 40 minutes.

title: Aunt Eva'S Old Fashion Peach Shortcake
text: Aunt Eva'S Old Fashion Peach Shortcake. Ingredients: 2 c. flour; 2/3 c. sugar; 2 tsp. baking powder; 1/4 c. melted margarine; 1 egg; milk; sliced, sweetened peaches (use fresh or frozen peaches). Directions: Sift first 3 ingredients. In a measuring cup, put melted margarine and egg, then finish filling to 1 cup with milk. Add to flour mixture and beat well. Put into greased and floured 9-inch cake pan. Bake at 350° for 20 to 25 minutes. Cool. Split in half and put peaches between and on top of shortcake. Serve with ice cream or Cool Whip. Makes a beautiful dish and it is delicious.

title: Dip For Fruit
text: Dip For Fruit. Ingredients: 1 jar marshmallow cream; 1 little tub fruited or plain Philadelphia cream cheese. Directions: Mix ingredients together real well. (Strawberry flavor is real good with peaches and nectarines.) Serve with bananas, apples, grapes and strawberries.

title: Hot Dog Hash
text: Hot Dog Hash. Ingredients: 3 cans sliced potatoes; 1 medium onion; 3 Tbsp. flour; salt and pepper; 1/2 c. milk; 1/2 lb. hot dogs, sliced; 3 Tbsp. butter; 1/2 c. shredded cheese. Directions: Combine potatoes and onion. Sprinkle on flour, salt and pepper. Add milk and hot dogs. Put in shallow baking dish. Dot with butter. Bake for 30 minutes at 425°. Top with cheese until melted.

title: Pecan Shortbread Cookies
text: Pecan Shortbread Cookies. Ingredients: 1 c. unsalted butter; 1 c. light brown sugar, loosely packed; 1/2 tsp. vanilla; pinch of salt; 2 1/4 c. cake flour, sifted; 1/2 c. pecans, finely chopped. Directions: In a mixing bowl, beat together the butter, light brown sugar, vanilla and salt until light and fluffy. Stir in the flour and nuts, just enough to combine. Do not overmix or cookies will be tough. Form the dough into a ball and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

title: Easy Bread Pudding
text: Easy Bread Pudding. Ingredients: 9 slices bread; 1 large can Pet milk; 2 Tbsp. margarine; 2 tsp. vanilla; 1/2 c. sugar; 4 eggs, separated; 2 c. milk; 1/4 c. sugar. Directions: Soak bread in Pet milk. Beat egg yolks and 1/2 cup sugar. Add milk and margarine. Mix everything and cook until it starts to boil and gets thick. Pour into buttered dish. Beat egg whites and add 1/4 cup sugar. Pour over bread mixture and brown in oven.

title: Whipped Margarine
text: Whipped Margarine. Ingredients: 1 lb. block regular margarine; 1 c. Mazola oil; 1 c. buttermilk. Directions: Let margarine set at room temperature for a while. Cream until soft. Add Mazola and whip more. Add buttermilk and continue beating. Fill 4 (1-cup) margarine containers or 2 large containers.

title: Turkey And Dressing
text: Turkey And Dressing. Ingredients: 1 turkey, seasoned and baked as directed; turkey stock (broth); 1 full recipe cornbread, cooked and crumbled; 1/2 loaf bread or 1 can biscuits, crumbled; 4 celery stalks, chopped; 1 large bell pepper, chopped; 1 small bunch green onions, chopped; 6 hard-boiled eggs, diced; salt and pepper to taste; sage to taste. Directions: In saucepan, combine celery, bell pepper and onions. Cook until tender and season lightly, if desired. In a large mixing bowl, combine crumbled cornbread and crumbled loaf bread. Add sauteed vegetables, boiled eggs and seasonings; toss well. You may also add Pepperidge herb dressing for taste. Now, add turkey broth and mix until desired consistency is reached. You may stuff turkey and arrange turkey around or just bake in pan until done. Use a 350° oven.

title: Velvety Chicken Pate
text: Velvety Chicken Pate. Ingredients: 1/4 c. butter; 1/2 lb. chicken livers, halved; 1 yellow onion, chopped; 1 tart apple, chopped; 2 Tbsp. brandy; 2 Tbsp. whipping cream; 1/4 c. butter (room temperature); salt and pepper to taste. Directions: In medium skillet, saute butter and chicken livers for 2 minutes. Add onion and apple and stir for 5 minutes until apple softens. Stir in brandy and cream. Transfer mixture to blender and blend 2 to 3 minutes or until smooth. Refrigerate this mixture for 1 hour.

title: Wild Rice Rose(Serve With Lamb Or Chicken)
text: Wild Rice Rose(Serve With Lamb Or Chicken). Ingredients: 1 c. wild rice; 1 c. grated sharp American cheese; 1 c. sliced mushrooms; 1 c. chopped olives; 1/2 c. chopped onion; 1 c. hot water; 1/2 c. salad oil (although I use 1/4); 1 c. canned tomatoes; 1/2 tsp. garlic salt. Directions: Soak rice overnight; drain. Wash and drain. Mix with remaining ingredients. Place in buttered 2-quart casserole dish. Cover and bake for 1 hour at 350°. Serves 4 to 6.

title: Chocolate Sin Cake
text: Chocolate Sin Cake. Ingredients: 6 oz. chocolate chips; 3/4 c. chopped pecans; 1 box chocolate cake mix; 4 eggs; 1/2 c. oil; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 small pkg. instant chocolate pudding; 8 oz. sour cream; 1 jar Hershey's Chocolate Shoppe topping (optional). Directions: Toss chocolate chips and pecans in 1 tablespoon cake mix. Beat together remaining ingredients, except topping, for 3 minutes, then fold in chocolate chips/pecan mixture.
