Pour into greased, floured Bundt or tube pan. Bake at 350° for 50 minutes. Cool on wire rack 10 to 15 minutes. Place cake on cake plate. Heat topping until warm (not hot) and fluid. Pour over cake. Serve warm.

title: Gram Christmas Punch
text: Gram Christmas Punch. Ingredients: 2 pkg. strawberry Kool-Aid, chilled; 2 cans Hawaiian Punch, chilled; 3 liters ginger ale, chilled; 2 qt. rainbow sherbet. Directions: Premix Kool-Aid according to directions on pack. Makes 1 gallon. Pour 1/2 Kool-Aid in punch bowl. Add 1/2 ginger ale and 1/2 sherbet. This will make 2 punch bowls full.

title: Scones From London, England
text: Scones From London, England. Ingredients: 2 c. sifted flour; 2 Tbsp. sugar; 1 Tbsp. baking powder; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/3 c. unsalted butter; 1 beaten egg; 3/4 c. milk; 1/3 c. raisins, dates or cherries. Directions: Mix dough. Place on floured surface and knead. Cut in half. Cut each into 8 pie-shaped pieces. Place half cuts on cookie sheet before cutting into 8 pieces. Bake for 12 minutes at 425°.

title: Turkey Pie
text: Turkey Pie. Ingredients: 3/4 c. chopped onion; 1 c. sliced celery; 1 to 1 1/4 c. diced carrots; water or mixture of water and broth to cook vegetables; 1 can condensed cream of chicken soup; 2 to 3 c. diced, cooked turkey; pie crust for top crust *. Directions: *If making your own crust, add a dash of thyme or poultry seasoning to flour. If using prepared crust, just cut strips to fit whatever pan you use.

title: Noodle Casserole
text: Noodle Casserole. Ingredients: 1/2 lb. ham; 1 lb. ziti; 1 c. heavy cream; 2 Tbsp. tomato paste; 1 to 2 egg yolks; salt; pepper; Parmesan cheese (optional). Directions: Cook ziti with salt. Cut ham into little squares. Drain ziti. Grease a casserole dish. Then put one layer ziti and one layer of ham into casserole dish.

title: Pizza Picnic
text: Pizza Picnic. Ingredients: 2 pkg. crescent rolls; 6 eggs; 8 oz. Parmesan or cottage cheese; 3/4 lb. ground beef; 1/2 oz. Provolone cheese; 1/2 lb. salami; 1/2 lb. boiled ham; 3 Tbsp. green pepper, chopped. Directions: Brown meat and set aside. Beat eggs until light. Add grated cheese and set aside. Grease a 10 x 13-inch pan. Line bottom with 1 package rolls. Spread 1/2 of cheese-egg mixture over rolls. Layer the following over cheese-egg mixture: meat and green pepper, half Provolone, ham, remaining Provolone, remaining egg mixture and salami slices. Cover with second package of rolls. Cover with foil. Bake at 350° for 1 hour. Uncover; bake at 375° for 15 minutes or until brown.

title: Evelyn'S Favorite Watermelon Pickles
text: Evelyn'S Favorite Watermelon Pickles. Ingredients: 3 1/2 qt. prepared watermelon rind; water (to cover); 8 tsp. alum; 1 qt. vinegar; 8 c. sugar; 1 Tbsp. whole cloves; 2 sticks cinnamon. Directions: Trim off the green and pink parts of the rind and cut into cubes. When you have 3 1/2 quarts prepared, cover with water and cook until just tender, about 20 minutes. Add alum to the water and let stand overnight. (If the weather is extremely hot and humid, keep the pan in the refrigerator.)

title: Zucchini Relish
text: Zucchini Relish. Ingredients: 10 c. ground zucchini, do not peel; 4 c. onions, cut-up; 4 Tbsp. coarse salt; 2 1/2 c. white vinegar; 1 Tbsp. each: nutmeg, dry mustard, turmeric, cornstarch; 4 1/2 c. sugar; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 1 red and 1 green pepper. Directions: Put zucchini, onion and salt in large bowl and mix well. Let stand overnight. Drain and rinse in cold water; drain again. Put mixture in a large kettle with remaining ingredients. Bring to a boil and simmer, uncovered, stirring occasionally for 30 minutes. Pour into jars and seal. Process in boiling water bath for 5 minutes.

title: Dobby Cake
text: Dobby Cake. Ingredients: 3 Tbsp. margarine; 1 c. sugar; 1 egg; 1 c. flour; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. soda; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. nutmeg; 1 tsp. vanilla; 3 c. diced, unpeeled apples; 1/2 c. chopped nuts. Directions: Cream the margarine, sugar and egg. Stir in flour, salt, soda, cinnamon, nutmeg and vanilla. Dough will be stiff. Fold in apples and nuts. Spread in pan sprayed with Pam (8 x 8 x 2-inch) and bake at 350° for 40 to 45 minutes.

title: Fried Cheese Balls
text: Fried Cheese Balls. Ingredients: 1 c. grated mild cheese; 2 tsp. flour; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. cayenne pepper; 2 egg whites, stiffly beaten; 3/4 c. fine cracker crumbs; 1/2 c. vegetable oil. Directions: Combine the cheese, flour, salt and cayenne pepper. Using your hands, gently stir in egg whites. Pat the mixture into small balls and roll them in cracker crumbs. Heat oil in skillet. When hot, put the balls in the pan without crowding them. Cover over medium-high heat, turning so all sides become golden brown. Drain on paper towels.

title: Marinated Shrimp Kabobs
text: Marinated Shrimp Kabobs. Ingredients: 1 lb. large unpeeled fresh shrimp; 1 (15 oz.) can pineapple chunks (undrained); 1 (8 oz.) bottle Italian salad dressing; 1 (8 oz.) can tomato sauce; 1 Tbsp. brown sugar; 1 tsp. prepared mustard; 1 medium-size green pepper, cut into 1-inch cubes; hot cooked rice. Directions: Peel and devein shrimp; set aside. Drain pineapple chunks, reserving 1/4 cup juice. Set pineapple aside.

title: Cranberry Banana Bread
text: Cranberry Banana Bread. Ingredients: 2/3 c. sugar; 1/3 c. butter, softened; 1/2 c. cranberries or raisins; 1 c. chopped pecans or walnuts; 2 eggs; 1 c. mashed bananas; 1 Tbsp. lemon juice; 2 c. all-purpose flour; 1 tsp. baking soda; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. baking powder. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Grease a 9 x 5-inch loaf pan. Beat sugar, butter and eggs in a large bowl until smooth. Stir in bananas and lemon juice. Mix flour, baking powder, baking soda and salt into bowl. Stir dry ingredients into creamed mixture. Fold berries and nuts. Pour into pan. Bake 50 to 60 minutes until toothpick is clean.

title: Orange-Cranberry Congealed Salad
text: Orange-Cranberry Congealed Salad. Ingredients: 1 large pkg. raspberry jello; 1 c. apples, diced; 1 c. celery, diced; 1 c. pecans, chopped; 1 can crushed pineapple, drained; 1 jar cranberry-orange relish. Directions: Drain pineapple; reserve juice. Mix one package of jello with 2 cups of hot water until completely dissolved. Add 2 cups cold liquids (juice from pineapple and cold water to make 2 cups liquid); stir. Chill jello until slightly jelled. Add other ingredients; mix well. Refrigerate until firm.

title: Cream Pie And Pudding(Microwave)
text: Cream Pie And Pudding(Microwave). Ingredients: 3/4 c. sugar; 3/8 c. flour; 1/4 tsp. salt; 2 eggs or 3 egg yolks; 2 c. milk. Directions: Microwave Directions: Combine sugar, flour and salt in deep 2-quart glass mixing bowl. Add eggs and milk. Beat with whisk until smooth. Microwave at Full power for 8 minutes, stirring 3 times. Proceed as recipe directs.

title: Mrs. Dobias' Kit Kat Bar
text: Mrs. Dobias' Kit Kat Bar. Ingredients: 2 c. crushed graham crackers; 1 c. oleo; 1/2 c. white sugar; 1 c. brown sugar; 1/2 c. milk; Club crackers. Directions: Mix all ingredients, except Club crackers, and boil 5 minutes, stirring constantly. Line a 9 x 13-inch pan with Club crackers; pour 1/2 mixture over crackers. Add layer of Club crackers; pour mixture over crackers. Add another layer of Club crackers. Then top with Topping.

title: Victorian Baked French Toast
text: Victorian Baked French Toast. Ingredients: 1 c. brown sugar; 1/3 c. butter; 2 Tbsp. light corn syrup; 8 slices French bread (1-inch thick); 5 eggs; 1 1/2 c. milk; 1 tsp. cinnamon. Directions: Cook brown sugar, butter and corn syrup in small saucepan until butter melts. Pour into 9 x 13-inch pan. Layer bread in pan, squeezing together to fit. Mix eggs, milk and cinnamon. Pour over bread (you can cover and refrigerate overnight). Bake at 350° for 30 minutes or until center is done and lightly browned.

title: Bernie'S Meatballs
text: Bernie'S Meatballs. Ingredients: 1 lb. chuck chop; 1/4 lb. ground veal; 1/4 lb. ground pork; 1/4 c. grated Parmesan cheese; 2 eggs; 1 tsp. salt. Directions: Mix all ingredients together. Form meatballs and brown.

title: Oven Porcupines
text: Oven Porcupines. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground meat; 1/2 c. uncooked rice; 1/3 c. water; 1/8 tsp. garlic salt; 15 oz. tomato sauce; 2 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/3 c. chopped onions; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. celery salt; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 1 c. water; 1 tsp. sugar. Directions: Heat oven to 350°. Mix meat, rice, 1/3 cup water, onion, salt, garlic and pepper. Shape into round balls. Place balls in an ungreased baking dish (8 x 8 x 2-inch). Stir together remaining ingredients and pour over meatballs. Cover with foil and bake 45 minutes. Uncover and bake 15 minutes longer.

title: Carolina Gold Barbecue Sauce
text: Carolina Gold Barbecue Sauce. Ingredients: 2 c. yellow mustard; 1/2 c. catsup; 1 1/2 c. light brown sugar; 1/4 c. apple cider vinegar; 1 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/2 tsp. Wright's liquid smoke; 2 tsp. coarse ground black pepper. Directions: Put all ingredients into saucepan over medium heat, mixing well with whisk. Heat thoroughly, whisking frequently to prevent burning. This is great on pork, beef and chicken (see Carolina Gold Barbecue Recipe in the Meat & Main Dishes section). If you're grilling the meat, don't put this on until the meat is cooked and ready to serve or it will burn.

title: No-Fat Carrot Cake
text: No-Fat Carrot Cake. Ingredients: 2 c. flour; 2 c. sugar; 2 tsp. baking soda; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1 tsp. allspice; 1 tsp. nutmeg; 2 c. grated carrots; 3 egg whites or 2 1/4 tsp. egg replacer, mixed in 1/4 c. water; 1 (20 oz.) can crushed pineapple with juice; 1 (8 oz.) can crushed pineapple, drained. Directions: In a large mixing bowl, mix all ingredients together and pour into a 9 x 13-inch nonstick pan. Bake at 350° for 40 to 45 minutes.

title: Golden Crumb Broccoli Casserole
text: Golden Crumb Broccoli Casserole. Ingredients: 2 pkg. frozen broccoli spears; 1 can cream of mushroom soup; 1/4 c. Hellmann's mayonnaise; 1/4 c. shredded sharp processed cheese; 1 Tbsp. chopped pimiento; 1 1/2 tsp. lemon juice; 1/3 c. cheese cracker crumbs. Directions: Cook broccoli until it is tender. Put in a casserole dish. Combine other ingredients and pour over broccoli. Top with cracker crumbs. Bake at 350° for 35 minutes. Serves 6 to 8.

title: Croutons
text: Croutons. Ingredients: 2 c. cubed bread; 1/4 c, oleo, melted; 1 tsp. parsley; 1 tsp. seasoned salt, garlic salt and onion salt or a combination; 2 Tbsp. Parmesan cheese. Directions: Toss bread cubes with butter and seasonings. Spread in a single layer in shallow pan. Bake in 300° oven, stirring occasionally until crisp and light brown, about 30 to 45 minutes. After cooling, store in airtight container for use in soups and on green salads. Store in refrigerator or freezer for extended storage.

title: Tostado Dip
text: Tostado Dip. Ingredients: refried beans; 1 pkg. taco mix; 1/2 pt. sour cream; avocado dip; grated Cheddar cheese; 4 large green onions, diced; 1 small can sliced black olives. Directions: Layer refried beans mixed with taco mix. Layer sour cream with carton of avocado dip. Layer grated Cheddar cheese. Layer green onions and black olives. Refrigerate until chilled. Serve with taco chips.

title: Pam'S Meat Loaf
text: Pam'S Meat Loaf. Ingredients: 2/3 c. dry bread crumbs; 1 c. milk; 1 1/2 lb. ground beef; 2 beaten eggs; 1/4 c. grated onion; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. sage; dash of pepper. Directions: Soak bread crumbs in milk; add meat, eggs, onion and seasonings. Mix well. Form in individual loaves; place in greased muffin pans. Cover with Piquant Sauce. Bake in a moderate 350° oven for 45 minutes. Makes 8 servings. Or form one loaf in 8 1/2 x 4 1/2 x 2 1/2-inch pan. Spread with sauce; bake 1 hour.

title: Cherry Dream Whip Dessert
text: Cherry Dream Whip Dessert. Ingredients: 2 c. flour; 1/2 c. brown sugar; 1 c. butter or oleo; 1 c. nuts. Directions: Crumble mixture. Pat into 9 x 13-inch pan. Bake at 400° for 15 minutes. Cool until it can be handled with fingers. Re-crumble and put back in pan; pat in hard.

title: Veal Broccoli
text: Veal Broccoli. Ingredients: 12 veal cutlets, cut about 1/4-inch thick; 2 Tbsp. butter; 1 medium onion, minced; 1 (4 oz.) can mushrooms; 1 c. bread crumbs; 2 Tbsp. chopped parsley; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 1/2 tsp. basil, minced; 1 clove garlic, minced; 2 Tbsp. all-purpose flour; 3/4 c. chicken stock; 3 Tbsp. butter. Directions: Pound veal on one side and set aside. In a large skillet over medium heat, melt butter and saute onion until tender. Cut mushrooms in half or drain canned mushrooms. Add to onion with parsley, salt, pepper, basil and garlic, stirring to combine. Add bread crumbs and 1/4 cup chicken stock.

title: Homemade Vanilla Ice Cream
text: Homemade Vanilla Ice Cream. Ingredients: 4 eggs; 2 c. sugar; 4 c. milk; 1 can Eagle Brand milk; 2 Tbsp. vanilla; 1/2 tsp. salt. Directions: Mix milk, Eagle Brand, vanilla and salt in small mixing bowl. In large mixing bowl, beat eggs until light; add sugar gradually beating constantly. Beat in mixture from small bowl. Pour into freezer and freeze according to freezer directions.

title: Mexican Corn Pudding
text: Mexican Corn Pudding. Ingredients: 1 c. yellow cornmeal; 1 c. grated cheese; 1 tsp. baking powder; 1 tsp. salt; 1 can cream-style corn; 2 eggs, beaten; 2 c. milk; 1 can diced chilies; 5 Tbsp. melted butter; garlic to taste. Directions: Just mix and bake 1 hour at 350°.

title: Mom'S Jelly Muffins
text: Mom'S Jelly Muffins. Ingredients: 1 2/3 c. flour; 1/2 c. sugar; 2 tsp. baking powder; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1/3 c. shortening; 3/4 c. milk; 1 egg. Directions: Preheat oven to 400°. Add ingredients according to order of recipe. Spoon into greased muffin tins or paper liners. Place 1/2 teaspoon to 1 teaspoon of any kind of jelly or jam on top of each muffin, pressing into batter. Try to bring batter over the top of jelly to cover it up. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes, until brown.

title: The Original Mexican Cornbread
text: The Original Mexican Cornbread. Ingredients: 1 c. sour cream; 2 c. self-rising meal; 2 eggs; 1 c. cream-style corn; garlic powder; 1 can Gebhardt green chopped chilies; 1 medium onion, chopped; 2/3 c. salad oil; 1 c. Colby Longhorn cheese, shredded. Directions: Combine all ingredients, except cheese. Make sure sides and bottom of iron skillet are well-oiled. Pour 1/2 mixture in hot skillet. Add shredded cheese. Pour other 1/2 on top of cheese. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes.

title: Charlotte Russe
text: Charlotte Russe. Ingredients: 1 Tbsp. unflavored gelatin; 1/2 c. cold milk; 3 egg yolks; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 1 1/2 c. milk, scalded; 2 to 4 Tbsp. cream sherry; 1 qt. whipping cream, whipped; 4 egg whites; about 8 ladyfingers, split lengthwise. Directions: Soften gelatin in 1/2 cup milk. Beat egg yolks and sugar until thick and lemon colored; stir in gelatin and scalded milk. Cook in top of a double boiler, stirring constantly, until thickened; cool. Stir in sherry and fold in whipped cream. Beat egg whites until stiff but not dry and fold into custard. Line a 3-quart bowl with ladyfingers; pour in filling and chill until set. Yields about 12 servings.

title: Never Fail Peanut Butter Fudge
text: Never Fail Peanut Butter Fudge. Ingredients: 3 c. white sugar; 2 c. brown sugar; 2/3 stick oleo; dash of salt; 3/4 c. milk; 1 1/2 c. peanut butter; 1 small jar Marshmallow Fluff; vanilla. Directions: Boil first 5 ingredients for 4 minutes. Add peanut butter, Marshmallow Fluff and vanilla. Beat well; put in greased pan.

title: Delicious Custard
text: Delicious Custard. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 1/4 c. cornstarch; 3/4 c. sugar; 1/2 tsp. salt; 2 c. milk; 2 slightly beaten egg yolks; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Melt butter. Blend in cornstarch, sugar and salt. Gradually add milk. Heat to boiling over direct heat. Stir into slightly beaten egg yolks. Return to heat and cook 2 minutes, stirring constantly. Add vanilla. Cool thoroughly. Makes 2 cups. Serve plain or with cream. (Excellent for banana cream pie.)

title: Fiesta
text: Fiesta. Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. ground turkey; 1 pkg. taco seasoning; 1 pkg. yellow rice dinner; 1 lb. Velveeta cheese; 2 cans Ro-Tel. Directions: Brown meat; drain. Add taco seasoning and 1/2 cup water. Simmer 5 to 10 minutes. Prepare rice according to directions on package. Melt Velveeta; add Ro-Tel. Mix well. Combine meat, rice and cheese dip. Pour into 9 x 13-inch dish. Top casserole with plain (broken up) Doritos chips. Bake at 400° for 15 minutes until bubbly. Top with lettuce, tomatoes and onions. Serve.

title: Christmas Crunch
text: Christmas Crunch. Ingredients: 1 c. light Karo syrup; 1 c. butter; 1 c. sugar; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1/2 tsp. soda; 1 (12 oz.) box Rice Chex; 1/2 c. slivered almonds; 2 c. coconut. Directions: Boil syrup, butter, sugar and salt together for 5 minutes at medium-high. Remove from heat. Add vanilla and soda; return to heat and cook 2 more minutes. Pour over Rice Chex, almonds and coconut; mix well. Pour out onto greased cookie sheet (large). Cool, cut and eat.

title: Mock Champagne
text: Mock Champagne. Ingredients: 4 cups sugar; 4 cups water; 4 cups grape juice or pineapple juice; 2 cups orange juice; 8 pints chilled ginger ale. Directions: Boil sugar and water for 3 minutes and cool. Add the juices and chill. Just before serving add chilled ginger ale. Makes 50 servings.

title: Chewy Coffee Cake
text: Chewy Coffee Cake. Ingredients: 1 box yellow cake mix; 1 stick oleo; 3 eggs; 8 oz. Philadelphia cream cheese; 1 box powdered sugar. Directions: Mix cake mix, oleo and 1 egg with fork until crumbly like pie dough. Pour into 13 x 9-inch ungreased cake pan. Beat together cream cheese, powdered sugar and 2 eggs. Pour over first mixture. Bake at 325° for 45 minutes. While still warm, sprinkle powdered sugar on top.

title: Montana Mud Cake
text: Montana Mud Cake. Ingredients: 2 sticks margarine; 2 c. sugar; 2 Tbsp. cocoa; 4 eggs; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 1/2 c. flour; 1 1/3 c. coconut; 1 c. chopped pecans. Directions: Cream first 3 ingredients. Add eggs and vanilla. Add flour, then add coconut and pecans. Mix well. Bake in floured and greased 9 x 12-inch pan in 350° oven for 30 minutes. Pour a jar of marshmallow cream over cake and top with frosting.

title: Pork Chop Cheese Pillows
text: Pork Chop Cheese Pillows. Ingredients: 4 lean, large pork chops; 8 slices Provolone cheese; 1 medium onion, sliced; 1 c. green pepper, diced; 1 c. celery, diced; 1 c. mushrooms, sliced; 1/2 c. water; 2 Tbsp. butter; 1 clove garlic; garlic powder; seasoned salt. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. In a small saucepan, melt butter. Saute onion and garlic clove until soft; set aside. Stab small holes in pork chops and sprinkle lightly with garlic powder and seasoned salt.

title: Tropical Turkey Salad
text: Tropical Turkey Salad. Ingredients: 6 oz. turkey, cooked and diced; 1 c. diet pineapple tidbits; 1/2 c. carrot, shredded; 1/4 c. celery, chopped; 2 Tbsp. plain yogurt; 2 tsp. mayonnaise; 1/4 tsp. curry powder; 1/8 tsp. garlic powder. Directions: Combine first four ingredients in a bowl. Mix remaining ingredients to use as a dressing for the turkey. Toss gently. Serves 2.

title: Grandma Helen'S Chinese Beef Skillet
text: Grandma Helen'S Chinese Beef Skillet. Ingredients: 1 lb. top round steak, cut in 1/2 inch strips; 4 Tbsp. cooking oil; 1/4 c. chopped onions; 1 clove garlic, minced; 1 c. water; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 2 c. sliced celery; 1 (6 oz.) pkg. Oriental pea pods; 1 (4 oz.) c. sliced water chestnuts; 2 Tbsp. cornstarch; 1/2 c. water; 3 Tbsp. soy sauce. Directions: Slice beef in very thin strips (easier when slightly frozen). Preheat frypan on medium heat for 1 1/2 to 2 minutes. Add oil and stir. Fry half of beef until brown; remove. Brown remainder in onion and garlic. Return browned beef to pan. Add celery; cover and simmer for 5 minutes, then add pea pods and water chestnuts until tender. Transfer meat and vegetables to serving dish. Combine cornstarch with 1/2 cup of water and soy sauce; stir into pan juices. Cook, stirring until mixture thickens. Pour over meat and vegetables. Serves 4 to 6.

title: Low-Fat Honey-Mustard Chicken
text: Low-Fat Honey-Mustard Chicken. Ingredients: 4 honey-mustard chicken breasts (from Sam's); 2 c. cooked white rice; 1 onion, finely chopped; 1 c. mushrooms, sliced; 1 can sweet peas and carrots (Veg-All). Directions: Spray a large pan with Pam. Place chicken breasts in pan. Cook on medium heat until done. Reduce heat and add onions, mushrooms, rice, peas and carrots. Fold all ingredients together and cover. Cook on low heat for 7 to 10 minutes. Serves four.

title: Caramel Pudding
text: Caramel Pudding. Ingredients: Unopened can(s) of Eagle Brand Sweetened Condensed Milk; Serve with whipped cream. Directions: Remove can labels. Using a canner or other large pan, boil unopened cans of Eagle Brand for 4 hours. Keep water line above cans! (Great to do on cold, dry winter days!) After cooked, store unopened can(s) in cupboard. Chill when ready to use. Open can at both ends and push out roll of caramel pudding. Slice (and maybe halve or quarter the slices) and serve with a dollop of whipped cream.

title: Peppery Hush Puppies
text: Peppery Hush Puppies. Ingredients: 2 c. yellow corn meal; 1/2 c. plus 3 Tbsp. all-purpose flour; 1 1/2 tsp. sugar; 1 tsp. salt; 2 tsp. baking powder; medium green pepper, finely chopped; 1/2 tsp. soda; 1 tsp. Creole seasoning; 1/2 c. buttermilk; 2/3 c. water; 1/2 c. melted margarine; 1 egg, well beaten; 1 c. grated onion; 2 jalapeno peppers, seeded and finely chopped. Directions: Combine dry ingredients; add buttermilk, water, margarine and eggs. Mix well. Stir in onion and peppers. Carefully drop batter by teaspoon into hot oil (375°). Fry 3 to 5 minutes, turning once, until hush puppies are golden brown. Drain well on paper towels. Yields about 2 1/2 dozen.

title: Tommi'S Tuna Casserole
text: Tommi'S Tuna Casserole. Ingredients: 1 lb. cooked elbow macaroni; 1 (8 oz.) container sour cream; 1 medium onion, chopped; 1 can cream of mushroom soup; 1 can tuna fish; 1 stick margarine, melted; bread crumbs. Directions: Saute chopped onion in small amount of margarine until cooked. Mix together cooked macaroni, sour cream, sauteed onion, cream of mushroom soup and tuna fish. Spoon into greased baking dish. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes. Mix melted margarine with enough bread crumbs to make topping. Place topping over macaroni and serve.

title: California Gazpacho
text: California Gazpacho. Ingredients: 3 medium tomatoes, peeled, seeded and diced; 3 c. no-salt-added tomato juice; 1/4 c. diced onion; 1/4 c. diced celery; 1 medium size green pepper, seeded and diced; 1/4 c. sliced green onions; 1 medium cucumber, chopped; 3 cloves garlic, minced; 2 Tbsp. chopped fresh parsley; 1 tsp. chopped fresh cilantro; 3 Tbsp. red wine vinegar; dash of hot sauce; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. freshly ground pepper. Directions: Combine all ingredients in a large bowl, stirring well. Cover and chill 8 hours or overnight. Ladle into soup bowls. Yields 6 cups (53 calories per 1-cup serving).

title: Corn And English Pea Salad
text: Corn And English Pea Salad. Ingredients: 1 c. white Shoe Peg corn; 1 small can English peas; 2 Tbsp. mayonnaise; 1 tsp. horseradish; 1 tsp. finely chopped onion. Directions: Drain corn and peas. Stir in other ingredients until mixed well. Chill at least 12 hours.

title: Nutty Peach Crunch
text: Nutty Peach Crunch. Ingredients: 1 large (29 oz.) can sliced peaches with syrup; 1 pkg. Betty Crocker butter pecan cake mix; 1/2 c. margarine, melted; 1 c. coconut; 1 c. chopped pecans. Directions: Heat oven to 325°. Layer ingredients in order listed in ungreased, oblong 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan. Bake 55 or 60 minutes. Let stand at least 15 minutes before serving. Good with vanilla ice cream on top.

title: Simple Baked Ham
text: Simple Baked Ham. Ingredients: 1 half ham or precooked ham; 1 small can crushed or sliced pineapple; 1 c. brown sugar. Directions: Select right size baking pan. Lay aluminum foil in pan and then put ham on top of foil. Put brown sugar and pineapple on top of ham. Wrap the foil around ham. Bake in oven at 350° to 400° for about 2 1/2 to 3 hours. Pour a little water around ham to prevent burning at bottom, if ham starts to burn.

title: Spiced Beets
text: Spiced Beets. Ingredients: 1 can sliced beets with liquid; 1 c. water; 1/2 to 1 c. sugar; 1/3 c. vinegar; 6 to 12 whole cloves. Directions: Add all ingredients and bring to a boil. Boil about 10 minutes.

title: Shrimp With Rice
text: Shrimp With Rice. Ingredients: 1 stick butter; 1 chopped onion; 1 c. sliced mushrooms; 1 chopped green pepper; 1 1/4 c. raw rice; 1/4 tsp. nutmeg; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 1 c. dry white wine; 3 c. hot water; 2 Tbsp. chopped parsley; 1/4 tsp. thyme; 1/2 bay leaf; 2 lb. raw shrimp. Directions: In Dutch oven, heat butter; add onion, mushrooms, green pepper, rice, nutmeg, salt and pepper and cook, stirring, until rice is brown. Add wine and simmer 5 minutes. Add hot water, parsley, thyme and bay leaf; cover and cook 10 minutes, depending on size of shrimp. Remove bay leaf and serve immediately.

title: Mo-Jo Potatoes
text: Mo-Jo Potatoes. Ingredients: 6 large potatoes; 1/2 tsp. thyme; 1/2 tsp. basil; 1/2 tsp. oregano; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 1/4 c. oil; 1 1/2 tsp. paprika; 1/4 tsp. cayenne pepper; 1/2 tsp. chili powder; 1 1/2 c. flour; 1 egg; 1/3 c. milk. Directions: Preheat oven to 450°. Wash and brush potatoes. Cut into wedges. Mix the seasonings into the flour. Mix the egg with the milk. Dip potato wedges into milk. *Dust with seasoned flour. Place on a pastry sheet. Drizzle with oil. Bake in oven 20 to 25 minutes until browned and tender.

title: Pork And Sausage Jambalaya
text: Pork And Sausage Jambalaya. Ingredients: 2 lb. cubed pork; 2 lb. cut sausage; 4 onions, cut fine; 3 tsp. garlic; 1/4 c. green peppers; 4 c. Mahatma long grain rice or 2 boxes Zatarian jambalaya mix. Directions: Put 1 cup water in pot. Add cubed pork and start browning. When pork turns light brown, add sausage and brown until dark brown. Remove excess grease from pot. Brown onions. Add green onions and bell pepper. Cook down. Add 4 cups water. Add seasoning and bring to a boil. Add rice and stir until water becomes thick. Cover and put fire as low as possible. Wait 10 minutes and turn rice over (do not stir) from the bottom of the pot. Re-cover. Wait 10 minutes and turn over again. Turn fire off. Let sit 5 minutes and serve.

title: Polynesian Chicken Wings
text: Polynesian Chicken Wings. Ingredients: 3 lb. wings; 1/4 c. dark brown sugar; 1/4 c. soy sauce; 3 Tbsp. oil; 3 Tbsp. pineapple juice; 1 1/4 Tbsp. ground ginger; 1 tsp. chopped garlic. Directions: Combine all ingredients and blend. Place chicken in glass baking dish. Add ingredients. Cover and chill overnight, turning often. Place skin side down on cookie sheet. Bake at 400° for 30 minutes. Turn and bake 30 minutes longer. Serve hot or cold.

title: Caramel Popcorn
text: Caramel Popcorn. Ingredients: 16 c. popped popcorn or more; 1/2 c. water; 1/2 c. butter; 1/2 tsp. cream of tartar; 2 c. sugar. Directions: Boil until slightly brown around edges. Add 1/4 teaspoon soda. Boil, stirring constantly. Pour over popcorn, mix until covered; lay out to dry.

title: Zucchini Soup
text: Zucchini Soup. Ingredients: 4 Tbsp. butter; 1 onion, shredded; 1 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. sugar; 1 c. milk; 1 can Pet milk; 1 c. Velveeta cheese, melted; 5 zucchini, shredded; 1 potato, peeled and shredded; 1/2 tsp. soy sauce; pepper; 3 c. chicken broth; nutmeg. Directions: Saute vegetables in butter until tender. Add salt, pepper, soy sauce and sugar. Add stock, milk and canned milk. Do not boil! Keep just below boiling for about 45 minutes. Serve with a dash of nutmeg.

title: Oven Barbecued Chicken Breasts
text: Oven Barbecued Chicken Breasts. Ingredients: 4 lg. halves, chicken breasts; 1/2 c. flour; salt and pepper to taste; 1/4 c. cooking oil; 1/4 c. margarine; 1 c. catsup; 1/2 c. water; 1/2 c. sherry wine; 2 Tbsp. lemon juice; 1/3 c. diced onions; 2 Tbsp. brown sugar; 2 Tbsp margarine; 1 Tbsp worcestershire sauce. Directions: Dredge chicken breasts in flour to which salt and pepper is added. Heat mixture of oil and margarine in skillet. Place chicken in the hot oil, turning to a light brown. Arrange pieces in a lightly greased baking dish. Blend and heat all ingredients for the sauce, pour over chicken. Cover and bake 325 for about 1 1/2 hour or until chicken is tender. Serves 4.

title: Steak And Rice Special
text: Steak And Rice Special. Ingredients: 1 lb. round steak, cut into 1-inch cubes; 1/4 c. cooking oil; 1 small onion, chopped; 1 c. water; 1/4 c. bell pepper, chopped; 1/4 c. A.1. steak sauce; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Flour and brown steak in oil, turning to brown all sides. Add onion and stir constantly until transparent. Add water and remaining ingredients, mixing well. Cover and simmer on low heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally. Serve over hot rice. Makes 4 servings.

title: Red Rouser
text: Red Rouser. Ingredients: vanilla ice cream; cranberry juice. Directions: Drop a scoop of vanilla ice cream into a tall glass and fill with cranberry juice. Stir until it all swirls together. If you like, you can add a piece of cherry or pineapple slice on the side.

title: German Style Pork Chops
text: German Style Pork Chops. Ingredients: 6 to 8 pork chops; 1 c. flour (cornstarch and/or extra flour to thicken); 1 large onion, sliced; 1 pkg. Lipton onion soup mix; 1 (8 oz.) beer (preferably German style); 4 c. water; salt and pepper to taste; butter to saute chops. Directions: Mix flour, salt, pepper and bread pork chops, then saute in skillet with butter to slightly brown both sides (1 to 2 minutes). In large pot, add water, soup mix, onion and beer. Bring to simmer. Then add breaded pork chops. Simmer on low for 45 minutes to 1 hour. Thicken with flour or cornstarch and serve over rice or noodles.

title: Strawberry Delite Salad
text: Strawberry Delite Salad. Ingredients: 2 c. crushed pretzels; 1 1/2 sticks oleo, melted; 8 oz. pkg. cream cheese; 1 c. sugar; 1 c. chopped pecans; 2 (8 oz.) pkg. Cool Whip; 1 large pkg. strawberry jello; 1 large carton frozen strawberries; 2 c. boiling water. Directions: Press mix pretzels and oleo in a 12 x 9-inch pan. Bake at 350° for 10 minutes. Let cool completely.

title: Fudge Brownie Pie
text: Fudge Brownie Pie. Ingredients: 1 (9-inch) unbaked pie crust; 1 c. semi-sweet chocolate chips; 1/4 c. butter or margarine; 1 (14 oz.) can sweetened condensed milk; 1/2 c. Bisquick; 2 eggs; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 c. chopped nuts. Directions: Preheat oven to 375°. Bake pie crust 10 minutes and remove. Reduce oven to 325°. Over low heat, melt chips with butter. In a large mixing bowl, beat chocolate mixture with remaining ingredients, except nuts, until smooth. Then add nuts. Pour into pie crust. Bake 35 to 40 minutes or until center is set. Cool. Serve with warm ice cream if desired.

title: Country Sausage Au Gratin
text: Country Sausage Au Gratin. Ingredients: 1 lb. Polish sausage; 1 onion, chopped; 1 Tbsp. vegetable oil; 1 pkg. au gratin potatoes; 2 1/2 c. hot water; salt and pepper; 4 carrots, cut in strips; 1 pkg. frozen or fresh broccoli; 1 c. shredded Cheddar or Mozzarella cheese. Directions: Cook and stir onions in oil for 5 minutes. Stir in the potatoes, sauce and water. Reduce heat and cook 10 minutes. Cook the carrots and broccoli; add to potatoes. Slice the sausage and add, heating everything together until good and warm. Add the cheese on top before serving.

title: Cherry Delight Pie
text: Cherry Delight Pie. Ingredients: 1 can Eagle Brand milk; 1 jar (16 oz.) Win-You-cherries; 1 graham cracker pie crust (9-inch); 1/4 c. lemon juice; 1 pkg. (8 oz.) cream cheese. Directions: Mix softened cream cheese and Eagle Brand milk, then slowly add lemon juice. Pour into pie crust. Bake on 350° for 30 or 35 minutes or until set.

title: Corn Meal Mush(A High-Protein Alternative To Pancakes.)
text: Corn Meal Mush(A High-Protein Alternative To Pancakes.). Ingredients: 3 c. water; 1 c. fine corn flour; 1 c. corn meal or coarse corn flour; salt to taste. Directions: The night before: Bring water and salt to boil in a large pot; remove from heat. Slowly add corn flours while stirring. Return to heat; cook over low heat until very stiff. Let cool to touch. Shape into a block about 4 inches high. Wrap in foil or plastic wrap and refrigerate.

title: Sloppy Joe Pie
text: Sloppy Joe Pie. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; 1/2 c. chopped onion; 8 oz. can tomato sauce; 7 oz. can corn, drained; 1/4 c. water; 1.5 oz. envelope Sloppy Joe seasoning mix; 10 oz. can Hungry Jack biscuits; 2 Tbsp. milk; 1/3 c. cornmeal; 1 c. shredded Cheddar cheese. Directions: Heat oven to 375°. In large skillet, brown ground beef and onion; drain. Stir in tomato sauce, corn, water (optional) and seasoning mix. Heat mixture until hot and bubbly; reduce heat and simmer while preparing crust. Separate dough into 10 biscuits; flatten slightly. Dip each in milk; dip in cornmeal. Arrange 7 biscuits around sides and 3 in the bottom of 9-inch pie pan; press biscuits to form crust. Sprinkle crust with 1/2 cup of the cheese. Spoon hot meat mixture over cheese; sprinkle with remaining cheese. Bake at 375° for 20 to 25 minutes or until crust is deep golden brown. Let stand 5 minutes before serving. Makes 8 servings.

title: Pecan Pie #1
text: Pecan Pie #1. Ingredients: 1 c. Karo syrup; 1 c. pecans, broken; 3 eggs; 1/2 c. white sugar; 3/4 c. brown sugar; 1 tsp. vanilla; pinch of salt; 1 uncooked pie shell. Directions: Mix together syrup, pecans, eggs, white sugar, brown sugar, vanilla and salt. Pour in uncooked pie shell. Bake at 325° for 1 hour.

title: Festive Date Balls
text: Festive Date Balls. Ingredients: 1/2 c. margarine; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. chopped nuts; 1 c. chopped pecans; 3/4 c. sugar; 2 1/2 c. Rice Krispies. Directions: Boil and stir margarine, sugar and dates for 3 minutes. Add Rice Krispies and pecans. Form into small balls and roll in powdered sugar.

title: Chicken Ala Orange With Rice
text: Chicken Ala Orange With Rice. Ingredients: 3 large chicken breasts; 2 Tbsp. margarine or butter; 1 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 3 Tbsp. brown sugar; 1 Tbsp. cornstarch; 1/8 tsp. ginger; 2/3 c. orange juice; 1/3 c. orange marmalade; 3 large peeled carrots, cut 2 1/2 x 1/4-inch strips; 1 large green pepper, cut into thin strips. Directions: Cook chicken with salt and pepper until done. Cut into strips. Brown in skillet with 2 tablespoons of margarine or butter. Remove from skillet.

title: Hamburger-Noodle Bake(Party Fare)
text: Hamburger-Noodle Bake(Party Fare). Ingredients: 4 lb. ground beef; 3 large onions, chopped (3 c.); 1 c. chopped green pepper; 16 oz. medium noodles; 3 cans tomato soup; 4 c. shredded American cheese; 1 (12 oz.) bottle chili sauce (optional); 1/4 c. chopped pimiento; 2 tsp. salt; 2 tsp. chili pepper; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 4 1/2 c. soft bread crumbs; 1/4 c. melted butter. Directions: In a large skillet, cook beef, onion and green pepper, half at a time, until meat is brown. Drain off fat. Cook noodles according to package directions; drain well. Return noodles to kettle. Stir in meat mixture and next 7 ingredients. Add 2 cups water and mix well.

title: Georgia Special
text: Georgia Special. Ingredients: 1 box Duncan Hines cake mix; 1 can mandarin oranges; 4 eggs; 1/2 c. oil. Directions: Mix well and cook at 350° until done. Cool.

title: Marjorie Boyd'S Pea Salad
text: Marjorie Boyd'S Pea Salad. Ingredients: 3 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen peas; 1/4 c. chopped onion; 1/4 c. chopped celery; 4 Tbsp. sweet pickle relish; 1 tsp. chopped pimento; 1/2 c. shredded American cheese; 1 tsp. salt; 1 Tbsp. sugar; 1/2 tsp. black pepper; 3/4 c. mayonnaise. Directions: Cook peas; drain. Mix ingredients and chill.

title: Unfried Catfish
text: Unfried Catfish. Ingredients: light vegetable oil cooking spray; 1/4 c. cornmeal; 1 tsp. dried thyme; 1 tsp. dried basil; 1/2 tsp. garlic powder; 1/2 tsp. lemon pepper; 4 tsp. blackening seasoning; 4 (4 oz.) catfish fillets; 1/2 tsp. paprika. Directions: Preheat the oven to 400°. Spray vegetable oil over baking sheet 3 times to coat. Put the cornmeal, thyme and basil on a large plate and mix well. Sprinkle 1/8 teaspoon of garlic powder, 1/8 teaspoon lemon pepper and 1 teaspoon of blackening seasoning on each of the catfish fillets. Coat the fillets thoroughly with the cornmeal mixture and transfer them to the prepared baking sheet. Dust each fillet with 1/8 teaspoon of the paprika. Coat the catfish lightly with the cooking spray.

title: Hamburger Or Sausage Quiche
text: Hamburger Or Sausage Quiche. Ingredients: 1 unbaked pie crust; 1/2 lb. ground beef or ground sausage; 1/4 c. mayonnaise; 1/2 c. milk; 2 eggs; 1 Tbsp. cornstarch; 1 1/2 c. grated cheese (Cheddar); 1/2 c. chopped onion. Directions: Brown meat; add salt and pepper to taste. Blend together mayonnaise, milk, eggs and cornstarch until smooth. Stir in meat, cheese and onion. Pour into pie crust. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes at 350°.

title: Auntie Joan'S Pepperoni Pie
text: Auntie Joan'S Pepperoni Pie. Ingredients: 4 eggs, well beaten; 1/2 c. diced pepperoni; 1 c. diced Mozzarella cheese; 1/4 c. grated Romano cheese; 1 unbaked 9-inch pie shell (defrosted, if frozen). Directions: Preheat the oven to 350°. In a large bowl, combine beaten eggs with pepperoni, Mozzarella cheese and Romano cheese. Pour into pie shell. Bake for about 45 minutes until the center is slightly puffed and a toothpick inserted in center of pie comes out clean. Do not overbake. Cut in wedges and serve hot.

title: Carrot Marshmallow Salad
text: Carrot Marshmallow Salad. Ingredients: 3 1/2 c. shredded carrots, coarse; 1/4 c. pineapple, cut into 1/2 inch pieces; 1 c. seedless raisins; 1 c. mayonnaise; 1 c. tiny marshmallows; 1/2 c. shredded coconut; 1 c. whipped cream. Directions: Peel carrots. Shred with coarse shredder into mixing bowl. Add marshmallows, pineapple, raisins and coconut. Toss lightly to mix. Combine the mayonnaise with carrot mixture. Carefully fold in the whipped cream.

title: Ada'S Carrot-Pineapple Jello Salad
text: Ada'S Carrot-Pineapple Jello Salad. Ingredients: 1 (3 oz.) pkg. lemon jello; 1 c. boiling water; 1/2 c. cold water; 1/2 c. pineapple juice; 1 (8 oz.) can crushed pineapple; 1 c. shredded carrots. Directions: Dilute lemon jello in boiling water. Add cold water and pineapple juice. Let cool. Add the crushed pineapple and 1 cup shredded carrots. Chill until set.

title: Yummy Potatoes
text: Yummy Potatoes. Ingredients: 1 (11 oz.) can condensed cream of chicken soup; 1 1/2 c. shredded Cheddar cheese; 1 c. sour cream; 6 medium potatoes, sliced thin; salt and pepper to taste; 1/4 c. thin sliced onion (optional). Directions: Combine cream of chicken soup and 1 cup cheese in saucepan. Heat over medium-low heat, stirring to prevent scorching. Add sour cream. Mix thoroughly. Place potatoes in casserole, one layer at a time, and top with the mixture 1/3 each time over the three layers. Cream mixture on top.

title: Spinach Cream Cheese Sandwich Mix
text: Spinach Cream Cheese Sandwich Mix. Ingredients: 1/2 (12 oz.) pkg. chopped spinach, raw; 8 oz. cream cheese; 1 can sliced water chestnuts, chopped; 1/4 c. onion, chopped fine; 1/2 c. mayonnaise; 1/2 c. sour cream; 1/2 tsp. lemon juice; 1 pkg. dry Knorr vegetable soup mix or Lipton or Knorr onion soup mix; 1/2 can ripe olives, drained and chopped (optional). Directions: Squeeze spinach very dry. Let cream cheese become very soft. Mix all ingredients and let stand overnight in the refrigerator. (Can use lite cream cheese and lite sour cream, but not lite mayonnaise.) Spread on bread for either 1 or 2 slice sandwiches. Makes 12 to 15 sandwiches.

title: Bacon Cheese Strata
text: Bacon Cheese Strata. Ingredients: 7 day old bread slices, cubed; 1 (2 oz.) jar Hormel homestyle bacon pieces; 1 onion, chopped; 3 eggs; 2 c. milk; 1/8 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 1 c. grated Cheddar cheese; 1/2 c. butter or margarine, melted. Directions: Layer cubed bread in buttered 9 x 13-inch pan. Cover bread with bacon pieces and onion. Beat together eggs, milk, salt and pepper. Pour over bread. Sprinkle grated cheese on top. Drizzle butter over cheese. Cover and refrigerate overnight. Bake at 325° for 1 hour. Serves 6 to 8 persons.

title: Honey Bun Cake
text: Honey Bun Cake. Ingredients: 1 box yellow cake mix; 3/4 c. oil; 4 eggs; 8 oz. sour cream; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 c. brown sugar; 2 tsp. cinnamon. Directions: Combine cake mix, oil, eggs, sour cream and vanilla. Pour half of mixture into a 9 x 13-inch greased and floured pan. Mix brown sugar and cinnamon and sprinkle half over batter; swirl with a fork into cake. Pour remaining batter on top and spread. Top with brown sugar and cinnamon; swirl with fork. Bake at 325° for 40 to 45 minutes. When done, pour glaze on top.

title: Zingy Tortilla Bites
text: Zingy Tortilla Bites. Ingredients: 24 oz. cream cheese, softened; 2 c. sour cream; 10 green onions, chopped; juice of 1/2 lime; 1 to 4 jalapenos, seeded and finely chopped; 1 (2 oz.) can chopped black olives, drained (optional); 1 1/2 c. mayonnaise; salt and pepper to taste; 20 large flour tortillas; 1 (16 oz.) jar medium picante sauce. Directions: Blend the cream cheese and sour cream in an electric mixer or processor. Add the next 7 ingredients and mix well. Spread the cream cheese mixture on each tortilla and roll up tightly as for jelly roll. Place the tortilla rolls on a tray and cover lightly with a damp towel and plastic wrap to prevent drying. Chill for 8 to 10 hours. Cut the tortilla rolls into 1/2 to 3/4-inch slices and arrange on a serving platter. Serve with the picante sauce. Yield: 10 dozen.

title: Freeze It And Forget It Fruit Salad
text: Freeze It And Forget It Fruit Salad. Ingredients: 3/4 c. syrup, drained from fruit; 1 slightly beaten egg; 1/4 c. sugar; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 1/2 Tbsp. flour; 2 Tbsp. vinegar; 1 c. drained diced pears; 3/4 c. drained pineapple tidbits; 3 mashed medium ripe bananas; 1/2 c. chopped maraschino cherries; 2/3 c. (small can) undiluted Carnation evaporated milk; 1 Tbsp. lemon juice. Directions: Combine syrup, egg, sugar, salt, flour and vinegar in saucepan. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until thickened. Cool. Add fruit to cooked mixture. Chill Carnation in refrigerator tray until soft ice crystals form around edges of tray (10 to 15 minutes.) Add lemon juice; whip very stiff (1 minute.) Fold into fruit mixture. Spoon into 6 cup mold. Freeze until firm about 5 to 6 hours.

title: West African Groundnut Stew
text: West African Groundnut Stew. Ingredients: 3 Tbsp. oil; 1/2 tsp. nutmeg; 1 Tbsp. chili powder; 1 clove garlic, minced; 6 c. water; 1/2 c. chunky peanut butter; 2 lb. beef cubes (1-inch or smaller), rolled in flour; 4 medium onions, sliced; 3/4 c. tomato paste; red pepper (if desired); 2 Tbsp. oil. Directions: In large heavy kettle, heat oil; add meat. When meat is browned, add onion, garlic, nutmeg, chili powder, sliced onion, tomato paste, water and red pepper. Simmer until meat is tender. A half hour before serving, heat peanut butter and oil in small saucepan. Stir over medium heat 5 minutes. Add beef stew and simmer over low heat 20 minutes. Serve over rice.

title: Macaroni And Cottage Cheese Casserole
text: Macaroni And Cottage Cheese Casserole. Ingredients: 1 lb. macaroni; 1 stick butter; 1 small/medium onion, diced; 16 oz. cottage cheese; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Cook macaroni as directed. Drain well. Saute butter and onion. Mix macaroni with butter and onion. Mix well. Add cottage cheese and stir. Makes about 16 servings.

title: Chocolate-Peanut Butter Drops
text: Chocolate-Peanut Butter Drops. Ingredients: 1 c. butter, softened; 1/2 c. granulated sugar; 1/2 c. packed brown sugar; 1/2 (8 oz. size) pkg. softened cream cheese; 1 1/2 tsp. vanilla; 1 egg; 2 c. all-purpose flour; 2/3 c. cocoa; 1/4 tsp. baking powder; 1/4 tsp. baking soda; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1 c. peanut butter chips. Directions: Heat oven to 375°. In a large mixing bowl, mix the butter, both sugars and cream cheese. Stir in vanilla and egg. Stir in flour, cocoa, baking powder, baking soda and salt. Stir in peanut butter chips. Drop dough by rounded tablespoonful at least 2 inches apart onto ungreased cookie sheet. Bake 9 to 12 minutes or until set but still soft. Cool 1 minute and remove from cookie sheet. Makes about 2 1/2 dozen cookies.

title: Hot Cheese Puffs
text: Hot Cheese Puffs. Ingredients: 1 (3 oz.) pkg. cream cheese, softened; 3/4 tsp. grated onion; 1/4 c. mayonnaise; 1 Tbsp. chopped chives; 1/8 tsp. cayenne pepper; 2 Tbsp. grated Parmesan cheese; bread slices. Directions: Combine all ingredients, except bread; blend well. Cut bread into 1 1/2-inch rounds; spread with cheese mixture. Bake for 15 minutes at 350°. Yield: 25 servings.

title: Frozen Pops
text: Frozen Pops. Ingredients: 3 oz. pkg. Jell-O (any flavor); 1 envelope Kool-Aid (any flavor); 1 C. sugar; 2 C. boiling water; 2 C. cold water. Directions: Dissolve Jell-O, Kool-Aid and sugar in boiling water. Add cold water; stir well. Pour into ice cube trays, small paper cups or frozen pop molds.

title: Orange Pineapple Punch(Make 1 Day In Advance)
text: Orange Pineapple Punch(Make 1 Day In Advance). Ingredients: 2 qt. water; 2 (20 oz.) cans crushed pineapple; 3 c. sugar; 3 ripe bananas, mashed; 1 (12 oz.) can frozen orange juice; 1 (6 oz.) can frozen lemonade; 1 (14 oz.) pkg. orange drink; 1 liter ginger ale, chilled; 1 jar maraschino cherries. Directions: In a 6-quart container, combine first 7 ingredients. Place punch in covered container and freeze. Remove to partially thaw. Break into chunks; add ginger ale and cherries. Stir until slushy. Makes 27 (6 ounce) servings.

title: Sugar Free Coconut Pie
text: Sugar Free Coconut Pie. Ingredients: 1 pkg sugar free cook and serve vanilla pudding; 1/2 tsp. vanilla extract; 1/2 tsp almond extract; 1/2 tsp. coconut extract; 1 pkg. of frozen coconut; 1 (8 oz.) carton Cool Whip. Directions: Cook pudding by directions. Then add extracts and 1/2 of the coconut. Cool and top with Cool Whip and the rest of the coconut.

title: Quick Rice Pilaf
text: Quick Rice Pilaf. Ingredients: 1 c. water; 1 c. frozen peas; 1 envelope instant onion soup mix; 1 tbsp. margarine or butter; 1 c. quick cooking rice. Directions: In medium saucepan, combine water, peas, soup mix and margarine or butter. Bring to boiling. Stir in rice. Cover and remove from heat. Let stand for 5 minutes. Makes 4 servings.

title: Healthy Sweet And Sour Chicken
text: Healthy Sweet And Sour Chicken. Ingredients: 5 to 6 chicken breasts (skinned and boned); 1 c. green onion, chopped; 2 green peppers (1 inch squares); 1 1/3 c. cornstarch; 1/3 c. soy sauce; 1 tsp. ground ginger; 1/2 c. plus 1 Tbsp. cider vinegar; 3/4 c. ketchup; 3/4 c. water; 3/4 c. sugar; 1/2 Tbsp. sesame oil; 1 1/2 Tbsp. vegetable oil. Directions: Cut chicken into 1 inch cubes. Marinate for 45 minutes in soy sauce, sesame oil and ginger. Mix ketchup, water, vinegar and sugar and set aside. Put marinated chicken and cornstarch in Ziploc bag and shake until well coated. Preheat skillet or wok, add 1 tablespoon vegetable oil and add chicken breasts a few at a time. Brown chicken, then remove from skillet. Put in 1 teaspoon vetetable oil in skillet and add vegetables, stir constantly and cook for 5 mintues. Pour sweet and sour sauce over vegetables; bring to boil, add chicken and cook 10 minutes to thicken. Serve with rice. 6 to 8 servings.

title: Mexican Bean Dip
text: Mexican Bean Dip. Ingredients: 2 cans refried beans; 2 oz. tub guacamole dip; 1 (16 oz.) carton sour cream; 3 Tbsp. salad dressing; 1 pkg. taco seasoning; 1 bunch chopped green onions; 2 c. chopped tomatoes; 1 can sliced black olives; 8 oz. shredded Cheddar cheese. Directions: In pan, layer refried beans, then guacamole. Next, mix sour cream, salad dressing and taco seasoning together. Spread on guacamole. Layer the green onions, tomatoes, olives and cheese. Serve with tortilla chips.

title: Pineapple Icebox Cake
text: Pineapple Icebox Cake. Ingredients: 1/2 lb. vanilla wafers; 1/2 c. margarine; 2 eggs; 1/2 pt. cream, whipped or medium size Cool Whip; 1 c. nuts; 1 1/2 c. confectioners sugar; 1 small can pineapple, drained. Directions: Roll wafers into crumbs; put 1/2 in Pyrex dish. Cream butter and sugar; add 2 eggs. Put on top of crumbs. Whip cream and fold in pineapple and nuts. Pour over other. Top with other half of vanilla wafer crumbs. Refrigerate.

title: Banana Poppy-Seed Muffins
text: Banana Poppy-Seed Muffins. Ingredients: 2 bananas; 1 egg; 1/2 c. sugar; 1/4 c. vegetable oil; 1/2 c. milk; 1 1/2 c. sifted flour; 2 1/2 tsp. poppy seed; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. baking soda; 2 tsp. baking powder. Directions: Preheat oven to 400°. Fill 12 cupcakes tins with paper liners. Mash bananas with a fork. In large bowl, mix egg, sugar and vegetable oil. Add and mix milk and mashed bananas. In a small bowl, stir flour, poppy seed, salt, baking soda and baking powder. Add flour mixture all at one time to the liquid mixture. Using a wooden spoon, stir just until dry ingredients are moistened. Using a metal or plastic measuring cup. Pour batter into cupcake liners until they are half full. Bake 20 minutes or until toothpick comes out clean.

title: Betty'S Dump Cake
text: Betty'S Dump Cake. Ingredients: 1 can (No. 2) crushed pineapple, undrained; 1 can (No. 2) cherry pie filling; 1/2 c. nuts, chopped; 1 stick butter or margarine; 1 box yellow cake mix. Directions: Butter 9 x 13-inch pan. Dump in cherry pie filling. Dump in pineapple. Sprinkle cake mix to cover. Do not stir. Sprinkle nuts on top. Cut butter into small pieces and dot top of cake. Bake in 350° oven for 30 to 35 minutes. Serve plain or with ice cream or whipped cream.

title: Crock-Pot Cheese Soup
text: Crock-Pot Cheese Soup. Ingredients: 2 slices ham, cubed; 8 slices bacon, cooked and crumbled; 3 carrots, diced; 6 to 8 potatoes, diced; 4 stalks celery, diced; 1 to 2 onions, diced; 1/2 c. chopped green pepper; 3 c. water; 6 beef bouillon cubes; 4 oz. can mushrooms (reserve liquid); 16 oz. frozen corn; 1/4 c. flour; 16 oz. Cheez Whiz. Directions: Combine first 9 ingredients in crock-pot. Cook 3 to 4 hours or until potatoes and carrots are tender. Add the mushrooms and corn. Make a paste with reserved liquid and flour. Add to soup. Cook on high until slightly thickened. Add cheese. Serves 10 to 12.

title: Upside-Down Pizza
text: Upside-Down Pizza. Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. hamburger; 1 onion; 1/2 green pepper; 1 can mushrooms; pepperoni; small jar spaghetti sauce; medium bag Mozzarella; 1 can crescent rolls. Directions: Brown hamburger with onion and green pepper. Drain. Mix mushrooms and pepperoni with meat mixture. Spread in pan. Pour spaghetti sauce over hamburger mixture. Sprinkle cheese on top. Spread crescent rolls over that. Bake in oven until rolls are done.

title: Chicken Pie With Sweet Potato Crust
text: Chicken Pie With Sweet Potato Crust. Ingredients: 3 c. diced cooked chicken; 1 c. sliced cooked carrots; 6 small white onions, quartered and cooked; 1 Tbsp. chopped parsley; 1 c. milk; 3 Tbsp. flour; 1 c. chicken broth; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Layer first 4 ingredients into a greased 2 1/2-quart casserole. Combine flour and a small amount of milk in saucepan, blending until smooth. Gradually stir in remaining milk and chicken broth. Place over low heat. Cook until thickened, stirring constantly. Add salt and pepper. Pour sauce over chicken and vegetables in the casserole. Cover mixture with sweet potato crust and bake at 350° about 45 minutes. Yield: 6 to 8 servings.

title: Saucy Sirloin Surprise
text: Saucy Sirloin Surprise. Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. boneless sirloin steak (1-inch thick); 2 Tbsp. shortening; 1 can golden mushroom soup; 3/4 c. water; 2 Tbsp. sherry; 1 large clove garlic, minced; 1 medium bay leaf; 1 (9 oz.) pkg. frozen whole green beans. Directions: Trim fat from meat. Cut into 1-inch cubes. In skillet, brown meat in shortening. Pour off fat. Add remaining ingredients, except beans. Cover; cook over low heat 1 hour. Stir now and then. Remove bay leaf. Chill overnight.

title: Macaroni And Cheese Custard
text: Macaroni And Cheese Custard. Ingredients: 1/4 lb. macaroni; 1/2 lb. grated cheese; 1 small can evaporated milk; 1 small can water; 1 tsp. salt; 2 Tbsp. butter; 2 eggs, well beaten. Directions: Boil macaroni in generous amount of water. Drain and mix with grated cheese, salt and butter. Lastly, stir in eggs. Pour in greased baking dish and bake to light brown.

title: Make Ahead Cheeseburger Lasagna
text: Make Ahead Cheeseburger Lasagna. Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. lean ground beef; 3 Tbsp. instant onion; 1 (15 oz.) can tomato sauce; 1 1/2 c. water; 1/2 c. ketchup; 1 Tbsp. prepared mustard; 1 (15 oz.) container Ricotta cheese; 8 oz. Cheddar and American cheese blend, shredded (2 cups); 1 egg, beaten; 12 lasagna noodles, uncooked; 4 oz. cheddar cheese, shredded (1 cup); 1 c. lettuce, shredded; 1 medium tomato, sliced; dill pickle slices. Directions: Spray 9X13 inch glass baking dish. Cook ground beef and onion until done. Add tomato sauce, water, ketchup, and mustard. Mix well and cook 5 minutes. Combine Ricotta cheese, 2 cups of cheddar and American blend and egg. Mix well.

title: Gone-All-Afternoon Stew
text: Gone-All-Afternoon Stew. Ingredients: 2 lb. beef chuck, trimmed of fat and cut in 1-inch cubes; 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen peas; 3 medium carrots, cut in 1/2-inch slices (1 1/4 c.); 3 medium potatoes, quartered (3 c., 1 1/2 lb.); 1 c. chopped onion; 1 (10 3/4 oz.) can condensed tomato soup and 1/2 soup can water; 1/4 c. red wine; 1/4 c. flour; 1 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 1 bay leaf. Directions: Heat oven to 275°. Mix all ingredients in a 3-quart casserole or Dutch oven. Cover and bake 5 hours or until meat is tender when pierced with a fork. Makes 6 to 8 servings.

title: Poulet De Normandy
text: Poulet De Normandy. Ingredients: 1 pkg. corn bread stuffing mix; 1 stick butter; 1 c. chicken broth; 2 1/2 to 3 c. diced chicken; 1/2 c. onion; 2 eggs; 1/4 c. onion tops or chives; 1/2 c. celery; 1/2 c. mayonnaise; 3/4 tsp. salt. Directions: Mix stuffing, chicken broth and butter together. Pat down in a 12 x 8-inch casserole. Add chicken mixed with onion, celery, mayonnaise and salt; mix 2 eggs with 1 1/2 cups milk. Pour over evenly. Cover with foil and marinate overnight. One hour before serving, spread 1 can of cream of mushroom soup over top. Bake at 350° for 40 minutes. Sprinkle with grated cheese. Put back in oven 10 minutes more.

title: Orange Slice - Date Cake
text: Orange Slice - Date Cake. Ingredients: 1 c. butter or oleo; 2 c. sugar; 3 1/2 c. flour; 4 eggs, one at a time; 1 tsp. orange rind, grated; 2 c. chopped nuts; 8 oz. chopped dates; 1 c. buttermilk; 1 tsp. soda; 1 (16 oz.) pkg. orange candy slices, chopped; 1 c. Angel Flake coconut; 2 tsp. orange juice. Directions: Mix cake in order given.

title: Ruth'S Dill Dip
text: Ruth'S Dill Dip. Ingredients: 2 c. mayonnaise; 2 c. sour cream; 2 tsp. Beau Monde; 2 tsp. dill weed or more; 2 tsp. dried onion (optional). Directions: Mix together and refrigerate overnight or for several hours.

title: Mushroom Medley
text: Mushroom Medley. Ingredients: 1 c. frozen peas; 1 large onion; 2 c. medium size fresh button mushrooms; 2 tsp. margarine; salt to taste. Directions: Prepare each vegetable separately to obtain right texture. In saucepan, cook peas until nearly done. Cover and set aside. Slice onion thickly. Cut each slice into eight pie-shaped pieces. Cook in water until done, but still firm. Add margarine; cover and set aside. Wash mushrooms. Stand on end and cut into quarters. Boil hard until done. Strain mushrooms and add to onions. Add peas and enough juice that mixture can be simmered. Add salt. Simmer and taste. Serve hot.

title: Shepards Pie
text: Shepards Pie. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; 1/2 c. onion; 1 can tomato soup; 2 c. green beans; 3 c. mashed potatoes; 2 eggs (whole), beaten; 1 c. grated cheese (any kind). Directions: Brown ground beef. Add onions. Cook until onion softens. Add tomato soup and beans. In a bowl put mashed potatoes plus eggs (beaten with a fork) and grated cheese.

title: Hearty Steak And Kidney Pie
text: Hearty Steak And Kidney Pie. Ingredients: 2 1/2 pkg. (10 oz.) pie crust mix; 2 lb. chuck, cut into 2 x 1-inch strips; all-purpose flour; 6 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 2 veal kidneys; 1 tsp. salt; 2 (10 3/4 oz.) cans beef broth (undiluted); 1 pt. fresh oysters or 1 (8 oz.) can oysters (optional); 1 lb. fresh mushrooms, washed and halved or 2 (8 oz.) cans mushrooms, whole or sliced; 1 c. chopped onion; 2 Tbsp. fresh parsley, chopped; 1/2 bay leaf, crumbled; 1 tsp. dried thyme leaves; 1 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 1 egg yolk. Directions: Cover kidneys with cold water. Add 1 teaspoon salt; let stand for 1 hour. Prepare pie crust mix according to package directions. Refrigerate until ready to use. Roll (beef) chuck strips in 1/4 cup flour on waxed paper, coating evenly; shake off excess flour. In large skillet, melt butter. Add strips of chuck and brown well on all sides. Brown only enough at one time to cover bottom of skillet. Takes about 25 minutes. Arrange around sides of 9 x 13-inch baking dish.

title: Health Bread
text: Health Bread. Ingredients: 4 cups whole wheat flour; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 1 c. sweet milk; 1 c. buttermilk; 1 c. nutmeats; 1 c. raisins. Directions: Mix all ingredients together. Let stand 20 minutes. Pour into a greased loaf pan. Bake in preheated 350 degree oven for 50-60 minutes.

title: Not So Sweet Strawberry Freezer Jam
text: Not So Sweet Strawberry Freezer Jam. Ingredients: 2 c. crushed berries; 2 c. sugar; 1/3 c. water; 1 pkg. Sure-Jell. Directions: Mix berries and sugar and let stand 10 minutes. Mix water and Sure-Jell and bring to a full boil, stirring constantly. Boil 1 minute. At once, stir into fruit. Continue stirring 3 minutes. Immediately ladle into containers. Leave 1/2-inch space at top. Cover and let stand at room temperature for 24 hours; then freeze.

title: Hominy Salad
text: Hominy Salad. Ingredients: 2 cans hominy; 1 onion, chopped and sauteed; 1 c. Velveeta cheese, diced; 6 slivers jalapeno peppers or to taste; salt; pepper. Directions: Mix and bake for 30 minutes in a 350° oven.

title: Canned Okra
text: Canned Okra. Ingredients: 1 gal. chopped okra; 1 gal. water; 1/2 c. canning salt; 1/2 c. vinegar. Directions: Put ingredients in a large pan and boil 10 minutes. Put in jars and seal. For fried okra, rinse thoroughly. Roll in meal and fry as you would any fried okra.

title: No Fat Brownies
text: No Fat Brownies. Ingredients: 1 c. + 2 Tbsp. flour; 1/2 tsp. baking powder; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. Butter Buds (optional); 1 1/2 c. granulated sugar; 3/4 c. cocoa; 1/2 c. applesauce; 8 egg whites, lightly beaten; 2 1/2 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Combine and mix dry ingredients. Add and mix lightly remaining ingredients. Pour into 9 x 13-inch pan (spray with Pam first). Bake 25 minutes.

title: Bacon And Egg Casserole(Microwave)
text: Bacon And Egg Casserole(Microwave). Ingredients: 1/4 lb. bacon, cut in 1 1/2-inch pieces; 1/2 c. chopped green pepper; 1 can (3 oz.) sliced mushrooms, drained; 6 large eggs; 1/2 c. canned Cheddar cheese soup, undiluted; 2 Tbsp. fine dry bread crumbs. Directions: Place bacon in 9-inch pie plate. Cover loosely with microwavesafe paper towel. Cook on High 3 minutes. Drain off fat, reserving 1 tablespoon in small custard cup; set aside. Stir pepper and mushrooms into bacon; cover and cook on High 2 minutes.

title: Yummy Hot Cider
text: Yummy Hot Cider. Ingredients: 3 (3-inch) sticks cinnamon; 2 tsp. whole cloves; 1 tsp. ground nutmeg; 1/2 gal. apple cider or apple juice; 1 c. sugar; 2 c. orange juice; 1/2 c. lemon juice. Directions: Tie spices in cheesecloth or tea strainer and place in slow cooker. Add apple cider (or juice) and sugar, stirring well. Cover. Simmer on low 1 hour. Remove spices and stir in orange juice and lemon juice. Continue heating 1 more hour. Serve cider from cooker set on low. Yield: 10 to 11 (1 cup) servings.

title: Pumpkin Applesauce Bread
text: Pumpkin Applesauce Bread. Ingredients: 2/3 c. shortening; 2 1/2 c. sugar; 4 eggs; 1 c. applesauce; 1 c. pumpkin; 3 1/3 c. flour, sifted; 2 tsp. baking soda; 1/2 tsp. baking powder; 1 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. mace; 2/3 c. apple juice; 1 c chopped walnuts. Directions: Cream together shortening and sugar. Add eggs, one at a time. Beat well after each. Stir in applesauce and pumpkin. Sift together dry ingredients and add alternately with apple juice. Stir in chopped nuts. Turn into 2 greased 9 x 5 x 3-inch loaf pans. Bake at 350° for 1 hour or until done. May be wrapped in foil and frozen.

title: Peanut Bars(No Bake)
text: Peanut Bars(No Bake). Ingredients: 1 c. melted butter; 1 c. peanut butter; 2 1/2 c. graham cracker crumbs; 2 1/2 c. powdered sugar; 6 oz. chocolate chips; 1 Tbsp. peanut butter. Directions: Mix butter, peanut butter, graham crackers and powdered sugar. Press into 9 x 13-inch baking pan. Melt chocolate chips and peanut butter. Spread on top of bars.

title: Swiss Pork Chops
text: Swiss Pork Chops. Ingredients: 1 envelope Lipton dry onion soup mix; 1 can golden mushroom soup (must be golden mushroom); 1 can Classic Coke (not diet). Directions: Pour over pork chops in baking pan. Bake 1 hour at 375° or until chops are done. May be used over other meats such as steak, beef or pork roast.

title: Amanda'S Picante Salsa
text: Amanda'S Picante Salsa. Ingredients: 30 to 40 medium tomatoes; 3 to 4 medium onions; 3 to 4 bell peppers; 10 to 20 jalapeno peppers; 3 Tbsp. salt; 1 tsp. pepper; 2 Tbsp. garlic powder; 2 Tbsp. sugar; 3/4 tsp. paprika; 1/3 c. vinegar. Directions: Dip tomatoes in boiling water to remove skins. Chop all vegetables and put in large pot or dishpan. (I use food processor for peppers.) Cook on medium heat for 20 to 30 minutes or until starts to thicken. Dissolve 3 tablespoons cornstarch with 1/2 cup water. Add to salsa; cook a few minutes longer to thicken. Put in hot jars to seal. Makes about 14 pints.

title: Ham And Egg Salad
text: Ham And Egg Salad. Ingredients: 2 c. finely chopped cooked ham; 1/2 c. diced celery; 1/2 green pepper, minced (1/2 to 3/4 c.); 1 hard-cooked egg, chopped; 6 Tbsp. mayonnaise; 1/2 tsp. green onion; 2 tsp. lemon juice; 1 Tbsp. Dijon mustard; 6 hard rolls; 6 leaves Boston lettuce; 1/2 tsp. ground ginger. Directions: In a bowl, mix together the ham, celery, green pepper, egg, mayonnaise, ginger, lemon juice and mustard. Stir to blend thoroughly. Divide ham salad evenly and spread on bottoms of rolls. Top with lettuce and then with tops of rolls.

title: "South Of The Border" Steak
text: "South Of The Border" Steak. Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. tenderized round steak; 2 1/2 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 1/2 tsp. chili powder; 1/2 tsp. ground red pepper; 1 1/2 tsp. garlic salt; 2 small cans chopped green chiles; 8 oz. Monterey Jack cheese, shredded; 1 (8 oz.) jar taco sauce (hot or mild). Directions: Brown steak in butter while adding seasonings to meat until light brown. Drain off any grease. Remove steak; put in an oven-safe dish. Pour taco sauce on top of steak, then sprinkle top of steak with chopped green chiles. Bake, covered, for 35 minutes at 350°. Remove cover. Add shredded cheese to the top of each piece of steak. Bake, uncovered, for 10 minutes, or until cheese is bubbly and melted well. Serve with Spanish rice.

title: Rye Loaf Dip
text: Rye Loaf Dip. Ingredients: 1 c. sour cream; 1 c. mayo; 1 1/2 tsp. grated onion; dash of garlic powder; 1 tsp. parsley; 1 tsp. dill weed; pkg. dried beef. Directions: Shred beef and mix all ingredients. Make the night before. Cut top out of loaf bread. Dig bread out, leaving a 1-inch rim. Pour mixture in bowl. Cut bread in dip sizes. When all bread cubes are gone, then cut loaf in bite sizes.

title: Chinese Roast Pork Or Chicken
text: Chinese Roast Pork Or Chicken. Ingredients: 2 lb. boneless pork loin, trimmed of excess fat or boneless chicken breasts; 3 Tbsp. oyster sauce; 3 Tbsp. hoisin sauce; 3 Tbsp. honey; 2 1/2 Tbsp. dark soy sauce; 2 1/2 Tbsp. light soy sauce; 2 Tbsp. blended whiskey; 1 tsp. five spice powder; pinch of white pepper. Directions: Quarter the pork lengthwise into 4 large strips. Pierce the meat with a fork. Whisk the other ingredients together in a bowl. Line a roasting pan with heavy-duty aluminum foil. Arrange the pork or chicken in a single layer. Pour the marinade sauce over the meat and allow it to marinate for at least 4 hours, or overnight, in the refrigerator.

title: Orange-Raisin Fruitcake
text: Orange-Raisin Fruitcake. Ingredients: 1 orange (extract juice and reserve); 1 1/2 c. raisins; 1/2 c. nuts (I use peanuts); 1/2 c. vegetable shortening; 1 c. sugar; 2 eggs, unbeaten; 2 c. flour; 1 tsp. soda; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 c. buttermilk. Directions: Extract orange juice, then put orange pulp, rind, nuts and raisins through food chopper. Keep 1/4 cup of nut mixture for topping. Mix remainder with orange juice, sugar and shortening until light and fluffy. Add eggs. Mix. Add flour, soda, salt and buttermilk. Mix well and pour into a tube pan. Bake 45 minutes at 350°.

title: Potato Sausage Casserole
text: Potato Sausage Casserole. Ingredients: 20 oz. cubed potatoes; 1/2 to 1 c. chopped onions; 1/2 c. celery, sliced; 1/2 c. carrots, sliced; 12 oz. turkey sausage; 1 (16 oz.) can green beans, drained; 1 can mushroom soup. Directions: Combine all ingredients in crock-pot and cook on high for 6 to 8 hours.

title: Peanut Logs
text: Peanut Logs. Ingredients: 2 c. chunky peanut butter; 3 c. Rice Krispies; 2 sticks oleo; 1 box powdered sugar; 1 (6 oz.) pkg. chocolate chips; 1/8 cube wax. Directions: Mix well peanut butter, Rice Krispies, oleo and sugar. Melt chocolate chips and wax. Roll by teaspoon size in hands. Keep your fingers wet and then dip the logs in chocolate mixture. Put on wax paper.

title: Spaghetti Salad
text: Spaghetti Salad. Ingredients: 1 lb. spaghetti; 3 cucumbers, diced; 4 tomatoes, diced; 2 stalks celery, diced; 1 green pepper, diced; 2 onions, diced; 1 small bottle Wish-Bone Italian salad dressing; 1/2 bottle Salad Supreme. Directions: In a saucepan, cook spaghetti until tender. Drain and cool. In a large bowl, combine all ingredients and stir.

title: Quick Baked Beans
text: Quick Baked Beans. Ingredients: 1 medium can pork and beans; 1/2 tsp. mustard; 1 tsp. brown sugar; 1 Tbsp. dark Karo syrup; 1 tsp. onion, minced; 4 bacon slices. Directions: Combine all ingredients. Pour into buttered baking dish. Place bacon strips over top. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

title: Crock-Pot Meal To Serve Over Noodles
text: Crock-Pot Meal To Serve Over Noodles. Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. round steak; 1 pkg. onion soup mix; 1 pkg. brown gravy mix; 2 c. water; 1 onion, chopped; 1 Tbsp. brown sugar; 3 Tbsp. wine vinegar; 1 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/2 tsp. ground ginger; dash of Tabasco sauce. Directions: Cut meat in pieces. Mix other ingredients. Pour over meat. Bake 1 1/2 hours at 350°. Serve over noodles. Can cook in crock-pot on low for 8 to 10 hours.

title: Animal Crackers
text: Animal Crackers. Ingredients: 1/2 c. oatmeal; 2 tsp. honey; 1/4 plus 1/8 tsp. salt; 3/4 c. flour; 1/4 tsp. soda; 1/4 c. real butter, softened; 4 Tbsp. buttermilk. Directions: Grind oatmeal in blender until fine. Add honey, salt, flour and soda. Stir. Cut in butter. Add buttermilk and stir. Roll very thin. Cut with animal cookie cutters. Bake at 400° until brown (10 to 12 minutes).

title: Cheese-Spinach Soup
text: Cheese-Spinach Soup. Ingredients: 1/2 c. chopped onion; 1/2 c. chopped celery; 1/4 c. butter; 1/4 c. flour; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 4 c. milk; 1 (10 oz.) frozen chopped spinach; 1 1/2 c. shredded American cheese. Directions: In 3-quart saucepan, cook onion and celery in butter until onion is tender. Stir in flour, salt and pepper. Add milk all at once. Cook and stir until thickened. Stir in spinach and cheese. Cook and stir until cheese is melted.

title: Creamy Shells With Broccoli And Ham
text: Creamy Shells With Broccoli And Ham. Ingredients: 1 (1 lb.) box medium size macaroni shells; 1/2 lb. cooked ham; 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen broccoli, thawed; 1 clove garlic, crushed; 1/2 c. margarine; 1 c. heavy cream; 1 c. grated Parmesan cheese. Directions: Cook shells according to package directions. Slice or cube ham; cut broccoli into 1/2-inch pieces. Saute garlic, ham and broccoli in margarine in a large skillet until broccoli is crisp and tender (approximately 5 minutes). Stir in cream. Cook over medium heat 3 minutes. Stir in cheese. Lower heat and cook, stirring often, until cheese is melted. Drain shells and toss with sauce. Serve.

title: Easy Beef Tips
text: Easy Beef Tips. Ingredients: 1 small boneless chuck roast; flour; water; salt and pepper; garlic powder to taste; 2 Knorr beef bouillon cubes; 1 small carton fresh mushrooms, sliced up. Directions: Take a small boneless chuck roast, cut into small squares and roll in flour. Cover the bottom of a roasting pan with flour and add enough water to make a medium paste. Place meat in pan. Add salt, pepper, garlic powder, bouillon cubes and sliced mushrooms. Add enough water to completely cover meat . Bake at 350° for about 1 1/2 hours or until tender. May need to add a little more water during cooking. This should make a thick gravy as it cooks. Serve over noodles or rice.

title: Wickedly Rich Chocolate Pie
text: Wickedly Rich Chocolate Pie. Ingredients: 1 pkg. German chocolate; 1 (13 oz.) can Carnation cream; 3 eggs, slightly beaten; 1/2 c. sugar; 1/4 stick butter; 1 1/3 c. Angel Flake coconut. Directions: Melt butter and chocolate over low heat. Add the rest of the ingredients and mix well. Pour into unbaked 9-inch pie shell. Bake 30 minutes at 400°.

title: Swiss Dill Loaf
text: Swiss Dill Loaf. Ingredients: 3 to 3 1/4 c. all-purpose flour; 3 oz. Swiss cheese, grated (about 3/4 c.); 1 pkg. RapidRise yeast; 1 Tbsp. sugar; 1 tsp. salt; 3/4 c. evaporated skim milk; 1/4 c. water; 1 Tbsp. butter; 1 egg white, beaten; dill seed (optional). Directions: In large mixing bowl, combine 2 cups flour, cheese, yeast, sugar, salt and dill weed. Heat water, milk and butter in saucepan over medium-low heat to 125°. Stir in dry ingredients; blend well. Continue adding flour to make soft dough. Knead 4 minutes, until smooth and elastic. Cover; let rest 10 minutes. Roll dough into a 12 x 8-inch rectangle. Roll up from short end of loaf; seal ends and seams. Place in greased 8 x 4 x 2 1/2-inch loaf pan; cover. Place large shallow pan on counter; half fill with boiling water. Set loaf on rack over boiling water; let dough rise until double in size, about 30 minutes. Cut 4 diagonal slashes in top. Brush with egg white; sprinkle with dill seed. Bake at 375° for 25 minutes.

title: Pistachio Salad
text: Pistachio Salad. Ingredients: pistachio pie filling (instant); 1 can crushed pineapple; 1 c. miniature marshmallows; 1/2 container Cool Whip (small). Directions: Mix together all ingredients and refrigerate. Serve on lettuce leaf. Use 9 x 13-inch pan.

title: Baked Pizza No. 2
text: Baked Pizza No. 2. Ingredients: pizza dough; 1 onion, sliced; olive oil; 1 garlic clove, chopped; salt and pepper; tomatoes (fresh); black pitted olives; anchovies; pimentos. Directions: Make dough. Spread on pizza pan. Cover with onions, which have been sliced and fried in oil, and garlic, pepper and salt. Lay on top of dough slices of fresh tomatoes with a few pitted ripe olives and a few filleted anchovies (washed of salt). Add several strips of pimentos. Bake in hot (400°) oven for 1/2 hour.

title: Rib Ticklin' Barbecue Sauce
text: Rib Ticklin' Barbecue Sauce. Ingredients: 1 c. dark Karo syrup; 1/2 c. honey; 1 c. mustard; 2 c. catsup; 1/2 c. molasses; 1 c. Worcestershire sauce; 1 c. cider vinegar; 2 c. onions, finely chopped. Directions: Mix all ingredients in saucepan and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and let simmer for 20 minutes. Brush sauce on meat the last 20 minutes of grilling.

title: Herb Grilled Vegetables
text: Herb Grilled Vegetables. Ingredients: 1/2 c. butter or margarine; 1 Tbsp. onion powder; 1 Tbsp. parsley flakes; 1 tsp. basil leaves, crumbled; 1 tsp. garlic powder; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. ground black pepper; 1 medium-sized eggplant, cut in 1/2-inch slices; 2 medium zucchini, cut in half, lengthwise; 2 medium tomatoes, cut in 1/2, crosswise*. Directions: *Italian Roma tomatoes also work well; split in half lengthwise.

title: Old-Fashioned Sugar Cookies
text: Old-Fashioned Sugar Cookies. Ingredients: 1/2 c. butter or margarine; 1 c. sugar; 1 egg or 2 egg yolks, well beaten; 1/2 tsp. vanilla; 1 1/2 c. sifted all-purpose flour; 1 tsp. baking powder; 1/4 tsp. salt. Directions: Cream butter or margarine. Beat in 1 cup of sugar, egg and vanilla. Sift flour, baking powder and salt together. Add to butter mixture. Mix well. Cover and refrigerate 3 to 4 hours or until dough is firm.

title: Hot Wings
text: Hot Wings. Ingredients: 5 lb. chicken wings; 2 Tbsp. butter; 1 large bottle Durkee hot sauce; 1 tsp. garlic powder; 1/2 tsp. celery salt; 1 Tbsp. lemon juice; 2 Tbsp. crushed red pepper; 1 Tbsp. vinegar; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 1 Tbsp. cayenne pepper. Directions: In saucepan, melt butter. Add remaining ingredients. Let simmer 10 minutes. In large bowl add sauce to wings. You may preboil the wings or use wings frozen. Put in refrigerator for 2 to 3 hours. Cover baking sheet with aluminum foil. Bake 35 to 45 minutes at 375°.

title: Magic Peach Cobbler
text: Magic Peach Cobbler. Ingredients: 1 stick butter or oleo; 1 c. sugar; 1 c. flour; 1 1/2 tsp. baking powder; 3/4 c. milk; 1 3/4 c. or more sliced peaches (either fresh or canned). Directions: Melt butter in 9 x 13-inch pan. Mix sugar, flour, baking powder and milk together and pour on butter. Do not stir. Add peaches on top of this in layers. Sprinkle with 2 or 3 tablespoons sugar. Bake 35 minutes at 350°.

title: Spaghetti And Meatballs(Caryn'S Favorite Meal!)
text: Spaghetti And Meatballs(Caryn'S Favorite Meal!). Ingredients: meatballs; meatball meat; spaghetti; sauce; boiling water; 1 chicken roaster; 3/4 c. olive oil; 1 1/2 tsp. rosemary; 2 Tbsp. paprika; 2 cloves crushed garlic; 1 Tbsp. parsley; 1/2 tsp. thyme (optional); black pepper to taste; salt to taste; potatoes; 1 lb. veal; 2 jars red sauce; 3 bags Mozzarella cheese; 1 bag French fries. Directions: Fry the veal for 2 hours. Put the red sauce onto the veal in the frying pan. Then put the Mozzarella cheese on and bake it in the oven at 100° for 30 minutes. Serve it with the French fries.

title: Oatmeal Meltaway
text: Oatmeal Meltaway. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. oatmeal; 1/2 c. sugar; 1/2 lb. margarine; 1 c. flour; raisins or chocolate chips (optional). Directions: Cream all ingredients except oatmeal and mix. Then add oatmeal and chill for 1 hour. Form into walnut size balls. Place on ungreased cookie sheets and flatten with fork. Bake at 350° for 15 minutes. Sprinkle with powdered sugar.

title: Peachy Keen Nog
text: Peachy Keen Nog. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. vanilla or peach ice cream; 2 fresh peaches, peeled and pitted or 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen peach slices, thawed; 2 eggs. Directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender container. Cover. Blend at medium speed until smooth, about 15 seconds. Serve immediately. Makes 2 servings.

title: Shrimp Toast
text: Shrimp Toast. Ingredients: 2 cans (4 oz.) shrimp, minced; 1/3 c. water chestnuts, minced; 1 Tbsp. cornstarch; 2 eggs; 1/2 tsp. sugar; 1/2 tsp. soy sauce; 6 slices stale bread, crusts removed. Directions: Mix all ingredients together. Spread on bread. Cut diagonally. Put about 1 inch oil in frying pan and heat. Place bread with filled side down and fry 15 seconds. Turn and fry other side until golden brown. Drain on absorbent paper.

title: Mil'S Dressing
text: Mil'S Dressing. Ingredients: 11 to 12 slices bread; 1 small onion, chopped; 1 stalk celery, chopped; salt and pepper; juice from meat or 2 1/2 c. boiling water and 1 chicken bouillon cube; 2 eggs; 1 to 2 Tbsp. oleo; 1/4 tsp. sage. Directions: Bake for 1 hour at 350°. Double recipe for 4 or more people.

title: Holiday Ham Pockets
text: Holiday Ham Pockets. Ingredients: 2 pkg. cream cheese (with chives and onions); 2 pkg. pita bread rounds, cut in halves; leaf lettuce, shredded; 2 pkg. Oscar Mayer thin sliced honey or boiled ham; 2 cans crushed pineapple, drained; 1 bunch spring onions, sliced. Directions: Spread insides of pita with cream cheese. Stuff with lettuce, ham, pineapple and onions. Cut in half again. Makes attractive appetizers and they are good.

title: Sherry Pound Cake
text: Sherry Pound Cake. Ingredients: 1 pkg. yellow cake mix; 1 pkg. instant vanilla pudding; 3/4 c. sherry wine; 1/2 to 3/4 c. salad oil; 1 tsp. nutmeg; 1 tsp. butter flavoring or rum flavoring; 4 eggs. Directions: Mix and beat together until smooth and creamy. Bake in Bundt pan in 350° oven for 45 minutes.

title: Applesauce-Spice Cake
text: Applesauce-Spice Cake. Ingredients: 1 pkg. yellow cake mix; 1 pkg. vanilla instant pudding; 1 c. applesauce; 1/2 c. water; 1/4 c. oil; 4 eggs; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. nutmeg; 1/4 tsp. allspice; 1/2 c. raisins. Directions: Combine all ingredients in large mixer bowl. Blend well, then beat at medium speed of electric mixer for 4 minutes. Pour into greased and floured 8 x 4 inch loaf pans. Bake at 350 degrees for 50 to 55 minutes or until cake tester inserted in center comes out clean and cake begins to pull away from sides of pan. Do not underbake. Cool in pan 15 minutes. Remove from pan and finish cooling on racks.

title: Crowd-Pleasing Potato Salad
text: Crowd-Pleasing Potato Salad. Ingredients: 1 gal. potatoes (about 16 large), cooked, peeled and sliced; 10 hard-cooked eggs; 1 c. chopped onion; 2 c. mayonnaise; 1 c. light cream; 1/3 c. vinegar; 1/3 c. sugar; 3 Tbsp. mustard; 1/2 tsp. salt. Directions: Place potatoes in large salad bowl. Separate eggs. Chop whites and add to potatoes with onion. Toss gently. In another bowl, mash yolks with mayonnaise, cream, vinegar, sugar, mustard and salt. Pour over potatoes and stir to coat. Cover and chill. Yields 16 to 20 servings.

title: Louisiana Pear Cake
text: Louisiana Pear Cake. Ingredients: 1/2 c. margarine, softened; 1 c. sugar; 1 egg; 1 1/2 c. flour; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. baking soda; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1 tsp. vanilla; 2 c. grated pears; 1/2 c. chopped pecans. Directions: Mix margarine, sugar and egg together. Sift flour, salt, soda and cinnamon together. Add to sugar and egg mixture. Add vanilla, pears and nuts and mix well. Pour into a greased and floured baking dish or 8 x 8-inch pan. Bake at 300° for 1 hour. Serve with whipped cream.

title: Four-Cheese Stuffed Shells
text: Four-Cheese Stuffed Shells. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c 1% low-fat cottage cheese; 1 c fat-free ricotta cheese; 1/2 c crumbled blue cheese; 2 Tbsp chopped fresh parsley; 1 large egg, lightly beaten; cooking spray; 2 tsp olive oil; 1 c chopped onion; 1 clove garlic. minced; 1 (14 1/2 oz) can stewed tomatoes, undrained and chopped; 1 Tbsp dried Italian seasoning; 1/4 tsp salt; 16 uncooked jumbo pasta shells; 2 Tbsp finely shredded fresh Parmesan cheese. Directions: Combine first 5 ingredients in a bowl; stir well, set aside. Coat a saucepan with cooking spray; add oil, and place over medium-high heat until hot. Add onion and garlic, and saute until tender. Stir in tomatoes, Italian seasoning, and salt; bring to a boil. Reduce heat, and simmer, uncovered, 15 minutes, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat and set aside.

title: Quick Casserole Dinner
text: Quick Casserole Dinner.
