Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. lean ground beef; 1/2 c. chopped onion; 1/2 c. chopped celery; 2 Tbsp. flour; 2 (8 oz.) cans tomato sauce with mushrooms; 1 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. oregano; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen peas, parboiled; 1 pkg. refrigerated biscuits; 1 c. shredded cheese (Cheddar). Directions: Brown beef, onion and celery in skillet. Drain fat; stir in flour, sauce, seasonings and peas. Simmer 5 minutes. Pour into shallow baking dish. Top with biscuits and cheese. Bake at 350° for 20 minutes. Serves 4.

title: Rice-A-Roni Chicken
text: Rice-A-Roni Chicken. Ingredients: 1 chicken or 3 to 4 breasts; 1 can cream of chicken or mushroom soup; 1 box Rice-A-Roni (chicken flavor). Directions: Boil and debone chicken. Save broth; cook rice by directions using broth instead of water. Combine all ingredients and bake at 350° for 20 minutes.

title: Chicken Panang Curry
text: Chicken Panang Curry. Ingredients: 1 (14 oz.) can Taste of Thai coconut milk; 1 Tbsp. Taste of Thai Panang Curry Base; 1 lb. boneless chicken, cut in strips; 2 Tbsp. Taste of Thai seasoning sauce; 2 tsp. lemon juice; 1 medium tomato, diced; 1 yellow bell pepper, diced; 3 scallions, diced; 2 c. mushrooms, sliced; 2 tsp. Taste of Thai garlic chili pepper sauce (use this cautiously; you might prefer a little less). Directions: In a large saucepan, combine coconut milk and curry base and cook until blended over medium heat. Add chicken, seasoning sauce and lemon juice and simmer for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add remaining ingredients, except mushrooms and garlic chili pepper sauce. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes. Add mushrooms and garlic chili pepper sauce to taste. Cover and simmer for 2 minutes. Serve hot with rice.

title: Meatballs (3)
text: Meatballs (3). Ingredients: 3 lb. hamburger; 1 pkg. onion soup mix; 2 eggs; 4 slices bread crumbs (moistened). Directions: Mix above together & make into balls. Bake on cookie sheet at 350° for 20 minutes.

title: Ricki'S Make-Ahead Potatoes
text: Ricki'S Make-Ahead Potatoes. Ingredients: 10 large potatoes, peeled and quartered; 1 c. (8 oz.) sour cream; 1 pkg. (8 oz.) cream cheese, softened; 6 Tbsp. butter or margarine, divided; 2 Tbsp. dried minced onion; 1/2 to 1 tsp. salt; paprika. Directions: Place potatoes in a Dutch oven or large kettle; cover with water and bring to a boil. Reduce heat; cover and cook for 20 to 25 minutes or until potatoes are tender. Drain and place in a bowl; mash. Add sour cream, cream cheese, 4 Tbsp. butter, onion and salt; stir until smooth and the cream cheese and butter are melted. Spread in a greased 13 x 9 x 2 inch baking dish. Melt remaining butter; drizzle over potatoes. Sprinkle with paprika. Bake at 350° for 40 minutes; uncover and bake 20 minutes longer. If potatoes are made ahead and refrigerated, let stand at room temperature for 30 minutes before baking. Yield: 12 servings.

title: Broccoli Or Cauliflower Soup
text: Broccoli Or Cauliflower Soup. Ingredients: 2 c. sliced broccoli or cauliflower; 13 3/4 oz. can chicken broth (Swanson); 1 c. cream; 1 (10 oz.) can condensed cream of potato soup; 1/4 c. water; 2 Tbsp. cornstarch; 1/8 tsp. pepper. Directions: Cook vegetables in large covered saucepan in chicken broth until almost tender (10 to 15 minutes). Do not drain. Set aside. In another bowl, gradually add cream of potato soup; mix thoroughly. Blend water in cornstarch (in small cup). Pour into potato soup mixture. Mix together, then pour all white mixture into vegetables and broth. Cook and stir until thick and bubbly (on medium heat).

title: Spicy Apple Cake
text: Spicy Apple Cake. Ingredients: 2 c. sugar; 3 eggs; 1 1/4 c. oil; 1/4 c. orange juice; 1 tsp. vanilla; 3 c. sifted all-purpose flour; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. baking soda; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1 c. finely chopped apples; 1 c. coconut; 1 c. chopped nuts (pecans, walnuts or almonds). Directions: Beat together sugar and eggs until fluffy. Add oil, juice and vanilla. Beat well. Mix dry ingredients together and stir into egg mixture just until blended. Do not beat. Stir in apples, coconut and nuts. Pour into a greased 9 x 13-inch baking pan. Bake at 325° for 1 hour or until cake tests done in the center. Pour the following glaze over cake while cake is still warm.

title: Lemonade Tea
text: Lemonade Tea. Ingredients: 1 c. sugar; 1/2 c. instant tea; 3/4 c. Country Time lemonade mix; 1 c. white grape juice. Directions: Mix all ingredients together in gallon container; fill to the top with water. Serve cold. Perfect on a hot day.

title: Skinny Soup
text: Skinny Soup. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground turkey or lean ground meat; 2 qt. water; 4 bouillon cubes; 5 potatoes, peeled and cubed; 5 carrots, peeled and sliced; 2 onions, chopped; 1 c. barley; 1/2 c. rice; 1 (46 oz.) can V-8 juice; 1 (28 oz.) can tomatoes; 1 (15 oz.) can kidney beans; celery salt (sprinkle); garlic salt (sprinkle); 1/4 c. parsley flakes; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Brown meat and set aside. In large pot, put water and bouillon cubes. Add potatoes, carrots, onions, barley, rice, juice and tomatoes. Cook 15 minutes. Add all remaining ingredients, including meat, and cook 45 minutes longer.

title: 55 Minute Rolls
text: 55 Minute Rolls. Ingredients: 1 c. warm milk; 2 Tbsp. shortening; 2 Tbsp. sugar; little salt; 3 c. flour; 1 Tbsp. yeast, dissolved in 1/4 c. warm water. Directions: Mix milk, shortening, sugar and salt; add yeast dissolved in water. Add flour. Let rise in dish 15 minutes. Roll out on floured board about 1/2 inch thick. Cut with cookie cutter. Dip in melted butter and fold in half. Let rise 40 minutes. Bake in 350° oven for 20 to 25 minutes.

title: Easy Pizza
text: Easy Pizza. Ingredients: 1 or 2 pkg. Pillsbury biscuits (canned); 1 c. sour cream; 1 lb. hamburger; 1/2 c. chopped onion; Italian seasoning to taste; salt and pepper to taste; 15 oz. tomato sauce; 1 c. Mozzarella cheese. Directions: Spread biscuits on bottom of a 9 x 13-inch pan. Spread sour cream over biscuits. Brown hamburger with onion, Italian seasoning to taste, salt and pepper to taste. Drain and pour over sour cream. Add tomato sauce and top with Mozzarella cheese. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

title: Grape Pie
text: Grape Pie. Ingredients: 1 regular pie crust recipe; 5 to 5 1/2 c. Concord grapes; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 1/4 c. flour; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1 pat margarine. Directions: Pop middle out of grape skins; save skins. Put pulp (middles) in saucepan and bring to a boil. Simmer 5 minutes. Put hot pulp through colander or food mill to remove seeds. Combine strained pulp with skins. Add sugar, flour and salt. Pour into unbaked crust. Dot with margarine. Add top crust with slits, lattice or circular cut outs on top of pie. Bake at 425° for 35 to 45 minutes.

title: Hermits(Portuguese)
text: Hermits(Portuguese). Ingredients: 1 c. sugar; 2 eggs; 1/3 c. milk; 1/2 c. raisins; 2 c. flour; 1/3 c. shortening; 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 1/2 c. chopped nuts; 1/2 tsp. cloves and cinnamon. Directions: Combine ingredients. Drop in spoonfuls on buttered pan or cookie sheet. Bake at 350° for 12 to 15 minutes.

title: Peg'S Shrimp Yummies
text: Peg'S Shrimp Yummies. Ingredients: 1 large pkg. cream cheese; 1 small bottle chili sauce; 1/4 to 1/2 lb. cooked salad shrimp; 1/2 c. or more chopped green onions. Directions: Place cheese on dish. Pour 3/4 sauce over cheese. Place shrimp on top and add rest of sauce. Sprinkle with green onions. Place crackers around dish.

title: Frosty Mandarin Orange Dessert
text: Frosty Mandarin Orange Dessert. Ingredients: 6 oz. pkg. orange Jell-O; 2 c. boiling water; 11 oz. mandarin orange sections (canned); 1 pt. orange sherbet. Directions: Dissolve gelatin in boiling water. Stir in orange sections with syrup. Add sherbet by spoon and stir until melted. Pour into 5-cup serving bowl. Yields 9 servings.

title: Grandmother Fowler'S Rolls
text: Grandmother Fowler'S Rolls. Ingredients: 3/4 c. warm water; 1 pkg. yeast; 1/4 c. shortening; 1 tsp. salt; 1/4 c. sugar; 1 egg; 2 1/4 c. flour. Directions: Place warm water in a mixing bowl. Stir in yeast until dissolved. Add shortening, salt, sugar, egg and 1/2 of flour. Mix well with electric mixer. Mix in remainder of flour well with a spoon or dough hooks. Turn out into a well greased large mixing bowl. Grease top of dough (I spray it with Pam). Cover and let rise in a warm place until double.

title: Greek-Style Pasta Salad
text: Greek-Style Pasta Salad. Ingredients: 8 oz. penne or other tubular pasta; 3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil; 2 Tbsp. red wine vinegar; 1 medium cucumber, peeled, seeded and chopped; 1 (2.2 oz.) can sliced ripe olives, drained; 3 medium tomatoes, diced; 2 scallions, chopped; 1 1/2 c. frozen green beans, thawed; 1/2 green bell pepper, chopped; 2 Tbsp. chopped flat leaf parsley; 2 tsp. oregano; 1 garlic clove, minced; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 1 c. crumbled Feta cheese (4 oz.). Directions: In a large pot of boiling salted water, cook penne until just tender, 10 to 12 minutes; drain well. Rinse with cold water and drain again. Return pasta to pot. Add olive oil and vinegar and toss to coat. Add cucumber, olives, tomatoes, scallions, green beans, bell pepper, parsley, oregano, garlic, salt and pepper. Toss to mix well. Sprinkle Feta cheese over salad. Serve chilled or at room temperature.

title: White Layer Cake
text: White Layer Cake. Ingredients: 1/2 c. Crisco; 1 1/3 c. granulated sugar; 2 1/2 c. white flour (sift once before measuring); 1/2 tsp. salt; 3 tsp. baking powder; 1 c. milk; 1 tsp. vanilla; whites of 3 eggs. Directions: Cream shortening and sugar. Sift flour with baking powder and salt. Add to creamed mixture alternately with milk. Add flavoring and mix well. Fold in well beaten egg whites. Bake in layers at 375° for 25 minutes, in nonstick pans, or well-greased, floured pans.

title: Famous Amos Chocolate Chip Cookies
text: Famous Amos Chocolate Chip Cookies. Ingredients: 1 c. margarine; 3/4 c. light brown sugar; 3/4 c. granulated sugar; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 tsp. water; 2 eggs; 2 1/4 c. sifted flour; 1 tsp. baking soda; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 (12 oz.) pkg. semi-sweet chocolate pieces. Directions: Beat softened margarine, brown and white sugars, vanilla, water and eggs in a large bowl with electric mixer until creamy and thoroughly blended. Preheat oven to moderate 375°. By hand, stir in flour, baking soda and salt until well mixed. Stir in chocolate pieces.

title: Fat-Free Fudge Brownies(Yield: 16 Servings)
text: Fat-Free Fudge Brownies(Yield: 16 Servings). Ingredients: 3/4 c. unbleached flour; 1/4 c. plus 2 Tbsp. cocoa powder; 1 c. sugar; 1/4 c. chopped walnuts (optional); 1/4 tsp. salt (optional); 1/3 c. unsweetened applesauce; 3 egg whites; 1 tsp. vanilla extract. Directions: Combine the flour, cocoa, sugar and salt, if desired, and stir to mix well. Stir in the applesauce, egg whites and vanilla extract. Fold in the nuts, if desired. Coat an 8-inch square pan with nonstick cooking spray. Spread the batter evenly in the pan and bake at 325° for 23 minutes or just until the edges are firm and the center is almost set. Cool to room temperature and cut into square and serve. (Calories: 80, fat: 0.4 g)

title: Chicken And Vegetable Rice Soup
text: Chicken And Vegetable Rice Soup. Ingredients: 1 can Pritikin chicken broth; 1 tsp. vegetable base (instant); 1/2 medium onion, chopped; 1/2 c. green beans; 1 medium carrot, chopped; 1 medium celery stalk, chopped; 1 ear corn kernels, removed; 1/2 c. uncooked brown rice (cook rice separately). Directions: Put chicken broth in large pot with vegetable base. Simmer on low. Next, add all vegetables and cooked rice. Stir occasionally and cook, covered, for 1 hour or until vegetables are tender.

title: Chocolate Covered Peanut Butter Balls
text: Chocolate Covered Peanut Butter Balls. Ingredients: 1/2 lb. margarine, melted; 1 lb. confectioners sugar; 7 or 8 oz. pkg. coconut; 1/2 lb. graham cracker crumbs; 1/2 c. peanut butter. Directions: Mix together and shape into balls. Dip into melted chocolate.

title: Sue'S Monkey Bread
text: Sue'S Monkey Bread. Ingredients: 3 cans refrigerator biscuits; 1/2 c. sugar; 2 to 3 tsp. cinnamon; 1 stick margarine; 1 c. brown sugar. Directions: Grease a Bundt pan. Cut biscuits in half. Shake in bag with sugar and cinnamon. Melt margarine and brown sugar until dissolved. Place biscuits in pan. Pour melted mixture over biscuits. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes.

title: Heath Coffee Cake
text: Heath Coffee Cake. Ingredients: 1 stick margarine; 1/2 c. white sugar; 1 c. brown sugar; 1 egg; 1 tsp. soda; 2 c. flour; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 c. buttermilk. Directions: Blend flour, sugars and margarine. Take out 1/2 cup of crumb mixture. To rest of mixture, add buttermilk, soda, egg and vanilla. Blend well. Pour into greased and floured 10 x 14 x 2-inch pan.

title: Baked Celery Casserole
text: Baked Celery Casserole. Ingredients: 6 c. sliced celery; 5 oz. water chestnuts, sliced; 1/4 c. chopped pimentos; 1 can cream of chicken soup; 1/4 c. slivered almonds; 4 Tbsp. butter; 1 c. (soft) bread crumbs. Directions: Cook celery 8 minutes in salted water. Drain. Mix celery, chestnuts, pimento and soup. Place in a buttered 2-quart casserole. Melt butter. Pour over bread crumbs. Add almonds. Sprinkle over casserole. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

title: Pink And Gold Salad
text: Pink And Gold Salad. Ingredients: 1 can Eagle Brand milk; 1 large carton Cool Whip; 1 (No. 2) can crushed pineapple, drained; 1 can Mandarin oranges, drained; 1 can cherry pie filling; 1 can Angel Flake coconut. Directions: Mix well and chill. This makes a lot.

title: Green Fried Tomatoes
text: Green Fried Tomatoes. Ingredients: green tomatoes; flour; salt; pepper. Directions: Cut tomatoes in 1/2-inch thick slices. Mix flour, salt and pepper. Dip tomatoes in mixture, covering both sides. Fry slowly in small amount of hot fat until browned; turn and brown other side.

title: Marinated And Grilled Shrimp
text: Marinated And Grilled Shrimp. Ingredients: 1/3 c. cooking sherry; 1/3 c. sesame seed oil; 1/3 c. soy sauce; 1/2 tsp. sugar; 1/4 tsp. garlic powder; 1/4 tsp. ground ginger; 2 lb. large fresh shrimp, peeled and deveined. Directions: Combine all ingredients, except shrimp, in a shallow dish and mix well. Add shrimp, tossing gently to coat. Cover and chill for 2 to 3 hours, stirring occasionally. Drain shrimp, reserving marinade. Place shrimp in 6 (14-inch) skewers. Grill over medium-hot coals for 3 to 4 minutes per each side, basting frequently with marinade. Yields 4 to 6 servings.

title: Fresh-As-Spring Salad
text: Fresh-As-Spring Salad. Ingredients: 1 1/4 lb. fresh asparagus spears (cooked); 6 cherry tomatoes (halved); 6 Tbsp. Italian dressing; 2 pkg. unflavored gelatin; 2 Tbsp. sugar; 1 1/2 c. boiling water; 1 c. mayonnaise; 3 Tbsp. lemon juice; 1 c. chopped cherry tomatoes; 1 1/2 Tbsp. finely chopped onion. Directions: Cut 6 asparagus spears into 4 inch lengths and marinate with cherry tomato halves in 2 tablespoons Italian dressing; cover and refrigerate. Cut remaining asparagus into 1/2 inch pieces; reserve.

title: Pear Zucchini Bread
text: Pear Zucchini Bread. Ingredients: 3 c. whole wheat flour; 2 tsp. pumpkin pie spice; 1 tsp. baking soda; 1/2 tsp. baking powder; 1/2 tsp. salt; 2 c. pears, chopped and peeled or 1 (16 oz.) can pear halves, drained and chopped; 1 c. sugar; 1 c. brown sugar, packed; 1 c. zucchini, finely shredded (unpeeled); 1 c. oil; 3 eggs; 1 Tbsp. vanilla; 1/2 c. pecans, chopped. Directions: Mix in medium bowl the first 5 ingredients. Mix well in a large bowl just until combined the remaining ingredients, adding pecans last. Pour into 2 (8 x 4 x 2-inch) loaf pans sprayed with nonstick spray. Bake 55 to 60 minutes (clean toothpick test). Cool for 10 minutes on wire rack. Remove bread from pans and cool thoroughly on rack. Wrap in plastic wrap and store overnight before slicing. Makes 2 loaves, 16 slices each.

title: Baked Hawaiian Chicken
text: Baked Hawaiian Chicken. Ingredients: 1 chicken, cut in eighths; 1/4 c. margarine; 1/2 c. ketchup; 1/4 c. lemon juice; 1/2 c. honey; 2 Tbsp. soy sauce; 1/2 tsp. ginger. Directions: In medium saucepan, melt margarine. Stir in ketchup, lemon juice, honey, soy sauce and ginger; stir until smooth. Marinate chicken with above for at least 1 hour, better if overnight.

title: Baked Peaches And Cream
text: Baked Peaches And Cream. Ingredients: 1 (1 lb. 13 oz.) can peach halves; 1/2 c. light brown sugar, firmly packed; 1/8 tsp. cinnamon; 6 Tbsp. cornflakes; 1 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 1 pt. vanilla ice cream. Directions: Preheat oven to 375°. Drain peaches, reserving 1/2 cup liquid. Arrange peaches, cut side up, in an 8 x 8 x 2-inch baking pan. Add reserved liquid. Combine sugar and cinnamon; sprinkle evenly over peaches.

title: Cheddar Cheese And Garlic Grits
text: Cheddar Cheese And Garlic Grits. Ingredients: 4 c. water; 1 tsp. salt; 2 garlic cloves, pressed; 1 c. uncooked regular grits; 1 (12 oz.) block sharp Cheddar cheese, shredded; 1/2 c. butter or margarine; 1 tsp. seasoned pepper; 1 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/4 tsp. hot sauce; 3 large eggs, lightly beaten; paprika. Directions: Bring first three ingredients to a boil in a large saucepan. Gradually stir in grits. Return to a boil; reduce heat and simmer, stirring occasionally 15 minutes or until thickened. Add cheese and next four ingredients, stirring until cheese melts. Remove from heat; let stand 10 minutes. Stir in eggs and pour into a lightly greased 11 x 7-inch baking dish. Sprinkle with paprika. Bake at 350° for 1 hour or until set. Yield: Approximately 8 to 10 servings.

title: Pink Punch
text: Pink Punch. Ingredients: 1 pkg. each: strawberry and cherry Kool-Aid; 1/2 c. sugar or to taste; 6 oz. frozen lemonade; 6 oz. frozen orange juice. Directions: Mix frozen juices according to directions. Add other ingredients and stir until dissolved. Stir into 2 quarts of cold water. Chill. When ready to serve, add 1 bottle ginger ale. Makes 1 gallon.

title: Grated Sweet Potato Pudding
text: Grated Sweet Potato Pudding. Ingredients: 2 c. grated raw sweet potatoes; 1 c. milk; 1 c. sugar; 2 eggs, slightly beaten; 2 Tbsp. melted butter; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1 tsp. nutmeg; 1/4 tsp. salt. Directions: Mix well and pour in greased 1 1/2-quart baking dish. Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

title: Hobo Potatoes(Pineliner'S Version)
text: Hobo Potatoes(Pineliner'S Version). Ingredients: 6 potatoes, peeled and cubed; 1 onion, chopped; 1 stick butter, cut into small pieces; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Mix all together and bake in a covered dish at 350° for 1 hour or wrap in aluminum foil and place on outdoor grill under rack next to the charcoal for about 30 to 45 minutes.

title: Cheesy Chicken And Rice
text: Cheesy Chicken And Rice. Ingredients: 4 cups cooked rice; 1/2 cup butter, divided; 1/4 cup flour; 2 teaspoon chicken bouillon; 2 cups milk; 1/2 to 1 teaspoon seasoned salt; 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder; 1/4 teaspoon pepper; 4 to 5 cups cooked chicken; 12 ounces processed cheese (velveeta); 2 cups sour cream; 1 1/3 cup crushed butter crackers (ritz). Directions: Prepare a 9 x 13 inch greased baking dish. Mix 1/4 cup butter, flour, milk, bouillon, seasoned salt, garlic powder and pepper until smooth. Bring to a boil and cool until thickened. Reduce heat and add chicken, process cheese and sour cream. Stir until melted. Add cooked rice and pour into baking dish. Take 1/4 cup butter and crushed crackers and brown in skillet. Use this as the topping. Bake at 425 degrees for 10 to 15 minutes.

title: Refrigerator Salad
text: Refrigerator Salad. Ingredients: 2 c. miniature marshmallows; 2 Tbsp. milk; 3 oz. pkg. cream cheese; 1 c. sour cream; 1/2 tsp. vanilla; 1 (16 oz.) can fruit cocktail, drained. Directions: Put marshmallows in bowl and add milk. Put in microwave for a few seconds until melted. Put cream cheese and sour cream in mixer. Add vanilla and mix well. Pour in marshmallow mix and fruit cocktail (drained). Mix together. Add 1/2 cups nuts, if desired. Make the day before and let chill overnight.

title: Spicy Creamy Grits
text: Spicy Creamy Grits. Ingredients: 2 c. half-and-half or whipping cream; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. granulated garlic; 1/8 tsp. ground black pepper; 1/2 c. quick-cooking grits, uncooked; 2 oz. cream cheese, cubed; 3/4 c. (3 oz.) sharp cheddar cheese, shredded; 1/4 tsp. sauce or red pepper. Directions: Bring first 4 ingredients to a boil in a large sauce pan. Gradually stir in grits. Return to a boil. Cover, reduce heat, and simmer, stirring occasionally for 5 to 7 minutes until thickened. Add cheeses and hot sauce or red pepper, stirring until cheeses melt. Serve immediately.

title: Refrigerator Yeast Rolls
text: Refrigerator Yeast Rolls. Ingredients: 1 tsp. sugar; 1 tsp. salt; 1 pkg. dry yeast; 1/2 c. water; 2 eggs; 7 c. all-purpose flour; 1 c. Crisco shortening; 1/2 c. sugar; 2 c. water. Directions: Combine 1st 5 ingredients in small mixing bowl and beat 3 minutes with heavy duty mixer at medium speed. In large mixing bowl, combine flour, shortening, sugar and water and mix thoroughly. Combine all ingredients and mix thoroughly. Cover and let dough rise in warm place, 85° free from drafts for 3 hours or until doubled in bulk. Punch dough down; cover; refrigerate overnight. With lightly floured hands, shape dough into 1 1/2-inch balls; place in 3 greased 9-inch cake pans. Let rise in warm, draft free (85°) place approximately 2 hours or until doubled in bulk. Bake at 400° for 10 to 12 minutes. Yields 2 1/2 dozen large rolls. Dough can be stored in refrigerator in greased and covered bowl 5 to 6 days.

title: Fran'S Cheese Balls
text: Fran'S Cheese Balls. Ingredients: 1 (3 oz.) jar Kraft Old English cheese; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese, softened; 4 oz. Roquefort cheese; 1 small onion, finely grated; 3 dashes Worcestershire sauce; chopped parsley, chives; chopped pecans. Directions: Have all ingredients at room temperature. Place first five ingredients in a bowl and mash with a fork. Stir until well blended. Divide in half. Shape into 2 smooth balls. Roll one in parsley-chive mix and the other in chopped nuts. Roll in plastic wrap and chill 2 or 3 days for best flavor. Have at room temperature before serving with crackers or toast points.

title: Rice Surprise Salad
text: Rice Surprise Salad. Ingredients: 1 c. cooked white rice; 2 c. miniature marshmallows; 1 (20 oz.) can crushed pineapple; walnut or pecan pieces (optional); 2 bananas; 1 (8 oz.) Cool Whip. Directions: Drain pineapple juice into a 2-quart bowl. Peel bananas and dip into pineapple juice, then slice into bite-size rounds. Combine bananas into bowl with crushed pineapple, rice, marshmallows and Cool Whip. Mix well and chill 2 hours before serving.

title: Indian Chicken Curry
text: Indian Chicken Curry. Ingredients: 1/3 c. butter; 1 large onion, chopped; 1 c. chopped celery; 2 tart apples, chopped and peeled; 1 Tbsp. curry powder; 6 Tbsp. flour; 1 c. chicken broth; 1/2 c. cream of coconut; 1 pt. half and half; 3 c. diced, cooked chicken. Directions: In a large saucepan, melt butter and saute onion, celery and apples for 5 minutes. Stir in curry and flour. Gradually stir in chicken broth, cream of coconut and half and half. Stir over moderate heat until sauce bubbles and thickens. Stir in chicken and season to taste with salt and pepper. Serve over rice.

title: Stuffing Squash Casserole
text: Stuffing Squash Casserole. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. instant stuffing mix; 3/4 c. boiling water; 1 Tbsp. margarine; 1 medium yellow squash, diced, cooked and drained; 1 egg, beaten; 2 Tbsp. grated Parmesan cheese. Directions: In a bowl, combine the stuffing mix, water and margarine; mix well. Let stand for 5 minutes. Add squash and egg. Transfer to greased 1-quart baking dish; sprinkle with Parmesan cheese. Bake, uncovered, at 350° for 20 to 25 minutes or until golden brown.

title: Beer Cake
text: Beer Cake. Ingredients: 2 c. sugar; 1 egg; 1/2 tsp. allspice; 2 tsp. baking soda; 1 c. raisins; 2 c. beer (one 16 oz. bottle); 1 c. shortening; 1/2 tsp. cloves; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 3 c. flour; nuts, if desired. Directions: Mix all ingredients together and bake at 350° for 1 hour.

title: Egg-Cheese Dish(Breakfast - Brunch)
text: Egg-Cheese Dish(Breakfast - Brunch). Ingredients: 8 eggs, beaten; 8 slices bread, crusts removed and cut into cubes; 2 3/4 c. Velveeta cheese, cubed; 4 c. milk; 3/4 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. white pepper; 1/4 tsp. dry mustard; 1/4 lb. bacon, partially cooked, drained, chopped (reserve). Directions: Grease a 9 x 13-inch pan generously. Spread the bread cubes over bottom of pan. Sprinkle cheese cubes over bread. In bowl beat eggs. Add milk and seasonings. Mix well. Pour over cheese-bread in pan carefully. Cover with plastic or foil and place in fridge overnight. Next morning sprinkle bacon bits over mixture and bake 1 hour at 325°.

title: Caramel Squares
text: Caramel Squares. Ingredients: 2 c. brown sugar, sifted; 1/2 tsp. salt; 2 c. flour; 2 eggs; 2 tsp. baking powder; 1/2 c. butter; 1 c. nuts, chopped; flavoring desired. Directions: Stir sugar and butter over fire until fudge-like and slightly golden. When cool, add beaten eggs, flavoring, salt and baking powder, then nuts. Place in pan and bake 15 or 20 minutes. Cool in pans and cut in squares.

title: Cauliflower Au Gratin With Almonds And Cheese
text: Cauliflower Au Gratin With Almonds And Cheese. Ingredients: 1 small or medium head cauliflower; 1 1/2 c. standard cream sauce; 3/4 c. grated cheese; 1/2 c. blanched, toasted almonds; cayenne pepper or paprika. Directions: Boil cauliflower in salted water until tender (about 20 minutes). Drain. Make cream sauce. If desired, add part of cheese to sauce. Reserve other and sprinkle over top of cauliflower. Place whole cauliflower (or broken into flowerets) into casserole. Pour sauce over cauliflower. Just before serving, sprinkle with grated cheese and almonds. Brown in moderate oven for about 10 minutes. Serves 4.

title: French Coconut Pie
text: French Coconut Pie. Ingredients: 4 eggs; 1 3/4 c. sugar; 1 1/2 sticks melted margarine; 6 tsp. lemon juice; 2 tsp. vanilla; 1 can coconut (3 oz.). Directions: Mix all ingredients together and pour in unbaked shells and bake at 350° for 40 to 45 minutes. Makes 2 pies.

title: Grandma Wade'S Oatmeal Cookies
text: Grandma Wade'S Oatmeal Cookies. Ingredients: 2 c. flour; 2 c. quick oatmeal; 1/2 tsp. baking powder; 1 tsp. baking soda; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1 c. corn oil margarine; 1 c. brown sugar; 1 c. sugar; 1/2 c. peanut butter; 2 eggs; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 c. semi-sweet chocolate chips; 1 c. raisins; 1/2 c. chopped walnuts or pecans. Directions: In a large mixing bowl, cream the margarine, sugars, peanut butter, eggs and vanilla. Combine dry ingredients and add to creamed mixture. When well mixed, fold in the chocolate chips, raisins and nuts, if desired. Drop by spoonful on cookie sheet. Bake 10 to 12 minutes in a 350° oven. Cool on pan for 1 minute, then remove to rack. Freeze in covered container.

title: D C H Chicken Salad A La Sandy Toerpe
text: D C H Chicken Salad A La Sandy Toerpe. Ingredients: 8 cooked and cubed Market Day chicken steaks; 1 c. chopped celery; 1 c. chopped green pepper; 4 chopped green onion tops; dash of ginger; 1 c. Miracle Whip lite; 1 c. Cool Whip lite. Directions: Mix together. Put on a plate with a small bunch of grapes or other fresh fruit. I also put a croissant or fresh roll on each plate.

title: Easy Spicy-Sweet Chicken
text: Easy Spicy-Sweet Chicken. Ingredients: 4 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves; salt; 2 tsp. vegetable oil; 3/4 c. picante sauce or salsa; 1/3 c. orange marmalade; 6 oz. fresh sugar snap peas or Chinese pea pods, cut in half if desired. Directions: Pound chicken to 1/2-inch thickness; sprinkle with salt to taste. Cook chicken in oil in 10-inch skillet over medium heat cooked through, about 5 minutes on each side. Remove from skillet and reserve. Increase heat to medium-high. Add picante sauce, marmalade and peas to skillet; mix well. Cook and stir 3 minutes or until sauce has thickened. Return chicken to skillet; spoon sauce over chicken and heat through, about 1 minute. Serve with additional picante sauce, if desired. Makes 4 servings.

title: Baked Mushrooms
text: Baked Mushrooms. Ingredients: 3 lb. mushrooms, sliced and sauteed in own juice; 1 pkg. Pepperidge Farm seasoned stuffing; 1 lb. shredded sharp Cheddar cheese; 1 c. half and half cream. Directions: Saute mushrooms in own juice. Set aside. Mix stuffing as directed on package. Set aside. Layer all ingredients in a 9 x 12-inch pan using 1/2 of each. Repeat layers. Dot with butter. Bake at 325° to 350° for 30 minutes or until thoroughly heated.

title: Old-Style Pineapple Pudding
text: Old-Style Pineapple Pudding. Ingredients: 1/3 c. cornstarch; 1/4 c. sugar; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. cold water; 2 1/4 c. pineapple juice and water; 1 Tbsp. lemon juice; 1 c. crushed pineapple; 3 egg whites, beaten. Directions: Mix cornstarch, sugar and salt and dilute with cold water, pineapple juice and boiling water. Cook in double boiler, stirring constantly until mixture thickens. Cool slightly; add lemon juice, pineapple and beaten egg whites and pour in molds to chill and set. Serve with custard or whipped cream.

title: Crab-Filled Buns
text: Crab-Filled Buns. Ingredients: 1 small can crab meat, flaked; 1 c. chopped ripe or green olives; 1 1/4 c. diced Swiss cheese; 1 Tbsp. minced green onion; 1/4 c. mayonnaise; 1 Tbsp. lemon juice; salt and pepper to taste; 8 hamburger buns, buttered. Directions: Combine crab meat, olives, cheese and onion. Add mixture of mayonnaise and lemon juice; add seasonings. Fill buttered buns with mixture; wrap each in foil. Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes.

title: Vegetable Dip In Rye Bread
text: Vegetable Dip In Rye Bread. Ingredients: 2 c. shredded Cheddar; 1 1/2 c. sour cream; 8 oz. cream cheese; 1 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1 small grated onion; 1 c. diced ham; 1 small can mild chillies; 1 round rye bread. Directions: Mix all ingredients (except bread). Cut top off bread (keep for lid). Dig out center of bread (keep bread for dipping). Fill bread with mixture. Replace top of bread. Triple wrap bread with foil and place in 350° oven for 1 1/2 hours (or until cheese is melted and mixture is hot all through). Mix pieces of bread with 2 tablespoons of butter and place in oven for 10 minutes. Serve dip warm with raw vegetables, crackers, bread sticks, etc.

title: Seasoned Seeds
text: Seasoned Seeds. Ingredients: 1 c. pumpkin seeds; 1 Tbsp. oleo; 1/2 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/4 tsp. garlic salt; 1/4 tsp. onion salt or seasoned salt. Directions: Remove membrane from seeds. Wash and pat dry. Place in a 9-inch glass pie plate. Add remaining ingredients. Microwave on High for 8 to 10 minutes until slightly toasted, stirring 4 to 5 times.

title: Jezebel Sauce
text: Jezebel Sauce. Ingredients: 1 (18 oz.) jar pineapple preserves; 1 (18 oz.) jar apple jelly; 1 (5 oz.) jar horseradish; 1 1/4 oz. dry mustard; 1 Tbsp. coarse black pepper. Directions: Blend all ingredients. Serve over cream cheese. Serve with crackers.

title: Mom'S Jello Cookies
text: Mom'S Jello Cookies. Ingredients: 4 c. sifted all-purpose flour; 1 tsp. double-acting baking powder; 1 1/2 c. butter; 1 c. sugar; 1 (3 oz.) pkg. Jell-O (red or green)*; 1 egg; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: *Do not substitute lo-cal type.

title: Pretty And Tasty Wedding Punch
text: Pretty And Tasty Wedding Punch. Ingredients: 1 large can pineapple juice; 1 (2 liter) bottle ginger ale, well chilled; 1/2 c. sugar; 1 tsp. almond flavoring. Directions: Place 1/2 cup sugar into mixing container. Add 1 teaspoon almond flavoring and pineapple juice. Stir well until sugar is dissolved. Chill thoroughly, overnight if possible. When ready to serve, pour the pineapple mixture into punch bowl, then add the chilled ginger ale.

title: Long Island Iced Tea
text: Long Island Iced Tea. Ingredients: 1 shot tequila; 1 shot gin; 1 shot vodka; 1 shot rum; 1 shot Triple Sec; 5 shots sour mix; 2 shots Coca-Cola. Directions: Mix together to make 1 serving. Multiply times 4 to make a pitcher.

title: Dilled Shrimp Dip
text: Dilled Shrimp Dip. Ingredients: 1/4 c. milk; 1 (8 oz.) cream cheese, softened; 1 (4 1/2 oz.) can shrimp, rinsed, drained and chopped; 1 tsp. lemon juice; 1 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/2 tsp. garlic salt; 1/4 tsp. dill weed. Directions: Blend milk gradually into cream cheese. Stir in lemon juice, shrimp, Worcestershire sauce, garlic salt and dill. Cover; refrigerate at least 1 hour before serving. Serve with crackers.

title: Beef And Mushroom Pie
text: Beef And Mushroom Pie. Ingredients: 2 slices bacon; 3 lb. beef chuck, cut into 1 1/2-inch cubes; 1 large onion, chopped; 1 carrot, chopped; 1 stalk celery, chopped; 2 cloves garlic, crushed; 1 c. beef broth; 1 c. red wine; 1/2 tsp. sweet marjoram; 1 bay leaf; salt and pepper; pastry for 2-crust pie; 1/2 lb. mushrooms; 2 Tbsp. butter; lemon juice. Directions: Fry bacon slowly in large, heavy skillet. Remove when cooked, crumble and set aside. Heat skillet until almost smoking, brown the beef cubes a few at a time on all sides. Place beef and crumbled bacon in casserole and keep warm. When all the beef has been browned, add the chopped vegetables to the skillet, reduce heat and cook until wilted. Stir in 2 tablespoons flour, cook for a minute or two, add the wine, broth and seasonings; bring to boil, stirring to smooth out sauce. Pour over beef, cover closely and simmer in a 325° oven about two hours or until meat is tender. Make certain it does not boil. Remove casserole from oven and cool. (This is best made the day before baking the pie to facilitate removal of excess fat.)

title: Chocolate Pillows
text: Chocolate Pillows. Ingredients: 1/2 c. peanut butter; 1 pkg (12 oz) chocolate chips; 1 pkg (6 oz) chocolate chips; 2 Tbsp sifted powder sugar; 1 Tbsp. melted oleo; Box of junior size shedded wheat. Directions: Mix first 5 ingredients and melt over hot water and remove from stove. Place shedded wheat on fork and dip in chocolate mixture and place on waxed paper. Cool 1/2 hour.

title: Caramel Crunch Apple Pie
text: Caramel Crunch Apple Pie. Ingredients: 28 Kraft caramels; 2 Tbsp. water; 1 (9-inch) unbaked pastry shell; 4 c. cooking apple slices; 3/4 c. all-purpose flour; 1/3 c. sugar; 1/2 tsp. ground cinnamon; 1/3 c. margarine; 1/2 c. chopped walnuts. Directions: In a saucepan, combine caramels and water, cooking and stirring over low heat until smooth. In a pastry shell, layer apples and caramel sauce. In a mixing bowl, combine flour, sugar and cinnamon. Cut in margarine until mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Stir in nuts. Sprinkle crumb mixture over apples and caramel sauce. Bake at 350° for 40 to 45 minutes, until apples are tender. Cool. Makes 6 to 8 servings.

title: Beer Batter Bread
text: Beer Batter Bread. Ingredients: 3 c. self-rising flour; 3 Tbsp. sugar; 1 (12 oz.) can warm beer; 3 Tbsp. butter or oleo. Directions: Mix dry ingredients in bowl. Add beer and beat 60 strokes with spoon. Fill a lightly greased 9 x 5-inch loaf pan and drizzle top with melted butter. Bake at 375° for 45 to 50 minutes or golden and tests clean. Remove from pan and cool on rack.

title: Papaya Seed Dressing
text: Papaya Seed Dressing. Ingredients: 1/4 c. tarragon vinegar; 1/4 c. sugar; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. dry mustard; 1/2 c. vegetable oil; 1/2 small onion, finely chopped; 1 Tbsp. fresh papaya seed. Directions: Put vinegar and dry ingredients in blender with motor running. Add oil in steady stream. Add onion, then papaya seed and blend until seed looks coarsely ground. Chill for 1 hour. Good on fruit or green salads.

title: Chocolate Caramel Dessert Bar
text: Chocolate Caramel Dessert Bar. Ingredients: 1 box caramel cake mix; 1/4 cup water; 2 eggs; 1/4 cup packed brown sugar; 1 cup chocolate chips; 1/4 cup butter. Directions: Mix cake mix, water, eggs, brown sugar and butter until smooth. Stir in chocolate chips. Spread mixture in 9 X 13 pan. Bake at 375 for 24 - 27 minutes until toothpick comes out almost clean.

title: Blueberry Cream Cheese Pie
text: Blueberry Cream Cheese Pie. Ingredients: 1 pastry shell, cooled; 1/2 c. powdered sugar; 4 oz. Philadelphia cream cheese; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 c. whipping cream; 1 can blueberry pie mix; 2 Tbsp. lemon juice; whipped cream topping (optional). Directions: Mix powdered sugar, cream cheese and vanilla until well blended. Whip whipping cream and fold into cream cheese mixture. Turn into pie shell. Refrigerate at least 3 hours. Mix blueberry pie mix with lemon juice and spread over the top. Add whipped cream, if desired.

title: Apple-Nut Kuchen
text: Apple-Nut Kuchen. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. flour; 2 tsp. double-acting baking powder; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1 large apple, sliced; 1/2 c. milk; 1/2 c. sour cream; 1/2 c. sugar; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. chopped walnuts; 1 egg, beaten; 3 Tbsp. oil; 1/2 c. sugar. Directions: Sift together flour, sugar, baking powder, salt and cinnamon. Mix one egg, 1/2 cup milk and 3 tablespoons oil; stir into flour mixture. Turn into well-oiled 9-inch pan.

title: Peaches And Cream Cheese Cake
text: Peaches And Cream Cheese Cake. Ingredients: 3/4 c. flour; 1 tsp. baking powder; 1/2 tsp. salt; 3 1/4 oz. pkg. dry vanilla pudding (not instant); 3 Tbsp. softened oleo; 1 egg, 1/2 c. milk; 1/2 c. milk; 1 (8 oz.) cream cheese, softened; 1/2 c. sugar; 3 Tbsp. reserved juice; 1 Tbsp. sugar; 1/4 tsp. cinnamon. Directions: Mix and spread over filling. Bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes. Filling will appear soft. Store in refrigerator.

title: Bacon Potato Treats
text: Bacon Potato Treats. Ingredients: 5 potatoes (unpeeled), sliced 1/2 inch thick; 1 onion, grated and cooked; 1/2 tsp. chopped jalapeno; 6 slices crisp bacon, chopped; 1 1/2 c. grated Gruyere cheese or Swiss; salt and pepper. Directions: Arrange slices of potato in rectangular dish, this may require a couple of layers, depending on the size of potatoes. Cover dish with plastic wrap and microwave on High 4 minutes. Rotate and microwave another 4 minutes. When potatoes are cooked, top with grated onion. Sprinkle with jalapeno; add bacon and cheese. Season very well with salt and pepper. Microwave on High 2 minutes, uncovered. Serve.

title: Shrimp On Rice
text: Shrimp On Rice. Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. medium shrimp, peeled and deveined; 1 stick butter or margarine; 2 tsp. garlic salt; 1/2 tsp. black pepper; 2 c. fresh mushroom (sliced); 2 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1 Tbsp. parsley flakes; 3 c. cooked rice. Directions: Melt the butter in a sauce pan, do not brown. Add Worcestershire sauce. Mix in the garlic salt and pepper. Put in the shrimp and mushrooms. Cook, stirring over medium heat for about 8 minutes. The shrimp should begin to turn pink. Stir to mix and add the parsley flakes. Remove from heat, cover and let sit for 4 to 5 minutes. Serve over rice.

title: Walnut Butter Cookies
text: Walnut Butter Cookies. Ingredients: 1 c. flour; 1/2 c. cornstarch; 1/2 c. powdered sugar; 3/4 c. butter (room temperature); 1/2 c. chopped walnuts. Directions: Preheat oven to 300°. Sift first 3 ingredients into a bowl. Add butter and mix well. Stir in walnuts. Drop on baking sheet and bake until light golden brown, about 20 to 25 minutes.

title: Butterscotch Graham Squares
text: Butterscotch Graham Squares. Ingredients: 12 graham crackers; 1/2 c. butter; 1/2 c. firmly packed brown sugar; 1/2 c. chopped pecans. Directions: Preheat oven to 325°. Break each graham cracker in half. Place close together on a 17 1/4 x 11 x 1-inch nonstick cookie pan with side on it. Place butter and brown sugar in a glass measuring cup. Cover loosely with plastic wrap. Microwave on High for 1 minute or until butter is melted. Remove from microwave. Stir. Let cool at room temperature 2 minutes. Add pecans; mix well. Put a teaspoonful on each cracker. Bake 7 minutes. When cool, remove and store.

title: Steamed Corn With Basil Butter
text: Steamed Corn With Basil Butter. Ingredients: 3 Tbsp. chopped fresh basil; 3 Tbsp. butter or margarine, softened; 3 Tbsp. whipped butter, softened; 1 tsp. grated orange rind; 6 ears fresh corn, husks and silks removed. Directions: Combine first 4 ingredients in a small bowl, stirring until blended. Set aside. Cook corn and spread butter mixture evenly over corn.

title: Pumpkin Or Squash Pie
text: Pumpkin Or Squash Pie. Ingredients: 4 eggs; 3/4 c. sugar; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. ginger; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. nutmeg; 2 Tbsp. butter, melted; 1 1/2 c. pumpkin or squash, cooked; 1 1/2 c. evaporated milk. Directions: Beat eggs and add sugar, salt and spices. Mix well (may use a blender). Add butter, pumpkin and milk and mix until well blended. Pour into unbaked pastry crust. Bake at 450° for 15 minutes. Then finish baking at 350° for 30 minutes or until knife inserted into center of pie comes out clean.

title: Apple Pecan Cake
text: Apple Pecan Cake. Ingredients: 1 c. Wesson oil; 2 1/2 c. flour; 1 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. soda; 2 c. sugar; 2 tsp. baking powder; 2 c. peeled and chopped apples; 2 c. pecans, chopped; 1 tsp. vanilla; 2 eggs, well beaten. Directions: Combine all ingredients in bowl and mix thoroughly. Bake in 350° oven for 45 minutes or until done.

title: Deer Or Beef Jerky
text: Deer Or Beef Jerky. Ingredients: 3 lb. beef or deer; 1 Tbsp. salt; 1 Tbsp. onion powder; 1 tsp. garlic powder; 1/2 tsp. black pepper; 1/2 tsp. red pepper. Directions: Cut meat in strips 1/4 inch thick, 3 inches long and 1/2 inch wide. Put meat in 2-inch deep pan. Mix other ingredients with 1 glass of water, then add enough water to cover meat. Soak meat for 24 to 36 hours. Stir every 10 hours to coat well. After soaking, place meat on paper towels to drain water. Sprinkle one side with red pepper. Move oven rack to top of oven. Place toothpick through end of meat and hang on rack. Put oven on lowest setting. Use spoon to crack door open. Cook 8 to 12 hours; if crunchy at 8 hours, take out. After cooling, store in jar in refrigerator.

title: Joe'S Fruit Cobbler
text: Joe'S Fruit Cobbler. Ingredients: 1 c. sugar; 1 c. flour; 2 tsp. baking powder; 1 can pie filling; 3/4 c. milk; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 stick butter. Directions: Melt butter in an 8 x 8-inch pan. Mix other ingredients, except pie filling, together. Add to melted butter. Pour 1 can of pie filling over this; do not stir. Bake in a 325° oven for 55 minutes.

title: Hawaiian Delight Pie
text: Hawaiian Delight Pie. Ingredients: 1 can Eagle Brand milk; 1/2 c. lemon juice; 1 large carton Cool Whip; 1 large can crushed pineapple, drained; 1 baked pie shell. Directions: Mix all ingredients, put in pie shell and chill.

title: Easy Cranberry Salad("Yum-Yum")
text: Easy Cranberry Salad("Yum-Yum"). Ingredients: 1 pkg. cranberries; 4 medium apples; 1 pkg. mini marshmallows; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 1 c. chopped nuts; 1 pt. whipped cream. Directions: Grind cranberries and apples; add marshmallows and 1 1/2 cups sugar. Mix well; let stand. Beat cream; add nuts to cream. Mix with the rest of ingredients. Freeze in serving size containers until ready to serve.

title: Southern Maryland Stuffed Ham
text: Southern Maryland Stuffed Ham. Ingredients: 1 (10 to 12 lb.) ham; 1 lb. greens (kale, collards or mustard); 1 medium cabbage, shredded; 2 Tbsp. crushed red pepper; 1 c. chopped celery. Directions: Cut/tear up cleaned greens. Combine with cabbage, celery and red pepper. Place ham on clean cheese cloth. Make small slits all around the ham. Stuff the green mixture into the slits. Tie 1 package cheesecloth around the ham and place in a roaster that is half filled with water. Boil for at least 1 hour or until ham is done. Remove cheesecloth and serve.

title: Lemon Graham Cracker Pie
text: Lemon Graham Cracker Pie. Ingredients: 1 (12 oz.) can evaporated milk, chilled; 1 small pkg. lemon jello; 1 c. boiling water. Directions: Combine jello and water. Let cool but not set. Whip evaporated milk until thick in a large bowl. Add juice of 1 lemon. Beat in 3/4 cup of sugar. Add cooled Jell-O mix. Pour into 2 graham cracker shells. Sprinkle with graham cracker crumbs or "sprinkles." Place in refrigerator until set.

title: Chili Oil
text: Chili Oil. Ingredients: 1 c. peanut oil (or other oil); 1 oz. crushed fried chile peppers. Directions: The secret to a good mustard dipping sauce is to add 1/3 part white vinegar to 2/3 parts water when mixing with your powdered mustard for dipping your prawns, won tons, etc.

title: Cole Slaw For Freezing
text: Cole Slaw For Freezing. Ingredients: 1 head cabbage; 1 pepper; 1 carrot; 1 tsp. salt. Directions: Shred these ingredients medium fine. Mix all together and let stand 1 hour. Make syrup for cole slaw and let cool.

title: Glazed Chicken
text: Glazed Chicken. Ingredients: 6 to 10 chicken pieces; 1 small bottle Russian dressing; 1 packet Lipton onion soup mix; 1 medium jar apricot preserves. Directions: Wash chicken pieces. In a dish, mix together Russian dressing, onion soup mix and apricot preserves. Dip each piece of chicken into mixture and place in pan. Bake in oven at 350° for 30 minutes. Turn chicken over. Brush with left over mixture and bake until done.

title: Railroad Pie
text: Railroad Pie. Ingredients: 1 1/4 lb. ground beef; 1 large onion, chopped; 1 (11 oz.) can whole kernel corn, drained; 1 (14 oz.) can Mexican-style stewed tomatoes; 1 Tbsp. chili powder; dash of black pepper; 1/2 c. chopped green pepper; 1 (8 oz.) container sour cream; 1 tsp. salt. Directions: Brown ground beef and onion in skillet; drain well. Combine remaining filling ingredients with meat mixture and pour into a 2-quart greased casserole.

title: Chop Suey Casserole
text: Chop Suey Casserole. Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. pork, cut up; 5 Tbsp. soy sauce; 1 c. celery, diced; 1 onion, chopped; 1 can cream of mushroom soup; 1 can mushrooms (undrained); 1 can bean sprouts; 1/4 pkg. fine noodles, cooked; chow mein noodles. Directions: Brown the pork in the soy sauce. Add the celery and onion. Add the mushroom soup, mushrooms, bean sprouts and noodles. Put into casserole dish and bake at 350° for 1 hour or until pork is done. During the last 10 minutes of baking, sprinkle chow mein noodles on top.

title: Double Fudge Brownie Bars
text: Double Fudge Brownie Bars. Ingredients: 1 (19.8 or 21.5 oz.) pkg. fudge brownie mix; 3/4 c. chopped nuts; 2 c. (12 oz.) semi-sweet chocolate chips; 1/4 c. margarine or butter; 1 (14 oz.) can Eagle Brand sweetened condensed milk (not evaporated); 1 tsp. vanilla extract. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Prepare brownie mix as package directs. Stir in nuts. Spread into greased 13 x 9-inch baking pan. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes or until center is set. In heavy saucepan, over medium heat, melt chips and margarine with sweetened condensed milk and vanilla. Spread evenly over brownie layer. Cool. Chill 2 hours or until topping is firm. Cut into bars. Store covered at room temperature. Makes 24 to 36 bars.

title: One Dish Ham Bake(Chicken, Turkey Or Beef Can Be Used.)
text: One Dish Ham Bake(Chicken, Turkey Or Beef Can Be Used.). Ingredients: 1 pkg. frozen broccoli cuts, rinsed and drained; 1 can cream of mushroom soup; 1 can Cheddar cheese soup; 1 c. milk; 3 c. meat, cut in small pieces; 1/2 tsp. onion powder (1/4 c. cut up onions can be used); 2 c. Bisquick; 1 1/2 c. milk. Directions: Mix broccoli, soups, 1 cup milk, meat and onion. Put in 9 x 13-inch pan. Mix Bisquick and 1 1/2 cups milk and pour evenly over soup mixture. Bake 25 to 30 minutes in 450° oven. Let stand for 5 minutes before serving. Serve with tossed salad and French bread for complete meal.

title: Thousand Island Dressing
text: Thousand Island Dressing. Ingredients: 4 c. Miracle Whip; 1 can tomato soup; 1 Tbsp. vinegar; 4 Tbsp. sweet relish; 2 Tbsp. chili sauce; 1 Tbsp. sugar; 1 Tbsp. dry mustard; 1 small grated onion; 1 clove grated garlic. Directions: Mix all ingredients together. Makes 1 1/2 quarts.

title: S'Mores Pudding Dessert
text: S'Mores Pudding Dessert. Ingredients: 9 full size graham crackers; 3 1/2 c. milk; 1 (5 oz.) vanilla pudding-pie filling mix (not instant); 3 (1 3/8 oz.) plain milk chocolate candy bars; 2 c. (about) miniature marshmallows. Directions: Oil or butter a 1 1/2 quart glass baking dish; then line bottom of dish with 1/3 of the whole size graham crackers. Cook pudding mix with the milk, using a little extra milk if needed, as directed on package. Pudding should not be too thick. Cool pudding not more than 5 minutes.

title: Gooey Buns
text: Gooey Buns. Ingredients: 1 lb. bologna; 3/4 lb. Velveeta cheese; 1/3 c. mustard; 3 hard-boiled eggs; 1 Tbsp. minced onion; 2 Tbsp. sweet pickles. Directions: Grind bologna and cheese. Add remaining ingredients and mix well. Butter 6 buns. Fill and wrap individually in tinfoil. Bake in a 325° oven for 25 minutes.

title: Oatmeal Cookie
text: Oatmeal Cookie. Ingredients: 1 c. shortening; 1 c. brown sugar; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 tsp. salt; 3 c. quick cooking oats; 1/2 c. nuts (optional); 1 c. sugar; 2 eggs; 1 1/2 c. flour; 1 tsp. soda. Directions: Cream shortening and sugars. Add eggs and vanilla; beat well. Add sifted dry ingredients. Add oatmeal and nuts. Mix well. Bake in 350° oven for 10 minutes.

title: Crabmeat Quesadillas
text: Crabmeat Quesadillas. Ingredients: 8 oz. fresh lump crabmeat, drained; 1/2 c. diced sweet red pepper; 1/3 c. chopped green onions; 1/4 c. plus 1 Tbsp. nonfat sour cream; 1 tsp. hot sauce; 1/2 tsp. ground cumin; 10 (8-inch) flour tortillas; 1 1/4 c. (5 oz.) shredded Monterey Jack cheese. Directions: Combine first 6 ingredients in a medium bowl; stir well. Spoon crabmeat mixture evenly over 5 tortillas. Spread to within 1/2-inch of edge. Sprinkle evenly with cheese and top with remaining tortillas.

title: Banana Dump Cake
text: Banana Dump Cake. Ingredients: 1/2 c. oleo; 2 c. sugar; 2 eggs; 1 c. mashed bananas; 1 c. sour milk; 2 c. flour; 1 tsp. soda; 1 tsp. baking powder. Directions: Dump in first 4 ingredients and mix well. Dump in remaining 4 ingredients, again mixing well. Pour into greased and floured oblong pan and bake at 350°.

title: Chicken Cheese Soup
text: Chicken Cheese Soup. Ingredients: 8 chicken breast halves, cooked and cubed; 5 slices bacon, cooked crisp and crumbled; 4 sliced carrots; 3 stalks celery; 2 c. cubed potatoes; 1 large onion; 2 qt. water; 12 chicken bouillon cubes; 1 1/2 c. real butter; 1 1/2 c. flour; 1 (16 oz.) container Cheez Whiz; 1 (16 oz.) pkg. frozen peas. Directions: Cover vegetables with water and simmer until tender. Bring the bouillon cubes and 2 quarts of water to boil, being sure that the bouillon cubes are dissolved. Melt butter in another pan; add flour to butter slowly. Stir constantly. In just a few seconds, it will be thick. Then add to this mixture, stirring quickly, 1 cup of the boiling water with bouillon cubes, then add a little more, then add all of this mixture to the water mixture. Stir thoroughly. Add this mixture to the vegetable mixture. Do not drain vegetables. Add Cheez Whiz to soup and stir until dissolved. Add peas to soup. Add chicken cubes. Add bacon crumbles. Simmer for 10 minutes. You're done.

title: Poppy Seed Torte
text: Poppy Seed Torte. Ingredients: 1 c. graham cracker crumbs; 1 c. flour; 1 c. nuts; 1/2 c. butter. Directions: Make like pie crust. Pat into 9 x 13 pan. Bake 15 minutes at 325°.

title: Shrimp Macaroni Salad
text: Shrimp Macaroni Salad. Ingredients: 1 (16 oz.) pkg. macaroni; 1 clove garlic, diced; 2 sprigs parsley, chopped; 1 or 2 c. mayonnaise (Hellmann's); 1 or 2 (6 oz.) pkg. frozen baby cooked shrimp, thawed; 1 stalk celery, chopped. Directions: Cook macaroni according to package directions firm, drain and wash in colander. Pour into bowl; add rest of ingredients. Add more mayonnaise, if desired, so it's not dry. Salt and pepper to taste.

title: Pumpkin-Chocolate Bread
text: Pumpkin-Chocolate Bread. Ingredients: 1 can pumpkin; 1 c. vegetable oil; 2/3 c. water; 4 eggs; 3 1/2 c. flour; 3 c. sugar; 2 c. chocolate chips; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 2 tsp. soda; 1 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 c. pecans. Directions: In a mixing bowl combine pumpkin, oil, water and eggs; mix well. Add sugar. Sift together in a small bowl, flour, soda and salt. Add flour mixture to the pumpkin mixture. Stir in cinnamon, chocolate chips and pecans. Bake at 350° for 55 to 60 minutes. Makes 3 loaf pans.

title: Christmas Cranberry Salad
text: Christmas Cranberry Salad. Ingredients: 1 c. sugar; 1 family size pkg. strawberry jello; 1 envelope plain gelatin; 1 c. pecans, ground; 1 lb. fresh cranberries, cooked; 1 (No. 2) can crushed pineapple; 1 can mandarin oranges, cut in half; 1 diced apple. Directions: Follow directions on gelatin using 2 cups of boiling water and plain gelatin. Add sugar. Mix to dissolve. Add the rest of the ingredients. Mix well and chill.

title: Easy Fresh Fruit Dip
text: Easy Fresh Fruit Dip. Ingredients: 16 oz. sour cream; 1 pkg. instant vanilla pudding. Directions: Add pudding to sour cream. Stir until smooth. If too thick, add a little bit of milk. Serve with sliced fresh fruit.

title: Yogurt Fruit Pie
text: Yogurt Fruit Pie. Ingredients: 2 graham cracker crusts; 1 (12 oz.) Cool Whip; 2 (8 oz.) yogurt; 2 c. fruit. Directions: Select the flavored yogurt that would go best with your fruit combination. Strawberry and banana use strawberry yogurt, pineapple (crushed) and coconut use pina colada, mandarin orange slices with lemon yogurt. Mix ingredients together; pour into pie shells and refrigerate 2 hours. Serve.

title: Irish Potatoes
text: Irish Potatoes. Ingredients: 1 (10 oz.) frozen spinach; 6 medium potatoes; 1 stick margarine; 1/2 pt. sour cream (1 cup); 1 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. dill seed; 1/2 tsp. dehydrated onion; 1/2 c. grated Cheddar cheese. Directions: Grease a 2-quart baking dish. Thaw spinach and drain thoroughly. Cook potatoes and mash. Add margarine, sour cream and seasonings. Stir in spinach. Turn into baking dish, sprinkle with cheese. Bake at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes. Yields 6 servings.

title: Eggplant Souffle
text: Eggplant Souffle. Ingredients: 1 large eggplant; dash of onion powder; dash of dill; 1/2 c. mushroom soup; 1/3 c. mayonnaise; 1 egg; 3/4 c. cracker crumbs; 1/4 stick butter or margarine, melted; 1 c. sharp grated cheese. Directions: Peel eggplant and cook in boiling water 7 to 10 minutes. Drain and season with dash of onion powder and dill. Mix remaining ingredients with 1/3 cup cracker crumbs and combine with eggplant. Put in 2-quart greased casserole. Sprinkle remaining cracker crumbs on top and dot with butter and dash of paprika. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes or until bubbly. Serves 4 to 6.

title: Hot Ryes
text: Hot Ryes. Ingredients: 1 c. finely grated Swiss cheese; 1/4 c. cooked and crumbled bacon; 1 (4 1/2 oz.) can ripe olives; 1/4 c. minced onions; 1 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/2 c. mayonnaise; 1 loaf party rye. Directions: Mix together all ingredients, except bread. Spread on party rye bread. Bake at 375° for 10 to 15 minutes or until browned. May be frozen after baking and reheated.

title: Spicy Taco Dip
text: Spicy Taco Dip. Ingredients: 1 pkg. Hidden Valley Ranch dressing (dry); 2 c. mayo; 1 c. milk; 1 c. sour cream; 1 c. Cheddar cheese, grated; 1 c. hot pepper cheese; 1 c. hot pepper rings, chopped fine and drained well; 1 c. green peppers, chopped; 1 c. black olives; 1 c. tomatoes; 1 bag nacho chips. Directions: Combine Ranch dressing, mayo, milk and sour cream. Pour into a 9 x 13-inch pan. Layer cheeses, peppers, black olives and tomatoes. Refrigerate 1 hour. Serve with nacho chips.

title: Grilled Ham Sandwich
text: Grilled Ham Sandwich. Ingredients: 1 (3 oz.) pkg. Philadelphia Brand cream cheese, softened; 2 tsp. Dijon mustard; 1 tsp. Kraft prepared horseradish; 8 whole wheat bread slices; 1/2 lb. Velveeta pasteurized process cheese spread, sliced; 4 boiled ham slices; soft Parkay margarine. Directions: Combine cream cheese, mustard and horseradish, mixing until well blended. Spread approximately 1 tablespoon cream cheese mixture onto each bread slice. For each sandwich, cover one bread slice with process cheese spread, ham and second bread slice. Spread sandwich with margarine. Grill until lightly browned on both sides. Makes 4 sandwiches.

title: Chocolate Vanities
text: Chocolate Vanities. Ingredients: 1/3 c. flour; 1/2 tsp. salt; 2 tsp. ligth corn syrup; 2 oz. melted chocolate; 2 1/2 c. walnuts, cut in large pieces; 2/3 c. sugar; 1/3 c. shortening; 1 egg (unbeaten); 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Sift flour, salt and sugar. Add remaining ingredients except nuts. Beat 200 strokes. Stir in nuts. Drop by teaspoonfuls 1 inch apart on greased cookie sheet. Bake at 350° just 8 minutes. Let cookies cool on cookie sheet for 2 minutes, then remove to wire rack. Makes 4 dozen.

title: Fruited Eggnog Bread
text: Fruited Eggnog Bread. Ingredients: 2 3/4 c. flour; 3/4 c. sugar; 1 Tbsp. baking powder; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. mace; 1 1/2 c. egg nog; 1/4 c. butter, melted; 1 egg; 1 Tbsp. rum; 3/4 c. chopped pecans; 3/4 c. mixed candied fruit. Directions: Mix flour, sugar, baking powder, salt and mace. Stir in egg nog, butter, egg and rum. Stir just until flour is moist. Fold in pecans and mixed candied fruit. Pour into 9 x 5-inch pan or two smaller loaf pans. Spray pans with cooking spray. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes to 1 hour or until toothpick comes out clean.

title: Homemade Egg Noodles
text: Homemade Egg Noodles. Ingredients: 1 c. flour; 1 tsp. salt; 1 egg; 2 tsp. water. Directions: Mix ingredients until well blended. Mixture will be extremely firm dough. Turn onto large floured surface. Sprinkle with flour and roll until extremely thin. Move dough around as rolling to make sure underside of dough is floured well to keep from sticking. Cover thin dough with thin layer of flour. Roll dough, beginning at one edge. Slice dough into 1/2 or 1/4-inch strips. Unroll and coat each noodle evenly with flour. Dry on surface for 1 hour or dry completely and store until needed.

title: Corned Beef In Foil
text: Corned Beef In Foil. Ingredients: 3 to 4 lb. corned beef; 1/4 c. water; 2 Tbsp. pickling spice; 1 orange, sliced; 1 onion, sliced; 1 celery stalk with leaves; 1 carrot, sliced. Directions: Soak corned beef in water to cover for 1/2 hour or longer if deeply corned. Place a large sheet of heavy-duty foil on a shallow pan. Remove corned beef from water and pat dry to remove any salt on surface. Place in center of foil and pour over it 1/4 cup fresh water. Sprinkle with the spice and arrange orange slices and vegetables over and around the meat. Bring long ends of foil up over meat and seal with a tight double fold. Seal other ends, turning them up so liquid can not run out. Bake in a 300° oven for 4 hours. Remember, always slice meat across the grain.

title: Pineapple And Rice Casserole
text: Pineapple And Rice Casserole. Ingredients: 3/4 c. fine bread crumbs; 1/2 c. milk; 1 lb. ground beef; 1 egg, slightly beaten; 3/4 c. finely chopped onion; 2 1/2 tsp. salt; 2 Tbsp. butter; 1 1/3 c. Minute rice; 1 can sliced pineapple; 1 green pepper, diced; 1/4 c. catsup. Directions: Combine bread crumbs, milk, beef, egg, 1/4 cup onion and 1 1/2 teaspoons salt. Gently form into 20 meatballs; brown in butter in large skillet. Add rice and remaining onion; stir gently until rice is lightly browned. Drain pineapple, measuring syrup. Add water to syrup to make 1 1/3 cups liquid. Cut each pineapple slice into 6 wedges; gradually add with the liquid, 1 teaspoon salt and remaining ingredients to rice mixture; stir thoroughly. Bring to boil. Reduce heat, cover and simmer 5 minutes.

title: Dill Weed Dip
text: Dill Weed Dip. Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) sour cream; 1 c. Hellmann's mayonnaise; 2 tsp. minced dry onions; 1 tsp. seasoned salt; 1 tsp. dill weed. Directions: Mix all ingredients well. Must be Hellmann's (do not substitute). This dip tastes better if you make it the night before and refrigerate.

title: Rookie'S Quiche
text: Rookie'S Quiche. Ingredients: 3 eggs; 3/4 can evaporated milk; 8 oz. Cheddar cheese, shredded; 1 pkg. frozen spinach, drained in colander; 1/4 c. salsa; 2 pie crusts. Directions: Mix together. Pour in 2 pie crusts. Bake 45 minutes in a 400° oven. Cool a few minutes and serve.

title: Creamy Icing
text: Creamy Icing. Ingredients: 1 box powdered sugar; 1/4 c. Crisco; 1/4 c. butter; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1/8 tsp. water. Directions: Whip all together until creamy. Will ice: 1 (13 x 9-inch) cake, 2 round cakes or 24 cupcakes.

title: Pepper Sauce
text: Pepper Sauce. Ingredients: 1/4 c. oil; 1/2 c. chopped onion; 2 c. tomato sauce; 1/2 c. water; 1 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 2 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 2 Tbsp. brown sugar; 2 Tbsp. lemon juice. Directions: Cook onion in oil and add rest of ingredients. Add 2 peppers, cut in pieces. Cook until soft, about 45 minutes to an hour.

title: Orange Fruit Salad(Low-Fat)
text: Orange Fruit Salad(Low-Fat). Ingredients: 3 c. cottage cheese (low-fat); 1 large tub lite Cool Whip; 1 large orange jello (sugar-free); 2 (11 oz.) cans mandarin oranges, drained; 1 large crushed pineapple, drained. Directions: Mix together all ingredients, except jello. Then add jello last. Mix well and keep in refrigerator until firm.

title: Cucumber And Cream Cheese Spread
text: Cucumber And Cream Cheese Spread. Ingredients: 2 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese; 2 tsp. lemon juice; 1/4 tsp. horseradish; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. hot sauce; 2 tsp. minced onion; 1/2 medium cucumber, peeled and chopped. Directions: Beat cream cheese until smooth. Add next 4 ingredients and mix well. Stir in onion and cucumber. Spoon into covered container. Chill until ready to use. Makes 2 cups.

title: Bible Cake
text: Bible Cake. Ingredients: 1/2 c. Genesis 18:8 (butter); 1 c. Isaiah 7:15 (honey); 2 Job 39:14 (eggs); 2 c. 1 Samuel 28:24 (flour); 2 tsp. 1 Kings 19:6 (baking powder); 1 tsp. Exodus 30:23 (cinnamon); 1/4 tsp. Luke 14:34 (salt); 3/4 c. 2 Samuel 16:1 (raisins); 1/2 c. Genesis 43:11 (chopped nuts); Judges 5:25 (butter). Directions: In a large bowl, soften and cream Genesis 18. Slowly beat in Isaiah. In small bowl, beat Job well and add to creamed mixture. Sift together 1 Samuel, 1 Kings, Exodus and Luke. Add to creamed mixture and mix thoroughly. Fold in 2 Samuel and Genesis 43. Grease a 9-inch tube pan with Judges. Line with waxed paper; pour in batter. Bake 30 minutes at 350°. Lower oven temperature to 325° and bake 20 to 25 minutes longer until cake is golden brown and tests done. Cool in pan on wire rack 15 minutes. Invert onto serving plate, remove waxed paper and cool completely.

title: Chiffon Cake
text: Chiffon Cake. Ingredients: 2 c. flour; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 3 tsp. baking powder; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. vegetable oil; 7 egg yolks; 3/4 c. cold water; 2 tsp. vanilla; 2 tsp. grated lemon rind; 7 egg whites; 1/2 tsp. cream of tartar. Directions: Preheat oven to 325°. Blend flour, sugar, baking powder and salt in bowl. Make a well and add in order: oil, egg yolks, water, vanilla and rind. Beat with spoon until smooth. Beat egg whites and cream of tartar in a large mixing bowl until the whites form very stiff peaks.* Pour egg yolk mixture over egg whites, gently folding with a rubber spatula just until blended. Pour into ungreased tube pan. Bake at 325° for 55 minutes, then at 350° for 10 to 15 minutes more. Let it hang until cold.

title: Crock-Pot Spareribs
text: Crock-Pot Spareribs. Ingredients: 4 to 5 lb. spareribs; 1/2 c. ketchup; 2 Tbsp. brown sugar; 1/2 c. A.1. steak sauce; 1/4 tsp. salt; 2 Tbsp. vinegar; 1/2 tsp. hickory salt; 1 tsp. instant minced onion. Directions: Brown ribs in a pan for 15 minutes. Drain fat. Place ribs in crock-pot. Combine other ingredients and pour over ribs. Cook on medium for 4 to 5 hours.

title: Round Steak Soup
text: Round Steak Soup. Ingredients: 1 lb. round steak, cut in strips; garlic salt; 2 potatoes, cut up; 2 carrots, cut up; 1 can whole green beans; 1 can stewed tomatoes; 1 can tomato soup. Directions: Sprinkle meat strips with garlic salt. Place in bottom of crock-pot. Add potato pieces and carrot pieces. Drain green beans and add to crock-pot. Pour in can of tomato soup and can of tomatoes. Cook on low for 8 to 10 hours. Serve warm.

title: Perfect Every Time Lasagna
text: Perfect Every Time Lasagna. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground round; 1 clove garlic, crushed or minced or garlic powder; 1 Tbsp. basil leaves; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 lb. can whole tomatoes; 2 (6 oz.) cans tomato paste; 6 lasagna noodles; 2 eggs; 3 c. large curd cottage cheese; 1/2 c. Parmesan cheese, grated; 2 Tbsp. parsley flakes; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 1 lb. or less Mozzarella cheese slices. Directions: Brown meat and add next 5 ingredients (through paste). Simmer, uncovered, 30 minutes.

title: Vanilla Peanut Butter Fudge
text: Vanilla Peanut Butter Fudge. Ingredients: 2 c. white sugar; 1 pkg. light brown sugar; 3/4 c. milk; 12 oz. peanut butter; 1 (7 1/2 oz.) Marshmallow Fluff; 1 c. chopped walnuts. Directions: In a saucepan, mix white sugar, brown sugar and milk. Cook 1 1/2 minutes. After it boils, add peanut butter, walnuts and Marshmallow Fluff; mix well. Pour into large 13 x 9 x 2-inch lightly greased pan. Cool.

title: Parmesan Bread With Red Peppers And Mushrooms
text: Parmesan Bread With Red Peppers And Mushrooms. Ingredients: 1 c. sweet red pepper, coarsely chopped; 1/2 medium onion, coarsely chopped; 1/2 c. mushrooms, sliced; 1 Tbsp. olive oil; 2 (1/4 oz. each) pkg. regular or fast rising yeast; 1 Tbsp. sugar; 1 1/2 c. warm water (110~); 1 egg; 3 to 4 c. all-purpose flour; 1/4 c. grated Parmesan cheese; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. cayenne pepper; 1/8 tsp. black pepper; milk; 2 Tbsp. Parmesan cheese. Directions: Saute vegetables in olive oil until crisp and tender. Cool. In a large bowl, stir yeast and sugar in warm water. Let stand 5 minutes. Stir in egg. Mix in 3 cups flour, 1/4 cup cheese, salt, cayenne pepper and black pepper. Mix in cooled vegetables and enough remaining flour to make a soft dough. Knead dough on a floured surface until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes.

title: English Pasty
text: English Pasty. Ingredients: double pie crust; 2 1/2 c. diced potatoes; 1/4 c. minced onion; 1 lb. pork loin, cut into bite-size pieces; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Place bottom crust in 8-inch pie pan. Spread diced potatoes evenly over bottom crust. Sprinkle minced onion over potatoes. Spread pieces of pork evenly on top of potatoes and onion. Season to taste. Cover with vented top pie crust and bake at 350° for 1 hour.

title: M & M Cookies
text: M & M Cookies. Ingredients: 1 c. Crisco; 1 c. brown sugar; 1/2 c. granulated sugar; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1/2 tsp. water; 2 eggs; 2 c. plus 4 Tbsp. all-purpose flour; 1 tsp. soda; 1 tsp. salt; 1 1/2 c. M & M plain candies. Directions: Blend Crisco and sugars. Beat in vanilla, water, and egg. Sift dry ingredients together and add to sugar and egg mixture. Mix well. Stir in M & M's. Drop by teaspoon onto ungreased cookie sheet. Bake at 375° for 10 to 12 minutes until golden brown. Yield: 6 dozen cookies.

title: Vegetable Stew
text: Vegetable Stew. Ingredients: red beans; navy beans; beef broth; 2 onions; several garlic cloves; celery; carrots; eggplant; herbs (your choice); olive oil. Directions: Soak beans in broth. Cook down with garlic that has been minced. Add onions, celery and carrots, all chopped. Add eggplant, peeled and chopped, and olive oil. Season with herbs. Cook on very low burner until done.

title: Red Torte Cake
text: Red Torte Cake. Ingredients: 3 Tbsp. flour; 1 c. milk; 1 c. sugar; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 c. butter. Directions: Cook flour and milk until thick, stirring constantly. Cool. Cream sugar, vanilla and butter. Add the flour and milk mixture. Ice cake.

title: Broccoli And Chicken Casserole
text: Broccoli And Chicken Casserole. Ingredients: 12 oz. egg noodles; 4 boneless, skinless breasts, cooked and chopped in bite size pieces; 16 oz. chopped broccoli, thawed; 1/2 c. sliced green onions; 1 can mushroom soup; 1 can potato soup; 1 c. skim milk; 3/4 lb. Swiss cheese, grated; mushrooms (fresh or canned); salt; pepper; paprika. Directions: Cook noodles according to package; drain. In baking dish, stir together (gently) the noodles, broccoli, chicken, onion and mushrooms. In a bowl, stir together the soup, milk, cheese, salt and pepper (reserve 1/2 cup cheese). Stir all into noodle mixture. Bake, covered, at 350° for 40 minutes. Sprinkle reserved cheese and paprika over all and return to oven for 10 minutes (or until cheese melts). Contains 290 calories per serving and 11 grams fat.

title: Corn Puddings
text: Corn Puddings. Ingredients: 1 can yellow creamed corn (17 oz.); 1 can yellow whole kernel corn (12 oz.); 1/4 c. sugar; 3 Tbsp. flour; 1/4 c. milk; 3 well beaten eggs; 2 Tbsp. butter or margarine. Directions: Mix flour and sugar together. Stir in corn and all other ingredients. Pour into greased baking dish. Bake until thick, but not too dry (approximately 30 minutes at 350°).

title: Quick Macaroons
text: Quick Macaroons. Ingredients: 3 large or 4 medium egg whites; 1 tsp. lemon juice; 1 c. shredded coconut; 1/2 c. sugar; 1/4 tsp. cream of tartar; 2 tsp. all-purpose flour. Directions: Heat oven to 300°. Line 12 muffin cups with paper liners. Put room temperature egg whites in medium all metal or glass bowl with cream of tartar and beat at high speed until foamy. Add sugar, 2 tablespoons at a time, beating until whites hold stiff glossy peaks. Add lemon juice and flour. Fold in with a spatula until blended. Fold in coconut. Spoon into the lined muffin cups. Bake 30 to 35 minutes. Cool on a rack. As soon as you can, remove them from the pan.

title: Croquant
text: Croquant. Ingredients: 1/2 c. finely chopped almonds; 1/3 c. sugar; 1/2 c. flour; pinch of salt; 1/2 stick sweet butter; 1 tsp. vanilla extract or use vanilla sugar. Directions: This mixture may be made in advance and kept in a covered jar in the icebox. It can be used over any fresh, canned or frozen fruit.

title: Potato Cheese Bake
text: Potato Cheese Bake. Ingredients: 6 to 8 baked potatoes; 1/2 lb. Velveeta cheese; 1 lb. bacon; 1 onion; 1 c. Hellmann's mayonnaise. Directions: Bake potatoes. Cube with skins on. Cook bacon halfway, then combine with potatoes and continue cooking. Add cheese and mayonnaise; mix together. Put in 9 x 13-inch pan for about 30 minutes at 300°.

title: Laura Ingalls Wilder Gingerbread
text: Laura Ingalls Wilder Gingerbread. Ingredients: 1/2 c. butter; 1 c. brown sugar; 1 c. molasses; 1 c. boiling water; 2 tsp. baking soda; 3 c. flour; 1 tsp. ginger; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1 tsp. cloves; 1 tsp. allspice; 3 eggs, beaten. Directions: Cream all ingredients. Add eggs last. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes in a 9 x 13-inch greased pan.

title: Onions And Rice
text: Onions And Rice. Ingredients: 3/4 c. thinly sliced green onions; 2 Tbsp. vegetable oil; 1 c. uncooked long grain rice; 1/2 c. minced green pepper; 1/4 c. minced fresh parsley; 2 c. water; 2 tsp. chicken flavored bouillon granules; 1/4 tsp. pepper; green onion fan. Directions: Saute green onions in hot oil in a large skillet until tender. Add rice and next 5 ingredients. Cover and simmer 20 minutes or until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed. Garnish with a green onion fan. Yields 4 to 6 servings.

title: Chicken Dijon
text: Chicken Dijon. Ingredients: 2 whole chicken breasts, split, boned and skinned; 2 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 1/2 Tbsp. all-purpose flour; 1/2 c. chicken broth; 1/4 c. half and half; 1 Tbsp. Dijon mustard. Directions: Saute chicken in butter over low heat about 20 minutes or until tender. Remove chicken to a warm platter. Blend flour into pan drippings; cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until bubbly. Gradually stir in broth and half and half; cook until smooth and thickened, stirring constantly. Stir in mustard. Add chicken; cover and simmer about 10 minutes. Yield: 2 servings.

title: Chili Casserole
text: Chili Casserole. Ingredients: 1 (6 1/2 oz.) pkg. tortilla chips; 1 (20 oz.) can chili; 1 (16 oz.) can Ranch Style beans; 1 (11 oz.) can Cheddar soup, undiluted. Directions: Place 2 cups broken chips in a lightly greased 2-quart baking dish; spread half of soup over chips. Spread chili and beans over soup and top with remaining soup. Bake, uncovered, at 350° for 30 minutes. Sprinkle with remaining chips and bake 5 minutes longer. Yields 4 to 6 servings.

title: Quick And Easy Vanilla Cookies
text: Quick And Easy Vanilla Cookies. Ingredients: 1/2 c. Crisco; 1 egg; 1 1/2 c. flour; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 c. sugar; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1/2 tsp. baking soda. Directions: Mix sugar, Crisco and egg together. Add vanilla, then add dry ingredients. Roll into balls (small). Flatten. Bake at 350° for 10 minutes. Makes about 50 cookies.

title: Ham And Lima Bean Soup
text: Ham And Lima Bean Soup. Ingredients: chopped ham; 1 can lima beans; 1 can tomatoes with green chilies; 1 can corn. Directions: Combine and cook on top of stove for 15 to 20 minutes. Quick and easy.

title: Recipe For A Happy Family
text: Recipe For A Happy Family. Ingredients: 1 Husband; 1 Wife; Children; 1 Bible for each; 1 Home; generous portions of Prayer; 3 c. Love, firmly packed; 1 pkg. Work; 1 pkg. Playing Together; 1 portion Patience; 1 portion Understanding; 1 small Paddle; 1 c. Kisses. Directions: Mix moderately and sprinkle with awareness. Bake in moderate oven of everyday life, using as fuel all grudges and past unpleasantness. Cool. Turn out onto platter of cheerfulness. Garnish with tears and laughter in large helpings. Serve God, country and community.

title: Sour Cream Coconut Cake
text: Sour Cream Coconut Cake. Ingredients: 2 c. sugar; 2 (8 oz.) carton sour cream; 2 pkg. frozen coconut; 1 (3-layer) cake, baked. Directions: Bake cake; split the 3 layers into 6 layers.

title: Pork Chops And Rice For Two
text: Pork Chops And Rice For Two. Ingredients: 2 center cut pork chops, at least 1/2-inch thick; 1 medium onion, sliced 1/4-inch thick; 1 (16 oz.) can tomatoes; 1/4 c. rice. Directions: Brown pork chops on both sides. Salt and pepper. Cover the chops generously with rice which has been partially cooked (about 5 minutes). Place onion slices on top of the rice, then top the rice with the tomatoes. Cover and cook slowly until chops are tender. The juice from the tomatoes may be poured around the chops and a little water added from time to time.

title: Chicken In Sour Cream
text: Chicken In Sour Cream. Ingredients: 6 to 8 boneless chicken breasts; 1 pkg. sour cream sauce mix; 1 can mushroom soup; enough milk to rinse out soup can. Directions: Put chicken in a baking dish. Mix other three ingredients and pour over chicken. Bake at 350° for 1/2 hour to 45 minutes.

title: Ranch Dressing Mix Plus
text: Ranch Dressing Mix Plus. Ingredients: 1 Cup Dried Parsley Flakes; 1/2 Cup Finely Crushed Saltines (about 15 crackers); 1/2 Cup Dried Minced Onion; 1/2 Cup Garlic Salt; 1/2 Cup Onion Salt; 1/4 Cup Garlic Powder; 1/4 Cup Onion Powder; 2 Tbsp. Dill Weed; 2 Cups Mayonnaise; 2 Cups Buttermilk; 1/4 Cup Chili Sauce; 2 Tbsp. Sweet Pickle Relish; 1 Medium Cucumber, peeled, seeded and pureed; 1 Tsp. Celery Seed. Directions: Combine first eight ingredients; mix well. Store in airtight container in a cool dry place.

title: Freezer Punch
text: Freezer Punch. Ingredients: 4 c. sugar; 5 bananas; 2 Tbsp. frozen lemonade concentrate; 2 small boxes frozen strawberries; 6 c. water; 1 c. orange juice; 1 can pineapple juice. Directions: In saucepan, mix together sugar and water. Bring to a boil. Boil 6 minutes. Cool. In blender, mix everything else, except strawberries. Mix until smooth. Will have to pour it back and forth as will not fit in one container. Pour both sugar mixture and blended mixture into 5-quart ice cream pail. Mix. Thaw strawberries and blend in with other ingredients. Place in freezer, stirring occasionally. Freeze for at least 24 hours. Remove from freezer 1 to 2 hours before serving. Mix with 7-Up, if desired.

title: New Year'S Bean Soup
text: New Year'S Bean Soup. Ingredients: 1 (1 lb.) bag mixed dried beans; 1 large onion; 1 large can (28 oz.) tomatoes; juice of a lemon; 1 clove garlic, chopped; 1 tsp. chili powder; salt and pepper to taste; ham bone or ham hocks. Directions: Soak beans overnight with 2 tablespoons salt. Drain next day, add 2 quarts water and ham bone. Simmer slowly for 3 hours and then add rest of ingredients and simmer another 30 minutes or more until beans are tender. Thicken if desired.

title: Vegetables In Cheese Sauce(Melts In Your Mouth.)
text: Vegetables In Cheese Sauce(Melts In Your Mouth.). Ingredients: 1 can Campbell's condensed cheese soup; 1/2 c. milk; 1/2 tsp. dried basil leaves, crushed; 1 clove garlic, minced; 2 c. cauliflower florets; 1 small onion, cut into thin wedges; 1 1/2 c. diagonally sliced carrots; 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen peas. Directions: In a 3-quart microwave-safe casserole dish, stir soup until smooth. Stir in milk, basil and garlic. Mix well. Add cauliflower, onion and peas. Stir to coat well. Cover with lid. Microwave on High 15 minutes or until vegetables are tender, stirring twice during cooking. Let stand, covered, 5 minutes. Makes about 5 1/2 cups.

title: Hot Cake And Sauce
text: Hot Cake And Sauce. Ingredients: 2 1/2 c. flour; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 3 level tsp. baking powder; pinch of salt; 1/2 c. shortening; 1 c. sweet milk; 2 eggs; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Mix ingredients and pour into greased 9 x 13-inch pan.

title: Quick Caramel Icing
text: Quick Caramel Icing. Ingredients: 1 c. light brown sugar, firmly packed; 1/8 tsp. salt; 2 Tbsp. margarine or butter; 1/3 c. cream or milk. Directions: Cook, stirring, until sugar is dissolved, then cool, without stirring, until mixture forms a soft ball. (Drop small amount in cold water to check.) Cool to lukewarm.

title: Whole Wheat Light Rolls
text: Whole Wheat Light Rolls. Ingredients: 2 pkg. yeast; 1/2 c. warm water; 1 3/4 c. scalded milk; 1/2 c. sugar; 1 tsp. salt; 3 Tbsp. shortening; 3 to 4 c. whole wheat flour; 3 c. white flour; 2 eggs, beaten. Directions: Combine milk, sugar, salt and shortening. Cool to lukewarm, then add eggs. Add 1 cup of each of the flours and mix well. Soften yeast in warm water and add to first mixture. Stir in remaining flour and put in greased bowl in refrigerator. Cover with greased wax paper. Two hours before baking remove from refrigerator and knead lightly. Shape into rolls and let raise. Bake at 425° about 10 minutes or until brown.

title: Cherry Stuff
text: Cherry Stuff. Ingredients: 1 pkg. crushed graham crackers; 1/4 c. sugar; 1/3 c. softened margarine; 1 pkg. Dream Whip, prepared as directed; 1 (8 oz.) softened cream cheese; 1 c. confectioners sugar; large can cherry pie filling. Directions: Combine cracker crumbs and sugar, and add margarine. Pat into a jelly roll pan and bake at 375° for 8 minutes; cool. Mix Dream Whip, cream cheese and confectioners sugar until smooth. Spread over cool crust. Spoon cherries over top; keep chilled.

title: "Souperior" Meat Loaf
text: "Souperior" Meat Loaf. Ingredients: 1 envelope Lipton beefy onion soup mix; 2 lb. ground chuck; 1 1/2 c. fresh bread crumbs; 2 eggs; 3/4 c. water; 1/3 c. catsup. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. In a large bowl, combine all ingredients. Shape into loaf and place in baking dish. Bake 1 hour or until done.

title: Corn Pudding(Vegetables)
text: Corn Pudding(Vegetables). Ingredients: 1 (24 oz.) pkg. frozen corn; 8 oz. cream cheese; 1 stick margarine. Directions: Melt margarine and cream cheese together. Stir in corn. Simmer on low for 45 minutes to 1 hour. Stir frequently to avoid sticking.

title: Leek-Spinach Dip
text: Leek-Spinach Dip. Ingredients: 1 container sour cream; 1 pkg. frozen chopped spinach; 1 box Knorr leek soup mix. Directions: Cook spinach as directed on package. Squeeze out excess water. Mix with leek soup and sour cream. Put in center of rye bread with bread cubes for dipping.

title: Potato Pie
text: Potato Pie.
