Ingredients: 1 (10-inch) unbaked pastry shell; 1 lb. cottage cheese; 2 c. mashed potatoes (instant works fine); 1/2 c. sour cream; 2 eggs; 2 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. cayenne; 1/2 c. scallions, sliced; 3 Tbsp. grated Parmesan cheese. Directions: Put the cottage cheese through a food mill to make it smooth. Beat the mashed potatoes into the cottage cheese. Beat in sour cream, eggs, salt and cayenne. Stir in scallions. Spoon into pastry shell. Sprinkle with grated cheese. Bake at 425° for 50 minutes or until golden brown.

title: Rocky Roads
text: Rocky Roads. Ingredients: 1 (12 oz.) pkg. semi-sweet chocolate bits; 1 c. chunky peanut butter; 3 c. miniature marshmallows; 3/4 c. raisins. Directions: Combine chocolate bits and peanut butter. Heat in microwave for 3 or 4 minutes, until soft and melted. Add raisins which have been dipped in boiling water to soften. (Omit this step if raisins are fresh.) Stir in marshmallows. Turn into an 8 x 8-inch buttered pan. Cut into pieces when cold. Store in an airtight container in freezer until ready to use.

title: Custard Pie(Makes Its Own Crust)
text: Custard Pie(Makes Its Own Crust). Ingredients: 4 eggs; 1/2 stick margarine; 1/2 c. Bisquick; 1/3 c. sugar; 2 c. milk. Directions: Put all ingredients in blender and blend 4 to 5 minutes. You could add raisins or nuts or coconut if you like. Pour into a greased pie plate and bake for 45 minutes or until knife comes out clean. Sprinkle nutmeg on top of pie. Enjoy!

title: Royal Chocolate Sauce
text: Royal Chocolate Sauce. Ingredients: 1 Tbsp. cornstarch; 3 Tbsp. cocoa; 1/2 c. butter or margarine; 1/2 c. honey; 1/2 c. water; 3/4 c. marshmallow creme; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Combine cornstarch, cocoa, margarine, honey and water in a heavy saucepan, bring to a boil, stirring constantly. Remove from heat; add marshmallow creme and vanilla. Stir until blended. Serve warm over ice cream or as a dip for fresh fruit and cake cubes. Makes 2 cups.

title: Rolled Chicken Breasts
text: Rolled Chicken Breasts. Ingredients: boned chicken breasts (2 per person); 1 jar dried beef (2 pieces per chicken breast); bacon, 1/2 piece per chicken breast; 2 cans cream of mushroom soup; 1 (16 oz.) carton sour cream. Directions: Roll each chicken breast. Place 2 pieces of dried beef around each rolled chicken breast. Wrap 1/2 piece uncooked bacon around each rolled chicken breast. Place chicken rolls in baking dish. Mix mushroom soup with sour cream and pour over chicken rolls. No salt or seasoning needed. Bake, uncovered, for 3 hours at 250°.

title: Frozen Bananas
text: Frozen Bananas. Ingredients: 1 (6 oz.) pkg. semi-sweet chocolate chips (Nestle); 6 ripe bananas. Directions: You will also need 6 wooden sticks.

title: "New Moon" Tea Cakes
text: "New Moon" Tea Cakes. Ingredients: 1/2 lb. butter; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 6 egg yolks; 1 1/2 c. sifted flour; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 1/2 tsp. baking powder; 7 egg whites; 1/2 c. ground walnuts; 2 Tbsp. powdered sugar; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Cream the butter and sugar well. Add egg yolks and beat well, about 5 minutes. Sift flour, salt and baking powder together and add in 3 parts to creamed mixture, blending it well. Add vanilla. Clean beaters and beat egg whites in another bowl until stiff, but not dry. Pour on top of the batter and with spatula or rubber scraper, fold in gently. Grease a 9 x 13-inch pan. Dust with flour. Pour in batter and spread to corners. Sprinkle with walnuts and bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes. Lightly dust with powdered sugar while warm. Cool and cut with a serrated round cookie cutter so you will have half moon shaped little cakes.

title: Chicken Noodle Soup
text: Chicken Noodle Soup. Ingredients: 4 lb. chicken, cut up (stewing chicken); 3 qt. water; 2 cans (14 oz.) chicken broth; 5 celery ribs, chopped and divided; 4 carrots, chopped and divided; 2 medium onions, chopped and divided; 2/3 c. coarsely chopped green pepper, divided; 1 1/4 tsp. pepper; 1 bay leaf; 2 tsp. salt; 8 oz. medium noodles. Directions: Combine chicken, water, broth, half of the celery, carrots, onions, green pepper, pepper and bay leaf; bring to a boil. Simmer 2 1/2 hours or until chicken is tender. Remove chicken. When cool enough to handle, remove meat from bones and cut up. Set chicken aside. Strain broth and skim fat. Return to kettle. Add salt and remaining ingredients. Simmer 10 to 12 minutes. Add noodles and chicken. Simmer 12 to 15 minutes.

title: Delicious Fruit Salad
text: Delicious Fruit Salad. Ingredients: 1 large can chunk pineapple; 1 large can mandarin oranges; 1 box instant lemon pudding; 1/2 c. maraschino cherries; 1 large can fruit cocktail; 1 box instant vanilla pudding; 4 to 5 bananas. Directions: Drain fruit. Save juice from the pineapple and fruit cocktail. Mix juice with instant pudding. Stir until thick. Pour over fruit. Add sliced bananas and cherries. Chill.

title: Facelift Casserole(Breakfast Casserole)
text: Facelift Casserole(Breakfast Casserole). Ingredients: 2/3 lb. sausage; 6 eggs; 2 c. milk; 1 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. dry mustard; 2 slices white bread, cubed; 1 c. grated Cheddar cheese. Directions: Brown and drain sausage and set aside. Beat eggs and milk, salt and mustard. Gently stir in bread, cheese and sausage. Put in 8 x 12-inch buttered pan and refrigerate overnight. Cook at 350° for 45 minutes. Let stand 5 to 10 minutes before serving. Yields 6 servings.

title: Cornish Hens
text: Cornish Hens. Ingredients: 2 birds; 1/4 c. orange marmalade; 1/4 c. brown sugar; 3 or 4 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce. Directions: Salt and pepper insides of birds. Stuff with cooked brown rice. Baste with sauce every 15 minutes. Bake at 375° for 1 hour and 15 minutes.

title: Hardy'S Biscuits
text: Hardy'S Biscuits. Ingredients: 1 pkg. yeast; 1/2 c. warm water; 4 c. self-rising flour; 3/4 c. shortening; 1 1/2 c. buttermilk; 1 tsp. soda; 1 Tbsp. sugar. Directions: Dissolve yeast in warm water and set aside. Mix dry ingredients in a large bowl. Cut in shortening, then add buttermilk and yeast water. Mix well. Cover dough with towel and let set overnight. Next morning, pour dough out on floured board and knead lightly for a minute or two. Roll out and cut biscuits. Bake at 500° until done.

title: Sweeter Muffins
text: Sweeter Muffins. Ingredients: 1 egg; 1/3 c. milk; 1/4 c. shortening; 1 1/2 c. flour; 1/2 c. sugar; 2 tsp. baking powder; 1/2 tsp. salt (if using plain flour). Directions: Grease muffin pan. In mixing bowl, beat egg slightly. Stir in milk and shortening. Add dry ingredients and mix well. Fill muffin cups about 2/3 full. Bake at 400° for 20 to 25 minutes. You can fill cups 1/3 full and put in 1/2 teaspoon of favorite jelly. Then finish filling with batter.

title: Fat Man'S Misery
text: Fat Man'S Misery. Ingredients: approximately 25 plain chocolate wafers; 1 1/2 sticks butter; 1 1/2 c. powdered sugar; 1 egg; 1/4 to 1/2 tsp. bourbon; 3/4 pt. whipping cream, whipped; 3/4 Tbsp. sugar; 1 1/2 tsp. vanilla; 1 1/2 c. chopped pecans. Directions: Grease 1 1/2-quart rectangle glass dish. Line the bottom (not the sides) with chocolate wafer crumbs. Cream butter and confectioners sugar, adding the egg and bourbon. Spread this mixture on crumbs. Cover and set in the refrigerator for an hour. Whip cream with sugar and add vanilla. Fold in chopped pecans and spread this mixture on top of mixture. Cover with more crushed wafers. Let stand in the refrigerator, covered, for overnight. Cut in 8 pieces.

title: Pasta And Ricotta Stuffed Red Peppers
text: Pasta And Ricotta Stuffed Red Peppers. Ingredients: 1 onion; 2 cloves garlic; 4 oz. Mozzarella cheese (about 1 c. grated); 2 oz. Parmesan cheese (about 1/2 c. grated); 4 red peppers; 4 oz. ditalini or small pasta; 1 Tbsp. olive oil; 1 c. Ricotta cheese; 1 egg; salt and pepper. Directions: Chop the onion. Mince the garlic. Grate the Mozzarella and Parmesan. Cut tops off peppers. Remove the seeds and ribs. Cook peppers in a pot of boiling salted water just until soft, about 30 minutes. Heat oven to 350°.

title: English Muffin Pizza
text: English Muffin Pizza. Ingredients: 2 English Muffins, halved; 20 slices of Pepperoni; 1 c. Shredded Monterey Jack Cheese; 2 tsp. Italian Salad Dressing; 1 medium Ripe Tomato, sliced in four; Soft Butter or Margarine. Directions: Toast each english muffin half. Lightly spread with butter. Place two paper towels atop two coupled paper plates. Separate pepperoni and place atop paper towels. Microwave on High for 20-30 seconds (do not blacken). Remove from microwave and dab off excess oil with paper towel. Spread 1/2 tsp. salad dressing on each muffin half. Place 1 slice tomato and 5 slices of pepperoni on each muffin half. Top each with 1/4 c. of cheese. Place muffins on non stick cookie sheet. Broil on high for 5 minutes or until browned (no more than 8 minutes). Cool 1 minute. Serve.

title: Watercress Salad
text: Watercress Salad. Ingredients: 2 bunches watercress; 1 Tbsp. Dijon mustard; 2 Tbsp. red wine vinegar; salt and pepper to taste; 4 Tbsp. oil; 1 hard-boiled egg, peeled and chopped; 2 Tbsp. chopped onion. Directions: Trim off the tough ends of the watercress and discard. Rinse the leaves and tender stems. Drain well. Put mustard, vinegar, salt and pepper in salad bowl. Blend with wire whisk. Add oil, beating with whisk. Add watercress, chopped egg and onion. Toss and serve.

title: Lemon-Lime Jello Salad
text: Lemon-Lime Jello Salad. Ingredients: 1 (3 oz.) pkg. lemon Jell-O; 1 (3 oz.) pkg. lime Jell-O; 2 c. boiling water; 1 (No. 2) can crushed pineapple, drained; 1 can sweetened condensed milk; 1/2 c. salad dressing; 1 lb. cottage cheese; 1/2 c. chopped pecans. Directions: Mix both packages of Jell-O in the hot water. Let cool. Add pineapple, milk, salad dressing, cheese and pecans. Pour into a greased 9 x 13-inch pan. Refrigerate until congealed. Cut in squares. If desired, top with fresh mint leaf and red and green cherries. Serve on lettuce leaf.

title: Nine Layer Vegetable Salad
text: Nine Layer Vegetable Salad. Ingredients: 1/2 head lettuce, chopped; 1 c. chopped celery; 1 c. chopped bell pepper; 1 c. chopped onion; 1 (17 oz.) can small English peas, drained; 1 1/2 c. mayonnaise; 1 tsp. sugar; grated Parmesan cheese; Bac*Os bits; 1 c. dried mushrooms, sliced lengthwise; 2 eggs, boiled and sliced; croutons. Directions: Layer vegetables in the order listed in a 10 x 10 x 2-inch casserole dish. Spread mayonnaise evenly over top. Sprinkle sugar, then Parmesan cheese, then Bac*Os bits over mayonnaise. Layer mushrooms evenly over top and then sliced eggs. Add croutons on top.

title: Italian Style Swiss Steak(Makes 4 Servings)
text: Italian Style Swiss Steak(Makes 4 Servings). Ingredients: 15 oz. lean boneless top round of beef, cut into 4 equal pieces; 1 Tbsp. plus 1 tsp. vegetable oil; 2 c. sliced mushrooms; 1/2 c. sliced onion; 1/2 c. sliced carrot; 1/4 c. sliced celery; 1 c. tomato sauce; 1/4 c. dry red wine; 1/2 tsp. dried oregano; 1/2 tsp. garlic powder; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. freshly ground black pepper; 1 c. sliced green bell pepper; 10 oz. sliced all-purpose potatoes. Directions: Preheat broiler. Broil beef on rack 4-inches from heat 2 minutes on each side. Set aside. In large nonstick skillet, heat oil. Add mushrooms, onions, carrot and celery; cook over medium heat until onions are translucent, about 2 minutes. Stir in tomato sauce, wine, oregano, garlic powder, pepper, salt and 1 cup water. Return meat to skillet. Cover and simmer over low heat 1 hour. Add green pepper and potatoes. Simmer until meat is tender, about 35 minutes.

title: Cranberry-Apple Jello Salad
text: Cranberry-Apple Jello Salad. Ingredients: 1 (3 oz.) pkg. lemon Jell-O; 1 c. boiling water; 1 c. water, cranberry or apple juice; 1 c. ground cranberries; 1 c. ground apples; 1 c. sugar; 1/2 c. walnut pieces. Directions: Grind cranberries and apples and mix with sugar. Dissolve Jell-O in boiling water or juice. Add cold water and refrigerate until Jell-O thickens, then add fruit mix.

title: Chocolate Cookie Sheet Cake
text: Chocolate Cookie Sheet Cake. Ingredients: 1/2 cup milk; 3 tablespoons cocoa; 4 tablespoons oleo. Directions: Remove from heat. Add 1 box powdered sugar. Beat. Add 1/2 cup chopped nuts and 2 teaspoons vanilla. Spread on cake as soon as it is taken from the oven.

title: Pizza Dough And Breadsticks
text: Pizza Dough And Breadsticks. Ingredients: 4 c. flour (all-purpose or bread); 1 cake (1 Tbsp.) yeast in 1 1/4 c. 85~ water; 2 Tbsp. vegetable oil or olive oil; 1 tsp. salt; 1 Tbsp. sugar. Directions: Combine yeast, water, salt and sugar. Add to flour and combine thoroughly. Knead 8 to 10 minutes. Cover and let rise 1 to 2 hours; covered with damp cloth. Punch dough down; divide in two equal parts. Grease two 15 x 9 x 1-inch pans with vegetable oil and lightly sprinkle cornmeal on pan. Pat out dough in pan or roll out and place in pan.

title: Veggie Beef Soup
text: Veggie Beef Soup. Ingredients: 2 lb. hamburger; 2 c. water; 1 can beef broth; 1 (12 oz.) can tomatoes (undrained), chopped; 1 envelope dry onion soup mix; 1 bay leaf; 1 c. sliced carrots; 1/4 c. chopped celery; 1/4 c. chopped parsley; 1/4 tsp. black pepper; 1/4 tsp. oregano leaves; 1/4 tsp. basil leaves. Directions: Brown hamburger; season with pepper, seasoning salt and garlic powder to taste. Set meat aside. Combine ingredients in a 5-quart stockpot. Bring to boiling. Reduce heat; cover and simmer for 20 minutes. Add meat and simmer for 20 minutes longer. Remove bay leaf.

title: Homemade Cocoa
text: Homemade Cocoa. Ingredients: 4 c. powdered sugar; 1 lb. box powdered milk (Carnation is best); 11 oz. Coffee-mate; 2 lb. box Nestle Quik plus 1 small can. Directions: Mix ingredients together slowly a little at a time. Use lemonade scoop for 1 cup cocoa. Add hot water. Keep in airtight container. Makes a large amount of cocoa.

title: Chocolate Cake And Icing
text: Chocolate Cake And Icing. Ingredients: 2 c. flour; 2 c. sugar; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. soda; 1 stick butter or margarine; 1 c. water; 1/2 c. shortening; 3 Tbsp. cocoa; 2 eggs, lightly beaten; 1/2 c. buttermilk; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Combine first 4 ingredients. Bring butter, water, shortening and cocoa to boil. Pour over flour mixture and mix well. Add eggs, buttermilk and vanilla. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes in a 13 x 9-inch pan.

title: Snack Bars
text: Snack Bars. Ingredients: 9 c. Rice Chex, crushed; 6 c. quick-cooking oats; 1 c. graham cracker crumbs; 1 c. flaked coconut; 1/2 c. wheat germ; 2 bags (one 16 oz., one 10 oz.) large marshmallows; 1 c. butter or margarine; 1/2 c. honey; 1 1/2 c. chocolate chips, mini M&M's or raisins (optional). Directions: In a very large bowl, combine the first five ingredients. In a saucepan over low heat, cook and stir marshmallows and butter until marshmallows are melted. Add honey and mix well. Pour over cereal mixture; mix well. Add chips, M&M's or raisins if desired. Pat two-thirds into a greased 15 x 10 x 1-inch pan and the remaining third into a 9-inch square pan. Cool. Yield: 4 to 5 dozen.

title: Chalupas
text: Chalupas. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground meat; cheese, grated; lettuce; tomatoes; 1 pkg. corn tortillas; 1 can refried beans; Crisco. Directions: Brown meat until done. Fry corn tortillas in Crisco. Spread beans and meat on tortillas. Add lettuce, tomatoes and cheese. Top with avocado dip or sour cream.

title: Wednesday'S Chili
text: Wednesday'S Chili. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. oil; 1 (10 oz.) can French (not creamy) onion soup; 2 lb. ground round; 1 Tbsp. chili powder; 2 tsp. ground cumin; 1/2 tsp. black pepper; few drops Tabasco sauce; 1 (21 oz.) can red kidney beans (undrained); 1 (8 oz.) can tomato sauce; 6 oz. tomato paste. Directions: In a medium skillet, heat oil and brown meat over medium-high heat until color disappears. Set aside.

title: Danish Sweet Rolls
text: Danish Sweet Rolls. Ingredients: 3 c. flour; 1/2 c. sugar; 1 c. Crisco or margarine; 1/2 tsp. salt; 2 beaten eggs; 3/4 c. milk, scalded and cooled; 1 pkg. yeast, dissolved in 1/4 c. water; 1 additional c. flour. Directions: Mix the first 4 ingredients like pie crust. Add the eggs, milk and yeast mixture. Beat. Add 1 cup flour. Dough will be soft. Let set in refrigerator for several hours. Take out. Cut into 4 parts and roll out each part (like pie crust). Spread with butter. Cut into triangular wedges. Roll each wedge from wide side to point. Let rise and bake 20 to 25 minutes at 350°. Frost and sprinkle with nuts.

title: Zucchini Carrot Bread
text: Zucchini Carrot Bread. Ingredients: 3 eggs; 1 c. vegetable oil; 1 c. grated zucchini; 1 c. grated carrots; 2 tsp. vanilla; 2 1/2 c. whole wheat flour; 1/2 c. All-Bran; 1 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. baking soda; 3 tsp. ground cinnamon; 1 1/2 c. brown sugar; 1 c. chopped nuts. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Grease and flour 9 x 5 x 3-inch loaf pan or 2 1/2 x 3 1/2 x 2-inch loaf pan.

title: Nut Roll
text: Nut Roll. Ingredients: 1 lb. box graham crackers; 1 box raisins; 1 lb. marshmallows; 1 large can milk; 1 lb. pecans; 1 bottle red cherries. Directions: Crush crackers, marshmallows and chip up cherries and nuts. Add pecans and mix. Add milk and mix well. Roll in cracker crumbs. Roll into balls and put in refrigerator in tinfoil.

title: Pumpkin Chiffon Cake
text: Pumpkin Chiffon Cake. Ingredients: 2 c. flour, sifted; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 3 tsp. baking powder; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. cloves; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. nutmeg; 7 large eggs, separated; 3/4 c. canned pumpkin; 1/2 c. oil; 1/2 c. water; 1/2 tsp. cream of tartar. Directions: Sift dry ingredients into bowl. Separate eggs and egg whites in large mixing bowl. In small bowl, blend egg yolks, pumpkin, oil and water. Make well in center of dry ingredients and add liquid mixture all at once, beat at medium speed for 1 minute. Beat egg whites until foamy. Add cream of tartar and beat until stiff. Pour pumpkin batter gradually over beaten egg whites and fold in until blended. Pour into greased tube pan and bake at 325° for 55 minutes, 15 to 20 minutes in 350° oven. Cool. Frost is desired.

title: Microwave Recipe For Bacon Stuffed Patties With Mushrooms And
text: Microwave Recipe For Bacon Stuffed Patties With Mushrooms And. Ingredients: 6 slices bacon; 1 lb. lean ground beef; 2 Tbsp. bread crumbs; 1 Tbsp. parsley flakes; 1 1/2 tsp. salt, divided; 1/8 tsp. garlic powder; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 8 oz. sliced fresh mushrooms; 1 medium onion, thinly sliced and separated into rings; 1 tsp. Worcestershire sauce. Directions: Place bacon on several layers of paper towels. Cover with paper towel. Microwave at High 4 to 6 minutes. Set aside. Preheat browning dish 5 minutes. Combine ground beef, bread crumbs, parsley flakes, 1 teaspoon salt, garlic powder and pepper. Shape into 8 patties, 1/4-inch thick. Crumble bacon. Place 1/4 of bacon on center of each of 4 patties. Top with remaining patties; press edges to seal. Place patties in preheated browning dish. Microwave at High 1 1/2 minutes. Turn over. Microwave 1 1/2 to 2 1/2 minutes, or until not quite desired doneness. Remove patties to serving platter. Cover and let stand while preparing vegetables. Add remaining ingredients to drippings. Microwave at High 2 to 4 minutes, or until vegetables are tender, stirring once. Spoon over patties. Microwave time: 15 to 19 minutes.

title: Yellow Rice Casserole
text: Yellow Rice Casserole. Ingredients: 1 (5 oz.) yellow saffron rice; 1 can Green Giant Mexicorn; 1 can cream of mushroom soup; 1 c. grated Cheddar cheese; 1 stick margarine. Directions: Cook rice following package directions. When done, stir in margarine and soup (do not add water to soup). In casserole dish, layer rice, corn and cheese. Repeat, ending with cheese on top. Bake in 350° oven for 25 to 30 minutes or in microwave on High for 7 minutes and allow to stand for 10 to 15 minutes before serving.

title: Chinese Country Ribs
text: Chinese Country Ribs. Ingredients: 3 to 4 lb. country style or spareribs; 1/4 c. soy sauce; 1/4 c. orange marmalade; 2 Tbsp. catsup; 1 clove crushed garlic. Directions: Combine soy sauce, marmalade, catsup and garlic. Brush both sides of ribs. Place in crock-pot. Pour in remaining sauce. Cover and cook on low, 8 to 10 hours. Makes 4 to 6 servings.

title: Cheese-Corn Bread
text: Cheese-Corn Bread. Ingredients: 2 c. flour; 1/4 c. sugar; 3 tsp. baking powder; 1 tsp. salt; 1 egg, slightly beaten; 1 c. milk; 1/2 c. shredded Cheddar cheese; 1 (7 oz.) whole kernel corn; 1/4 c. shortening, melted or salad oil. Directions: Heat oven to 400°. Grease square pan, 8 x 8 x 2-inch or 9 x 9 x 2-inches. Measure all ingredients in order listed into a bowl; stir just until flour is moistened. Batter will be lumpy. Spread evenly in pan. Bake 8-inch pan 45 minutes; 9-inch pan 35 minutes or until golden brown.

title: "Berry" Delicious Fruit Shortcake
text: "Berry" Delicious Fruit Shortcake. Ingredients: 2 c. flour; 2 tsp. baking powder; 1/2 tsp. salt; 3 Tbsp. sugar; 1 egg, beaten; 2/3 c. milk; 1/2 c. margarine, melted; 4 c. strawberries; 2 bananas; 1 c. crushed pineapple; 1/2 c. sugar or 8 packets Equal; 1 (8 oz.) container whipped topping; 1/2 c. sliced almonds or chopped nuts. Directions: Combine flour, baking powder, salt and sugar. Add combined milk, melted margarine and beaten egg, mixing until well moistened. Spread into greased and floured 8-inch cake pan. Bake at 450° for 12 to 15 minutes or until golden brown. Cool 10 minutes and remove from pan.

title: Beef Stock
text: Beef Stock. Ingredients: 5 lb. mixed beef and veal bones; 2 large carrots, scrubbed; 2 large ribs celery, cleaned; 2 large onions, cut in half; water; 2 Tbsp. tomato paste; 6 parsley sprigs; 1 tsp. leaf thyme; 18 peppercorns; 2 medium garlic cloves, crushed. Directions: Preheat oven to very hot, about 500°. Place bones in a single layer in a roasting pan. Break carrots and celery into large pieces; sprinkle among the bones, ditto with the onions. Roast for 30 to 45 minutes, until meat, bones and vegetables begin to brown, even turn a little. Dump these into a deep stock pot. Pour about a half inch of water into the roasting pan; dissolve any bits which have stuck to the bottom of the pan. Pour over bones. Add more water to several inches above bones. Bring to a boil; stir in tomato paste. Add parsley, thyme, peppercorns and garlic. Cook, barely simmering, for several hours, adding more hot water as necessary. Strain and boil slowly until reduced and flavor is intensified. Allow to cool; skim any fat from the top. Refrigerate, tightly covered, or freeze. Makes about 2 quarts.

title: Colorado Taco Salad
text: Colorado Taco Salad. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef, cooked and drained, cool; 1 small head lettuce, shredded; 3 to 4 tomatoes, cubed; 1 can light kidney beans; 1 onion, diced; 1/2 lb. shredded Cheddar cheese; 1 bag Doritos corn chips; 1 bottle Thousand Island dressing. Directions: Mix first 6 ingredients together and gently fold in Doritos corn chips. Gently toss with dressing. Garnish with extra cheese and tomatoes.

title: Sliced Tongue
text: Sliced Tongue. Ingredients: 2 qt. water; 1/2 Tbsp. butter; 1/2 onion; 1 lemon (juice); 1/2 Tbsp. sugar; 1/2 tsp. flour. Directions: Boil the tongue in water until tender. Remove from liquid, skin and cut into slices. Melt butter in a pan, add onion and fry over slow fire until yellow. Add the boiling liquid, season with salt, pepper, juice of lemon, sugar and flour mixed with a little water. Add the sliced tongue and boil about 15 to 20 minutes.

title: My Favorite Recipe
text: My Favorite Recipe. Ingredients: 1 c. love; 1 c. kindness; 1 c. appreciation; 3 c. pleasant companionship; 2 qt. deserving praise; 1 tsp. carefully chosen advice; 1 c. cheerfulness; pinch of sorrow. Directions: Blend love and kindness. Alternately add, in small portions, appreciation and pleasant companionship sifted with deserving praise. Flavor with carefully chosen advice. Lightly fold in cheerfulness and sorrow. Pour with tender care into clean hearts. Let bake until well matured; turn out on the surface of society. Humbly ask God's blessings and it will serve all mankind.

title: Refrigerator White Rolls
text: Refrigerator White Rolls. Ingredients: 1 pkg. active dry yeast; 1/4 c. warm water; 3/4 c. milk, scalded; 1/4 c. shortening; 1/4 c. sugar; 1 tsp. salt; 3 c. sifted all-purpose flour; 1 egg. Directions: Soften yeast in warm water. Combine milk, shortening, sugar and salt and add. Cool to lukewarm. Add 1 cup flour; beat well. Beat in yeast mixture and egg. Add remaining flour; beat well. Place in greased bowl; grease top and cover. Keep in refrigerator 2 hours, or until needed.

title: Hot Pineapple Casserole
text: Hot Pineapple Casserole. Ingredients: 2 cans chunk pineapple, drained; 5 Tbsp. flour; 1 c. sugar; 1 1/2 c. shredded cheese; 2 sleeves Ritz crackers, crushed; 1 stick margarine. Directions: Mix together flour and sugar; set aside. Grease casserole dish. Pour drained pineapple in dish. Add sugar and flour mixture to top of pineapple. Sprinkle cheese over previous layer, then the Ritz crackers. Slice margarine in small pieces and place on top. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

title: Jiffy Cinnamon Rolls
text: Jiffy Cinnamon Rolls. Ingredients: 4 to 5 1/2 c. all-purpose flour, divided; 1 (9 oz.) box one-layer white cake mix; 2 pkg. quick rise yeast; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. vanilla; 2 c. warm water; 1 egg, slightly beaten; 2 Tbsp. butter; 1/2 c. sugar; 1 Tbsp. cinnamon. Directions: In a large mixing bowl, combine 3 cups flour, cake mix, yeast, salt and warm water. Mix until smooth, add enough remaining flour to form a soft dough. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead until smooth, about 6 to 8 minutes. Roll dough into a 9 x 18-inch rectangle. Spread with butter and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon. Roll dough jelly-roll style, starting with the long end. Slice the dough into 1-inch circles, place on greased cookie sheet. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 15 minutes. Bake at 350° for 15 to 18 minutes. Frost, if desired. Makes about 18 rolls.

title: Special Chicken Breast
text: Special Chicken Breast. Ingredients: 1 (about 2 1/2 oz.) jar sliced beef, rinse and drain; 4 whole chicken breasts, split, skinned and deboned; 2 cans cream of mushroom soup; 1 c. sour cream; 6 slices bacon, cut in half. Directions: In shallow baking pan (13 x 9 x 2-inch), arrange single layer of beef. Top with chicken. Blend soup and sour cream. Pour over all. Top with bacon. Cover and bake 3 hours at 250°. Then bake uncovered for 1 additional hour.

title: Pecan Crunch
text: Pecan Crunch. Ingredients: 1 lb. butter (may use half margarine); 2 c. finely chopped pecans; 12 oz. semi-sweet chocolate chips; 2 c. sugar. Directions: Sprinkle buttered 10 x 15-inch pan with 1 cup nuts and half the chocolate chips. In heavy saucepan, melt butter and sugar. Using a candy thermometer, heat mixture to 300°. (This takes approximately 15 minutes.) Pour this over mixture in pan. Sprinkle with remaining chocolate chips and let set until chocolate is soft. Spread evenly over candy and top with remaining pecans. Serves 24.

title: Jingle Bell Fudge
text: Jingle Bell Fudge. Ingredients: 12 oz. butterscotch chips; 1/2 c. chunky peanut butter; 2/3 c. sweetened condensed milk; 1/2 c. walnuts or pecans, chopped. Directions: Combine butterscotch chips and peanut butter in top of double boiler. Place over hot, not boiling water, until butterscotch melts; remove from water. Stir until blended; add milk and stir until blended. Spread in foil-lined 8 inch square pan. Press chopped nuts into surface, if desired, and chill until firm. Cut into 1-inch squares. Yield: 64 servings.

title: Layer Salad
text: Layer Salad. Ingredients: 1 head chopped lettuce (dry well); 1/2 c. chopped celery; 1/2 c. chopped bell peppers; 1 onion; 1 pkg. or can English peas, uncooked; 2 c. mayonnaise; 2 Tbsp. sugar; grated cheese; 8 strips bacon, cooked and crumbled. Directions: Layer first 5 ingredients in large dish. Cover with mayonnaise; sprinkle with sugar and grated cheese. Cook bacon and crumble over mixture. Cover dish tightly with foil and refrigerate overnight.

title: Goup
text: Goup. Ingredients: Minute rice; sliced raw potatoes; lean ground beef; salt and pepper; onions; 1 can tomato soup; 1/2 can water; parsley. Directions: For first layer, place a thin layer of Minute rice. Then a thin layer of sliced raw potatoes, then a thin layer of lean ground beef, salt and pepper. Then put layer of onions. Then spread tomato soup with water over layers. Repeat layers approximately 3 times. Sprinkle with parsley. Bake, covered, at 350° for 2 1/2 to 3 hours.

title: Southern Fried Chicken
text: Southern Fried Chicken. Ingredients: 1 (2 1/2 to 3 lb.) chicken, cut up; 2 tsp. salt; 1 1/2 tsp. black pepper; 1 egg plus 2 Tbsp. milk or water, beaten; 1 1/2 tsp. red pepper; 1 1/2 tsp. paprika; 1 1/2 c. flour; 2 c. Crisco oil. Directions: Wash chicken; drain on paper towel. Season chicken with salt and pepper. Dip in egg mixture. Add spices to flour; coat chicken. Fry slowly in hot oil until browned on both sides (about 35 minutes), turning as needed.

title: Chicken And Filling
text: Chicken And Filling. Ingredients: 1 chicken, cooked or baked and deboned; 8 to 10 c. bread cubes; 2 medium onions, chopped; 1/2 c. grated carrots (optional); 1/2 c. diced celery (optional); 1 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. pepper; 2 tsp. paprika; 1 c. broth. Directions: Mix chicken with remaining ingredients. Put in roasting pan or baking dish. Bake at 300° for 1 hour. Save rest of broth for gravy to serve on top. Very simple and good for company or home use.

title: Chicken Tostada Salad
text: Chicken Tostada Salad. Ingredients: 1/4 c. cider vinegar; 3 Tbsp. honey; 1 1/2 tsp. cumin; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 2 chicken breasts, cooked; 1/2 tsp. garlic salt; 1 lb. frozen corn; 1 c. seeded, chopped tomatoes; 1 (15 oz.) can black beans, rinsed; 5 sliced green onions; 1 head leafy green lettuce; 1 1/2 c. shredded low-fat Monterey Jack cheese; 3 c. broken-up baked Tostitos; 1 c. salsa; 1 c. nonfat sour cream. Directions: In small jar, mix vinegar, honey, cumin, salt and pepper. Shake well. Slice chicken into 1/2-inch strips and toss with garlic salt. Cook and drain corn. Add to chicken. Stir, cover and chill 1/2 hour. Add tomatoes, beans, onions and lettuce. Toss. Pour dressing over salad and toss. At serving time, add cheese and Tostitos and toss gently. Garnish with salsa and sour cream.

title: Crazy Crust Pie
text: Crazy Crust Pie. Ingredients: pie filling (any kind); 1 c. flour (self-rising); 1 c. sugar; 1 c. milk; 1 c. butter. Directions: Melt butter in baking dish. Mix flour, sugar and milk together. Pour into baking dish. Add pie filling. Do not stir. Bake at 350° for one hour or until golden brown.

title: Mucho Nacho Salad
text: Mucho Nacho Salad. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; 4 tomatoes; 8 oz. shredded cheese; 1 pt. medium hot salsa; 1 large bag nacho cheese chips; 1 head lettuce; 1 medium onion; 8 oz. creamy Ranch dressing. Directions: Brown ground beef and cool. Chop lettuce, tomatoes and onion. Mix, then add shredded Cheddar cheese and creamy Ranch dressing. Sprinkle more cheese on top. Serve with nacho chips. May be crushed and added to each individual salad or served on the side. Use salsa to suit your taste. This makes a very large main salad.

title: Seafood Pizza Spread
text: Seafood Pizza Spread. Ingredients: 1 can or 1/2 lb. crabmeat; 1 cup small boiled shrimp; 1 8-oz. cream cheese; 1 Tbsp. lemon juice; 2 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 2 Tbsp. mayonnaise; 1 Tbsp. minced onion; garlic powder to taste; 1 cup cocktail sauce. Directions: Drain crabmeat (if using canned). Blend cream cheese, lemon juice, Worcestershire sauce, mayonnaise, onion and garlic powder. Spread on a round serving plate. Spread cocktail sauce over cream cheese. Sprinkle shredded crabmeat and shrimp on top. Refrigerate several hours before serving. Serve with crackers.

title: African Chop Suey
text: African Chop Suey. Ingredients: 2 lb. chop suey meat; 2 small onions, chopped; 1 c. celery, chopped; 1 can water; 1 can mushrooms (optional); 3 Tbsp. molasses; 1/2 c. raw rice; salt and pepper; 1 can cream of chicken soup; 1 can cream of mushroom soup. Directions: Brown meat; add onions, celery, soups, water, mushrooms, molasses, rice, salt and pepper. Bake 2 hours, uncovered, at 325°.

title: Best Ever Pie Crust
text: Best Ever Pie Crust. Ingredients: 2 c. shortening; 4 c. flour; 1 c. milk; 2 tsp. salt. Directions: Combine all ingredients in bowl. Mix well. Dough will be soft. Store tightly covered in refrigerator overnight or up to 2 weeks.

title: Strawberry Parfait
text: Strawberry Parfait. Ingredients: 2 pkg. Dream Whip, whipped; 1 large box strawberry Jell-O; 2 c. boiling water; 1 lb. pkg. frozen strawberries; 1 c. marshmallows; 1 small angel food cake. Directions: Mix water with Jell-O. Stir in frozen strawberries until thawed. Cut cake in small pieces; spread in large pan. Fold whipped cream, marshmallows and strawberry mixture together. Pour over cake. Sit in refrigerator for two hours.

title: Baker'S Dough Clay
text: Baker'S Dough Clay. Ingredients: 1 c. flour; 1 c. salt; water. Directions: Mix 1 cup of flour with 1 cup of salt in a large mixing bowl. Add water, a little at a time, until it feels like clay. You can also add food coloring to the dough. After you have formed the clay, bake it in the oven at 200°. Check it every 5 minutes until it is hard. You can paint it if you have not colored it. Enjoy!

title: Citrus Punch
text: Citrus Punch. Ingredients: 2 (6 oz.) cans frozen grapefruit juice concentrate, thawed; 2 (6 oz.) cans frozen orange juice concentrate, thawed; 2 (6 oz.) cans frozen limeade concentrate, thawed; ice cubes or fruited ice ring; 2 liters (33.8 oz.) club soda or sparkling water, chilled; 2 med. seedless oranges, thinly sliced; 6 - 8 strawberries, sliced. Directions: In a pitcher, combine first 3 ingredients. Chill until ready to serve. Pour citrus mixture into small punch bowl; add ice cubes (or ice ring), as desired. Add club soda or sparkling water. Just before serving, garnish with orange slices and strawberries.

title: Molded Spring Salad
text: Molded Spring Salad. Ingredients: 1 pkg. lime jello; 1 c. boiling water; 1 c. Hellmann's salad dressing; 1 c. small curd cottage cheese; 1/8 tsp. salt; 1 c. cooked peas; 1/2 c. chopped green peppers; 1 Tbsp. minced onion; thin sliced radishes. Directions: Dissolve jello in boiling water. Beat in salad dressing with mixer. Stir in cottage cheese and add salt. Chill until thick and syrupy. Fold in peas, radishes, green peppers and onion. Pour into lightly oiled mold. Chill until set.

title: Shrimp Dip
text: Shrimp Dip. Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese; 1 c. mayonnaise; 2 (4 1/2 oz.) cans drained tiny, small or broken shrimp; 1/2 c. diced celery; 3 Tbsp. minced onion. Directions: Mix all ingredients together; serve with crackers.

title: English Walnut Pie
text: English Walnut Pie. Ingredients: 3 eggs; 1 c. light corn syrup; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 carton whipped cream; 1/2 c. brown sugar; 1/4 c. butter, melted; 1 c. broken walnuts; 9-inch unbaked pie shell. Directions: Mix all ingredients, except whipped cream, and pour into pie shell. Bake at 375° for 50 to 55 minutes, until top is crackly brown and filling just quivers when you nudge the pie plate. Cool and garnish with whipped cream and nuts.

title: Yogurt-Grilled Chicken
text: Yogurt-Grilled Chicken. Ingredients: 1 lb. chicken breasts (boneless and skinless); 2 c. plain yogurt; 4 cloves minced garlic; 3 Tbsp. lime juice; 1 tsp. Tabasco sauce; 1/4 c. dry white wine; 1/4 c. sour cream. Directions: Mix all ingredients in a 1-gallon Ziploc bag. Marinate in bag several hours or overnight in the refrigerator. Lift from marinade and grill over charcoal grill. (Based on Iranian cooking with yogurt!)

title: Chili Cheese Quiche(Appetizer)
text: Chili Cheese Quiche(Appetizer). Ingredients: 2 (4 oz.) cans green chilies, chopped; 8 eggs; 1/2 c. flour; 1 tsp. baking powder; 3/4 tsp. salt; 3 c. Jack cheese, shredded; 1 1/2 c. cottage cheese. Directions: Beat eggs 4 to 5 minutes. Stir flour, salt and baking powder together. Add to eggs and mix well. Fold in Jack cheese, cottage cheese and chilies. Pour into greased 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan and bake in 350° oven for 40 minutes. Let stand 10 minutes before cutting. Can be served warm or cold.

title: Creamy Baked Cheese Cake
text: Creamy Baked Cheese Cake. Ingredients: 1 1/4 c. graham cracker crumbs; 1/4 c. sugar; 1/3 c. margarine, melted; 2 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese, softened; 1 (14 oz.) can Eagle Brand sweetened milk (not evaporated milk); 3 eggs; 1/4 c. ReaLemon from concentrate; 1 (8 oz.) container sour cream; 1 (21 oz.) can cherry pie filling, chilled. Directions: Preheat oven to 300°. Combine crumbs, sugar and margarine. Press on bottom of 9-inch spring-form pan. In mixer bowl beat cream cheese until fluffy. Gradually beat in sweetened condensed milk until smooth. Add eggs and ReaLemon. Mix well. Pour into prepared pan. Bake 50 to 55 minutes or until center is set. Top with sour cream. Bake 5 minutes longer. Cool. Cover top with pie filling. Chill. Refrigerate leftovers. Makes one 9-inch cheese cake.

title: Barbecued Cocktail Franks
text: Barbecued Cocktail Franks. Ingredients: 1 lb. hot dogs or cocktail franks; 2 c. barbecue sauce; 3/4 c. Bick's savory tomato relish. Directions: Cut each hot dog into 4 pieces. Combine barbecue sauce, relish and hot dogs in saucepan. Cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally, just until heated through. Serve hot with cocktail picks. Makes about 48 appetizers.

title: Baked Cranberry Sauce
text: Baked Cranberry Sauce. Ingredients: 4 c. fresh cranberries; 2 c. sugar; 1/2 tsp. ground cinnamon; 1 (13 oz.) jar orange marmalade; 3 Tbsp. lemon juice; 1 c. coarsely chopped walnuts, toasted. Directions: Wash and drain cranberries. In large bowl, combine sugar and cinnamon. Add cranberries and mix well. Spoon into a 9-inch square pan and cover with foil. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes. Add orange marmalade, lemon juice and walnuts to hot cranberry mixture, stirring well. Store in refrigerator.

title: Cream Puffs(Yield: 6 Medium Puffs)
text: Cream Puffs(Yield: 6 Medium Puffs). Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 1/2 c. boiling water; 1/2 c. flour; 1/8 tsp. salt; 2 eggs. Directions: In saucepan, melt butter in boiling water. Add flour and salt all at once; stir vigorously. Cook and stir until mixture forms a ball that doesn't separate. Remove from heat; cool slightly and add eggs, one at a time, beating with a spoon after each addition until smooth. Drop by heaping tablespoonfuls 3 inches apart on lightly greased baking sheet. Bake at 450° for 15 minutes. Reduce heat to 325° and bake 10 minutes. Remove from oven; cut off tops. Turn oven off; return cream puffs to oven to dry 20 minutes. Cool on rack.

title: Easy Five Can Casserole
text: Easy Five Can Casserole. Ingredients: 1 can chicken noodle soup; 1 can cream of mushroom soup; 1 can evaporated milk; 2 c. boned chicken; 1 medium sized can chow mein noodles. Directions: Combine all ingredients, mixing lightly. Turn into baking dish and bake at 350° to 360° for 20 to 25 minutes or until brown.

title: Scalloped Potatoes With Pork Chops
text: Scalloped Potatoes With Pork Chops. Ingredients: 6 pork chops, cut 1/2-inch thick; 2 Tbsp. cooking oil; salt; pepper; 3 Tbsp. butter or regular margarine; 3 Tbsp. flour; 1 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 2 c. chicken broth; 6 c. sliced pared potatoes; 1 medium onion, sliced and separated into rings. Directions: Brown pork chops in hot oil. Sprinkle with salt and pepper to taste. Melt butter in saucepan. Stir in flour, 1 1/2 teaspoon salt and 1/4 teaspoon pepper. Add chicken broth; cook, stirring constantly until mixture comes to a boil. Place potatoes in bottom of 11 x 7 x 1 1/2-inch baking dish. Top with onion rings. Pour chicken broth mixture evenly over top. Top with pork chops. Cover with foil. Bake in 350° oven 1 hour. Remove cover and continue baking 30 minutes or until meat is tender. Makes 6 servings.

title: Delight
text: Delight. Ingredients: 1 c. flour; 1 stick margarine, melted and cooled; 1 c. chopped nuts; 1 (8 oz.) cream cheese; 1 (8 oz.) Cool Whip; 1 large box instant cocoa, vanilla or other pudding; 3 c. milk. Directions: Mix cream cheese and Cool Whip; put on crust. Mix pudding and milk. Pour on other filling. Top with more Cool Whip and chopped nuts. Keep refrigerated.

title: Super Cake
text: Super Cake. Ingredients: 20 oz. can crushed pineapple; 1 c. sugar; lemon cake mix; 1 small pkg. instant lemon pudding; 1 large Cool Whip. Directions: Boil crushed pineapple and sugar for 5 minutes; cool. Bake cake according to directions. While still hot, spread cooked pineapple on top. Sprinkle instant pudding on top of pineapple "dry." Cover cake with Cool Whip. Sprinkle with pecans and coconut. Good!

title: Garlic Pasta Chicken
text: Garlic Pasta Chicken. Ingredients: 2 1/2 lb. chicken breasts (boneless); 2 bunches broccoli (fresh); 1 large red bell pepper, chopped; 24 oz. Rotelle noodles (1 1/2 boxes); 2 Tbsp. garlic cloves, chopped; 1 Tbsp. garlic salt; 1 c. cooking wine; olive oil. Directions: In large skillet, generously cover bottom with olive oil. Saute garlic salt and fresh garlic. Add chopped chicken. Cook, stirring, 15 minutes. Add chopped red pepper. Stir and cook 8 to 10 minutes.

title: Honey Ham
text: Honey Ham. Ingredients: 3 to 4 lb. smoked ham (pork shoulder); 3 Tbsp. sugar; 3 Tbsp. water; 1 1/2 tsp. honey. Directions: Cut meat off bone. Use 12 ounces. Steam 30 minutes. Cool. Remove skin; cut off fat. Slice into small pieces (2 or 3 inches round). Steam again, 30 minutes. Remove juices. Boil water. Add sugar and ham. Stir 1 to 2 minutes until sugar dissolves and bubbles appear. Add honey until mixture is complete, about 30 seconds.

title: Italian Style Halibut On Grill
text: Italian Style Halibut On Grill. Ingredients: 2 c. Italian salad dressing; 2 Tbsp. lemon juice; 2 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper; Tabasco sauce; 2 lb. halibut steaks (may substitute cod or ocean perch); Parmesan cheese. Directions: In bowl, combine Italian salad dressing, lemon juice, salt, pepper and Tabasco sauce. Place halibut steaks in sauce. Let stand 30 minutes, turning once. Place fish on grill, basting occasionally, with dressing mixture. Cook fish for 5 to 7 minutes on each side or until fish flakes easily when tested with fork. Before serving, sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.

title: Sausage Bread Loaf
text: Sausage Bread Loaf. Ingredients: 1 pkg. frozen bread (3 loaves); 1 pkg. sausage (1 lb.); 2 lb. ground meat (lean); 1 pkg. Mozzarella cheese (1 lb.); 1 pkg. Cheddar cheese (1 lb.); 2 large jars sliced mushrooms; 1 large jar sliced jalapenos (optional); 1 bunch green onion tops; 1 medium container Parmesan cheese. Directions: Cut loaves in half and allow to rise. Brown ground meat and remove excess grease. Cut sausage into 1/8-inch slices. Brown and also drain. Grate the cheeses. Cut onion tops. Allow meat to cool. Each ingredient should be in separate bowls. Roll out dough very thin and round. Spread butter generously over dough. In center, add each ingredient in layers. Roll into loaf. Bake at 350° until brown. Makes 6 loaves.

title: Ground Beef Ragout(With Pate Grand-Pere - Dumplings)
text: Ground Beef Ragout(With Pate Grand-Pere - Dumplings). Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. margarine; 1 lb. ground beef; 1/4 c. onion; 3/4 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 1 small can tomatoes; 1 c. water; 1 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1 tsp. sugar. Directions: Cook beef in margarine. Add onion, salt and pepper. Brown, stirring often. Add other ingredients, mix, cover, and let simmer. Prepare dumplings (pate grand-pere).

title: Onion Yeast Rolls
text: Onion Yeast Rolls. Ingredients: 2 pkg. dry yeast; 1 Tbsp. sugar; 1 c. warm tap water; 1/3 c. sugar; 2 tsp. salt; 3 tsp. baking powder; 2 c. room temperature whole milk; 3 c. thawed frozen chopped onion; 1/3 c. oil; 1 egg; 5 c. flour. Directions: Dissolve yeast in 1 tablespoon sugar and warm tap water in a large measuring cup. Meanwhile, measure dry ingredients. When yeast is dissolved, add measured dry ingredients, then add 5 cups flour. Beat until smooth. Mix in enough remaining flour (3 cups) to make dough easy to handle. Turn dough on floured surface; knead until smooth. Place in greased bowl with enough oil to coat dough. Turn greased side up. Cover with plastic wrap; let rise until double in size. Punch down and refrigerate. Cover with plastic and foil.

title: Hot Brandied Fruit
text: Hot Brandied Fruit. Ingredients: 2 (17 oz.) cans apricot halves; 1 (29 oz.) can pear halves (can); 1 (29 oz.) can peach halves; 1 (20 oz.) can pineapple slices; 1 (10 oz.) jar maraschino cherries; 1/2 c. melted butter; 3/4 c. firmly packed brown sugar; 1/2 c. brandy; 10 whole cloves; 4 cinnamon sticks. Directions: Drain fruit. Reserve juices. Combine juices and stir well. Set aside 1/2 cup juices. Combine butter and sugar. Stir until smooth. Add reserved juice, brandy and spices. Stir until blended. Combine fruit in 12 x 8 x 2-inch dish. Pour brandy mixture in. Microwave on High 10 to 12 minutes or until thoroughly heated. Stir twice.

title: Creamy Pea Salad
text: Creamy Pea Salad. Ingredients: 2 16 oz. pkg. frozen peas, thawed; 1/2 c. diced cheddar cheese; 1/2 c. diced mozzarella cheese; 1 medium onion, chopped; 1 c. mayonnaise or salad dressing; salt and pepper, to taste; 4 bacon strips, cooked and crumbled. Directions: In a bowl, combine the peas, cheeses, onion, mayonnaise, salt and pepper; mix well. Refrigerate until serving. Sprinkle with bacon. Makes 6 to 8 servings.

title: Fruited Pistachio Pudding
text: Fruited Pistachio Pudding. Ingredients: 1 pkg. sugar-free instant pudding mix; 2 Tbsp. salad dressing or mayonnaise; 1 (8 oz.) can crushed pineapple (undrained); 1 carton frozen whipped topping, thawed; 1 (16 oz.) can fruit cocktail (undrained). Directions: In a large bowl, combine pudding mix, whipped topping and salad dressing; mix well. Fold in remaining ingredients. Leave in bowl. Serve immediately or chill.

title: Cream Pull Candy
text: Cream Pull Candy. Ingredients: 3 c. sugar; 3 Tbsp. white syrup; 1/8 tsp. soda; 1 c. water; 1/2 pt. whipping cream. Directions: In large (8-quart) pan, mix sugar, syrup, soda and water. Boil until mixture bubbles freely. Then place the thermometer in, and don't let it touch the bottom of the pan. Next add the whipping cream slowly all around in the pan (don't pour it all in one place). Let this boil to the hard boil stage (260°). Do not stir. Pour out onto marble slab or heavily buttered cold platter. When cool enough to handle, pull until creamy white and holds its shape (about 20 minutes). Cut with scissors and put in dish to cream. Be sure to butter your hands and remove all rings.

title: Overnight Crab Casserole
text: Overnight Crab Casserole. Ingredients: 1/2 lb. (8 oz.) crab meat or imitation crab; 4 oz. grated Cheddar cheese; 2 hard-boiled eggs, coarsely chopped; 1 c. milk; 1 c. uncooked shell macaroni; 1 can (10 1/2 oz.) cream of mushroom soup; 1 Tbsp. chopped onion. Directions: Combine all ingredients in deep casserole dish (1 quart size). Cover and refrigerate at least 8 hours. Bake covered at 350° for 1 hour. May sprinkle little paprika on top.

title: Best Pecan Pie
text: Best Pecan Pie. Ingredients: 1/2 c. butter; 1 c. corn syrup; 1 c. sugar; 3 eggs, beaten; 1/2 tsp. lemon juice; 1 tsp. vanilla; salt to taste; 1 c. chopped pecans; 1 unbaked pie shell. Directions: Brown butter in saucepan over medium heat until golden brown, stirring constantly to prevent burning. Cool. Beat corn syrup, sugar and eggs in bowl. Add lemon juice, vanilla and salt; mix well. Stir in pecans and browned butter.

title: Crisp Cucumber Pickles
text: Crisp Cucumber Pickles. Ingredients: 4 qt. sliced cucumbers; 6 medium white onions, sliced; 2 green peppers, chopped; 1/3 c. salt; 1 1/2 tsp. turmeric; 3 c. vinegar; 1 1/2 tsp. celery seed; 2 Tbsp. mustard seed; 5 c. sugar. Directions: Put cucumbers, onions and peppers in a large pan or bowl. Then add salt and cover with ice. Mix throughly. Let stand for 3 hours. Drain.

title: Philly Three-Step Cheesecake
text: Philly Three-Step Cheesecake. Ingredients: 2 (8 oz.) cream cheese, softened; 1/2 c. sugar; 1/2 tsp. vanilla; 2 eggs; 1 graham crust (9-inch or 6 oz.). Directions: Mix cream cheese, sugar and vanilla at medium speed with electric mixer until well blended. Add eggs; mix until blended. Pour into crust. Bake at 350° for 40 minutes or until center is almost set. Cool. Refrigerate 3 hours or overnight. Serves 8.

title: Sukiyaki Salad
text: Sukiyaki Salad. Ingredients: 1/2 c. raw spinach leaves, coarsely broken up; 1/2 c. other salad greens, broken into small pieces; 1/2 c. bean sprouts, drained; 1/2 c. celery, thinly sliced diagonally; 1/4 c. water chestnuts, thinly sliced; 1/2 c. raw mushrooms, thinly sliced lengthwise; 1/2 c. green pepper, thinly sliced lengthwise; 1/2 c. cabbage, thinly sliced; 1/2 c. watercress. Directions: In a large bowl, combine ingredients. Blend 2 tablespoons soy sauce and garlic to taste with 1/2 cup of your favorite oil and vinegar dressing. Toss with salad.

title: Crispy-Tart Cole Slaw
text: Crispy-Tart Cole Slaw. Ingredients: 1 large head cabbage, shredded; 1 medium onion, chopped; 1 green pepper, finely chopped; 1 Tbsp. salt; 1 c. sugar; 1/2 c. oil; 1 (2 oz.) jar pimentos, finely chopped; 1 tsp. mustard seed; 1 c. boiling water; 1 Tbsp. celery seed; 1 c. white vinegar. Directions: Combine shredded cabbage and green pepper in large bowl. Sprinkle salt over top and pour boiling water over all; let stand at room temperature for 1 hour. Drain in colander. Combine sugar, vinegar, oil, celery and mustard seed. Add to cabbage mixture. Stir in chopped pimento. Cover and refrigerate.

title: Okra Casserole
text: Okra Casserole. Ingredients: sliced tomatoes; sliced onion; sliced okra; 1 lb. ground beef; 1 lb. sausage. Directions: Cook and crumble meat. Arrange in layers in casserole dish. Bake at 350° until vegetables are tender.

title: Ripley Tomatoes
text: Ripley Tomatoes. Ingredients: 4 c. canned tomatoes, cut up; 2 1/2 c. herb Stove Top stuffing; 2 Tbsp. sugar; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. oregano; 1/4 tsp. rosemary, crumbled; 1/4 tsp. black pepper; 1/2 tsp. nutmeg; 1/4 c. oleo. Directions: In a buttered 2-quart casserole mix tomatoes and 2 cups stuffing together. Stir in onion, sugar and seasonings. Dot with oleo. Sprinkle remaining stuffing on top. Bake at 375° for 45 minutes.

title: Bacon-Wrapped Shrimp
text: Bacon-Wrapped Shrimp. Ingredients: 1 (16 oz.) pkg. Black Label bacon; 40 shrimp; 1 (10 oz.) bottle teriyaki sauce. Directions: Cut bacon in half; wrap bacon around the shrimp. Secure with wooden toothpick. Place in 9 x 13-inch baking dish. Pour teriyaki sauce over shrimp. Cover and refrigerate 1 hour. Bake at 400°, uncovered, for 20 to 25 minutes or until bacon is crisp.

title: Minute Cherry Crisp
text: Minute Cherry Crisp. Ingredients: 1/2 pkg. (3/4 c. firmly packed) Pillsbury pie crust mix; 1/2 c. sugar; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 2 Tbsp. butter or margarine, melted; 1 (1 lb. 5 oz.) can prepared cherry pie filling. Directions: Preheat oven to 400°. In a small mixing bowl, combine pie crust mix, sugar and cinnamon. Add butter. Mix until crumbly. Spread pie filling in 8-inch square pan. Sprinkle with pie crust mixture. Bake 35 to 40 minutes or until light golden brown. Yields 6 to 8 servings.

title: Punch Bowl Cake(Small Size)
text: Punch Bowl Cake(Small Size). Ingredients: 1 yellow cake mix or angel food mix, baked ahead; 1 large pkg. instant vanilla pudding, prepared; 2 cans cherry pie filling; 2 cans crushed pineapple, drained; 16 oz. size whipped topping; 3 bananas, sliced; extra Cool Whip; coconut; cherries. Directions: In a small punch bowl, assemble 10 layers in the following order: thin layer of crumbled cake, all of vanilla pudding, thin layer of Cool Whip, thin layer of cake (crumbled), all cherry pie filling, thin layer of Cool Whip, thin layer of crumbled cake, all crushed pineapple, thin layer of Cool Whip and sliced bananas. Decorate top with Cool Whip, coconut and cherries.

title: Wendy'S Frosty
text: Wendy'S Frosty. Ingredients: 1/2 gal. chocolate milk; 9 oz. Cool Whip; 1 can Eagle Brand milk. Directions: Mix well. Can add additional chocolate syrup, if desired. Pour into freezer can. Freeze according to manufacturer's instructions. Makes 1 gallon.

title: Five-Cup Salad
text: Five-Cup Salad. Ingredients: 1 c. mandarin oranges; 1 c. coconut; 1 c. sour cream; 1 c. marshmallows; 1 c. pineapple. Directions: Fold all ingredients together thoroughly. You may chill it before serving or serve at once.

title: Slowly Deviled Beef
text: Slowly Deviled Beef. Ingredients: 2 lb. lean stew beef, cut in 1-inch cubes; 1 envelope seasoning mix for Sloppy Joes; 6 oz. can tomato paste; 1 c. coarsely sliced celery; 1 green pepper, cut into squares; 1/2 c. water; 2 Tbsp. vinegar. Directions: Place all ingredients in slow cooker; stir to mix ingredients. Cover and cook about 10 hours on low or 4 to 5 hours on high. Serve with hot cooked noodles or rice. Has a nice tangy flavor. Makes 6 servings.

title: Modern Pioneer Stew
text: Modern Pioneer Stew. Ingredients: 1 pkg. Lipton onion soup mix; 2 lb. beef stew meat, cut up; 1 can cream of mushroom soup; 1/2 to 3/4 can water; splash of red Burgundy wine (optional); potatoes, cut up; carrots, cut up; celery, cut up. Directions: Sprinkle Lipton mix over bottom of casserole dish or pan. (I use 2 1/2-quart casserole.) Put meat on top of soup mix. Mix can of soup with water; add splash of red wine. Pour soup mixture over meat. Add the cut up vegetables on top. Season with salt and pepper. Cover tightly. Bake 2 1/2 to 3 hours in 300° oven. Serve with Bisquick biscuits or crusty French bread.

title: Aunt Sandy'S No-Bake Cookies
text: Aunt Sandy'S No-Bake Cookies. Ingredients: 1 stick margarine; 1/2 c. milk; 1/2 c. cocoa; 2 c. sugar; 3 c. quick oats; 1/2 c. peanut butter; 1/2 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Cook first 4 ingredients together until they boil for 2 to 3 minutes. Remove from stove and add the other ingredients all at once. Mix together quickly. Drop by spoonfuls onto wax paper. Let harden. The secret to this recipe is to work quickly and time carefully.

title: Pickled Peppers And Wieners
text: Pickled Peppers And Wieners. Ingredients: banana peppers; wieners; 2 1/2 c. vinegar; 1 c. sugar; 2 1/2 c. water; 1/4 c. Wesson oil; 1 Tbsp. salt. Directions: Cut off ends of peppers; remove seeds. Place wiener inside of pepper. Place inside of canning jars (wide-mouth jars work best). Pour boiling mixture over peppers and seal. Process in hot water bath for 20 minutes, or place in pressure cooker at 5 pounds pressure for 10 minutes. This should make 3 quarts.

title: Delicious Dirt
text: Delicious Dirt. Ingredients: 1 (20 oz.) pkg. Oreo cookies, grind in blender; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese; 1/2 stick butter or margarine; 1 c. powdered sugar; 2 (3 1/2 oz.) pkg. vanilla instant pudding; 1 (12 oz.) Cool Whip; 3 1/2 c. milk. Directions: Mix cream cheese, butter and sugar together until blended. Add pudding and milk and beat on low speed until thickened. Stir in Cool Whip by hand.

title: Low-Fat Green Chili Casserole
text: Low-Fat Green Chili Casserole. Ingredients: 4 to 5 cans green chilies (found in Mexican section); 4 egg whites; 1/2 can evaporated skim milk; 2 to 3 c. Monterey Jack cheese (reduced fat); 2 to 3 c. Cheddar cheese (reduced fat); 1 to 2 small cans tomato sauce. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Open cans of green chilies and rinse in colander with water; set aside. Take egg whites and evaporated milk; whisk until just frothy. Spray ungreased 8 x 9-inch pan with Pam or other low saturated fat oil. Put a layer of chilies, then sprinkle mixture of 2 cheeses (I use quite a bit). Then pour 1/3 of milk and egg white mixture. This mixture does not need to cover the layer. Repeat this layering (chilies, cheese and milk mixture) until you are at the top. Pour tomato sauce on top to cover. Bake until bubbly throughout, anywhere from 20 to 30 minutes. Take out. Serve with salad and bread.

title: Tuna Macaroni Casserole
text: Tuna Macaroni Casserole. Ingredients: 1 box macaroni and cheese; 1 can tuna, drained; 1 small jar pimentos; 1 medium onion, chopped. Directions: Prepare macaroni and cheese as directed. Add drained tuna, pimento and onion. Mix. Serve hot or cold.

title: Sweet Potatoes And Honey
text: Sweet Potatoes And Honey. Ingredients: 6 sweet potatoes or yams; small jar honey; 2 Tbsp. orange juice; salt to taste. Directions: Peel sweet potatoes. Cut into 1/4-inch slices lengthwise. Boil for 15 minutes. Drain and remove to warm casserole dish (greased). Mix honey and juice. Pour mixture over sweets. Bake in 350° oven until tender.

title: Broccoli With Olive Butter
text: Broccoli With Olive Butter. Ingredients: 2/3 c. butter or margarine; 2 cloves garlic, crushed; 4 tsp. freshly squeezed lemon juice; dash of black pepper; 12 stuffed olives, sliced; 4 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen broccoli spears, cooked; seasoned salt. Directions: Heat butter and garlic over low heat in small saucepan, about 15 minutes. Add lemon juice, pepper and olives; heat thoroughly, but do not boil. Cook broccoli according to package directions. Drain and arrange in heated serving dish. Sprinkle lightly with seasoned salt, then pour sauce over all. Yields 12 to 14 servings.

title: Herbed Mushroom Salad
text: Herbed Mushroom Salad. Ingredients: 1/4 lb. fresh mushrooms, sliced; 3 Tbsp. salad oil; 1 Tbsp. lemon juice; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. marjoram; 1/4 tsp. basil; 1/2 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1 tsp. soy sauce; shredded lettuce. Directions: Place mushrooms in small bowl. Combine all ingredients except shredded lettuce and pour over mushrooms. Marinate a few hours or overnight. Arrange shredded lettuce in salad bowls; top with mushrooms.

title: Quick Rice And Meat Stir-Fry
text: Quick Rice And Meat Stir-Fry. Ingredients: 2 c. cooked rice; 1 1/2 to 2 c. cooked meat, cubed (leftover pork, beef, chicken); 1 c. cooked broccoli or 1 pkg. frozen broccoli; 1 onion, chopped; 6 to 8 mushrooms (fresh), sliced; 2 Tbsp. oil; 1 Tbsp. soy sauce. Directions: Cook onion in large skillet with oil. Add mushrooms and meat. If using frozen broccoli, cook in microwave first and the add to meat, mushrooms and onion mixture. Add cooked rice. Then add soy sauce and heat through. Makes 4 servings.

title: Blueberry Cream Pie
text: Blueberry Cream Pie. Ingredients: 1 c. commerical sour cream; 2 Tbsp. all-purpose flour; 3/4 c. sugar; 1 tsp. vanilla extract; 3/4 tsp. salt; 1 egg, beaten; 2 1/2 c. fresh blueberries; 1 unbaked 9-inch pastry shell; 3 Tbsp. all-purpose flour; 3 Tbsp. butter, softened; 3 Tbsp. chopped black walnuts. Directions: Combine first 6 ingredients; beat 5 minutes at medium speed of an electric mixer or until smooth. Fold in blueberries. Pour filling into pastry shell. Bake at 400° for 25 minutes.

title: Turkey, Swiss And Mushroom Crepes
text: Turkey, Swiss And Mushroom Crepes. Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. turkey or chicken; Mushroom Sauce; 3/4 lb. Swiss cheese; 6 crepes. Directions: Lay turkey breast, skin up, on some sprigs of rosemary and oregano and bake in a covered dish with 1/2 cup water. Salt and pepper. Cut turkey into 1/2-inch cubes. Put approximately 3 tablespoons of turkey and 1 tablespoon grated Swiss cheese in the center of each crepe. Add 2 tablespoons of hot Mushroom Sauce. Roll up the crepe. Add 1 tablespoon Mushroom Sauce over the top of the crepe. Garnish with rosemary sprigs.

title: Prairie Graham Bread
text: Prairie Graham Bread. Ingredients: 1 egg; 3/4 c. sugar; 1 tsp. salt; 2 c. buttermilk; 2 tsp. baking soda; 1 1/2 c. graham flour or whole wheat flour; 1 1/2 c. white flour. Directions: Mix all ingredients in order listed until well blended. Pour into greased 9 x 5-inch bread pan. Bake 1 hour at 350° or until done. Makes 1 loaf.

title: Broccoli With Linguine
text: Broccoli With Linguine. Ingredients: 1/2 lb. fresh broccoli; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. linguine; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. sliced mushrooms; 2 c. half and half; 1/4 c. butter/margarine; 1 c. grated Parmesan cheese. Directions: Cook broccoli in a small amount of boiling salted water for 10 minutes. Drain and chop. Cook linguine according to package directions; drain. Combine linguine, broccoli, mushrooms, half and half and butter. Simmer 5 minutes. Add cheese. Yield: 6 servings.

title: Gooey Cake(Serve As A Brownie)
text: Gooey Cake(Serve As A Brownie). Ingredients: 1 box yellow cake mix with pudding in it; 1 stick slightly melted butter or margarine; 1 egg; 1 box powdered sugar minus 1/4 c.; 2 eggs; 1 (8 oz.) cream cheese (room temperature). Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Mix cake mix, butter and egg. Batter will be stiff. Take spatula and pat into 9 x 13-inch greased and floured pan. Set aside. Mix powdered sugar, eggs and cream cheese with mixer for 5 minutes until good. Pour over top of cake mix. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes. Take remaining powdered sugar and sprinkle over the top when cake is done. Let cool and serve. Caution: Overeating this stuff will cause severe stomach ache, it's very rich! I know!

title: Oreo Spider Cookies
text: Oreo Spider Cookies. Ingredients: Oreo cookies; red licorice; white frosting. Directions: Scrape filling out of Oreo cookies. Cut red licorice into thin strips (to look like spider legs). Put frosting on an Oreo piece; drape red licorice over frosting so that the licorice hangs out both sides. (Use however many red strips you want for however many legs you want.) Put an Oreo piece on top to form cookie. Put little dots of frosting on top for eyes. Enjoy this fun treat!

title: Red Beet Eggs
text: Red Beet Eggs. Ingredients: 1 (1 lb.) can beets; 1 c. cider vinegar; 1/3 c. sugar; 3/4 tsp. salt; 2 Tbsp. water; 8 large eggs, hard-cooked. Directions: Drain beets, reserving juice. Mix sugar, salt, vinegar, water and beet juice in a small saucepan. Heat until sugar dissolves. Peel eggs. Put beets and eggs in a bowl. Cover with the liquid and refrigerate for several days.

title: The Zucchini Bread
text: The Zucchini Bread. Ingredients: 2 c. zucchini, grated; 1 c. sugar; 1 egg; 2/3 c. oil; 1/2 c. each: walnuts and raisins; 1 1/2 c. flour; 2 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. nutmeg; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. baking powder. Directions: This is so easy and so great to have on hand in the freezer. Toasts like a dream or serve with cream cheese.

title: Smothered Deer Tips
text: Smothered Deer Tips. Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. deer stew meat; 2 1/2 Tbsp. cooking oil; 1 onion, chopped; 1 clove garlic, minced; salt and pepper; 3 Tbsp. flour; 2 1/2 c. water; 1 Tbsp. Kitchen Bouquet (optional). Directions: Cut stew meat into small pieces and brown in cooking oil. Remove meat and saute onion and garlic in the oil. When onion is tender, put the meat back into oil with onion and garlic. Add flour and toss until blended. Salt and pepper to taste. Lower heat and slowly add water until sauce is not too thick. Add Kitchen Bouquet to darken and simmer on low flame until meat is tender, about 1 hour.

title: Sweet And Sour Chicken And Rice
text: Sweet And Sour Chicken And Rice. Ingredients: 6 oz. skinless chicken breast; 1 c. fresh mushrooms; 1/4 c. sweet and sour sauce; 1 Tbsp. soy sauce; 1/4 tsp. garlic powder; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 1 c. cooking rice. Directions: Cut up chicken into cubes. Put in skillet and cook until chicken is no longer pink. Add all ingredients and cook on low heat. Boil rice and mix rice with chicken mixture.

title: Jumbo Pasta Shells
text: Jumbo Pasta Shells. Ingredients: 9 jumbo shells; 1 large jar spaghetti sauce; 10 oz. spinach; 1 1/2 lb. Ricotta cheese; 1/2 lb. Mozzarella cheese; 2 eggs; salt to taste; garlic salt to taste. Directions: Save 1 cup Mozzarella cheese for topping.

title: French Fries
text: French Fries. Ingredients: 4 large, long Idaho potatoes; peanut oil; kosher salt or coarse sea salt. Directions: Peel and slice potatoes lengthwise into stick 1/4-inch thick. Place in a bowl, cover with water and refrigerate 16 hours. Drain potato sticks and lay out on dish towels to dry. Be sure they are completely dry before frying. In a deep-fryer with a candy thermometer clipped to the side, heat 2-inches of peanut oil to 300°. Add just enough potatoes to cover the base of the frying basket, and cook until slightly limp, 1 1/2 to 2 minutes. Do not brown. Lift basket and drain fries. Transfer potatoes to a baking rack and separate sticks. Repeat with remaining potatoes. Increase heat to 375°. Again, add potatoes in batches to the oil. Fry until chestnut brown on edges and crisp. Drain and transfer to a bowl lined with paper towels. Immediately season with salt, tossing to coat. Serve. Yields 4 servings.

title: Parmesan Baked Chicken
text: Parmesan Baked Chicken. Ingredients: 4 to 6 pieces chicken; 1 c. instant potato flakes; 1/4 c. Parmesan cheese; 1 Tbsp. garlic powder; 1 egg; 1 tsp. milk; 1 stick margarine. Directions: Mix together dry ingredients. Beat together milk and egg. Roll chicken in egg mixture. Then roll in potato flake mixture. Melt a stick of margarine in a casserole dish. Place chicken in the dish and roll in margarine. Bake at 400° for 45 minutes or until juices run clear and chicken is golden brown.

title: Sour Cream Waffles
text: Sour Cream Waffles. Ingredients: 1/2 liter (1 pt.) rich sour cream; 6 to 8 Tbsp. water; 175 g (1 1/4 c.) flour; 1 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. baking powder. Directions: Mix all ingredients to a smooth batter and let stand and swell 5 to 10 minutes. Bake the waffles over relatively low heat in an electric waffle iron or one heated on the stove. Should be crisp and golden brown.

title: Creamy Vanilla Icing
text: Creamy Vanilla Icing. Ingredients: 5 Tbsp. flour; 1 c. milk; 1 c. sugar; 1 stick butter; 1/2 c. shortening (Crisco); 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Mix milk and flour, then cook until thick, stirring constantly; cool. Cream sugar, shortening and sugar; add vanilla. Add flour mixture and mix well.

title: Gram'S Sand Tarts
text: Gram'S Sand Tarts. Ingredients: 1 c. butter; 2 c. sugar; 3 1/2 c. flour; 4 tsp. baking powder; 2 eggs. Directions: Mix flour, baking powder and sugar. Rub in butter with a pastry blender. Mix in beaten eggs and mix with your hands until blended. Roll into the size of cookies you desire. Wrap in wax paper and put in refrigerator overnight. Next morning, slice thin and bake 10 minutes at 350°.

title: Monkey Cake
text: Monkey Cake. Ingredients: 4 cans biscuits, cut in 1/4 each; 1 c. white sugar; 1 Tbsp. cinnamon. Directions: Roll biscuit in sugar and cinnamon mixture. Put in a well greased tube or loaf pan. Cream together 1/4 cup margarine and 1 cup brown sugar. Pour over cake. Bake at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes.

title: Chicken Enchilada(Microwave)
text: Chicken Enchilada(Microwave). Ingredients: 2 1/2 c. diced, cooked chicken; 1 (15 oz.) can evaporated milk; 1 can condensed cream of chicken soup; 1 pkg. taco seasoning mix; 1/2 c. chopped celery; 1 can chopped green chillies, drained; 6 corn tortillas, torn; 3/4 c. grated cheese. Directions: In 2-quart casserole, mix all the ingredients. Microwave on High for 11 to 13 minutes. Rotate dish 1/2 turn; cook 5 minutes.

title: Wild Rice And Duck
text: Wild Rice And Duck. Ingredients: duck breasts (approximately 6); 4 boxes Uncle Ben's wild rice; 1 can (family size) cream of mushroom soup; 1 c. mushrooms; 1 large onion, chopped; 1 lb. hot sausage; Worcestershire sauce to taste. Directions: Boil chunks of duck breasts until tender; discard water. Cook rice as directed on package. Brown sausage and onion. Mix all ingredients; pour in greased pan. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

title: Super Bowl Casserole
text: Super Bowl Casserole. Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. ground beef or sausage; 1 large onion, chopped; 1 can Ranch Style beans with juice; 1 can whole kernel corn with juice; 1 can stewed tomatoes; 2 cans Ro-Tel tomatoes; 20 flour tortillas; 1 lb. Velveeta cheese; 1 lb. Cheddar cheese. Directions: In Dutch oven, brown ground beef or sausage with onion. Drain grease. Pour in beans, corn, stewed tomatoes and one can Ro-Tel tomatoes. Take 20 flour tortillas, broken up, put half in 14 x 10-inch pan. Layer meat, cheeses, tortillas, meat and cheeses. Pour 1 can Ro-Tel tomatoes over top. Let sit for two hours, covered with foil. Bake at 350° for 1 hour, uncovered. Serves 10 to 12.

title: Dirt Pie
text: Dirt Pie. Ingredients: 2 pkg. instant French vanilla pudding (unprepared, keep in dry form); 8 oz. cream cheese, softened; 3 1/2 c. milk; 3/4 c. powdered sugar; 1 pkg. Oreos; 8 or 12 oz. whipped topping. Directions: In deep bowl, put pudding, cream cheese and 1/2 cup of milk. Mix until creamy with hand mixer. Add powdered sugar and mix thoroughly, then add remaining milk, 1/2 cup at a time, and mix until creamy. Crumble enough Oreo cookies to line bottom of dish as a crust in a lasagna pan. Add the pudding mix and top with whipped topping. Refrigerate until serving. Prior to serving, crumble a few more Oreos on the top to provide a crisp, crunchy taste.

title: Yorkshire Pudding
text: Yorkshire Pudding. Ingredients: 1/4 c. hot roast beef drippings; 2 eggs; 1 c. milk; 1 c. sifted flour; 1/2 tsp. salt. Directions: Pour into an 11 x 7 x 1 1/2-inch baking dish and keep hot 1/4 cup roast beef drippings. Beat until thick and piled softly 2 eggs. Add to beaten eggs and beat with rotary beater until smooth milk, flour and salt. Pour mixture over hot meat drippings and bake at 400° for 30 to 40 minutes or until puffed and browned. Cut into squares and serve immediately with standing rib roast of beef.

title: Angel Cornbread
text: Angel Cornbread. Ingredients: 1/2 c. corn meal (yellow or white); 1 pkg. dry yeast; 1 Tbsp. sugar; 1 tsp. salt; 1 c. flour; 1 1/2 tsp. baking powder; 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 1/2 c. oil; 2 c. buttermilk; 2 eggs, well beaten. Directions: Mix dry ingredients together well (excluding yeast). Dissolve yeast in 1/4 cup warm water. Mix together well the dissolved yeast, oil and buttermilk with the dry ingredients. Pour batter into a well-greased cornbread stick or muffin pans and bake at 450° for 12 to 15 minutes. Makes 3 dozen. Serve hot or cold.

title: French Bread Spread
text: French Bread Spread. Ingredients: 8 oz. Mozzarella cheese; 2/3 c. mayo; 4 Tbsp. Parmesan cheese; 4 Tbsp. chopped onions; 5 oz. chopped mushrooms; 1 French bread, cut in half. Directions: Mix ingredients together and spread over French bread. Bake at 375° for 10 to 12 minutes. Serve warm.

title: Can Opener Stew
text: Can Opener Stew. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; 1/2 medium onion, chopped; 1 can large cut Veg-All vegetables; 1 can whole kernel corn; 1 can stewed tomatoes; 2 small cans tomato sauce; 1 can vegetable beef soup; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Brown ground meat with chopped onion. Add all other ingredients (with liquids). Simmer for 30 minutes. Makes a lot!

title: Mom'S Oatmeal Cookies
text: Mom'S Oatmeal Cookies. Ingredients: 1/2 c. butter; 1 c. brown sugar; 1 c. white sugar; 2 eggs; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 tsp. salt (optional); 1 tsp. soda; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 3 c. oatmeal; 1 3/4 c. flour; 1 c. raisins (optional); 1 c. walnuts (optional). Directions: Mix all ingredients together. Drop onto lightly greased cookie sheet by rounded teaspoonfuls. Bake at 350° for 8 to 10 minutes. Best when chewy or soft.

title: Low Calorie Spiced Tea Mix
text: Low Calorie Spiced Tea Mix. Ingredients: 3 Tbsp. Tang; 1 c. lemon flavored ice tea mix with Equal; 1 Tbsp. apple pie spice; 1 (.49 oz.) pkg. lemonade mix with Equal. Directions: Combine all the ingredients. Mix well. Store in an airtight container. Makes 48 servings; one serving equals 3 calories.

title: Texas Chili
text: Texas Chili. Ingredients: 3 lb. boneless beef chuck, thinly cut in 2-inch strips; 1/4 lb. beef suet, chopped; 1 (28 oz.) can tomatoes; 1/2 c. beef broth; 2 onions, sliced; 4 cloves garlic, crushed; 2 Tbsp. diced green chilies; 1 Tbsp. crushed red pepper; 2 tsp. salt; 2 tsp. oregano; 1 1/2 tsp. cumin; 3 bay leaves, crumbled. Directions: Combine all ingredients in a slow cooker at simmer setting. Simmer for about 5 hours. Serves 6 to 8.

title: Purple Cabbage Cole Slaw
text: Purple Cabbage Cole Slaw. Ingredients: 1 head cabbage; 2 onions; 3/4 c. sugar; 1 c. white vinegar; 3/4 c. vegetable oil; 2 tsp. sugar; 1 tsp. dry mustard; 1 tsp. celery seed. Directions: Shred cabbage. Chop onion. Mix. Top with 3/4 cup sugar. Boil vinegar, oil, 2 teaspoons sugar, mustard and celery seed. Cool. Pour over cabbage mixture. Stir. Refrigerate 6 hours. Keeps a long time in Tupperware.

title: Yogurt Dinner Rolls(Low Calorie)
text: Yogurt Dinner Rolls(Low Calorie). Ingredients: 1/4 c. warm water; 2 Tbsp. sugar; 1 pkg. active dry yeast; 1 c. plain nonfat yogurt; 2 Tbsp. acceptable margarine, melted; 1 egg; 1 tsp. leaf oregano; 2 tsp. basil; 2 Tbsp. grated onion; 3/4 c. all-purpose flour; 3/4 c. whole wheat flour; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. all-purpose flour; 3/4 c. whole wheat flour. Directions: In a bowl, combine water, sugar and yeast. Set aside for about 5 minutes, or until bubbly; then add yogurt, margarine, egg, herbs and onion. Set aside.

title: Cherry Coffee Cake
text: Cherry Coffee Cake. Ingredients: 4 eggs; 1 c. vegetable oil; 1 c. white sugar; 2 c. flour; 1 tsp. baking powder; 1 can cherry pie filling; sugar; cinnamon. Directions: Cream the sugar with the oil. Add eggs, one at a time, beating until well blended. Add flour sifted with the baking powder. Pour 1/2 into a greased 9 x 13-inch pan. Spread the cherry pie filling over the dough. Sprinkle lightly with sugar and cinnamon. Add remaining dough and sprinkle with more cinnamon and sugar. Bake at 325° for 50 minutes. Drizzle with powdered sugar glaze when still slightly warm.

title: Taffy
text: Taffy. Ingredients: 2 c. sugar; 1/2 c. white vinegar; 1/2 c. light corn syrup; 1/4 tsp. cream of tartar; 1/2 tsp. butter or margarine; 1 tsp. vanilla extract. Directions: Butter a large baking sheet. In a black iron pot (about 2 quart size), combine the sugar, vinegar, corn syrup, cream of tartar and butter. Bring to a boil and cook until it reaches 250 degrees on a candy thermometer. Remove from heat and quickly stir in the vanilla extract. Immediately pour into baking pan. Allow to cool slightly until the edges become firm enough to pull toward the center of the pan. Continue pulling edges toward the center until mixture is firm enough to handle. This mixture will be very hot, so be careful when handling it. Don't butter your hands because this makes it difficult to pull the taffy. The taffy, when ready to be pulled, should not stick to your hands. If it does, you can rub a little cornstarch onto the palms of your hands.

title: Johnny Mozetta(Large Recipe; Feeds About 30 People)
text: Johnny Mozetta(Large Recipe; Feeds About 30 People). Ingredients: 4 lb. ground round steak; 2 stalks celery, chopped; 1 lb. onions, chopped; 2 lb. green sweet peppers; 4 cloves garlic, chopped; 2 cans tomato soup; 2 cans tomato paste; 2 cans tomato sauce; 1 can enchilada sauce; 2 cans mushrooms (stems and pieces will do); 2 jars stuffed olives; 4 pkg. egg noodles; 1 1/2 lb. yellow cheese, grated. Directions: Fry onions, peppers and celery together. Fry meat and garlic together. Combine with first items in a large roaster. Add to preceeding mixture, all sauces, soup, olives and mushrooms. Let simmer 15 minutes. Add cooked noodles. Mix well. Cover with cheese. Bake 1 hour or until cheese browns. Can be made the day before and put into refrigerator. Uncover and reheat in a slow oven for about 2 hours.

title: Chicken Acapulco
text: Chicken Acapulco. Ingredients: 4 chicken breasts, cut up and cooked; 3 Tbsp. margarine; 4 Tbsp. flour; 1 c. sour cream; 1 c. cream of chicken soup; 1/3 to 1/2 can diced green chilies; 1/4 Tbsp. onion salt; 2 c. shredded Monterey Jack cheese. Directions: In a 9 x 13-inch pan layer chicken. Melt margarine and add flour until smooth, add sour cream until it just bubbles. Add soup, chilies and onion salt. Blend well, pour over chicken. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes. Add cheese and bake until melted. Serve over rice.

title: Moose Shoulder
text: Moose Shoulder. Ingredients: 2 lb. moose shoulder; 1 clove garlic; fat; 3 c. stock (boiled from bones); cattail shoots. Directions: Cut meat into 1-inch cubes. Chop garlic. Brown meat in fat. Add garlic and cover with stock. Cover and simmer for 2 hours. Add cattails for the last 20 minutes of cooking.

title: Leona'S Caramels
text: Leona'S Caramels. Ingredients: 1 c. butter; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1 (15 oz.) can sweetened condensed milk; 1 1/4 c. brown sugar; 1 c. light Karo syrup; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Melt butter in heavy 3-quart saucepan. Add sugar and salt. Stir in syrup. Mix well. Gradually add milk, stirring constantly. Cook on medium heat until firm ball stage (245°) or for about 15 minutes. Remove from heat. Add vanilla and pour into well buttered 9 x 9-inch pan. Cool for 4 to 5 hours. Flip on board and cut with sharp knife and wrap each piece in wax paper.

title: Stuffed Mushrooms Parmesan
text: Stuffed Mushrooms Parmesan. Ingredients: 1 lb. mushrooms; 1 Tbsp. grated Parmesan; 1 Tbsp. chopped parsley; 3 Tbsp. butter; dash of pepper; 1 clove garlic, minced; 1/2 c. fine dry bread crumbs; 1/2 tsp. salt; 3 Tbsp. butter, melted. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Wash mushrooms and select 12 of the largest. Remove stems and set aside. Chop stems and remaining mushrooms. Place chopped mushrooms in a skillet. Add butter and cook over low heat for 5 minutes. Add remaining ingredients, except melted butter. Mix well. Fill mushrooms with stuffing. Place in greased baking pan. Drizzle with melted butter. Sprinkle with additional Parmesan cheese. Bake at 350° for 15 minutes.

title: Fried Squash Bread
text: Fried Squash Bread. Ingredients: 1 c. corn meal; 1 egg; 1/4 c. buttermilk; 2 summer squash, diced; water. Directions: Cook squash in water until soft; leave 3/4 cup water in pot. Combine other ingredients with squash and water; mix together. Fry in hot oil until golden brown.

title: Broccoli And Cheese Stuffed Potato
text: Broccoli And Cheese Stuffed Potato. Ingredients: 4 oz. baked potato; 1 c. steamed broccoli; 1 1/2 Tbsp. nonfat sour cream; 1 1/2 oz. nonfat hard cheese, shredded. Directions: Wrap potato in paper towel. Microwave on High 5 minutes. Open and stuff with cheese. Press sides together to melt cheese. Top with broccoli and sour cream. Serves 1.

title: "Philly" Cheese Ball
text: "Philly" Cheese Ball. Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) Cracker Barrel brand Cheddar cold pack cheese food; 1 (8 oz.) Philadelphia Brand cream cheese; 2 Tbsp. margarine; 2 tsp. chopped pimento; 2 tsp. chopped green pepper; 2 tsp. chopped onion; 1 tsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/2 tsp. lemon juice. Directions: Combine cheese food, softened cream cheese and margarine; mix until well blended. Add remaining ingredients; mix well. Mold into bell shapes using the Cracker Barrel container coated with margarine. Chill until firm. Remove. Yields 2 bells.

title: Mom'S Cornflake Candy
text: Mom'S Cornflake Candy. Ingredients: 1 lb. milk chocolate; 2 sq. bitter chocolate; 1 c. walnuts; 5 c. cornflakes. Directions: Melt chocolate over hot water. Add cornflakes and nuts; mix well. Drop by tablespoon on waxed paper. Let set overnight until firm. Do not set in refrigerator.

title: Cinnamon Snaps
text: Cinnamon Snaps. Ingredients: 1/2 c. butter, softened (1 stick); 3/4 c. plus 3 Tbsp. sugar; 1 egg; 1 tsp. vanilla extract; 1 1/2 c. all-purpose flour; 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 1/2 tsp. baking powder; 1/4 tsp. salt (optional); 2 tsp. ground cinnamon. Directions: Heat oven to 375°. Beat butter and 3/4 cup sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in egg and extract. Combine flour, baking soda, baking powder and salt.
