Gradually add to butter mixture, mixing well. Combine cinnamon and remaining 3 tablespoons sugar in small bowl. Roll dough into 1-inch balls and roll in sugar/cinnamon mixture. Place 2 inches apart on lightly buttered cookie sheet. Bake 11 to 12 minutes or until light golden brown. Let stand on cookie sheet. Transfer to wire cooling racks. Makes about 2 dozen cookies.

title: Sour Cream Lasagna
text: Sour Cream Lasagna. Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. egg dumpling noodles; 1 1/2 lb. ground beef; 1 tsp. salt; dash of pepper; 1/4 tsp. garlic salt; 1/4 tsp. liquid smoke; 1 (8 oz.) can tomato sauce; 1 Tbsp. brown sugar; 1 c. cottage cheese; 1 (12 oz.) carton sour cream; 6 green onions, chopped; 3/4 c. grated Cheddar cheese. Directions: Cook noodles according to package directions; rinse and drain. Brown hamburger in skillet. Add next 6 ingredients; mix well. Cook for 5 minutes; remove from heat. Combine noodles with cottage cheese, sour cream and green onions. Alternate layers of noodle mixture and meat mixture in 2-quart baking dish, ending with meat mixture. Top with Cheddar cheese. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes, or until heated through.

title: Bisquick Caramel Rolls
text: Bisquick Caramel Rolls. Ingredients: 2 c. Bisquick; 1/2 c. cold water; 2 Tbsp. margarine; 1/4 c. brown sugar; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1/4 c. plus 2 Tbsp. brown sugar; 1/4 c. plus 2 Tbsp. margarine. Directions: Mix Bisquick and water. Roll out and spread with 2 tablespoons margarine. Sprinkle with 1/4 cup brown sugar and 1 teaspoon cinnamon. Roll up and cut into slices. Melt 1/4 cup plus 2 tablespoons margarine and add brown sugar. Place spoonfuls of mixture in bottom of muffin cups. Place a slice of dough in each cup. Bake at 400° for 20 minutes or until golden brown.

title: Weight Watchers Soup
text: Weight Watchers Soup. Ingredients: 1 hen or fryer, cooked and deboned; 2 cans English peas; 2 cans corn, cream-style; 3/4 c. ketchup; 1/4 c. Worcestershire sauce; 1 Tbsp. hot sauce; 2 cans large tomatoes or juice. Directions: Use broth from chicken. Throw in chicken and other ingredients. Cook until thick.

title: Chocolate Crackles(Cookies)
text: Chocolate Crackles(Cookies). Ingredients: 1 (1 lb. 2 1/2 oz.) pkg. devil's food cake mix; 2 eggs, slightly beaten; 1 Tbsp. water; 1/2 c. vegetable shortening; confectioners sugar. Directions: Combine cake mix, eggs, water and shortening. Mix with a spoon until well blended. Shape dough into a ball the size of a small walnut. Roll balls in confectioners sugar. Place on greased baking sheets. Bake in moderate oven (375°) for 8 to 10 minutes. Makes 48 cookies. These cookies made with cake mix have a moist, fudge center.

title: Pineapple-Cheese Casserole
text: Pineapple-Cheese Casserole. Ingredients: 1/2 c. sugar; 1/2 c. flour; 1 large can crushed pineapple; 1 c. grated Cheddar cheese. Directions: Mix sugar, flour and pineapple juice drained from can. In greased medium glass casserole, put half of cheese, layer of pineapple and top with rest of cheese. Pour juice mixture over all. Bake at 250° for 1 hour until brown on top.

title: Chicken And Cheese Casserole
text: Chicken And Cheese Casserole. Ingredients: 1 box wild rice with herbs, cooked; 1 chicken, chopped; 1 jar pimento; 1 can cream of celery soup; 1 small box Velveeta cheese, chopped. Directions: Mix and bake until cheese melts at 350°.

title: Seven Or Nine Grain Bread(Bread Machine Bread)
text: Seven Or Nine Grain Bread(Bread Machine Bread). Ingredients: 7/8 c. water; 1 egg; 2 1/2 c. whole wheat flour; 1/2 c. seven or nine grain cereal; 1 1/2 tsp. salt; 2 Tbsp. oil; 2 Tbsp. honey; 2 tsp. active yeast. Directions: Select light crust setting.

title: Oven-Baked French Toast
text: Oven-Baked French Toast. Ingredients: 1 loaf (8 oz.) French bread; 6 eggs; 1 1/2 c. milk; 4 Tbsp. sugar divided; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1/8 tsp. salt; ground cinnamon (optional); powdered sugar; 1 lb. strawberries, stemmed and sliced (about 3 c.); 1/2 tsp. lemon zest; 1 tsp. lemon juice. Directions: Spray 9 x 13 inch baking dish. Cut bread into 1-inch-thick slices; arrange closely in single layer in prepared pan. In large bowl beat eggs, whisk in milk, 3 Tbsp. of sugar, vanilla and salt; pour over bread. Cover and refrigerate at least 1 hour or over night. Preheat oven to 400 degrees. Sprinkle bread with cinnamon, if desired. Bake uncovered for 30 minutes or until golden brown. Remove from oven; sprinkle with powdered sugar. Slice strawberries place in bowl. Add lemon zest, juice, and remaining 1 Tbsp. sugar, mix lightly. Serve over warm French toast. Yields: 8 servings.

title: Sour Cream Cornbread
text: Sour Cream Cornbread. Ingredients: 1 (8.5 oz.) can cream-style corn; 1 small carton sour cream; 2 eggs; 2 Tbsp. sugar; 1/2 c. oil; 1 c. self-rising corn meal; 2 tsp. baking powder. Directions: Combine corn, sour cream, eggs, sugar and oil. Beat well. Add corn meal and baking powder. Mix well. Pour in a greased 10-inch baking dish. Bake at 400° for 30 minutes or until top is golden brown. Serve warm.

title: Chuck Wagon Chili Soup
text: Chuck Wagon Chili Soup. Ingredients: 12 oz. ground beef (chuck); 1 1/2 c. chopped onions; 2 Tbsp. minced garlic; 3 Tbsp. chili powder; 2 tsp. ground cumin; 1 small butternut squash, peeled, seeded and cut in 3/4-inch chunks (approximately 4 c.); 4 c. beef broth (from cubes); 1 (28 oz.) crushed tomatoes; 1 (16 oz.) barbecue baked beans; 1 (15.5 oz.) red kidney beans, rinsed; 1 (15.5 oz.) pinto beans, rinsed. Directions: Saute beef in a 5-quart pot over medium heat 4 minutes or until brown. Discard fat. Saute onions and garlic in pot 4 minutes or until soft. Stir in chili powder and cumin, then squash, broth and 1 extra cup water. Cover and gently boil 15 minutes until squash is almost tender. Add tomatoes and beans; bring to a boil. Reduce heat; cover and simmer 15 minutes to develop flavors. Serve with shredded Cheddar cheese and sliced scallions.

title: Gail'S Creation
text: Gail'S Creation. Ingredients: 1 c. LeSueur peas; 1 c. Del Monte canned yellow corn; 1 c. cooked pasta seashells; 1 pkg. mild Cheddar cheese. Directions: Heat peas and corn; add salt to taste. Drain peas, corn and pasta. Reserve small amount of liquid from each to be added later. Add all 3 together in pot. Melt grated cheese until coated well. Add butter, salt and pepper to taste and reserved liquid.

title: Chicken And Ham Party Salads
text: Chicken And Ham Party Salads. Ingredients: pieces of white chicken; 1 c. mayonnaise; 1 c. celery; 1 Tbsp. salt and pepper. Directions: Put chicken through meat grinder (hand operated). Mix with other ingredients in a bowl.

title: The Best Chili
text: The Best Chili. Ingredients: 2 sliced onions; 2 lb. ground beef or moose; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 1 medium can tomatoes; 1 small can tomato paste; 1 Tbsp. sugar; 1 1/2 tsp. chili powder; 4 oz. can mushrooms; 1 tsp. chili powder; 1 (16 oz.) kidney beans. Directions: Cook sliced onions in butter until soft. Add next 6 ingredients and cook for 1 hour over low heat. Next, add mushrooms, 1 teaspoon chili powder and kidney beans and simmer for 15 minutes more.

title: Cheese Souffle(Recipe Originated With Joanne Hall, Kokomo, Indiana)
text: Cheese Souffle(Recipe Originated With Joanne Hall, Kokomo, Indiana). Ingredients: 1 stick butter; 1 pkg. Cracker Barrel cheese; 10 slices white bread, remove crusts and cube; 2 eggs, separated; 1/2 tsp. salt; 2 c. milk; 1 dash Tabasco; 1/2 tsp. dry mustard. Directions: Melt cheese and butter in pan. In large bowl, break bread. Beat egg yolks and the rest of the ingredients together with bread. Then add melted cheese and butter mixture. Beat egg whites and fold into mixture. Bake at 325° for 40 minutes until browned. Serves 6.

title: Easy Chicken Divan
text: Easy Chicken Divan. Ingredients: 2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen asparagus or broccoli spears; 2 c. sliced chicken or 3 boned chicken breasts; 2 cans cream of chicken soup; 2/3 to 1 c. mayonnaise; 1/2 tsp. curry powder; 1/2 c. soft bread crumbs or cracker crumbs; 1 Tbsp. melted butter; 1 tsp. lemon juice; 1/2 c. grated cheese; pimiento strips. Directions: Cook asparagus according to package directions; arrange in greased baking dish. Place chicken on top. Combine soup, mayonnaise, lemon juice and curry powder. Pour mixture over chicken; sprinkle with cheese and top with buttered bread crumbs. Garnish with pimiento. Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes.

title: Country Corn Muffins
text: Country Corn Muffins. Ingredients: 1 c. yellow cornmeal; 1 c. all-purpose flour; 1 Tbsp. plus 2 tsp. baking powder; 1/4 tsp. salt; 2 Tbsp. sugar (optional); 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 1 egg; 1 (8.5 oz.) can cream-style corn; 1 (8 oz.) carton commercial sour cream; 2 Tbsp. vegetable oil. Directions: Combine first 6 ingredients in a large bowl; make a well in center of mixture. Combine remaining ingredients; add to dry ingredients, stirring just until moistened. Spoon into greased muffin pans. Bake at 400° for 12 to 15 minutes or until golden brown. Yield: 2 dozen.

title: Barbecue Hamburger Sandwich Mix
text: Barbecue Hamburger Sandwich Mix. Ingredients: 1 1/2 to 2 lb. lean hamburger meat; 1 small onion; 1 c. tomato sauce; 2 dashes Worcestershire sauce; 1/2 lemon; salt and pepper; 1/2 bottle catsup; 2 cans water (use tomato sauce can); 3 Tbsp. chili powder. Directions: Lightly brown hamburger meat in small amount of shortening. Add chopped onion and saute. Add other ingredients. Simmer, stirring occasionally, until mixture is thick, but not dry. Serve over hamburger buns.

title: Pecan Pie Muffins
text: Pecan Pie Muffins. Ingredients: 1 c. chopped pecans; 1 c. firmly packed brown sugar; 1/2 c. flour; 1/2 c. butter or margarine, melted; 2 eggs. Directions: Mix first 3 ingredients together in large bowl. Make a well in center. In another bowl, mix eggs until foamy. Mix butter into eggs. Put egg mixture into dry ingredients. Mix well. Pour into foil cupcake liners. Bake at 350° for 20 to 25 minutes. You must use foil cupcake liners.

title: Red Pepper Round Steak
text: Red Pepper Round Steak. Ingredients: 1 (1 lb.) round steak; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/8 tsp. pepper; 2 Tbsp. chopped onion; 2 Tbsp. vegetable oil; 1 large sweet red pepper, chopped; 1 beef-flavored bouillon cube; 1 c. hot water; 1 (14 1/2 oz.) can tomato wedges, drained; 1 Tbsp. cornstarch; 1/4 c. water; 2 tsp. soy sauce; hot cooked noodles or rice. Directions: Partially freeze steak; slice diagonally across grain into 1/4-inch wide strips. Cut strips into 2-inch pieces. Sprinkle steak with salt and pepper.

title: Hunky Pory Cookies
text: Hunky Pory Cookies. Ingredients: 1 c. shortening; 1 1/2 c. sugar; 2 eggs; 2 tsp. cream of tartar; 1 tsp. soda; 1/2 tsp. salt; 2 3/4 c. flour; 1 1/2 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Cream sugar and shortening; add eggs and vanilla. Add dry ingredients. Chill in refrigerator at least 2 hours. Roll in balls the size of walnuts. Roll in mixture of 2 tablespoons cinnamon and 2 tablespoons sugar. Bake on an ungreased cookie sheet for 10 minutes.

title: Chutney
text: Chutney. Ingredients: 12 oz. jar orange marmalade; 3 Tbsp. vinegar; 2 Tbsp. sugar; 1 Tbsp. brown sugar; dash of salt; 1/4 tsp. curry powder; 1/4 tsp. ground ginger; 1 1/2 tsp. Worcestershire sauce. Directions: Boil all ingredients together until thickened to jam consistency. Cool and serve over cream cheese on crackers.

title: Derby Fruit Dip(Especially Strawberries)
text: Derby Fruit Dip(Especially Strawberries). Ingredients: 8 oz. sour cream; 1 to 2 Tbsp. brown sugar (use to taste); 1 to 2 tsp. vanilla (use to taste); small amount bourbon or spiced rum. Directions: Mix ingredients together and refrigerate overnight.

title: Crisco Pound Cake
text: Crisco Pound Cake. Ingredients: 1 c. Crisco; 2 c. sugar; 6 eggs; 1 Tbsp. vanilla flavoring; 2 c. cake flour. Directions: Cream together Crisco and sugar. Add eggs and beat very well. Add cake flour then vanilla flavoring. Beat very Well! Bake at 325° for 1 hour.

title: Broccoli And Chicken
text: Broccoli And Chicken. Ingredients: 2 c. chicken, cooked and cubed; 8 oz. sour cream; 2 pkg. chopped broccoli; 2 cans cream of mushroom soup. Directions: Mix together all ingredients and top with Pepperidge Farm dry stuffing mix. Top with butter and heat in a microwave to serve.

title: Low-Fat Chicken And Gravy
text: Low-Fat Chicken And Gravy. Ingredients: 4 to 6 boneless and skinless chicken breasts (may also use turkey breasts); 1 can Campbell's Healthy Request cream of celery soup; 1 can Campbell's Healthy Request cream of chicken soup; 1 can chicken broth; salt and pepper; poultry seasoning. Directions: Place chicken or turkey in ovensafe dish. Pour soup and broth over meat. Season with salt, pepper and poultry seasoning to taste. Bake, covered, in 350° oven about 1 hour or until meat is tender and no longer pink.

title: Spanish Bean Soup(Florida Cracker Style)
text: Spanish Bean Soup(Florida Cracker Style). Ingredients: 1 meaty ham bone; 2 cans garbanzo beans; 3 large potatoes, 1/2-inch chunks; 4 chorizos, cut in 1/2-inch chunks; 3 Vidalia onions (large slices); 1 head garlic or more; garlic herb seasoning; 1/4 tsp. black pepper. Directions: In a large heavy pot, put in bone and cover with water. Add 2 onions and garlic. Bring to a low boil and cook for 30 minutes. Now add pepper, herb seasoning, chorizos and garbanzo beans. Simmer for 45 minutes. Add potatoes and the last onions. Taste, add more garlic if needed. Simmer for 1 hour. Serve with warm Cuban bread.

title: Meat Dish
text: Meat Dish. Ingredients: 1 box spaghetti, cooked and drained well; 1 lb. hamburger meat (not cooked); 1 large onion; 1 green pepper; 3 pieces celery, cooked; salt and pepper; 1 can Hunt's tomato sauce or 1 (No. 3) can tomato juice. Directions: Add onion, green pepper and celery to spaghetti and meat; salt and pepper. Put in baking dish. Pour over all the tomato sauce or tomato juice. Bake 45 minutes at 325°.

title: Teriyaki Beef
text: Teriyaki Beef. Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. round steak, cut into 1/4-inch thick strips; 1/2 c. soy sauce; 5 Tbsp. sugar; 8 Tbsp. dry sherry; 2 cloves fresh garlic, minced; 1/2 lemon, thinly sliced; 2 tsp. fresh ginger root, finely chopped, or about 1/2 tsp. powdered ginger. Directions: Blend all the liquid ingredients and the seasonings. Marinate the steak strips in this sauce for 2 hours (or more). Drain and cook quickly over charcoal or in a small amount of cooking oil in a very hot frying pan. Quickly is the key word; the steak slices should be pink inside and are much better underdone than overdone.

title: Toasted Walnut Madeleines(Dessert)
text: Toasted Walnut Madeleines(Dessert). Ingredients: 2 oz. walnuts; 1 c. unsalted butter (at room temperature); 1/2 c. sugar; 3 large eggs; 2 Tbsp. Amaretto; 1/4 c. heavy cream; 1/4 tsp. almond extract; 1 1/3 c. flour; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. baking powder. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Toast walnuts on baking sheet in oven until lightly browned, 5 to 7 minutes. Set aside to cool. Butter miniature muffin molds and place in refrigerator.

title: Mostaccioli(Serves 6)
text: Mostaccioli(Serves 6). Ingredients: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. mostaccioli; 1 lb. ground beef; 3 Tbsp. oil; 1 clove garlic, finely chopped; 1/3 c. chopped onion; 4 c. tomatoes; 6 oz. can tomato paste; 3/4 c. water; 1 tsp. oregano; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 1/3 c. grated Parmesan cheese. Directions: Brown ground beef, onion and garlic in oil. Break up tomatoes well and add with tomato paste, water, oregano, salt and pepper to the ground beef. Cover and cook over low heat 1 1/2 hours. Spoon off excess fat. Cook mostaccioli as directed. Drain and place on serving platter. Top with sauce and sprinkle with cheese.

title: Wine Merchant Soup
text: Wine Merchant Soup. Ingredients: 6 c. beef broth; 1 c. chopped onions; 1 c. chopped celery; 1 c. chopped carrots; 2 Tbsp. butter; 2 Tbsp. oil; 1 c. sliced mushrooms; 1 c. Burgundy wine; 1 Tbsp. cornstarch; 1/2 c. cold water. Directions: Heat beef broth in large kettle. Saut onions, celery and carrots in butter and oil until just tender. Transfer with slotted spoon to broth and simmer. Use same oil and butter to saut mushrooms. When done, pour mushrooms and liquid into soup. Add wine and bring to a boil. Combine cornstarch and cold water, then add to soup. Heat until slightly thickened and serve. Serves 6.

title: Chocolate Chip Date Cake
text: Chocolate Chip Date Cake. Ingredients: 1 c. butter (do not substitute); 1 c. sugar; 2 eggs; 1 1/2 c. flour; 1 Tbsp. cocoa; 1 tsp. vanilla; 1 c. chocolate chips; 1/2 to 3/4 c. chopped nuts; 1 tsp. baking soda; 1 small pkg. dates, chopped. Directions: Put dates in 1 cup boiling water. Add baking soda; set aside to cool. Cream butter and sugar, then add eggs. Mix cocoa with flour. Add flour mixture and date mixtures alternately to butter mixture, then add vanilla.

title: Honolulu Chicken
text: Honolulu Chicken. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. cooking oil; 1 pkg. skinless, boneless chicken breasts; 1/4 c. flour; pinch of pepper; 1 c. peach preserves; 1/2 c. commercial barbecue sauce; 2 Tbsp. soy sauce; 1/4 c. onion; 1 large green pepper; 2 cans sliced water chestnuts; 1 large carrot. Directions: Mix flour, salt and pepper in bowl. Coat chicken in flour mixture and cook in oil until brown. Remove drippings from pan. Return chicken to pan. In a bowl mix peach preserves, barbecue sauce, soy sauce and onion; mix well. Cover chicken with sauce and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low and cook 40 minutes. Add water chestnuts, green pepper slices and carrot slices. Cook an additional 10 minutes. Serve over rice.

title: Bacon Chestnuts
text: Bacon Chestnuts. Ingredients: 1 c. chili sauce; 1 c. brown sugar; 1/2 c. mayonnaise; 2 cans whole water chestnuts; 1 lb. bacon, cut in half. Directions: Mix chili sauce, brown sugar and mayonnaise together. Wrap chestnut with 1 strip bacon. Spear with toothpick. Wrap 2 cans chestnuts. Place in single layer on baking dish. Add sauce. Bake at 325° for 1 hour. Serve warm.

title: Peanut Butter Crisscross
text: Peanut Butter Crisscross. Ingredients: 2 c. shortening; 2 c. sugar; 2 c. brown sugar; 4 eggs; 2 tsp. vanilla; 2 c. peanut butter; 6 c. flour; 4 tsp. baking soda. Directions: Mix sugar, shortening, eggs, brown sugar and vanilla. Add baking soda and peanut butter, then the flour. Use small scoop or spoon. Drop on ungreased cookie sheet. With fork, flatten out and make crisscross on top of cookie and bake at 350° for 10 minutes. Makes 10 dozen.

title: Brunch For A Bunch
text: Brunch For A Bunch. Ingredients: 1 lb. hot bulk sausage; 3 c. or 2 lb. bag hash brown potatoes, thawed; 1/2 tsp. salt; 3 c. shredded Cheddar cheese; 1/2 c. chopped green peppers; 12 eggs, beaten; 2 c. milk. Directions: Cook sausage in skillet until brown, stirring to crumble. Drain. Place hash browns in lightly greased 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish. Sprinkle with salt. Layer with sausage, cheese and green peppers. Combine eggs and milk, stirring well. Pour over green peppers. Bake at 350° for about 50 minutes. Serves 12 to 15.

title: Crab Canape
text: Crab Canape. Ingredients: 1 c. crabmeat; 1 Tbsp. butter; 2 Tbsp. flour; 2 Tbsp. Parmesan cheese, grated; 3/4 c. light cream; 1 egg yolk; 2 Tbsp. dry sherry; 1/2 tsp. salt; pepper; 36 canape shells. Directions: Melt butter in saucepan over low heat. Add flour and stir until smooth. Add cheese and cream. Cook until thickened, stirring constantly. Remove from heat and stir in crabmeat, salt and pepper. Beat egg yolk lightly with sherry and add to crab mixture. Chill until serving time. Just before serving, spoon into canape shells. Place on cookie sheet and bake at 350° for 5 to 10 minutes until top is bubbly.

title: Beef Tips And Rice
text: Beef Tips And Rice. Ingredients: 2 lb. beef tips; 1 can cream of mushroom soup; 1 can chicken soup; 1 can golden mushroom soup; 1 pkg. dry onion soup mix. Directions: Brown beef tips. Mix all together. Cook in oven at 325° for 3 hours. Serve over rice.

title: Caramel Delight Pie
text: Caramel Delight Pie. Ingredients: 1 c. pecans, chopped; 1 (7 oz.) pkg. coconut; 1 stick butter or margarine; 1 (8 oz.) box cream cheese; 1 can sweetened condensed milk; 1 (16 oz.) container Cool Whip; 1 jar caramel ice cream topping; 2 large graham cracker pie crusts. Directions: In a skillet, brown pecans and coconut in butter; set aside. Blend cream cheese and condensed milk together. Add all of the Cool Whip. Divide into pie crusts. Top with the coconut-pecan mixture, then drizzle the caramel topping over the top. Keep refrigerated.

title: Recipe For A Happy Marriage
text: Recipe For A Happy Marriage. Ingredients: 1 c. consideration; 2 c. flattery, carefully concealed; 2 c. praise; 1 pinch in-laws; 1 generous dash of cooperation; 1 c. contentment; 2 children (at least); 1 c. blindness to faults of others; 2 c. milk of human kindness; 1 gal. faith in God and each other; 1 reasonable budget; 3 tsp. pure extract of I'm sorry; 1 c. confidence; 1 c. encouragement; 1 c. several hobbies. Directions: Take one child. Mix with generous pats of good humor and understanding. Blend in discipline as needed. Surround with music, love of nature, pets and friends. Bake in a warm, pleasant atmosphere. Use large amounts of patience. Do not be discouraged if at times objects do not seem to be turning out to your perfection. Continue basting. Before you realize it, your delight turns out even better than you had even hoped for. Release when properly aged.

title: Brunswick Stew(Vegetarian)
text: Brunswick Stew(Vegetarian). Ingredients: 2 cans corn Niblets; 1 can stewed tomatoes; 1 medium onion, chopped; 1 green pepper, chopped; 6 slices crisp bacon; 2 Tbsp. bacon drippings; 1 small clove garlic, minced; 1 pkg. frozen okra (optional); 1/4 tsp. red pepper; 1/2 tsp. salt. Directions: Brown bacon, remove from pan and reserve 2 tablespoons bacon drippings. Saute onion and pepper until slightly tender, pour into large skillet or Dutch oven. Add corn, tomatoes, bacon and 2 tablespoons bacon drippings. Add garlic, pepper and salt. Simmer 10 to 15 minutes, adding okra (if desired) for the last six minutes. Stir lightly twice during cooking.

title: Jamaican Fruit Cake
text: Jamaican Fruit Cake. Ingredients: 2 lb. moist raisins; 2 lb. moist currants; 1 lb. citron, cut; 1 lb. pecans, chopped; 1 lb. almonds, chopped; 4 1/2 c. flour; 2 tsp. nutmeg; 1 tsp. mace; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1 lb. soft butter; 1 lb. brown sugar; 12 eggs; 16 oz. blackberry jelly; 1/2 c. cream; 1/2 c. canned fruit juice. Directions: Presoak fruit in wine or rum. Combine fruits and nuts. Sift flour and mix 2 cups with fruit and nuts. Cream butter until soft and blend with sugar. Add eggs, singly beating well. Add jelly.

title: Goblin Bread
text: Goblin Bread. Ingredients: 1 1/2 sticks oleo; 1 box light brown sugar; 3 eggs; 2 2/3 c. self-rising flour; 1/2 large bag chocolate chips; 1/2 c. pecans; 1/2 bag peanut butter chips. Directions: Mix well and spread into cake pan. Bake at 350° for 35 minutes.

title: Oil And Vinegar Salad Dressing
text: Oil And Vinegar Salad Dressing. Ingredients: 2/3 c. salad oil; 2/3 c. cider vinegar; 1 tsp. each: salt, pepper and dried Italian herbs. Directions: Put all ingredients in a pint jar; shake to mix well.

title: Sour Cream Raisin Pie
text: Sour Cream Raisin Pie. Ingredients: 2 Tbsp. cornstarch; 3/4 c. sugar; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1 tsp. nutmeg; 2 egg yolks; 1 c. sour cream; 1 c. raisins; 1/2 tsp. lemon juice; 1/2 c. nuts, chopped; 1 baked pie shell; meringue. Directions: Mix cornstarch, sugar, salt and nutmeg; add egg yolks. Mix. Add sour cream, raisins, lemon juice and nuts. Cook until thick. Pour into baked pie shell. Spread meringue on top and brown.

title: Strawberry Angel Food Cake
text: Strawberry Angel Food Cake. Ingredients: 1 box angel food cake mix; red food coloring; 2 egg whites; 1 c. sugar; dash of salt; 2/3 c. sliced strawberries. Directions: Prepare angel food cake according to directions. Just before baking, add red food coloring and stir into cake. Bake according to directions. Combine egg whites, sugar and strawberries in top of double boiler; beat with mixer to mix. Place over rapidly boiling water; beat constantly with mixer. Cook 7 minutes, or until mixture stands in peaks. Remove from boiling water; beat until cool. Fold in 2/3 cup strawberries. Spread over cake.

title: Copper Pennies(Carrots)
text: Copper Pennies(Carrots). Ingredients: 1 pkg. young carrots, thickly sliced; 2 medium onions, sliced; 1 large bell pepper, chopped; 1/2 c. salad oil; 1/2 c. sugar; 1/2 c. apple cider vinegar. Directions: Steam carrots until a fork pricks them easily. Boil the sugar, oil and vinegar together for 2 to 3 minutes. Pour carrots, onions and pepper into boiled mixture, stirring to coat them well.

title: Apple Cake Squares
text: Apple Cake Squares. Ingredients: 1 1/2 peeled chopped apples; 1 c. chopped pecans; 1 c. sugar; 1 c. flour; 1 tsp. baking soda; 1 tsp. salt; 1 egg; 3 Tbsp. butter, melted; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Mix 2 tablespoons sugar and 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon for topping.

title: Alabama Corn Bread
text: Alabama Corn Bread. Ingredients: 3 eggs; 1 (17 oz.) can creamed corn; 1 c. sour cream; 1/2 c. Crisco oil; 2 pkg. Martha White Cotton Pickin cornbread mix. Directions: Mix all together and bake in a 9 x 13-inch pan for 25 to 30 minutes at 350°.

title: Fried Venison
text: Fried Venison. Ingredients: 2 eggs; 1 c. milk; 1 lb. venison steaks or tenderloin (1/2-inch thick)*; 2 c. flour; salt; pepper; vegetable oil. Directions: Mix the eggs and milk together in a shallow dish. When blended, add the steaks to the mixture. Cover; place in the fridge for 2 to 3 hours, turning over steaks occasionally. Add salt and pepper to the flour to taste. Remove steaks from the milk and egg mixture. Roll in the flour; cook in oil over medium-high heat. Turn only once when the first side is brown. When done, dry meat on paper towels, while being careful not to knock off any of the breading.

title: Apple Honey Cake
text: Apple Honey Cake. Ingredients: 3 c. flour; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1 tsp. soda; 1 tsp. salt; 3 eggs, slightly beaten; 1 1/4 c. oil; 1/2 c. honey; 2 c. sugar; 1 c. chopped nuts; 3 c. fresh apples; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Mix flour, cinnamon, soda and salt together and add slightly beaten eggs. Add oil, honey and sugar. Cream and add to flour mixture. Fold in chopped nuts, fresh apples and vanilla. Bake at 350° for 1 hour and 15 minutes.

title: Rice And Chicken Casserole
text: Rice And Chicken Casserole. Ingredients: 1 can cream of chicken or cream of celery soup; 1 soup can milk; 3/4 c. uncooked regular rice; 1 envelope onion soup mix; 4 chicken breast halves. Directions: Heat oven to 350°. Mix chicken soup and milk; reserve half of the mixture. Mix remaining soup, rice and 1/4 of the onion soup mix. Pour into ungreased baking dish. Place chicken breasts on top. Pour reserved soup mixture over chicken breasts. Sprinkle with remaining onion soup mix. Cover with aluminum foil and bake for 1 hour. Uncover and bake 15 minutes longer.

title: German Style Spinach
text: German Style Spinach. Ingredients: 6 strips bacon; 2 Tbsp. flour; 2 c. milk; salt and pepper; 1 Tbsp. chopped onion; 2 pkg. frozen spinach. Directions: Fry bacon until crisp and remove from skillet. Crumble; set aside. In bacon drippings, blend in flour. Slowly add milk, stirring to make a smooth sauce. Add salt, pepper and chopped onion. Cook spinach following directions. Add cream sauce and let simmer until thick (5 to 8 minutes). Serve and top with crumbled bacon.

title: Easy Skillet Supper
text: Easy Skillet Supper. Ingredients: 1 c. elbow macaroni; 1/2 lb. raw beef or cubed cooked beef; 2 Tbsp. butter; 2 c. tomato juice; 1 medium onion, chopped; 1 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 1 c. cubed cheese (optional). Directions: Melt butter in frypan at low heat. Add macaroni; stir to coat thoroughly with butter. Add tomato juice; bring to rapid boil. Stir in beef and onion, salt and pepper. Cover; reduce heat to 250° and cook for 15 to 20 minutes. Remove cover and sprinkle with cheese. Serve with tossed salad.

title: Puppy Chow(Party Mix)
text: Puppy Chow(Party Mix). Ingredients: 1 box Crispix cereal; 1 (20 oz.) bag chocolate chips; 1 stick margarine; 2 Tbsp. peanut butter; 5 c. powdered sugar. Directions: In large bowl with a lid, melt chocolate, margarine and peanut butter. Pour cereal in the bowl. Stir well with spoon. Pour powdered sugar in with mix. Put the lid on the bowl and shake well. Enjoy!

title: Bishop Asbury Pound Cake
text: Bishop Asbury Pound Cake. Ingredients: 4 sticks butter, softened; 4 c. sugar; 10 eggs, at room temperature; 2 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice; 2 tsp. vanilla; 4 c. sifted cake flour (may use 3 1/2 c. all-purpose flour). Directions: Preheat oven to 325°. Grease and flour a 12-cup tube pan. Cream together butter and sugar using a heavy-duty electric mixer. Add eggs one at a time and with the mixer running continuously at medium speed, add lemon juice and vanilla. Finally, add the flour a little bit at a time. Pour thoroughly mixed batter into prepared pan and bake for 1 1/2 hours or until a straw test shows that it is done. Remove to a rack to cool about 15 minutes, then turn out onto the rack.

title: Jan Hagel(Swedish Cookie)
text: Jan Hagel(Swedish Cookie). Ingredients: 1 c. margarine; 1 c. sugar; 1 egg yolk; 1 egg white; 2 c. flour; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 c. chopped walnuts. Directions: Cream together the margarine, sugar and egg yolk. Add flour and cinnamon. Pat into jelly roll pan. Beat the egg white with 1 teaspoon water until fluffy; brush on dough. Sprinkle with nuts. Bake for 20 minutes at 350°.

title: Lemon Surprise
text: Lemon Surprise. Ingredients: 2 c. flour; 2 sticks butter or margarine, softened; 3/4 c. chopped pecans or walnuts; 2 c. powdered sugar; 1 (3 oz.) and 1 (18 oz.) block cream cheese; 1 can lemon pie filling; 1 (9 oz.) container Cool Whip. Directions: Combine flour, butter and chopped nuts. Spread on bottom of 9 x 13-inch greased pan (glass). Bake mixture at 350° for 30 minutes; cool. Combine powdered sugar and cream cheese. Spread on top of cooled nut mixture. Layer lemon pie filling on top. Top with Cool Whip. Refrigerate overnight.

title: Crab Meat Canapes
text: Crab Meat Canapes. Ingredients: 1 (5 oz.) jar Old English cheese spread; 1/2 tsp. seasoned salt; 1/2 tsp. garlic powder; 1/2 c. melted butter; 1 Tbsp. mayonnaise; 1 (7 oz.) can crab meat; 6 English muffins, split. Directions: Combine cheese spread, seasonings, butter, mayonnaise and crab meat in bowl; mix well. Spread on muffin halves. Freeze just until firm. Cut into quarters. Store, wrapped, in freezer. Preheat oven to 350°. Place on baking sheet. Bake for 15 minutes. Makes 4 dozen.

title: Peanut Butter And Chocolate Chunk Cookies
text: Peanut Butter And Chocolate Chunk Cookies. Ingredients: 1/2 c. butter, softened; 3/4 c. sugar; 2/3 c. brown sugar; 2 egg whites; 1 1/4 c. chunky peanut butter; 1 1/2 tsp. vanilla extract; 1 c. all-purpose flour; 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 1/4 tsp. salt; 5 (2.1 oz.) chocolate covered crispy peanut butter candy bars, cut into 1/2-inch pieces. Directions: Beat butter at medium speed with an electric mixer until creamy; gradually add sugar and brown sugar, beating well. Add egg whites, beating well. Stir in peanut butter and vanilla; set aside.

title: Tangy Dill Dip(Low Calorie)
text: Tangy Dill Dip(Low Calorie). Ingredients: 1 c. plain low-fat yogurt; 1/4 tsp. dill seed; 1/4 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper. Directions: Combine and serve fresh vegetable slices.

title: Manicotti Florentine
text: Manicotti Florentine. Ingredients: 1/2 c. onion, chopped; 1 garlic clove, minced; 1 Tbsp. butter; 2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen chopped spinach, cooked and drained; 1 lb. Ricotta cheese; 4 oz. Mozzarella cheese, shredded; 1/2 c. Parmesan cheese; 3 eggs, slightly beaten; 1 Tbsp. lemon juice; 1 tsp. salt; dash of pepper; 12 large manicotti noodles, cooked and drained; 1 (16 oz.) jar spaghetti sauce. Directions: Saute onion and garlic in butter until tender. Combine sauteed vegetables, spinach, cheeses, eggs, lemon juice, salt and pepper; mix well. Fill noodles with spinach mixture, place in 13 x 9-inch baking dish. Cover; bake at 350° for 40 minutes. Heat sauce; pour over noodles. Sprinkle with additional Parmesan cheese, if desired.

title: Caramel-Peach Crunch Dessert
text: Caramel-Peach Crunch Dessert. Ingredients: 1/2 c. flour; 1 c. uncooked rolled oats; 3/4 c. packed dark brown sugar; 1 tsp. cinnamon; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 c. margarine, melted; 1 (29 oz.) can cling peach slices, drained; vanilla ice cream. Directions: Preheat oven to 400°. In a bowl, combine flour, oats, sugar, cinnamon and salt. Add butter. Mix well.

title: Lazy Housewife Pickles
text: Lazy Housewife Pickles. Ingredients: 5 c. sliced cucumbers; 1/2 c. sliced onions; 1/4 c. chopped green peppers; 1 c. sugar; 1/2 c. cider vinegar; 1 Tbsp. salt; 1 Tbsp. celery seed. Directions: Mix well and refrigerate. Ready to eat in a few hours. Keeps well a long time, covered, in refrigerator.

title: Eggs In A Basket
text: Eggs In A Basket. Ingredients: 6 slices soft whole wheat bread; softened butter; 6 eggs; 1/4 c. grated cheese. Directions: Preheat oven to 400°. Trim crusts off bread; spread both sides of bread with butter. Fit bread slices into cups of muffin tin. Place in oven until lightly toasted; remove from oven. Break an egg into bread and top with cheese. Return to oven and heat 12 to 15 minutes. Slip baskets carefully from the tins. Cool a bit.

title: Coca-Cola Fried Chicken
text: Coca-Cola Fried Chicken. Ingredients: 1 fryer, cut up or 3 lb. chicken breast; 12 oz. Coca-Cola; 1 c. ketchup. Directions: Remove skin from chicken and place in skillet. Mix Coca-Cola and ketchup together. Pour mixture over chicken. Bring to a boil, then lower heat and simmer. Let simmer until chicken is tender.

title: Apricot Dessert
text: Apricot Dessert. Ingredients: 1 angel food cake, broken into pieces; 46 oz. apricot nectar; 1 1/4 c. sugar; 7 Tbsp. cornstarch; 1 c. chopped walnuts. Directions: Mix cornstarch and sugar. Add nectar and cook, bringing to a boil. Stir constantly and cook until thickened. Cool slightly.

title: Aunt Donna'S Cheese Ball
text: Aunt Donna'S Cheese Ball. Ingredients: 2 pkg. (8 oz.) cream cheese; 3/4 c. crumbled Blue cheese; 1 c. shredded sharp Cheddar cheese; 1 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce or red cooking sherry; parsley, finely snipped; small crisp crackers; 1/4 c. onions (optional). Directions: Place cheeses in small bowl. Let stand at room temperature until soft. Blend in onions and Worcestershire sauce at low speed. Beat on medium speed until fluffy, scraping side and bottom of bowl frequently. Cover and chill 3 to 4 hours. Shape into ball. Roll in parsley and pecans. Cover and chill 2 hours or until firm.

title: Country Cookies
text: Country Cookies. Ingredients: 2 c. vegetable shortening; 2 c. granulated sugar; 2 c. firmly packed dark brown sugar; 4 large eggs; 2 tsp. vanilla; 3 c. all-purpose flour; 2 tsp. baking soda; 2 tsp. salt; 4 c. quick cooking oats; 2 c. shredded coconut. Directions: In the large bowl of an electric mixer, cream shortening, granulated sugar and brown sugar. Add eggs and vanilla; beat until blended. Add flour. Sprinkle baking soda and salt over top. Beat until no traces of flour can be seen. With a wooden spoon, work in oats and coconut.

title: World Famous Meat Loaf
text: World Famous Meat Loaf. Ingredients: 2 lb. ground round steak; 2 eggs; 1 1/2 c. bread crumbs; 3/4 c. ketchup; 1 tsp. Accent; 1/2 c. warm water; 1 pkg. Lipton onion soup mix. Directions: Beat thoroughly. Put into loaf pan. Cover with 2 strips bacon, if you like that flavor. Pour over all one 8 ounce can Hunt's tomato sauce. Bake one hour at 350°. Serves six.

title: Loris'S Egg Salad
text: Loris'S Egg Salad. Ingredients: 6 hard-boiled eggs; 1 small jar pimento; 1/3 c. Miracle Whip; 1 chopped sweet gherkin pickle; 1/4 c. chopped onion; 1/4 c. chopped green pepper; 1 tsp. salt; dash of paprika. Directions: With a potato masher, mix eggs, onion, pepper and pickle. Stir in mayonnaise. Add drained pimentos and seasonings. Chill and serve.

title: Mom'S Egg Noodles
text: Mom'S Egg Noodles. Ingredients: flour; 4 eggs; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/16 tsp. baking powder (I just take a pinch in my fingers). Directions: Beat eggs, salt and baking powder together good. Then add flour to make a stiff dough. Flour linen table cloth and sprinkle with flour. Put dough on floured table cloth and knead until smooth. Roll out on cloth and let dry. When dry, fold in half, then in half again until a narrow roll. Then slice to desired thickness. Cook in roast or chicken broth.

title: Homemade Salsa Recipe
text: Homemade Salsa Recipe. Ingredients: about 6 to 8 finely chopped tomatoes (Roma tomatoes work best); 1 large finely chopped onion; green and red onions (if you have them); 1 jalapeno, minced (1/2 jalapeno with seeds removed if you prefer it mild); 1 garlic clove, minced; 1/3 c. finely chopped fresh cilantro; juice squeezed from 1 to 2 limes; salt and pepper to taste. Directions: Mix everything together and use with Tostitos chips or garnish on your favorite Mexican dish. It's also very delicious on white chili.

title: Low-Fat Honey-Basil Chicken
text: Low-Fat Honey-Basil Chicken. Ingredients: 1 c. raspberry vinegar; 3 Tbsp. Dijon mustard; 2 Tbsp. low sodium soy sauce; 2 Tbsp. honey; 2 Tbsp. minced fresh basil; 1/2 tsp. dried thyme; pinch of ground black pepper; 4 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves. Directions: In a shallow glass baking dish, mix the vinegar, mustard, soy sauce, honey, basil, thyme and pepper. Add the chicken and turn to coat all sides. Allow to marinate at room temperature for 15 minutes. Transfer the chicken to a grill or broiling rack, reserve the marinade and place in a 1-quart saucepan.

title: Double Crust Pizza
text: Double Crust Pizza. Ingredients: 2 loaves frozen wheat bread dough, thawed; 1 lb. ground beef; 1 medium onion, thinly sliced; 2 Tbsp. cornmeal; 1 c. grated Parmesan cheese; 1 (6 oz.) can mushrooms, drained; 8 oz. sliced Mozzarella cheese; 1 small green pepper, cut in thin strips; 1 (15 1/2 oz.) jar extra thick spaghetti sauce; 2 tsp. crushed thyme; 1 tsp. crushed fennel seed; milk; cornmeal. Directions: Shape each loaf of bread dough into a ball; cover and let rise until nearly double. Punch down, cover and let rest 10 minutes.

title: Blueberry Cheese Pie
text: Blueberry Cheese Pie. Ingredients: 1 can blueberry pie filling; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese, softened; 1 can Eagle Brand milk; 1/2 c. lemon juice; more sugar and lemon (if desired). Directions: Add Eagle Brand milk and lemon juice to softened cream cheese. Pour 1/2 blueberry pie filling in a graham cracker crust. Fold in Cool Whip. Pour over pie mix. Add Cool Whip, milk and lemon juice. Then put the rest of the pie filling on top. Let cool several hours.

title: Crispy Potato Quiche(Serves 6)
text: Crispy Potato Quiche(Serves 6). Ingredients: 1 (24 oz.) pkg. frozen shredded hash browns, thawed; 1/3 c. melted butter; 1 c. shredded hot pepper cheese; 1 c. shredded Swiss cheese; 1 c. diced, cooked ham; 1/2 c. half and half; 2 eggs; 1/4 tsp. seasoned salt. Directions: Press thawed hash browns between paper towels to remove moisture. Fit hash browns into greased 10-inch pie plate, forming a solid crust. Brush crust with melted butter, making certain to brush tip edges. Bake at 425° for 25 minutes. Remove from oven. Sprinkle cheeses and ham evenly over bottom of crust. Beat half and half with eggs and seasoned salt. Pour over cheeses and ham. Bake, uncovered, at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes or until knife inserted in center comes out clean. Frozen hash browns cut the preparation time for this spicy quiche.

title: Baked Spinach Cheese Squares
text: Baked Spinach Cheese Squares. Ingredients: 2 pkg. frozen spinach; 1/2 c. flour; 2 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. pepper; 6 eggs, well beaten; 1 large (16 oz.) carton cottage cheese; 4 c. cheese, grated. Directions: Thaw spinach. Blend all ingredients and put in an 11 x 7-inch buttered casserole. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes. Cut in squares to serve.

title: Never Fail Fudge
text: Never Fail Fudge. Ingredients: 4 c. sugar; 5 Tbsp. cocoa; 1 c. evaporated milk; 2/3 c. white syrup; 1/8 tsp. salt; 1/4 lb. butter or oleo; 2 tsp. vanilla; 2 c. chopped nuts. Directions: Combine sugar, cocoa, syrup and milk. Stir well. Add salt and butter. Place on medium heat and bring to a rapid boil, as soon as mixture begins to boil, time for 6 minutes.

title: Zesty Sauerkraut
text: Zesty Sauerkraut. Ingredients: 16 oz. sauerkraut, rinsed; 1 Tbsp. butter; 2 medium onions, chopped; 2 bacon slices, crumbled; 8 oz. beer; 2 Tbsp. molasses; 2 Tbsp. mustard; 1/2 green or red pepper, chopped. Directions: Saute onion and pepper in butter. Broil bacon and crumble. Rinse sauerkraut and place in baking dish. Add the beer, molasses and mustard to onions, peppers and bacon. Cook and stir for 3 minutes. Pour over sauerkraut and bake 30 minutes at 350°.

title: Okra Omelet
text: Okra Omelet. Ingredients: 2 c. okra, cut crosswise 1/4-inch; 1 egg; 1/4 tsp. pepper; 4 Tbsp. bacon drippings; 1/2 c. milk; 1/2 c. hush puppy mix; 1/2 tsp. flavor enhancer; 1 tsp. salt. Directions: Mix all ingredients except the bacon drippings in a bowl. Pour the bacon drippings in a baking dish. Pour the mixture in a pan and pat down flat. Place pan in preheated oven at 325° for 30 minutes. Turn omelet and bake 5 to 10 minutes. Cut to serving sizes and serve warm. Serves 4 to 6.

title: Easy Banana Nut Bread
text: Easy Banana Nut Bread. Ingredients: 1 c. sugar; 1/2 c. shortening; 3 eggs, beaten; 2 c. flour; 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 1/4 tsp. salt; 3 ripe bananas, mashed; 1/2 c. chopped nuts. Directions: Cream shortening and sugar in mixer bowl until light. Beat in eggs, one at a time. Combine flour, baking soda and salt in large bowl. Add to shortening mixture alternately with bananas, mixing well after each addition. Stir in nuts. Spoon into greased 5 x 9-inch loaf pan. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes or until brown. Cool in pan for 15 minutes. Remove to wire rack to cool completely.

title: Old Fashioned Sugar Cookies
text: Old Fashioned Sugar Cookies. Ingredients: 2 cups butter - No substitute; 2 cups powdered sugar; 1 cup sugar; 2 eggs; 2 tsp. vanilla; 1 tsp. baking soda; 1 tsp. cream of tartar; 1 tsp. salt; 4 1/2 cups flour. Directions: Cream butter and sugar. Add eggs, vanilla, baking soda, cream of tartar and salt. Mix well. Slowly add flour. Mix and chill. Roll into balls and place on greased cookie sheet. Flatten and sprinkle with sugar. Bake at 350° for 10 to 15 minutes. Cool in pan 2 minutes then place on wire rack. Can be decorated.

title: Cranberry-Pear Pie
text: Cranberry-Pear Pie. Ingredients: 4 medium pears, (Bosc, Bartlett or Anjou); 1 c. sugar; 1/4 c. all-purpose flour; 1 Tbsp. finely shredded orange peel; 1 tsp. ground cinnamon; 2 c. cranberries; pastry for double crust pie; 1 Tbsp. margarine or butter, cut up. Directions: Peel, core and slice pears (approximately 5 cups). In mixing bowl combine sugar, flour, orange peel and cinnamon. Add pears and cranberries; toss to coat. Set aside.

title: Pineapple-Carrot Bread
text: Pineapple-Carrot Bread. Ingredients: 3 c. all-purpose flour; 2 c. sugar; 1 tsp. ground cinnamon; 1 tsp. soda; 3/4 tsp. salt; 3 eggs; 2 c. shredded carrots; 1 c. vegetable oil; 1 (8 oz.) can crushed pineapple, drained; 1 c. chopped nuts (pecans or walnuts); 2 tsp. vanilla; 3/4 c. confectioners sugar; 1 to 2 1/2 tsp. milk. Directions: In a large bowl, combine flour, sugar, soda, salt and cinnamon. In another bowl, beat eggs; add carrots, oil, pineapple, nuts and vanilla. Add dry ingredients and stir until moistened. Spoon into two greased and floured 8 1/2 x 4 1/2 x 2 1/2-inch loaf pans. Bake at 350° for 60 to 75 minutes, until loaves test done. Cool for 10 minutes in pans before removing to wire racks. Cool. Combine confectioners sugar and milk. Drizzle over loaves.

title: Hot Sponge Cake
text: Hot Sponge Cake. Ingredients: 2 eggs; 1 c. sugar; 1 c. self-rising flour; 3 Tbsp. butter; 1/2 c. milk; 1 tsp. vanilla. Directions: Beat eggs; beat in sugar. Beat in flour. Melt butter in milk and pour over dry ingredients. Add vanilla. Bake in 10-inch square pan at 350° for 25 minutes. May frost or serve plain with fruit, such as strawberries.

title: Spiced Apple Wedges
text: Spiced Apple Wedges. Ingredients: 4 small apples (about 1 lb.); 3/4 c. sugar; 2 Tbsp. margarine; 1/2 tsp. cinnamon; 1 1/2 c. water. Directions: Peel and core apples. Cut into wedges and set aside. In a large skillet, combine sugar, margarine and cinnamon. Add water. Cook and stir over medium heat until water boils and sugar dissolves. Add apple wedges to syrup. Return to boiling. Reduce heat. Simmer, uncovered, 10 to 15 minutes or until just tender.

title: Hamburg Porcupines
text: Hamburg Porcupines. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground beef; 1 c. cooked rice; 1 egg; 1/2 c. chopped onion; 1 can tomato soup; 1/2 can water; 1/4 tsp. mustard. Directions: Mix 1/4 cup soup with beef, rice, egg and onion. Roll into meatballs and brown in shortening. Blend remaining soup, water and mustard. Add to meatballs; cover and simmer for 25 to 30 minutes on low.

title: Oatmeal Cookies With Dried Cranberries And Apricots
text: Oatmeal Cookies With Dried Cranberries And Apricots. Ingredients: 1/2 c. (1 stick) butter or margarine, softened; 1/3 c. each: granulated sugar and light brown sugar, packed; 1 large egg or egg substitute; 2 tsp. vanilla; 2/3 c. all-purpose flour; 1/2 tsp. baking soda; 1/2 tsp. salt (optional); 1 c. rolled oats; 3/4 c. each: dried cranberries and apricots, snipped into bits. Directions: Put rack in center of oven. Heat oven to 350°. Lightly grease baking sheet. Cream butter or margarine and sugar with an electric mixer or food processor fitted with a metal blade. Add egg and vanilla. Mix until smooth. Add flour, baking soda, salt and oats. Mix until just combined. Stir in cranberries and apricots. Drop batter by tablespoon onto a prepared baking sheet, spacing cookies 2 1/2-inches apart. (They spread considerably.) Bake until cookies are a medium brown, about 11 minutes. Let cool 1 minute on cookie sheet. Use metal spatula to transfer to wire rack to cool completely.

title: Tuna Fish Pick-Me-Up
text: Tuna Fish Pick-Me-Up. Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. crushed crackers; 2 cans tuna fish (6 1/2 oz.); 1/3 c. minced onion; 1/2 tsp. salt and pepper; 1/4 c. green peppers, minced; 1 egg, beaten; 1/3 c. milk; 1 tsp. butter, melted; 1 (10 oz.) can cream of celery soup; 2 sliced, hard-boiled eggs. Directions: Crush crackers coarsely into a large bowl. Add tuna, onion, salt, pepper, green peppers, egg and milk. Blend well and form into four individual loaves. Place loaves in greased, shallow baking dish. Brush with melted butter. Bake in oven for 30 minutes at 375°. Heat soup and sliced eggs. Pour over loaves placed on a platter. Can be made into one large loaf.

title: Spaghetti Fruit Salad
text: Spaghetti Fruit Salad. Ingredients: 1 c. xx sugar; 2 eggs; 1/2 c. lemon juice; 1/2 tsp. salt; 8 oz. spaghetti, broken into 2-inch pieces; 1 (20 oz.) can pineapple tidbits; 3 medium tart apples, diced; 1 (8 oz.) Cool Whip, thawed; 1/4 chopped walnuts; maraschino cherries, halved. Directions: In a saucepan, combine sugar, eggs, lemon juice and salt. Cook and stir over medium heat until temperature reaches 160° until mixture is thickened. Cool completely. Cook spaghetti according to package directions. Drain and rinse in cold water. Place in a large bowl. Drain pineapple, reserving juice. Pour juice over the spaghetti. Stir in apples. Toss gently. Drain. Stir in the egg mixture. Add pineapple. Cover and refrigerate overnight. Fold in whipped topping just before serving. Garnish with walnuts and cherries.

title: Maryland Crab Cakes
text: Maryland Crab Cakes. Ingredients: 1 lb. backfin crabmeat; 1 egg; 2 tsp. prepared mustard; 3 to 4 Tbsp. mayonnaise; 1 slice bread, crumbled; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/2 tsp. paprika. Directions: Combine and mix all ingredients thoroughly. Chill for 2 hours. Shape into cakes. Melt 3 tablespoons of butter in a skillet over a quick fire. Brown the cakes on both sides. Yield: 6 cakes.

title: Rotini Salad
text: Rotini Salad. Ingredients: 3 c. (1/2 pkg.) Hogdson Mill rotini; 1 medium green pepper, chopped; 1 c. black olives, drained and sliced; 3 c. fresh broccoli, chopped; 1/2 c. saffron or safflower oil; 1 pkg. Good Seasons Italian dressing; 1 medium tomato, chopped; 1 c. diced cheese; 2 c. red grapes; celery, chopped (optional); 1/3 c. tarragon vinegar. Directions: In a bowl, blend together the oil, vinegar and Italian dressing. In a large bowl, combine other ingredients. Stir. Pour dressing over and stir. Chill. Keeps good in refrigerator. May add other vegetables such as onions, carrot slices, cauliflower, etc.

title: Chili Rellenos
text: Chili Rellenos. Ingredients: 1 can California green chilies (6 to 8); 1/2 lb. Jack cheese, cut into 1/2 x 1/2-inch strips; 1/2 c. flour; 3 eggs, separated; 3 Tbsp. flour; 1/4 tsp. salt; oil for frying. Directions: Drain canned chilies. Stuff with cheese. Roll each chili in flour to completely coat. Shake off excess.

title: Peanut Butter Ice Cream Pie
text: Peanut Butter Ice Cream Pie. Ingredients: 1 (9-inch) graham cracker crust; 1 qt. vanilla ice cream, softened; 1/2 c. white Karo syrup; 1/3 c. creamy peanut butter; 2/3 c. chopped dry roasted, unsalted peanuts. Directions: Prepare crust. Cool. Press half of softened ice cream into crust. In small bowl, stir corn syrup and peanut butter until blended. Pour half of mixture over ice cream. Sprinkle with half of peanuts, repeating layering. Freeze until firm, about 5 hours. Let pie stand at room temperature for 5 minutes for easy cutting.

title: Dumbo'S Double-Thick Chocolate Malted
text: Dumbo'S Double-Thick Chocolate Malted. Ingredients: 1/2 c. milk; 2 scoops chocolate ice milk or ice cream; 3 Tbsp. chocolate-flavored malted milk. Directions: Pour the milk into a small mixing bowl. Add 1 scoop ice cream. Beat with an eggbeater until the ice cream is mixed in. Add the rest of the ice cream and the malted milk. Beat again until mixture is foamy. Pour into a glass to serve. Serves 1.

title: Baked Spareribs "Aloha"
text: Baked Spareribs "Aloha". Ingredients: 3 lb. (2 strips) lean spareribs; salt and pepper; 1/2 c. finely diced onion; 1/4 c. diced green pepper; 2 (8 oz.) cans tomato sauce; 1 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce; 1/3 c. cider or wine vinegar; 1/4 c. brown sugar; 1/2 tsp. dry mustard; 1 (No. 2) can pineapple tidbits (use all). Directions: Cut after every third rib, about halfway through the strip. Sprinkle with salt and pepper. Place in shallow roasting pan. Bake in moderate oven (350°) for 1 hour. Carefully drain off all excess fat. While ribs are roasting, mix remaining ingredients and let stand to blend flavors. Pour over ribs after 1 hour baking. Bake 45 to 50 minutes longer, basting frequently to coat ribs with the flavorful sauce.

title: Yankee Enchiladas
text: Yankee Enchiladas. Ingredients: 1 medium pkg. Fritos corn chips; 1 head lettuce; 1 medium onion, chopped; 1/4 lb. grated Cheddar cheese; 1 lb. ground beef; salt; 2 Tbsp. flour; 1 can tomato sauce; 2 cans water; 1/2 tsp. oregano; 1 tsp. chili powder; 1/4 tsp. garlic sauce. Directions: On platter put chips around edge. Prepare salad with lettuce, onion and Cheddar cheese. Heap salad in center of platter. Prepare sauce.

title: Yogurt Dressing
text: Yogurt Dressing. Ingredients: 1 c. plain yogurt; 1/4 c. olive oil; 2 Tbsp. chopped onion; 2 Tbsp. chopped parsley; 1/2 tsp. dry mustard; 1/2 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. pepper. Directions: In a jar or bowl, combine yogurt, olive oil, onion, chopped parsley, mustard, salt and pepper. Shake vigorously. Yields approximately 1 1/4 cups. Serve with tossed salad, sliced tomatoes or cold vegetables.

title: Italian Butter Nut Balls
text: Italian Butter Nut Balls. Ingredients: 1 c. butter, melted; 2 eggs, separated; 3 c. flour; 1 tsp. lemon extract; 1 c. sugar; 1 tsp. baking powder; 1 c. ground walnuts. Directions: Mix butter, sugar and egg yolks. Add flour, baking powder and lemon. When done, form small balls. Dip in egg whites which have been slightly beaten. Then roll in ground nuts. Press cherry on top. Bake at 375° for 12 minutes.

title: Cog Au Vin
text: Cog Au Vin. Ingredients: 4 slices bacon; 4 (6 oz.) sliced mushrooms; flour; salt and pepper; 2 cloves garlic, crushed; 1 1/2 Tbsp. parsley; 1 1/2 c. white wine; 6 to 8 pearl onions; 6 to 8 boneless chicken breasts; 6 Tbsp. butter; 3/4 tsp. tarragon; 1/2 tsp. thyme. Directions: Fry bacon until crisp, drain and crumble. In a bit of bacon grease, saute onions and mushrooms, then remove from pan. Lightly dredge chicken in flour, salt and pepper. Add butter to frying pan and brown chicken. Remove to casserole and sprinkle with garlic and herbs. Add bacon, onions and mushrooms and pour on wine. Cover and bake at 350° for one and one-half hours. Serves 4 to 6.

title: Venison Lasagna
text: Venison Lasagna. Ingredients: 1 lb. ground venison; 6 oz. lasagna noodles; 1 onion, chopped; 1/4 tsp. dried oregano; 1 (15 oz.) jar spaghetti sauce; 1 c. small curd cottage cheese; 1 pkg. Mozzarella cheese. Directions: Cook noodles in salted water. Brown meat and onion in oil. Drain. Add oregano and sauce to meat. Simmer for 15 minutes. Layer noodles, cottage cheese, meat sauce and Mozzarella cheese in pan. Repeat. Bake at 375° for 30 minutes.

title: Albondigas(Meatball Soup)
text: Albondigas(Meatball Soup). Ingredients: 2 large tomatoes, diced; 4 green onions, diced; 3 Tbsp. cilantro, diced (fresh, not dried); 3 Tbsp. mint, diced (fresh, not dried); 3 Tbsp. oregano, diced (fresh, not dried); 4 cloves garlic, minced; salt to taste; pepper to taste; 4 qt. water; 1 heaping Tbsp. flour; 5 lb. ground beef; 1 egg; 1 heaping Tbsp. flour; warm corn tortillas. Directions: Put 1/2 of first 8 ingredients in a large bowl and other 1/2 of same ingredients in a large soup pot. To pot, add approximately 4 quarts of water and 1 heaping tablespoonful of flour. Boil.

title: Easter Bread(Cutzuppi)
text: Easter Bread(Cutzuppi). Ingredients: 2 to 3 c. flour; 1/4 c. sugar; 1 tsp. salt; 1 pkg. dry yeast; 2/3 c. milk; 2 eggs (room temperature); 2 Tbsp. margarine; 3 Tbsp. anise seed; 3 tsp. anise extract; raw eggs, colored. Directions: In a bowl, mix 1 cup flour, sugar, salt and dry yeast. In a saucepan, mix milk and margarine. Heat milk enough until margarine is slightly melted. Gradually add to dry mix and stir well. Add both eggs and 1/2 cup flour. Add seed and extract. Make thick, smooth batter. Gradually add flour to make a soft dough. Turn onto lightly floured table. Knead well. Place in a greased bowl and let rise in a warm place until size doubles, about 1 hour. Punch down. Divide dough in 2. Shape - e.g. cross. Place a colored egg on top as decoration.

title: Squash And Corn Casserole
text: Squash And Corn Casserole. Ingredients: 6 medium yellow squash, sliced; 1/2 c. onion, diced; 1 c. creamed corn; 2 Tbsp. butter or margarine; 1/2 c. Cheddar cheese, grated; 1/2 c. croutons or toasted bread crumbs; 3/4 tsp. salt; 1/4 tsp. black pepper; 1 dash garlic powder; 1 dash paprika. Directions: Saute squash and onion in butter until tender. Stir in corn, salt, pepper, garlic and paprika; mix well. Spoon mixture into casserole dish. Top with croutons or bread crumbs and grated cheese. Bake at 300° for 30 minutes.

title: Party Mashed Potatoes
text: Party Mashed Potatoes. Ingredients: 8 to 10 potatoes, boiled and mashed; 1 (8 oz.) pkg. cream cheese; 1 carton sour cream with chives; 1/2 c. butter or margarine; salt and pepper; garlic salt (optional). Directions: Mix together and place in buttered casserole dish. (I use a 9 x 13-inch pan.) Refrigerate until ready to bake. Bake 1 hour at 350°. Serves 10 to 12.

title: Ground Beef With Cornbread(Serves 4)
text: Ground Beef With Cornbread(Serves 4). Ingredients: 1 1/2 lb. lean ground beef; 1/2 c. chopped green pepper; 1/4 c. chopped onions; 1/2 tsp. salt; 2 tsp. chili powder; 2 (15 oz.) cans tomato sauce; 1 (15 oz.) can pinto beans, undrained; 2 c. shredded sharp cheese; cornbread topping (your favorite mix). Directions: Brown beef, pepper, onions and salt. Add chili powder and tomato sauce. (If there is any grease, drain well.) Pour beef and tomato mixture into oiled 9 x 13-inch baking dish. Pour beans over meat mixture. Spread cheese over beans; set aside.

title: Ma Rene'S Tea Cakes
text: Ma Rene'S Tea Cakes. Ingredients: 1 c. sugar; 1 c. margarine; 2 large eggs; 4 c. self-rising flour; 1 tsp. vanilla extract; 1/2 tsp. lemon extract. Directions: Preheat oven to 350°. Beat sugar and margarine together until creamy. Add eggs and beat thoroughly, one at a time. Add flour, one cup at a time, mixing well. Add extracts and beat thoroughly.
